# Email Template Creation and Management
28 min
Copy
Email templates help your team work efficiently while still creating an excellent candidate experience. Have your application confirmation and rejection email copy ready to add to Ashby! [You can find sample email templates here!](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/18m9VOXhy6w92Ytvg-1IKeWqA0dy_D9II3DegRluNmgY/edit?usp=sharing "You can find sample email templates here!")﻿
The templates in the linked doc above are examples and, if used, substitution tokens﻿ would need to be added manually when copying them into Ashby.
Using this guide, you'll set up [email templates](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-templates "email templates") to streamline candidate communication. When a candidate reflects on your interview process, they'll likely think about two things:
  * The experience they have with your interviewers, and


  * How communicative your team is


Training your team is up to you, but with Ashby, you can create high-quality templates your team can use to create consistently excellent candidate experiences.
If email templates have already been created for your organization, see [Sending Emails from the Candidate Profile](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sending-emails-from-the-candidate-profile)﻿ learn how to apply them when sending emails to candidates.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#permissions-access)Permissions & access
See the chart below for information on the permissions needed to create and edit email templates. Elevated Access users will have access to these for the roles and departments they have been given the access role for.  
| Access Role  | Can edit company-wide email templates?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Only Organization Admins can configure company-wide email templates by default. Elevated Access users can be given access to edit company-wide email templates if needed. More on this at [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#email-templates-overview)Email templates overview
Watch the below video, or follow the written steps below, to understand how to set up email templates.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#creating-email-templates)Creating email templates
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#step-1-start-your-email-template-draft)Step 1: Start your email template draft
To access the email templates admin area, navigate to Admin from the top menu, Organization Setup on the left side, and then Email Templates. From here, you'll click the + New Template button the the right side. 
Email templates save automatically, so you won't need to hit a save button while working on them.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#step-2-use-the-email-editing-features)Step 2: Use the email editing features
The email editor is used both to create email templates in Admin, and a similar editor is used to send one-off emails from the candidate profile. You can write or copy/paste the content of your email template directly into the editor. The email editor supports the following features:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/570a9fcc5b99fa0c816bb219f880e304.png)
﻿
  * Text formatting


  * Code lines and blocks


  * Numbered and bulleted lists


  * Hyperlinks


  * Substitution tokens (including custom and email signature)


  * Images


For images, we would recommend using a minimum resolution of 1000px on the largest dimension of the image (for example 1024px x 256px for an image with a 4:1 ratio or 512 x 1024 for an image with a 1:2 ratio)
  * Write with AI (more on this at [AI Content Assistant](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-content-assistant)﻿)


  * HTML Headers and Footers


  * File attachments


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#step-3-add-substitution-tokens)Step 3: Add Substitution Tokens
Substitution Tokens allow you to pull information from the candidate’s profile or the job they’re being considered for into the emails you send out from Ashby. They can be added to both the email's subject and body.
For a full list of substitution tokens, please check out [Substitution Tokens](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens)﻿.
When adding substitution tokens into your email template, it will look something like this:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4bbf90bb81007832760c7dcd24d8672d.png)
﻿
When the template is used to send an email to a candidate, the substitution tokens will populate the associated details, like this:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c4f6be6c8ea205720b9cf6e0200a6f3d.png)
﻿
To add substitution tokens, you can click the T icon in the toolbar either by the subject line or in the toolbar under the body of the email. From here, you can search for the specific token you need. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/w2q3qG14goT9STuEmIGY6_email-templates-acent-ashby-2026-03-13-at-122816-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You’re also able to create substitution tokens from candidate custom fields. To do so, see [Create a candidate custom field](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#create-a-candidate-custom-field)﻿ and be sure to check the Enable in Email Templates box on the token’s settings.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#step-4-add-required-placeholders)Step 4: Add required Placeholders
Adding placeholders to an email will cause Ashby to refuse to send the email until the placeholder has been deleted or replaced with the relevant text. This helps ensure that all information is included prior to the email being sent out.
To add a placeholder, click the brackets at the bottom of the email editor. The below screenshot shows an Offer Template with an offer details placeholder added between the brackets.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1f9aa885bdbfa941f641e05f6e0baf24.png)
﻿
You’ll be asked to add in text for your placeholder. This will be shown in the template and will need to be removed or replaced when an email is generated using this template. Click Insert to then add your placeholder to the email template.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/84746bb94d655695f1b9ae0653f95fe6.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#what-a-placeholder-looks-like-when-emailing-a-cand)What a placeholder looks like when emailing a candidate
When generating an email with a placeholder on a candidate's profile, the placeholder will be highlighted in yellow to signify that it needs to be deleted or replaced with suitable text before sending the email.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/39f07b618e7468a2f44164462a211827.png)
﻿
If you try to send the email without deleting or removing the placeholder, you will receive an error message.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1687318f7eb058e07ca311675ac33ec2.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#final-step-update-additional-details)Final Step: Update additional details
If your email template should have someone CC'd or BCC'd by default, you can add them to these fields.
Additionally, if this template should only be used by specific departments or teams, you can click Edit Group Assignment near the top right of the editor and select the specific departments, teams, or groups the template should be visible to.
Once all details have been filled in, your template should be ready for use!
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#updating-and-archiving-email-templates)Updating and archiving email templates
If you need to change an email template after creating it, simply click into the template and make your updates. Changes will be saved automatically and will apply to any future uses of the template.
If the template is no longer needed, you can select More in the upper right corner of the template and choose Archive.
You can also duplicate templates to make multiple versions or tweaks by selecting More and then Duplicate.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#common-template-types-automations)Common template types & automations
There are a handful of template types we most commonly see created:
  * Application confirmation


  * Rejection


  * Interview invite


  * Offer


﻿[We have examples of each type](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/18m9VOXhy6w92Ytvg-1IKeWqA0dy_D9II3DegRluNmgY/edit#gid=753367080 "We have examples of each type") you can use as a starting point when creating templates. Once you’ve created these templates, you can use them in different areas of Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#application-confirmation-template)Application confirmation template
Set up an application confirmation email as the first message your inbound candidates receive upon applying via your job board. 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/59b9ddc5c477e141622dba60e3f5b9b9.png)
﻿
Once the template is created:
  * Navigate to Admin > Jobs & Applications > Application Email Automations. You should land on the **Inbound** tab by default


  * Select your application confirmation email template from the **Email Template** dropdown. This should then set the Send a Confirmation Email? status to Enabled.


  * Use the Preview Email option to preview the email sent to the referral, or click Edit Email to be taken to the email template to make further edits.


You can create additional application confirmation templates and link them to specific job postings. Within your job, head to the job posting in question, then navigate to the Automation tab to set an alternative application confirmation email.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/86174433ff64da32f20aa6245a587fc6.png)
﻿
Application confirmation emails (once configured) will only be sent to the candidate if they submit an application via your job board. This notification will not be sent if you add the candidate manually and consider them for a job or consider them for a job via the Chrome extension.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#referral-confirmation-template)Referral confirmation template
Referral confirmation emails are only sent to candidates submitted via the Direct Referral option. Those who have applied via a referral link will receive an inbound application confirmation instead (if enabled). This will also not be sent to existing candidates if the referrer is added manually via the Credited To field on the candidate profile.
If you’d like referred candidates to receive a confirmation email at the point they were submitted:
  * Navigate to Admin > Jobs & Applications > Application Email Automations.


  * Click the Referrals tab


  * Select your email template using the **Email Template** dropdown, this should then set the Send a Confirmation Email? icon to Enabled


  * Use the Preview Email to preview the email sent to the referral, or click Edit Email to be taken to the email template to make further edits


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8aade1a44f2d6eeb6c1f8aeb92a91a89.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#rejection-email-templates)Rejection email template(s)
When archiving a candidate, you are prompted with the option to send an email. Create one or more templates to streamline that process. To set up, navigate to [Email Templates](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-templates "Email Templates"), then click New... Configure the email using custom text and tokens as described above (saves automatically).
Once the template is created it can be used when archiving a candidate.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/af4777bd36de2b57cfa4e5b7c949f04a.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#offer-email-templates)Offer email template(s)
When it's time to make an offer, you have the option to create an offer email within the candidate profile. You can set up templates to streamline that process. To set up, navigate to Admin→Organization Setup→Email Templates, then click New... Configure the email using custom text and tokens described above (saves automatically).
Once the template is created, it can be used when extending an offer.
After generating an offer letter, you can draft and email and select an existing offer template that includes any standard language and attachments.
![After generating an offer letter, you can draft and email and select an existing offer template that includes any standard language and attachments. ](https://cdn.zappy.app/afc69223cc40824cbe132580ef8d007f.png)
﻿
﻿  

#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#im-seeing-a-missing-value-for-message-when-pulling)I**’m seeing a Missing Value For message when pulling up an email template. Why?**
This error will show if the value cannot be found. This is particularly common when:
  * You’re trying to send a direct booking link but an interview activity hasn’t been generated and a booking link hasn’t been created for the candidate.


  * You’re trying to send a candidate availability link but an interview activity hasn’t been generated and a candidate availability link hasn’t been created for the candidate.


  * If you’re trying to send out a survey and the survey request and link haven’t been generated yet.


  * If an interview has not been scheduled yet (panel information can only be pulled for interview emails if the interview has already been scheduled).


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/51dac655543e89e6bcb6c50c0518db50.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-add-an-email-signature-in-ashby)**Can I add an email signature in Ashby?**
Email signatures can be added to Ashby via two routes:
  * If you have the [Google Workspace](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/google-workspace)﻿ integration in place, then your Gmail signature should sync over and can be added to emails


  * You can set up an email signature in Personal Settings > [Email Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/email "Email Settings")﻿


Within Email Settings, you have the following options:
**Enable Email Signature:** Opt to automatically include your email signature in all emails sent from Ashby moving forward
**Before Quoted Content:** Enable this to insert your email signature before quoted text in replies and remove the "--" that precedes it
**Email Signature to use:** Determine whether you would like to use your Gmail signature (if applicable) or a custom one you can set up in Ashby
**Gmail/Custom Signature:** You can preview your Gmail signature here, or add your custom signature here.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/pZKkDtcIIJdUYFBZRAc2I_ashby-2026-01-29-at-111923-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When creating your custom signature, you can embolden, italicize or underline text. You can also insert bulleted or numbered lists, links or images.
If you've made changes to your Gmail signature and don't see the correct signature when previewing it in Ashby, you can click Refresh to import the new signature immediately. Please note that the Refresh button will be greyed out if you haven't authorized your Gmail account from the Google Workspace Settings page in Personal Settings.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kXfJj6gejPEnDPn0mQ5SX_ashby-2026-01-29-at-112139-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-delete-an-email-template)C**an I delete an email template?**
You can archive an email template by clicking More when in the edit screen for your chosen template and selecting Archive. It’s not currently possible to delete email templates completely.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-schedule-an-email-to-a-candidate)Can I schedule an email to a candidate?
If you don’t want to send an email to a candidate right away, you can schedule an email. Click Email on the candidate’s profile to create a draft. While drafting the email, click the Send drop-down to determine when the email should be sent. You can choose from three options:
Immediately: Send the email as soon as Send is clicked on the draft.
Date & Time: Specify a date and time for the email to be sent.
After Time Passed: Specify the number of days, weeks, etc that the email should be sent. You will have the option to determine the days the email should be sent on (a weekday, any day of the week or a specific day of the week) and also the time the email should be sent.
Click Schedule to confirm. The email activity under Current Activities on the candidate’s profile should show Scheduled Email. To amend or rescheduled a scheduled email you have drafted, click View on the Scheduled Email activity. You can then make any adjustments and click Reschedule to confirm your changes.
Please note: You can only view and reschedule a scheduled email if you are the user who originally drafted and scheduled the email. Users with access to email the candidate will have the option to delete the scheduled email activity.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6ee6f47624458ddb4bc9ef9aa234bd5d.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e0cef0793d1eb6a6a0f685e3093f6b38.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/eddcd20ac36ba541193ad2f87dc723cb.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#how-does-my-email-signature-get-synced-over-from-g)H**ow does my email signature get synced over from Gmail?**
If you have the Google Workspace integration in place, the signature default you have set in the For New Emails Use dropdown will be added to emails you send via Ashby. You can find this dropdown within the General setting of your inbox settings in Gmail.
If you make changes to your signature (or switch out to another signature within your Signature Defaults, make sure to save your settings in Gmail, then log out and log back in to your Ashby account. This will ensure these updates are synced prior to the next email sync, so you can use your updated email signature without having to wait.
When drafting your email, ensure the Include Email Signature from Gmail option is enabled in the email toolbar. You can also enable this option when creating email templates.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e3fb5828ffe4f1d85f18cbd84255ee29.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d07f6ff595d775ea1cff7ecd29090224.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-add-custom-headers-or-footers-to-my-email-te)Can I add custom headers or footers to my email template?
You can, yes. On the toolbar for your email templates, you will see arrow icons pointing up (Insert HTML Header) and pointing down (Insert HTML Footer).
When you click either option to add a header or a footer, a field will open up that will allow you to enter your HTML. Click Save to apply your changes.
For header and footer graphics, we would recommend using a minimum resolution of 1000px on the largest dimension of the image (for example 1024px x 256px for an image with a 4:1 ratio or 512 x 1024 for an image with a 1:2 ratio).
Quick note that Ashby allows for dynamic resizing in the email template editor for images between 200px to 830px using the handles on images.
﻿  

﻿  

﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9b18df44a1172bddca3d4a3e659931df.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5c59f93a37f4c4ba1ec8bd55ff390ac8.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-specify-a-font-and-font-color-when-setting-u)Can I specify a font and font color when setting up my email templates?
You can, yes. Font and font color controls can be found in the editor toolbar when configuring your email templates.
To add in a font, click the Font option in the toolbar, then add in the name of your chosen font. If you’d like to add multiple fonts, you can separate font names using a comma. **Please note:** Fonts will be applied in the order they are listed.
When adding a different font color to your email template, you can either select the color using the Color option in the editor toolbar prior to typing, or highlight the text in your finished template and adjust the color via the Color option at the end.
This is only currently possible with email templates and this can’t be set up on individually drafted emails or sequences.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3fee7331abd9e38b9d3b88f7528e71fc.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f4b297a4aa663b685601641af916022d.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/67fa030cd711f982c5a3ea7c8daedb40.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-send-emails-in-bulk-using-an-email-template)Can I send emails in bulk using an email template?
You can! For more information, check out [Taking Bulk Actions on Candidates.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#taking-bulk-actions-on-candidates)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-amend-the-senderfrom-name-shown-in-emails-se)Can I amend the sender/from name shown in emails sent from the Ashby no-reply email address?
You can amend your display name for emails send from the Ashby no-reply email address on the No-Reply Address Display Name field in the Messaging section of Admin > Job Boards > [Theme (Job Board)](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/theme "Theme \(Job Board\)").
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/901c4f84e0f3cb2520de57ce0c91d72b.png)
﻿
The display name cannot be empty and has a maximum length of 30 characters.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-determine-a-default-sender-when-creating-an)Can I determine a default sender when creating an email template?
No, the sender would be determined at the time of drafting up the email, or when setting up automation for emails.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-set-up-a-shared-email-alias)Can I set up a shared email alias?
You can! More on this at [Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-add-an-email-template-to-a-group-after-ive-c)Can I add an email template to a group after I’ve created it?
You can, yes. Click on the three dots underneath the template name, then click + Add to Group. Select your chosen group from the list to link your email template to it.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#where-can-i-view-email-tracking-data)W**here can I view email tracking data?**
Tracking data like the open rate and click rate can only be viewed for sequences﻿. Navigate to your chosen sequence within Sourcing > [Sequences](https://app.ashbyhq.com/sourcing/sequence-templates "Sequences") to view the sequence tracking data as needed. If you need to make amendments to your email tracking settings, you can do this within Personal Settings > Email Settings.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-use-gifs-and-emojis-in-my-email-templates-an)Can I use GIFs and emojis in my email templates and emails?
﻿  

You can, yes. 
With GIFs, they will need to be uploaded via the Insert Image option on the email or email template toolbar. There is a file limit of 24MB. If a GIF is dragged and dropped into the email or email template, it will not render properly in the email.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b78a9b2eaea4a0b4453c611cfebba0ba.png)
﻿
While there isn’t an in-app emoji keyboard, emojis are supported in emails and email templates.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management#can-i-set-custom-text-for-a-url-substitution-link)Can I set custom text for a URL substitution link?
You can now set custom text for a URL pulled into an email via a substitution token (like a direct booking link or candidate availability link). At the point of drafting the email, select the email template using your URL substitution token. Click on the three dots to the right of the URL generated and added to your email, then then select Set Text to Display. You will then be able to type in your custom text and click Continue to update the display text accordingly.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2c5d7033580e5e8b89c6469d5bdf4321.png)
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Candidates
# Candidate Profile
20 min
Copy
This guide walks through the candidate profile, highlighting where candidate information is stored and the actions you can take.
**Prerequisites and permissions:**
  * Limited Access users do not have access to view candidate profiles.


  * Elevated Access users have access to profiles for the roles and departments they have been given the access role for. Your access role may limit your ability to see emails and notes within the candidate's feed.


  * Organization Admins have access to view all candidate profiles (aside from confidential roles they haven't been given explicit access to and those they have their permissions restricted for with access roles).


  * Agency users can only view the candidate profiles that have their agency listed as the source.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#overview)Overview
The candidate profile is where all information about a candidate is stored. You'll likely use this profile to schedule and email candidates, view candidate information, and progress the candidate through the job pipeline.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#how-to-add-a-candidate)How to add a candidate
To create a new candidate profile in Ashby:
  1. Click + Add on the top navigation bar, then Candidate.


![The Add button on the top navigation bar](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BlgJaykV5r0V27X41PS7J_home-acent-ashby-2026-05-26-at-20324-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Add the candidate's details, then click Next.


  * If you have a resume, upload it using the Upload File button. It will then be parsed so candidate information is filled out from the provided file.


  1. If you'd like to add the candidate to a job's pipeline, consider them for the job.


  * This is optional — click Skip if you just want to create a profile for the candidate.


  * You can also add a source and credit the candidate to a user at this stage.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#navigating-the-candidate-profile)Navigating the candidate profile
This screenshot shows an example candidate profile:
![Candidate profile overview](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vdu_AcXn8QWfWEnvuCyTq_irene-mcintosh-customer-support-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-05-26-at-20712-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. **Candidate name:** The candidate's current company and icons linking to social profiles are also listed with the candidate's name. If an email address and phone number are listed on the candidate profile, they appear under the candidate's name. Click on the email address or phone number to copy it to your clipboard.


  1. **Add tags:** Search for and add tags to the candidate's profile, making them easier to organize and search for. More on this at [Tags](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags)﻿.


  1. **Views:** There are three view options:


  1. **Candidate:** An overview of the candidate details, including a list of all roles they've been considered for and projects they are a part of.


  1. **Considered for jobs:** Jump between candidate job considerations to get more information on each consideration, such as the interview progress.


  1. **Blocked applications:** If you have application limits in place and are an Organization Admin, you can see the total number of blocked applications. The three most recent blocked applications are also listed under **Show Recent**.


  1. **Activities and progress:** View current activities (such as interviews to be scheduled or drafted emails to be sent), along with interview progress, the hiring team, and source details of the job consideration.


  1. **Summary:** View the candidate details, such as their email address, phone number, social links, any candidate custom fields, and additional uploaded files. Candidate custom fields are also listed here to be filled out. You can read more on configuring custom fields at [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿.


  1. **Resume:** If the candidate has a resume or CV uploaded, it can be viewed here. Resumes are displayed on job considerations and on the candidate view of the candidate profile.


  1. **New activity:** Click this to add an assessment or debrief activity ad hoc for the candidate's job consideration.


  1. **Interview progress:** View the stages the candidate has passed through as part of your job pipeline.


  1. **New interview:** Create a new interview activity ad hoc to schedule the candidate. More on this at [Manually Scheduling Interviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/manually-scheduling-interviews)﻿.


  1. **Attribution:** View the source and the user this candidate's job consideration is credited to.


  1. **Hiring team:** The hiring team for this user's job consideration. Based on their notification settings, those on the hiring team receive notifications for events that occur for this job consideration.


  1. **Follow up:** If you'd like to be reminded to return to a candidate's profile later, select a date and create a follow-up here. You can add a note to your follow-up that can be viewed when the user hovers over the note icon on the Follow up button.


![The note icon on the follow up button](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/LvAi_6OM1kD2CgOBpajP5_zight-2026-05-27-at-115434.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. **Email:** Draft and send an email to the candidate.


  1. **Change stage:** Move the candidate to another stage in your pipeline.


  1. **Archive:** Reject the candidate or lead for the specified job consideration and move them to the Archived stage of the interview process. You'll be asked to select one from the list of archive reasons and have the option to send or schedule a rejection email.


  1. **More:** Access additional options on the candidate's profile, including creating a survey request, enrolling the candidate in a sequence, considering them for another role, and leaving feedback for the candidate without scheduling an interview.


  * Actions you can take within the More menu include.


  * **Enroll in sequence:** Enroll the candidate in sequences. More on this at [Sequences](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences)﻿.


  * **Schedule new interview:** Schedule the candidate for an interview.


  * **Submit feedback:** Add feedback for a candidate interview or submit feedback without scheduling an interview.


  * **Request feedback without scheduling:** Use this option to send a request to another user to submit their feedback without scheduling an interview.


  * **Consider for job:** Consider the candidate for another job.


  * **Transfer:** Transfer this job consideration to a new job. More on this at [Can I transfer a candidate's job consideration to another job or interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile "Can I transfer a candidate's job consideration to another job or interview plan?").


  * **Export candidate data:** Select the candidate data you'd like to export and generate a PDF of your chosen data.


  * **Add to project:** Add the candidate to projects.


  * **Create diversity survey request:** Create a diversity survey request for the candidate. More on this at [Diversity Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/diversity-surveys)﻿.


  * **Create EEO survey request:** Create an EEOC survey request for the candidate. More on this at [EEOC Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys)﻿.


  * **Create a candidate experience survey request:** Create a candidate experience survey request for the candidate. More on this at [Candidate Experience Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-experience-surveys)﻿.


  * **Create questionnaire request:** Create a questionnaire request for the candidate. More on this at [Questionnaires](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/questionnaires)﻿.


  * **Send e-signature request:** Generate an e-signature request to send out to the candidate. More on this at [E-Signature Documents](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-documents)﻿.


  * **Follow:** Follow candidates to get notifications on actions taken on this candidate and their job considerations. More on notifications at [Notifications & Notification Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/notifications-and-notification-settings)﻿.


  * **Find emails:** Look up emails and social links using our data enrichment providers.


  * **Merge:** Merge the candidate profile with a duplicate. More on this at [How do I merge duplicate candidate profiles?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-merge-duplicate-candidate-profiles)﻿.


  * **Add referral:** Add a referral to this candidate's profile from an employee.


  * **Change application status:** Manually amend the status of the application. This should rarely be used.


  * **Mark as do not contact:** Prevent future email outreach from Ashby. This also cancels all scheduled emails to this candidate.


  * **Manage subscriptions:** Manage and amend the candidate's subscription status for receiving sequence emails, etc.


  * **Anonymize:** Anonymize the candidate's data. More on this at [Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates)﻿. This action cannot be undone.


  * **Delete:** Remove this candidate from Ashby. More on this at [Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates)﻿.


  * **Check for Fraud:** Perform a manual fraud check on this candidate. For more on this, check out [Running Candidate Fraud Detection Checks & Reviewing Fraud Signals](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/running-candidate-fraud-detection-checks-and-reviewing-fraud-signals)﻿.


  1. **Feed:** A list of all notes and feedback left on the candidate's profile, along with any email communication, referrals, and forms that the candidate has completed. More on this at [Candidate Profile: Candidate Feed](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile-candidate-feed)﻿.


  1. **+:** Record any outreach activity and submit feedback without scheduling an interview.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#help-i-cant-schedule-or-email-a-candidate)Help! I can't schedule or email a candidate!
Check with an admin to ensure you have the correct permissions. You can find more about access roles at [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#can-i-unarchive-a-candidate-if-they-were-rejected)Can I unarchive a candidate if they were rejected or archived by accident? How do I restore their job consideration and move them back into the active pipeline?
If you have archived a candidate by mistake, click Change Stage on their profile and select the interview stage you would like to move them back to. If you would like to keep a record of the archived job consideration (if the candidate was initially rejected but then considered again later for the same role), you can opt to consider the same candidate for the role again.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#how-does-color-coding-work-with-status-banners-and)How does color coding work with status banners and on the candidate pipeline view?
You can read more on this at [What do the card colors mean on the Candidate Pipeline report?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-do-the-card-colors-mean-on-the-candidate-pipeline-report)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#can-i-schedule-an-email-to-a-candidate)Can I schedule an email to a candidate?
If you don't want to send an email to a candidate right away, you can schedule an email. Click Email on the candidate's profile to create a draft. While drafting the email, click the Send drop-down to determine when the email should be sent. You can choose from three options:
  * **Immediately:** Send the email as soon as Send is clicked on the draft.


  * **Date and time:** Specify a date and time for the email to be sent.


  * **After time passed:** Specify the number of days, weeks, etc. that the email should be sent after. You have the option to determine the days the email should be sent on (a weekday, any day of the week, or a specific day of the week) and also the time the email should be sent.


![The options when scheduling an email.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RM_iCD48NTDQYQKUfAiPd_ariadne-shepard-digital-marketing-manager-acent-ashby-2026-05-27-at-115815-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Click Schedule to confirm. The email activity under **Current Activities** on the candidate's profile shows **Scheduled Email**. To amend or reschedule a scheduled email you have drafted, click View on the **Scheduled Email** activity. You can then make any adjustments and click Reschedule to confirm your changes.
![A scheduled email activity on the candidate's profile.l](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Jiei1-B8fut_2DJY1uXpd_a-scheduled-email-activity.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can only view and reschedule a scheduled email if you are the user who originally drafted and scheduled the email.
Users with access to email the candidate have the option to delete the scheduled email activity. To delete a scheduled email, click the three dots to the right of View on the scheduled email activity and select Delete.
![Deleting a scheduled email activity](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/1oDsJyWPL2pamrsWvvJZh-20260527-110244.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#how-do-i-consider-a-candidate-for-a-job-from-their)How do I consider a candidate for a job from their profile?
If you'd like to consider the candidate for a role, click More on their profile, then Consider for Job. You can then select the role you'd like to consider the candidate for, determine the stage in the pipeline they should be added to, and their source.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#can-i-make-files-uploaded-to-the-other-files-secti)Can I make files uploaded to the Other Files section of the candidate profile private?
You can. To make a file under **Other Files** private, click the three dots beside the file, then Edit.
![Editing a file on the candidate's profile.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Cw0bk_0q4svffwRe9xn9D_ariadne-shepard-digital-marketing-manager-acent-ashby-2026-05-27-at-120418-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
On the following page, you will be able to set the file as public or private using the Privacy drop-down. 
![Editing the privacy on a file.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/rcbZ9_Sknmdt8eMKs0gT-_editing-a-file-s-privacy.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Like with private notes and fields, only those with Organization Admin or Elevated Access - Admin Private access can view these fields.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#if-i-add-a-follow-up-to-a-candidates-profile-will)If I add a follow-up to a candidate's profile, will I still receive a notification to follow up with the candidate even if they've been archived?
Yes. Archiving the candidate does not automatically cancel the follow-up task. If the follow-up isn't resolved on the candidate profile, a notification is still sent.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#what-happens-if-a-candidate-was-sourced-and-added)What happens if a candidate was sourced and added to a job as a lead, then applied for the same job themselves?
In this case, the candidate continues to have just one job consideration on their profile for the role but is moved to the Application Review stage.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#if-i-request-feedback-from-a-user-without-an-inter)If I request feedback from a user without an interview, will they be able to view the full candidate profile?
If a user is asked to submit feedback without an interview, they can see the candidate's resume, a briefing, and some basic candidate details. If they do not have the permissions to view the candidate profile already, they are not able to see it as part of their task to provide feedback.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#can-i-add-multiple-users-to-the-credited-to-field)Can I add multiple users to the Credited To field?
No, only one user can be added to this field.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#how-do-i-leave-feedback-for-a-candidate)How do I leave feedback for a candidate?
If you have been scheduled for an interview, a feedback task shows on your home page (more on this at **Home Page**). This task shows for Limited Access users, Elevated Access users, and Organization Admins. If you would like to submit feedback without scheduling an interview for the candidate, click More, then Submit Feedback on the candidate's profile. You can then select your feedback form and complete your feedback. You need Elevated Access - Hiring Manager access or above to take this route.
If you'd like another user to leave feedback, click More on the candidate's profile, then Request Feedback without Scheduling. You can then determine the interview type (and feedback form) and the user who should leave feedback. Once confirmed, this generates a feedback request on the user's home page. All users can see feedback request tasks on their home page, but only those with Elevated Access - Hiring Manager access (or higher) can generate the request. Only one user can be specified at a time and you can select from the shared interviews you've created in **Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > Interviews**.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#how-do-i-edit-or-add-new-archive-reasons-where-do)How do I edit or add new archive reasons? Where do I set up a new archive reason?
Add or edit archive reasons by heading to **Admin > Jobs & Applications > **[**Archive Reasons**](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-archive-reasons "Archive Reasons"). Click + New to add a new archive reason, or click on an existing archive reason to make any edits.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#how-do-i-add-or-amend-a-candidates-social-links-on)How do I add or amend a candidate's social links on their profile (for example, their LinkedIn profile link)?
While viewing the candidate profile, head to the **Summary** tab. Navigate to the **Social Links** section, then click Add to add a new social link. To edit a social link, click the three dots beside the link in question, then select Edit. Once you've made your amendments, click Submit to confirm the changes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#what-permission-level-do-i-need-to-change-a-candid)What permission level do I need to change a candidate's stage or archive their job consideration?
To change the stage a candidate's job consideration is in or archive their job consideration, you need Elevated Access - Hiring Manager access or higher to the job, team, or location.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#what-happens-when-i-update-a-candidates-resume-wit)What happens when I update a candidate's resume with a new file?
The resume uploaded on the application form is displayed on both the application and candidate profile.
If the application's resume is updated, it updates both the application and the candidate profile's resume.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#what-happens-if-i-delete-the-resume-on-the-candida)What happens if I delete the resume on the candidate profile or a candidate's job consideration?
If you delete the resume from the application, only the application's resume is deleted. The candidate profile's resume is then used for the application. If you delete the candidate profile's resume and a resume exists on the application, the application resume is now displayed on the candidate profile.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#if-a-candidate-submits-an-outdated-resume-can-they)If a candidate submits an outdated resume, can they refresh or add their new resume to their profile themselves?
No. In these cases, receive the new resume or CV from the candidate via email and then manually replace the resume within the candidate profile in Ashby. The candidate can provide a new resume if they are applying for another job via your job board.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#when-should-i-transfer-a-candidate-to-another-job)When should I transfer a candidate to another job and when should I consider a candidate for another job instead?
Under the More option on the candidate's profile, you see the option to transfer a candidate's job consideration and the option to consider them for another job.
![The option to consider a candidate for a new job or transfer them](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3Dok43LhBi58pyT0wMiHt_ariadne-shepard-digital-marketing-manager-acent-ashby-2026-05-27-at-114210-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Transferring** a candidate moves their existing job consideration to a different job pipeline.
When you transfer a candidate, the following happens:
  * All historical data is retained, including:


  * Application history


  * Notes


  * Application forms


  * Surveys


  * Interview feedback


  * Referral forms


  * The Started At date stays the same.


  * Reporting continues to reflect the original application timeline.


Transfer a candidate's job consideration when:
  * The role is essentially the same or a similar opportunity (for example, a renamed job or corrected posting) that the candidate should be considered for instead.


  * You want to preserve all historical context and timing for this specific job consideration and just move it to a different job.


  * Time-to-hire continuity matters, taking into account when the candidate was first considered for a role.


**Considering for another job** creates a brand-new job consideration for the candidate.
When you consider a candidate for another job:
  * The Started At date reflects the date the new job consideration was created and added to the candidate's profile.


  * Time-to-hire and pipeline metrics start fresh for the new job consideration.


  * The candidate profile details remain the same, but a new job consideration is added to their profile.


Use Consider for Job... when:
  * The candidate is being considered for a new or different opportunity.


  * You want reporting dates to reflect both job considerations.


  * The opportunity represents a new hiring process.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile#how-can-candidate-profiles-be-linked-to-employee-p)How can candidate profiles be linked to employee profiles upon hire?
See [Visibility Options for Users](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/visibility-options-for-users)﻿ for more information.

interview-plans:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Initial Setup
# Interview Plans
23 min
Copy
In this guide, we’ll talk through what an interview plan is, the types of interview plans and how to set them up.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#overview)Overview
Interview plans determine the stages your candidates will pass through when interviewing with your team. Interview plans are created in Admin, then assigned to jobs. All jobs require an interview plan. You can see the stages of your interview plan displayed in the Candidate Pipeline section of the role it’s assigned to.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#permissions-interview-plan-setup)Permissions: Interview Plan Setup
See the table below for information on the roles that have access to set up and edit shared interview plans.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Only Organization Admins can configure shared interview plans by default. Elevated Access users can be given access to edit shared interview plans if needed. More on this at [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#viewing-an-interview-plan-setup)Viewing an interview plan setup
Here’s an example of an interview plan:
![Shows the Interview Plan for a Designer role with the Lead, Application Review, and Active sections visible.](https://cdn.zappy.app/0cae5204dbfaf31438bda6be3b2f3c0a.png)
﻿
Interview plans have multiple sections, including: 
**Lead:** These stages are used for sourced candidates, both when finding suitable candidates and when reaching out to them to gauge their interest. Additional stages can be added to customize the section.
The _New Lead, Reached Out_ and _Replied_ stages cannot be removed from the interview plan.
**Application Review:** This stage reflects that the candidates are being assessed for further action based on their applications. It is not customizable.
**Active:** This section allows you to add the stages that reflect your hiring process from when an application is received, right up to an offer being extended.
**Offer:** This section contains the stages that accompany extending an offer to a candidate and can be customized on certain plans.
See [Configuring Offer Stage Admin Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/configuring-offer-stage-admin-settings)﻿ for more information on editing this section.
**Completed:** These are your stages that show the application result - either Archived (or rejected), or Hired.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#viewing-an-interview-plan-on-a-job)Viewing an interview plan on a job
When you click into a job, you can see these stages laid out in the **Candidate Pipeline** tab. You can click on the stage sections at the top of the **Candidate Pipeline** view to view your leads within the **Leads** section, and view candidates in **Application Review** , your **Active** stages and those who are in the **Offer** stage. Hired and Archived candidates are also accessible via the Hired and Archived buttons.
![Shows the candidate pipeline highlighted with a box under the Customer Support Specialist role.](https://cdn.zappy.app/48f887ae7dcff91795ef1b49312f682c.png)
﻿
When clicking on an option, you’ll be able to see the stages you’ve set up. In Active, for example, you can see each of the interview stages that were added to your interview plan and then the candidates listed in each stage.
![Shows the Active interview stage under the Customer Support Specialist role, with the interview stages visible and candidates within each stage also listed.](https://cdn.zappy.app/0b90bbc84934cff25e4f2a4dd2e1bfdc.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#creating-an-interview-plan)Creating an interview plan
There are two types of interview plans in Ashby: shared and custom. Shared interview plans are templates that can be applied across multiple jobs, while custom interview plans are unique to specific jobs.
A grouped interview plan is a special plan used to consolidate the stages across your other plans into one unified set of stages for reporting. For more on grouped interview plans, check out [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#creating-a-shared-interview-plan)Creating a shared interview plan
Shared interview plans help to standardize your interview stages across many jobs. We recommend setting up shared interview plans.
To create a shared plan, navigate to Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > Interview Plans and click + New Shared Plan. Input a plan name, then click Submit _._
![Shows the New Shared Plan button in the top right of the screen.](https://cdn.zappy.app/3034182449557451c1c4d841cbe8b3ad.png)
﻿
To add an active stage, click into the Add active stage textbox and search for or create the new stage name. Then click + Save to add the stage. 
![Shows the add active stage field highlighted, where additional stages can be added to an interview plan section.](https://cdn.zappy.app/370f6711731f7571fb4a710f38eb155e.png)
﻿
**﻿  
**
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#configure-your-interview-plan)**Configure your interview plan**
There are four options for editing your interview plan stages:
  1. Use the dragging icons to the left of the stage names to change the position of the stages you create


  1. Click on the name to open the menu to select or create a new name for an existing stage


  1. Click the 🗑️ icon to delete a stage


  1. Click the ↪️ icon to revert a change


  1. Confirm your edits by clicking Save Edits _._


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c909b3c23e8e4d77c552a35f2d1bee75.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#creating-a-custom-interview-plan)Creating a custom interview plan
Custom interview plans make it easy to edit the stages for a single job without having admin permissions or affecting other jobs.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#create-a-new-plan-for-a-job)Create a new plan for a job
When creating a new job, you can skip adding one of your existing shared plans. When you do so, you’ll have the option to add a custom plan to the job. Click + Add Custom Plan to create a new custom plan.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cc3d57ad8876fd1c2d5dcced7dbf0df9.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#replace-a-shared-plan-with-a-custom-plan)Replace a shared plan with a custom plan
If you create a job with a shared plan but decide later you’d like to customize it (for example, add additional stages for the plan for just this role), you can click Edit Stages for this Job. This will create a custom plan that will replace the shared one that was added to the job previously.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cbe0ba2ff47991a32b1d29cb211ff5d3.png)
﻿
If you’ve created a custom plan, but decide you’d like to switch to an existing shared one, click Switch to Shared Plan. This will let you select the replacement and map the stage from the previous to the new plan to ensure candidates transfer seamlessly.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5688cf9c800576f673dab93664c51029.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#turning-a-custom-plan-into-a-shared-plan)Turning a custom plan into a shared plan
If you’ve created a custom plan and want to make it accessible for more jobs, you can convert it a shared plan by clicking Edit Plan Stages… then Promote to Shared Plan _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5553a065c1cc08c196382a32c3935681.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#edit-stages-on-a-custom-plan)Edit stages on a custom plan
Once you’ve created a plan, you need to add stages. In most cases, you’ll do this in the Active Stage Name section of the plan, as these are the stages where your candidates are actively interviewing with your team.
To add an active stage, click into the Add Active Stage checkbox, then search for or create the new stage name. Click + Save to add the stage to your interview plan. Stages will be added between the Application Review and Offer default stages, and you can use the dragging icons to the left of the stage names to rearrange all stages between the two.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9d67ded1f3cab96ffd744b1deab47a84.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#configuring-your-custom-plan-stages)Configuring your custom plan stages
There are four options for editing your interview plan stages:
  1. Use the dragging icons to the left of the stage names to change the position of stages you create


  1. Click on the name to open the menu to select or create a new name for an existing stage


  1. Click the 🗑️ icon to delete a stage


  1. Click the ↪️ icon to revert a change


  1. Confirm your edits by clicking Save Edits _._


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f6f97ca86740c40d9b24b96c40314ea4.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#add-activities-to-your-interview-plans-recommended)Add activities to your interview plans (recommended)
See [Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities)﻿ for more information on the activities you can add to your interviews plans on jobs
Adding activities helps streamline your processes by storing and automatically prompting you to complete key tasks when a candidate moves into a stage. Activities can only be added to an interview plan once it has been assigned to a job or job template. 
For example, when you add a Schedule Interview activity to the **Initial Screen** stage of your interview plan, as shown...
![Schedule Interview activity on the interview plan](https://cdn.zappy.app/1d627a1fe17180866dd3ec52e49d0819.png)
﻿
...a corresponding activity will appear in the **Activities & Progress** feed any time a candidate moves into that stage in the pipeline.
![Schedule Interview activity on a candidate profile](https://cdn.zappy.app/ac5c19ed5bf589fd936befc2d1b96432.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#reuse-configured-interview-plans)Reuse configured interview plans
While activities can’t be added directly to the interview plans in Admin, there are two ways to reuse configurations: you can either create job templates or make copies of jobs.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#create-job-templates)Create Job Templates
﻿[Job Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates)﻿ are reusable versions of entire jobs that you can choose what you want to configure upfront, then use as a starting point when creating new jobs. If you add an activity to an interview plan on a job template, when you create a job using that template, the activity will be added to the newly created job's interview plan, saving you time.
Job templates can be configured with as much or as little detail as possible to give you a headstart on setting up a new job. To make a template, visit Jobs > Jobs then click on Job Templates.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0d16d64b2f3dc19e9030e534cf9e2150.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#copy-existing-jobs)Copy Existing Jobs
A copy is an exact replica of an existing job. To make a copy, visit a job, then click More> Copy in the top right corner of the page. Any activities on the original job's interview plan will then be copied over to the newly created job.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/42aca3592cee49ceaaa0c5a17dc1a065.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#why-are-some-of-the-stages-greyed-out-and-uneditab)**Why are some of the stages greyed out and uneditable?**
_New Lead, Reached Out, Replied_ Candidates will automatically move through the New Lead, Reached Out, and Replied stages based on your email communication with them. As such, these stages cannot be removed.
_Application Review_ The Application Review stage has Application Review mode, which is unique to that stage.
_Offer_ An offer activity will automatically appear when a candidate moved into the Offer stage.
_Hired_ If you’ve integrated an HRIS, you’ll be prompted to transfer your new hire’s data when they move into the Hired stage.
_Archived_ A candidate will move to the Archived stage when their job consideration is archived, or to Hired when hired for the role. It’s not possible to add additional custom stages to the Completed section of an interview plan. 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#can-i-have-multiple-interview-plans-on-a-job)Can I have multiple interview plans on a job?
Adding multiple interview plans to a job is no longer supported. If there are optional stages in your interview plan, we recommend indicating that in the title of the stage, and skipping those stages with candidates where appropriate.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#this-is-very-flexible-whats-the-best-practice)This is very flexible; what’s the best practice?
  * Edits can be made to standardized processes across many jobs


  * Reporting will be automatically grouped by the stages on the shared plans


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#can-i-add-stages-to-the-completed-section-of-my-in)Can I add stages to the Completed section of my interview plan?
No, it’s not possible to add additional Completed stages to your interview plan. The Archived and Hired stages cannot be edited. When adding Active stages to your interview plan, they cannot be added after the Offer stage.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#can-i-delete-an-interview-plan)Can I delete an interview plan?
You can archive an interview plan by navigating to Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > Interview Plans _._ Click on the interview plan in question, then click _Archive._ The plan can no longer be used when creating jobs moving forward.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans#can-i-restore-an-archived-interview-plan)Can I restore an archived interview plan?
You can! Switch the Active filter to Archived when viewing your interview plans at Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > Interview Plans.
Select the archived interview plan in question, then click Publish to reactive it. It can then be used when creating jobs moving forward.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a7ea78fbc4385cf93865ce721e92b81c.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/61df75bcbfaf934058c6fae3a877d3ef.png)
﻿
﻿

_
﻿

how-does-editing-interview-plans-impact-:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-does-editing-interview-plans-impact-passthrough-rate-reports -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)

Interviews & Scheduling FAQs
# How does editing interview plans impact passthrough rate reports?
4 min
Copy
This guide covers amending interview stages on your interview plans and how that can impact reporting. Please note that grouped interview plans are referenced to often: for more on setting up a grouped interview plan, check out [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿
For more information on setting up interview plans, please check out [Interview Plans](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-does-editing-interview-plans-impact-passthrough-rate-reports#overview)Overview
In short, if you are planning to make edits to your interview plan, we’d recommend editing stage names where possible, rather than deleting and adding new stages. If stages are removed and/or added, please ensure you have a grouped interview plan and your interview plan stages mapped to your grouped interview plan.
Then, when running passthrough rate reports, ensure that the Group Stages? checkbox is checked to preserve all data from archived interview plan stages.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-does-editing-interview-plans-impact-passthrough-rate-reports#can-i-edit-the-names-of-the-stages-on-my-interview)Can I edit the names of the stages on my interview plans without impacting passthrough rate reporting?
If you edit the name of an existing stage, rather than removing a stage and adding a new one, then this will simply update the name of the stage and your passthrough reporting will remain intact.
So here, the Technical Round on an interview plan has been edited and re-named to Second Round. It can take a few minutes for the stage to be updated on your passthrough rate report.
On refreshing the report, the passthrough results for that stage remain the name, the name of the stage has just been updated. If you need to update any stages, we would recommend renaming them, rather than deleting stages from your interview plan and adding in new ones.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6614eb9dbad3ef4f7511dd9a7dffa86a.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6db941e76cb9a7d7898859721c6ba575.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d3d815faab5457ba18822ec351b6c170.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-does-editing-interview-plans-impact-passthrough-rate-reports#how-about-if-i-delete-a-stage)How about if I delete a stage?
If you delete a stage that is mapped to a stage in your grouped interview plan, then the stage results will only be shown in your passthrough rate report when you opt to group your stages (so showing the grouped interview plan stages as your passthrough stages).
If you choose to report on your ungrouped stages, the deleted stage in your interview plan will not be displayed on your passthrough rate report. The passthrough rate percentage from the stage before the deleted stage to the stage after the deleted stage will be reflected.
So, in our example, the Second Round stage from the interview plan has now been deleted. That stage will no longer show in the report when I opt to not check the Group Stages? checkbox (so showing the stages of my individual interview plans on the report).
Our second round stage was linked to the Technical Evaluation stage of the Grouped Interview plan, so when grouping the results, the results for the deleted interview stage will be shown.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/638af4a8dc21474a2b52e6b3ae03bd2f.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ec39c408364b7148e3d2ac6085400f78.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-does-editing-interview-plans-impact-passthrough-rate-reports#what-happens-if-i-create-a-new-stage-on-my-intervi)What happens if I create a new stage on my interview plan?
If you opt to create a new stage for your interview plan and you have candidates in stages beyond the newly created stage, they will show as having passed through that stage.
In our example, a new Technical Quiz stage was created and added to the interview plan. It was also linked to the Technical Evaluation stage of our grouped plan.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/41c7d95067f3d2bdbd1993e2caa3f0f1.png)
﻿
Now when running my passthrough rate report, if the Group Stages? checkbox isn’t checked, the Technical Quiz stage will display passthrough data for any candidates that have reached stages beyond that stage.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9ca0562ae5af67a584e68e14c8f30e9c.png)
﻿
﻿
