<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Applications
# Application Limits
15 min
Copy
You can set application limits to prevent candidates from excessively applying to multiple roles and repeat applications from rejected candidates.
Application Limits are available on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#application-limits-overview)Application Limits Overview
Candidates may apply to the same job multiple times after being rejected or apply excessively to multiple open roles before a decision is made, resulting in lost time for recruiters who must review superfluous applications. [Application Limits](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/application-limits/configurations "Application Limits") will allow your team to configure rules that will automatically block excessive applications. You can also opt to include a message to candidates on the application page to inform them of these limits before they apply again.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#what-types-of-rules-are-currently-supported)What types of rules are currently supported?  
| Rule Type  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Throttling applications  | Limit the number of times a candidate can apply to any job within a scope over a certain number of days.  |  
| Repeat applications for the same job  | Block rejected candidates from re-applying to the same role within a certain number of days.  |  
| Repeat applications for any job  | Block rejected candidates from re-applying to any role within the scope within a certain number of days.  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#configure-application-blocking-rules)Configure Application Blocking Rules
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#create-a-new-rule)Create a new rule
Navigate to [Application Limits](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/application-limits/configurations "Application Limits") to build a rule. Click + New Application Limit Configuration in the top right corner to start creating a new rule.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0bc13c659183d7e412c6917c9b4389c5.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#configure-a-new-rule)Configure a new rule
You have the following options for configuring a rule:
  * Description


  * Scope (filtering to establish the jobs that this configuration will apply to)


  * Blocking Rules


  * Throttling Applications


  * Repeat applications for the same job after a rejection


  * Repeat applications for any job after a rejection


  * Display Message to Candidate (what the candidate will see if they apply and their application is blocked).


  * Application form callout (displaying a message at the top of the application form explaining the application limit to the candidate).


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5746867b6efe16ce6c8d94d991901e90.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#ordering-and-editing-rules)Ordering and editing rules
If you have multiple rules, then you can sort them in order of preference. If an application applies to multiple limiting rules, then the rule that is highest on the list will be applied.
To edit an application limit configuration, you can click on it to open up the right-hand panel and make amendments from there. Hit ‘Save’ to ensure the changes are applied to the rule.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#example-rules)Example Rules
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#global-rule)Global Rule
You can leave the Scope section empty for the application limits you configure to be applied to all jobs.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/941b204b5b2b626e33915318c5484d6e.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#department-rule)Department Rule
If you’d like to set up application limits for a specific department or team, click the + Add Field to Match option and search for and select the Department option.
From there, you’ll be able to use the Search for team… drop-down to select the department in question. You can select multiple departments if you’d like the application limits to apply to more than one.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cac39f1f4dc3b9865559e1811e6c5ac0.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#using-application-blocking-rules)Using Application Blocking Rules
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#candidate-view-of-blocked-application)Candidate view of blocked application
**Application form callout**
Should you choose to add an application form callout to your rule, the message entered will display above the application form for the candidate to read prior to submitting their application.
﻿  

![Application form callout ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/6TFLO0bM6TJ2oyEZG5qbX_screenshot2023-06-07at90956pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Blocked application message**
If the candidate’s application is blocked by your application limits, they will see the message you add in the Display Message to Candidate field as part of configuring your rule.
If this field is left blank in your rule setup, the candidate will see the message added in the After Candidate Submits Application field on the [Themes (Job Board) page](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/theme "Themes \(Job Board\) page").
﻿  

![Message for a blocked application](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/anI5JdR1XLbuOcnQno3-G_screenshot2023-06-07at91931pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#view-blocked-application-submissions)View blocked application submissions
You can click the [Blocked Application Submissions](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/application-limits/blocked-application-submissions "Blocked Application Submissions") tab on the Application Limits page to pull up a list of blocked applications.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3372527044c244a3875f9a69dc8048b5.png)
﻿
Candidate: The name of the applicant. Click on their name to view their profile.
Job: The role they applied for. Click on the job title to be taken to the job settings.
Source: The application limit that caused the application to be blocked. Click on the source listed to pull up the details of the rule that was activated.
Blocked At: The date and time the application was blocked.
Status: The current status of the application.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#unblock-an-application-submission)Unblock an application submission
If a candidate’s application was blocked and you’d like to add it back to the pipeline for review, there is the option to unblock it.
![Unblock option](https://cdn.zappy.app/e1959eb4eb7e7c0f3dda24777e4b733f.png)
﻿
In the Blocked Application Submissions section, the option to unblock the candidate is available in the Status field of the application in question. Click this option and confirm the unblocking to remove the application’s blocked status.
![Confirming the unblocking](https://cdn.zappy.app/9b0af6392823bf0a770f932fcd41697e.png)
﻿
Once unblocked, the candidate will then be added into the Application Review stage of the job’s pipeline.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#are-application-limits-applied-to-applications-sub)Are application limits applied to applications submitted via Indeed?
No, currently application limits are not applied to applications submitted via Indeed.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits#can-i-see-blocked-applications-on-the-candidate-pr)Can I see blocked applications on the candidate profile?
If you have Organization Admin access, then you will be able to see blocked applications in the Views section of the candidate’s profile. The three most recent blocked applications will be listed but the total number of blocked applications will be displayed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/w-B3m3tPIqfHN_gxPp1mW_blockedapplications.png)
﻿
﻿

job-postings:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Jobs & Openings
# Job Postings
23 min
Copy
Job Postings are used when you want your job to appear on your Ashby or third-party job boards, allowing you to collect applications from candidates. Create a job posting to add the job description, additional locations, the application form, and override compensation information for the specific instance of the job you're hiring for. This article outlines the steps required to successfully create and publish a job posting.
**Prerequisites:**
  * To _set up_ a job posting, you must create a job first. 


  * To _publish_ a job posting, your job must be set to "Open", and may require an active Opening to be assigned depending on your settings and plan type. 


You can read more on creating and opening jobs at [**Creating & Posting Jobs.**](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings "Creating & Posting Jobs.")﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#creating-and-publishing-a-job-posting)Creating and publishing a job posting
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#step-1-start-the-job-posting-draft)Step 1: Start the Job Posting draft
Navigate to the job you'd like to post by selecting Jobs and then Jobs from the top navigation bar. Then, select the Job Postings tab within the job. Click New Draft to get started.
![Shows the Job Postings menu option on the left and the New Draft button on the right.](https://cdn.zappy.app/6ee06ede5f7c010e980cb23b92387818.png)
﻿
You can create a draft job posting while your job is in a Draft state but your job must be set to Open in order to publish your job posting.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#step-2-add-the-job-description-and-basic-info)Step 2: Add the Job Description and Basic Info
Once you’ve created your draft, you’ll have five different tabs where you can fill in posting details: 
  * Description


  * Basic Info


  * Application Form


  * Automation


  * Compensation


We'll cover the Description and Basic Info tabs in this section.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#description)Description
![Shows the description fields for a new job.](https://cdn.zappy.app/5295d2fae3ba4b405229d41c6b8b36c6.png)
﻿
**Social Description:** The description information added to the Social Description field will show when the posting is shared on social media (for example, linked in a post on LinkedIn). There is a limit of 200 characters.
**Description:** The information added here will be shown to applicants when they view this job on your Ashby job board (on the Overview tab). The description will also be pulled to any third-party job boards you choose to integrate with. You can add links, images and videos to your description using the toolbar at the bottom of the field.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#basic-info)Basic Info
![Shows the basic info tab of a job.](https://cdn.zappy.app/db3868da80008c7d44ff4e635a4f44b9.png)
﻿
  * **Title:** Enter the title as you would like it to appear on the posting. By default, the title used when creating the Job will appear. 


Amending the title here will not impact the title of the job internally in Ashby. For example, if the Head of Marketing job title was amended to display as Head of Digital Marketing in this field, the job posting would reflect the new title but the job will still show as Head of Marketing to an internal user when viewing the job in Ashby.
  * **External Visibility:** Here you can choose to list your job publicly on your Ashby job board or third-party job boards (Listed), or not (Unlisted). If a job posting is published but set as Unlisted, an applicant can still access it if they have a direct link to the posting. For unlisted postings added to the internal job board, users would still need to sign in to be able to view the posting.


  * **Third-Party Job Boards:** If you have any third-party job board integrations in place, you can choose the job boards that you’d like to display this posting using the checkboxes in the drop-down.


If you are using an XML feed to pull your job postings to LinkedIn (rather than the direct integration listed in [**Integrations**](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/marketplace "Integrations")), deselecting LinkedIn from the checkboxes here will not stop your job posting from being listed on LinkedIn. This option will only work for the direct LinkedIn integration.
  * **Location:** Determine the location that should be listed on your posting. Changing the location here, like changing the title, will not impact the location listed on the job internally in Ashby.


  * **Additional Locations:** If candidates can work from various locations, you can opt to add additional locations here. Each additional location will be displayed in the Locations section of the job posting on your Ashby job board.


When a job posting has multiple locations (a main location and additional locations) listed, third-party job boards will automatically create separate job postings for each location. For example, if a job posting has a main location of New York and additional locations of San Francisco and Los Angeles, three separate job postings would be created on the third-party job board.
  * **Application Deadline:** Set a date and time that applications must be submitted by. Candidates will see the deadline date and time in their own time zone. If you set a deadline, a setting will appear that, when enabled, allows you to automatically un-publish the posting once the deadline has passed.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#step-3-associate-or-edit-the-application-form)Step 3: Associate or Edit the Application Form
On the Application Form tab, you can choose which application form candidates will see when they apply for the job.
![Shows the application form tab of a job.](https://cdn.zappy.app/b4bc49028cab6d3ef41fbb2278bb9004.png)
﻿
The Default Application Form for your organization will automatically be assigned for this job posting; however, you can opt to switch it for a customized form or unlink and make edits to the form that are unique to this posting.
To understand how to create a customized application form, see [Create application forms in Admin.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-forms#create-application-forms-in-admin-recommended "Create application forms in Admin.")﻿
To understand how to change the form associated with this job posting, or to unlink and edit the default form, see [Change the application form associated with a job posting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-forms#change-the-application-form-associated-with-a-job "Change the application form associated with a job posting").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#step-4-configure-application-automations)Step 4: Configure Application Automations
Ashby can automatically send application confirmation emails, as well as auto-reject candidates based on certain criteria. These settings are configured on the Automations tab within the Job Posting.
![Shows the automation tab of a job.](https://cdn.zappy.app/2c03a8c107a0cdac61adc31922766d94.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#auto-confirm-application)**Auto-Confirm Application**
If your organization has [**Application Email Confirmations**](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/application-email-confirmation "Application Email Confirmations") set up, this automation will automatically be enabled and set to "Use organization's default template." If you’d like to determine a specific email template to use for applications submitted for this posting, you can use the drop-down to select a specific template.
﻿[Email Template Creation and Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management)﻿ is a great article to learn all about creating email templates for various roles and reasons. 
If you need to create the default application confirmation email template for your organization, see the section on [Application confirmation templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/emails-and-email-templates#application-confirmation-template "Application confirmation templates").
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#auto-reject-application)**Auto-Reject Application**
In order to enable the ability to auto-reject candidates based on certain criteria, you must first create a configuration by selecting the Create a Configuration button and setting your criteria. For information on setting up and editing these configurations, check out [**Auto-Reject Applications**](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings "Auto-Reject Applications"). Once you've created your configurations, you'll be able to turn on the Auto-Reject Enabled toggle.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#step-5-confirm-compensation-details)Step 5: Confirm Compensation details
![Shows the compensation tab of a job.](https://cdn.zappy.app/6cdaf94e808e2e7788dbbf6c3a4456bd.png)
﻿
On the Compensation tab of your job posting, you can determine whether you’d like to display the compensation on the posting (both in the posting details and on the posting listing when viewing jobs on your Ashby job board), as well as optionally add additional compensation information. If no additional information is added, compensation information for the job posting will default to the information included on the job.
Full details on setting up compensation information can be found in our article on [Compensation](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/compensation)﻿. 
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#final-step-publish-your-job-posting)Final Step: Publish your job posting
To publish your posting, the job it is tied to must be set to Open (to accept inbound applications) and a job description must be added to the job posting.
If you have set compensation to be displayed on your posting, you will also need to ensure that you have at least one valid salary range in place in the Compensation tab to have the ability to publish your posting.
When ready to publish, hit the Publish button in the upper right corner of the posting. If the button is greyed out, that indicates there is required information missing in your job posting. Hover over the button to see details, and update the posting until the button turns purple.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#viewing-your-job-posting)Viewing your job posting
For more information on customizing your job board, check out [**Job Board: Configuring Your Setup**](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings "Job Board: Configuring Your Setup").
Once published, you should see a View Posting button in the upper right corner of the admin view of your job posting. Click this button to view how candidates will see the posting on your job board.
![Shows what the Overview of the job posting will look like when clicked on by a candidate from your job board.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/XOHg78jHn7OK9mvumBSyT_head-of-recruiting-kara-joseph-new-demo-2026-02-17-at-111656-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When opening the posting, you will see the following:
  * **Job Posting Title:** The job title appears in the upper left. It is taken directly from the Title field in the Basic Info tab of the job posting. 


  * **Location:** The location and any additional locations added to the job posting will show on the left side.


  * **Employment Type:** This information is pulled from the Employment Type field in the Settings tab of the job itself (not the job posting).


  * **Location Type:** This is pulled from the job posting's Basic Info tab. Indicates to candidates whether the job is on-site, hybrid, or remote.


  * **Department:** This information is pulled from the Team field within the job’s Settings tab.


  * **Deadline to Apply:** If you have set a deadline in the job posting's Basic Info tab, it will be listed here (shown in the applicant’s time zone).


  * **Compensation:** If you have opted to display compensation information, it will be displayed here.


  * **Overview:** On the right two thirds of the screen, this is where the description you’ve added to your posting in the Description field will be displayed.


  * **Application:** The application form can be completed by clicking into the Application tab.


Additionally, if you have the job posting set to Listed in the Basic Info tab, then it will show with other listed jobs when viewing your job board: 
![Shows what the job listing will look like on your job board.](https://cdn.zappy.app/f287d5f55653892116cfd5ad7cf88dd5.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#managing-job-postings)Managing job postings
Once created, your job postings will live under the Job Postings tab of the corresponding job. From here, you can manage various aspects of the job posting, including setting it as the default, unpublishing, or deleting the posting all together.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#default-job-posting)Default job posting
The first posting you create for a job will automatically become the default posting, and this is indicated under the **Status** column of the **Job Postings** tab.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kfPYw5QCoADC-rcQS7gU1_group-product-manager-acent-ashby-2026-04-03-at-12049-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can change which posting is the default posting by clicking into it and selecting More, and then Make Default.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/fAtEONMagRBz-IJ1Hhor__group-product-manager-acent-ashby-2026-04-03-at-23130-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
The posting that is marked as the default impacts certain elements of the hiring process for the role:
  * **Application Requests:** When application request links are manually or automatically sent inviting candidates to apply, the details shared will be based on the default job posting, and the default job posting will be assigned to the application. This also means that any automations that are configured on the default job posting will be applied to these applications. 


You can learn more about sending application requests in [Application Requests](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests)﻿.
  * **Email Template Tokens:** For applications that don't have an explicit job posting assigned (e.g. manually created applications, or applications transferred from another job), email template tokens like "job posting description" and "job posting URL" fall back to the default posting.


  * **Interview Briefing Documents:** The job posting section shown to interviewers in briefing documents also uses this same fallback to the default posting for applications without an explicit posting attached.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#unpublishing-and-deleting-job-postings)Unpublishing and deleting job postings
You can unpublish a published job posting by clicking into the posting and selecting Unpublish in the top right. Unpublishing a listing will remove it from job boards until it is published again.
Additionally, job postings can be deleted if needed by selecting More > Delete. 
  * Deleting a posting cannot be undone.


  * You cannot delete a published job posting, so unpublish it first. 


  * If you have multiple postings and need to delete the default posting, you must first set a different posting as the default. 


  * If you have only one job posting, you are able to delete it.


Congrats! You've learned how to create, view, and manage your job postings. If you'd like to understand how to build job posting reports, check out [Reporting on Job Postings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-job-postings)﻿.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#can-i-create-multiple-job-postings-for-one-job)Can I create multiple job postings for one job?
You can, yes. On the Job Postings tab, click the New Draft button again to create an additional job posting. Applications from all postings on the job will be added to the same pipeline.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#is-it-possible-to-see-the-number-of-clicks-on-a-jo)Is it possible to see the number of clicks on a job posting?
It is not currently possible to track clicks or other activities on a job posting within Ashby.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#how-does-linkedin-display-a-job-posting-with-multi)How does LinkedIn display a job posting with multiple locations?
When a job posting has multiple locations (a main location and additional locations) listed, LinkedIn will automatically create a separate job posting for each location. So if a job posting has a main location of New York but additional locations of San Francisco and Los Angeles set in Ashby, three separate job postings would be created in LinkedIn.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#how-do-i-get-a-direct-link-to-a-job-posting)How do I get a direct link to a job posting?
If you have opted to set your posting to Unlisted and would like to send a direct link to the posting to a lead, navigate to your published posting and click the View Posting button. You can then copy the URL from the posting to share.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/60c45de672cc92e7e715263a6b49d3f6.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#can-i-preview-a-job-posting)Can I preview a job posting?
To preview a job posting, you could opt to set it as Unlisted and then click the View Posting option on the job posting to preview it.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#what-if-i-have-one-job-with-multiple-locations)What if I have one job with multiple locations?
  * Add multiple job postings to a job, each with a different location.


  * Your job board will have a job posting for each location.


  * Add multiple locations to one job posting.


  * This will make the career page look cleaner (no duplicate jobs).


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#does-the-ashby-xml-feed-include-the-workplace-type)Does the Ashby XML feed include the workplace type (Remote, Hybrid or On-Site) in the job posting details?
Yes, the XML feed now includes this information so the workplace type will be indicated on 3rd party job boards like LinkedIn and Indeed.
You can specify the location type within the Location Details section of your location settings at Admin > Organization Setup > [Locations](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/locations "Locations").
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/108edf6bd76767ed7fe6407a22ff4ded.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#my-job-posting-is-currently-unlisted-and-candidate)M**y job posting is currently unlisted and candidates are still able to apply - why?**
If your job posting was listed on your Ashby job board at any time, then the job posting links can be scraped by external Job Boards meaning candidates can still submit applications. If a candidate also has the specific job posting URL, they will be able to apply for the job via that posting, even if that posting is unlisted. Candidates will be able to share the unlisted job posting URL too. To stop candidates applying via that job posting completely, it would need to be unpublished.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#what-is-the-difference-between-an-unlisted-and-an)What is the difference between an unlisted and an unpublished job posting?
**Unlisted:** The job posting is published but can only be access via direct link only and the posting will not appear on your main Ashby job board. Candidates will be able to apply via the direct link.
**Unpublished:** Candidates are not able to access this job posting at all and cannot apply for the job via this posting until it is published.
﻿  

﻿

Offers & E-Signatures
# Do I need a Dropbox Sign Integration to use Offers?
1 min
Copy
No, you do not! The Dropbox Sign integration is built-in and can be enabled just by adding the E-Signature Tokens to your Offer Letter Template. Check out [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿ and [E-Signature Documents](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-documents)﻿ for more information on setting up and using E-Signature!
You do not need a Dropbox Sign account to sign e-signature documents. These can be signed directly from the prompt in the Dropbox Sign emails sent to the signatories specified in Ashby when generating the e-signature document.
You also do not need a Dropbox Sign account to access the completed e-signature document. Users with the correct permissions can access the completed e-signature document in the candidate’s profile, and all signatories also receive an email from Dropbox Sign confirming that everyone has signed the document, including the candidate, once all parties have completed the signing process.
If you would prefer to use Docusign, you will need to integrate using your company’s account. More on this at [Docusign](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign)﻿.

# Utilizing Stage Groups
7 min
Copy
This document reviews how to configure interview stage groups and how to utilize the stage group fields for filtering and grouping in reporting.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups#introduction-stage-groups)Introduction: Stage Groups
![Active Applications by Stage Group & Department.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/xxUJcdG3FjBTctGv9knIe_activeapplicationsbystagegroupdepartment.png?format=webp)
﻿
Ashby permits the definition of key interview milestones which we call stage groups, where each group is a collection of individual interview stages. You can create, name, and adjust the order of your stage groups as needed and map your individual interview plan stages into the appropriate group. Once done, any interview plans with different stages that map to the same overall stage of progress can be reported on conveniently to produce results across various jobs, departments or groupings in aggregate.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups#set-up-stage-groups)Set up Stage Groups
For Ashby Analytics customers you can configure your stage groups by navigating to Admin > Data Mapping > [Grouped Interview Plan](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/data-mapping/grouped-interview-plan "Grouped Interview Plan"). Note that you have the ability to select whether the stage group is shown in the funnel and passthrough tables by default.
If you're an Ashby All-In-One customer head on over to [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿ for a guide on setting up your grouped interview plan.
You can add, remove, or re-order stage groups at any time. As your interview stages evolve, make sure to review your stage mapping periodically to ensure all stages are mapped appropriately.
![Screen Shot 2023-06-07 at 7.51.18 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yTAcFH-NSiGZjKJg-_pbf_screenshot2023-06-07at75118am.png?format=webp)
﻿
After your primary stage groups are set up, you can proceed to map your various interview stages into their appropriate groups from the [Interview Stage Mapping](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/data-mapping/stage-mapping "Interview Stage Mapping") page.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups#using-stage-groups-in-reporting)Using Stage Groups in Reporting
Once stage groups are setup, you can leverage their availability with the following fields which enable advanced filtering and grouping.
Fields designed for filtering and grouping in reports are:
  * Current interview stage group: Allows selecting and filtering by the stage group that the application is currently in.


  * Furthest stage group reached: Allows selecting and filtering by the furthest stage group the application was last active in.


Fields designed for filter selection:
  * Reached stage group: Allows filtering applications that have reached a certain stage group, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will return all applications that have passed through this stage or have skipped this stage but passed through a later stage.


  * Passed through stage group: Allows filtering applications that have passed through a certain stage group, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will only return applications that have passed through that stage, not applications that skipped the stage group. Note, passed through stage group will include candidates currently in the stage group.


  * Most recent active stage group: Allows selecting and filtering by the stage group the application was in when being hired or rejected.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups#analysis-examples)Analysis Examples
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups#visualizing-your-candidate-pipeline-with-current-i)Visualizing your candidate pipeline with current interview stage group
One of the most common reporting functions is to evaluate applications by their current interview stage group. In this example you can select active candidates, filter to exclude application review, and focus in the active interview stage groups across departments or jobs.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups#visualizing-qualified-candidates-outcomes-with-rea)Visualizing qualified candidates outcomes with reached stage group
The reached stage group field can be useful in selecting candidates that have made it to a certain point or beyond in your funnel. A common use case is to select qualified candidates on whether or not they have reached the screen stage or beyond. This analysis looks at the percent of qualified candidates hired and forms a screen to hired ratio report.
﻿
﻿

# Passthrough Report Features & FAQ
20 min
Copy
This document reviews the function, features, and common questions about passthrough tables.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#create-a-passthrough-report)Create a Passthrough Report
You can find the passthrough table in the report template library. All passthrough tables are made by making an initial selection of applications and optionally filtering that selection down further and grouping by one or more fields of interest. The following sections outline each step in creating a passthrough table, along with the many options for investigating details and common questions that come up.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#select-passthrough-rates-with-groupings-report-tem)Select Passthrough Rates With Groupings report template
![Screen Cast 2022-12-05 at 11.10.30 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/P5zNbDu9Iwh2hCHpJY8m7_screencast2022-12-05at111030am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#make-an-initial-selection-select-a-time-range)Make an initial selection: select a time range
Every passthrough report begins with an initial selection of applications. This is done by a flexible time range selection, where you can select all applications that had recent activity, ended their consideration, or started in a specific time period. The applications that match this initial selection represent the cohort of applications that are then summarized by their progress through the interview pipeline in the passthrough table itself.
![In this example, the passthrough report will include applications with interviews starting in the last 6 months. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kz604dpdVnTaufEzRuXf8_screenshot2022-12-05at111435am.png?format=webp)
﻿
In this example, the passthrough report will include applications with interviews starting in the last 6 months.
Some common fields used for an initial selection are shown below, but this initial selection permits using any time-related field and is highly customizable:
  * Started At - selects all applications that started (applied or user added them to Ashby) in a specific time period


  * Consideration Ended At - selects all applications that exited your process (archived or hired) in a specific time period


  * Interview’s Start Time - selects all applications with an interview that started in the specific time period


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#filter-your-initial-selection-optional)Filter your initial selection (optional)
Your initial selection of applications might require some additional refinement. You can use the filters section to add additional conditions, such as selecting for applications in a specific department, belonging to a specific recruiter or hiring manager, and so on.
![The advanced filter interface allows filtering by any combination of fields to further select applications of interest for your passthrough report. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/KAVdkFmXNRvsXLgd6UynT_screenshot2022-12-05at14552pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
The advanced filter interface allows filtering by any combination of fields to further select applications of interest for your passthrough report.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#group-results-optional)Group results (optional)
With an initial selection made and optional filtering added, your passthrough table will generate a result based on your interview plan. You will see a single row summarizing the progress across all applications that have exited consideration (i.e. are no longer active).
![This is an example passthrough report with nothing selected in the Group By section. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/FZAZFKPyhhcH_d2mrRxNQ_screenshot2022-04-29at113957am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This is an example passthrough report with nothing selected in the Group By section.
It is often of interest to see the total passthrough report broken down by segments, such as source type, department, or recruiter.
To accomplish this you can add a field to the Group By section, and the results will present the applications per field value and include the top-level rollup across all groups.
![Screen Shot 2022-12-05 at 1.51.58 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qZqru_VOnpiVmYgullDWO_screenshot2022-12-05at15158pm.png)
﻿
![This results on this passthrough report are grouped by Department. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/uO7sidRJggSd3TfCEv4io_screenshot2022-04-29at114304am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This results on this passthrough report are grouped by Department.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#group-by-multiple-fields)Group by multiple fields
If you would like to see a passthrough table with sub-grouping breakdowns, you can add multiple fields to the Group By section. The results will include passthroughs unique to each sub-group, a roll-up per group, and a roll-up of all applications at the top level.
Adding multiple group-by values permits more details in the passthrough report.
![Screen Shot 2022-12-05 at 1.50.35 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3wdPdzI1fiyW-B10dEWYn_screenshot2022-12-05at15035pm.png)
﻿
![This example shows the results grouped by Department and Source Type. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Z6C29TgCqB6786wcfDT8O_screenshot2022-04-29at114738am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This example shows the results grouped by Department and Source Type.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#passthrough-report-configuration-options)Passthrough report configuration & options
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#group-stages)Group stages
If your host ATS (Greenhouse or Ashby) permit multiple interview plans, you may find it useful to ungroup the stages and look at a specific stage by stage passthrough rates of a particular interview plan. You can do this by deselecting the Group Stages? option.
![When the stages are no longer grouped, the interview plan name and individual plan stages are presented.](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/gnvfAlyk0S5nL4bXSwJpD_screencast2022-12-05at22447pm.gif)
﻿
When the stages are no longer grouped, the interview plan name and individual plan stages are presented.
It is suggested that you do this in combination with a stringent filter, otherwise you will get one row per unique interview plan.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#hide-review-lead-stages)Hide review & lead stages
By default, the option to hide review and lead stages is on. If you’d like to see the total application volume from initial review through your funnel, you can uncheck the Hide Review Stages? option. Your passthrough will now start from the top of the funnel.
![Screen Cast 2022-12-05 at 2.26.34 PM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/QJbiohWF7WpLZVcdROp-S_screencast2022-12-05at22634pm.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#hide-stages)Hide stages
If there are specific stage groups that do not apply to the report of interest, you can optionally hide them by using the Hide Stage Groups option. When stages are hidden, the effective passthrough rate is calculated taking into account the hidden stages.
The Group Stages? box needs to be checked in order to hide stage groups.
The number of hidden stages is shown underneath Hide Stage Groups.
![Screen Cast 2022-12-05 at 2.27.46 PM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/h1gCIwB5Pnhq8rYCr2j32_screencast2022-12-05at22746pm.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#group-stages-by-outcome)Group stages by outcome
You can use the Group By Stage Outcome? option to summarize the outcome of applications per stage by whether they skipped the stage or passed through it (i.e. were moved into the stage) and by the archive reason category.
![Screen Shot 2022-12-05 at 2.29.57 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/XsIm4LXxr5GFBfU66U1tB_screenshot2022-12-05at22957pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#show-active-applications-count)Show active applications count
When enabling Show Active Count & Project Hires, you will notice an additional column will appear on the left-hand side under each stage. This column shows you the number of candidate applications currently active in that stage.
The count on the right-hand side of each stage shows the number of candidate applications that have passed through that stage. These applications may still be active in process in a downstream stage or archived.
![Screen Shot 2022-12-05 at 2.32.32 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qJxnIuhozG0HzibKVEVls_screenshot2022-12-05at23232pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#show-projected-hires)Show projected hires
Projected hires count is also turned on when you enable active count, but it can be hidden under Hide Stages if there is not a need to show projected hires. The calculation for projected hires considers your current pipeline, along with historical passthrough rates.
![Screen Cast 2022-12-05 at 2.34.41 PM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/EqA50ft9cbiIy4Lzd3M5q_screencast2022-12-05at23441pm.gif)
﻿
If the i icon appears in the projected hires column, this means there is not enough data to calculate projected hires for that line.
![Screen Shot 2022-12-05 at 2.37.33 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/HqoxQxeUL9aRe21kEcxaW_screenshot2022-12-05at23733pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#how-are-passthrough-percentages-calculated)How are passthrough percentages calculated?
Passthrough reports by default only include applications that have exited consideration, meaning they have either been archived or hired. Each application counts once and only once for each stage up until the furthest stage group they have reached. The passthrough percent from Stage A to Stage B is calculated as the count of applications that reached Stage A divided by the count of applications that reached Stage B.
![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/A_8GmG1budugu-uK6iiWG_ptrexample.png)
﻿
Example passthrough calculation from one stage to the next.
When the show active option is enabled, the passthrough calculation is more sophisticated. When calculating the passthrough from Stage A to Stage B, applications active in Stage A must be removed from the calculation as whether or not they move forward to Stage B is not yet determined. Ashby takes care of this for you automatically.
![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/uSh33dVdRXUCqKCavrR2I_ptrwithactive.png)
﻿
When active counts are included, the applications active in Stage A must be excluded as whether or not they count in Stage B is not yet determined.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#how-are-job-considerations-that-skipped-stages-inc)How are job considerations that skipped stages included in the report?
Job considerations that skipped stages are still counted as having passed through all previous stages (Otherwise, the drop-off rate would be distorted).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#how-are-projections-calculated)How are projections calculated?
When the show projections option is enabled, the cumulative passthrough rate from each stage forward through all downstream stages is applied to the active application count. This is done per stage across all active candidates, summing up the likelihood of all active candidates to proceed all the way to the hired outcome.
When grouping by a field that has a many-to-one relationship to applications (e.g. hiring manager, recruiter), the projected hires total is calculated by the values in the totals row at the very top of the table.
When grouping by a field that has a one-to-one relationship to applications (e.g. department), the projected hires total is calculated as the sum of each individual row’s projected hire.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#why-are-my-passthrough-rates-different-than-what-g)Why are my passthrough rates different than what Greenhouse reports?
Greenhouse passthrough rates include active applications in the current stage of interest as not passed through to the next stage, whereas Ashby automatically removes applications where their accounting in the passthrough calculation is not yet determined. Given this, you may see lower numbers for passthroughs in your Greenhouse report vs the Ashby passthrough table.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#why-are-the-passthrough-rates-all-100)Why are the passthrough rates all 100%?
The most common cause of passthrough rates being 100% across the funnel is that there’s a filter selecting only for hired candidates. If you’re seeing 100% partially through the funnel, your initial selection, filters or grouping may have implicit conditions that select for candidates that make it to a certain stage.
A common example of this is to filter or group by Opening’s Job or Requisition’s Job in Greenhouse or Lever, respectively. Candidate are only associated with Openings and Requisitions when at the end of the funnel, thus grouping by those properties is implicitly selecting for only candidates that have reached the end of the funnel. The solution in this case is to group by the Application’s Job (or Opportunity’s Job in Lever).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#why-arent-all-the-expected-applications-showing)Why aren’t all the expected applications showing?
The initial selection excludes the applications of interest
The selected timeframe for the passthrough table report in Ashby implies filters on the applications that are included or excluded. For example, if you select a timeframe of interview start time, you will only see applications that have had an interview start in the time range selected. This can potentially exclude applications that you intend to view.
The application is still active in process
The passthrough table default is to exclude active applications. If you do not see the counts you expect, first review whether the candidates are still active in process. To view active counts, you can select the Show Active Count & Projected Hires box in the View Options section.
The application is in a review or lead stage
If you do not see the count you expect, it may be because the candidate’s application is in a review or lead stage, neither of which are shown by default. To view the application review stage, uncheck the Hide Review Stages? box in the View Options section. To view the lead stages, uncheck the Hide Lead Stages? box in the View Options section.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#why-do-application-counts-change-when-enabling-sho)Why do application counts change when enabling Show Active Count & Projected Hires?
When the option to show active applications is enabled, you may notice that the count of applications that have passed through a stage increases. When this option is enabled, all currently active applications that are in downstream stages are counted as having passed through upstream stages.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#why-is-the-count-of-interviews-or-activities-in-th)Why is the count of interviews or activities in the timeframe is wrong?
It is common to misinterpret the passthrough report as an activity summary based on the [initial selection.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq)﻿ Though you may see a count such as 10 applications at the onsite stage group the passthrough table is not specifying when exactly those applications passed through that stage. Additionally, the passthrough table does not count scheduled interviews but only whether an application has made it to a stage or beyond. All that is summarized is whether the candidates originally selected and filtered have applications that reached various stage groups.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#can-i-export-a-passthrough-report-to-a-spreadsheet)C**an I export a passthrough report to a spreadsheet?**
No. At this time, passthrough reports in Ashby can only be exported as PDFs. Exporting passthrough reports to a spreadsheet format (CSV or Excel) is not currently supported.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq#what-timeframe-does-the-hire-count-projected-metri)**What timeframe does the Hire Count Projected metric represent?**
The Hire Count Projected metric is not tied to a specific timeframe. It estimates the number of hires expected based on the current candidates in your pipeline and historical passthrough rates between stages. Because it only considers the current pipeline, it does not include future applicants. For forecasting hires against specific timelines or targets, the Recruiting Planner is typically the better tool.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Jobs & Openings
# Openings Management
25 min
Copy
Openings allow you to set up positions that are linked to jobs you create in Ashby, so if you are hiring multiple candidates for the same job, you can create openings, rather than multiple jobs to track headcount. Openings are a great tool to use for evergreen roles, as you can track the opening and closing date of each opening individually, while keeping the same job open.
Openings Management is available on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#why-use-openings)Why use openings?
Headcount planning is essential to achieving operational excellence in hiring. Doing so helps your team to understand if you have the knowledge and skills on the team to achieve your goals, and to reduce costs on hiring workflows that don’t yield results. Openings in Ashby let you track your open headcount and hiring goals as well as metrics for each individual headcount accurately.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#overview)Overview
﻿
See the table below for information on the roles that have access to configure openings settings and set up openings.  
| Access Role  | Has access to enable/configure opening settings?  | Can see openings (for departments and jobs they have access to)?  | Can create an opening?  | Can link an opening to a job?  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  | ❌  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  | ❌  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  | ✅ *  | ✅ *  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  | ✅ *  | ✅ *  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  | ✅ *  | ✅ *  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌  | ✅ *  | ✅ *  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌  | ✅ *  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌  | ✅ *  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  | ✅ *  | ✅  | ✅  |  
*If you provide a user with _Manage Openings_ access as part of their permissions, they will be able to see all openings for the jobs and departments they have access to along with any openings that aren’t linked to a job. Users with Manage Openings permissions will also be able to create openings. More on this at [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿ . If the user does not have _Manage Openings_ set as part of their permissions, then they will be able to see openings linked to the jobs they've been given access to. Team or department assignment alone does not grant visibility to standalone openings. If _Manage Openings_ is not enabled within their permissions, the user will not be able to see openings that aren't linked to any jobs.
Only Organization Admins can configure openings settings, but Elevated Access users with the _Manage Openings_ permissions can create an opening and link it to a department. Only those with Elevated Access - Admin Access or higher can link an opening to a role that they have access to.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#openings-best-practices)Openings: Best Practices
Openings allow you to ensure that hiring only happens against approved headcount. To that end, you should follow these best practices:
  * When you are actively hiring for a job, it should be linked to at least one approved opening. 


  * Whenever you make a hire, the offer should be created in Ashby and linked to an approved opening (and if you enable Strict Mode, Ashby will require this). For more on creating offers and sending them out to candidates, check out [Offers and Offer Letters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters)﻿


  * It is recommended to input your forward-looking hiring plan into Ashby (e.g. for the next quarter or next half year). You can input openings as Approved but not Open just yet. Ashby allows you to input openings ahead of opening the corresponding jobs (often, you know you want to hire for a certain role, but it’s far enough out that you don’t have a job description yet and haven’t completed an intake meeting). 


An opening should be marked as Open as soon as you actively start hiring for it. This allows you to track time-to-fill accurately (the time from when a specific opening was opened to when it was filled with a hire) on the opening level, allowing for accurate reporting.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#enabling-openings-in-admin)Enabling openings in Admin
To enable the openings feature:
  * Navigate to Admin > Jobs & Applications > Openings


  * Toggle on the Enable Manual Opening Management option


  * Configure the additional options to suit your organization


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/-efGI3rgUitqzOoVORRZu_openings-acent-ashby-2026-01-09-at-35610-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Enable Manual Opening Management** - use this to enable/disable the openings feature for your org
**Opening Identifier Prefix** - you can append an opening identifier to the beginning automatically generated numeric value.
**Next Opening Identifier Number -** Add in the next opening identifier here (if you’d like it to start on a specific number).
**Require Openings for Jobs** - when toggled on, your team will be required to link openings when opening new jobs. We recommend leaving strict mode off while setting up openings so your team isn’t blocked.
**Require Openings for Hires** - when toggled on, offers can’t be generated and candidates can’t be moved to the Hired stage without having an opening attached to their offer.
**Enable Requisition ID on Jobs** - openings replace the existing Requisition ID field on the settings page for a job, but you have the option to retain this field as you work to transition between the two (this is uncommon). The requisition ID can be added or amended in the _Requisition ID_ field on the _Settings_ tab within a job.
**Auto-Name Job Requisition Identifiers?** - If enabled, job custom requisition identifierswill be automatically generated.
**Job Requisition Identifier Prefix** - If enabled, confirms the prefix to be used for job requisition identifiers when creating a new job.
**Next Requisition Identifier Number -** If enabled, confirms the next number to be used in a job requisition identifiers when creating a new job.
**Require Team** - If enabled, the Team field on Opening will be required going forward. A draft opening may still be created without a Team.
**Require Location** -If enabled, the Location field on Opening will be required going forward. A draft opening may still be created without a Team.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#create-openings)Create Openings
If you set up an opening, it defaults to draft mode. This allows you to create future openings without linking them to a job and open them immediately.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#create-a-draft-opening-on-the-openings-page)Create a draft opening on the Openings page
To create an opening on the Openings page:
  1. Navigate to [Jobs>Openings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/openings "Jobs>Openings")﻿


  1. Click Create Opening in the top right corner of the page.


  1. Fill out the form, and click Create Draft.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Nj4ITY3PdTpgWKj1JtBL8_openings-management-archbee-document-2026-02-02-at-95245-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ca8c5839cf6b9b1359320e9b231d1c30.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#create-a-draft-opening-on-a-specific-job)Create a draft opening on a specific job
To create an opening for a specific job:
  1. Navigate to the Openings tab of the job


  1. click Create Job Opening in the top right corner of the page


  1. Fill out the form, and click Create Draft.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/DVRRKX3M6GTVFIhzIkmXR_openings-management-archbee-document-2026-02-02-at-95921-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#create-a-draft-opening-via-the-top-navigation-bar)Create a draft opening via the top navigation bar
  1. Click on the + Add button on the top navigation bar and select Opening.


  1. Fill out the form and click Create Draft.


﻿
You can also import openings. For more on this, check out [Bulk Import Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#importing-openings)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#approving-and-setting-openings-as-open)Approving and setting openings as open
To approve or start the approval process for a draft opening, click the Draft status dropdown within the opening details. If there's an approval process in place, you will see a Create Approval Request option. Click this to review the approval process and create an approval request. More on configuring approvals at [Approvals](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals)﻿.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/lqdvLK6f8IsxOhW9pPMtg_opening-acent-ashby-2026-02-02-at-101809-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If there is not an approval process configured with criteria matching that of the opening, you will see the option to approve the opening when clicking the Draft status. This will apply the Approved status to the opening.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/DR_gECcl28rZ82ifks8kQ_opening-acent-ashby-2026-02-02-at-102159-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
After the opening is listed as Approved, click the Approved status within the opening details, then Open to open the opening and start hiring for it.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SXaVm1LHMgxj7mZdyy8FN_opening-acent-ashby-2026-02-02-at-102316-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#view-openings)View Openings
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#view-all-openings)View all openings
To review all openings in one place navigate to Jobs > Openings. Options on this page include:
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/nLcu_X6THwpsc3BQAud25_openings-acent-ashby-2026-01-16-at-113956-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. **Create Opening** - create a new opening in the results section of the page.


  1. **Filter** - you can filter openings using opening fields.


  1. **Saved Filters** - you can save filters to access a subset of your openings quickly.


  1. **Results** - the full list of openings


  1. **Opening** - each row represents an opening. Click on the ID of an opening to view the details.


Click on an opening to make any amendments, add details or update the opening status.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/S8U5ClhVpwGACgF324fUt_opening-acent-ashby-2026-01-16-at-114545-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#on-a-specific-job)**On a specific job**
To view an opening on a specific job, navigate to the job, then click Openings in the left menu. Organization Admins can create new openings from this page. Elevated Access Users can add an existing opening to a job. Options on this page include:
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/OfIgkUm-WELRxLd0fiEKl_lead-designer-acent-ashby-2026-01-16-at-114845-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. **Filter** - you can filter openings using opening fields


  1. **Saved Filters** - you can save filters to access a subset of your openings quickly


  1. **Add Existing Opening to Job** - apply an opening you've already configured to this job


  1. **Create Job Opening** - create a new opening for this role


  1. **Seach:** Search for specific openings if you have a long list


  1. **Opening** - each row represents an opening. Click on the ID of an opening to view the details


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#use-openings)Use Openings
Once openings are created, they can be linked to jobs and candidates.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#open-jobs-and-link-to-openings)Open jobs and link to openings
If your organization requires jobs to be linked to openings, attach the approved opening when opening the job. The Open Job screen that is displayed when you opt to open the job will ask you to select an opening from the Opening drop-down. Please note that if you link a draft opening to a job, then open the job, the opening will automatically open as well.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/GO_gW93N_iXqc5SOk7UIv_image.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#detaching-an-opening-from-a-job)Detaching an opening from a job
To remove an opening from a job, click on the opening in question, either from within the Jobs > Openings section of Ashby, or within the Openings section of the job. In the Jobs tab, click the X on the role you'd like to remove the opening from. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/uc0CSwtGQPcUzZw1vy4Ej_openings-re-draft-archbee-document-2026-02-02-at-95652-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Want to attach the opening to a new job? Click the Select Jobs dropdown to select another job.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#extend-offers)Extend offers
When extending an offer for a job, you can link it to an opening; once that opening is filled, you’ll need to create a new opening on the job before extending any new offers.
If you have Require Openings for Hires enabled within your Openings settings, then you will need to specify an opening when creating an offer.
When adding an opening to an offer, make sure that the opening is set to Open. If the opening isn’t directly linked to the job, then you can switch off the Only show openings of job? option to choose from all of the openings with the Open status.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vCPiy2-EVnGMzzTfJZHGo_openings-re-draft-archbee-document-2026-02-02-at-95750-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#reporting-on-openings-versus-jobs)Reporting on Openings versus Jobs
If you have openings enabled in Ashby, you will have the option to report on them separately from jobs. If you select Openings as the subject when setting up a custom report, then each individual opening will be reflected in the results. If you opt to set Jobs as the subject, then data relating to the job the openings are linked to will be displayed in the results.
**Reporting Time to Fill for openings**
If you opted to generate a Time to Fill for Openings report, if you have multiple openings for a single job, each opening represents the active filling of a position for that job. The average calculated is taken from when the opening was first opened, to when it was filled.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3f07VQ-lWZpiK14sTtqQr_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-01-16-at-115944-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
As such, the dates of openings being created and filled will be used. On the Jobs Dashboard in Core Dashboards, the time to fill report shows how long jobs have been open, how many hires were made against these jobs, and calculates a ratio.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#what-fields-are-supported-on-openings)What fields are supported on openings?
  * Opening ID


  * Description


  * Team


  * Jobs


  * Location


  * Roles


  * Add Member


  * Employment Type


  * Is Backfill?


  * Target Hire Date


  * Target Start Date


  * Compensation Range


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#can-i-set-the-team-and-location-fields-on-my-openi)Can I set the Team and Location fields on my openings to be required?
You can, yes! Head to Admin > Jobs & Applications > [Openings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/headcount-planning "Openings"), then enable the Require Team and Require Location options. Going forward, draft openings can be created without the required information but can’t be moved out of their draft state until the required fields are completed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/963ec32bbf6ef1845d043e5f9e016911.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#is-the-compensation-range-visible-to-everyone)Is the compensation range visible to everyone?
Currently all users who can see openings can also see the comp range; we are still refining permissions, and are planning to change this.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#whats-the-difference-between-jobs-and-openings)What’s the difference between jobs and openings?
The table below outlines what is included in each, and what you’d use them to capture.  
| ﻿  
 | **Jobs**  | **Openings**  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| **Include**  | - The public job description - The interview plan and all of its activities - All active candidates under consideration for that job  | - Your hiring plan (where one opening represents one open headcount) - Which candidate was hired against which open headcount - How long it took to fill an individual headcount  |  
| **Use to capture**  | - The types of hires you want to make - How you want to assess candidates for these types of jobs - How you want to advertise these jobs on your career page  | - Headcount information to accurately report on hiring goals, progress, and timing - Get approval where needed when additional headcount is added to a plan  |  
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#what-about-the-requisition-id-field-on-a-jobs-sett)What about the Requisition ID field on a job’s settings page?
Ashby supports a Requisition ID field on jobs to be compatible with some uncommon use cases. The main reasons to use this field instead of openings is:
  * For every job, you will ever only make one hire


  * For customers with custom integrations: You are integrating with a third-party system that is syncing headcount plans into Ashby and you want each of these headcounts to create a unique job


It is recommended to sync third-party headcount data into openings instead of jobs
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#what-statuses-are-applied-to-openings)What statuses are applied to openings?
You can view the status of an opening within the Status column, either when viewing openings across the company or on the job it’s linked to.
An opening can have one of the following statuses:
Draft: This opening is still in draft and can be opened, marked as approved or submitted for approval (if approvals are required).
Approved: The opening has been approved and is ready to open.
Open: This opening can now be filled by a hired candidate.
Filled: A candidate has been hired and has filled this opening.
Closed: The opening has been closed out without a candidate being hired into it. This can be moved back to a _Draft_ state if needed by clicking _More_ when in the closed opening’s details.
Archived: This opening is hidden from the default openings view and would need to be restored before it could be moved back to a draft state.
To restore an archived opening, open up the archived opening’s details, click More, then Restore. You also have the option to delete an archived opening completely. Please note: You can delete an archived opening but not an archived job.
﻿  

﻿  

![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qszYxRyJx2M4NiQ1v7Iqn_openings-management-archbee-document-2026-02-02-at-100107-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/iMQbZqbgCEkblNarkyvq6_openings-management-archbee-document-2026-02-02-at-100155-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#how-do-openings-work-when-assigned-to-confidential)H**ow do openings work when assigned to confidential jobs?**
If the opening is only assigned to a job set as confidential, only those with access to the confidential job will be able to see the opening. If the opening is also linked to other non-confidential jobs, others can access the opening but won’t see the confidential job linked to it.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management#how-do-i-configure-close-reasons-for-openings)How do I configure close reasons for openings?
When you close out an opening, you will be asked to provide a reason. Use the Reason for closing dropdown to assign a reason.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BscaYtknSWNPCEHcwX8SZ_opening-acent-ashby-2026-03-16-at-35539-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Close reasons for openings pull from close job reasons in Admin > Organisation Setup > Close Job Reasons. If you do not need any reasons listed, navigate to [Close Job Reasons](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/close-job-reasons "Close Job Reasons") to set up reasons before closing out the opening.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews & Scheduling FAQs
# Am I warned when scheduling if an interviewer has already been a part of the interviewing process?
2 min
Copy
When manually scheduling interviews, the _i_ icon beside the interviewer’s details will turn yellow if they have already been a part of the process or if they referred the candidate for the role. You can hover over the _i_ icon for additional information and decide whether you would like to continue or replace the interviewer.
Please note this is currently only available when scheduling interviews using the Manual Schedule option. The same warnings will not be shown when generating direct booking links or creating schedule interview activities.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/am-i-warned-when-scheduling-if-an-interviewer-has-already-been-a-part-of-the-interviewing-process#overview)Overview
When scheduling an interview using the Manual Schedule option,i icons appear beside each interviewer on the calendar interface. You can hover over these icons to view information like their interview limits and interviewer preferences. You can read more on setting interview loads and preferences in [Interviewers: Assigning, Rescheduling, and Setting Interviewer Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits)﻿.
If an interviewer has already interviewed the candidate for this job consideration, or if they referred the candidate, then the _i_ icon will show in yellow, indicating a warning.
The interviewer information will still be displayed when you hover over the i icon but with a Warnings section. In this example we can see the interviewer has already interviewed the candidate. The details of the previous interview are also included for your reference if applicable.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1f712049a65aab5c62b9968ad53e2425.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b242d148ff8c9795644ee8836640fcde.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/am-i-warned-when-scheduling-if-an-interviewer-has-already-been-a-part-of-the-interviewing-process#will-the-warnings-show-if-im-choosing-alternate-in)Will the warnings show if I’m choosing alternate interviewers to schedule from an interview pool?
Yes, when you opt to select an alternate from an interviewer pool, any interviewers who referred the candidate or who have already interviewed them will have a yellow i icon beside their details. Again, you can hover over the icon to view any warnings.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4e4379a6d28c3a85e3ddde998b8cf47e.png)
﻿
![Screenshot 2025-01-10 at 12.35.32.png](https://cdn.zappy.app/978e017db2e2c6651cd972f2ca00d618.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿
