Candidates
# Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates
8 min
Copy
In Ashby, you can delete candidates or anonymize them to remove data from your account. Both options are available under the More drop-down in a candidate’s profile.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#overview)Overview
A candidate’s profile can be deleted or anonymized if they aren’t being actively considered for any jobs. If the candidate is still being considered for a role, they will need to be archived on all job considerations before they can be deleted or anonymized. Both the options to delete or anonymize a candidate can be found within the More section of their profile.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/053ba6113a092322c077f483c3c1c7db.png)
﻿
Please see the table below for information on the access roles that are able to anonymize and delete candidates in Ashby.  
| Access Role  | Can Delete/Anonymize a candidate?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin (Private)  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#anonymizing-candidates)Anonymizing Candidates
To anonymize a candidate, click More on the candidate profile, then Anonymize. Candidates that are anonymized will have PII removed from their profile but information can still be used for reporting (archive reasoning, stage reached, etc). Anonymization cannot be undone.
﻿
When a candidate is anonymized, an activity will be added to their profile with a timestamp of the date and time they were anonymized.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/51e9f1db469d86fd852c699478425c09.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#deleting-candidates)Deleting Candidates
To delete a candidate, click More on the candidate profile, then Delete _._ When you initially delete a candidate, it’s treated as a soft deletion. 
﻿
You can restore candidates or fast track a permanent deletion via the [Restore Deletions](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/restore-deletions "Restore Deletions") page. If the candidate is not restored after ten days, then they will be permanently deleted. The date of permanent deletion is listed on the Restore Deletions page.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#can-i-configure-rules-to-help-maintain-compliance)Can I configure rules to help maintain compliance with data laws?
We have data privacy and compliance features that can assist with this. You can find our guide on those features at [Data Privacy and Compliance](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#a-candidate-has-multiple-job-considerations-or-app)A candidate has multiple job considerations or applications on their profile for the same job. Can I delete an application on their profile?
It’s not possible to merge or delete job considerations on a candidate’s profile. If the candidate should only be considered for the role once, archive the other job considerations you don’t plan to move forward with.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#how-does-anonymizing-a-candidate-work-with-the-ema)H**ow does anonymizing a candidate work with the email events on the candidate’s feed?**
Once a candidate has been anonymized, a record of email events will still show on the candidate’s feed (including the date and time the email was sent). However, the subject, body and the candidate’s email address will be anonymized.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#can-i-automate-anonymizing-or-deleting-candidates)C**an I automate anonymizing or deleting candidates?**
No, candidates would need to be anonymized or deleted manually in Ashby.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates#are-ai-notetaker-recordings-notes-and-transcripts)A**re AI Notetaker recordings, notes and transcripts deleted when a candidate is deleted or anonymized?**
Yes, recordings, transcripts, and notes are all deleted when a candidate is anonymized.
﻿  

﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)

# Tracking the source of an application
10 min
Copy
You can use custom tracking links to automatically track inbound candidate sources from beyond Ashby’s built-in job board integrations. More on these at [How do I set up job board connections?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-set-up-job-board-connections)﻿**﻿**
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#overview)Overview
A custom tracking link﻿ has a tag added to the end of the URL that relates to a specific source in Ashby. When a candidate applies through a tracked link, the source will automatically be applied to that specific job consideration.
You do not need to add a custom tracking link for built-in job board integrations like Indeed. When those integrations are enabled, the associated job board will automatically be applied as the source.
If you are using the automated posting or job wrapping options in LinkedIn, we do recommend setting up a custom tracking link. More on the LinkedIn integration options at [ LinkedIn Job Posting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application " LinkedIn Job Posting").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#permissions-custom-tracking-links)Permissions: Custom tracking links
See the table below for information on the roles that have access to create custom tracking links.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Only Organization Admins can configure custom tracking links by default. Elevated Access users can be given access to this feature if needed. More on this at [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application "Additional User Permissions").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#creating-custom-tracking-links)Creating custom tracking links
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#set-up-a-source)**Set up a source**
Set up a source at [Admin > Organization Setup > Candidate Sources](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/sources "Admin > Organization Setup > Candidate Sources"). Click + New Source and in your source name and assign a source type.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ee51d6586ddd2b9c3257ccb45f86e8ff.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#creating-your-custom-tracking-link)**Creating your custom tracking link**
Add a corresponding job board tracking link at [Admin > Job Boards > Custom Tracking Links](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/custom-tracked-links "Admin > Job Boards > Custom Tracking Links"). Click + New to create a new custom tracking link.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a4deb8e5eacafcfa467e0959dec78e9d.png)
﻿
If you are using [Multiple Brands](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/multiple-brands)﻿, you will need to select the brand you'd like to configure a custom tracking link for using the dropdown to the right of the + New button.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/JzJpw7611c-d9r2GVOTsA_custom-tracking-links-select-brand.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When setting up your tracked link, you’ll be required to add in a title and determine the source applied to candidates who apply via that link.
You will also have the option to select a user to automatically be added to the Credited To field and add in a tracking code as well. If you opt to not add in a tracking code, one will be generated automatically.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/09ca71a2f0bdda8121a81d247b53399c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#using-custom-tracking-links)Using custom tracking links
Once the link has been created, it can be shared or added to job boards. When a candidate applies through the tracked link, the source specified will automatically be applied to their job consideration. You can view a candidate’s source in the Attribution section of their profile.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8ac08138f87b4e99d908cd17a88ff985.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#tracking-links-for-individual-jobs)Tracking links for individual jobs
When creating a tracking link, the link will go to the main job board. To get the link for a specific job, go to the main custom tracking link generated for the job board in [Admin > Job Board > Custom Tracking Links](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/custom-tracked-links "Admin > Job Board > Custom Tracking Links").
On your job board, click on the specific job, then copy the link from the browser’s URL field.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#can-custom-tracking-links-only-be-used-for-externa)Can custom tracking links only be used for external job boards?
Custom Tracking Links can be used any time you’d like to track an external source. Though most commonly used with job boards, they can be used to allow employees or friends of the company to share links externally (e.g. on social media) and still be credited as the source.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#what-do-i-enter-in-the-tracking-code-field-when-ad)What do I enter in the ‘Tracking Code’ field when adding a tracking link?
You can make the tracking code whatever you’d like, or you can click ‘Add’ without entering a tracking code. If you do the latter, a tracking code will be automatically generated. The goal is to have a unique code applied to allow us to track applications and associate them with a specific source.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#does-ashby-support-google-analytics-or-other-conve)Does Ashby support Google Analytics or other conversion tracking?
Ashby doesn’t integrate with Google Analytics, so we recommend using the iFrame embed version of the career page and embedding your pixel there.
More on this can be found at[Embedding Ashby Job Boards in an external Careers Page](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/embedding-ashby-job-boards-in-an-external-careers-page)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tracking-the-source-of-an-application#the-third-party-job-board-allows-me-to-enter-the-u)The third-party job board allows me to enter the UTM code directly - can I just add in the code there, then have it applied to postings via that route instead?
A lot of third-party job boards support this and yes, you should be able to add the tracking code there, then it will be applied to all postings on that third-party job board moving forward.
﻿

# Core Dashboards
10 min
Copy
Core dashboards are a great place to start when exploring reports and getting insights on jobs, interviews, offers and your general pipeline. These dashboards represent a suite of analytics that provide insights across various TA domains, to ensure that your team has access to the data they need.
Core Dashboards are available across all plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
Custom Dashboards are available on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#overview)Overview
To access your core dashboards, head to Reports, then to [Core Dashboards](https://app.ashbyhq.com/dashboards/core "Core Dashboards"). We have six ready-made dashboards providing instant insights across all your recruiting activities in Ashby.
**Executive Dashboard:** A high-level overview, providing on hires, offer acceptance rate, all currently open roles and interview frequency.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d2f0c2664d6ecb3be8af4e5e0b917aef.png)
﻿
**Jobs Dashboard:** Reports on your open and closed jobs, time to hire and open and filled openings.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/432d763e892dddb28acdcfa5cfccea45.png)
﻿
**Sourcing Dashboard:** Track your sequences and sourced candidates here.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/96a8cb63c2b2cbb4cf23a2f716187d7d.png)
﻿
**Interview Dashboard:** Track key scheduling metrics like your upcoming and completed interviews, your feedback completion rate and overall feedback scores here. You will need to set up a grouped interview plan to view this dashboard. More on this at [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿﻿
This is a separate dashboard from the Interview Dashboard under Views on the home page. For more on scheduling requests and tracking scheduling with the other Interview Dashboard, check out [Advanced Scheduling Automation Add-On](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#LqCDO)﻿.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7fa3d204fb829545eb8db1c8bc87b530.png)
﻿
**Offers Dashboard:** Track your offer acceptance rate, reasons why a candidate declined an offer, the overall average time to offer by both department and recruiter.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3b46888a7cdf082aea198f1f1be9b83b.png)
﻿
**CRM Summary Dashboard:** Track how many candidates are being added each week, how many candidates are in the Active stages of a job’s pipeline, how many leads are not being considered for a job currently and your weekly archive volume.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/88c0f6d84b2281a49992b95f8592f7a5.png)
﻿
**DEI Dashboard:** This dashboard is only available to those who have purchased the Diversity Inference add-on. Please reach out to your Sales Executive if you want to utilize this feature.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#core-dashboard-reports)Core Dashboard Reports
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#executive-dashboard)Executive Dashboard
**Hires**
This report lists all job considerations﻿ with the Hired outcome. Includes the candidate’s name, the job, team, recruiter, hiring manager, the source and when the candidate was hired.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/66ee358e19580f01d569618e78a13b36.png)
﻿
﻿  

**Hired Not Started**
A list of hired candidates who have a future start date. This report includes the candidate’s name, the job, the job’s team, the recruiter, hiring manager, source, the date the candidate was hired at and their start date (pulled from their offer details).
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/30238a596f6677731f732ea08043283f.png)

A count of all completed interviews. The report counts one interview per stage per candidate.
If a candidate has multiple interviews scheduled in one stage, this would count all interviews as one for the candidate.

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#can-you-schedule-a-core-dashboard-to-be-sent-out-a)Can you schedule a core dashboard to be sent out automatically?
This isn’t possible currently but is something on our roadmap! It is possible to schedule custom dashboards. More on scheduled dashboards at [Scheduled Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduled-dashboards)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#can-i-add-a-dashboard-level-filter-to-the-core-das)Can I add a dashboard-level filter to the core dashboards?
This isn’t possible with core dashboards, no.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#how-often-are-reports-and-dashboards-refreshed)H**ow often are reports and dashboards refreshed?**
  * Report results are refreshed live when a report or dashboard is opened. The results are then cached for 1 hour. If report parameters or dashboard filters are changed within that hour, the report or dashboard will be refreshed.


  * Reports can be refreshed manually at any time on the dashboard or when reviewing a report on its own.


  * To refresh a report on a dashboard, click on the “…” in the top right-hand corner of the report tile and click Refresh Report.


  * Refreshing the page will not refresh a report.


﻿  

![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bMGqqxfPmxkuBxSMManDl_screenshot2022-11-04at102607am.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards#can-i-export-a-core-dashboard)Can I export a core dashboard?
This isn’t currently possible.
﻿

# Open Rates and Reporting on Sequences
2 min
Copy
For more on the sequence pipeline and metrics check out [Sequences](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#sequence-reporting)﻿.
Due to privacy tools like Apple Mail privacy protection and Gmail image proxying pre-loading images in emails, opens can be automatically triggered, causing an inflated open rate. The root cause of these results is industry-wide changes in email privacy tools and isn’t specific to Ashby email sequences.
To combat this impacting your reporting on sequences, we recommend focusing on click and conversion metrics. Bots rarely impact click metrics and conversions always require human action.
If you are seeing inflated click rates, this could be due to corporate scanners like Mimecast and Barracuda that automatically click links to check for malware.
Below you can find a list of definitions for common terms around sequence metrics for your reference:
  * **Open Rate:** Percentage of recipients who open a specific email out of the total number of recipients.


  * **Click Rate** : Refers to the measure of how many people clicked on a hyperlink, call-to-action, or image within the email’s content out of all the recipients. In other words, the unique individuals who clicked, divided by the total number of recipients. This is commonly a way to measure engagement and interest.


  * **Conversions:** Counts the number of recipients who complete a goal action after reading the email. In Ashby, this would translate to a candidate replying to the email.


If you have further questions about inflated sequence metrics, please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com.

# Migrations
3 min
Copy
Ashby supports three types of migration﻿: API migration, file-based migration and self-serve bulk import. 
API Migration
File-based migration
Self-serve bulk import
This route directly integrates with the Legacy ATS. Once an API Key is input, the migration will complete automatically within 2-3 days, depending on the amount of data needing to be migrated. This option is currently only supported for all Greenhouse and Workday, along with some Lever migrations.
This option requires retrieving exported data from the legacy ATS and providing that information to Ashby to manually import. This option is currently supported for a limited number of migrations.
This route uses the [bulk import tool](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options)﻿ . It only supports importing candidate data in a CSV file which generally means it is not a comprehensive view of the candidates' lifecycle with a team. We can also import folders of resume files in bulk - please contact your CSM or reach out to Support for assistance with this. This option is supported for all migrations. If you do not see your legacy ATS listed below, this is likely the route you would need to take to import candidate data.

| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| ﻿[Lever](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-ashby-recruiting-api-migration-guide)﻿  | 🟡  | 🟢  | 🟢  |  
| **Workday**  | 🟢  | 🔴  | 🟢  |  
| ﻿[Workable](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/historical-import-migrations)﻿  | 🔴  | 🟢  | 🟢  |

 | 🔴  | 🟡  | 🟢  |

# Advanced Scheduling Automation Add-On
34 min
Copy
The Advanced Scheduling Automation add-on extends Ashby's scheduling capabilities with a suite of tools designed to reduce manual coordination work and give your team more visibility into the scheduling process. This article outlines the key features included in the add-on — including Interviewer Training, Auto Schedule, Scheduling Requests, the Interviews Dashboard, and Hold Scheduling — and describes feature setup and usage in detail.
This add-on is available on the All-in-One Recruiting Plus Plan as a purchasable add-on and is included in the Enterprise plan. Those on the Foundations Plan must upgrade to the Plus Plan to purchase it.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ➕  | ✅  |  
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#interviewer-training)Interviewer training
The Interviewer Training feature automatically tracks shadows and reverse shadows as interviewers progress through a structured training program, moving them into the qualified pool once they meet the requirements. This removes the need to manually manage who is ready to interview and ensures your interview panels stay consistent as your team grows.
For full setup instructions, including how to configure training stages, add trainees, and manage progression, see [Interviewer Training](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewer-training)﻿.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#auto-schedule)Auto schedule
The Auto Schedule feature automatically generates schedules based on your team's calendars. Schedulers can select from a short list of options, then finish out the scheduling process.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#setup-requirements)Setup requirements
  * Ensure you have interviewers set up on schedule interview activities. Their calendars are used to find suitable times when providing proposals. You can read more on configuring interviewers in [Interviewers: Assigning, Rescheduling, and Setting Interviewer Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits)﻿.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/76a41d2a4548ea4aa5dead4a316c782d.png)
﻿
  * If there are multiple events under a schedule interview activity and they should happen in a certain order, ensure you have adjusted the **Timing** configuration for each event. Timing settings are only available with the Advanced Scheduling Automation add-on or for Enterprise customers.


Understand timing settings in [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#usage)Usage
  1. On the schedule interview prompt on the candidate's profile, click Schedule, then Auto-Schedule.


  1. Confirm the interview activity settings as a first step. You can opt to unlink the interview from the interview activity to make any last-minute changes.


  1. Select the dates you're considering for the interview, confirm the primary timezone, and determine whether interviews with multiple events can be scheduled over multiple days, or allow breaks between interviews for flexibility.


  1. Once these details are confirmed, you are presented with proposed times. Select a time from the list. You can then view the panel and configure your invites prior to scheduling the interview.


If interviewers have a lot of availability across the interview dates specified, all possible time slots may not be listed in the **Proposed Schedules** tab.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#scheduling-requests-and-interviews-dashboard)Scheduling requests and Interviews Dashboard
Scheduling Requests are simple forms directly in Ashby that your team can use to create scheduling tasks for Recruiting Coordinators. Once submitted, interviews with requests can be viewed in the Interviews Dashboard.
The Interviews Dashboard is a tool that anyone can access to manage interview scheduling, check on the scheduling status of interviews, and take action on Scheduling Requests. The scheduling status of those interviews and requests updates in real time.
The Interviews Dashboard is where scheduling requests and interviews can be tracked and actioned. For the interview core dashboard containing key interview metrics and data, see [Core Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards)﻿.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#setup-requirements-1)Setup requirements
  1. Enable scheduling requests by navigating to **Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > Scheduling Requests & Queues**.


  1. Switch on the Enable scheduling requests? option to allow scheduling requests to be created on Interview Schedule Activities on candidate profiles.


  1. Create scheduling request queues. See the [Scheduling request queues](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#scheduling-request-queues)﻿ section below for detailed instructions.


  1. (Optional) Create scheduling request forms. See the [Scheduling request forms](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#scheduling-request-forms-optional)﻿ section below for detailed instructions.


  1. (Optional) Create additional priority levels. See the [Create additional priority levels](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#create-additional-priority-levels-optional)﻿ section below for detailed instructions.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#scheduling-request-queues)Scheduling request queues
Scheduling Request Queues enable your team to assign Scheduling Requests to a queue instead of directly to a specific Recruiting Coordinator. These can be helpful when you have multiple coordinators responsible for scheduling within a certain part of the business, or across your whole company.
Each queue can have multiple members. When a Scheduling Request is assigned to a queue, members of that queue see those requests in the Interviews Dashboard and can claim them based on their bandwidth. Recruiting Coordinators can be members of more than one queue, and membership is easy to adjust — for example, if you need someone to cover a queue while another member is out of office.
To create queues for scheduling requests:
  1. Click + New on the **Scheduling Request Queues** tab.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/eda545c1e460ed0d90628725404e6ea6.png)
﻿
  1. Name your queue.


  1. Add members to your queue. These are users who will be prompted to handle the scheduling requests assigned to this queue.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e59a8424154224bcc9cc0cde9cf13ded.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#scheduling-request-forms-optional)Scheduling request forms (optional)
If you'd like to require a request form, switch on Require request forms for scheduling requests?
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4372f35dd60b26b108c20db126855489.png)
﻿
To create and edit existing forms, navigate to **Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > Scheduling Requests**:
  1. Click + New on the **Scheduling Request Forms** tab.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5daa4410050f63b25c7ecb42caafc740.png)
﻿
  1. Name your scheduling request form.


  1. Add fields to your form by clicking Add Question. If you'd like to split a longer form into sections, click Add Section.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1bcd435e44c6d5cd13151aa31608173b.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#create-additional-priority-levels-optional)Create additional priority levels (optional)
Scheduling Requests can be classified with a priority level to ensure that the most important scheduling tasks are picked up first. Three priority levels are available by default, and these can also be customized. Priority labels and descriptions are visible in the Interviews Dashboard to easily distinguish between different types of requests.
To create additional priority levels to assign to scheduling requests, navigate to the **Priority** tab of **Scheduling Requests** :
  1. Click + New on the **Priority Levels** tab and name your new priority level.


  1. Add a description or details as needed.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/74855f56fa35e35f22984e8601f79180.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#add-the-interviews-dashboard-to-your-homepage)Add the Interviews Dashboard to your homepage
Once you've configured your scheduling request settings, you can prompt your team to start submitting scheduling requests. As part of this, we recommend that you and those involved with completing or tracking scheduling requests add the Interviews Dashboard to their home page.
To add the **Interviews Dashboard** to your homepage, locate the **Views** list on the left side of your homepage, click + Add, then select Interviews Dashboard. The Interviews Dashboard is where you can track any generated scheduling requests.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f5101b3d58e95d448ec78812802b4343.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#creating-a-scheduling-request)Creating a scheduling request
To create a Scheduling Request:
  1. Click the Create Scheduling Request button (or Schedule > Create Schedule Request) on the scheduling activity.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1404eb34ac48709f0986dd32ab9951af.png)
﻿
  1. Add the interview priority, the request assignee (or queue), and optionally select a request form and complete it prior to submitting the request.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a3f3579f9b1a92c88834679f235b96d7.png)
﻿
**Customizing schedule details**
  * If you need to view and amend any of the interview schedule details linked to the schedule request, click the **Schedule** tab.


This can be helpful for cases where necessary changes to the schedule for a specific candidate's interview are already known. Instead of writing out detailed instructions in your request for a Recruiting Coordinator to follow, you can make those adjustments immediately and save several steps for your team.
  * If you'd like to customize the schedule for a specific candidate, you can unlink it from the linked template pulled from the activity on your job's interview plan. To do this, click Customize Schedule.


Note that this unlinks the schedule from the template on your job's interview plan.
From here, you'll be using a custom template for this activity, so changes to the template on the interview plan no longer impact this specific interview activity. You can make changes to the interview schedule (for example, add, remove, or rearrange interview events), change interview length, adjust interviewer slots, update meeting rooms, and toggle whether to include a debrief.
  * If you need to revert any changes and go back to the original template provided by the interview schedule activity on your interview plan, click Switch to Linked Master Template.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c6b75970b1a6c18a4bfc600f853cc518.png)
﻿
  * Once you've reviewed your schedule and schedule request details, click Submit to submit your request. Any changes to your schedule save automatically, whether you choose to submit the request or not. This means that the assignee picking up the request works on the latest version of this schedule activity and doesn't need to make any amendments themselves — they can just open the request and schedule it with the needed amendments already made.


  * If you need to make changes to the request, click View Scheduling Request, then click Edit Request. Make any changes, then click Submit Request again to confirm your changes.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/22bdaa0ee1f3641444edcd56e04aab12.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#viewing-scheduling-requests)Viewing scheduling requests
You can view scheduling requests and the status of other interview activities via the Interviews Dashboard on your home page.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/934d211c5278f1733ab0c5134931307e.png)
﻿
  * To add additional filters, click the Filter button to select fields to filter by. Once you've configured your filters, click Save as New to save your filter so you can return to it easily in the future.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/04bf0093c974974f140566fad51f27b1.png)
﻿
  * If a scheduling request has been sent to a queue, you can claim the request to assign it to yourself to action. To do this, click Claim in the **Request Details** column.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/143649277c148ec0a340ecb2e8278b97.png)
﻿
  * If you need to make any adjustments to the scheduling request, hover over the information in the **Request Details** column. You can then click Edit on the Scheduling Request pop-up to make any edits without leaving this page.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/39d3a29a6120d3dbe740336ce96bebfb.png)
﻿
  * Requests can also be viewed while scheduling for easy reference.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a86189944f1790c9a8105cefb76d24cd.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#interview-schedule-activity-status)Interview Schedule Activity status
Interview Schedule Activities have a status assigned to them within the **Status** column of the Interviews Dashboard. The status of an activity updates automatically as it progresses through the scheduling process. You can read more about each status below:  
| Status  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Needs Scheduling/Needs to be Scheduled  | A scheduling request has not been generated for this interview and it is yet to be scheduled  |  
| Scheduling Requested  | A scheduling request has been submitted for this interview  |  
| Waiting on Candidate Booking  | A direct booking link has been sent out to the candidate but they have yet to schedule  |  
| Waiting on Candidate Availability  | An availability link has been sent out to the candidate but they have yet to submit their availability for an interview  |  
| Candidate Availability Submitted  | The candidate has provided their availability via a candidate availability link and is waiting to be scheduled  |  
| Scheduled  | The interview has been scheduled (this status shows regardless of whether calendar invites have been sent or not at the point of scheduling)  |  
| Waiting on Feedback  | The date and time of the interview has passed and interviewer feedback is yet to be completed  |  
| On Hold  | The interview date and time has been put on hold, rather than scheduled  |  
| Complete  | The interview date and time has passed and feedback has been completed or skipped  |  
Looking for information on statuses that display on the candidate profile? See [Understanding Candidate Activity Statuses](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-candidate-activity-statuses)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#scheduling-request-status)Scheduling Request status
Scheduling Requests also have an associated status, which can be seen in the Interviews Dashboard.  
| Status  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Unsubmitted  | A scheduling request draft was started but has not yet been submitted.  |  
| Unassigned  | The scheduling request has been submitted and added to a queue, but has not yet been assigned to a specific Recruiting Coordinator  |  
| Assigned  | The scheduling request has been directly assigned to a Recruiting Coordinator, or claimed by one off a queue  |  
| Completed  | The interview related to this Scheduling Request has been scheduled, marking the request as completed.  |  
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#auto-schedule-automations)Auto schedule automations
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#auto-replace-interviewer-on-decline)Auto-replace interviewer on decline
When an interviewer declines an interview before the configured cutoff time (24 hours by default), they are automatically swapped for an available alternate interviewer in the schedule and the invites are resent.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#setup-steps)Setup steps
  1. Navigate to the job, then the Interview Plan tab.


  1. On the relevant schedule interview activity in the job's interview plan, click Modify > Edit.


  1. Add interviewers and make sure alternates are available. You can do this by using an interviewer pool or adding multiple interviewers to a slot. This allows Ashby to replace the original interviewer automatically if they decline.


  1. Go to the Automation tab within the same schedule interview activity.


  1. Toggle the Automatically Replace Interviewer on Decline? setting on.


  1. Use the default cutoff of 24 hours, or set a custom cutoff duration that is shorter or longer.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/rQASFFNSWDVDBDSizsgZ__director-of-platform-engineering-acent-ashby-2026-05-20-at-22539-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#automatically-scheduling-upon-receiving-candidate)Automatically scheduling upon receiving candidate availability
Automation can be put in place that automatically emails you with interview proposal times when a candidate submits their availability. You can then select a proposed time and schedule the candidate from there.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#setup-steps-1)Setup steps
Before using this automation, open the relevant schedule interview activity on your interview plan and go to the Automation tab.
  1. Turn on Generate suggested schedules when availability is submitted? This tells Ashby to generate proposed interview times automatically after the candidate submits their availability.


  1. Make sure the job has a **Hiring Team** assigned. When the candidate submits their availability, Ashby sends the suggested schedules to users on the hiring team so they can review the options and complete scheduling.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f4de0c0cbc9c9c9518460b3828450aad.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#usage-1)Usage
From the candidate's profile, open the interview activity and select **Schedule** > **Request Candidate Availability**.
Once the candidate submits their availability, Ashby generates suggested schedules and notifies the hiring team so they can choose a time and finish scheduling the interview.
To learn more about candidate availability links, see [Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#hold-scheduling)Hold scheduling
The Hold feature enables you to place [HOLD] calendar events on interview calendars. This does not email or send invites to the candidate. The option to add a hold event is available when either manually scheduling or using auto-schedule options to book an interview. The option is available at the **Calendar Invites** stage of scheduling.
﻿
When used, you have the following options:
  * **Hold Silently:** Events are placed on interviewers' calendars, but no notification is sent for the initial invite or for subsequent updates while the interview schedule is held.


  * **Hold & Notify:** Events are placed on interviewers' calendars, and they are notified for the initial invitation. They are not updated for subsequent updates while the interview schedule is held.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/82181fe636e55c928340450acf3e302d.png)
﻿
The Hold option is not available when rescheduling an interview.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#usage-2)Usage
  * Click the Hold button in the scheduling workflow for either auto-schedule or manual scheduling.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#do-schedule-requests-show-as-a-task-on-the-users-h)Do schedule requests show as a task on the user's Home page?
Not currently, but they can be accessed from the Interviews Dashboard.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#if-an-interviewer-has-been-scheduled-in-one-interv)If an interviewer has been scheduled in one interviewer slot, can they also be auto-scheduled in place of an interviewer on the same interview (i.e. take up multiple slots across one or multiple events within an interview)?
Interviewers who have already been selected for other interview slots are excluded as possible auto-replacements. If no other interviewers are available, an auto-replacement will not take place.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on#what-fields-are-available-for-reporting-on-schedul)What fields are available for reporting on schedule requests?
For scheduling requests, you have the following fields for reporting.
Subject: Job Consideration > Interview Schedule  
| **Has Scheduling Request?**  | Whether a scheduling request has been submitted for this interview schedule.  |  
| --- | --- |  
Subject: Job Consideration > Interview Schedule > Request  
| **Requested By**  | The person who submitted the scheduling request  |  
| --- | --- |  
| **Assignee**  | The person who has been assigned the request  |  
| **Submitted At**  | The point in time at which the request was created.  |  
| **Priority**  | The priority assigned to the scheduling request  |  
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Analytics: Best Practices
# Reporting on Candidates vs Applications
4 min
Copy
This document summarizes the case for reporting on applications as a subject rather than candidates. Applications contain the meaningful features, such as job, department, and interview details that often drive the metrics of interest. As a single candidate can have many applications, our recommendation is to report metrics based on applications.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-candidates-vs-applications#one-candidate-many-applications)One Candidate, Many Applications
![candidtes to applications.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/zn7YKpDzxqM6p83VOzkHc_candidtestoapplications.png)
﻿
In the recruiting process and reporting context we naturally speak of and refer to candidates. That's a natural inclination as we're often discussion the person under the resume. However, given a single candidate can have multiple applications, the details about the individual application start to matter when it comes to reporting. This is sometimes called a one-to-many relationship: one candidate, many applications.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-candidates-vs-applications#reporting-features-live-on-the-application)Reporting Features Live on the Application
The most common features of reporting are, in almost all cases, derived from the candidate's Application. The application contains such details as:
  * The specific Job the candidate is considered for


  * The department and team


  * The start date of the application


  * The recruiter and hiring manager


  * The application timeline: start date, stage progression and so on


  * The interview details specific to the job


This isn't a comprehensive list, but the key take away is that the above features are unique to the application, and therefore can be different for each application even if the underlying candidate is the same person.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-candidates-vs-applications#accounting-for-your-recruiting-investments)Accounting for Your Recruiting Investments
If a single candidate has two different job applications, each of which go through an interview cycle and ultimately result in a hire (or not) for one job, you want your reporting to capture the entirety of your team's invested efforts. If two applications from the same candidate got a the full treatment of panel interviews and scheduling you'll capture that by reporting at the application level.
![Frame 9.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/UKloFAZu3XRX5yAdXi2d__frame9.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-candidates-vs-applications#when-should-i-report-on-candidates)When Should I Report on Candidates?
There are times where counting the underlying candidates may make sense, and Ashby permits you to do that level of reporting if needed. However, in our experience, it is often quite rare that such reports are informative or actionable. A good heuristic is if you find yourself wanting to incorporate any of the reporting features summarized above (e.g. department, or interview stage), you should be reporting on Applications.

# Pin Reports to User Home Pages
2 min
Copy
Ensure Ashby users see the reporting content they need to by pinning custom reports and dashboards to their home pages.
Looking for how to navigate and pin a report to your own home page? Check out [Home Page](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/home-page#overview)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pin-reports-to-user-home-pages#home-page-configuration)Home Page Configuration
﻿
New users start with no reports or dashboards in their home page by default. Admin users have the option of pinning a custom selection of any report or dashboard to each user's home page to ensure they see the required reporting when they log in to Ashby.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2db1df5babf40e48513eb8f3427caf7b.png)
﻿
_How to configure an employee's home page_
  1. Click the Admin section of your Ashby instance


  1. In the Employees section, click the employee's name to open their profile


  1. Select the Home Page tab to view their current home page configuration


  1. Optionally add, remove, or rearrange the home page content. You will have the option to add a report, a dashboard or an interview dashboard to the employee’s home page.

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews & Scheduling FAQs
# What is a grouped interview plan?
9 min
Copy
If you are using multiple different interview plans, you can use this feature to bucket your interview stages across plans into a common set of stages for reporting.
Grouped interview plans are one of the most powerful ways to bring consistency, clarity, and scalability to your hiring process. When used well, they create a standard backbone for interviews across teams and improve data quality, reduce manual work, enable better forecasting, and help teams to deliver a more predictable candidate experience.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#grouped-interview-plan-overview)Grouped interview plan overview
If you are using multiple different interview plans, you can set up a grouped interview plan to bucket your interview stages into a common set of stages for reporting.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#why-should-i-set-up-a-grouped-interview-plan)Why should I set up a grouped interview plan?
A strong Grouped Interview Plan helps with creating the following:
  * A standardized framework that reduces one-off customization


  * Clean, comparable data through consistent stage and activity mapping


  * Scalable interview structures that don’t require ongoing adjustments


  * Structured data capture that powers accurate, repeatable reporting


  * A repeatable setup process that supports hiring growth without redesigning interview flows


Because the grouped interview plan defines your company’s reporting structure, stability at this layer is essential. Grouped interview plans are built for long-term consistency, not continuous adjustment.
The grouped interview plan also plays a critical role in how you configure conditions across Ashby. Features like [Candidate Experience Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-experience-surveys)﻿, [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿, and other automated workflows rely on stage groupsfields to trigger consistently at the right moment in the process. Without a grouped interview plan, you would need to align conditions across multiple stage names, creating unnecessary complexity and increasing the chances of misconfiguration. With a grouped interview plan, these triggers become seamless and reliable.
The Grouped Interview Plan sits at the top of a hierarchy that other pieces map into:
  * The **Grouped Interview Plan** defines the stage groups.


  * ﻿[Interview Plans](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans)﻿ map their stages into those stage groups.


  * ﻿[Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities)﻿ sit on stages inside Interview Plans and power automation.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#how-to-configure-a-grouped-interview-plan)How to configure a grouped interview plan
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#adding-stages-to-your-grouped-interview-plan)Adding stages to your grouped interview plan
To add stages to your grouped interview plan:
  * Navigate to Admin, then select Data Management from the left sidebar, then [Grouped Interview Plan](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/data-mapping/grouped-interview-plan "Grouped Interview Plan")﻿


  * Check the Enable Group Interview Plan? checkbox.


  * To add the high-level interview plan stages to the Lead, Active and Completed sections, scroll to the section in question, type the new stage name into the field to the left of the + Add button, then click + Add.


![Adding a new stage to the grouped interview plan](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/19ZOb4bvX0rPoKUA4qn1r_grouped-interview-plan-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-25721-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Any changes you make will be saved automatically.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#deleting-stages-from-your-grouped-interview-plan)Deleting stages from your grouped interview plan
To delete a stage from your grouped interview plan, click on the trash can icon to the right of the stage in question.
![Click the trash can icon to remove a stage group](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/MiJWdQ50jr1yylwc3JQqJ_grouped-interview-plan-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-34138-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If the stage you're trying to delete is mapped to your interview plans, you will be given a choice: either you can remove the grouped stage group mapping from those interview plan stages or select a different grouped stage to map your interview plan stage to. 
![Checking the group stages checkbox](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/CCDH4epuEmKu1HHZeG6Fz_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-30959-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#map-plan-stages-to-grouped-interview-plan)Map plan stages to grouped interview plan
Once your grouped plan is configured, you can map the stages within each of your interview plans to the corresponding stages in your grouped plan. To map stages:
  * Navigate to Admin, then Interviews & Scheduling in the left-hand sidebar, then Interview Plans.


  * Select an interview plan to map


  * Use the Stage Group drop-down menus to select the grouped interview plan stage you'd like to map your interview plan stage to.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/0dVvnMX7iAi5V3eXv0QGx_interview-plans-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-30256-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#grouping-stages-when-reporting)Grouping Stages when reporting
When creating a report relating to your interview pipeline, you will see the checkbox option to Group Stages?
Checking this box will use your grouped interview plan stages, bucketing your individual interview plans and showing a streamline version of the report. If you are using the new report builder, this is in the **Vizualization** section of the report builder.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/CCDH4epuEmKu1HHZeG6Fz_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-30959-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#how-many-stages-should-my-grouped-interview-plan-h)H**ow many stages should my Grouped Interview Plan have?**
Most Grouped Interview Plans work best with 3–6 active stage groups. These stage groups should represent the major milestones you want to measure consistently across roles. Essentially, if you need to report on passthrough or performance for a step in the process, it likely deserves its own stage group in your grouped interview plan.
The grouped interview plan should only include high-level milestones. Do not include hold stages or administrative steps. Those belong in interview plans and can still be reported on by stage name if needed.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan#ive-created-my-grouped-interview-plan-and-now-when)I**'ve created my Grouped Interview Plan and now when I pull reports, I am getting a lot of "unspecified" results or data is missing entirely. Why?**
Firstly, confirm that all of your interview plan stages are mapped to the grouped interview plan. Any interview plan (shared or custom) that isn’t mapped will appear as “unspecified” in reporting because Ashby doesn’t know which stage group those stages belong to.
You can review and update this in Admin → Interview Plans (available to Organizational Admins or Elevated Users with permission to edit Interview Plans). Make sure each plan is mapped to the correct stage groups in the grouped interview plan so all reporting can roll up cleanly. If the Stage Group says "Unmapped," it needs to be mapped to the appropriate Grouped Interview Stage. 
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Emails & Candidate Texting
Emails
# Substitution Tokens
33 min
Copy
You can add substitution tokens to your email templates to pull information from the candidate profile, upcoming interview details and more when generating an email draft. This guide is a list of the substitution tokens available. For more information on configuring your templates, check out [Email Template Creation and Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management)﻿.
For substitution tokens that generate email content using AI, check out [the AI Tokens section of this guide.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#ai-tokens)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#candidate-information)Candidate Information
Candidate’s First Name: The first name of the candidate, as listed on their candidate profile in Ashby.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/33d6ffe6efaf4027bde7369c2b733ca0.png)
﻿
﻿  

Candidate’s Full Name: The candidate’s full name, as listed on their candidate profile in Ashby.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fadbef98976a581e5561f1628246dee8.png)
﻿
﻿  

Candidate’s Company: The most recent company the candidate has worked at, pulled from the Experience section of the candidate profile.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2241c026839661c52f75191b590b7ac7.png)
﻿
﻿  

Candidate’s School: The most recent school the candidate attended. This information is pulled from the Education section of the candidate profile.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/249d9cc31dfbce3071e770eac3d020d4.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

Candidate’s Current Title: The candidate’s most recent job title, pulled from the Experience section of the candidate’s profile.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/496aabd93eca59c5dbcd0e28949ce7f2.png)
﻿
﻿  

Candidate’s Phone Number: The candidate’s phone number, pulled from their candidate profile in Ashby.
Candidate’s LinkedIn Profile URL: The candidate’s LinkedIn profile link, pulled from their candidate profile in Ashby.
Candidate’s Email Address (Primary, Personal): The candidate’s email address, pulled from the Email (Personal) field on their candidate profile.
Candidate’s Candidate Profile Link: The link to the candidate’s profile in Ashby (as if it was copied directly from the address bar when viewing their profile). This can direct interviewers directly to the candidate’s profile in Ashby if they have permission to view candidate profiles.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/da1d7b9a55e04db544fd98a7eb1fa5f4.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#sender-and-organization-information)Sender and Organization Information
Sender’s First Name: The first name of the user the email is being sent from. If you are using another user’s email address (more on this at [Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿), then they are seen as the sender. The sender’s first name is pulled from the name field in their Personal Settings.
Sender’s Last Name: The last name of the user the email is being sent from. If you are using another user’s email address (more on this at [Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿), then they are seen as the sender. The sender’s last name is pulled from the name field in their Personal Settings.
Sender’s Full Name: The full name of the user the email is being sent from. If you are using another user’s email address (more on this at [Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿), then they are seen as the sender. The sender’s full name is pulled from the name field in their Personal Settings.
Sender’s Job Title: The job title of the user the email is being sent from. This is pulled from the Job Title field in the user’s Personal Settings. If you are using another user’s email address (more on this at [Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿), then they are seen as the sender.
Organization’s Name: The Organization name, pulled from the Organization Name field on the [Theme (Job Board) page](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/theme "Theme \(Job Board\) page").
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d52b709ed808a1e3718bd6485c354f70.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#job-consideration-and-hiring-team-information)Job Consideration and Hiring Team Information
When pulling Hiring Manager, Recruiter, Recruiting Coordinator or Sourcer information, those roles are determined by the roles outline in the Hiring Team on the candidate’s job consideration.
Please note that the job title, team, team hierarchy and posting title tokens can’t be added to sequences currently.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/22f64ea0295a638e65b7112ffab04403.png)
﻿
Job Considerations’s Hiring Manager’s First Name: The first name of the hiring manager listed in the candidate’s job consideration. The name is pulled from the Hiring Manager’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Hiring Manager’s Name: The first name of the hiring manager listed in the candidate’s job consideration in the Hiring Team section. The name is pulled from the Hiring Manager’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Recruiter’s First Name: The first name of the recruiter listed in the candidate’s job consideration in the Hiring Team section. The name is pulled from the Recruiter’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Recruiter’s Name: The name of the recruiter listed in the candidate’s job consideration in the Hiring Team section. The name is pulled from the Recruiter’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Recruiting Coordinators’s First Name: The first name of the recruiting coordinator listed in the candidate’s job consideration in the Hiring Team section. The name is pulled from the Recruiting Coordinator’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Recruiting Coordinators’s Name: The name of the recruiting coordinator listed in the candidate’s job consideration in the Hiring Team section. The name is pulled from the Recruiting Coordinator’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Sourcer’s First Name: The first name of the sourcer listed in the candidate’s job consideration in the Hiring Team section. The name is pulled from the Sourcer’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Sourcer’s Name: The name of the sourcer listed in the candidate’s job consideration in the Hiring Team section. The name is pulled from the Sourcer’s name field within their Personal Settings.
Job Considerations’s Title: The title of the job the candidate is being considered for. The job title here is taken from the title listed internally on the role in Ashby.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e4456b5f3c76cdba0bae96e3091da509.png)
﻿
﻿  

Job Considerations’s Team: The department or sub-department assigned to the job the candidate is being considered for. For more on setting up departments, check out [Set up Departments & Locations](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations)﻿.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ead0af3b2d3b743652900970816faf5e.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

Job Considerations’s Team Hierarchy: The complete team hierarchy for the role the candidate is being considered for (so both the team and sub-team are included). For example Engineering|Product Engineering|Backend Engineer.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e449dad205f7d1b3f007a10aeb16f413.png)
﻿
﻿  

Job Considerations’s Posting Title: The job title listed in the posting for the job the candidate applied for. If the candidate did not apply for the role, the title on the job board posting for the role, or the job title as listed on the Jobs page in Ashby will be shown instead (in that order).
Job Consideration’s Posting Description: The description of the external job posting the candidate applied to. If the candidate didn’t apply for the role via a job posting, then the description from the default job posting for the job is listed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/83fea3022a41fb91a95cfc17995fc414.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e80c9b7110e1aa13eef3a201e1c4316a.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

Job Consideration's Application Form Submission: Injects a candidate's application form answers into the email body as rich text. Thetoken includes all standard application question types (short text, long text, single/multi-select, dates and URLs). With file uploads, only the file name is listed, the substitution token does not include a link to the file or attach a copy of the file to the email. If the candidate has left any fields blank, these display as "—" in the email. If there is no submission available, the token renders empty but the email can still be sent.
Private fields are always excluded from appearing when using this substitution token. Private fields are never sent out to candidates via this token regardless of the sender permissions.
This token behaves differently depending on the context the email is being sent in:
  * If you are looking at a specific job consideration for the candidate when drafting the email, the token renders the application's latest submission for that particular job consideration.


  * Without the specific job consideration (e.g, you're just looking at the candidate profile without a job consideration selected): it renders the candidate's most recent application, regardless of status (including applications that have been archived).


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3LRSoojNhxx7N3oUlYlOm_ariadne-shepard-marketeer-acent-ashby-2026-02-23-at-15423-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Job Consideration's Street Address: The street address assigned to the location linked to the job the job consideration is for.
Job Consideration's Postal Code: The postal code assigned to the location linked to the job the job consideration is for.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#interview-booking-links-and-information)Interview Booking Links and Information
Direct Booking Link: Include the booking link generated by an interview activity. The candidate can then view interviewer availability and select a suitable time for their interview. More about direct booking at [Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/19f39769fc55c5fa8123b0443cd7ad28.png)
﻿
﻿  

Candidate Availability Link: Include the availability link generated by an interview activity. The candidate can then add in their availability and submit it so an interview can be scheduled manually. More about candidate availability links can be found at [Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/71a56b1a8932984653003cab0c2c2acd.png)
﻿
﻿  

Take Home Submission Link: A link that allows a candidate to submit their take-home submission. More on this at [Take-home Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/take-home-activities)﻿.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7d1ec6fa852ef52626a18296194d611c.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview Panel Summary: A summary of the scheduled interview panel, showing interviewer names and the interview start time. The interview must already be scheduled for this information to be pulled into the email.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/77b3cd636cf0d943af1cba39f23012a4.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview Panel Summary (for Upcoming Interviews in Interview Reminders): A summary of the scheduled interview panel for interviews on the same day the email is sent. If you have interviews scheduled as part of the panel on other days, they will not be included in the email.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/653d95fc2c8636b89b6c72743b7d0066.png)
﻿
﻿  

Calendar Invite Location (Video Conferencing Link, Phone Number or Custom): Include the location listed for the interview in the calendar invite. This can be a Google Meet or Zoom link, a phone number, or a custom value. This is set within the Location field of the interview details.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/01b352c3c43681d771cb85a93e55aac6.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

Interview Meeting Room: If a meeting room is scheduled for this interview, list this in the email.
Interview(s) Total Time: The total time of all interviews, listed in hours and minutes (for example 1hr 45min).
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7764083bda85f30d5cb4236b62b05c32.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview Title: The title of the individual interview scheduled in at the time of sending.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/489a178e1660f4cc7bc71dedde392a72.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview Start Date (First Date): The start date of the interview. If multiple interviews are scheduled, only the date of the first one will be listed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/eb392c253ee0b476ef65e1ee61ba40b4.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview Start Date (All Dates): The start date of the interviews in the panel. If multiple interviews are scheduled, all dates will be listed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cafb39eca507c3cb7242c7fcd8538a61.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview End Date: The end date of the interview. If multiple interviews are scheduled, then the end date of the last interview of the panel will be listed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f4b5f69178f118ffebf337593e57af98.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview Start Time: The start time of the interview. If multiple interviews are scheduled, the start time of the first interview will be listed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7d8281db756dae12e5a7fbedcd8a22f9.png)
﻿
﻿  

Interview End Time: The end time of the interview. If multiple interviews are scheduled, the end time of the last interview will be listed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/393f64f8aa7504b817482bfc2366ded0.png)
﻿
﻿  

First Scheduled Interviewer’s First Name: The first name of the first interviewer scheduled on the panel. This is designed for 1:1 interviews. If the interview panel has multiple interviewers, the first interviewer’s first name will be shown.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6f2cddbcd1da3ca5f2b8fbddf465d2db.png)
﻿
﻿  

First Scheduled Interviewer’s Name: The full name of the first interviewer scheduled on the panel. This is designed for 1:1 interviews. If the interview panel has multiple interviewers, the first interviewer’s full name will be shown.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/738f7cd30c8e6a3e6b434bd8d258a774.png)
﻿
﻿  

First Scheduled Interviewer’s Job Title: The job title of the first interviewer scheduled on the panel. This is designed for 1:1 interviews. If the interview panel has multiple interviewers, the first interviewer’s job title will be shown.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0a3975395ad15505c7b99ff579b2bead.png)
﻿
﻿  

First Scheduled Interviewer (Including Name, Job Title, Social Links): The first interviewer’s name, job title and social links (taken from their Personal Settings), will be displayed. This is designed for 1:1 interviews and only the first interviewer’s details will be shown if multiple interviewers are on the panel.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5e61612e9a96c213197a26a91607181d.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#other-useful-dates)Other Useful Dates
Employment Start Date: The start date listed in the most recent version of the offer extended. If the candidate has already accepted an offer, the start date listed in that offer will be shown.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4f4386ef5d8a7d6b0c3d330532413eef.png)
﻿
﻿  

Day of Week: The day of the week on which the email is sent to the candidate. This is based on your timezone.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f443516ab4b8e0387ca83e41411963a2.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#surveys-and-questionnaires)Surveys and Questionnaires
With each of the options below, once a survey request has been created, the substitution token will be replaced with a link to the survey or questionnaire requested.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d585f6fb48399000a694d951815cb570.png)
﻿
﻿  

Diversity Survey Link: A link to complete a diversity survey will be included in the email. More on setting up and automating diversity survey distribution at [Diversity Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/diversity-surveys)﻿.
EEOC Survey Link: A link to complete an EEOC survey will be included in the email. More on EEOC surveys at [EEOC Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys)﻿.
Candidate Experience Survey Link: Include a candidate survey link in your email. More on configuring candidate experience surveys at [Candidate Experience Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-experience-surveys)﻿.
Data Consent Request Link: Include a link for the candidate to provide consent via a consent form. More on this at [Data Privacy and Compliance](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#F_h7n)﻿﻿
Questionnaire Link: Add a link to a questionnaire for candidates to complete. More on this at [Questionnaires](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/questionnaires)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#events-information)Events Information
The below substitution tokens pulled details from an event you’ve created. You can add these tokens to event invitation or event confirmation email templates. For more on events, check out [Events](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/events)﻿.
Hiring Event Name: The name of the hiring event.
Hiring Event Location: The location set in the Location field of your event
Hiring Event Location Description: The location information set in the Location Description field of your event
Hiring Event Location Type: The location type as set in the Location Type field of your event
Hiring Event Date: The date of your hiring event
Hiring Event Time: The timezone, start time and end time of the hiring event (if set)
Hiring Event Start Time: The start time of the hiring event
Hiring Event End Time: The end time of the hiring event
Hiring Event Timezone: The timezone set in the hiring event details
Hiring Event Audience Member’s Time Slot: The time slot the audience member selected when registering for the event (if time slots are enabled for this event)
Hiring Event Audience Member’s Registration Form Submission: The audience member’s registration form submission
Hiring Event Audience Member’s RSVP Link: A link that the email recipient can use to update their RSVP status after they’ve registered. Any amendments to the RSVP status made here will update in Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#ai-tokens)AI Tokens
Please note that for more information on using AI tokens in sequences, please check out [AI Tokens in Outreach Emails](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-tokens-in-outreach-emails)﻿.
You can opt in to use AI-Generated Email Tokens by heading to Admin > Organization Setup > [Opt-In Features](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/early-access "Opt-In Features").
AI Generated Feedback Summary: Automatically create short, context-aware feedback summaries in non-sequence emails (like rejections, status updates, or next steps). This fits within the context of the surrounding email content, so works both for rejections and for feedback that you may want to give mid-interview loop.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e4c8979946c32b9c5281b2a1ab49f8de.png)
﻿
You'll see a preview of the generated feedback based on submitted scores and notes and will need to approve it by checking the check mark to add it to the email. The summary can be re-generated easily or deleted.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e0fa9b3619554bd33b4176422689a990.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#im-seeing-a-missing-value-for-message-when-pulling)I’m seeing a Missing Value For message when pulling up an email template. Why?
This error will show if the value cannot be found. This is particularly common when:
  * You’re trying to send a direct booking link but an interview activity hasn’t been generated and a booking link hasn’t been created for the candidate. You can read more on booking links at Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links 


  * You’re trying to send a candidate availability link but an interview activity hasn’t been generated and a candidate availability link hasn’t been created for the candidate. You can read more on candidate availability links at Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links 


  * If you’re trying to send out a survey and the survey request and link haven’t been generated yet.


  * If an interview has not been scheduled yet (panel information can only be pulled for interview emails if the interview has already been scheduled).


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/51dac655543e89e6bcb6c50c0518db50.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#can-i-set-a-custom-field-as-a-substitution-token)Can I set a custom field as a substitution token?
You can, yes! Please note that custom fields can only be configured on the following plans:
When creating your custom field, you can check the Enable in Email Templates box on the token’s settings. This token can now be used in email templates moving forward. Please note, this is only possible with candidate custom fields and with short answer field types.
If you’d like more information on configuring custom fields, check out [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿﻿
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/55a9cb9ad84eebd21aea3ba2132c2d60.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#where-does-the-timezone-in-the-interview-panel-sum)Where does the timezone in the Interview Panel Summary token come from?
The Interview Panel Summary token uses the Interview Schedule's Primary Interview Timezone to decide the timezone shown in the calendar invites. This can be set or amended within the Meeting Hours tab of your schedule interview activity.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d8863e0ac712bb9ee43afd05394e2d36.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#can-offer-tokens-be-used-like-substitution-tokens)Can offer tokens be used like substitution tokens in email templates?
﻿  

No, this isn’t currently possible.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#can-i-set-custom-text-for-a-url-substitution-link)Can I set custom text for a URL substitution link?
You can now set custom text for a URL pulled into an email via a substitution token (like a direct booking link or candidate availability link). At the point of drafting the email, select the email template using your URL substitution token. Click on the three dots to the right of the URL generated and added to your email, then Set Text to Display. You will then be able to type in your custom text and click Continue to update the display text accordingly.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2c5d7033580e5e8b89c6469d5bdf4321.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens#is-there-a-way-to-remove-formatting-from-substitut)Is there a way to remove formatting from substitution tokens?
Adding _RAW to the end of any **numeric** or **currency** substitution token will strip formatting from the field. For example, a large number MY_FIELD might be formatted as 1,000,000, but MY_FIELD_RAW would be rendered as 1000000. This can avoid problems when using Word Tables.
﻿
