[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Getting Started with Analytics
# Quality of Hire Reports
10 min
Copy
Check out [Quality of Hire Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-surveys)﻿ to get more information on setting up Quality of Hire forms and sequences.
Quality of Hire Surveys are available on the Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-reports#template-reports)Template Reports
We recommend you explore Quality of Hire reporting by starting with the template reports to help familiarize yourself with the available data. These templates can be found in the [Template Library](https://app.ashbyhq.com/reports/templates/metrics "Template Library") under Reports. Scroll to the Quality of Hire section.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f345650f7ec1f9dc1e81a4f4a456930f.png)
﻿
All quality of hire template reports analyze the numerical 1-5 value from the Quality of Hire Rating survey question.
To see the individual values submitted, you can use any of the Score Composition templates. If you would like to see averages, select any of the Average template reports.
We have the following template reports:
  * Quality of Hire Score Composition by Tenure: This report looks at Quality of Hire submissions from every individual step and counts the total submissions by their 1-5 value, grouped by the tenure of the employee in months at the time of submissions. The default is to select submissions made over the last 12 months.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/88c099fd4f0159fe9aeb488e4dd12edd.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Average Quality of Hire Score by Tenure: This report looks at Quality of Hire submissions from every individual step and forms an average, the tenure of the employee in months at the time of submissions.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/63d2e1357a0020aba5872afe25c8eab7.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Quality of Hire Score Composition Over Time: this report forms a time series and counts the number of survey submissions in each month, grouped by the individual score value. This shows the overall composition of Quality of Hire submissions over time. In the case of multiple Quality of Hire submissions existing for a given time group (e.g. month), the latest submission within that period is used per employee.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c80712fc16064222f3c05a62d49a0267.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Average Quality of Hire Composition over Time: this report forms a time series of the average Quality of hire submission per time group. By default, it looks at the last 12 months by month.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a50fe2b3edb0388180c801fdadd6346b.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Current Quality of Hire Score Composition: this report presents the most recent quality of hire assessment value per employee and groups the results by the score value. This forms a quality distribution (1 through 5) across your employee base. By default, this report looks only at enrollments that started in the last 12 months.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c1e5744dcaeb320e417cd042d9e6ac0b.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Average Current Quality of Hire: this report presents the average current quality of hire score across your employee base. If an employee had a historically low or high score, that will not be accounted for in this report: only the most recent assessment of each employee is considered.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5f6e06a81d66e555ca5f46fc16db097b.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Quality of Hire by Start Date Cohort: this report presents a time series of the current quality of hire scores per employee, as grouped by your employee start date. This will allow seeing how quality of hire has changed over time relative to your employee start dates, in addition to what volume of employees you are sampling.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3870fc0cc0085277c9953f4ca31844ce.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Average Quality of Hire by Start Date Cohort: this report presents a time series of the average current quality of hire score, as grouped by your employee start date. This will allow assessing overall average QoH by start date cohorts and their other properties.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ebb78b39ebb55976c8e423f0961a383e.png)
﻿
﻿  

  * Quality of Hire Completion Rates: this is a diagnostic report that allows tracking how many survey steps have been requested, how many have been completed, along with the completion rate (%) and average time to completion. Think of it as a scorecard completion rate equivalent, focused on the hiring managers responsible for completing QoH surveys.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6474ecf3a3d6bcf4a2d22142de123ecd.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-reports#custom-quality-of-hire-report-building)Custom Quality of Hire Report Building
Ashby enables reporting of values given at each step or at the overall enrollment by exposing two distinct reporting subjects: Quality of Hire Enrollments and Quality of Hire Survey Steps
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7e69269295780e448a23961acba5e490.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-reports#quality-of-hire-enrollments)Quality of Hire Enrollments
The Quality of Hire Enrollments reporting subject is focused on the overall enrollment, i.e. the sequence the hiring manager participants in.
The key fields relate to the Quality of Hire sequence status, which manager is enrolled, and the current quality of hire assessment of the employee.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0118ca69c57b726b133674204309fbba.png)
﻿
Ashby considers the assessment made at the most recent step to represent an employee’s quality of hire, as assessed by the manager.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-reports#quality-of-hire-survey-steps)Quality of Hire Survey Steps
The Quality of Hire Survey Steps reporting subject is focused on the data collected at each step of a Quality of Hire sequence.
The fields available for reporting allow access to the status, who submitted the survey, and other step-specific details.
This subject also permits analyzing historical QoH values to see the change over time for any individual employee.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0583acd2292fc95f002b6129ec8e384b.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-reports#using-the-quality-of-hire-reporting-subjects)Using the Quality of Hire Reporting Subjects
For more on custom reports involving Quality of Hire fields, check out [Custom Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports)﻿. The general paradigm is followed, wherein you can navigate to Reports > [Build](https://app.ashbyhq.com/reports/build "Build") to begin custom reporting building by way of picking a report type, such as a Count over Time report. From there you can select one of the Quality of Hire subjects and proceed to build a report as desired.
Note, if you are building general Quality of Hire reports for analysis, you may need to ensure you have proper permissions. More about permissions at [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)

Activity Configuration
# Conditional Activities
6 min
Copy
Conditional activities allow you to set criteria for activities on your interview plan. If candidates meet that criteria when moved to the stage the activity is in on your pipeline, the activity will be generated and added to their job consideration.
For more on configuring activities in general, check out the following guides: [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿, [Take-home Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/take-home-activities)﻿ and [Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/conditional-activities#conditional-activities)Conditional Activities
Conditional Activities can only be configured on the Enterprise plan.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
Now you have the option to create conditional activities, or add criteria to your existing activities. Only candidates who meet the criteria you set will have the activity generated on their job consideration when moved to a new stage in your pipeline.
So, one example could be that for a Backend Engineer role, a candidate could only be prompted to be scheduled for a technical quiz interview if their experience level has been determined as junior. This could have been determined in a previous interview and added as a [custom field](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿ to the candidate’s profile, or perhaps determined by the candidate themselves when filling out their [application form](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-forms)﻿.
﻿
To add a condition to your activity, navigate to the Interview Plan tab within your job, then either create a new activity using the Add activity to... option, or amend an existing one by clicking Modify, then Edit.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ab0ac5b73f4b19d7bffc0cc64a6bda1e.png)
﻿
To add your conditions, click on the Conditions tab. Click Enable condition ?, then Add Field to Match to select your filters and determine the criteria the candidate would need to meet for this activity to be generated for them when moved to this stage.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ea8e6333c354902621008af6b376aecb.png)
﻿
Once you’ve determined your criteria, click Save to confirm your criteria and activate your conditions. You can click Reset to remove any unsaved changes.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a3a6971dbf7d6b6bc3926b847c02fff0.png)
﻿
Moving forward, if a candidate is moved to the stage your conditional activity is in, then the activity will only be automatically generated and added to their job consideration if they meet your criteria.
If the candidate meets your criteria, the activity will be added to the Current Activities section of their job consideration.
![If the candidate meets your criteria, the activity will be added to the Current Activities section of their job consideration.](https://cdn.zappy.app/ce541995093c6ebcc66720e3bf7fe5cd.png)
﻿
If the candidate doesn’t meet the conditions set, then the activity will not be generated when they move to the specified stage.
![If the candidate doesn’t meet the conditions set, then the activity will not be generated when they move to the specified stage.](https://cdn.zappy.app/953f2db030e1720a287f8560700c3f1c.png)
﻿
Any schedule interview activities that are set as conditional will be marked with a Condition indicator on your Interview Plan page within your job for your reference.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3fbac728d4db49dd9944c535fb3d7356.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/conditional-activities#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/conditional-activities#can-i-create-conditional-interview-stages-within-m)C**an I create conditional interview stages within my interview plan?**
This currently isn’t possible.
﻿  

﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby AI Features
# Substitution Tokens: AI-Generated Feedback Summary
2 min
Copy
Add the AI Generated Feedback Summary token in application status, rejection or next-step email templates to provide a brief summary of feedback provided within the email sent to a candidate.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens-ai-generated-feedback-summary#overview)Overview
﻿
With the AI Generated Feedback Summary token, you can automatically create short, context-aware feedback summaries in non-sequence emails (like rejections, status updates, or next steps). This fits within the context of the surrounding email content, so works both for rejections and for feedback that you may want to give mid-interview loop.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e4c8979946c32b9c5281b2a1ab49f8de.png)
﻿
You'll see a preview of the generated feedback based on submitted scores and notes and will need to approve it by checking the check mark to add it to the email. The summary can be re-generated by clicking the Regenerate icon or deleted by clicking the trash can icon. You will not be able to send the email out to the candidate without approving or deleting the generated summary.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e0fa9b3619554bd33b4176422689a990.png)
﻿
﻿

what-is-a-confidential-job:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Jobs & Openings
# What is a confidential job?
15 min
Copy
Setting a job as confidential allows you to restrict visibility to a select group of users. This is useful for sensitive roles or internal backfills that should not be visible to the broader organization or listed on your public Ashby job board.
When a job is made confidential, it is only visible to:
  * The job creator.


  * Users explicitly granted access to the role.


  * **Org Admins** with the "access to all confidential roles" permission enabled.


This feature is available on Plus, Enterprise, and Legacy Plus plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#creating-a-job-and-setting-it-as-confidential)Creating a job and setting it as confidential
While using the job creation wizard to set up your job, check the Is Confidential? checkbox to mark the job as confidential.
While this isn’t a commonly used option, this can help if you are setting up jobs that only users who have been given specific access should be able to see.
![Job creation wizard pop up showing the Is Confidential? checkbox at the bottom.](https://cdn.zappy.app/abf3e613679e0df17069634f53aa8de9.png)
﻿
﻿  

#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#setting-an-existing-job-as-confidential)Setting an existing job as confidential
If you have already created a job and want to strictly limit user access to the job, click More when viewing the job, then select Make Confidential _._
Only the user who created the job or Organizational Admins can mark the job as confidential or non-confidential.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/010428cbcb65e977bf0bd692ea6e379e.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#sharing-the-confidential-jobs-job-posting-with-can)Sharing the confidential job’s job posting with candidates
Because confidential jobs are unlisted, they will not appear on your public job board. However, you can still share the posting with specific candidates via a direct link.
To find the link, navigate to **Job Postings** within the job and select View Posting. You can then copy the URL from your browser's address bar to share it with potential candidates.
![Shows the View Posting button in the top right of a job posting.](https://cdn.zappy.app/3e937f42cbf9393624db0afe87e04502.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#permissions-access)Permissions & Access
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#can-organization-admins-view-confidential-jobs)Can Organization Admins view confidential jobs?
**Org Admins** can view confidential jobs if they created the job, were explicitly added to it, or have a global permission enabled.
To grant an **Org Admin** access to all confidential jobs:
  1. Navigate to **Admin > Organization Setup > Employees**.


  1. Select the user and go to the **Permissions** tab.


  1. Toggle the Has access to all confidential jobs and projects within assigned permissions option to Yes.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/LarbDTtnKvHZvfC0nOwrf_employees-acent-ashby-2026-04-15-at-23826-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#how-do-i-assign-users-access-to-a-confidential-job)How do I assign users access to a confidential job?
You can grant specific users access to a confidential job without giving them global access.
To assign access:
  1. Navigate to **Admin > Organization Setup > Employees**.


  1. Select the user and go to the **Permissions** tab. (The user must have **Elevated Access** or **Org Admin** status).


  1. Scroll to the **Job Access Roles** section and click + Add.


![The Job Access Roles section in an employee profile featuring the '+ Add' button.](https://cdn.zappy.app/374c7bf07597144bf8e3293cab96033f.png)
﻿
  1. Select the desired access role and choose the specific confidential job from the **Job** dropdown.


![The 'Add Job Access Role' modal displaying dropdowns to select an access role and a specific confidential job.](https://cdn.zappy.app/4ad52607b200580a102144560b868011.png)
﻿
  1. Click Submit to save.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#reporting)Reporting
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#who-can-view-confidential-job-data-in-reports)Who can view confidential job data in reports?
Only users with access to the confidential job can see its data in reports. If a user lacks access, report results will only reflect the non-confidential roles they are permitted to see.
This protection extends to candidate profiles. If a candidate is only considered for a confidential role, their profile remains hidden from unauthorized users. If an unauthorized user attempts to access a direct link to such a profile, they will see a permissions error message.
![Shows the permissions error message a user would see if they do not have permissions to view a candidate profile.](https://cdn.zappy.app/945a5452eb227f70fbdb311185c5bced.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#can-interview-calendar-invites-be-set-to-private-f)Can interview calendar invites be set to Private for confidential jobs?
Yes. If a job is marked confidential during creation, the Is Private? toggle is automatically enabled for all interviews in that pipeline. If you mark a job as confidential after creation, new interviews will be private by default, but existing ones must be updated manually.
When an invite is private, details are hidden from non-attendees viewing the interviewer’s calendar.
**Note:** If scheduling on a shared/group calendar where users have edit access, details may still be visible. We recommend using personal calendars for these instances.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#will-confidential-jobs-be-pulled-through-to-third)Will confidential jobs be pulled through to third-party job board integrations like LinkedIn and Indeed?
No. Because the job posting is set to **Unlisted** , it will not be shared with third-party job board integrations.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#what-happens-if-a-candidate-is-considered-for-both)What happens if a candidate is considered for both a confidential and a non-confidential job?
The candidate remains searchable and visible to anyone with access to the non-confidential job. However, the confidential job consideration will remain hidden from users who do not have explicit access to that confidential role.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#if-a-user-has-access-to-the-department-a-confident)If a user has access to the department a confidential job is linked to, can they see the confidential role?
No. Departmental permissions only apply to non-confidential roles. Access to a confidential job must be granted specifically via a **Job Access Role**.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#can-i-set-a-candidate-as-confidential)Can I set a candidate as confidential?
This is not currently a feature in Ashby.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#if-i-set-up-specific-shared-interview-types-and-fe)If I set up specific shared interview types and feedback forms for confidential roles, can they be seen by other Org Admins?
Yes. Shared interview types and feedback forms are visible to all users with permission to manage those areas. To maintain complete privacy, we recommend creating custom interview types or feedback forms directly within the confidential job or using a confidential job template.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#how-do-openings-work-when-assigned-to-confidential)How do openings work when assigned to confidential jobs?
If an opening is only linked to a confidential job, it is only visible to users with access to that job. If an opening is linked to both confidential and non-confidential jobs, unauthorized users can see the opening but will not see the link to the confidential job.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-confidential-job#how-do-job-approval-processes-work-with-confidenti)How do job approval processes work with confidential jobs?
In the **Scope** section of an approval workflow setup, you can select the Is Confidential? checkbox.
  * **Checked:** The approval process only applies to confidential jobs meeting your criteria.


  * **Unchecked:** The approval process will not apply to confidential jobs.

set-up-departments-and-locations:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Admin & Organization Settings
Organization Setup
# Set up Departments & Locations
6 min
Copy
Departments and locations are required for job setup. Enter just a couple and come back later, or add all of your departments and locations now.
In this step, you'll set up at least one department and one location. Both are required to set up jobs. Most job boards require a physical location, even for remote jobs. Setup the location that makes the most sense for your organization.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations#department-setup)Department Setup
﻿
To set up a department, navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > [Departments & Teams](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/teams "Departments & Teams"). Click + New Department _,_ then enter the department or team name. You will also have the option to set a candidate-facing name for your department. If you set a candidate-facing name for your department, it will be displayed on the job posting listed on your Ashby job board if you opt to list your posting.
You can also specify the department the new team/department is a part of (if it is a sub-team to be grouped under a department - if it is a top-level department, leave the is under field blank). When ready, click Create. You can adjust the department a sub-team is under or remove parent teams after creating the department.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations#location-setup)Location Setup
﻿
To set up a location, navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > [Locations](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/locations "Locations") and click + New Location to create a new location, or click + New Region to create a region to group locations by. When setting up locations always make sure to add in location details if you want those jobs to appear on external job boards like LinkedIn and Indeed. Location details include the following:
**Name:** The name of the location. This is both internal and candidate-facing if you don't set a candidate-facing name. This is required.
**Is under region:** Determine the region the location is under 
**Address:** Enter a street address 
**City:** Add in the city name 
**State/province/region:** Add in further region details
**Postal Code:** Input the postal code 
**Country:** Determine the country here
**Location Type:** Specify the type of location (for example Remote or Hybrid) from the Location Type dropdown. 
**Candidate-Facing Name:** If you'd like to have a different name listed on your job board and for job postings that have this location set, you can set this name here.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/IHONFe91gov66clKSvxee_locations-acent-ashby-2026-03-06-at-100509-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations#when-editing-a-location-i-can-see-yellow-dots-in-c)When editing a location, I can see yellow dots in certain areas. What do these mean?
When amending a location, you’ll see two sections: Location Usage and Location Details _._
In the Location Usage section _,_ you’ll see the number of jobs and job postings linked to this location. If the dot beside a value is green, it means no jobs or job postings are currently using the location. If the marker beside a value is yellow, then there are jobs or job postings linked to this location that will be updated if you make changes.
If you see yellow dots or markers beside any fields in the Location Details section of your location (so in the fields containing location information), then these highlight the fields that are blank and potentially need to have information added to them. Once information is entered, the markers should turn green.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4d204562272ac5fbb5ebd7caffae14dd.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/010561c2b038272f7f93465e927e7459.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations#can-i-pull-my-interview-locations-from-my-job-loca)Can I pull my interview locations from my job locations list?
No, interview locations are different to job locations. Interview locations are set within the Communications tab of your interview activity and can either be a custom location, a phone number, or video conferencing (Google Meet, Microsoft Teams or Zoom). For more on setting a default interview location, please check out [Setting Scheduling Defaults](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations#how-do-i-give-a-user-access-to-jobs-within-a-speci)How do I give a user access to jobs within a specific department?
User permissions can be configured within Admin > Organization Setup > Employees. For more on providing users access to specific departments, check out [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations#can-i-show-the-locations-street-address-in-my-job)Can I show the location's street address in my job postings?
You can set street addresses to be displayed on your job postings. To display the location address across all job postings on your job board, navigate to Admin > Job Boards > Theme (Job Board) and check the Show Location Address?checkbox with the Job Board Options section.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/PxM7Gfuy1SSlCmhFApRs4_show-location-address-checkbox.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If an address has not been assigned to some of your locations, then just the location name (or candidate-facing location name) will be displayed on job postings that have been assigned that location.
﻿  

﻿  

﻿

sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Sourcing & Outreach
# Sequences: InMail and Connection Request Stages
12 min
Copy
You can now create sequences with additional stage types like Linkedin InMail or Connection Requests. Check out [Sequences](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences)﻿ for a full guide on setting up a sequence.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#video-overview)Video Overview
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#sequences-access)Sequences Access
See the chart below for information on the permissions needed to enroll candidates in a sequence.  
| Access Role  | Can enroll candidates in a sequence?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency User  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked with an agency)  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#sequence-setup)Sequence setup
When setting up the stages within your sequences, you’ll be able to select the stage type to any of the following:
  * Send an email to a candidate.


  * Notify you or a team member to request a Connection on LinkedIn


  * Notify you or a team member to send an InMail on LinkedIn


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5ff18dd98e2c62312913214b6070dc03.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#taking-action-on-the-notification)Taking action on the notification
If you set up a stage type to either LinkedIn InMail or LinkedIn Connection, when that stage is reached, you or the sender specified when enrolling candidates will be notified. You’ll receive both an email notification and a task on your home page to send the connection request or InMail.
Clicking the task on your home page will take you to the candidate’s LinkedIn profile. The email will also contain a link to the candidate’s profile so you can quickly take action.
The LinkedIn Connection message or InMail copy will show in a pop-up once on the candidate profile.
1) Copy: Copy the text from the template to add to your connection request or InMail message. You can either click _Copy_ or click the text itself to copy it.
2) Skip Stage: Opt to skip this stage and continue the sequence.
3) Mark Complete: Confirm that this step has been taken and the sequence can continue.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1865bddfc4dfe89f55901d42dbe2554d.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2f1f37ba35576d3cc9ba3d7283e72c4b.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#logging-linkedin-outreach-and-responses-in-ashby)Logging LinkedIn Outreach and Responses in Ashby
If the message is sent as part of a sequence, then it will automatically be synced to the candidate’s profile when _Mark Complete_ is clicked on the pop-up.
Any manual LinkedIn outreach and responses received can be recorded in Ashby. This can be done:
  * On the candidate’s profile


  * Via the Ashby Chrome Extension


  * When setting a response on a sequence.


You’ll be able to select the activity type (outreach or response), along with adding in the outreach type (LinkedIn InMail or LinkedIn Connection). You will then have the option to add in the text used in the outreach message.
The first manually logged LinkedIn reply will also be counted in the Sequence Reply Rate report in [Sourcing Home](https://app.ashbyhq.com/sourcing/home "Sourcing Home") if a sequence is running for the candidate in question. A message will show whether the response has been linked to a sequence or not prior to logging the activity.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8a10086697df7f380d987236b83931aa.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/31aa08b50236f3246b34160d9659f274.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#logging-activity-on-a-candidate-profile)Logging Activity on a candidate profile
Click the + icon at the top of the feed on the right-hand side of the candidate’s profile. From there, specify whether you are logging outreach activity, or a response to outreach.
On the following pop-up, specify the outreach type and add in the message text if needed. Click Log Activity to confirm and add the activity.
﻿  

![Adding log activity to a candidate’s profile.](https://cdn.zappy.app/3d490f03e2d6dba8862f45071faf8555.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9c1df9c8eee8a1b35c728344eb8f23ba.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#logging-activity-on-the-chrome-extension)Logging Activity on the Chrome Extension
While viewing the candidate’s LinkedIn profile, Click the Log Activity button at the bottom of the extension.
On the following Log Activity screen, select whether you’re recording outreach or a response, the outreach type and add in the message text if needed. Click Log Activity to confirm.
Once confirmed, you will see your message on the Feed tab in the Chrome extension.
The Chrome Extension now has a Last Communication widget on the Profile tab so you can check the last outreach or response activity there too.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bbdc3edf7ca19bdb7bf8e70adc36f5ff.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#logging-activity-on-the-sequence-record)Logging Activity on the Sequence record
When setting the response as Interested or Not Interested manually, you will be required to set a Response Type (LinkedIn Mail, LinkedIn Connection or Email) alongside the response status. You will also have the option to add in the message text. Click Set Response to confirm.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cf972413eca4b8a8f11028e16d33ba34.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#reporting-on-sequences-with-linkedin-outreach)Reporting on Sequences with LinkedIn Outreach
**Templates**
We have a range of default templates for sourcing reporting in the [Templates ](https://app.ashbyhq.com/reports/templates "Templates ")section of Reports. The templates allow you to track outreach activities sent and how many received responses, both by outreach type (initial, follow-up, etc) and by outreach activity (via email, LinkedIn InMail, LinkedIn Connection).
**Custom Reporting**
You can filter by Outreach Activity’s Type or set Outreach Activities as a subject to specifically pull initial and follow-up LinkedIn Outreach statistics or activities for custom reporting.
**Sequence Home Reporting**
The first manually logged LinkedIn reply will also be counted in the Sequence Reply Rate report generated in in [Sourcing Home ](https://app.ashbyhq.com/sourcing/home "Sourcing Home ")if a sequence is running for the candidate in question.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/99e57c5f200e7d44fcd7c9ab47170d01.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7aeb023144c4961dc070d0609ceb1a8e.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9d2b3bf356439cb5aa974a3e1affc5ff.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#can-you-automatically-trigger-an-email-sequence-af)Can you automatically trigger an email sequence after initially sending an InMail if there is no response?
Yes! Once the stage is marked as complete for sending an InMail or skipped, the sequence will continue to run and so would send out an email as expected when reaching that next stage.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#can-i-start-a-sequence-with-a-linkedin-inmail-or-l)C**an I start a sequence with a LinkedIn InMail or LinkedIn Connection?**
You can, yes. Previously, the first stage needed to be an automated email but now that’s not required.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#my-sequence-status-has-a-paused-status-and-wont-re)My sequence status has a Paused status and won't resume when I click Resume Sequence. Why?
If the sequence's next step is a LinkedIn InMail or connection step, then the step needs to be marked as completed before the sequence can continue. Clicking Resume Sequence will not work in this case. More on marking a step as complete above in [Taking Action on the Notification](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages#taking-action-on-the-notification)﻿.
﻿  

﻿

<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Initial Setup
# Interviewers: Assigning, Rescheduling, and Setting Interviewer Limits
28 min
Copy
﻿[Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿This guide covers assigning, rescheduling and setting interview limits for interviewers. If you are an interviewer, we’d recommend reading over [Getting Started for Interviewers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-interviewers)﻿﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#how-to-assign-interviewers)How to assign interviewers
The written information below serves as a summary. We’d recommend watching the video for a full step-by-step to assign interviewers.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#assigning-interviewers-and-setting-up-interviews-s)Assigning interviewers and setting up interviews slots
﻿
When setting up schedule interview activities﻿, you can click _Interviewers_ within _Configure_ to add interviewers. From there you can build your panel using the Interviewer Slot and Employee options. 
More on schedule interview activities at [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#adding-interviewer-slots)Adding interviewer slots
You can create a slot using the Add Interviewer Slot button and determine the total number of interviewers that can be on your interview panel. Adding interviewers slots uses AND logic, meaning that each identified interviewer will participate. This is indicated by the AND between each slot, showing that a user assigned in interviewer slot #1 will be on the panel, along with a user assigned to interviewer slot #2.
![In this example, the candidate will only see times when both interviewers are available to take the interview together.](https://cdn.zappy.app/7a74311fab0d3769cb3221b06a61306e.png)
In this example, the candidate will only see times when both interviewers are available to take the interview together.
﻿
If you have multiple interviewing slots with users assigned to each slot, the availability shown is based on the times that each combination of interviewers from your interviewer slots are available.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#adding-specific-employees-as-interviewers)Adding specific employees as interviewers
This drop-down is where you assign who can be booked for the interviewer slot in question. You can assign multiple employees to an interviewer slot if anyone from a certain group of employees can take that slot to interview a candidate. You will see OR between selected users, showing that either Interviewer 1 or Interviewer 2 will take that specific interviewer slot on the panel.
![In this example, the candidate will see the availability for each interviewer and the interviewer will be assigned based on the interview time selected.](https://cdn.zappy.app/53782de8ff05ed454f3805d45581b3d9.png)
In this example, the candidate will see the availability for each interviewer and the interviewer will be assigned based on the interview time selected.
﻿
If multiple users are assigned to one interview slot, the candidate will see the availability for all possible interviewers. 
The interview is assigned based on the date and time selected. 
If multiple interviewers assigned to the same slot are available at the selected time, the interview will be assigned to the interviewer with the fewest interviews. 
All interviews scheduled in the past 30 days and the next 30 days will be considered in the interview count.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#adding-interviewers-based-on-hiring-team-role)Adding interviewers based on hiring team role
If you'd like to assign interviewers based on their role in the hiring team, or set the interviewer as the user who moved the candidate to this particular stage, those are also options in the Employees dropdown when creating interviewer slots.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/4RCUOu_qHrxog48eSk-Nt_marketeer-acent-ashby-2026-02-13-at-105132-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If no interviewers are assigned to the schedule interview activity, the interview activity may be created on the candidate profile without an interviewer. You can read more about this behavior in[Why are interviewers missing from a schedule interview event?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#why-are-interviewers-missing-from-a-scheduled-inte)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#adding-interviewer-pools)Adding interviewer pools
Want to assign an interviewer pool instead? Change the Specific Employees dropdown to Employees from Pool, then determine your pool or pools to include. You can also specify whether only qualified employees, only trainees or all employees in the pool or pools can be selected from to be assigned to this slot.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/2EZt3UhcS36MYpoLZPFwI_marketeer-acent-ashby-2026-02-13-at-105546-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**What's an interviewer pool?** You can set up interviewer pools to group your employees and assign them to interviewer slots more easily. For example, you could add those who are in a specific timezone to a pool and assign that pool to an interviewer slot to pull interviewers who will have suitable availability to interview candidates for a role in a specific location. You can read more on configuring pools at [Interviewer Pools](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewer-pools)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#using-the-new-interview-button-quick-schedule-and)Using the ‘+ New Interview’ button: Quick Schedule and Direct Booking links.
Two interview options are discussed here; generating a direct booking link and quick scheduling. The following guides cover both options for instant scheduling via the ‘+ New Interview’ link on a candidate profile:
﻿[Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿[Manually Scheduling Interviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/manually-scheduling-interviews)﻿﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#instant-direct-booking-links)Instant Direct Booking Links
When using the + New Interview option to generate a booking link, interviewers can be assigned on the pop-up that follows after choosing the Create Direct Booking Link option.
Choose users from the Interviewer drop-down as part of the interview’s settings. Each interviewer you assign via this route will be assigned a slot on the panel.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#manual-schedule)Manual Schedule
If you schedule in the candidate directly via the Schedule New Interview option, then you can add in interviewer slots and assign interviewers to them accordingly.
You can also add in alternates for each interviewer slot. Once added, you can view the alternates’ calendars when selecting an interview time to compare their availability with the current interviewer’s. You can then opt to switch out the current interviewer for the alternate if needed.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#rescheduling-or-changing-interviewers)Rescheduling or changing interviewers
﻿
To change out an interviewer on a scheduled interview, you will need to reschedule it. To reschedule the interview, click Reschedule on the interview event, then Manual Reschedule.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/JB2a9A10R3mog4ynAAFKP_shelley-pearce-marketeer-acent-ashby-2026-02-17-at-101040-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When rescheduling, you can opt to keep the same date and time but remove the original interviewer (via the Clear User option). From there you can use the drop-down menu to select another interviewer. You will have the option to change the interview type and also remove the interviewer slot completely.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/443ea6b2a19f9896227d72a74a7c98e9.png)
﻿
  * **Clear User** : This will remove the selected interviewer but will keep the interview slot. You can then use the Select User… dropdown to select another interviewer.


  * **Remove Slot** : Removes the slot completely to reduce the size of your panel by 1.


If you opt to resend calendar invites when rescheduling, then the new interviewer will receive a calendar invite and the candidate will receive an updated invite. We usually recommend resending the calendar invite when rescheduling.
You are able to remove an interviewer who has provided feedback already when rescheduling. Once removed, their feedback will no longer show in Ashby.
Reschedule the interview to switch over interviewers on the panel.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#what-do-interviewers-see-when-an-interview-is-book)What do interviewers see when an interview is booked in Ashby?
Once an interview is scheduled (or quick scheduled with the scheduler choosing to send calendar invites) the interviewer will receive a calendar invite. They will also see the upcoming interview on their Home page when they log into Ashby. If they click on the upcoming interview, they will be able to see the interview details, the interview briefing, the feedback form they need to complete and the feedback left for the candidate thus far.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f317bf40e638c225bc4450a312a092a3.png)
﻿
  1. Details - The date and time of the interview (listed in the interviewer’s timezone), along with the interviewers assigned.


  1. Briefing - This is pulled from the interview assigned to the [schedule interview activity](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿ or selected at the time of generating the direct booking link for the candidate. You can amend or add briefings to your interview templates in [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews").


  1. Your Feedback - The feedback form for the interviewer to complete post-interview. Feedback entered into the form from this interview page is auto-saved. Additional feedback forms can be set up in [Feedback Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-forms "Feedback Forms").


  1. All Feedback**-** On this tab, the interviewer may be able to see all previous feedback for the candidate. You can control whether or not interviewers can view this feedback in Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > [Feedback Visibility](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-visibility "Feedback Visibility").


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#setting-interviewer-limits)Setting interviewer limits
You can set daily or weekly limits for your interviewers to ensure that they don’t get overwhelmed. These limits can be set per employee or at the organization level.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#setting-interviewer-limits-per-employee)Setting interviewer limits per employee
You can click on a user on the [Employees](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/users "Employees") page and head to the **Interviewer Info** tab to add in the maximum number of interviews the interviewer can take each day and each week.
Once the maximum number of interviews has been reached, that interviewer’s availability will not be shown to the candidate when booking for that day or week. These limits are not applied when using the Manual Schedule option for booking interviews.
Interview events that do not require feedback will not count against interviewer limits. Only Organization Admins have access to adjust interviewer limits.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#setting-org-wide-interviewer-limits)Setting org-wide interviewer limits
Interview limit defaults can be set org-wide by navigating to **Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > Interviewer Limit Defaults**.
Defaults set here will apply to any interviewer, but are overridden if that interviewer has different limits set up on their employee profile.
On this page, you can define how many interviews your interviewers are allowed to participate in per day and per week.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/S34kBFyJ0mGCmo8CTzBak_interviewer-limit-defaults-acent-ashby-2026-04-07-at-23140-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
## ﻿  

#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#can-i-make-a-note-of-any-requirements-or-requests)Can I make a note of any requirements or requests from specific interviewers on their profile?
You can, yes! On the [Employees](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/users "Employees") page, select the user in question and navigate to the _Interviewer Info_ tab on their profile. From there, you can add any requirements or requests from them to the Preferences field. This information will then be available when scheduling in an interview.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/49bbf8b6f311e76145fd786397c62143.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/26e2ed0c1b04598e88032f910a7e7705.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#can-i-send-a-request-to-a-user-to-leave-feedback-e)Can I send a request to a user to leave feedback even if they weren’t on the panel for an interview?
You can, yes! On the candidate’s profile, click the More button, then Request Feedback without Scheduling to get started.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f3e432bf6f065729bd7c76553492e874.png)
﻿
You can then specify the interview type and the user who should complete the feedback form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6cec14e7278363dfaa7c7436d1f50c83.png)
﻿
Once submitted, the user will see the request on their home page, both in the Interviews section and in their tasks _._ Clicking on either the task or interview prompt will take them to the feedback form they should complete.
Please note that this option is to request feedback from an interviewer not already on the panel, rather than reminding them to leave feedback for an interview they have been scheduled for.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#do-interviewers-receive-email-reminders-to-leave-f)Do interviewers receive email reminders to leave feedback for an interview?
If an interviewer is on a panel for an interview where feedback is required by default, they will receive one email a day prompting them to leave feedback. By default, they will receive an email a day for up to ten days (or until their feedback is completed).
To confirm an interview is set to have feedback required by default, click on the interview in question in Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews").
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/448ac3c3e3c05aff50feea50a176611b.png)
﻿
To adjust the interviewer notification settings, navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > [Interviewer Notifications](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/notifications/ "Interviewer Notifications"). You can amend the following:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4b3141541c1bebba65398125a43b418f.png)
﻿
  * Set whether interviewer notifications are stopped after a candidate is hired.


  * Set whether interviewer notifications are stopped after a candidate is archived.


  * Adjust the number of days notifications are sent for.


Please note that the Interviewer Notification settings will apply to all interviews that require feedback. It’s not possible to set specific notification settings per interview.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#can-interviewer-information-like-their-role-or-lin)Can interviewer information (like their role or LinkedIn profile URL) be added to the candidate-facing interview details?
You can set interviewer information like their role and social links to be visible to the interviewing candidate, yes. Head to Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > [Scheduling Communication](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/scheduling-communication "Scheduling Communication") and enable the Include Job Title _and_ Social Links in Agenda? option.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bb07f8b72338ba24426676f52655d703.png)
﻿
Once enabled, if you’d like certain social links (or social links for a specific interviewer) to now show within the interview event details, head to the interviewer’s user profile in Admin > Organization Setup > Employeesand scroll to the Social Linkssection on the Basic Infotab. From there, you can click on the eye icon beside a social link to set it to not show to the candidate.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fe77bec26d484de5c6b455aa31264969.png)
﻿
Your interviewers can add in details like their job title and their social links within the Personal Info section of their [Personal Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/personal-info "Personal Settings"). This information can also be added on their behalf in the Basic Info tab of their profile on the Employees page.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b34375455c4721933c40183bed1996a1.png)
﻿
Once their information has been added, their job title and social links will show on both the confirmation page when the candidate books via a direct booking link and when the Interview Panel Summary token is used.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4a478125645bdc05c93084d620d50e68.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f4dfcb796899da8403e5d1909576c511.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#can-i-copy-an-existing-activity-from-an-interview)C**an I copy an existing activity from an interview plan stage on one job to an interview plan stage on another?**
You can, yes! When you are on a job’s interview plan and click Add Activity To… on an interview plan stage you’ll see the option to add a new activity and a Copy Activities option.
Click Copy Activities, then select the job, interview plan, interview stage you’d like to copy activities from. Choose the activities you’d like to copy. The selected activities will be copied into the original stage you chose.
﻿
Once an activity is copied over, if the activity uses a shared interview type or feedback form (more on those at [What are ‘Shared’ Interviews, Application Forms, and Feedback Forms?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms)﻿ ), then those will be used in the copy. Any custom interview types or feedback forms will be copied over and can be amended without impacting the original activity that was copied.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#can-i-pause-an-interviewers-membership-in-a-pool-o)C**an I pause an interviewer’s membership in a pool or stop them from being scheduled for any interviews?**
You can, yes! You can either pause an interviewer within a specific pool (so they can’t be booked for interviews where that pool is assigned) or globally (so they aren’t automatically scheduled for any interviews).
**Pausing an interviewer from within a pool.**
To pause an interviewer’s membership in a pool, head to Admin>Interviewing & Scheduling>[Interviewer Pools](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviewer-tags "Interviewer Pools") and click on the pool in question. Click Actions next to the interviewer in question, then click Pause Membership _._
When an interviewer is paused, a Paused tag will show beside their name in the pool.
You can also unpause the interviewer at any time by clicking on them from within the interviewer pool again, and selecting Resume Membership _._
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/358a39506fc8294a6302f52b263461a6.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9c5ed65dcdd4a70129eeddb6cf678cc8.png)
﻿
**Pausing an interviewer across all interviews** Please note that the option to pause interviewers is only available on the Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ✅  | ✅  |  
If you'd like to pause an interviewer for all interviews (for example, if the interviewer is on vacation), navigate to their user profile within Admin > Employees and head to the Interviewer Info tab. Turn on the Pause Interviewer option - you will also have the option to add a reason for the pause that will show up when a user tries to manually schedule that interviewer. 
You can also schedule an interviewer pause. Click Set start date to determine the date and time the pause should start. Click Set end date to determine when the pause should end. If you don't opt to set an end date, the pause will continue indefinitely until it is disabled on the user's profile. 
Click Submit to confirm the pause. You can switch the pause off at any time by switching off the Pause Interviewer option.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/gP0798uYbcjzxy0QvWe0s_employees-acent-ashby-2026-01-26-at-41429-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/G3Rma6-ZjbicpSzjOWlNr_employees-acent-ashby-2026-01-26-at-41506-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits#why-are-interviewers-missing-from-a-scheduled-inte)**Why are interviewers missing from a scheduled interview event?**
When a candidate is moved into a stage that triggers an interview activity, the activity is created immediately based on the current configuration of the interview plan's activity.
If no interviewers are assigned at that time, the interview activity will be created without interviewers.
![Ashby interview scheduling interface displaying a 30-minute ‘Intro Call with Hiring Manager’ under a phone screen schedule, with configuration options visible but no interviewers assigned.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/FqpFv6hKcISqMamSbkfaJ_product-documentation-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-04-03-at-105624-am.jpg?format=webp)
Schedule Interview Activity with no Interviewers
﻿
If you add an interviewer to the schedule interview activity in the interview plan, that update can appear on the interview activity on the job consideration.
However, if the scheduling process has already started and a different interviewer has already been selected, Ashby will not override that selected interviewer with the newly added one.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)

Surveys & Questionnaires
# EEOC Surveys
11 min
Copy
Ashby supports the ability to collect [EEOC surveys](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/surveys/eeoc-settings "EEOC surveys") from applicants on jobs pages or at any stage of the hiring process.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#eeoc-surveys-overview)EEOC Surveys Overview
Ashby supports the ability to collect an EEOC Survey﻿ from applicants on jobs pages or at any stage of the hiring process. Users can configure rules for how and when to collect them from candidates. You can then analyze the results of these surveys to measure performance against your DEI goals.
See the table below for more information on the access roles that have the ability to set up surveys in Ashby and who can send out a survey.  
| Access Role  | Can Configure Surveys?  | Can Send Out Surveys to Candidates?  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin (Private)  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#set-up-eeoc-surveys)Set up EEOC surveys
You can set up EEOC surveys in the [Admin Section](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/surveys/eeoc-settings "Admin Section") of the app under Jobs & Applications > Surveys.
![Screen Shot 2022-02-18 at 1.03.51 PM.png](https://cdn.zappy.app/628e7970584d3488ea593a3d590e48f7.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#collecting-surveys)Collecting Surveys
EEOC surveys can be collected in three different ways:
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#collecting-on-the-application-tab-of-each-job)C**ollecting on the application tab of each job**
You can set up a filter to determine which job postings the survey should be collected on.
If you enable the Collect alongside application on job postings? toggle, you will then be able to click the + Filter button to determine the criteria that needs to be met in order for the survey to be shown on specific job postings. If you leave the filter plan, the EEOC form will be collected on all postings.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0efe0a6488755a12256383c34066a348.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#directly-requesting-surveys)D**irectly Requesting Surveys**
You can directly request a survey by taking the following steps:
  * On the candidate profile, click the More dropdown


  * Select Create EEOC Survey Request


  * You will be asked to choose which survey to send and which application it should be associated with.


  * Create the survey request using the Create button, or optionally also schedule an email with a link to the survey using the Create and Email button.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c27c11d22a18bd6675898a633872d208.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#setting-up-automation-rules)Se**tting Up Automation Rules**
You can set up automation rules so that you don’t need to directly request surveys from candidates manually.
You can set up a series of conditions that trigger automatically sending a survey to a candidate. Our article on [Utilizing Stage Groups](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups)﻿ walks through the different fields you can select to customize the automation.
To set up automation for your EEOC survey:
  * Head to the Automation Settings section of the EEOC Survey page and create a new draft configuration by clicking Create a Draft Configuration


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bcd2e19f90fd81cf11fa59b99d79d73d.png)
﻿
  * Specify your conditions by clicking + Add Field to Match, choosing fields and then creating filters based on those


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6ad66366ee7b6ed941a98417aea7cac5.png)
﻿
  * Select an email template from the Email Template dropdown that will be used to send out your survey


  * Configure any email delay using the Delay dropdown


  * Specify a sender email address


  * Activate the configuration


  * Toggle on the Enable Survey Automation toggle once the draft has been activated


The conditions of each rule will get checked whenever a candidate changes stages. Ashby will find the first rule that matches and a survey will be created and an email with a link to the survey will be scheduled.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#view-results)View results
**Reporting and analysis**
You can report on the questions in a EEOC survey in our standard reports. EEOC Surveys are their own reporting subject. For more on reporting subjects, check out the [Ashby Subject & Field Definitions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions)﻿ guide.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#how-do-i-view-pending-surveys)How do I view pending surveys?
You can view any pending survey requests on the candidate profile under Current Activities. From here you can email the survey again or delete the request.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e6e598a773c475e31fac34d439838d75.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#what-does-the-survey-look-like-to-the-candidate)What does the survey look like to the candidate?
When a candidate follows the link in a survey request email, they’ll be brought to a page where they can fill in the survey.
![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/zqPN2Atdgfb2_hGuj44an_screenshot2022-02-18at10734pm.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#when-are-a-surveys-automation-conditions-evaluated)When are a survey’s automation conditions evaluated?
The conditions in survey delivery automation settings are evaluated during stage transition events. This means a candidate will need to be moved forward to the next or archived in order for a survey to be sent.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#are-surveys-anonymous)Are surveys anonymous?
Yes - by default, survey responses are anonymous.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#can-i-limit-who-can-edit-dei-reports)C**an I limit who can edit DEI reports?**
You can, yes! To get started, switch on the Restrict editing DEI reports feature in Admin>Data Management>Reporting Permissions [here.](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/reporting-permissions "here.")﻿
When the setting is enabled, users without the access to edit or create DEI reports will not be able to choose DEI fields as Fields to Show or Group By fields. Saved reports containing those fields will still be visible, but the reports will not be editable.
  * Once enabled, you will then be able to determine who can edit these reports in each user’s profile on the [Employees](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/users "Employees") page.


  * When in a user’s profile, you will see the option to allow them to create and update DEI reports on the Permissions tab.


  * Admins with Can Create and Update DEI Reports permission can grant or revoke the permission for others. The permission is automatically granted to the admin user who turns on the Restrict Editing DEI Reports setting.


Please note that this does not impact the visibility of diversity data in shared reports. Those with Organization Admin access or Elevated Access - Admin Private access can view diversity data in reports.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/520bf20de3bee0955f431730bebcbb65.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0e612b021989099319736879bacee65c.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c5997275294b11c1c85fc70a56a97949.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#what-information-is-asked-for-on-the-eeoc-survey)What information is asked for on the EEOC survey?
  * Gender


  * Race


  * Veteran Status


If the Is your organization a government contractor? option is set to yes, the survey will ask the candidates to self-identify the following (again, all questions are optional):
  * Gender


  * Race


  * Veteran Status


  * Voluntary Self-identification of Disability


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys#can-i-edit-the-eeoc-survey-questions)C**an I edit the EEOC survey questions?**
No, all questions are multiple choice with pre-populated answer options and can not be edited.
﻿

# Dashboard Filters
8 min
Copy
This document reviews dashboard filters and how to add or remove them from dashboards.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#overview)Overview
Dashboard filters are a powerful feature in Ashby that allow you to add or configure filters at the dashboard level that can be quickly applied to all reports on a dashboard. They allow you to build dashboards that can be filtered to specific departments, recruiters, etc., without having to copy reports and dashboards.
![An example of a dashboard in which all reports are filtered down to the Marketing department.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wwA7Ee6SjpuSSCLl1Hzw5_screenshot2022-12-05at82413am.png?format=webp)
﻿
An example of a dashboard in which all reports are filtered down to the Marketing department.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#tutorial)Tutorial
The Loom video below will teach you how to use dashboard filters in Ashby.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#add-a-filter-and-connect-it)Add a filter and connect it
  1. To add a filter, navigate to your dashboard (make sure you are on the Dashboard Edit View) and click Add Filter.


  1. Find the field you would like to filter by. For filters attached to a user, use the User field. You will be able to identify what user role you are filtering for at the report level.


  1. Connect the filter to each of the reports you want the filter to be applied to. When you click on the filter icon, you will be prompted to select/search for the field you want to attach the filter to. Pay attention to the subject the filter is anchored on. For example, a recruiter at the job level and the job consideration level could be different.


  1. Green - All dashboard filters on the dashboard have been applied to the report Grey - Some dashboard filters on the dashboard have been applied to the report Red - No dashboard filters on the dashboard have been applied to the report


  1. If you would like, you can require a filter to be applied to all of the reports on a dashboard. To do this, check the box next to Require value. You will see the alert below if any of the reports are not connected to the required filter.


Dashboard filters are applied in the dashboard view and will not be saved to the underlying reports.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#viewing-an-underlying-report-on-a-dashboard-with-a)Viewing an underlying report on a dashboard with a filter
If you apply filters at the dashboard level, you can click the Go to Filtered Report icon to view the report. The unsaved dashboard filters will be applied when viewing the report (but will not be saved to the report automatically). You can click Save when viewing the filtered report to add the filter changes to your existing report. If you make any changes to the underlying report and save it, these changes will be reflected on the dashboard.
If you need to get to the underlying report without any dashboard filter context being applied, the action menu on any report tile provides the option (the three dots to the right of the Go to Filtered Report button) to go directly to the report via Go to Report.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#delete-a-filter)Delete a filter
To delete a dashboard filter, select the Trash icon next to the filter and click Delete.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#can-i-connect-a-recruiting-planner-report-to-a-das)Can I connect a Recruiting Planner report to a dashboard filter?
This isn’t currently possible.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#help-im-accessing-the-dashboard-from-my-homepage-a)Help! I’m accessing the dashboard from my homepage and don’t have the option to connect filters. What’s going on?
When accessing the dashboard via the Views column on your homepage, be sure to click Edit on the dashboard. You should then be able to configure dashboard filters.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b42194d2b91a5f3afd7c25f342f49dad.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters#can-i-set-my-dashboard-filter-to-exclude-certain-v)Can I set my dashboard filter to exclude certain values?
You can, yes. When you specify a value for your filter, you will see an Is dropdown between the filter type and the chosen value. You can use the dropdown and select Is Not to exclude the specified value from your results rather than only show values matching your filter.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/47ccbe9721ba7a795aeaca93fc0fdf83.png)
﻿
﻿
