offer-and-e-signature-document-condition:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Offers & E-Signatures
# Offer and E-Signature Document Conditional Tokens
11 min
Copy
Conditional tokens let you automatically include additional text in an offer letter or e-signature document when specific conditions are met on the offer. For example, you can use a conditional token to include a relocation assistance paragraph only when the offer indicates that relocation assistance is provided.
Offer and e-signature document conditional tokens are available on the Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ✅  | ✅  |  
**Prerequisites:**
  * You must be an **Org Admin** or**Elevated Access user** with Offer letter templates permissions to create and manage conditional offer tokens.


  * You must be an **Org Admin** to manage conditional e-signature document tokens.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#overview)Overview
Review the video and read the steps below to learn how to use conditional tokens.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#creating-a-conditional-token)Creating a conditional token
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#offer-conditional-tokens)Offer conditional tokens
Navigate to **Admin > Offers > Offer Tokens**. Scroll down to the **Conditional Tokens** section and click + New.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qn5HchSCVtogclzbq86tI_offers-acent-ashby-2026-05-08-at-13839-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#e-signature-document-conditional-tokens)E-signature document conditional tokens
Navigate to **Admin > Integrations > E-Signature Documents** and select the **Tokens** tab. Scroll to the **Conditional Tokens** section and click + New.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/u3nPbRMIsIOYgxtm3jH1L_e-signature-documents-acent-ashby-2026-05-08-at-14016-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#setting-up-your-conditional-token)Setting up your conditional token
When you create a conditional token, you are prompted to fill in the following fields:  
| Field  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| **Name / Title**  | The name of your token. This is used to identify the token in the list.  |  
| **Description**  | Optional additional context to help others understand what the token is for.  |  
| **Token**  | Auto-generated once you name your token. Click the token to copy it for use in your offer letter template.  |  
| **Condition**  | The rule that must be met for the token's content to appear in the generated offer. If the condition is not met, the text is omitted from the offer.   |  
**Example:** A conditional token named "Relocation assistance" can be configured so that its content only appears in the offer when the _Relocation assistance provided_**custom offer field** is set to **Yes**. When set to **No** , the token content does not appear.
See [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿ to learn how to create them.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#how-to-add-a-conditional-token-to-an-offer-templat)How to add a conditional token to an offer template
Once you have created your conditional token, follow these steps to add it to your offer letter or e-signature document template:
  1. In the **Conditional Tokens** section, click the token to copy it to your clipboard.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bV8bwDJ00ud15A7YtwoBM_offers-acent-ashby-2026-05-08-at-14922-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Open your offer letter or e-signature document template in your preferred editor (such as Word or Google Docs).


For more on creating offer or e-signature templates, see [Offer Letter Template Formatting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-letter-template-formatting)﻿ and [E-Signature Documents](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-documents)﻿ .
  1. Paste the token into the location in your template where the conditional content should appear. The pasted token looks like a pair of opening and closing tags with placeholder text.


![Conditional token pasted into template showing placeholder text.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/4hJNr6H7BGZJ0kCLj4LW-_example-offer-letter-2-2026-05-08-at-14812-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Replace the placeholder text (shown as replace-this-text-in-your-template) between the opening and closing tags with the content you want to display when the condition is met. Make sure your text stays between both tags.


![Conditional token with the placeholder text replaced with the content that should show when conditions are met.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/-MWXLG0yNdW58TlIkqtqr_example-offer-letter-2-2026-05-08-at-14533-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Any content you place outside the opening and closing tags will appear in all offers, regardless of whether the condition is met.
  1. Once you are happy with the template, upload it to Ashby by navigating to **Admin > Offers > Offer Letter Templates** or **Admin > Integrations > E-signature Documents > E-signature Document Templates** depending on the type of template you're creating.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#conditional-tokens-in-practice-relocation-assistan)Conditional tokens in practice: relocation assistance example
The screenshots below show how a conditional token behaves based on whether the offer meets the configured condition.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#when-the-condition-is-met-relocation-assistance-pr)When the condition is met (relocation assistance provided: yes)
The conditional token content appears in the generated offer letter.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dgtCjoytECUfdFmGqzcBQ_offer-letter-google-docs-2026-05-08-at-15836-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens#when-the-condition-is-not-met-relocation-assistanc)When the condition is not met (relocation assistance provided: no)
The conditional token content does not appear in the generated offer letter.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/IfdIDQ7RYiR26f_k01lYZ_offer-letter-google-docs-2026-05-08-at-20028-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
﻿

how-do-i-update-the-status-on-a-job-cons:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-update-the-status-on-a-job-consideration -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Applications
# How do I update the status on a job consideration?
1 min
Copy
This guide covers statuses that can be applied to job considerations how how to manually update the status of a job consideration. 
Please note that manually updating the status on a job consideration should be rarely used. If you are looking for more information on handling a rejected or rescinded offer, check out [Offers and Offer Letters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#rescinding-or-withdrawing-an-offer-and-handling-de)﻿.
Click More on the candidate profile, and scroll to the bottom of the list and select the Change Application Status option. You have the option to change the status to:
  * Archived


  * Assessment Submitted


  * Candidate Accepted Offer


  * Candidate Availability Submitted


  * Candidate Rejected Offer


  * Hired*


  * Last Active


  * Needs Decision


  * Needs Offer


  * Needs Offer Approval


  * Needs Scheduling


  * No Status


  * Scheduled


  * Waiting On Assessment


  * Waiting On Candidate Availability


  * Waiting On Candidate Booking


  * Waiting On Candidate Offer Response


  * Waiting On Feedback


  * Waiting On Take Home Submission


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/df1c16d3e2280fd7e4112197d79a6408.gif)
﻿
The ‘Hired’ stage will not appear in the Change Stage dropdown if there are active Offer Approvals set up in the [Admin > Approvals > Offers section](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/approvals/offer "Admin > Approvals > Offers section"). You will need to complete the offer process by creating the offer, then clicking the Offer Accepted option within the offer’s settings. Once that has been done, the candidate’s stage will automatically change to ‘Hired’.
﻿

candidate-reviews:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Sourcing & Outreach
# Candidate Reviews
16 min
Copy
You can now create requests and assign them to users to get their feedback on specific candidates or leads.
Limited Access users will only be able to view the reviews that they themselves have been assigned to or have completed but can be assigned to candidate reviews. All candidate reviews will be visible for those with Elevated Access or above within Sourcing > Candidate Reviews.
Candidate review requests can be generated on the Foundations, Plus, Legacy Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#creating-a-review-request)Creating a review request
Review requests can be sent while viewing candidates in projects﻿ or from within a [Candidate Search](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search)﻿, from the audience list on an event, from the submissions list for a sourcing form and the candidate pipeline for a job. You can also create new review requests from the list of candidates in the _Review_ section of an existing candidate review.
Like with any other bulk action, select the candidates you would like to request a review for using the checkboxes to the left of their name. Then click Request Review from the bulk actions list.
In the request review wizard, add in the following:
Title: Provide a title for your request for reference.
Reviewer: From the drop-down, select the user who will be assigned to review the selected candidate or candidates.
Notes: Add in any clarifying information here for the reviewer.
Then click Send Request to confirm and send the request to the assigned user.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ba4be5106c7bb72af0b35a4b4f2c9ea6.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a12f522c46742457f798c4e9448fc811.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#reviewing-a-candidate)Reviewing a candidate
Once a user has been assigned a candidate review, this will show as a task on their home page.
They can click Start Review to view the candidate details, add a score and write any notes.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/032b8125059c6f42c24134fd3959c8ee.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#candidate-review-page)Candidate review page
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0b98e2277859097b993a44641b3aeb2f.png)
﻿
1: All Click this toggle to view candidate information and their resume in one place.
2: Info Click this to only view the candidate information like their email, location, work history and education. This will hide the resume.
3: Resume Click this to only view their resume and hide the candidate information from view.
4: Reviewed Here you can view the total number of candidates in the review and click the arrows to jump between candidates needing review. You can also use the arrows on your keyboard to move between candidates.
5: Requested By The user who requested the review
6: The note with additional information will be displayed underneath the details of the user who requested the review.
7: Score Give an overall score for the candidate from 1 (Strong No) to 4 (Strong Yes). This is a required field.
8: Notes Add in any clarifying information here if needed.
If the reviewer has access to the candidate profile, then their name will appear hyperlinked. The user can then click the candidate’s name to view their profile.
To complete the review, click Submit _._ If multiple reviews have been requested, clicking this button will also automatically move the reviewer on to the next candidate.
If the reviewer would like to exit the review screen, they can close out the window, or hit ESC on their keyboard. The review screen will also close out once all reviews have been submitted.
The reviewer will then be returned to the review page, listing all of the candidates in the review, along with their information and any feedback scores or notes left. If all reviews have been completed, then the review will automatically be updated to Completed.
To mark the review of all candidates as completed (even if all reviews haven’t been submitted), the reviewer can click on the status drop-down under the review title and click Complete Review Request.
Once the review’s status is Complete, this will send a notification to the review requester and the reviewer will not be able to submit any more reviews.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/50a9c8de731d5947ccdd1162c0b14649.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a24eb070ab2a483a9c827d5ab1749609.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e88b9dbb00b5744f690b69f6eb3c971b.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#viewing-reviewed-candidates)Viewing reviewed candidates
To view all candidate reviews and their status, navigate to Sourcing > [Candidate Reviews.](https://app.ashbyhq.com/sourcing/reviews/all "Candidate Reviews.")﻿
By default you will see all requested reviews listed, along with their status, reviewer, requester, the request notes and the timestamp showing when the request was submitted.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/44033bec0aa544d4de7b6850bc04459a.png)
﻿
Click on a review to view a list of all the candidates included in said review, along with their scores and notes. 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5723449d2ad60337aab33b33368150f9.png)
﻿
Candidates can be sorted alphabetically or reverse-alphabetically by name or by score (ascending or descending). Click on the field you’d like to sort by to make any adjustments.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b5b91e3b24f28ef690b44a8ba06eaf79.png)
﻿
The results, showing the number of candidates reviewed, the average score and the Approved rate (percentage of candidates that scored a 3 or higher) will be displayed on the right-hand side of the list.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/69fb47e7bea8fef422a58812b2187991.png)
﻿
Candidate reviews can also be viewed on each candidate’s profile within their feed. Click the Feedback tab if you’d like to view the review feedback specifically.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#ai-generated-candidate-review-summary)AI-Generated candidate review summary
If you’d like to have an AI-generated summary of the feedback given in a candidate review request, navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > Opt-In Features and enable the AI-Generated Candidate Review Summary option.
Once a candidate review is complete, the summary will be generated and displayed under the review results.
You can click on each theme listed to view the review that prompted the theme to be listed, along with the score and relevant note.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fb640928bdf9464d0bb599a1aa3e8e7f.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/120bc82a60e395b42ab630cf50f881e8.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#saved-views-and-reviewing-filters)Saved views and reviewing filters
When viewing the candidate review list, you can also use pre-created or configure your own filters to access the reviews most useful to you.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b6c00282df9749034b87e85baa7fe383.png)
﻿
  1. **New Filter** On this tab, you can create your own filter. Click _Save as New_ (4) to give your new filter a title and then save it


  1. **Received Requests** Use this filter to view all review requests that you have been assigned to


  1. **Sent Requests** Use this filter to view all review requests that you have sent


  1. **Save as New** Use this button to save a new filter once you’ve set your criteria


  1. **Filter:** Use the + Filter button to add fields to filter by. A status filter is included by default but you can remove this by clicking the trash can icon (7)


  1. **Basic/Advanced toggle** Use this to switch between basic filters and advanced filters.


  1. **Trash Can** Use this to remove any filters you don’t want to apply.


  1. **Search** Search for a review request by name.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#taking-bulk-action-on-candidates-in-a-candidate-re)Taking bulk action on candidates in a candidate review
If you'd like to take action on multiple candidates in review at once, use the checkboxes by each listed candidate within the candidate review to determine who the action should be taken on. From there, buttons will be displayed above the list of candidates showing you the bulk actions that can be taken on the selected profiles.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RIPCIezM2_2js1zDiIjSe_gray-rangers-acent-ashby-2026-03-25-at-125403-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Bulk action options include:
  * Consider for Job


  * Add to Project


  * Set Tags


  * Change Stage


  * This option is hidden when candidates span multiple jobs or have no active applications.


  * Email


  * Email in Sequence


  * Request Review


Additional bulk actions can be found in the More dropdown.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#notifications)Notifications
If users would like to receive notifications via email or Slack for when a candidate review is requested or completed, they can enable these in Personal Settings > Notifications _._ For more on notifications, please check out [Notifications & Notification Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/notifications-and-notification-settings)﻿.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5c1811e6c250672da189af8dca7ea811.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#which-keyboard-shortcuts-are-supported-in-candidat)W**hich keyboard shortcuts are supported in candidate review?**
When reviewing, the reviewer can use the arrow keys on their keyboard (← or →) to move between candidates. They can also use option + L when reviewing a candidate to open their LinkedIn profile (if they have a LinkedIn profile associated with their candidate profile).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#can-limited-access-users-configure-requests-for-ot)Can Limited Access users configure requests for other users to complete?
No, Limited Access users can only complete requests assigned to them and view requests that they have been assigned or have completed.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#can-candidate-reviews-be-set-as-confidential)C**an candidate reviews be set as Confidential?**
Not currently - all candidate reviews will be visible for those with _Elevated Access_ or above within Sourcing > Candidate Reviews.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews#can-a-limited-access-user-view-their-past-reviews)Can a Limited Access user view their past reviews?
They can - they can access all past reviews by clicking the _Past Reviews_ option on their home page.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1162de8173690b4504269f7bfa3a810b.png)
﻿
﻿

<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Report Templates & Core Dashbo...
# Pipeline History
11 min
Copy
This document gives a basic introduction to the topic of historical pipeline reporting and an overview of the Pipeline History template report.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#overview)Overview
![A pipeline history report for all active interview stages across an organization's entire recruiting process.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Gc-5ymh0XDIgLotjFoPEk_pipelinehistory-allapplications.png?format=webp)
﻿
A pipeline history report for all active interview stages across an organization's entire recruiting process.
Ashby’s Pipeline History report allows you to address the question: how many candidates were present in which stages over time? This is achieved by analyzing the full history of your application stage entries and exits and provides an easy way to visualize trends of candidate applications in your process over time.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#tutorial)Tutorial
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#pipeline-history-examples)Pipeline History examples
Example pipeline history for a single job, where application review is optionally shown. As the weeks progress, candidates progress through to the offer stage.
The Pipeline History report can address a few different reporting needs. At the most granular, you may want to report on the history for a specific, individual job. More broadly, you may want to look at departmental, team, or organization-wide pipeline history trends across many jobs and their associated applications in aggregate.
  * Pipeline history for a given job


  * Pipeline history for a group (department, team, entire organization)


  * Pipeline history for a specific stage (e.g. number of candidates in the Offer stage over time)


  * Pipeline history for specific hiring team members (recruiter, HM)


The standard Pipeline History report will group counts by the historical stage group an application was in, but with Ashby, you can optionally group by any field that has historical pertinence. You may want to see your pipeline by important DEI metrics, departmental breakdown, or possibly source.
![Example pipeline history for the job analyzed above, this time focusing on the active stages only \(excluding Application Review\) and grouping by Gender.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dUZnRS9nEOyhu0MFJBQI7_weeklypipelinehistorybygendercustomermanager.png?format=webp)
﻿
Example pipeline history for the job analyzed above, this time focusing on the active stages only (excluding Application Review) and grouping by Gender.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#create-a-pipeline-history-report)Create a pipeline history report
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#build-the-report)Build the report
**From the Template Report Library**
To analyze your pipeline history, we recommend using our Pipeline History template report from our Template Report Library. From your home page, select Reports > Templates > Pipeline History.
![Screen Cast 2022-12-08 at 7.27.06 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/pX4mgy9aUCByCknnaY6Jg_screencast2022-12-08at72706am.gif)
﻿
**From Count over Time report**
You can access the reporting subject Historical Applications when creating a Count over Time report. From your home page, select Reports > Build > Count Over Time. Then change the subject to Historical Applications.
![Screen Cast 2022-12-08 at 7.28.06 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/squ2_UDRF4gWORxJeP-Yw_screencast2022-12-08at72806am.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#filter-your-pipeline-history-report)Filter your Pipeline History report
![The historical application fields for filtering.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/8tAi9OZvdAXNs2KVQJn2R_screenshot2022-12-01at74207pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
The historical application fields for filtering.
  * **Historical Interview Stage Group/Name** : can be used to filter the pipeline history report to include or exclude specific stages or stage groups as needed. For example, selecting a historical stage group of Onsite would filter the pipeline history to show the the weekly count of all applications in the onsite stage.


  * **Historical Interview Stage Type:** allows you to select all stages of a specific category easily (e.g. all lead stages, or only active interview stages).


  * **Was in Stage:** allows selection on the historical time period of interest (e.g. the last 12 weeks, current YTD or last week).


  * **Current Application:** follow this path to find easy reference to all of the current application, candidate and job data fields _as of today._ An example of this may be filtering by all applications of a specific source type.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#group-your-pipeline-history-report)Group your Pipeline History report
The Pipeline History template report defaults to grouping by stage or stage group, depending on your Ashby configuration. The application stage history provides a means of reconstructing all historical counts. Other fields accessed when selecting Current Application are static (e.g. the candidate DEI information or application source). These fields are good candidates for grouping.
![Active pipeline history grouped by Source Type to reveal the composition over time.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/MyK4M93dQ8yeI3FJt1qrs_pipelinehistorybysourcetype.png?format=webp)
﻿
Active pipeline history grouped by Source Type to reveal the composition over time.
Take care when grouping your pipeline history by fields that may not have been preserved historically.
﻿  

However, you are advised to take care when grouping by a Current Application field that it may be liable to change on the application, such as the Job’s Hiring Manager or the Application’s Recruiter. This may result in showing a historical count grouped by a value at the present time that was not the value at a historical point in time.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#is-this-a-snapshot-at-a-specific-time)Is this a _snapshot_ at a specific time?
No. Pipeline history is _not_ a snapshot at a specific point in time. A snapshot approach would capture your candidate pipeline at one point in time (e.g. every Monday at midnight) and be liable to miss any stage presence between that time and the next snapshot. For example, if a candidate’s application was in the Screen stage on Monday when a snapshot was taken, and then was moved through a Technical Screen, an Assessment, and was moved to Onsite, the second snapshot the following week would capture only the Onsite stage, missing the stage transition events over the last week.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#can-an-application-count-more-than-once-in-a-given)Can an application count more than once in a given time period (e.g. week)?
Yes. As a candidate’s application moves through stages, the pipeline history report will count their presence in each stage that they enter or are in during the time interval of interest (e.g. weekly). For example, a candidate with an application that starts in Screen and moves to Technical Assessment within one week would be captured and reflected in the pipeline history report as a unique count in each respective stage when grouped by stage.
If there is no Group By selection applied, an application will count once and only once in the given time interval.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#whats-the-difference-between-historical-applicatio)What’s the difference between _Historical Applications_ and _Current Application_?
Application History refers to the reconstructed stage history of each application. This is a unique set of data which Ashby allows selection and filtering of based on the stage presence at any point in time. The Current Applicationpath references the candidate’s application at the present moment in time. For example, an application will have been at a Screen stage at some point two weeks ago, but at present, its current stage may be Onsite.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pipeline-history#what-is-a-historical-job-consideration)W**hat is a Historical Job Consideration?**
A Historical job consideration is the stage or stage-group the job consideration was in on the given calendar date.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Applications
# Application Review
8 min
Copy
There are two ways to advance and archive candidates who are in the Application Review stage: bulk application review and reviewing via the candidate’s profile. This guide will cover how to use either option to move candidates through your pipeline.
Ashby also has some AI tools to help optimize your process, check out the guides below for more:
﻿[AI-Assisted Application Review](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review)﻿[Candidate Fraud Detection Overview & Admin Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review#overview)Overview
Our bulk application review page allows you to review all candidates in the application review stage, either across all jobs or for any given job. Candidate review allows you to review one-off candidates by navigating to their candidate profiles, leaving feedback and archiving or advancing the candidate accordingly.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review#changing-stages-and-archiving-with-bulk-review)Changing stages and archiving with bulk review
When there are hundreds of applications to review for a single job posting, it's important to be able to do so efficiently. Use our bulk application review feature to quickly organize, annotate, and take action on many applications at once.
﻿
You can navigate to the bulk application review page by heading into a job by heading to Jobs > Jobs. You can then click Application Review above the stages in your active pipeline.
![Application Review stage for a single job](https://cdn.zappy.app/9d49d910bc97a03089d3e018c656fe7e.png)
Application Review stage for a single job
﻿
If you’d like to review applications across all jobs in bulk, you can head to the [Candidate Pipeline, ](https://app.ashbyhq.com/candidates/pipeline/active "Candidate Pipeline, ")then select the Application Review stage of the pipeline.
![Application Review stage on the Candidate Pipeline](https://cdn.zappy.app/a5057c33cc74a1e4df96fa5d0c491f01.png)
Application Review stage on the Candidate Pipeline
﻿
On the Application Review page, you’ll see a list of applicants to review. You can review them in bulk by clicking the Review X Applications button.
![Bulk Application Review button](https://cdn.zappy.app/6ae8d121d00ada33383f63ae14e4ef11.png)
Bulk Application Review button
﻿
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4eec6a64f86b1cb0536178faffc7a347.png)
﻿
**1)** Candidate name
**2)** Role they have applied for
**3)** Tabs to jump between showing all application information, showing only application form answers and showing only the candidate’s resume. **4)** Information added in the application form fields.
**5)** Candidate resume.
**6)** The option to move to the next application.
**7)** The option to specify the order in which applications should be reviewed, either starting with the newest application or the oldest.
**8)** Access to layout settings and [keyboard shortcuts](https://cdn.zappy.app/243dc745ad70397caff30ac897919979.png "keyboard shortcuts").
**9)****Advance:** Advance the candidate to the next stage.
**10) Custom Next Steps…:** Take additional custom action on the application, including rejecting the candidate but adding them to a project, transferring the candidate to be considered for another role, or emailing the candidate.
**11)****Reject:** Reject the candidate - you must select a rejection reason prior to rejecting them.
**12)****Enable Rejection Email:** Slide the toggle on to set up a rejection email - you can also set this email to be scheduled to go out after a specific amount of time has passed. You’ll be prompted to select an email template, a from address and determine whether the email should be scheduled or sent immediately.
**13)****Score:** The overall score for the candidate’s application.
**14)****Tags:** Add tags to the candidate’s profile. Read more at [Tags](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags)﻿﻿
**15)****Notes:** Add notes directly to the candidate’s profile.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review#changing-stages-and-archiving-on-the-candidate-pro)Changing stages and archiving on the candidate profile
When you want to review and advance or archive an individual candidate, you can do so from their candidate profile.
﻿
  1. To review individual candidates, navigate to the candidate profile using [candidate search](https://app.ashbyhq.com/candidate-searches/new "candidate search") or finding them in the [candidate pipeline](https://app.ashbyhq.com/candidates/pipeline/active "candidate pipeline")﻿


  1. Click on the Review button on their Application Review activity to add feedback on their application and/or submit a decision.


  1. You can also click Change Stage and select the stage you'd like to move them to from their profile if you’d like to advance them.


  1. To archive a candidate, you can also click Archive, select an archive reason, and optionally email the candidate a rejection email.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c69ec5a19e830c01ddfccbe9e6a3c567.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review#when-using-the-bulk-application-review-mode-can-yo)When using the bulk application review mode, can you see if the candidate applied for another role?
Yes! If a candidate has applied for multiple roles, you’ll be able to see their other applications in bulk application review. You can also view all jobs the candidate is being considered for in the Considered For Jobs section on their profile.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review#which-feedback-form-is-used-when-reviewing-applica)Which feedback form is used when reviewing application forms? Can I change the feedback form for application reviews?
The default feedback form is used for feedback at the Application Review stage. If you’d like to change the feedback form, this would need to be done for each job or job template. Navigate to Jobs > Jobs and select the job in question. Then head to the Interview Plan tab. Click Modify on the Review Application activity on the interview plan.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/801d76e10a5b8f09082498e3fc3ef1da.png)
﻿
You can click _Change Link_ to then switch the feedback form out for another shared feedback form you have configured, or click _Unlink & Edit_ to make specific edits to that form. Any changes made to an unlinked form will only apply to that specific activity and will not have an impact on the default feedback form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/135fbfeb0fde09fe350b798d52f75d60.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review#what-is-the-difference-between-in-stage-since-and)What is the difference between In Stage Since and Needs Decision in the Status column of Application review?
Needs Decision means there’s a structured activity in the stage (for example, a custom review application activity) and action is required, while In Stage since is when a candidate is in a stage that doesn’t have a structured activity and so a call to action isn’t required.
**Please note:** If you are using the default review application activity in the Application Review stage of your interview plan, this is not seen as a structured activity. If you opt to add a custom review application activity, this will be seen as a structured activity.
﻿

candidate-files-and-file-categories:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-files-and-file-categories -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Candidates
# Candidate Files and File Categories
8 min
Copy
Additional files, like the candidate’s cover letter can be added to the candidate’s profile directly. This guide will walk through how to enable and create file categories, how to assign file categories to file upload questions on an application form and also how a user can assign a category when uploading files manually to a candidate profile within Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-files-and-file-categories#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-files-and-file-categories#adding-file-categories)Adding file categories
To enable file categories:
  * Navigate to Admin, then Organization Setup and File Categories.


  * Enable the Enable File Categories option.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/66d1eae6874e079d872990783b61ba6d.png)
﻿
Some file categories will be available on enabling, including the following:
  * Cover Letter


  * Other


  * Reference Letter


  * Transcript


  * Writing Sample


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fe6779d07f845021ad23b959aedaa62c.png)
﻿
To edit an existing file category, click the three dots on the right-hand side of the category, then select Edit. You’ll also have the option to archive the category so it isn’t available to assign to a file when uploading it to a candidate profile moving forward.
If the file category’s status is Inactive, then clicking the three dots will give you the option to restore the category.
To create a new file category, click + New. You’ll then be able to give you category a name. Click Save to confirm and create your new file category.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ec861303bc74ec550ab3345fc283a737.png)
﻿
On saving your new category, it will automatically be set as active.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/50f6fcf3883b1f6262014e9f440307ee.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-files-and-file-categories#adding-file-upload-questions-to-forms)Adding file upload questions to forms
If you’d like to give the candidate the option to upload files as a part of their application, you can add a file question to an application form. More on configuring these at [Application Forms](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-forms#create-application-forms-in-admin-recommended "Application Forms").
File upload questions support files up to 50MB in standard document, image, and video file types such as:
  * **Documents:** PDF, DOC, DOCX


  * **Images:** JPG, JPEG, PNG


  * **Videos:** Common video formats such as MP4


Executable file types (such as .exe) and certain text-based formats (such as .md) are not supported. If a candidate needs to submit content in an unsupported format, we recommend converting the file to a supported document type, such as PDF, before uploading.
When creating your question, if you have file categories configured, a Save to Profile? checkbox will be available. If you check this checkbox, then the file uploaded via the application form will automatically be added to the Other Files section within the Summary tab of the candidate’s profile.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fb4bbb3e8ecb69b2797286fddce72a18.png)
﻿
On checking the checkbox, you will be able to specify a file category to put the uploaded file under.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ca946a1a72482f9ebbb3a88e88d153de.png)
﻿
If you do not have file categories enabled, or opt to not check the Save to Profile? checkbox, then the uploaded file can be found within the Application activity on the candidate’s feed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4f6e717d008df1b7920a7dd77ebe4a4c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-files-and-file-categories#uploading-a-file-to-the-candidates-profile)Uploading a file to the candidate’s profile
To upload a form to the candidate’s profile, head to the Summary section, then to the Other Files section.
Click + Add, then select the file you’d like to upload. If you have file categories enabled, you will be able to confirm the file category the uploaded file should be added to and also add in an internal file description.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d7474b65208207fce1bae714c214c3c8.png)
﻿
You will also be able to set the file as public (so visible to all users with access to the candidate profile) or private (so only visible to those with Organization Admin or Elevated Access - Admin Private access to the job, department, or location the job consideration is linked to).
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/25da21152be8b016c0bcd9d4d7eea06e.png)
﻿
﻿

Ashby Admin Onboarding
# Agency Onboarding Guide
10 min
Copy
This onboarding guide is for recruiting agencies setting up their Ashby account. For companies looking to add agency users to their Ashby account, check out [Agencies Setup](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agencies-setup)﻿.
Welcome to Ashby! We’re so excited to help you get started and hit those goals!
As an agency, you’re likely to set up and use Ashby in a rather different way from companies hiring for their own teams. This guide will take you through the key steps to get your account setup, along with some more advanced features that you can implement. Let’s do this!
For a general overview of Ashby, check out our [101 Webinar](https://ashby.ewebinar.com/webinar/ashby-university-101-17218?session=replay&showform=1 "101 Webinar").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide#set-up-basic-integrations-and-your-job-board-theme)Set up basic integrations and your job board theme.
This section covers the basic technical setup to get everything working. This section should take less than 20 minutes to complete.
  * Firstly, we'd recommend setting up your integration with either [Google Workspace](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/google-workspace)﻿ or [Microsoft 365](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/microsoft-365)﻿ so you and your team can use all of Ashby's features.


  * ﻿[Job Board: Configuring Your Setup](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-board-configuring-your-setup)﻿


  * Set up the [Zoom](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/zoom)﻿ integration (if your team uses Zoom)


Looking to integrate with other software? Check out the [Integrations Marketplace](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/marketplace "Integrations Marketplace") and our [Integrations](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/LjC0h-TOLOefjI3YhqyB9)﻿ guides for further information. **Note:** Most integrations must be set up by an Admin user of the partner’s platform. Some job board integrations like [Wellfound (formerly AngelList Talent)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/wellfound-formerly-angellist-talent)﻿ must be set up on the partner’s end.
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide#add-and-open-client-jobs-for-applications)Add and open client jobs for applications
This section covers how to add jobs and job-specific information, like the department and location information. Agencies usually set up the client company as a department, with respective functions (Engineering, Sales, etc) as a sub-team. We would recommend having a job description ready to speed up the setup process.
  * ﻿[Set up departments & locations ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide "Set up departments & locations ")to assign to your jobs


  * Create [Interview Plans ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide "Interview Plans ")to establish clear stages within each job's pipeline


  * Open and post your first [Jobs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/l-NcIsZrB9ztdr4U3idZr)﻿


  * Want to test your job? [Create test candidates and test out the workflow](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/create-test-candidates-and-test-out-the-workflow)﻿


**Note:** The [Indeed/Glassdoor](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/marketplace/indeed-glassdoor "Indeed/Glassdoor")integration is enabled by default. Please disable it while you’re setting up your jobs to ensure test postings aren’t listed.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide#setting-up-re-usable-templates-in-admin)Setting up re-usable templates in Admin.
You can create templates to help establish job pipelines in advance and create a clear email tone for candidate outreach. **We highly recommend setting these up in advance to save time in the long run.**
  * ﻿[Interview Plans ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide "Interview Plans ")allow you to determine the pipeline stages across jobs in advance.


  * ﻿[Feedback Forms and Interviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide "Feedback Forms and Interviews"): Ensure that you have structured feedback forms and interviews in place for a consistent experience for candidates and interviewers.


  * ﻿[Email Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management)﻿ speed up candidate communication and ensures consistency.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide#tools-for-sourcing-and-outreach)Tools for sourcing and outreach
This section covers the different tools we have in place for reaching out to leads, adding them into Ashby and taking action on them. Handy for all your sourcing needs!
  * Install the [Chrome Extension](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension)﻿ to easily add and take action on leads via LinkedIn, Gmail and GitHub


  * Create [Sequences](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences)﻿ to engage potential candidates and gauge their interest


  * Looking to import candidates into Ashby? Check out the [Bulk Import Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options)﻿ to speedily add your data to Ashby


If you’d prefer to integrate with sourcing tools you currently use, our full list of integration guides for sourcing tools can be found [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide "here").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide#job-status-and-reporting)Job Status and Reporting
Pull data and export reports to share with your clients to keep them informed on job pipelines.
You can also check out the following guides to further acquaint yourself with Ashby's reporting capabilities:
  * ﻿[Ashby Analytics: Key Reporting Concepts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Report Catalog: Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates)﻿


  * ﻿[Ashby Subject & Field Definitions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions)﻿


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide#onboard-your-team)Onboard your team
Share the following guides with your team to help them get started with Ashby! Your team members will also be able to select their role in our in-app widget for additional guidance.
  * ﻿[Getting Started for Hiring Managers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers)﻿


  * ﻿[Getting Started for Interviewers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-interviewers)﻿


  * ﻿[Getting Started for Recruiters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-recruiters)﻿


  * ﻿[Getting Started for Recruiting Coordinators](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-recruiting-coordinators)﻿


  * ﻿[Getting Started for Sourcers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-sourcers)﻿


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agency-onboarding-guide#some-additional-recommendations)Some Additional Recommendations
Hooray! You’ve completed this onboarding guide. Find below some additional tools that we’d recommend checking out.
  * ﻿[Public Booking Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/public-booking-links)﻿


  * ﻿[Meeting Hours](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours)﻿


  * ﻿[Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿


  * ﻿[Surveys & Questionnaires](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/surveys-and-questionnaires)﻿

Organization Setup
# Custom Fields
43 min
Copy
Custom fields allow you to add additional fields to a candidate’s profile, a job, an offer, or an opening that you and other users can fill out, allowing for a further level of customization.
Candidate, Job and Project custom fields can be configured on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#custom-fields-overview)Custom Fields Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-custom-fields-object-types)Creating Custom Fields: Object Types
Custom fields can be associated with six primary object types in Ashby.  
| Object Type  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Candidate  | These fields are used to capture details specific to individual candidates. They appear on the Summary tab of the candidate profile.  |  
| Job  | These fields are used to capture details specific to individual jobs. They appear on the Settings page of a job.  |  
| Offer  | These fields are used to capture details about an offer. They appear on the offer activity that appears when a candidate is in the offer stage.  |  
| Opening  | These fields are used to capture details specific to individual openings. They appear at the bottom of the opening form.  |  
| Projects  | These fields are used to capture details specific to projects﻿. The custom fields will show within the _Settings_ section of the project.  |  
| Employee  | These fields are used to add additional information to user profiles on the _Employees_ page. Custom fields for employees﻿ can be access on the _Basic Info_ tab.  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-custom-fields-candidate-custom-fields)Creating Custom Fields: Candidate Custom Fields
When adding in a custom field for candidates, the new field will be added to the Summary tab of their profiles, ready for you to fill out. Newly created custom fields will be retroactively added to existing candidate profiles, as well as new ones.
Candidate custom fields can be used for adding information like preferred name, full legal name, pronouns and more!
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#field-types)Field Types  
| Field Type  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Yes/No  | Toggle yes or no  |  
| Short Answer  | Input a single line of text  |  
| Date  | Select a single date  |  
| Multiple Choice  | Select a radio button  |  
| Checkboxes  | Select one or more checkboxes  |  
| Number  | Input a single numeric value  |  
| Currency  | Determine a currency or select a default currency  |  
| Long Unformatted Answer  | Input multiple lines of text  |  
| URL  | Add a URL into this field  |  
| Employee  | A single-select dropdown showing all employees in your Ashby instance  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#create-a-candidate-custom-field)Create a candidate custom field
Ensuring you are on the candidate custom field view, click + New in the top right corner of the page. 
![The custom fields page open on the Candidate tab](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/m7HDdskYRFUzea2qeYTmB_custom-fields-acent-ashby-2026-05-20-at-41012-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
A configuration form will appear with the following options:
  * **Title** : Name your field.


  * **Description** : Optionally, add a description that appears below the title.


  * **Field Type** (see options above)


Once you’ve created the custom field, you will see additional options when editing the custom field.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/80UBv-wt7RBRFVSs0Cppe-20260324-113009.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Is Private?** : If checked, only those with Elevated Access or Organization Admin access can view the field. This could be used for fields with sensitive information like candidate salary expectations.


  * **Substitution Token:** Create a token to pull data from this field into an offer letter or e-signature document. If you opt to generate a token for a multiple choice custom field, you will get a follow up option _Customize Substitution Text_. More on this [below](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿.


  * **Enable in Email Template?** : If checked, the data can be included in emails. A token will be created that you can add to email templates to pull this information over. More on this at [Substitution Tokens](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens)﻿.


  * **Show in Chrome Extension?** : If checked, the field will be accessible in the Chrome extension and within Ashby.


The options Enable in Email Template? and Show in Chrome Extension? are only available for candidate custom fields.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#find-candidate-custom-fields-on-the-candidate-prof)Find candidate custom fields on the candidate profile
Once created, candidate custom fields will appear on the Summary tab of the candidate profile. These fields can also be used:
  * In [email templates](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-templates "email templates") as custom tokens


  * In the [interview briefing](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interview-briefing-settings "interview briefing") as custom viewable fields


  * In searches and reports


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a0b51e5b0c6625ef3e5b70b43d966411.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-custom-fields-job-custom-fields)Creating Custom Fields: Job Custom Fields
Job custom fields can be created to add additional internal information about a job. Just as with a candidate, you can fill in the field title, add a description and pick a field type for data input. With a job custom field, you have the additional field type option of a number range.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#field-types-1)Field Types  
| Field Type  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Yes/No  | Toggle yes or no  |  
| Short Answer  | Input a single line of text  |  
| Date  | Select a single date  |  
| Multiple Choice  | Select a radio button  |  
| Checkboxes  | Select one or more checkboxes  |  
| Number  | Input a single numeric value  |  
| Currency  | Determine a currency or select a default currency  |  
| Number Range  | A range of numbers with a lower and upper bound  |  
| Long Unformatted Answer  | Input multiple lines of text  |  
| URL  | Add a URL into this field  |  
| Employee  | A single-select dropdown showing all employees in your Ashby instance  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#create-a-job-custom-field)Create a job custom field
Ensuring you are on the job custom field view, click + New in the top right corner of the page to open up the New Custom field menu.
![The custom fields page open on the Job tab](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RTuviInFfkVaJzTHG4xt3_custom-fields-acent-ashby-2026-05-20-at-40744-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
A configuration form will appear with the following options:
  * **Title** : Name your field.


  * **Description:** Optionally, add a description that appears below the title.


  * **Field Type:** Determine the type of information that should be added to this field.


  * **Is Required?:** If checked, the job cannot be opened or moved out of it’s draft state unless this field is completed.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/mSilOIcx4Q0gOW3X3MQjw-20260324-113642.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When editing the custom field, you will see additional options.
  * **Is Private?** : If checked, only those with Elevated Access or Organization Admin access can view the field.


  * **Require Reapproval?:** If the value in this field is changed and you have an approval process in place for the job, reapproval will be required.


  * **Substitution Token** Create a token to pull data from this field into an offer letter or e-signature document. If you opt to generate a token for a multiple choice custom field, you will get a follow up option Customize Substitution Text. More on this [below](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿.


  * **Restrict which jobs include this field?:** Here you can set criteria using filters. Only the jobs meeting your set criteria will have this custom field.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#find-job-custom-fields-on-the-jobs-settings)Find job custom fields on the jobs’ settings
Once created, the custom field will show in the Settings tab within a job. Newly added custom fields will show for all jobs, regardless of when the job was created. These fields can be used:
  * In searches and reports


  * To record job-specific requirements or preferences


If you try to open a job without completing required job custom fields, you will see a Missing Value message when amending the job status.
A draft job can be created and amended without completing the required fields.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/487f8a6cdfc5efc3e917236572e9d756.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7208e866530dd1d71d3ff3e4d24c802e.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-custom-fields-offer-custom-fields)Creating Custom Fields: Offer Custom Fields
Ensuring you are on the offer custom field view, click + New in the top right corner of the page to open up the New Custom field menu.
![The custom fields page open on the Offer tab](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/AxJz_RBmF3Pg8hXz1VV7M_custom-fields-acent-ashby-2026-05-20-at-41132-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Offer custom fields can be added to an offer to collect additional information from a candidate. Optionally, these fields can be set up as tokens to automatically populate information in the candidate's offer letter. For more information on creating offer letters, visit [Offers and Offer Letters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#field-types-2)Field Types  
| Field Type  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Yes/No  | Toggle yes or no  |  
| Short Answer  | Input a single line of text  |  
| Date  | Select a single date  |  
| Multiple Choice  | Select a radio button  |  
| Checkboxes  | Select one or more checkboxes  |  
| Number  | Input a single numeric value  |  
| Currency  | Determine a currency or select a default currency  |  
| Long Unformatted Answer  | Input multiple lines of text  |  
| URL  | Add a URL into this field  |  
| Employee  | A single-select dropdown showing all employees in your Ashby instance  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#create-an-offer-custom-field)Create an offer custom field
  * **Title** : Name your field.


  * **Description** : Optionally, add a description that appears below the title.


  * **Field Type** (see options above)


  * Depending on type selected, an options panel may appear.


  * **Is Required?** : Determine if the field must be filled out.


Once you’ve created the custom field, you’ll have additional options when editing it too:
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/YYME6htvF2V8uLLyiWEao_custom-fields-acent-ashby-2026-03-24-at-111849-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Instructions:** If instructions are provided on the offer custom field, this show to team members when they fill out a candidate's offer (instead of showing the custom field's description). These instructions can also be set with rich text formatting, which will also show when filling out the candidate's offer.


  * **Require Reapproval** : If the field's data is changed, the offer must be re-approved.


  * **Substitution token** : Create a token to pull data from this field into an offer letter or e-signature document. If you opt to generate a token for a multiple choice custom field, you will get a follow up option _Customize Substitution Text_. More on this [below](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#multiple-choice-custom-fields-customize-substituti)﻿.


  * **Default Value:** Determines the default value to use when this field is used in a fillable form. Please note that the default value can only be pulled from a custom field with the same field type (for example, Multiple Choice in this case), or can be set to be a static value instead.


  * **Hide this fields if a default is chosen?** If a default value is found using the Default Value option above, hide this field in the offer form.


  * **Make this field non-editable?** If a default value is selected, then the field cannot be overridden manually within the offer details.


  * **Restrict which offer forms include this field?** : Specify which jobs or departments should display this custom field on offers.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#find-offer-custom-fields-on-the-candidate-profile)Find offer custom fields on the candidate profile
When you click Create Offer on the profile for a candidate in the Offer stage, the field will show on the Create New Offer pop-up. These fields can be used:
  * To capture specific offer details or terms.


  * To replace fields in .docx, .pdf, and Dropbox Sign e-signature requests


  * To record special instructions or notes related to the offer.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/affd41b596e5fe617f6844a8a0809fa7.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#multiple-choice-custom-fields-customize-substituti)Multiple Choice Custom Fields: Customize Substitution Text
If you opt to generate a token for a multiple choice custom field, you’ll have the option to customize the text added by the token to your offer letter or e-signature document, depending on the choice selected for your custom field.
  * Once you’ve generated the token within the Substitution Token section of the custom field, click Customize Substitution Text to open up the Customize Substitution Text window.


  * The window will show each option that can be selected for your multiple-choice custom field.


  * Select an option from the Options list, then add in the text that should replace the substitution token within the offer letter or e-signature document when that option is selected within the offer details.


  * For example, if Full-Time is selected for the offer in this case, the token will be replaced by ‘full-time employment’ if added to the offer letter template.


  * If you don’t want the token to be replaced by text for specific options, toggle on the Replace with no text option. This option removed the token from the document if added and will not add in any substitute text in it’s place.


  * Click Save to save any amendments made to the option substitution text. Make sure to save amendments to each option as you go to avoid losing your changes.


  * The number of custom text options set for each option will be listed within the Customize Substitution Text button for your reference. So here, text options have been set for two out of the four options available.


﻿

﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/15ce86c6460f161ff789fc07138920e2.png)

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-custom-fields-project-custom-fields)Creating Custom Fields: Project Custom Fields
Now you can add custom fields specifically for your projects﻿. You can use these to specify and house information in your project’s Settings page. You can show these custom fields as additional columns when viewing projects or filter by custom fields as needed.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#field-types-3)Field Types  
| Field Type   | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Yes/No  | Toggle yes or no  |  
| Short Answer  | Input a single line of text  |  
| Date  | Select a single date  |  
| Multiple Choice  | Select a radio button  |  
| Checkboxes  | Select one or more checkboxes  |  
| Number  | Input a single numeric value  |  
| Long Unformatted Answer  | Input multiple lines of text  |  
| URL  | Add a URL into this field  |  
| Employee  | A single-select dropdown showing all employees in your Ashby instance  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-a-project-custom-field)Creating a Project Custom Field
To create a custom field for your projects, head to [Custom Fields](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/custom-fields/project "Custom Fields") within Admin, from there, click the Projects tab. Click New to create a new custom field.
![The custom fields page open on the Project tab](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/XyX6JrazQBlKqDyZz0WEN_custom-fields-acent-ashby-2026-05-20-at-41416-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When creating a new custom field, you’ll be asked to add the following;
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Vul-zNX7OMtyrc_xDcd-S-20260324-114032.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Title:** The name of your field, used for filtering and the header if you add a column for this custom field to your project.


  * **Description:** A description that will show internally to clarify the information to be added to this custom field.


  * **Field Type:** Specify the format of the information added to this field.


  * **Is Required?:** Does this information need to be included?


  * **Show in Project List by Default?** Check this if the custom field should be shown as a column when viewing your project list.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#completing-project-custom-fields)Completing Project Custom Fields
To add information to the custom fields on a project, navigate to Sourcing, then [Projects](https://app.ashbyhq.com/projects "Projects"). Click on the project in question, then click Settings _._ Your custom fields will be listed under the Basic Info section.
If a custom field is set as Required, you’ll see a warning message below an empty field requesting that you select an option or complete the field.
If a required field was previously filled and the information is removed, when navigating to another field, you’ll see a message prompting you to complete the field. If you navigate away from the page leaving the field empty, the field will revert to containing the previously entered information.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9accece5ab9af6987a521ffe3d880016.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2ad466819099ffe5b4c5896d2da11129.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#filtering-by-a-project-custom-field)Filtering by a Project Custom Field
To filter by a project’s custom field, ensure that the Advanced toggle is on for filtering. From there, click Add Field to Match to select the custom field you’d like to filter by.
Once the filter has been selected you can then specify the field information you’d like to use to find specific projects.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bab601450ec803dc39718d8177b1afc7.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e6c0c6fa8489fcc6a42a97f19cef26e5.png)
﻿

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-custom-fields-opening-custom-fields)Creating Custom Fields: Opening Custom Fields
﻿Openings﻿ custom fields can be created to add additional internal information about an opening. Just as with a job, you can fill in the field title, add a description and pick a field type for data input. With an opening custom field, you have the additional field type option of a compensation range. Learn more about openings in [Openings Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#field-types-4)Field Types  
| Field Type  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Yes/No  | Toggle yes or no  |  
| Short Answer  | Input a single line of text  |  
| Date  | Select a single date  |  
| Multiple Choice  | Select a radio button  |  
| Checkboxes  | Select one or more checkboxes  |  
| Number  | Input a single numeric value  |  
| Compensation Range  | A compensation range and interval  |  
| Number Range  | A range of numbers with a lower and upper bound  |  
| Long Unformatted Answer  | Input multiple lines of text  |  
| URL  | Add a URL into this field  |  
| Employee  | A single-select dropdown showing all employees in your Ashby instance  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#create-an-opening-custom-field)Create an opening custom field
Ensuring you are on the opening custom field view, click + New in the top right corner of the page. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/eSRg7TNcyHAMmLpQlheUV_custom-fields-acent-ashby-2026-05-20-at-41333-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
A configuration form will appear with the following options:
  * **Title** : Name your field.


  * **Description** : Optionally, add a description that appears below the title.


  * **Field Type:** Determine the type of custom field you’d like to configure.


  * **Is Required?** : If checked, an opening cannot be moved from it’s draft state unless the required field is completed.


When editing the custom field, you will see additional options:
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/e-NPKr__55ELdo6B48CHV-20260324-114945.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Substitution Token:** Click the Click to Generate Token button to create a substitution token for this custom field.


  * **Restrict which openings include this field?** Set criteria here to determine the openings that should include this field.


You can edit default opening custom fields to set them as required. You will not be able to set criteria to limit the jobs that include default fields.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#find-opening-custom-fields-on-openings)Find opening custom fields on openings
Once created, the custom fields will appear at the bottom of the opening form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7a6a9ab907237ab9e1f4d315e9ef8e44.png)
﻿
When clicking the Draft tab below the opening ID to adjust the opening status, if any required fields aren’t completed, you will see a Missing Value message. A draft opening can be created and amended without the required fields being completed. The Missing Value message will only be shown when the opening status is changed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9b400fdbc7d520f36d21270babae4230.png)
﻿

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#creating-custom-fields-archive-detail-custom-field)Creating Custom Fields: Archive Detail Custom Fields
You can now create custom fields so you can capture additional data when archiving or rejecting a candidate in Ashby. Please note that this option is only available on the Plus and Enterprise plans.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#field-types-6)Field Types  
| Field Type  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Yes/No  | Toggle yes or no  |  
| Short Answer  | Input a single line of text  |  
| Date  | Select a single date  |  
| Multiple Choice  | Select a radio button  |  
| Checkboxes  | Select one or more checkboxes  |  
| Number  | Input a single numeric value  |  
| Currency  | Determine a currency or select a default currency  |  
| Long Unformatted Answer  | Input multiple lines of text  |  
| URL  | Add a URL into this field  |  
| Employee  | A single-select dropdown showing all employees in your Ashby instance  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#create-an-archive-detail-custom-field)Create an archive detail custom field
Ensuring you are on the Archive Detail custom field view, click + New in the top right corner of the page. 
![The custom fields page open on the Archive Detail tab](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/2yB3tigiYZIlXpntRQeOd_archive-detail-custom-fields.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
A configuration form will appear with the following options:
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/nWaa300smoyup5lqRsVvd-20260324-115550.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Title** : Name your field.


  * **Description** : Optionally, add a description that appears below the title.


  * **Field Type** (see options above)


  * Depending on type selected, an options panel may appear.


  * **Is Required?** Determine whether a value must be added to this field.


On editing an existing archive detail custom field, you’ll see some additional options.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/DKrucUx47inbYlvmz4Pth-20260324-115712.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Is Private?:** Determine whether or not all users can see this field, or if only those with Organization Admin or Elevated Access - Admin Private access can see this field.


  * **Restrict which archive forms include this field?** If you check this box you can set criteria for when this field should appear when archiving a candidate. For example, you could opt to add a filter based on archive reason, so if a candidate gets a competing offer, you can add further details for your reference.


Click + Add Field to Match to select the field to filter by, then specify the value or values for your criteria.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#find-archive-detail-fields-when-archiving-a-candid)Find archive detail fields when archiving a candidate.
If you click Archive on a candidate’s job consideration to reject them, you archive details custom fields will show on the archive form.
If you have restricted the archive forms that include your custom fields, only those that meet your criteria will display your created fields.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fada82013fbab9f160ac24487abe1410.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#listing-archive-form-details-in-custom-list-report)Listing archive form details in custom list reports
Once you’ve completed the archive form, you can run reports using your archive detail custom fields to view this information. Your archive detail custom fields can either be displayed on a report (as fields to show) or as filters on an report.
For example, you could build a custom list report, filtering for archived candidates, then opt to add your archive detail custom fields as fields to show so this information is easily accessible.
Navigate to Reports > Build Reports > Custom List.
**Subject:** Job Considerations
**Time Field & Interview:** Job Consideration’s Started at to show [insert your chosen time range here] per [set your interval here].
**Filter:** Job Consideration’s Outcome is _Archived_.
**Fields to Show:** Job Consideration’s Candidate, Job Consideration’s Job, any archive reason custom fields you’d like to include. You can also opt to include information like the furthest stage group reached and the job consideration’s archive reason.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bdc7c013153b82b8163b7c1eaa554988.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#i-cant-view-a-custom-field-i-should-see-what-do-i)I c**an't view a custom field I should see. What do I do?**
Check with an Organization Admin to see if the Is Private option is enabled for that field. They can either disable it or adjust your permissions.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#how-do-i-archive-or-restore-a-custom-field)How do I archive or restore a custom field?
You can archive or restore a custom field when editing it. Click the Archive/Restore button in the top right corner of the field to take the appropriate action.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#why-cant-i-archive-certain-custom-fields)W**hy can't I archive certain custom fields?**
Default custom fields (e.g., Reason for Outreach) cannot be archived. Check for the Default tag when editing them to identify default fields.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#how-do-i-sort-my-fields)H**ow do I sort my fields?**
Fields will appear in the same order they appear on the Custom Fields page in Admin.
If you’re using a multiple choice or checkbox field, the options are able to be sorted alphabetically and reverse alphabetically.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#can-i-show-information-added-to-a-candidate-custom)Can I show information added to a candidate custom field to an interviewer on their interview briefing?
You can, yes. Head to Interviewer Briefing Info within Admin. From there, you’ll be able to select the fields you’d like to show to your interviewing panel when they look at the interview briefing.
Want to get this information from the candidate’s application form? Check out our guide on [Form Field Connectors](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors)﻿ to find out how to link application form fields to candidate custom fields.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#can-i-import-data-into-custom-fields-when-importin)Can I import data into custom fields when importing via the bulk CSV importer?
You can, yes. Custom fields can be selected at the mapping stage. More information on custom fields can be found in our [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿ guide.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#i-have-a-lot-of-custom-fields-what-filtering-optio)I **have a lot of custom fields - what filtering options are available?**
When filtering custom fields, you can filter by status (Active or Archived), you can filter by field type (e.g Yes/No, Short Answer) or you can filter by field source (e.g a custom offer field syncing over from an integration HRIS).
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1c39f65151cb9b1e0b614157ec3df664.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields#can-i-use-archive-detail-custom-fields-to-migrate)C**an I use archive detail custom fields to migrate and map Greenhouse rejection questions?**
Yes, Greenhouse rejection questions will be mapped as archive detail custom fields in Ashby during migration. If you need to have these remapped, please reach out to your CSM or support@ashbyhq.com.
﻿

Ashby Analytics
