[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Getting Started with Analytics
# Saved Reports & Dashboards
17 min
Copy
If you have a large number of saved reports and dashboards, the Saved Reports layout will allow you to filter and locate your saved content easily.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#overview)Overview
When navigating to Reports > Saved Reports or Dashboards > Saved Dashboards, you can filter, create saved views and search for specific reports or dashboards by title. If you’d like, you can also create topics to group your reports by.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#saved-reports-recruiting-plans-dashboards)Saved Reports, Recruiting Plans & Dashboards
With Saved Reports, you’ll be able to jump between saved reports, recruiting plans and dashboards with ease using the Reports, Recruiting Plans and Dashboard tabs.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/29b5287edee22a9875d93154aebe9aad.png)
﻿
Reports: View all saved reports that you have access to. The table will list the title of the report, when the report was last updated, any dashboards the report has been added to and the author. Click New Report to create a new report from this view.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3c0f476bc2a7f28d53fa974e83945faf.png)
﻿
﻿  

Recruiting Plans: View all Recruiting Plan reports here. More on these at Recruiting Planner. By default the table will list the recruiting plan title, when it was last updated, any topics and dashboard the plan is linked to and the author of the recruiting plan report. Click New Recruiting Plan to create a new recruiting plan report from this view.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/533e11d871c8f48ba340b9afe8e55b1d.png)
﻿
﻿  

Dashboards: View all of the dashboards you have access to here. By default the table will list the dashboard title, when the dashboard was last updated and the author. Click New Dashboard to create a new dashboard from this view.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/abc986408c1958d0883de479c37c7732.png)
﻿
﻿  

If you’d like to add additional columns of data to your tables, click the Add Column button. You will then be able to select additional fields to add to the table display.
To hide columns already on display, click the button with the column image and switch off the fields that you’d like to hide.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fd3f4884d9e0be6c45680da8994aa29e.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ac31f8815c72f246ca10462e5f8cba90.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#saved-content-filters-and-views)Saved Content Filters and Views
You can now add filters and create views for your saved content to quickly find the data you need. Both basic and advanced filtering options are available.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#filters)Filters
_Basic Filtering_
If you have the Advanced toggle off when filtering your saved content, you can add a filter by clicking + Filter.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3fea472db4403899f9dc3789bc603a95.png)
﻿
_Advanced Filtering_
If you have the Advanced toggle on when filtering your saved content, you can add a filter by clicking + Add Field to Match, then selecting the field in question.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8f993231be51a877ebc80ee1d45601de.png)
﻿
You can filter via the following fields for saved reports:  
| Field  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Author  | The user who created the report  |  
| Type  | The type of report  |  
| Title  | The title of the report  |  
| Topics  | The topics associated with the report  |  
| Dashboards  | The dashboards that this report is on  |  
| Last Updated  | The date and time the report was last updated  |  
| Has Alerts  | Whether the report has any alerts configured for it  |  
| Access Level  | The access level of the report*  |  
| Is Access Restricted  | Whether access to the report is restricted to a specific set of users  |  
| Locked By  | The user who locked the report  |  
| Accessible to User  | Allows filtering reports by whether a given user has access to them  |  
You can filter via the following fields for saved recruiting plans:  
| Field  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Author  | The user who created the recruiting plan  |  
| Title  | The title of the recruiting plan  |  
| Topics  | The topics associated with the recruiting plan  |  
| Number of Targets  | The number of targets in this recruiting plan  |  
| Dashboards  | The dashboards that this recruiting plan is on  |  
| Last Updated  | The date and time the recruiting plan was last updated  |  
| Start Date  | The start date of the recruiting plan’s date range  |  
| End Date  | The end date of the recruiting plan’s date range  |  
You can filter via the following fields for saved dashboards:  
| Field  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Accessible to User  | Allows filtering dashboards by whether a given user has access to them  |  
| Access Level  | The access level of the dashboard*  |  
| Is Access Restricted  | Whether access to the dashboard is restricted to a specific set of users  |  
| Has Scheduled Deliveries  | Whether the dashboard has any scheduled deliveries configured  |  
| Topics  | The topics associated with the dashboard  |  
| Author  | The user who created the dashboard  |  
| Title  | The title of the dashboard  |  
| Updated At  | The date and time the dashboard was last updated  |  
*Saved report and dashboard access levels are as follows: Private: Only accessible to the author Public: Accessible to all elevated access users Private & Shared: Accessible to the author and at least one other user
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#saved-views)Saved Views
Once you’ve added the appropriate filters, you can save the view you’ve created by clicking Save As New. Give your filter view a name and then click Create.
This will add a new tab within the filters section that you can easily click to view the filtered view in the future.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/158112ea2c8814ce583df3fbd7c4f6c1.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/da23991981c850612f70f8c9bd4e523d.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#topics)Topics
Topics allow you to group your reports by theme so they can be easily filtered as needed. Only those with Organization Admin access can create, edit or delete topics.
How to create a topic:
  1. Navigate to Reports > Saved Reports or Dashboards > Saved Dashboards


  1. Click Create Topic to create your first topic. Once you’ve created your first topic, hover over All Topics on the topic list, then click the + icon to add an additional topic


  1. You will be prompted to give your topic a name. You can also assign your topic to a parent topic if you are creating a new sub-topic. If you opt to not assign a parent topic, then the created topic will be treated as a top-level topic


  1. Click Create Topic


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f9dcdf1387fc25dde366a5712221d422.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e3335ef331972249d197b9fa5e4e0362.png)
﻿
How to assign a topic to a report, recruiting plan or dashboard:
  1. Navigate to the report, recruiting plan or dashboard


  1. Click on the topics button underneath the saved content’s title (if you haven’t assign a topic to the report yet, this will show as No topics)


  1. Select the topic from the list to assign it to the report, recruiting plan or dashboard


  1. Once a topic is added, it will be listed in the topics button in place of No topics


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/81ebdc640c736ecaca830730d6792d1a.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2774f43cdc0d7a9cb93509cb4efc5dbc.png)
﻿
Alternatively, if you have the Topics column in the table view of your saved reports, recruiting plans or dashboards, you can over over the Topics field for the report in question and click the pencil icon to assign and amend the topics linked to the report or dashboard in question.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/44624f310270241e686695de9b2d8729.png)
﻿
﻿  

How to unassign a topic from a report, recruiting plan or dashboard:
  1. Navigate to the report, recruiting plan or dashboard


  1. Click on the topics button underneath the saved content’s title


  1. Click the X beside the topic in question, then confirm you would like to remove it from the report


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/eb13dfcc1d9a999155a79064f9746c4a.png)
﻿
How to delete or edit a topic
  1. Navigate to Reports > Saved Reports or Dashboards > Saved Dashboards


  1. Hover over the topic in question and click the ellipsis that appears


  1. Choose whether to edit or delete the topic. You’ll also have the option to create a sub-topic for the parent topic here


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e5340557e9519417b63e1cce2769509c.png)
﻿
﻿  

Please note that deleting a topic will unassign it from all associated content. All sub-topics within the deleted topic will be moved up a level
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#deleting-saved-content)Deleting Saved Content
You now have the option to delete a report or dashboard. Please note that the report or dashboard must be archived before you'll see the option to delete it completely. Report and dashboard deletion cannot be undone.
To delete a report:
  * Navigate to the report, click More, then Archive.


  * Once the report has been archived, click More again, then Delete.


Please note that any configured alerts will be deleted along with the report and it will be removed from all dashboards.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b8060b346b64dc49a4a7dc54ead6e782.png)
﻿
To delete a recruiting plan:
  * Navigate to the recruiting plan, click More, then Archive.


  * Once the recruiting plan has been archived, click More again, then Delete.


Please note that this will remove the recruiting plan from any dashboards it was linked to.
﻿  

﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/84762eb1d377ddf3273e64f836d866d3.png)
﻿
﻿  

To delete a dashboard:
  * Navigate to the dashboard, click More, then Archive.


  * Once the dashboard has been archived, click More, thenDelete


All of the dashboard's saved views will also be deleted and any scheduled deliveries set up on this dashboard will be stopped on deletion. 
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/54b57b8037988180d6c2c62a22496051.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#can-i-assign-a-topic-to-a-report-or-dashboard-befo)Can I assign a topic to a report or dashboard before saving it?
No. Topics can only be assigned to saved content. As such, the report or dashboard would need to be saved first.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#can-i-assign-multiple-topics-to-a-report-or-dashbo)Can I assign multiple topics to a report or dashboard?
Yes, you can assign multiple topics to a report or dashboard at a time to allow for flexible organization. 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#can-i-opt-to-include-or-exclude-archived-reports-a)Can I opt to include or exclude archived reports and dashboards from the table view?
You can! Use the Show archived reports or Show archived dashboards toggle to include any archived reports or dashboards in the saved content table view.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fe820c80d5c369f210a9a1b69824524e.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards#can-i-bulk-archive-restore-andor-delete-reports-re)Can I bulk archive, restore and/or delete reports, recruiting plans and dashboards? Can I bulk-assign topics to them?
You can, yes. Use the checkboxes beside each report/recruiting plan/dashboard to select the content in question and you'll then see the available options listed. If you are selecting active content, you will see the option to Archive in bulk. If you are selecting archived content, you will see the option to Restore or Delete. 
You will have the option to assign topics to either active or archived content.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4df4086636ccbb3defa4472052ca4e48.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3b887874314bc36ebcf47c20e35b1597.png)
﻿
﻿

docusign:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Integrations
Other
# Docusign
13 min
Copy
You can integrate your Ashby account with your company’s Docusign account, allowing you to send e-signature offers and documents via Docusign, rather than using Ashby’s default Dropbox Sign option.
You will need to have a Docusign account on the Standard plan or above to set up this integration.
When setting up the integration, we recommend logging in with a shared service account (e.g “recruiting@company.com”) rather than an individual user’s account. Notifications to candidates from Docusign may include references to the account user’s name and email address and e-signature requests will appear as from this account.
The account used to set up the integration may also receive notifications about e-signature requests, so using a service account can help avoid individual users getting unwanted notifications. Depending on your Docusign account, you may also be able to further customize the email notifications that go to candidate using the [Docusign Brand feature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zjl1583277333205.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Brand feature").
To enable the Docusign integration:
  * Navigate to Admin > Integrations > Integrations.


  * Open the Docusign tile and click Authorize with Docusign.


  * Log in to Docusign on the following page.


  * Review the access Ashby is requesting for the integration and click Allow Access to confirm.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c3f62f9e37e2be354a80a80c471f4c30.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/582b8c4b4d854b92c2d1ba605fd48abe.png)
﻿
﻿  

You will then need to create a connect key in Docusign. To do this:
  * Head into your Docusign account and scroll to the _Integrations_ section 


  * Click Connect, then Connect Keys _._ Note: if you are unable to find the Connect section, see the guidance [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#i-am-unable-to-find-the-connect-section-when-setti)﻿. 


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6762a2cc68a791774a850440460ed54a.png)
﻿
  * Create a new key by clicking Add Secret Key. Copy this key to your clipboard. 


  * Head back to the Docusign integrations tile in Ashby and paste the key into the Connect Key field and click Update. 


﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#setting-docusign-as-your-default-e-signature-provi)Setting Docusign as your default e-signature provider
To set Docusign as your default e-signature provider for offers:
  * Navigate to Admin > Offers


  * Head to the Settings tab


  * Ensure the E-Signature Provider dropdown is set as Docusign.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9776c8e76d560f32c3361da097c24d64.png)
﻿
﻿  

To set Docusign as your default e-signature provider for e-signature documents:
  * Navigate to Admin > Integrations > E-Signature Documents


  * Head to the Settings tab


  * Ensure the E-Signature Provider dropdown is set as Docusign. If you’d only like to use Docusign for offers, then you can set the E-Signature provider to Dropbox Sign here if you’d like to use Dropbox Sign for documents.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6dcb30b638fe9efba3f80110d1871bab.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#sending-offer-letters-using-docusign)Sending offer letters using Docusign
For more on generating offer and e-signatures in general, check out [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿ .
Once you’ve created an offer and generated an offer letter in Ashby, you’ll be able to confirm that the offer letter will be sent out via Docusign in the Offer E-signature section.
Once you’ve sent your e-signature request/envelope via Docusign, you will be able to track the status of the request within the offer details in Ashby.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c21dfe45aea28fcf84c502dccf0ab7dd.png)
﻿
The requests and created envelopes can also be tracked and viewed within your Docusign account.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d21132954b3f724726372a832c97f29c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#docusign-e-signature-activities)Docusign E-Signature Activities
E-Signature activities allow you to be prompted to send out e-signature requests for documents other than offers as a part of your process (for example, an NDA) when a candidate lands in a specific stage. For more on configuring e-signature activities, check out the E-Signature documents guide [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-documents)﻿.
You can configure an e-signature activity within a job or job template’s interview plan.
  * Navigate to the job or job template in question 


  * Click the Interview Plan tab 


  * Click Add Activity To on the appropriate stage of your interview plan, then + Add Activity


  * Select the E-Signature Document option 


  * On the following popover, you’ll be prompted to select the e-signature document template and the e-signature provider. The provider selected here will override the default provider set in Admin > Integrations > E-Signature Documents.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4139b8a4721f5ff3ef496826c70ce133.png)
﻿
  * Once you’ve configured your e-signature document activity, when the candidate enters that stage of your pipeline, you’ll be prompted to send out an e-signature request. 


  * Click Send E-Signature Document on the activity to open up the Sign popover where you can confirm the following: 


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/be90208040c58ea41298c8ce92c0cde1.png)
﻿
  * Company Signatory


  * Signing Order


  * Whether the request should expire and, if so, when.


  * The title/subject and message within the notification emails sent.


  * Any users that should be CC'd into the request. Users that are added to the CC field will also receive notifications about the request.


  * The document to be sent (with the option to change the document if needed)


  * The option to send a self-test and preview the e-signature document before sending it.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f8ed57858beb0bb4d4b3c0d4fec01e5c.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#sending-out-an-e-signature-document-manually)Sending out an e-signature document manually
Looking to send out an ad-hoc e-signature document to a candidate? Click More on the candidate profile, then Send E-Signature Document.
On the following popover, you’ll be able to determine the document template, the e-signature provider and the number and currency formatting. Then click Generate E-Signature Document to add the activity to the candidates profile and be taken to the Sign popover.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7969414679c58acdbd293451c886d1b2.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#previewing-e-signature-offers-and-documents-sent-v)Previewing E-Signature offers and documents sent via Docusign
The preview editor is available for both Dropbox Sign and Docusign but Docusign does provide some more advanced preview options like editing recipients.
The preview editor can be enabled under E-Signature Settings in Admin [here](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/offers/settings "here").
When enabled, the signature request can be previewed in a document editor in the Send E-Signature Request or Sign popover. When sending an e-signature request, click Preview to access the editor and review the document.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/495d85223e42e2ceba711804df3833ce.png)
﻿
The preview option also allows you to add special document inputs for the signatories to complete (for example, additional text boxes or checkboxes).
With the Docusign preview editor, you can click on the drop-down with a signatory’s name in it to either switch between signatories to make the appropriate edits or edit the configuration for recipients using advanced Docusign settings.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3cb7aa1f75a3cfc7dcdca8c942b19b30.png)
﻿
For example, you can click the Needs to Sign dropdown and switch this to an in-person signing. If you have a more advanced Docusign plan, you can also configure witness signatories and more here.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cfcca7b1089bc9dc38e89e6bcda46253.png)
﻿
Edits would need to be made in the preview editor for each e-signature request currently. We are looking to add more support for these features to make the process less manual in the future.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#accessing-e-signature-documents-in-docusign)Accessing e-signature documents in Docusign
E-Signature documents and offer details can be accessed from the offer activity on the candidate's profile. If an e-signature document was generated via Docusign, so long as you have access to the documents in Docusign, you'll be able to access them within your Docusign account too.
If some users shouldn't be able to access certain documents in Docusign, Docusign does have a way to set document visibility - you can read more on this in their [Document Visibility guides](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=eui1578456411411.html&_LANG=enus "Document Visibility guides").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign#i-am-unable-to-find-the-connect-section-when-setti)I **am unable to find the Connect section when setting up my Docusign integration Connect Key**
Certain Docusign accounts may not have the Connect module enabled by default. You can reach out to Docusign support or your Docusign customer success manager to request that the module be enabled.
﻿  

﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Analytics Glossary
# Ashby Subject & Field Definitions
44 min
Copy
In this guide, we list all of the Ashby subject and field values, along with definitions for each. This glossary will help you determine how to filter or group data, or which filters to add to include the information you need in a custom list report. 
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#subject-definitions)Subject Definitions  
| Subject  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Job Consideration  | A candidate’s record on a specific job.  |  
| Job  | A job set up within your ATS.  |  
| Job Postings  | The job posting created for a job within your ATS (Available for Ashby All-In-One customers only)  |  
| Interview  | Scheduled interviews in your ATS. Interviews can be counted as one per stage (i.e. an onsite panel with 4 different interviews will count as 1 onsite interview) or one per scheduled interview (i.e. an onsite panel with 4 different interview sill count as 4 interviews).  |  
| Interview Schedules  | Covers the interview schedule status, including interview stage, interview stage group, RSVP declines and more.  |  
| Interviewer Participation  | Scheduled interviewers. Each interviewer counts as an interview participation. These tie closely to feedback forms.  |  
| Interview Stage Transition  | Moves to and from stages. Accidental stage moves will be detected and counted.  |  
| Feedback Form  | We pull in the person who submitted, when they submitted, and the overall recommendation (score out of 4) from feedback forms.  |  
| Sequence  | Sequences sent in Ashby.  |  
| Application Form  | Refers to the application form candidates submit when they apply.  |  
| Candidate  | Candidates in Ashby.  |  
| Outreach Activities  | Tracking the status and type of outreach activity (email, LinkedIn Connection Request, LinkedIn Inmail, etc)  |  
| Diversity Survey  | Custom diversity surveys created in Ashby.  |  
| EEOC Survey  | Custom EEOC surveys created in Ashby.  |  
| Sourcing Forms  | Custom sourcing forms created in Ashby  |  
| Questionnaire  | Custom questionnaires created in Ashby  |  
| Event Audience Members  | Attendees in the audience of events you create in Ashby.  |  
| Opening  | Headcount for a job using the Openings feature in Ashby.  |  
| Candidate Experience Survey  | Custom candidate experience surveys created in Ashby.  |  
| Employees  | The employees you add as users in Ashby  |  
| Quality of Hire Survey Enrollments  | Track the status of Quality of Hire surveys  |  
| Quality of Hire Survey Steps  | The steps you include in your Quality of Hire surveys  |  
| Projects  | List for tracking talent that you aren't actively considering for a job.  |  
| Project Members  | Report on candidates being added to projects.  |  
| Candidate Review Requests  | Report on candidate reviews (both completed and pending)  |  
| AI Notetaker Recordings  | Report on the recording mode and the availability of a transcript or recording of an interview that had the AI Notetaker enabled.  |  
| Candidate Recording Consent Choice  | Report on the recording consent choices of candidates.  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#time-frame-filter-definitions)Time Frame Filter Definitions  
| Subject  | Time Frame Filter  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Job Consideration  | Activity Status Due  | When the current activity is due.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Archived At  | The point in time at which a job consideration was marked as archived.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Consideration Ended At  | The point in time at which a job consideration was either marked as ‘Hired’ or ‘Archived’.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Hired At  | The point in time at which a candidate was hired for a job.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Offer Stage Entered  | The point in time the job consideration entered the offer stage of the application process.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Started At  | The time at which the job consideration was created. Either when a candidate applied or when a user considered them for the job in Ashby.  |  
| ﻿  
 | … Stage Group Interview At  | The time of the first scheduled interview in a given stage group.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Offer Decided At  | The time at which the offer was accepted or rejected by the candidate.  |  
| Job  | Was Open  | When grouping or filtering by this field, returns all jobs that were open in a given timeframe.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Created At  | The time at which a job was created.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Opened At  | The time at which a job was opened.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Closed At  | The time at which a job was closed.  |  
| Interview  | Start Time  | The time when an interview took place. If the interview is a multi-panel interview, this time refers to the start time of the first interview.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Created At  | The time when an interview was created by the organizer. If the interview is a multi-panel interview, this time refers to when the first interview was created.  |  
| Interviewer Participation  | Start Time  | The time when an interviewer event started.  |  
| ﻿  
 | End Time  | The time when an interviewer event ended.  |  
| Interview Stage Transition  | Entered At  | Point in time at which a stage was entered.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Left At  | Point in time at which a stage was left.  |  
| Feedback Form  | Submitted At  | The point in time at which the feedback form was submitted.  |  
| Sequence  | Started At  | The point in time a sequence was started.  |  
| ﻿  
 | First Reply Received At  | The date of the first reply received from the candidate.  |  
| Application Form  | Submitted At  | The point in time at which an application form was submitted.  |  
| Candidate  | Last Email Sent At  | The last point in time at which an email was sent to the candidate.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Last Email Received At  | The last point in time at which an email was received from the candidate.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Added At  | The point in time at which the candidate was added to the system.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Hired At Date  | The point in time the candidate was first hired. When used in filters, finds candidates that have been hired during the given date range.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Follow Up Date  | The date for a pending follow up with the candidate.  |  
| Diversity Survey  | Submitted At  | The point in time at which a Diversity form was submitted.  |  
| EEOC Survey  | Submitted At  | The point in time at which an EEOC form was submitted.  |  
| Opening  | Created At  | The point in time an opening was created.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Opened At  | The point in time an opening was opened.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Closed At  | The point in time an opening was closed or filled.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Was Open  | When grouping or filtering by this field, returns all openings that were open in a given time frame.  |  
| Candidate Experience Survey Submission  | Survey Request Created At  | When the survey request for this submission was originally created.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Submitted At  | The point in time at which a Candidate Experience Survey was submitted.  |  
| Project  | Created At  | The point in time at which the project was created.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Updated At  | The point in time at which the project was last updated.  |  
| Candidate Review Request  | Created At  | The point in time at which the request was created.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Submitted At  | The point in time at which the review was submitted.  |  
| Project Member  | Added to Project At  | When the candidate was added to the project.  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#fields-definitions)Fields Definitions
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#job-consideration-fields)Job Consideration Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Current Interview Stage Name  | The title of the current interview stage name.  |  
| Current Interview Stage Group  | The title of the current interview stage group.  |  
| Current Interview Stage Type  | The type of the current interview stage: Lead, Application Review, Active, Hired, or Archived.  |  
| Days in Current Interview Stage  | The number of days the job consideration has been in the current interview stage.  |  
| Days in Current Interview Stage Name  | The number of days the job consideration has been in the current interview stage group.  |  
| Average Overall Recommendation  | The average overall recommendation score given by interviewers for the job consideration.  |  
| Started At  | The time at which the job consideration was created. Either when a candidate applied or when a user considered them for the job in Ashby.  |  
| Passed Through Stage Group/Name  | Allows filtering applications that have passed through a certain stage group/name, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will only return applications that have passed through that stage group/name, not applications that skipped the stage group/name. Note, passed through stage group/name will include candidates currently in the stage group/name.  |  
| Reached Stage Group/Name  | Allows filtering applications that have reached a certain stage group/name, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will return all applications that have passed through this stage group/name or have skipped this stage/stage name but passed through a later stage group/ name.  |  
| Reached Stage Type  | Allows filtering job considerations that have reached a certain stage type, e.g. ‘Application Review’. Will return all job considerations that have passed through a stage of this type or have skipped such a stage but passed through a later stage.  |  
| Furthest Stage Group/Name Reached  | Allows selecting and filtering by by the furthest stage group/title this job consideration has reached.  |  
| Most Recent Active Stage Group  | Allows selecting and filtering by the stage group the application was in when being hired or rejected.  |  
| Outcome  | The overall outcome of the job consideration. Can be one of Lead, Active, Archived, or Hired.  |  
| Outcome with Archive Reason  | The overall outcome of the job consideration, including the type of archive reason. Can be one of Lead, Active, Rejected by Org, Rejected by Candidate, Rejected Other, or Hired.  |  
| Activity Status  | Shows with status a job consideration is in. E.g. if it Needs Scheduling, is Waiting on Feedback, etc.  |  
| Activity Status Due At  | When the current activity is due.  |  
| Job  | The job the candidate applied to or is considered for.  |  
| Applied via Job Posting  | The job posting the candidate applied to.  |  
| Source  | The source through which the job consideration was created.  |  
| Source Type  | The type of the source through which the job consideration was created.  |  
| Source Site  | Only available when the source is _Sourced: Ashby Chrome Extension_. Lists where the job consideration was sourced from via the Chrome Extension (LinkedIn, LinkedIn Recruiter, GitHub).  |  
| Hiring Manager  | The Hiring Manager assigned to this job consideration.  |  
| Recruiter  | The Recruiter assigned to this job consideration.  |  
| Recruiting Coordinator  | The Recruiting Coordinator assigned to this job consideration.  |  
| Sourcer  | The Sourcer assigned to this job consideration.  |  
| Assessment Sent Date  | The date the assessment was sent.  |  
| Assessment Finished Date  | The date the assessment was finished.  |  
| Archive Reasons  | Filtering by reason the candidate was archived.  |  
| Credited To  | The person the job consideration is credited to.  |  
| Extended Offer?  | Whether or not your organization extended an offer for this job consideration.  |  
| Offer Stage Entered Date  | The point in time the job consideration entered the offer stage of the application process.  |  
| Archive Reason  | The reason a job consideration was archived.  |  
| Archive Reason Type  | The type of the reason a job consideration was archived. Can be one of Rejected by candidate, Rejected by org, or Other.  |  
| Auto-Rejected  | Whether this application was rejected automatically based on rejection criteria in job settings.  |  
| Hired At  | The point in time at which a candidate was hired for a job.  |  
| Archived At  | The point in time at which a job consideration was marked as archived.  |  
| Consideration Ended At  | The point in time at which a job consideration was either marked as ‘Hired’ or ‘Archived’.  |  
| Candidate  | The individual candidate of this job consideration.  |  
| Application Review Average Score  | The average score given by all users in application review. If an application passes through application review multiple times, this will consider all scores.  |  
| Application Review Assignee  | The person assigned to review the job consideration.  |  
| Involves  | Returns any job considerations where the user is on the hiring team or is specifically following the candidate.  |  
| Time in Process (Days)  | Number of days from application start or most recent unarchive to consideration end (archived or hired). For active applications, we report the time from application start or most recent unarchive to the current date.  |  
| … Stage Group Interview At  | The time of the first scheduled interview in a given stage group.  |  
| Has Resume Uploaded?  | Whether this candidate has a resume uploaded for this job consideration or not.  |  
| Workday Latest Hire Push Status  | Whether an attempt was made to push the job consideration to Workday and, if so, the status of the latest attempt.  |  
| Workday Latest Hire Push Attempted At  | The time at which the latest attempt was made to push the job consideration to Workday.  |  
Other Subjects/Related Subjects available in reports with the Job Consideration Subject:
  * ﻿[Candidates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#candidate-fields)﻿


  * ﻿[Job](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#job-fields)﻿


  * ﻿[Interview](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#interview-fields)﻿


  * ﻿[Interview Schedule](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#interview-schedule-fields)﻿


  * ﻿[Stage Transitions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#interview-stage-transition-fields)﻿

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#job-fields)Job Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Job  | The job’s identity.  |  
| Title  | Represents the name or title of the job.  |  
| Requisition ID  | Alphanumerical identifier assigned to the job that is unique to your company’s internal processes.  |  
| Creator  | The user who created the job.  |  
| Hiring Manager  | The Hiring Manager who is on the hiring team of this job.  |  
| Recruiter  | The Recruiter who is on the hiring team of this job.  |  
| Recruiting Coordinator  | The Recruiting Coordinator who is on the hiring team of this job.  |  
| Sourcer  | The Sourcer who is on the hiring team of this job.  |  
| Team  | The department or team directly associated with this job. E.g. if a job is associated with ‘Product Marketing’ which is nested under ‘Marketing’, then this field will return ‘Product Marketing’.  |  
| Department  | The top-level department associated with this job. E.g. if a job is associated with ‘Product Marketing’ which is nested under ‘Marketing’, then this field will return ‘Marketing’.  |  
| Location  | The job’s location that is used as the default location for job postings of a job. Allows searching for jobs based on their location or the region in which the location is located.  |  
| Job Status  | The current status of the job. Can be Open, Closed, Archived, or Draft.  |  
| Employment Type  | The type of employment offered for the job. Can be Full time, Part time, Intern, Contract, or Temporary.  |  
| Was Open  | When grouping or filtering by this field, returns all jobs that were open in a given timeframe.  |  
| Created At  | The time at which the job was created.  |  
| Opened At  | The time at which the job was opened.  |  
| Closed At  | The time at which the job was closed.  |  
| Open Duration (in days)  | How many days this job has been open for.  |  
| Open Headcount  | Number of Open Headcount for this job.  |  
| Active Candidate Count  | How many candidates have applications in active interview or offer stages. Excludes candidates with applications in application review.  |  
| Number of Candidates in …  | The number of job considerations currently in a given stage group.  |  
| Is Template?  | Currently, job templates are shown as draft or archived jobs. Use this field to filter job templates out.  |  
| Close Reason  | Returns any jobs which were closed for a particular reason.  |  
| External Job Board Visibility  | The status of a job’s posting on your external Ashby job board. Jobs can have the posting status of listed, unlisted or no posting.  |  
| Internal Job Board Visibility  | The status of a job’s posting on your internal Ashby job board. Jobs can have the posting status of listed, unlisted or no posting.  |  
Other Subjects available in reports with the Job Subject:
  * ﻿[Opening](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#opening-fields)﻿


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#job-posting-fields)Job Posting Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Job Posting Title  | The title given to the job posting.  |  
| Status  | The status of the job posting (published or unpublished)  |  
| Social Description  | The short text description of posting for social sharing. The text added to the _Social Description_ section of your job posting.  |  
| External Visibility  | Whether the post is set as listed or unlisted on your public-facing job board.  |  
| Internal Visibility  | Whether the post is set as listed or unlisted on your internal job board.  |  
| Location  | The location listed on the job posting.  |  
| Application Deadline  | The deadline set for applications (if enabled). Will appear as a date with a timestamp or a null value.  |  
| Automatically Unpublish?  | Only available to display if an application deadline has been set.  |  
| Application Form Title  | The title of the application form assigned to the job posting.  |  
| Application Form Type  | Whether the application form is a shared or custom application form.  |  
| Job  | The job the job posting is assigned to.  |  
| Auto-Confirm Application Enabled?  | Whether an application confirmation email has been configured for this job posting.  |  
| Auto-Confirm Application Email Template  | The email template that is sent out as part of the application confirmation email setup.  |  
| Auto-Reject Application Enabled?   | If auto-reject rules have been configured and activated for the job posting.  |  
| Auto-Reject Application Email Template  | The email template that will be sent out as part of the auto-reject configuration.  |  
| Created At  | The date and time the job posting was originally created.  |  
| Last Published At  | The date and time of the last publication of the job posting. Postings can be unpublished and re-published so this is a most-recent event timestamp.  |  
Other subjects available in reports with the Job Posting subject:

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions#candidate-fields)Candidate Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Last Email Sent At  | The last point in time at which an email was sent to the candidate.  |  
| Last Email Received At  | The last point in time at which an email was received from the candidate.  |  
| Added At  | The point in time at which the candidate was added to the system.  |  
| Added By  | The user who added the candidate to the system.  |  
| Name  | The full name of the candidate.  |  
| Phone Number (Primary, Personal)  | The candidate’s primary, personal phone number.  |  
| Email Address(es)  | All of the candidate’s email addresses.  |  
| Email Address (Primary, Personal)  | The candidate’s primary, personal email address.  |  
| LinkedIn Profile URL  | A URL to the candidate’s LinkedIn profile.  |  
| Hired At Date  | The point in time the candidate was first hired. When used in filters, finds candidates that have been hired during the given date range.  |  
| Follow Up Date  | The date for a pending follow up with the candidate.  |  
| Follow Up Assignee  | The person assigned to follow up with the candidate.  |  
| Tags  | The candidate tags associated with the candidate.  |  
| Most Recent Company  | The candidate’s most recent company from their employment history.  |  
| Worked At  | The companies the candidate has worked at.  |  
| Years of Experience  | The number of years the candidate has calculated by summing up the tenure of each entry in the candidate’s employment history.  |  
| Resume Full Text Search  | Allows to do a full-text search through all the candidate’s resumes.  |  
| Reason for Outreach  | The reason you are reaching out to this candidate.  |  
| Salary Expectations  | Salary expectations.  |  
| Sources  | The sources through which the candidate’s Job Considerations were created.  |  
| Source  | The source through which the candidate was created.  |  
| Source Site  | Only available when the source is _Sourced: Ashby Chrome Extension_. Lists where the candidate was sourced from via the Chrome Extension (LinkedIn, LinkedIn Recruiter, GitHub).  |  
| Credited To  | The person who receives credit for sourcing the candidate.  |  
| Project  | The talent projects the candidate is a member of.  |  
| Involves  | Returns any candidates where the user is on the hiring team or is specifically following the candidate.  |  
| Candidate Profile URL  | A URL to the candidate’s profile in Ashby.  |  
| Current Job Title  | Returns candidates matching the current (i.e. latest) job title. Note: Only available Ashby AIO and Greenhouse Analytics customers.  |  
| Held Job Title  | Returns candidates matching the previously held (including current) job title. Note: Only available Ashby AIO and Greenhouse Analytics customers.  |  
| Is Marked as Do Not Contact  | Whether the candidate is marked as “Do Not Contact”.  |  
| Is In Violation of Data Compliance?  | Whether a candidate is in violation of any data compliance rules.  |  
| Inactivity Based Data Retention Date  | The date after which a candidate’s data should be anonymized or deleted according to the organization’s inactivity based data compliance rules.  |  
| Data Retention Consent End Date  | The candidate has given consent to keep their data up to this date.  |  
| Has Blocked Applications?  | Whether the candidate has had any applications blocked.  |  
| Location  | Allows filtering candidates within a specific location.  |  
| AI Opt Out?  | Allows filtering candidates who have opted out of AI application processing.  |

| Manager   | Represents the manager of the employee.  |

| Qualified Member of Interviewer Pool   | The employee is a fully qualified member of the specified Interviewer Pool.  |  
| Interviewer Pool Status  | The employee's status within an interviewer pool where they are a member.  |  
| Provisioned?  | Whether the user has been provisioned or not.  |  
| Active User?  | Whether the employee is an active user or not.  |  
| Start Date  | The date the employee started working for the company.  |  
| Role   | Used to find which employees have certain roles.  |  
| Has Logged In?  | Whether the user has ever logged in.  |  
| Last Active Date   | The date of the user's most recent activity. This includes any interaction within Ashby, across all features and tasks.  |  
| Timezone   | Used to find which employees are in certain timezones.  |  
| Is Linked to a Candidate?  | Indicates whether the employee is linked to a candidate.  |  
| LinkedIn Profile URL  | A URL to the employee's LinkedIn profile.  |  
| In Management Hierarchy of  | All employees in the reporting chain of an employee.  |  
| Has Calendar  | Whether the employee has a calendar associated with their profile.  |  
| Is Paused Interviewer  | Whether the employee has all their interviews paused.  |  
| Has AI Notetaker Enabled?  | Whether the employee has AI Notetaker enabled, making interviews they participate in eligible to be recorded.  |  
| Has Email Sync Enabled?  | Whether the employee has email sync enabled.  |

| Field  | Definition  |

# Evergreen Jobs/Postings
6 min
Copy
This document reviews Evergreen Jobs/Postings in Ashby. If you are looking to set up evergreen jobs and openings in Ashby All-In-One, please check out [Openings Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/evergreen-jobspostings#introduction)Introduction
Many companies have jobs/postings/requisitions that remain open indefinitely. These are commonly referred to as evergreen jobs, evergreen postings, posting requisitions, or posting jobs. Even though evergreen roles make reporting complicated, here are a couple of reasons they would be used:
  * Continuously collecting applications for roles where hiring will always be needed to ensure there is always a healthy pipeline at any point in time.


  * Collecting applications for a generalized role. Once they have applied, the team or application will determine what specialization or team they will be placed in.


  * Sharing pipelines across multiple recruiters.


These reports can skew reports and data, especially reports analyzing time. Here are some best practices we would recommend if your team is utilizing evergreen jobs/postings or plans to.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/evergreen-jobspostings#best-practices)Best Practices
**Make Evergreen Jobs/Postings easy to identify**
Regardless of how your organization is utilizing evergreen jobs, everyone can agree that reporting on evergreen jobs is different than reporting on one to one jobs. Many teams rely on individuals and recruiting leadership to keep track of what jobs are evergreen, but in order to make reporting easier and scalable, there should be an easy way to identify what jobs are evergreen.
  * **Custom Field** - Creating a custom field at the job or requisition level is a great way to identify evergreen jobs. We recommend creating a custom field that not only allows you to identify evergreen jobs, but any other types of jobs that may impact reporting. For example, you can add a “Posting Type” field that includes the options Evergreen, Sourcing Bucket, General Opportunity, etc...


  * **Naming Convention** - Ashby is able to filter and identify jobs by their title. Another way to easily identify evergreen roles is to implement a strict naming convention. Some teams label their jobs internally with [Posting], [Evergreen], etc... in the front of these job names.


**Have a clearly defined process that is shared across all Evergreen Jobs or similar jobs**
Some organizations hire candidates on the evergreen job; others have the applications collect in the evergreen job and then eventually move or transfer applications to a hire job or requisition. It is important the process is consistent to ensure the best quality data. If candidates are not being processed equally, you run the risk of having limited and/or inaccurate data. Below are some questions and discussion points that we would recommend clarifying and streamlining for evergreen roles.
  * **Do you hire candidates on Evergreen Jobs/ requisitions? Do you transfer their application to a new job? Do you duplicate the application and add it into a new job?**


  * **If you are moving candidates out of the Evergreen Job to hire them, at what point does your team move the application?**


  * By consistently moving applications at the same point in the process, this ensures the data on each application is also consistent.


  * **Do candidates get interviews scheduled and conducted in the Evergreen Job?**


  * Most ATS’s will wipe interview data if an application is moved into a new job. If interview data is important to your team, it is important to sort applications and place them into new jobs if necessary before interviewing starts.


  * If you do not hire candidates on Evergreen Jobs, it is recommended that you do not schedule candidates for interviews in the Evergreen Job. The interview data on the Evergreen Job will get wiped when the application is transferred or duplicated into the proper hire job.


  * **How do applications get assigned the correct recruiter?**


  * Most Evergreen Jobs will have multiple recruiters working within it. In order to give recruiters credit and to help organize their individual candidates, it is important to have a process or policy on assigning recruiters to applications earlier on. This will ensure you capture as much data as possible.


  * **Will these jobs ever close? If so, what is the process for sunsetting an old Evergreen Job and starting a newer one? At what cadence does this happen?**


  * Some organizations like to open new Evergreen Jobs annually. This means there needs to be a point in which existing applications get moved over into the new job.


  * By preparing in advance to move candidates over to the newer job, your organization can ensure that existing applications are processed and newer applications will start their process in the new job rather than adding more applications that will need to remain on the older job.


**Avoid single action hire jobs in your ATS**
A single action hire job is a job that is opened just to send someone an offer and hire them after they have gone through the process in either a different job or outside of the ATS. If the candidate was processed in another job in the ATS, data on that hire will be limited. Greenhouse and Lever both lose data when you transfer or add applications to a new job. By creating a single action hiring job, the only data you can track accurately on these hires are their hire date, offer information, and application date.
For more information on moving active job considerations within Ashby All-In-One, please check out [Can I transfer a candidate’s job consideration to another job or interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan)﻿
The discussion around Evergreen Jobs is always ongoing, and there still isn’t a perfect way to structure or deal with them.
_Importance of transfer functionality_
As managing Evergreen Roles can depend heavily on transferring candidate applications between jobs, the pertinent summary of functionality across our primary domain is given below.
![Screen Shot 2022-12-22 at 7.21.20 AM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Mm5rL4JTr2NYxri9Dllwv_screenshot2022-12-22at72120am.png)
﻿
An example of the data loss summary in a Greenhouse job transfer
![Untitled](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Edlfvs02oaFYDK-OtV4T9_untitled.png)

which-features-are-available-on-each-ash:

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Billing & Plans
# Which features are available on each Ashby All-In-One Plan?
5 min
Copy
In this guide, we’ll cover the different Ashby plans currently available and the in-app features available on each plan.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/which-features-are-available-on-each-ashby-all-in-one-plan#what-are-ashbys-all-in-one-recruiting-plans)What are Ashby's all-in-one recruiting plans?
There are four Ashby plans
  * Foundations Plan


  * Legacy Plus Plan


  * Plus Plan


  * Enterprise Plan


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/which-features-are-available-on-each-ashby-all-in-one-plan#features-available-on-each-plan)Features available on each plan
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/which-features-are-available-on-each-ashby-all-in-one-plan#foundations)Foundations
Ashby All-In-One Foundations comes with ATS, Scheduling and Sourcing tools, default dashboards, a report template library, basic offer functionality, and 100+ integrations.
**What’s included?**
  * Powerful ATS features like [Creating & Posting Jobs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/l-NcIsZrB9ztdr4U3idZr)﻿, customizing your job board (more on this at [Job Board: Configuring Your Setup](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-board-configuring-your-setup)﻿), [Application Review](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review)﻿, [Candidate Reviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-reviews)﻿, [Candidate Texting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EQo_-candidate-texting)﻿, [Auto-Reject Applications](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications)﻿ and more!


  * Sourcing tools like [Sequences](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences)﻿, shared [Projects](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/projects)﻿ and the [Chrome Extension](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension)﻿﻿


  * Powerful Scheduling Automation - for more on this, check out [Scheduling & Interviews: An Introduction](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction)﻿﻿


  * ﻿[Core Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards)﻿, [Report Catalog: Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates)﻿ and an [Alerts Library](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library)﻿﻿


  * ﻿[Bulk Import Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options)﻿


  * Basic Offer & E-Signature Functionality (more on using these options at [Offers and Offer Letters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters)﻿ and [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿). Only one offer letter template can be added in Ashby if you are on the Foundations plan.


  * 100+ Integrations including deep integrations with [Google Workspace](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/google-workspace)﻿, [Microsoft 365](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/microsoft-365)﻿, [Slack](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/slack)﻿ and [Zoom](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/zoom)﻿﻿


  * The ability to set up agencies and agency users - more on this at [Agencies Setup](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agencies-setup)﻿﻿


  * Opt-in AI tools - more on these at [AI Features in Ashby](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-features-in-ashby)﻿ (not including the AI Reporting Building Assistant)


  * ﻿[Candidate Experience Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-experience-surveys)﻿ (maximum of one survey), [EEOC Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys)﻿, [Diversity Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/diversity-surveys)﻿ and [Questionnaires](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/questionnaires)﻿﻿


  * ﻿[Permissions Automation](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-automation)﻿


  * ﻿[Data Privacy and Compliance](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance)﻿ features.


  * ﻿[Referrals and Referral Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/referrals-and-referral-links)﻿ (although custom referral rules cannot be configured on the Foundations plan)


  * ﻿[Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿ so you can send emails on behalf of another user or set up an alias.


  * ﻿[API Access](https://developers.ashbyhq.com/reference/introduction "API Access")﻿


**Paid add-ons include:**
  * SSO setup - more on this at [SSO and SCIM](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim)﻿﻿


  * Additional AI credits and email lookups for sourcing. More on purchasing email lookups can be found at [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿ and more on AI credits at [AI Credits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-credits)﻿


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/which-features-are-available-on-each-ashby-all-in-one-plan#legacy-plus)Legacy Plus
The Ashby All-In-One Legacy Plus plan comes with the features available within the Foundations plan, along with custom advanced analytics, additional scheduling and workflow automation, approvals, and advanced customization options.
**What’s included**
The Legacy Plus plan includes everything listed for the Foundations plan above, along with:
  * ﻿[Approvals](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals)﻿


  * ﻿[Custom Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports)﻿


  * Custom Dashboards (more on setting up a dashboard at [Building Your First Dashboard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard)﻿)


  * The AI Report Building Assistant - more on this at [Report Builder & AI-Assisted Report Builder](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-builder-and-ai-assisted-report-builder)﻿﻿


  * ﻿[Scheduled Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduled-dashboards)﻿


  * Confidential Jobs, Projects & Sequences


  * ﻿[Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿


  * Unlimited [Candidate Experience Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-experience-surveys)﻿﻿


  * ﻿[Application Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits)﻿


  * ﻿[E-Signature Documents](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-documents)﻿


  * ﻿[Form Field Connectors](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors)﻿


  * ﻿[Internal Job Board](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board)﻿


  * ﻿[Manage Access Roles](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/manage-access-roles)﻿ (limited to three custom access roles)


  * ﻿[Quality of Hire Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-surveys)﻿


  * Unlimited Offer Letter templates (more on these at [Offers and Offer Letters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters)﻿) and [offer letter template conditions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters)﻿.


  * Custom referral filters - more on these at [Referrals and Referral Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/referrals-and-referral-links)﻿﻿


  * ﻿[Juro](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/juro)﻿ integration


**Paid add-ons include:**
  * ﻿[SSO and SCIM](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim)﻿﻿  



  * Additional AI credits and email lookups for sourcing. More on purchasing email lookups can be found at [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿ and more on AI credits at [AI Credits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-credits)﻿


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/which-features-are-available-on-each-ashby-all-in-one-plan#plus)Plus
The Ashby All-In-One Plus plan comes with the features listed above for the Foundations and Legacy Plus plans, along with additional survey options and recruiting events. SSO and SCIM setup are included within the price of this plan.
**What’s included**
The Plus plan includes everything listed for the Foundations and Legacy Plus plans above along with:
  * ﻿[Events](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/events)﻿


  * ﻿[Hiring Manager Experience Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/hiring-manager-experience-surveys)﻿


  * The ability to pause interviewers (more on this at [Interviewers: Assigning, Rescheduling and Setting Interviewer Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits)﻿).


  * ﻿[Offer and E-Signature Document Conditional Tokens](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-e-signature-document-conditional-tokens)﻿


**Paid add-ons include:**
  * Additional AI credits and email lookups for sourcing. More on purchasing email lookups can be found at [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿ and more on AI credits at [AI Credits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-credits)﻿


  * ﻿[Advanced Scheduling Automation Add-On](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on)﻿﻿  



###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/which-features-are-available-on-each-ashby-all-in-one-plan#enterprise)Enterprise
The Ashby All-In-One Enterprise plan includes all of the features listed in the Plus and Foundations plans listed above, plus the following:
  * ﻿[Manage Access Roles](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/manage-access-roles)﻿ (Unlimited access roles)


  * ﻿[Advanced Scheduling Automation Add-On](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Newsletters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters)﻿


  * ﻿[Global Application Questions & Global Auto-Reject Rules](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules)﻿


  * Additional integration options like [Workday](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/workday)﻿.


**Paid add-ons include:**
  * Additional AI credits and email lookups for sourcing. More on purchasing email lookups can be found at [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿ and more on AI credits at [AI Credits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-credits)﻿.

setting-scheduling-defaults:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Initial Setup
# Setting Scheduling Defaults
12 min
Copy
Scheduling defaults allow you to set company-wide communication and direct booking/candidate availability settings. These default settings will be used while scheduling unless you specify different values on Interview Plans or individual Interview Schedules.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#scheduling-defaults-overview)Scheduling Defaults Overview
Scheduling defaults allow you to pre-configure details like the location, direct booking email template, and calendar invites across all jobs.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#permissions-scheduling-defaults)Permissions: Scheduling Defaults
See the table below for information on the roles that have access to configure scheduling defaults.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Only Organization Admins can set scheduling defaults as part of their access role but Elevated Access users can be given access to this feature if needed. More on this at [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#communication)Communication
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#settings)Settings
Settings control key details of calendar invites including the location which is used to automatically generate conferencing links or input the candidate’s phone number.
![Communication Settings.jpg](https://cdn.zappy.app/62f39e799620caa941b8ba5eb69cd755.png)
﻿
  1. **Location:** Add video conferencing, phone calls, or custom text to all calendar invites. Please note, if you select Zoom here, a host will need to be specified to ensure direct booking links are generated.


  1. **Is Private?:** Hides calendar invite details (e.g. title, attendees) from non-attendees.


  1. **Direct Booking Email Template:** This [email template](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿ will be used when sending a Direct Booking request from the candidate profile. 


  1. **Candidate Availability Email Template:** This email template will be used when sending a Candidate Availability request from the candidate profile. 


  1. **Candidate Interview Confirmation:** The email template specified here will be sent as an interview confirmation once the candidate has scheduled.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#calendar-invites)Calendar Invites
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/dc579c49d13d36ee793acb069cd3c970.png)
﻿
  1. **Candidate Invites:** Input a title and description that will show in the candidate’s calendar invite. Candidates invites do not support formatting.


  1. **Interviewer Invites:** Input a title and description that will show in the interviewer calendar invite/invites. Interviewer invites can be formatted.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#direct-booking-candidate-availability)Direct Booking & Candidate Availability
These settings will apply to any Direct Booking or Candidate Availability requests that you create based on this schedule.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#available-times)Available Times
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/439311d15aa98f58d009d7502fa9d9f1.png)
﻿
Direct Booking requests allow candidates to schedule a time to interview from appointment slots determined by interviewers' calendar availability and any meeting hours you define. It can only be used for schedules with a single event.
Candidate Availability requests allow candidates to submit one or more timeslots during which they are available. You can then view these times while using Manual Schedule to schedule a preferred time for both you and the candidate.
  1. **Minimum Scheduling Notice (Hours):** Determines the earliest time a candidate can select (starting from the current date).


  1. **Rolling Days Available:** Determines the range of dates available to a candidate on a meeting request.


  1. **Direct Booking Availability Increments (Minutes):** Determines the frequency of the available timeslots for this event when a candidate schedules via a Direct Booking Link.


  1. **Minimum Days of Availability:** Determines the minimum number of days the candidate must select when submitting times via a Candidate Availability Link.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#meeting-buffers)Meeting Buffers
Use Meeting Buffers to add time before or after an interview that cannot be booked, or to prevent candidates from booking time slots close to any existing meetings.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8c40c4eaeda64eabd1e4cf49013a2d40.png)
﻿
After a Direct Booking interview is booked, Meeting Buffers are shown as Unavailable for meetings on interviewers' calendars in Quick Schedule.
  1. **Pre-Meeting Buffer (Minutes):** Adds time ahead of the booked meeting that cannot be booked.


  1. **Post-Meeting Buffer (Minutes):** Adds time after the booked meeting that cannot be booked.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#reminders)Reminders
More information on configuring interview reminders can be found at [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#set-up-interview-reminders)﻿
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/4NUJEbhhcxSvJeWCX7axY_scheduling-defaults-acent-ashby-2026-02-02-at-25930-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Here you can configure interview reminders to go out to both candidates and interviewers at a set interview before the interview start time. You will also have the option to configure candidate scheduling reminders. These reminders are automatically sent if the candidate has yet to take the following action:
**Candidate Availability Link:** If the candidate hasn't submitted their availability by the time specified in your reminder configuration, then an email or text will be generated from the template selected and sent to them as a reminder.
**Direct Booking Link:** If the candidate hasn't scheduled an interview using the direct booking link provided by the time specified in your reminder configuration, then an email or text will be generated from the template selected and sent to them as a reminder.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#why-cant-i-format-candidate-calendar-defaults)Why can’t I format candidate calendar defaults?
Each email provider has different standards around formatting calendar invites; we’ve disabled formatting for candidate invites to create the best experience for all candidates.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults#can-i-pull-my-interview-locations-from-my-job-loca)C**an I pull my interview locations from my job locations list?**
No, interview locations are different to job locations. Interview locations are set within the Communications tab of your interview activity and can either be a custom location, a phone number, or video conferencing (Google Meet, Microsoft Teams or Zoom).
﻿

# How do I make offer emails private?
1 min
Copy
All emails will be marked as private once a candidate is moved to the Offer stage. For other stages, you'll need to manually mark notes and emails private by clicking More on the note or emal in question within the candidate's feed, then selecting Make Private.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/26070e996a046aaeff9d1be55eeaad3a.png)
﻿
You can opt to hide emails sent to and from candidates in the Offer and Hired stages in the [Email Visibility](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-settings "Email Visibility") section. You can also opt to disable the email sync for hired candidates completely and skip the email sync for candidates on confidential jobs. More on email visibility﻿ can be found at [Visibility Options for Users](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/visibility-options-for-users#email-visibility)﻿.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b09b7a46abbfa01c4024238f864f0770.png)
﻿
﻿

# Data Privacy FAQ
8 min
Copy
This guide outlines frequently asked questions from security teams or legal teams. Please note that this FAQ page is for informational purposes only. Although we do our best to keep this information up-to-date, you should always refer to our [Terms](https://www.ashbyhq.com/resources/terms "Terms"), [Privacy Policy](https://www.ashbyhq.com/resources/privacy "Privacy Policy") or your signed agreements with Ashby for the most accurate information. Please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com if you have questions not addressed in this page.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#data-privacy-faq-overview)Data Privacy FAQ Overview
Protecting the privacy of our customers’ data is very important to us at Ashby, just as we know that protecting the privacy of your candidates’ and employees’ data is important to you.
We offer flexible tools you can use to integrate Ashby with your compliance processes, letting your team stay focused on an efficient and effective recruiting pipeline. Our tools are being used by satisfied customers around the world who are subject to regulatory environments including GDPR, CCPA, and others.
If you’re an Ashby user or admin, all the information you need to use our features is right here within the Ashby Knowledge Base, including documentation for:
  * ﻿[User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿


  * ﻿[Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates)﻿


  * ﻿[Compensation](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/compensation)﻿


  * ﻿[Data Privacy and Compliance](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance)﻿


  * ﻿[Surveys & Questionnaires](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/Rlbg-surveys-and-questionnaires)﻿


The rest of this document contains answer to common questions from customers with more specific technical, operational, or implementation concerns to help you decide if Ashby is the right fit for you.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#frequently-asked-questions)Frequently Asked Questions
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#ashby-permissions)Ashby Permissions
**What are the Ashby permission levels?**
There are three levels of global access that can be assigned to users:
  * Limited Access


  * Elevated Access


  * Organization Admin


Users are assigned Limited Access by default. Elevated Access and Organization Admins can be given specific access roles to jobs, locations and departments to provide or limit access as needed. For more information on permissions and to view the default access roles, check out [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿﻿
**Can I create custom access roles?**
Yes, you can create custom access roles to determine the specific areas of Ashby that users with that role can access. For more information on this, check out [Manage Access Roles](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/manage-access-roles)﻿.
**Can changes to user permissions be viewed?**
Yes. On the Employees page in Admin, Organization Admins can click on a user’s profile and navigate to the History tab to view a log of permissions changes made. The log includes a timestamp, the changes made and the name of the user who made the changes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates)Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates
Looking for instructions for deleting or anonymizing a candidate? See [Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates)﻿.
Ashby offers two options for permanently erasing candidate PII: Hard deletion and anonymization.
  1. Hard deletion permanently removes all data associated with the candidate. This includes PII as well as metadata and historical information about the hiring process and outcome. This may be preferred when the data is erroneous or is otherwise reducing reporting accuracy.


  1. Anonymization permanently removes PII from the candidate record. Metadata and non-identifiable information is retained for historical reporting purposes. Most customers prefer this as their default deletion method, since it meets regulatory requirements for data erasure without sacrificing hiring process insights.


﻿  

![Screenshot 2023-08-15 at 12.42.17 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/FiLlcMo_hmGEBahoML1VN_screenshot2023-08-15at124217pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#when-is-the-data-permanently-erased)**When is the data permanently erased?**
When you anonymize a candidate from within Ashby, the erasure takes place immediately and cannot be un-done from within Ashby. When you delete a candidate, it is first “soft deleted”, but this can be un-done by the same user, an admin, or by Ashby Support. After 10 days, it is permanently erased from our application database.
After the backup retention window of 30 days has expired, the data will be permanently erased from Ashby's backend.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#what-fields-are-considered-pii-for-the-purpose-of)**What fields are considered PII for the purpose of anonymization?**
Any candidate data field which is tagged internally as potential PII is required to be anonymized. When new candidate data fields are added to the platform, we evaluate them to determine if they constitute PII and mark them as such. The list of anonymized fields includes but is not limited to:
  * Name


  * Email addresses


  * Phone numbers


  * Education & Employment History


  * Candidate Custom Fields


  * Social Links


  * Notes


  * Follow Ups


  * Files


  * Emails


  * Scorecards


Any required fields which can’t be totally removed, such as candidate name, are replaced with random IDs, such as e.g. “Candidate 014a9974-b9d4-4619-9276-8e69326dc8f0”, or the string “This data has been anonymized.”
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#does-ashby-have-a-fedramp-ato)**Does Ashby have a FedRAMP ATO?**
No, Ashby is designed to store and process recruiting-related data, not sensitive public or governmental records. If you decide to use Ashby, you’ll need to take responsibility for not sharing this sensitive data with us.
However, we do have customers with stricter compliance requirements who are happily using Ashby after implementing technical measures that guarantee any sensitive data is filtered out before it reaches our systems.
If you’re considering that, please reach out!
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-faq#can-ashby-execute-a-hipaa-baa)**Can Ashby execute a HIPAA BAA?**
No, Ashby is designed to store and process recruiting-related data, not sensitive protected health information. If you decide to use Ashby, you’ll need to take responsibility for not sharing this sensitive data with us.
However, we do have customers with stricter compliance requirements who are happily using Ashby after implementing technical measures that guarantee any sensitive data is filtered out before it reaches our systems.
If you’re considering that, please reach out!
