Interviews & Scheduling FAQs
# What's the difference between interview plans and interviews?
9 min
Copy
To automate areas of your interview process, we recommend setting up interview plans, interviews and schedule activities. These three tools work together to set up your candidate pipeline, structure the interview and feedback process and prompt you when a candidate needs scheduling.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/whats-the-difference-between-interview-plans-and-interviews#overview)Overview
An interview plan sets up the pipeline of your interview process, so you can add in or amend the stages a candidate will go through from when they are added as a lead through to when they are hired. These can be set up in [Interview Plans](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interview-plans "Interview Plans"). Only one interview plan can be assigned to a job at a time.
The interview tool allows you to set up templates that include a briefing for interviewers and assign a feedback form for them to complete post-interview. They can be configured in [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/whats-the-difference-between-interview-plans-and-interviews#interview-plans)Interview Plans
For an overview on how interview plans work, check out [Interview Plans](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans)﻿﻿
Interview plans determine the stages your candidates will pass through when interviewing with your team. Interview plans are created in Admin, then assigned to jobs. All jobs require an interview plan. You can see the stages of your interview plan displayed in the Candidate Pipeline section of the role it’s assigned to.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/whats-the-difference-between-interview-plans-and-interviews#viewing-an-interview-plan-setup)Viewing an interview plan setup
Here’s an example of an interview plan:
You can see that it’s split into three sections; Lead, Active and Completed.
Lead: These stages are used for sourced candidates, both when finding suitable candidates and when reaching out to them to gauge their interest. Please note that the New Lead, Reached Out and Replied stages cannot be removed from an interview plan.
Active: These are stages that reflect your hiring process from when an application is received, right up to an offer being extended.
Completed: These are your stages that show the application result - either Archived (or rejected), or Hired.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/whats-the-difference-between-interview-plans-and-interviews#viewing-an-interview-plan-on-a-job)Viewing an interview plan on a job
When you click into a job, you can see these stages laid out in the Candidate Pipeline tab. You can click on the stage sections at the top of the Candidate Pipeline view to view your leads within the Leads section, and view candidates in Application Review _,_ your Active stages and those who are in the Offer stage. Hired and Archived candidates are also accessible via the Hired and Archived buttons.
When clicking on an option, you’ll be able to see the stages you’ve set up. In Active for example, you can see each of the interview stages that were added to your interview plan and then the candidates listed in each stage.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6faa1880f726f9e89871a3e8ec5c6a8e.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/48f887ae7dcff91795ef1b49312f682c.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b3a3fbb9f73eda2e303068e234177a8b.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0b90bbc84934cff25e4f2a4dd2e1bfdc.png)
﻿
﻿  

If you have multiple interview plans, we’d recommend setting up a grouped interview plan. This will allow you to categorize interview stages to allow for easy reporting in the future. You can find information on configuring this at [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/whats-the-difference-between-interview-plans-and-interviews#interviews)Interviews
For more information on setting up interviews, check out [Feedback Forms and Interviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/feedback-forms-and-interviews)﻿﻿
Interviews allow you to create interview instructions for your interviewers and assign feedback forms for them to fill out once the interview has been completed. These templates are used when setting up schedule activities and using the Add shared interview option.
﻿
Once an interview is scheduled, the interviewer will see the upcoming interview on their home page.
They can click on the interview to see the date and time of the interview, the briefing, the feedback form to be completed and any previous feedback (account settings permitting). Once the interview is completed, they will see a task on their home page to fill out the feedback form assigned.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d5a8a5d475f8980fde6a204bb8eb52f8.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/364d64ccd7f6c7130ff73cc9d7ce60a1.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/whats-the-difference-between-interview-plans-and-interviews#schedule-activities)Schedule Activities
For more on setting up schedule activities, check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿.
Schedule Interview Activities can be set up on the Interview Plan of a job or job template. When adding a schedule interview activity to your interview plan, once a candidate is moved to the stage with that activity, you’ll be prompted to schedule them in for an interview.
﻿
When adding the activity to your interview plan, you can assign interviewers, assign an interview that you created in [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews"), and configure your settings in advance. As such, once you receive the activity prompt for a candidate when they move from stage to stage, you can email out a direct booking or candidate availability link, or book them in manually from that prompt.

# Adding Lines to Reports & Forecasts
5 min
Copy
This document reviews how to add various line types to count over time and segmentation reports. These line types can be horizontal reference lines for target thresholds, or trend lines for progress tracking. The option to add forecasts and progress tracking is also covered.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/adding-lines-to-reports-and-forecasts#add-goal-lines-to-reports-to-count-over-time-repor)Add goal lines to reports to count over time reports
﻿
Adding goal lines to reports in Ashby is currently supported for count over time and segmentation report types. To add a line, simply click "Add Line" in the View Options section of the report configuration. Once a line is added you will have a new set of line options depending on your report configuration. The two types of lines, reference lines and goal lines, and an optional forecast summary are described below.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/adding-lines-to-reports-and-forecasts#reference-lines)Reference Lines
A reference line is a straight-line threshold to be rendered on any report. The reference line has three possible value choices:
  * **Custom** : a line is rendered at the numeric value specified by the user input


  * **Average** : a line is rendered at the average value of the reported data


  * **Median:** a line is rendered at the median value of the reported data


**Add a reference line with a custom value**
  1. Select + Add Line in the View Options section


  1. Enter an optional label for your line


  1. Select Reference Line as the line type


  1. Select Custom for the value and enter the target value


![Screen Shot 2022-12-27 at 7.37.46 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/HqGuSjNOLjwSxemRIyJIe_screenshot2022-12-27at73746am.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/adding-lines-to-reports-and-forecasts#trend-lines-forecasts)Trend Lines & Forecasts
Trend lines can be added to reports to represent a linear target over time. A trend line type requires an end value which represents the target for the end of the time period. The start value of the line is automatically provided to fit the linear growth over the time grouping and target end value provided. However, a custom start value can be entered.
When a count over time Count As is switched to Running Total, the option to forecast progress against the trend line is made available. Enabling the forecast summary provides a forecast based on historic progress in the report.
![Screen Shot 2021-09-03 at 4.17.14 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Jd1_zpOxuoycFxpTqqC0R_screenshot2021-09-03at41714pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Adding a trend line with a forecast**
  1. Select + Add Line in the View Options


  1. Set the report counting to Running Total


  1. Select the Trend Line line type


  1. Enter a target value you wish to reach by the end of your time period. The Start Value is optional and will default to a linear extrapolation from your end value


  1. Toggle on Include forecast?


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/adding-lines-to-reports-and-forecasts#forecast-details)Forecast Details
The forecast line is determined by your reported performance to date and does a linear extrapolation. When mousing over the forecast line, the current time elapsed, percent to goal, and estimate of your end value based on past activity are shown.
![details.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/xgfn5PurJwLPBFWgZbCls_details.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿

# Report Catalog - Advanced
48 min
Copy
This catalog focuses mostly on advanced reports, if you want to explore more basic reports take a look at our [Report Catalog - Essentials](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-essentials)﻿. For report templates, check out [Report Catalog: Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates)﻿
This document provides an overview of various types of reports you can run in Ashby. We use this document during implementation, to pick your initial set of reports, and it is also a great reference for expanding your reports over time.
All reports are grouped into high level categories.
Note, that with Ashby's flexible report builders, we can build any other kind of report you'd like, that is not shown here. If you're interested in that and have trouble building the report yourself, contact your Ashby point person!
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#before-you-get-started)Before you Get Started
As you read through the report catalog, keep in mind that all of the following reports can be filtered and grouped by any field and can be reported on for any timeframe you'd like.
We have some general recommendations on how to filter the reports:
  * Most companies tend to break up their hiring process into a few major areas (e.g. business and product & engineering). This is helpful since these different types of roles can have a vastly different recruiting process. When using any of the reports below, you most likely should run them separately for each of these major areas.


  * If you are hiring across different locations with very different local labor markets or recruiting processes, you will likely want to further separate your reports by location / region


  * For each of the reports below, filtering and/or grouping by Source, Department, Hiring Manager, etc. will allow you to compare relative performance between different parts of your team and different channels.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#conversion-rate-reports)Conversion Rate Reports
Conversion reports allow you to find inefficiencies in your hiring funnel. Are there parts of your funnel in which too many or too few candidates are moving to the next stage? You can answer these questions with the reports below.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#funnel-report)Funnel Report
Focuses on the overall, **historical,** pass-through rate from stage to stage.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/VZMzSus-Dw5xCqYMOkv5AAmJ.png)
﻿
By looking at the conversion rates of previously completed applications, you can get an estimate of how candidates that are currently in process might progress. You can also see if you are dropping too few or too many candidates at a particular stage.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#grouped-passthrough-report)Grouped Passthrough Report
Compare funnel metrics across different groups. Each subgroup includes a rollup of conversion rates.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/CxcvF99fvoGY1Bbk2xci10Ya.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#grouped-passthrough-report-with-projected-hires)Grouped Passthrough Report with Projected Hires
In the View Options of the passthrough report with groupings template, enable Show Active Count & Projected Hires option to enable projected hires based on the currently active applications selected in the report filters.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/OmAcY-ziqGJV73ghpiGrI06e.png)
﻿
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#skip-funnel-report)Skip Funnel Report
This type of report allows you to see conversion rates between two critical stages of your funnel, while skipping over the individual conversion rates for other stages. For example, you can answer: which % of recruiter screens end up in offers?
The most basic report of this kind looks like this:
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/4bWf9Vr6CkaeFcSrjV_nz_uO.png)
﻿
As any other report in Ashby, you can group this report type any field, e.g. source type:
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/fkT_rpm48w-2tCKcSttZmOn5.png)
﻿
Using the count over time report, you can also take a look at the skip funnel metric over time:
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/fF7N1btmMcEoedajfRX6nW50.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#ratios)Ratios
A different way to visualize conversion rates is to use ratios. You can create ratios by utilizing the "Generic Segmentation Report" and picking the visualization option "Single Number".
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/g2Q4O2RgLNafDifH94Z38dZl.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#conversion-rates-from-a-particular-stage-over-time)Conversion Rates from a particular stage over time
To spot trends in conversion rates over time, you can focus on a particular stage and take a look at how many candidates pass through that stage.
There are a few variations of this report, but one popular one is to look at onsite interviews and their outcome over time.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/4rYa1sLmA934SJVl-KBgalSI.png)
﻿
This report shows which percentage of onsite interviews that occurred each week ended up hired, rejected by you, rejected by the candidate or are still active. This kind of report lets you quickly spot if your conversion rate is changing from your historical average.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#candidate-flow-report)Candidate Flow Report
This report allows to take a look at the exact stage transitions that occurred in a specific timeframe. It can be either used to give status updates on recent activities on individual jobs or to take a detailed look at drop-off rates and drop-off reasons.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/K_M3XlttfNT_2Tv63scs8EkL.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#qualified-candidate-volume-over-time)Qualified Candidate Volume Over Time
This report type uses a calculated field to define what a qualified candidate is. In most cases we like to define it as anyone who has made it passed the application review stage(s). Looking at this metric you can easily see how the volume & quality of candidates has changed over time.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/Qv03Fk8id6L0rtY1-lLTcKEb.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#activity-reports)Activity Reports
Activity reports help you report on the volume of your activities. E.g. how many hires are you making? How many new candidates are you getting into interview process? How many interviews are you scheduling across different stages?
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#new-applications-by-source)New Applications by Source
See how the volume of new applications by source is changing over time. This report can give you a sense if new initiatives are performing as expected (e.g. if you just started sourcing for a new role).
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/DjsBtb3CAGdWpWr4gsuiAFD_.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#interviews-by-stage-over-time)Interviews by Stage Over Time
This is another way to look at if you are getting enough candidate volume to reach your hiring goals. Based on the conversion reports you will now how many interviews you need at specific stages in the funnel to make one hire. This report will show you if you're trending towards reaching that goal or not.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/AqweDBfDkIvmOvNXAv_HNPO1.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#hires-by-department)Hires by Department
You can use this report to track if you're reaching your overall hiring goals by department.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/OGKE98E8alhaZQvjheqEoBHv.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#offer-reports)Offer Reports
Offer reports show you how well you're doing at closing candidates that made it all the way through your interview process.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#overall-offer-acceptance-rate)Overall offer acceptance rate
This report allows you to get a simple trend of your offer acceptance rate over time. As with all reports you can look at this data by department, geography, or any other field you're interested in.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/6tokHxjrmuq1CaOjFnBaaqaB.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#offer-acceptance-rate-with-offer-numbers)Offer acceptance rate with offer numbers
This report allows you to look at offer volume and acceptance rate side-by-side. It's also well suited to be grouped by various fields (e.g. time, hiring manager, job, department, source, etc.).
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/ZVxjkNd3xfgAs4Z1dOZ1_ypY.png)
﻿
Here is another example of this report, this time grouped by source.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/8cTqHlIF8jJTLW6pkrfz3UJV.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#currently-pending-offers)Currently Pending Offers
You can use Ashby not only for historical data, but also for keeping track of current activities, e.g. how many offers are currently pending. The most simple version of this report is simply a tile on a dashboard that shows the total amount of pending offers (again, filtered by any field you want). As always in Ashby, you can then click into the number to see the underlying candidates.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/GdOEGPr5W3t6_F44hP0nq53V.png)
﻿
Alternatively you can show pending offers in aggregate. For example, below, all pending offers by department.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/Kur4kzes52VSJWBGdJq7gU46.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#source-quality-reports)Source Quality Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#hires-by-source-over-time)Hires by Source Over Time
This report can show you which candidate sources are contributing most to hires, and how that has changed over time.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/jsHjis5nyMFkGRSNW8JCsvxt.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#onsite-interview-outcomes-by-source)Onsite Interview Outcomes by Source
When looking at source quality, you don't only want to look at the absolute amount of hires contributed to each source, but also at the amount of effort your team is putting into each hire based on their source. One good measure for this is looking at onsite interview outcomes by source.
The heat map below allows you to quickly detect hotspots. For example you can see that, 71% of onsite interviews for candidates from Hired.com end in your team rejecting the candidate (note that we're using demo data for these examples).
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/TkdOrqOJ3YoKzLF4k4pTNU3Y.png)
﻿
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#archive-reason-reports)Archive Reason Reports
For archive reason analysis, be sure to check out [Best Practices: Managing & Analyzing Archive Reasons](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#candidate-rejection-reasons-over-time)Candidate rejection reasons over time
Use this report, to keep track of why candidates are pulling out of the interview process and how that has changed over time.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/HXXWodqZuPhDCPyZ7jjHmuvY.png)
﻿
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#hiring-speed-reports)Hiring Speed Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#time-in-process-by-job)Time in process by job
This report shows you how long candidates are on average spending in the hiring process. It gives you both an overall metric as well as a breakdown by stage.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/8r-LqWNCvMc9rQ0OhGoAKQXI.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#time-in-process-by-outcome)Time in process by outcome
Use filters to examine the final outcome of all candidates by their time in process. You can further examine the outcome of candidates that make it to the offer stage to see how big of an impact speed in process makes for final outcomes!
![Time in Process by Outcome.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/U7rM4SlhFzDmI40UxYi7X_timeinprocessbyoutcome.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#time-to-hire-by-custom-groupings-source-type-depar)Time to Hire by custom groupings: source type, department, gender, and so on.
From the Time to Hire template report, you can quickly adjust the filters and group by options to dynamically explore many different results. With this example, you’ll learn how to explore hiring speed by any grouping of interest.
![Time to Hire by Source Type.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/gceQzKSCrNwphZoEe9bi2_timetohirebysourcetype.png?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#current-pipeline-reports)Current Pipeline Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#current-pipeline-with-candidate-names)Current Pipeline with Candidate Names
This report allows you to explore the pipeline for individual jobs, while taking a look at candidate details.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/uHiu6KEEQxGjcFYiKuMKJF2I.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#current-pipeline-with-aggregate-numbers)Current Pipeline with Aggregate Numbers
This type of report can give you a high level overview of where active candidates are currently in process, showing the jobs they are interviewing for and the stage they are currently in.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/lnIeu1cn95v8MRfGyTDuZ1OP.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#load-balancing-and-capacity-planning-reports)Load Balancing and Capacity Planning Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#interviews-scheduled-by-coordinator)Interviews scheduled by coordinator
This report can give you a sense of how your scheduling load is balanced across coordinators.
Note that this report currently counts all scheduled panels (e.g. an onsite consisting of 4 interviews is counted as 1 in here), and counting is based on interviews that are scheduled and the coordinator assigned to the application to which the interview belongs. So it will not give you a perfectly accurate picture that considers, e.g. re-schedules. However we do find this very useful for high-level load balancing.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/QcNlSBBCXIdJFhx9SkSU1aGv.png)
﻿
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#interview-load)Interview Load
Explore how many hours interviewers on your team have spent interviewing. As any other report, you can look at this over time, group it by interview stage, etc.
  * Candidate hours: hours a candidate spends in interviews


  * Interviewer hours: aggregate hours across all interview participants


  * When this report is grouped by **Interviewer**


  * Candidate hours: aggregate calendar hours of each particular interviewer


  * Interviewer hours: aggregate hours across all interview participants


![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/_WECZsygDE_tAf8CXyxIjAix.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#job-reports)Job Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#open-job-count-by-job-status)Open job count by job status
This report allows you to see the total count of open jobs for a given time interval, grouped by the job status. This chart will give a visual sense for how often jobs remain open, in aggregate.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/oO07yUnD9F0EPLMfmk1QgSoQ.png)
﻿
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#jobs-by-active-candidate-count)Jobs by active candidate count
You can use a custom list report with the Active Candidate Count field to filter for Jobs that have at least 1 active candidate and juxtapose this with those in review, active processes or archived.
![Screen Shot 2022-08-10 at 2.38.36 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/0_zsXeIHapIQg_AdQmQW4_screenshot2022-08-10at23836pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#feedback-completion-reports)Feedback Completion Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#interview-feedback-sla)Interview Feedback SLA
This report shows how to identify interviewers that have not yet completed feedback for applicants that are still under consideration. Starting with a segmentation report on the Interview Participation subject, the filter selects for active applicants and incomplete feedback submission. In this example, an additional filter option selects for jobs in the Sales department. The sorted results show which Jobs have the largest number of pending feedback submissions by the individual interviewers.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/8HLJ42sO-evzDArCPuE3LLAW.png)
﻿
Similarly, with the same filter conditions you can adjust the grouping to be over the interviewer and interview scheduled in stage group. This chart reveals which individuals are delayed in submitting feedback, delineating between interview stage:
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/d-pl0g_NGV5seY3E8_ZAXi7P.png)
﻿
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#interviewer-calibration-reports)Interviewer Calibration Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#feedback-distribution-by-interviewer-template)Feedback distribution by interviewer (Template)
This report lets you see how frequently interviewers submit "strong yes"/"yes"/"no"/ "strong no" feedback. In the example below you can see the values expressed as %.
Typically you want to filter this report down to e.g. only hired candidates, or interviews for a particular job at a particular stage. That way you can detect if interviewers that interviewed under similar circumstances potentially skew more positive/negative.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/GT0qYK7DRBMPHkos_2t3ujf2.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#feedback-correlation-with-application-outcome)Feedback correlation with application outcome
With this report you can look at how the feedback a particular interviewer provided correlated with the outcome of the application.
Again, as in the other calibration reports, you will likely want to narrow this report down to a specific stage & job, to ensure that the data is relevant (for example, it is quite common for the earliest interview to be a "yes" even though the candidate might end up being rejected, simply because the chance of a candidate being rejected increases as they move down the funnel). Most commonly this report is used to calibrate on onsite interviews.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/mUFPV2Masdi5q0p-U0N2eF7Y.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#feedback-correlation-with-how-far-an-application-m)Feedback correlation with how far an application makes it through the interview process
This type of report can vary quite a bit, based on your job setup.
Generally it allows you to answer questions like: "how predictive is my technical phone screen of how far a candidate will make it in the process?".
If want to answer questions along these lines, let us know, and we're happy to set you up with a customized template!
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#sequence-reports)Sequence Reports
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#click-sorted-sequences)Click-Sorted Sequences
This example report looks at recent sequences where the Reply Status is Not Set but the sequence has been opened or has clicks. The results are sorted by clicks to float potentially high-interest candidates.
![Filters for the Click-Sorted Sequences report. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/GIHF4ITgXNIdKCGeV1Cr8_screenshot2023-01-24at73357am.png?format=webp)
﻿
Filters for the Click-Sorted Sequences report.
![Results section for the Click-Sorted Sequences report. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/mLwu89kJt3atMzqcWDs9A_screenshot2023-01-24at73926am.png?format=webp)
﻿
Results section for the Click-Sorted Sequences report.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#diversity-reports)Diversity Reports
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#intersectional-race-gender-pivot-table)Intersectional Race & Gender Pivot Table
This example shows how to use [Ashby’s pivot tables](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options)﻿ to produce an intersectional report on EEOC Gender and Race.
![dei example.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/GQd3IeWJnYPbl4PVl8wBZ_deiexample.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced#recruiter-screens-by-gender)Recruiter Screens by Gender
In this example we look at reporting on scheduled interview events by gender to examine the overall composition of recruiter screens over time. We use our intelligent Candidate’s Gender field which preferentially looks for EEOC data first and falls back on inferred gender results as a backup to maximize reporting coverage.
![Recruiter Screens by Gender.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/G8sPqZOKCb3yf-Iat0ySB_recruiterscreensbygender.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
﻿

Assessments
# Setting Up Assessment Integrations
4 min
Copy
This guide will walk you through setting up an assessment integration on an interview plan.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-assessment-integrations#set-up-an-assessment-activity)Set up an Assessment Activity
The first step is to add an assessment to an interview plan. To do this, we first need to choose a job, so go to your [job dashboard](https://app.ashbyhq.com/jobs/pipelines "job dashboard").
From there, select a job where you’d like to add a CodeSignal (the example used for this guide) assessment to its interview plan. Once you’re on a job page, choose Interview Plan on the left side:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/baa5da3bbc3556f27945637a43977b2e.png)
﻿
On the next screen, add your assessment as an activity to a stage of the interview plan. Click Add Activity To on the stage of the interview plan you’d like to create the activity for, then + Add Activity:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/09c8ccaba6b297220a800556888fee01.png)
﻿
If you have some configured, you'll see your assessment integrations in the Integrations section in the Add Activity list.
Select an assessment integration and then choose the assessment type from the dropdown on the next screen. The list in the drop down is pulled from your account with the assessment partner (e.g. the list of available CodeSignal tests in your CodeSignal account).
Once you select an assessment type, you'll be able to configure reviewers on the added activity.
Reviewers added here will be notified to leave feedback on the assessment (just like a take home or scheduled interview), when Ashby is notified that the assessment has been completed. Reviewers are not required.
![Untitled](https://cdn.zappy.app/41e3b7a2f191ac06b1932a6e8435e1ba.png)
﻿
![Untitled](https://cdn.zappy.app/54a52f1a3efd4c07b095618b44eb131d.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-assessment-integrations#start-the-assessment)Start the Assessment
Once a candidate has moved to the stage of the interview plan, where you added the assessment activity, you’ll see a new Start Assessment button appear on the candidate’s profile. Clicking that will show a modal, where you’ll be able to adjust, one final time, the kind of assessment type (from the configured assessment partner) that the user is going to take.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/699b57e003fe2e37329e481e648c4c59.png)
﻿
Once the assessment has been started, the candidate will be notified by the assessment partner, and Ashby will display updates here, in the drop down. Here's what a completed assessment looks like:
![Untitled](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3YS1tI9CUL9RUEt6SqlYr_untitled.png?format=webp)
﻿

﻿[Bryq](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bryq)﻿﻿  

﻿[Checkr](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-assessment-integrations "Checkr")﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Building Custom Reports & Dash...
# How To Copy a Dashboard
4 min
Copy
This document reviews the copy dashboard functionality. This feature allows you make copies of existing dashboards and specify how you want each underlying report to copy over to your new dashboard. 
It is not currently possible to create copies of core dashboards.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-to-copy-a-dashboard#overview)Overview
To make a copy of an existing dashboard, select More > Copy Dashboard.
  * The copy dashboard functionality is limited to admin licenses holders that have the ability to edit and create content in Ashby.


![Screen Cast 2022-11-28 at 11.39.27 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3w6qyboAzDWPki8ke-ZP6_screencast2022-11-28at113927am.gif)
﻿
When selecting Copy Dashboard, you'll be presented with an interface to optionally use the existing reports or create new copies of reports for your new dashboard.
  * **Use current** : Ashby allows the same report to be added to multiple dashboards. This will copy the report into your new dashboard using a reference to the existing report. The primary benefit of this choice is avoiding needless duplication of a report you do not plan to change.


  * **Save as new** : If you plan to modify a report on the new dashboard, use this option to save the report as a new copy. On your new dashboard, you can modify the report(s) without impacting the original dashboard they were copied from.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SxccatgonOlLJUItBxbbS_screenshot2021-08-04at40029pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Copy Dashboard Options**
  1. Give your new dashboard a name


  1. Select whether you want to include the existing reports or save new copies


  1. Optionally rename the reports you will copy over as new


  1. Select Copy to create your new dashboard


﻿  

#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-to-copy-a-dashboard#creating-a-copy-of-a-report)Creating a copy of a report
If you'd like to duplicate a report:
  1. Head to **Reports > Saved Reports **and select the report in question


  1. Click Save and Save as New Report... to create a new copy of your report


  1. Give your report copy a title and click Continue


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/4WNcrR9oqbjDNH-dxnB4d_count-by-segment-report-acent-ashby-2026-05-05-at-105226-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-to-copy-a-dashboard#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-to-copy-a-dashboard#what-happens-if-i-copy-a-confidential-dashboard)W**hat happens if I copy a**[ confidential dashboard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control)﻿**?**
The new dashboard copy will inherit the same privacy and access controls of the source dashboard. These can be modified on the new dashboard after a copy is made, if required. Please review the confidential reporting documentation for more information [Confidential Reporting & Content Access Control](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-to-copy-a-dashboard#why-would-i-save-a-report-as-a-new-copy-or-why-wou)Why **would I save a report as a new copy, or why would I use the current report in a new dashboard?**
Depending on your use case, you may want to have a uniquely filtered or modified report per dashboard copy. In this case, you'd want to use the save as new option per report. However, sometimes it's useful to reference the same report on multiple dashboards. The primary advantage of this is that if you need to update the underlying report, you can do so in one, and then all dashboards which reference the report will be updated.
﻿

Admin & Organization Settings
Job Board Setup
# Internal Job Board
7 min
Copy
When you enable the Internal Job Board option, admins and hiring managers can post roles on a job board that can only be accessed by your employees. Employees will need to be logged into Ashby to be able to access the job board.
**Internal Job Boards are available on Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:**  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#enabling-the-internal-job-board)Enabling the Internal Job Board
You can enable the internal job board option on the [Internal Job Board](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/internal-job-board "Internal Job Board") page in the Job Boards section of Admin. Once enabled, you can click the ‘Share’ button to copy the link to your internal job board. This link can then be shared with your employees so they can log in and view internal roles.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/90429eebb17c9e4e51f38a930a944e7b.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#posting-a-job-internally)Posting a job internally
When creating your job posting for the role in question, you can click the drop-down menu for Job Boards in the Basic Info tab. From here, you can post your job to your external job board, your internal job board, or both using the checkboxes.
Be sure to check the List on External Job Board and/or List on Internal Job Board check boxes if you’d like the postings to be listed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/428885293041e915153a4fd3414dfc06.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/18cba574eead0255a90023bff140a4c5.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#accessing-the-internal-job-board)Accessing the Internal Job Board
Employees will be able to access the internal job board via a shared link or from their [Home](https://app.ashbyhq.com/home/upcoming "Home") page once the internal job board option is enabled.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a852a47c58423d4a00a6288b617e2f33.png)
﻿
The internal job board has a similar layout to the external job board but logos and Top and Bottom Descriptions set up on the [Theme (Job Board)](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/theme "Theme \(Job Board\)") page will not be displayed. Job Post Description Opening and Closing text will still be shown when clicking on each job posting. Jobs can still be filtered by Department, Location and Employment Type.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b360db81818b7a7b23eb25343d96d10c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#why-is-my-job-posting-showing-as-unlisted)Why is my job posting showing as Unlisted?
This is usually because the List on _External Job Board_ or the _List on Internal Job Board_ checkbox is unchecked for the job posting. Ensure that you’ve checked either checkbox (or both, if you’re listing the job both externally and internally).
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#can-employees-without-ashby-access-view-the-intern)Can employees without Ashby access view the internal job board?
No. Employees will need to log in to view the Internal Job Board.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#can-i-display-the-hiring-team-and-the-requisition)Can I display the hiring team and the requisition ID for a job on my internal job board?
You can, yes! To toggle this option on, head to Admin > Job Boards > [Internal Job Board](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/internal-job-board "Internal Job Board").
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/95aa2d3082cb39c6b262f9b1623b9cfc.png)
﻿
You’ll see the following options:
**1: Show Requisition ID on Job Board:** Show the requisition ID on each job on the internal job board.
**2: Show Hiring Team Info on Job Board:** You can toggle on the _Show Hiring Team Info on Job Board_ option to show the hiring team for each job posting on your job board.
**3: Roles to Display in Hiring Team Info:** Here you can determine which roles will be shown in the hiring team info.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/943343095e65852c9381c94c87c9e5d4.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/71d88c6d75c0d63eabcfa362e313a2c3.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c8c61b8b9ef046a1629a9460138bf450.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#how-can-i-identify-an-internal-candidate-or-applic)How can I identify an internal candidate or application from the internal job board?
A candidate who has applied from a posting listed on your internal job board will have their source listed as Internal: Internal Transfer. You can opt to filter for candidates with this source when doing a candidate search, or check the source listed in the Attribution section of the candidate’s profile.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6baa17c573a4d2b6166125acc22472e6.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#can-agency-users-access-the-internal-job-board)Can agency users access the internal job board?
No, agency users will not be able to access the internal job board from their home page. They're also prevented from directly visiting the internal job board URL and direct internal job posting URLs.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board#can-applicants-be-moved-to-a-different-stage-in-th)Can applicants be moved to a different stage in the interview plan if they apply via the internal job board?
No, candidates who apply via your internal job board will be added to the Application Review stage.
﻿

<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Admin & Organization Settings
Data Management
# Data Privacy and Compliance
31 min
Copy
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#data-privacy-and-compliance-overview)Data Privacy and Compliance Overview
Ashby provides tools to help you maintain proper compliance with local and federal data privacy regulations with regards to storing candidate data. You can configure these tools in the [Admin > Data Management > Data Privacy & Compliance](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-compliance "Admin > Data Management > Data Privacy & Compliance")﻿
See the table below for more information on the access roles that have the ability to configure rules in Ashby to help maintain compliance.  
| Access Role  | Can Configure Rules?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin (Private)  | ❌  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
On the Data Privacy and Compliance page, you'll have access to three tabs:
  * ﻿[Inactivity Rules](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EA7Xb9exEKnQhjBBYTAvP#9mbSk)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Consent Rules](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EA7Xb9exEKnQhjBBYTAvP#zG2XR)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Privacy & Legal ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EA7Xb9exEKnQhjBBYTAvP#rPrpq)﻿﻿  



![The Data Privacy and Compliance rule configuration page in Ashby. The Inactivity Rules tab is selected.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/LMLSr3pbgH1ks6TPVVXa4_data-privacy-compliance-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-113834-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#data-compliance-inactivity-and-consent-rules)Data Compliance: Inactivity and Consent Rules
You can use a combination of inactivity and consent-based rules to put a flexible policy into place that helps you keep track of which candidate data should be removed from your system. 
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#inactivity-rules)Inactivity Rules
Inactivity rules are set based on when the candidate became inactive. There are two types of rules:
  * **Inactive Lead Rule** - a rule that governs how long you should keep the data of Lead candidates. This is a retention rule for candidates that are seen as inactive because they have no applications or only have applications that are in Lead stages.


![The menu to edit an inactive lead rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vGGMrAEuHx3C3wKCjo0sn_example-inactive-lead-rule.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Inactive Applicant Rule** - a rule that governs how long you should keep the data of Inactive candidates. This is a retention rule for candidates who are seen as inactive because they only have applications in the Archived stage.


![The menu to edit an inactive applicant rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sHjoPPBc8iheEs_WXovGM_example-inactive-application-rule.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#amending-the-default-inactivity-based-rule)Amending the default inactivity-based rule
After clicking on one of your default inactivity-based rules, you can further configure them by:
  * Updating basic information like adding an internal description for reference.


  * Amending the retention period, which defines the time period after which the Inactive Lead or Inactive Applicant should be marked as needing to be anonymized or deleted.


  * Configure a Rule Filter using the Add Field to Match option to add fields and establish criteria. Candidates who meet the set criteria will have the rule applied to them.


Once you’ve reconfigured the rule, you will have the option to re-evaluate your inactivity or consent-based rule for candidates matching your rule filters in the **Danger Zone** section. Click Re-evaluate X Candidates to take this action.
Re-evaluating the rule for candidates will not delete or anonymize candidates automatically, it will only flag those in violation of your configured rule.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#creating-an-additional-inactivity-based-rule)Creating an additional inactivity-based rule
You can create additional inactive lead and inactive application rules. These allow you to set different location criteria and retention periods, meaning greater flexibility and compliance control over candidate data. See [below](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EA7Xb9exEKnQhjBBYTAvP#B0cNG)﻿ for examples of rules configurations.
To create a new inactivity-based rule:
  1. Ensure you are on the Inactivity Rules tab of the Data Privacy & Compliance page.


  1. Click + New and select either +Inactive Lead Rule or +Inactive Applicant Rule


![The Data Privacy and Compliance page with the New button highlighted.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/UpEAeciLBp5pD2C0XpnRL_data-privacy-compliance-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-113935-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. For additional inactivity rules, complete the following information:


**Title:** You can click the current title of the rule to amend and rename it.
**Description (Optional):** Add an internal description for your rule.
**Retention Period:** If the inactive lead or applicant matches your rule, the retention period will determine how long after the candidate was created (for an inactive lead rule) or how long after the candidate's final application was archived (for an inactive applicant rule) before they are considered in violation of your configured rules.
**Location:** Here you can determine your location criteria:
**Global:** Covers all locations configured in Ashby. Please note that you can only configure one rule per type with the Global Location setting (to avoid conflicts).
**Specific Locations:** This option will include a dropdown that will allow you to specify the locations that this rule should apply to. For more on configuring locations, check out [Set up Departments & Locations](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations)﻿.
**Advanced:** Switch on the Advanced option and click + Add Field to Match to set your criteria using a range of location fields and values. You can also set further criteria in the **Additional Criteria** section.
If you do not enable the **Advanced** toggle and specify location fields, the locations you select for data rules within the Locations section refer to the locations assigned within your job's Settings section.
![The Locations section of configuring a new data retention rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/J5OMtQbYNG8ofbXmNnRgF_new-inactive-applicant-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-115705-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Once you're ready to enable your rule, switch on Is Enabled in the **Basic Info** section.


![The Basic Info section of a data retention rule with Is Enabled highlighted.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/GHUPk5MKnHx_1JTGcX-CO_is-enabled-toggle.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. In the **Danger Zone** section, you will also have the option to re-evaluate candidates who meet your rule criteria and apply the rule to them if they qualify. Click Re-evaluate X Candidates to take this action. 


Re-evaluating the rule for candidates will not delete or anonymize candidates automatically, it will only flag those in violation of your configured rule.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#consent-rules)Consent Rules
Consent-based rules are set based on the date consent was given by a candidate.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#amending-the-default-consent-based-rule)Amending the default consent-based rule
After clicking into the default consent-based rule, you can further configure it by:
  * Adding a description for internal reference.


  * Choosing the consent request period, which defines how long of a period of consent we will ask of the candidate when requesting consent via your jobs page or via automation settings.


  * Create a rule filter, which restricts which Candidates this rule should apply to.


  * Set up consent collection on Job Posting pages in the Jobs Page Settings section. The consent collection option will show underneath the application form on the job posting.


  * Set up [Automation Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EA7Xb9exEKnQhjBBYTAvP#IzFwn)﻿ to automatically send out an email with a link to a consent form based on a set of rules.


  * This can be used to collect consent from non-applicants, such as sourced candidates (for example, those added via the Chrome extension) or candidates that were manually added to Ashby.


  * You can also set up consent extension automation settings to automatically request a consent extension prior to the consent retention date.


![The edit menu for the default consent-based rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sLi6SgKifYUGdSQhMgAhV_default-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-13346-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#creating-an-additional-consent-rule)Creating an additional consent rule
You can create additional consent rules so you can set different location criteria and retention periods. For examples of multiple rule configurations, check out the examples [below](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EA7Xb9exEKnQhjBBYTAvP#B0cNG)﻿.
To create a new consent rule:
  1. Ensure you are on the Consent Rules tab of the Data Privacy & Compliance page.


  1. Click + New and select + Consent Rule


![The consent rules tab with the New button highlighted.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/rJwNwI8NsmTotjPQ3uU2m_data-privacy-compliance-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-14625-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
For additional consent rules, complete the following information:
**Title:** You can click the current title of the rule to amend and rename it.
**Description (Optional):** Add an internal description for your rule.
**Consent Request Period:** The request period defines how long of a period of consent we will ask of the candidate when when requesting consent via your jobs page or via automation settings.
**Custom Consent Form Text:** Optional custom consent form text specifically for this rule. If this is left blank, the consent form text set within the Direct Consent Request Settings will be used instead.
![The basic info section of a consent rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/mOxMFa8G6ibZ7FtMKJyuX_consent-rule-basic-info.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Location:** Here you can determine your location criteria:
**Global:** Covers all locations configured in Ashby
**Specific Locations:** This option will include a dropdown that will allow you to specify the locations you have configured in Ashby that this rule should apply to. For more on configuring locations, check out [Set up Departments & Locations](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/set-up-departments-and-locations)﻿.
**Advanced:** Switch on the Advanced option and click + Add Field to Match to set your criteria using a range of location fields and values. You can also set further criteria in the **Additional Criteria** section.
If you do not enable the **Advanced** toggle and specify location fields, the locations you select for data rules within the Locations section refer to the locations assigned within your job's Settings section.
![The location section of the consent rule menu with the Advanced toggle on, displaying the Additional Criteria section.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RP1SROibyiDXwEexGRDjk_locations-and-additional-criteria.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#consent-rules-jobs-page-settings)Consent rules: Jobs Page Settings
On the Jobs Page Settings tab of your consent rule, you can enable the Collect Consent on Jobs Pages option. Once enabled, you can then configure criteria so only the job pages that meet the filters you set will request consent.
![The Jobs Page Settings section of a consent rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIPHfTpomHdOHDZV2H-CI-20260318-115118.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#consent-rules-automation-settings)Consent rules: Automation Settings
Within the Automation Settings tab, you’ll see two options, Initial Consent and Consent Extension.
![The Automation Settings tab of a consent rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/HD48pq556Y2p844zVD43d_default-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-13841-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#initial-consent-automation-settings)**Initial Consent Automation Settings**
Initial Consent automation settings allow you to determine the conditions in which the first consent request should be sent. Click Create a Draft Configuration to create a draft.
![The Automation Settings tab of a consent rule with the Create a Draft Configuration option highlighted.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/amynBtUlgP2kc81TGI_D9_new-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-20127-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
In the Condition section, you can add filters by clicking Add Field to Match _._ You can then specify any criteria candidates should meet to then be sent the initial consent request email.
Use the Email Template field to determine the template that should be sent to the candidate and the Delay dropdown to specify when the email should be sent.
If you need to add an additional automation rule to create a separate set of criteria, click Create New Automation Rule.
![The menu to create a new automation rule.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/A-BWrZyuAluARLBpiPPYF_new-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-20330-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Once you’re ready to use your rule or rules, click Activate Draft Configuration _._ When a candidate meets the conditions you’ve set in your automation, they’ll receive the email you’ve specified in the Email Template field.
![The Activate Draft Configuration button.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/hO5ICE4Zb3BRIbwiQNE-o_new-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-20435-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#consent-extension-automation-settings)**Consent Extension Automation Settings**
Consent Extension automation rules allow you to determine when a consent extension request email should be sent out to the candidate.
Click Create a Draft Configuration to start setting up your automation rule.
![The Create a Draft Configuration button for consent extension automation.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/7ExNMDvUUf6sS_VDD8laQ_new-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-20854-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can use the Advance notice period field to determine how far in advance the request should be sent. In the pictured example, the consent extension request email will be sent 21 Days prior to the data listed in the Consent Retention Date field on the candidate’s profile.
You can then determine the email template and the sender email address using the Email Template and From email address fields.
If you’d like to add a filter to your rule, click Add Field to Match in the Filter section to set your criteria. If this section is left blank, all candidates that the consent rule applies to will be sent an extension request prior to their consent expiring.
To create an additional consent extension automation rule, click Create New Automation Rule.
![The settings for the automation.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/UGBb-HZWLX441JcAwRVwg_new-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-20947-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Once you’re ready to use your rule, click Activate Draft Configuration _._
![The Activate Draft Configuration button is highlighted within the Consent Extension tab.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SXGfmSzuPNkyINjefChJS_new-consent-rule-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-21323-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Once you activate your draft consent extension configuration rule, it will be applied to all candidates meeting your criteria.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#open-for-examples-of-rule-configurations)Open for examples of rule configurations
The below examples are purely illustrative. Please consult with your legal team and organization's policies when configuring your own data compliance rules within your Ashby account.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#example-based-on-candidate-location)Example based on candidate location
In our first example, our test company has different retention periods for inactive candidates who are located in Germany. As such, an additional inactive applicant rule can be created here with the appropriate retention period and the Advanced option switched on in the Locations section. The Advanced option allows them to use the Candidate's Location field, meaning that this is the field that is evaluated when applying the rule.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/r63-h8Penq73foVnR2h-L_illustrative-example-inactive-applicant-rule.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#example-based-on-job-posting-location)Example based on job posting location
In this second example, the test company is looking to set a different consent request period and different consent form text for job postings with their locations in the United States.
When configuring an additional consent rule based on job posting location, the Advanced toggle should be switched on again and the Candidate's Job Consideration's Job Posting's Location field can be used to determine the value fields.
The consent period can then be clarified in the **Consent Period Request** section and the alternative consent form text can be added to the **Custom Consent Form Text** field.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wcbIMy8ouuZbIHl-KdlSA_example-consent-rule-job-posting-location.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#privacy-legal-settings)Privacy & Legal Settings
On the Privacy & Legal tab of the Data Privacy & Compliance page, you can set the following:
  * Recruiting Privacy Policy


  * Legal Entity Name


  * Automated Processing Legal Notice


![The Privacy and Legal tab of the Data Privacy and Compliance page in Ashby.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vVGxQRBylJMKDVEFPGopQ_data-privacy-compliance-acent-ashby-2026-03-17-at-21535-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#recruiting-privacy-policy)**Recruiting privacy policy**
Add a link to a privacy policy here that contains the details of how you will manage candidate data. This link will appear on any consent forms that candidates complete.
You can set a **Recruiting Privacy Policy** in the [Data Privacy & Compliance](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-compliance "Data Privacy & Compliance") section of Admin, under the Privacy & Legal section tab. When you do so, a link to the policy will appear on any Consent Forms that candidates fill out.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#legal-entity-name)Legal Entity Name
The legal entity name used for data consent requests, EEOC, and other legal contexts. You can leave this blank to use your Organization Name.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#automated-processing-legal-notice)Automated Processing Legal Notice
Here you can configure a legal notice to inform candidates that their data may be processed using AI.
For more on Ashby's AI tools, check out [Ashby AI Features](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-ai-features)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#viewing-data-compliance-status-on-candidate-profil)Viewing Data Compliance Status on Candidate Profile
You can view a Candidate’s Data Retention dates (as determined by the rules above) on the Candidate Profile under the Summary tab. You will see a section called Data Retention which displays the date after which the candidate will be considered in violation of your rules.
![A candidate profile, open to the Summary section.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/6KUyoI8J51uBXf-7ZzGSJ_data-privacy-and-compliance-ashby-knowledge-base-2026-03-17-at-30601-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If you have consent extension automation settings in place, then you’ll see the date that the consent extension request will be sent.
Find Data Retention details for a Candidate under the Summary section on the Candidate Profile
You can click Override to get the following options:
**Override & Ignore:** Choose to ignore data retention rules for the candidate completely.
**Override Date:** Choose a date for data retention that takes precedence over any other rules.
![The options to override or ignore data retention rules on the candidate profile.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jD9Oi5j9lFoFqkJuw00eM_data-privacy-compliance-updates-archbee-document-2026-03-17-at-30625-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If you opt to choose a new date for data retention via the Override Date option, then the consent extension request date will adjust to fit the new date.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#when-are-candidates-considered-in-violation)When are candidates considered in violation?
For more on reporting on data retention rules and violations, check out [Reporting on Data Retention Rules, Violations of Rules and Consent Choice](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-data-retention-rules-violations-of-rules-and-consent-choice)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#what-happens-if-multiple-compliance-rules-apply-to)What happens if multiple compliance rules apply to a candidate or lead?
Multiple compliance rules may apply to a candidate or lead if:
  * They apply to a job posting with multiple locations applied to it and one or some locations have different inactivity or consent rules configured for them.


  * If a candidate otherwise meets the criteria set for more than one inactivity and/or consent rule.


If you have multiple rules configured and multiple rules apply to a candidate, the rule with the longest retention period will apply to the candidate. For example, if Rule A says delete or anonymize data after 1 year and Rule B says delete or anonymize data after 3 years, the candidate's retention date will be set by Rule B (3 years).
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#how-does-location-hierarchy-work-with-compliance-r)How does location hierarchy work with compliance rules?
Location hierarchy across jobs and job postings is supported in compliance rules. For example:
If the rule is scoped to the United States region and the job location is set to California, the rule **will** match because the United States location is the region that the California location is linked to.
If the rule is scoped to the California location and the job location is set to the United States region, the rule **will not** match because California is a descendant of the United States region and the specification will be respected.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#should-i-anonymize-or-delete-candidates)Should I anonymize or delete candidates?
When candidates are marked as in violation, any action taken (deleting or anonymizing) will need to be done manually.
Anonymizing candidates will get rid of any PII, but retain enough information to provide useful data for your analytics and reporting workflows. It is important to know that anonymization cannot be undone.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#can-i-override-a-data-retention-date-when-importin)Can I override a data retention date when importing candidates via CSV?
You can, yes. Once you’ve uploaded your CSV file, you can map the date listed in your file to map to the Data Compliance Override Date field in Ashby. If you would also like to include an override reason, you can map these fields to the Data Compliance Override Reason field in Ashby.
When importing these fields, this will be included on the candidate profile and take precedence over any other data retention dates.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7f3c19045c8c4bde22ba7d3f9f80b563.png)
﻿
﻿  

You can find more on importing candidates via CSV at [Bulk Import Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#how-does-anonymizing-a-candidate-work-with-the-ema)How does anonymizing a candidate work with the email events on the candidate’s feed?
Once a candidate has been anonymized, a record of email events will still show on the candidate’s feed (including the date and time the email was sent). However, the subject, body and the candidate’s email address will be anonymized.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#what-happens-if-a-candidate-receives-an-initial-co)What happens if a candidate receives an initial consent email but doesn’t click the link and follow the steps to provide consent?
In this case, the Consent Retention Rate field on the candidate profile will still show No Consent Collected, as the candidate has yet to provide consent.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#im-seeing-multiple-consent-form-completion-events)I’m seeing multiple consent form completion events within the Forms section of a candidate’s job consideration - why?
If a candidate has applied for a role multiple times, they may have also completed the consent form multiple times too as part of the application. As duplicate applications are merged, only one job consideration will show on the candidate profile but each application and consent form completed will be listed within the singular job consideration for your reference. More on this at [How do I merge duplicate candidate profiles?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-merge-duplicate-candidate-profiles)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#how-are-inactivity-based-rules-and-consent-based-r)H**ow are inactivity-based rules and consent-based rules set in Ashby applied to hired candidates?**
Candidates in the Hired stage are treated as active candidates. This is because hired candidates have an active employment relationship with your organization, which provides a legitimate business interest for retaining their data.
The employment of the hired candidate also creates a legal basis for data retention that supersedes the need for consent-based or inactivity-based retention rules to be applied. As such, they will not be considered or marked as in violation of the rules you configure in Ashby.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#what-happens-when-a-rule-is-disabled)What happens when a rule is disabled?
You can disable a data compliance rule by switching off the Is Enabled? toggle when viewing the rule details. When a rule is disabled, all candidates who had that rule as their applied rule are re-evaluated. If another applicable rule exists, it becomes the new applied rule. If no other rule applies, the candidate's retention date for that category is cleared.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#what-happens-when-a-rule-is-archived)What happens when a rule is archived?
You can archive a rule by opening up the rule details and clicking the Archive button.
A rule must be disabled before it can be archived. Once archived, it cannot be re-enabled (it is effectively retired). The same re-evaluation that happens on disable applies here as well. Archived rules are hidden from the active rules list but remain in the system for audit purposes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#what-happens-to-automated-consent-emails-when-a-co)W**hat happens to automated consent emails when a consent rule is archived or disabled?**
Any scheduled automated consent extension emails tied to that rule are canceled. This prevents outdated or irrelevant emails from being sent to candidates.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance#if-multiple-consent-rules-apply-to-the-same-job-po)I**f multiple consent rules apply to the same job posting, which consent form is shown to applicants?**
The consent form from the rule with the longest retention period is displayed. This ensures candidates are asked for consent under the most protective rule.
﻿

Emails & Candidate Texting
Candidate Texting
# Candidate Texting
25 min
Copy
This guide assumes your team has completed A2P 10DLC registration and walks through texting a candidate via Ashby once this setup is complete. If you’ve yet to set up candidate texting, please complete [Candidate Texting Onboarding](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding)﻿. Texting will not work if onboarding isn’t completed first. Texting currently requires setup support, please contact your CSM or Support to enable it for your account.
Candidate Texting is available on the Foundations, Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
