# Advanced Scheduling Metrics
37 min
Copy
If your company spends a significant amount of time on recruiting coordination, advanced scheduling metrics enable you to monitor and analyze the efficiency of your teams, identify potential bottlenecks, and uncover meaningful opportunities to improve.
We’ve introduced several new reporting subjects and filter fields within Ashby’s reporting suite, letting you:
  1. See how your scheduling process is performing at a glance.


  1. Dig deep into granular details with powerful drill-down tools.


Please note that Advanced scheduling metrics are included as part of the Advanced Scheduling Add-on. More on this at [Advanced Scheduling Automation Add-On](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on)﻿﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#overview)Overview
Please review the video below for an overview of the new subjects and fields.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#new-reporting-subjects)New Reporting Subjects
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#interview-schedule-versions)Interview Schedule Versions
A coordinator attempting to schedule an interview may go through multiple iterations of scheduling efforts due to cancellations, modifications, or rescheduling.
Each time a new schedule is confirmed for an interview (whether by modification or rescheduling), it will be represented by a new Interview Schedule Version.
For example, an onsite that is scheduled, then goes through three subsequent reschedules will produce four distinct Interview Schedule Versions. This is important because it provides you with a clear view of the actual scheduling volume that is happening across your team, as opposed to just counting each interview - some interviews are simple, and some take a lot more effort!
Interview Schedule Versions have several distinguishing characteristics to provide visibility into the scheduling activities of your team and the reasons why schedules may have required some sort of change. Below you will find a list of the fields that can be displayed or used as filters within your reporting.
A reschedule involves adjusting interview participants, start/end times, or amending the number of interviews. A modification is any other kind of adjustment (for example, updating the interview briefing, adding a Zoom link, etc).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#fields)Fields  
| **Field Name**  | **Description**  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Version  | The numeric version of this schedule version (for example, the original schedule would be 1 and the first reschedule would be 2).  |  
| Is Latest Version?  | Yes/No field - whether this is the latest version of the interview schedule.  |  
| Scheduled By  | Who the interview version was scheduled by.  |  
| Scheduled By Type  | The method used for scheduling this interview version (for example, Auto-Schedule, Manual Schedule).  |  
| Created At  | The date and time when this interview schedule version was created.  |  
| Scheduled At  | The date and time when this interview schedule version was scheduled.  |  
| Cancelled At  | The date and time when this interview schedule version was cancelled (if applicable).  |  
| Hold Requested?  | Whether holds were used when scheduling this interview schedule version.  |  
| Interviews Start At  | The date and time the first interview will start at for this interview schedule version.  |  
| Interviews End At  | The date and time the last interview will end at for this interview schedule version.  |  
| Number of Events  | The number of events within the schedule interview version.  |  
| Number of Interview Participants  | The number of interviewers scheduled within the schedule interview version.  |  
| Has Participant Decline?  | Whether any interviewers participating in this schedule interview version have responded to the calendar invite with Declined.  |  
| Has Candidate Decline?  | Whether the candidate participating in this schedule interview version has responded to the calendar invite with Declined.  |  
| Is Multi-Day Schedule?  | Yes/No - Do the events in this schedule interview version span across multiple days.  |  
| Version Schedule Type  | The schedule type for the schedule interview version (for example Original Schedule, Modification or Reschedule).  |  
| Schedule  | Pull additional fields from the current interview schedule linked to this schedule interview version.  |  
| Job Consideration  | Pull additional fields from the job consideration linked to this schedule interview version.  |  
| Location Type  | The meeting location type for the interview (e.g In person, Zoom, Google Meet).  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#report-examples)Report Examples
Below are some common questions and examples of reports that can help you find the data points needed.
**Question:** How much of your scheduling effort is going towards initial interview scheduling vs. reschedules over time? What does that look like by coordinator, job, or interview stage?
**Report Type:** Segmentation **Subject:** Interview Schedule Versions **Time Range:** Interview Schedule Version’s Created At **Filters:** Optional (can filter by job, stage, department, etc) **Group By:** Interview Schedule Version’s Scheduled By Interview Schedule Version’s Version Schedule Type
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/689bd4f31acac7e04239a7e58a36e705.png)
﻿
**Question:** Across all interview scheduling activity, how much is your team using each scheduling method?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time **Subject:** Interview Schedule Versions **Time Range:** Interview Schedule Version’s Created At **Filters:** Optional (can filter by job, stage, department, etc). **Group By:** Interview Schedule Version’s Scheduled By Type
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d78e6243041fc2ea982eaaa3b601cf89.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#interview-schedule-summaries)Interview Schedule Summaries
While Interview Schedule Versions provide granular views into each scheduling action, Interview Schedule Summaries are a powerful concept that enable you to get insights across all interview schedule versions for interview activities. An onsite that goes through one initial scheduling and three subsequent reschedules will have four versions, but only one summary.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#fields-1)Fields  
| **Field Name**  | **Description**  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Latest Version Number  | The total number of versions of this interview schedule (for example, if an interview has been rescheduled once, this value would be 2 - one for the original schedule and 2 for the reschedule).  |  
| Title  | The title of this interview schedule.  |  
| Interview Stage  | The title of this interview schedule's interview stage.  |  
| Interview Stage Group  | The title of this interview schedule's interview stage group.  |  
| Original Version's Scheduled At  | The date and time the original version of the interview schedule was scheduled at.  |  
| Latest Version's Scheduled At  | The date and time the latest version of the interview schedule was scheduled at.  |  
| Scheduled By  | The user who scheduled the interview schedule.  |  
| Scheduled By Type  | The method used for scheduling the latest version of this interview schedule.  |  
| Cancelled At  | The date and time at which the latest version of the interview schedule was cancelled.  |  
| Original Version's Start Time  | The start time of the first interview of the original version of the interview schedule.  |  
| Latest Version's Start Time  | The start time of the first interview of the latest version of the interview schedule.  |  
| Latest Version's Number of Interview Events  | The number of interview events within the latest version of the interview schedule.  |  
| Latest Version's Number of Interview Participants  | The number of unique interviewers scheduled to be a part of the latest version of the interview schedule.  |  
| Availability Requested?  | Whether candidate availability was requested for this interview schedule.  |  
| Hold Requested?  | Whether holds were used when scheduling this interview schedule version.  |  
| Used Direct Booking?  | Whether direct booking links where used when scheduling this interview schedule.  |  
| Had Participant Decline?  | Whether any interviewers participating in this schedule interview version have responded to the calendar invite with Declined.  |  
| Had Candidate Decline?  | Whether the candidate participating in this schedule interview version has responded to the calendar invite with Declined.  |  
| Version Schedule Type  | The category of scheduling activity type across all versions of this schedule.  |  
| Job Consideration  | Pull additional fields from the job consideration linked to this schedule interview version.  |  
| Availability First Submitted At  | The timestamp when availability was first submitted at for this interview schedule.  |  
| Availability Last Submitted At  | The timestamp when availability was last submitted at for this interview schedule.  |  
| Availability Submission Count  | The total number of availability submissions for this interview schedule.  |  
| Version Schedule Type (Confirmed Only)  | The category of scheduling activity type across versions after the initial confirmation, excluding any changes made during a hold. Original Schedule means no changes were made after the schedule was first confirmed. Useful for understanding the amount of disruption caused by changes to the interview schedule after confirming with the candidate.  |  
| Stage Entered At  | The time at which this interview schedule first entered its current stage.  |  
﻿  

When reporting on Interview Schedule Summaries, if a summary includes any versions that were reschedules, the summary will be classified as a Reschedule for the Version Schedule Type field (so if a Summary has versions that are both modifications and reschedules, it will be classified as a Reschedule). If a Summary includes only modification versions, it will be classified as a Modification. And if a summary only has a single initial version, it will be classified as Original Schedule.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#report-examples-1)Report Examples
Below are some common questions and examples of reports that can help you find the data points needed.
**Question:** How many interview schedules involved a rescheduling event at all (i.e. across any version)?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time (or Segmentation if you’d like to look at a total figure for a timeframe). **Subject:** Interview Schedule Summaries **Time Range:** Interview Schedule Summary’s Original Version’s Scheduled At **Filters:** Optional **Group By:** Interview Schedule Summary’s Version Schedule Type
**Note:** Be sure to adjust your view options to show a percentage on the Y axis if you’d like to view this as a percentage.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9545e9b683a1b3ee2490026a2b5b057b.png)
﻿
**Question:** How often are reschedules or modifications happening by department, job, interview stage, etc?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time
**Subject:** Interview Schedule Summaries
**Time Range:** Interview Schedule Summary’s Latest Version’s Scheduled At
**Filters:** Interview Schedule Summary’s Version Schedule Type equals Reschedule OR Modification.
**Group By:** Interview Schedule Summary’s Job Consideration’s Job, Interview Schedule Summary’s Job Consideration’s Job’s Department or Interview Schedule Summary’s Interview Stage.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/EoWhWtI-O0bocP24zjdjd_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-03-24-at-42828-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Question:** How many interview schedules involve a candidate entering their availability more than once across departments?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time
**Subject:** Interview Schedule Summaries
**Time Range:** Interview Schedule Summary’s Latest Version’s Scheduled At
**Filters:** Availability Submission Count is greater than or equal to 2
**Group By:** Department
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ddb050fc33737ab9ee7bc989a58394df.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#interviewer-rsvps)Interviewer RSVPs
This subject is particularly helpful for understanding interviewer declines and improving poor interviewer habits in response promptness and interview attendance. You can measure interviewer participation insights and trends on granular level, with deep visibility into things like RSVP response times (how quickly do different participants respond to interview invites?) and notice times (how close to the time of the interview are participants responding?).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#fields-2)Fields  
| **Field Name**  | **Description**  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Interviewer  | The employee who is conducting the interview.  |  
| Status  | The interviewer's response to the interview's calendar invitation.  |  
| RSVP Note  | The note left by the interviewer when submitting their RSVP.  |  
| Submitted At  | The date and time the interviewer submitted the RSVP for the event.  |  
| Interview Title  | The title of the interview that the interviewer RSVP'd for.  |  
| Interview Role  | The interviewer's role for this interview.  |  
| Interviewer Pool  | The interviewer pool that this interviewer was chosen from.  |  
| Is Feedback Required?  | Whether the interviewer was required to leave feedback for this interview.  |  
| Response Time (Hours)  | The duration (in hours), between the invite being sent and the interviewer's response.  |  
| Notice (Hours)  | The duration (in hours) between the interviewer's response to the calendar invite and the start time of the interview.  |  
| Was Rescheduled After?  | Whether the interview was rescheduled after the interviewer submitted their RSVP.  |  
| Schedule Version  | Pull additional fields from the interview schedule.  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#report-examples-2)Report Examples
Below are some common questions and examples of reports that can help you find the data points needed.
**Question:** Which participants are declining interviews most often?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time **Subject:** Interviewer RSVPs **Time Range:** Interviewer RSVP’s Submitted At **Filters:** Interviewer’s RSVP Status equals Declined Interviewer’s RSVP Interview Title is not Contains Debrief. **Group By:** Interviewer RSVP’s Interviewer Interviewer RSVP’s Was Rescheduled After? (optional, to view reschedules based on declines)
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1863563e2e544cb26966ed867945a2cf.png)
﻿
**Question:** Which jobs or interviews suffer from the highest decline rates?
**Report Type:** Segmentation
**Subject:** Interviewer RSVPs
**Time Range:** Interviewer RSVP’s Submitted At
**Filters:** Optional
**Group By:** Interviewer RSVP’s Schedule Version’s Job Consideration’s Job (for reporting on jobs) or Interviewer RSVP’s Interview Title (for reporting on interview types)
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/baea37d1961a6a8adb43aca5d135fd24.png)
﻿
**Question:** How can I review individual declines, reasons given, and whether those interviews ended up being rescheduled as a result?
**Report Type:** Custom List
**Subject:** Interviewer RSVPs
**Time Range:** Interviewer RSVP’s Submitted At
**Filters:** Optional
**Fields to Show:** Interviewer RSVP’s Interviewer, Interviewer RSVP’s Status, Interviewer RSVP’s RSVP Note, Interviewer RSVP’s Submitted At, Interviewer RSVP’s Interview Title, Interviewer RSVP’s Was Rescheduled After?
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/42cdf4d05a6a5a2fb4b89fef60c5723a.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#interviewer-pools)Interviewer Pools
With the advanced scheduling add-on, you'll also have access to report on your interviewer pools, including whether training is enabled and any pending approvals.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#fields-3)Fields
The following fields are available under the Interviewer Pools subject:  
| Field Name  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Title  | The title of the pool.  |  
| Is Archived?  | Whether an interviewer pool is archived.  |  
| Requires Training?  | Whether training is enabled. Interviewer pools with training but no stages will be returned.  |  
| Training Requires Approval?  | Whether any training stages require approval.  |  
| Has Pending Approvals?  | Whether any trainees are awaiting approval.  |  
| Number of Qualified Interviewers  | Number of pool members that are qualified. Doesn't include deactivated employees.  |  
| Number of Trainees  | Number of trainees. Doesn't include deactivated employees.  |  
| Number of Trainees Pending Approval  | Number of trainees pending approval. Doesn't include deactivated employees.  |  
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#report-example)Report Example
**Question:** How do I pull a list of interviewer pools with trainees awaiting approval?
**Report Type:** Custom List
**Subject:** Interviewer Pools
**Filters:** Interviewer Pool Has Pending Approvals set to Yes
**Columns/Fields to Show:** Title, Number of Trainees Pending Approval
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/VTTj445UdykDc_esVVxKx_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-25649-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#interviewer-pool-members)Interviewer Pool Members
Use the Interviewer Pool Members subject to report on individual members of an interviewer pool.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#fields-4)Fields  
| Field Name  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Pool  | The interview pool that the interviewer is a member of.  |  
| Role  | The current role of the interviewer.  |  
| Added At  | The time when the user was initially added to the pool, either as trainee or qualified interviewer.  |  
| Removed At  | The time when the user was removed from the pool, either as trainee or qualified interviewer.  |  
| Training Started At  | Date of first interview as trainee (shadow or reverse shadow).  |  
| Time to Complete Training  | Number of days the interviewer was in training, starting from the date the interviewer was enrolled. This field is empty for employees that are not enrolled in training or are still in training.  |  
| Time in Training  | For interviewers in training, this field contains the number of days they have been enrolled. Otherwise, this field is empty.  |  
| Added to Pool as Trainee At   | If the employee was added as a trainee, this field will contain the date they were initially added to the pool.  |  
| Qualified At  | Date added as qualified or approved or automated graduation  |  
| Shadow Interviews Completed  | Total shadow interviews completed  |  
| Reverse Shadow Interviews Completed  | Total reverse shadow interviews completed  |  
| Training Interviews Completed  | Total training interviews completed (sum of shadow and reverse shadow training interviews completed)  |  
| Upcoming Shadow  | The start date of the first upcoming interview where the employee is a shadow interviewer  |  
| Upcoming Reverse Shadow  | The start date of the first upcoming interview where the employee is a reverse shadow interviewer  |  
| Upcoming Shadow Count  | The count of upcoming interview where the employee is a shadow interviewer  |  
| Upcoming Reverse Shadow Count  | The count of upcoming interview where the employee is a reverse shadow interviewer  |  
| Upcoming Training Interview (Shadow or Reverse Shadow)  | The start date of the first upcoming interview where the employee is either a shadow or reverse shadow interviewer  |  
| Most Recent Shadow   | The start date of the most recently completed interview where the employee is a shadow interviewer  |  
| Most Recent Reverse Shadow   | The start date of the most recently completed interview where the employee is a reverse shadow interviewer  |  
| Most Recent Training Interview (Shadow or Reverse Shadow)  | The start date of the most recently completed interview where the employee is a shadow or reverse shadow interviewer  |  
| Is Pending Approval?  | Whether the member is pending approval on stage progression in training steps  |  
| Current Stage Type   | The current stage type (Shadow or Reverse Shadow) of the employee. If the employee is not in training, then this field will be empty  |  
| Is Paused   | Whether the interviewer's membership is currently paused.  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#report-example-1)Report Example
**Question:** How do I track completed shadow and reverse shadow interviews per interviewer pool member?
**Report Type:** Custom List
**Subject:** Interviewer Pool Members
**Filters:**(Optional) Filter by interviewer pool.
**Columns/Fields to Show:** Employee Name Interviewer Pool, Interviewer Pool Member's Role, Interviewer Pool Member's Training Started At, Interviewer Pool Member's Training Interviews Completed, Interviewer Pool Member's Shadow Interviews Completed, Interviewer Pool Member's Reverse Shadow Interviews Completed
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/amaLTeIY2yyFMHrUP5Jq3_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-03-03-at-101408-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#interview-schedules-new-fields)Interview Schedules (New Fields)
For those who are sending Scheduling Requests to coordinators or coordination teams, you can now get visibility on scheduling request volume when reporting using this subject. You can find these additional fields under Requests when setting the subject of your report to Interview Schedules.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#fields-5)Fields  
| Additional Field Name  | Field Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Interview Schedule’s Request Requested By  | The user who submitted the request.  |  
| Interview Schedule’s Request Assignee  | The user assigned to the interview schedule request.  |  
| Interview Schedule’s Request Submitted At  | When the interview schedule request was submitted.  |  
| Interview Schedule’s Request Priority  | The priority level assigned to the interview schedule request.  |  
| Location Type  | The meeting location type for the interview (e.g In person, Zoom, Google Meet).  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#report-examples-3)Report Examples
Below are some common questions and examples of reports that can help you find the data points needed.
**Question:** What does scheduling request volume look like over time?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time
**Subject:** Interview Schedules
**Time Field:** Interview Schedule’s Request Submitted At
**Filters:** Optional
**Group By:** Interview Schedule’s Interview Stage Group or Interview Schedule’s Title.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bb2db268397a7ccbc34c56a5f8c6c239.png)
﻿
**Question:** How much scheduling volume is happening by request priority over time?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time
**Subject:** Interview Schedules
**Time Field:** Interview Schedule’s Request Submitted At
**Filters:** Optional
**Group By:** Interview Schedule’s Request’s Priority
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1ccf00d6560be556bd0fac8f04ded658.png)
﻿
**Question:** How many requests are being assigned to each coordinator over time?
**Report Type:** Count Over Time
**Subject:** Interview Schedules
**Time Field:** Interview Schedule’s Request Submitted At
**Filters:** Optional
**Group By:** Interview Schedule’s Request’s Assignee
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/43a24c8aa087b51d419381f2d3a9c490.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#advanced-scheduling-templates)Advanced Scheduling Templates
Within Reports > Templates or in the Reporting Templates section of the Reports > Report Builder, you will be able to access the a selection of scheduling report templates.
Below you’ll find a visual reference showing where each key metrics falls within the scheduling timeline. You can click on the image to get an enlarged view. Each template is also listed beneath the timeline with the metrics used to calculate each template for your reference.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#scheduling-turnaround-times)Scheduling Turnaround Times
This report shows how quickly interviews are scheduled once a candidate enters an interview stage.
**How to use this report** Use this report to measure the efficiency of your scheduling process. Faster turnaround times mean candidates spend less time waiting for interviews to be confirmed, improving the overall candidate experience.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average time from when a candidate enters an interview stage (scheduling begins) to when the interview is confirmed on the calendar.


  * Reflects both recruiter responsiveness and candidate engagement.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/17d0dd88649f5f41af190f70bd9e4ab5.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#time-to-schedule)Time to Schedule
This report shows how quickly interviews are scheduled after candidates provide their availability.
**How to use this report** Use this report to track recruiting team responsiveness to candidate availability. Shorter times indicate efficient scheduling and a smoother candidate experience.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average time from when a candidate submits availability to when the interview is confirmed.


  * Highlights how long candidates wait before seeing their interview scheduled.


  * Only includes interviews where candidate availability was requested and submitted in Ashby (e.g. excludes direct booking).


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/026932dd70d190f45d9dfbe823930eb9.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#scheduling-lead-times)Scheduling Lead Times
This report shows how far in advance interviews are typically scheduled (excluding reschedules).
**How to use this report** Use this report to understand interviewer engagement and capacity. A shorter lead time generally indicates that interviewers are making themselves available in advance, while longer lead times can suggest tighter schedules or difficulty securing interviewer participation.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average time between when an interview is confirmed on the calendar and its initially scheduled start time.


  * Excludes delays from any reschedules.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a1844d4fc9def9f9a2fa7aa65fe2dc03.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#adjusted-scheduling-lead-times)Adjusted Scheduling Lead Times
This report shows how far in advance interviews are typically scheduled (including reschedules).
**How to use this report** Use this report to understand interviewer engagement and capacity. A shorter lead time generally indicates that interviewers are making themselves available in advance, while longer lead times can suggest tighter schedules or difficulty securing interviewer participation.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average time between when an interview is confirmed and when it actually takes place.


  * Includes delays caused by reschedules.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/942db3139458317125d7d99b8e11b79e.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#total-scheduling-duration)Total Scheduling Duration
This report shows the total time it takes for an interview to actually happen once a candidate enters an interview stage.
**How to use this report** Use this report to evaluate the overall speed of your scheduling process. It captures both how quickly scheduling is completed and how long it takes for the interview to actually occur. Can be viewed as a combination of Turnaround Time + Adjusted Lead Time.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average time from when a candidate enters an interview stage to when the interview happens.


  * Includes any delays caused by reschedules.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/46b5525af6c3bf7af7ee01b1fa862841.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#time-to-interview)Time to Interview
This report shows how quickly candidates reach their initially scheduled interview after providing availability (excluding reschedules).
**How to use this report** Use this report to understand candidate wait times from their perspective. It highlights how long it typically takes to get candidates into their next interview once they have submitted availability.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average time from when a candidate submits availability to the start time of their initially scheduled interview.


  * Only includes interviews where candidate availability was requested and submitted in Ashby (e.g. excludes direct booking).


  * Excludes delays from rescheduling.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2e2ec22895ef9b876c4a0c0f0554e386.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#adjusted-time-to-interview)Adjusted Time to Interview
This report shows how long it actually takes for candidates to have their interview after providing availability, including delays from reschedules.
**How to use this report** Use this report to measure real candidate wait times. It accounts for any reschedules and helps identify bottlenecks or reliability issues that slow things down.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average time from when a candidate submits availability to when the interview actually happens.


  * Only includes interviews where candidate availability was requested and submitted in Ashby (e.g. excludes direct booking).


  * Includes delays from rescheduling.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/320b563a8729c988716d5d6fee9e3a07.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#rescheduling-impact)Rescheduling Impact
This report shows how much rescheduling is extending your interview timelines.
**How to use this report**
Use this report to assess the cost of rescheduling on both interviewer engagement and candidate experience. It highlights whether reschedules are meaningfully delaying interviews and where improvements might be needed.
**What's in the data?**
  * Calculated as the average difference between Scheduling Lead Time (initial) and Adjusted Scheduling Lead Time (actual).


  * Quantifies the impact of delays introduced by rescheduling.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f4e20f36a9bcd756c8a8fb5905003617.png)
﻿
Remember to click Save once you’ve created your report so it will be added to your saved reports!
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#if-i-start-auto-scheduling-an-interview-but-have-t)I**f I start auto scheduling an interview, but have to tweak it along the way (e.g. swapping out an interview on the shared plan), will the Interview Scheduling Version get logged as manually scheduled?**
If you start auto-scheduling and tweak something along the way within the auto-schedule flow (e.g. click "use schedule" from one of the auto-schedule options presented, and then update one of the interviews in the following screen), the Scheduled By Type
will still be logged as "Auto Schedule". Put another way, the Scheduled by Type is set at the beginning based on how you start a new scheduling process. If the interview was scheduled using auto-schedule, then was rescheduled using manual schedule, there would be two versions listed, one for each scheduling type.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#is-there-a-way-to-see-whether-a-candidate-decline)I**s there a way to see whether a candidate decline resulted in needing to reschedule?**
﻿  

  * What’s possible today: 


  * You can see whether there was a candidate decline on certain interview schedules (or versions of the interview schedule, and if it was rescheduled or modified multiple times)


  * We can also see if a schedule was rescheduled via direct booking link, which could be viewed as a reschedule due to a candidate.


  * What’s not yet possible: 


  * We do not currently have reporting on decline reasons for candidates, or the ability to see how closely the decline (from a candidate) happened to the time of the interview.


﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#can-you-see-which-candidate-declinedrescheduled-ba)C**an you see which candidate declined/rescheduled based on the original date of the interview was?**
Yes, you can see each specific instance and which specific candidate responded with a decline. You can also see what the Interview Start At time was for that specific schedule version. Please note, we can only see if (true/false) there was a candidate decline on one of these Schedule Versions. From the data, you should also be able to infer if it was rescheduled, because that would show up as another version for the same candidate and the same interview/stage, but with a higher "version" number — which indicates a new schedule being created (whether due to rescheduling or other modification minor modification).
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#what-happens-if-an-interview-is-rescheduled-and-th)W**hat happens if an interview is rescheduled and then the reschedule is modified? What will the Version Schedule Type show up as when reporting on Interview Schedule Summaries?**
This will result in three Interview Schedule Versions, of the types original, reschedule and modified respectively. At the Interview Schedule Summary level, the Version Schedule Type will be marked as a Reschedule because reschedules supersede modifications when aggregated at the summary level.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#are-interview-schedule-summaries-based-on-the-inte)A**re interview schedule summaries based on the interview stage, interview schedule activity, or individual interview event?**
They are based on interview schedule activity. Whether your interview schedule activity has one interview event, or multiple events (e.g. an onsite), it will be represented by a single interview schedule summary. If there is more than one event in an interview schedule activity, rescheduling that activity (whether only one event changes, or multiple events change) will count as one new interview schedule version (marked as a modification or reschedule depending on the change), and will be counted as part of the single interview schedule summary for all versions for that activity. If you have more than one interview schedule activity within an interview stage, each will have their own interview schedule summary.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#if-i-reschedule-an-interview-more-than-once-in-a-s)I**f I reschedule an interview more than once in a short time, will that count as multiple reschedules?**
Yes. If you reschedule an interview activity twice in quick succession, it will still count as two reschedules (two new versions) regardless of how little time has elapsed in between.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#with-interview-schedule-versions-what-is-the-diffe)W**ith interview schedule versions, what is the difference between a modification and a reschedule?**
A reschedule involves adjusting interview participants, start/end times, or amending the number of interviews. A modification is any other kind of adjustment (for example, updating the interview briefing, adding a Zoom link, etc).
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-metrics#what-are-the-different-scheduled-by-types-and-what)W**hat are the different Scheduled By****types and what do they mean?**
Here’s a list showing each scheduling type and a definition:
**Manual Schedule**
  * Represents interview schedules created by using the _Manual Schedule_ option within the Ashby interface


**Auto Schedule**
  * Represents schedules created via Ashby's _Auto-scheduling_ feature


**Direct Booking**
  * Represents schedules where candidates booked their own interview times through a Direct Booking link, which allows candidates to self-select from available time slots


**Ashby Public API**
  * Represents schedules created programmatically via Ashby's Public API (Used for integrations and automated scheduling through external systems)


**External Reschedule**
  * Represents schedules updated automatically due to two-way calendar sync automation (e.g. when external calendar changes trigger automatic updates in Ashby)


**Automation (Interviewer Declined)**
  * Represents automatic rescheduling triggered when an interviewer declines a calendar invite (the system automatically finds a replacement interviewer or reschedules)


**Automation (Training Path Update)**
  * Represents automatic updates to fix instances where interviewer training roles change or get out of order, to ensure training sequences remain properly ordered


﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Admin & Organization Settings
Job Board Setup
# Routes for integrating your Ashby job board with your careers page
7 min
Copy
When it comes to connecting your Ashby job board with your website or existing careers page, you have a number of options. The different options provide different levels of customization and flexibility, so you can find an option that suits your company's needs.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/routes-for-integrating-your-ashby-job-board-with-your-careers-page#linking-out-to-your-ashby-job-board)Linking out to your Ashby job board
A full guide to this option along with examples can be found at [Linking out to your Ashby job board](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/linking-out-to-your-ashby-job-board)﻿. This is the quickest route but is limited in terms of customization options (they are limited to the customizations listed in the [Job Board: Configuring Your Setup](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-board-configuring-your-setup)﻿ guide).
This is a straightforward option that allows you to point customers to your Ashby job board. To get your Ashby job board URL:
  * Navigate to Admin > Job Boards > Theme (Job Board)


  * Click the Job Board button in the top-right corner


  * Copy the URL on the following page


You can then add this URL to a button on your website, use it for a hyperlink and more.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/routes-for-integrating-your-ashby-job-board-with-your-careers-page#embed-your-job-board-on-your-careers-page)Embed your job board on your careers page
For a guide on implementing this option, check out [Embedding Ashby Job Boards in an external Careers Page](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/embedding-ashby-job-boards-in-an-external-careers-page)﻿. You can further customize your job board with CSS. This route is also a straightforward option but requires adding code to your website.
If you would prefer to have applicants stay on your website when applying to a job, you can opt to embed your job board into your careers page. You can enabled the embedding option within Admin > Job Boards > Theme (Job Board). You will then be provided with the code for your careers page.
You will have the option to add CSS to your embedded job board for further customization, giving you some flexibility with the styling of your job board.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/routes-for-integrating-your-ashby-job-board-with-your-careers-page#use-the-lightweight-api-to-post-jobs-to-your-jobs)Use the lightweight API to post jobs to your jobs page
For more information on this option, check out [Using the lightweight job posting API to list openings on your site.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/using-the-lightweight-job-posting-api-to-list-openings-on-your-site)﻿﻿
This approach allows you to use our [JSON API for listing openings](https://developers.ashbyhq.com/docs/public-job-posting-api "JSON API for listing openings") and then linking out to the hosted careers page for the actual application. Applications will still be submitted via the Ashby job board but the opening details will be displayed on your career's page, giving you more flexibility with how the job posting data is displayed.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/routes-for-integrating-your-ashby-job-board-with-your-careers-page#create-a-completely-custom-jobs-page)Create a completely custom jobs page
For further information on this option, check out [Build an entirely custom careers page using the Ashby API](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/build-an-entirely-custom-careers-page-using-the-ashby-api)﻿
This approach to posting your jobs to your external careers page is the most amount of effort, but also provides full flexibility with how you design your careers page. The Ashby API documentation can be found [here](https://developers.ashbyhq.com/docs/creating-a-custom-careers-page "here").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/routes-for-integrating-your-ashby-job-board-with-your-careers-page#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/routes-for-integrating-your-ashby-job-board-with-your-careers-page#are-custom-domains-supported)A**re custom domains supported?**
No, custom domains aren't currently supported, we suggest embedding your job board via script or using an iFrame.
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/routes-for-integrating-your-ashby-job-board-with-your-careers-page#can-we-customize-the-ashby-job-board-further)Can we customize the Ashby job board further?
Yes, you have the option to upload a CSS file in the Job Board Options section of [Theme (Job Board)](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/theme "Theme \(Job Board\)"). You can download an example [here](https://www.ashbyhq.com/job-board-embed-examples/full-job-board#css "here"). You can also implement [at-rules](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/At-rule "at-rules") for further customization.
﻿  

﻿

# Ashby Analytics
9 min
Copy
Looking to pull key metrics in Ashby? Our Analytics documentation will help you get acquainted with Ashby's flexible reporting features.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics#getting-started-with-analytics)Getting Started with Analytics
These guides will help you get acquainted with building reports and dashboards in Ashby, including configuring filters and grouping data to pull the key metrics you need.
🗺️ [Navigating Ashby Analytics](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/navigating-ashby-analytics)﻿
📈 [Ashby Analytics: Key Reporting Concepts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts)﻿
🛠️ [Building Your First Dashboard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard)﻿
📊 [Dashboard Filters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters)﻿
💾 [Saved Reports & Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards)﻿
🔓 [Permissions & Sharing Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards)﻿
➡️ [Understanding Interview Stage Transitions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-interview-stage-transitions)﻿
❌ [Reporting Errors & Resolutions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-errors-and-resolutions)﻿
📁 [Exporting Reports and Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/exporting-reports-and-dashboards)﻿
🧮 [Calculated Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/calculated-fields)﻿
👀 [Report View Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options)﻿
❓ [How do I enable Advanced filters?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-enable-advanced-filters)﻿
🏆 [Quality of Hire Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-reports)﻿
You can also learn more about getting started with Analytics in [Ashby Academy](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/home "Ashby Academy") - the following courses are available for you and your team:
🎓 [Analytics 100](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/course/analytics-100 "Analytics 100")
📊 [Analytics 101](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/course/ashby-analytics-101 "Analytics 101")﻿
📈 [Analytics 102](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/course/ashby-analytics-102 "Analytics 102")﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics#analytics-best-practices)Analytics: Best Practices
Want to ensure your data hygiene is on point? These guides will help you follow best practices to ensure accurate reporting.
⭐️ [Best Practices: Maintaining Data Quality](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality)﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews & Scheduling FAQs
# What are ‘Shared’ Interviews, Application Forms, and Feedback Forms?
6 min
Copy
We highly recommend using shared application forms, feedback forms, and interviews rather than custom, as the custom versions cannot be reported on unless they are connected to a custom field via [Form Field Connectors](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#what-does-shared-mean)What does ‘Shared’ mean?
‘Shared’ refers to application forms, feedback forms and interviews that are created to be used across as many jobs as needed. Shared interviews are set up in [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews"), shared feedback forms are created on the [Feedback Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-forms "Feedback Forms") page and shared application forms are set up in [Application Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/application-forms "Application Forms").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#what-does-custom-mean)What does ‘Custom’ mean?
Custom refers to an application form, feedback form or an interview that has been customized specifically for a job, interview or interview activity. It cannot be used across multiple jobs, interviews or schedule interview activities. It can be promoted to be a shared form or interview.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#shared-application-forms)Shared Application Forms
In the Settings section for a job, you can select the application form you’d like applicant to complete when applying for that role. You’ll see two options when assigning or amending the application form; Unlink & Edit and Change Link.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/82ede94c4a0399b5535716bbf6565bc5.png)
﻿
**Unlink & Edit:** Using the Unlink and Edit option to amend the application form will only edit the form for that specific role, creating a custom application form. The amended application form will not be saved as a shared application form to be used across other jobs.
**Change Link:** You can switch from one shared application form to another using this option. If you’re looking to set up additional application forms to use across multiple jobs, we’d recommend doing this on the [Application Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/application-forms "Application Forms") page.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#shared-feedback-forms)Shared Feedback Forms
You can assign feedback forms to your interviews on the [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews") page. Similarly to application forms, you’ll see the Unlink & Edit and Change Link options when selecting your feedback form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f46b0e6ad0cbfab1e083cfabe4ef2234.png)
﻿
Unlink & Edit: Just like application forms, you can click this option to amend the current form and create a custom one. Any changes made here will only apply to this job and will not be applied to the shared feedback form.
Change Link: Use this option to switch to a different shared feedback form set up on the [Feedback Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-forms "Feedback Forms") page. Shared feedback forms can be used across as many jobs as you’d like.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#shared-interviews)Shared Interviews
When adding a schedule interview activity to a stage in a job’s interview plan, you will see the option to Create New Interview or Add Shared Interview.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d48e3bec991a60f20a887059169013ff.png)
﻿
Create New Interview: If you opt to create a new interview, this will be a one-off custom interview that can only be used for that schedule activity and for that job.
Add Shared Interview**:** If you would like to create interviews that you can use across multiple jobs, we’d recommend setting up an interview in the [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews") section of your Ashby account. When clicking Add Shared Interview, you can then select your shared interview of choice from the list.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#how-do-i-switch-from-a-custom-form-or-interview-to)How do I switch from a custom form or interview to a shared one?
The Delete & Link to Shared option will allow you to remove the custom feedback form, interview or application form. From there, you’ll then be able to select a shared option instead.
**Please note that the custom form or interview will not be saved or retrievable after switching to a shared form.**
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2853d028de3e37071019c740d8034dfa.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms#can-i-promote-a-custom-interview-or-feedback-form)Can I promote a custom interview or feedback form and make it into a shared one?
You can! If you edit the custom interview or feedback form within the schedule activity or interview, then you’ll see a Promote to Shared option. You can choose to rename the interview or feedback form before confirming the promotion.
When editing a custom application form within the Job Posting section of a job, you will also see the Promote to Shared option to change the application form into a shared one.
Once promoted, you can find shared interviews in [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews") and shared feedback forms in [Feedback Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-forms "Feedback Forms"). Shared application forms can be found in [Application Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/application-forms "Application Forms").
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8f49e69d20f95a4300f92d293021baa9.png)
﻿
﻿

Interviews & Scheduling FAQs
# Why is there no availability on my direct booking links?
2 min
Copy
This guide will cover some common reasons as to why your availability isn’t showing up correctly when scheduling interviews via a direct booking link.
Sometimes direct booking links don’t reflect the availability you expect for scheduling interviews. Common reasons include:
  * You have a busy event on your calendar at that time


  * Your personal, team, or interview [Meeting Hours](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours)﻿ exclude that timeframe


  * You have a Pre and/or Post Meeting buffer﻿ in place on the corresponding [scheduling interview activity](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿﻿


In all of these cases, you can use the Troubleshoot Availability option to confirm the reason, and use the information to fix it. The troubleshooting tool will display all possible time slots with an exclamation mark or a check mark beside it.
  * Exclamation mark: This time is not available for booking. You can click on the time slot to see why.


  * Check mark: This time slot is available for booking.


﻿
﻿
﻿

# Lever API Key Setup
1 min
Copy
As part of integrating your Ashby Analytics account with Lever, your CSM will ask you to create and enter an API key into Ashby. This guide will take you through the steps.
If you are looking to migrate from Lever to Ashby All-In-One, please follow the instructions provided for Lever migrations in [Lever → Ashby Recruiting API Migration Guide](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-ashby-recruiting-api-migration-guide)﻿.
To set up your Lever sync, create a Lever API key with read permissions enabled for all endpoints. [The Lever documentation here](https://help.lever.co/hc/en-us/articles/360042364412-How-do-I-create-an-API-key-with-access-to-confidential-data- "The Lever documentation here") explains this, but in short:
  1. Generate a new key


  1. Select Select all read permissions to grant read-only rights to all data under the Permissions header


  1. Scroll to the bottom of the page and click DONE. Note: this is required to finalize the API key permissions


  1. Add in a name for your API key and generate the key


  1. Sign in to your Ashby account and navigate to the [Lever API configuration](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/lever "Lever API configuration"). Copy and paste your key into the Lever API Key field


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/75db7af6c91c2c031a05f13153d2275a.png)
﻿

﻿

Bulk Import
# Bulk Import Options
37 min
Copy
This guide will teach you how to upload candidate data using the Email importer or the Bulk importer. If you want to import data from your legacy ATS, we may be able to achieve this with one of the migration options we support. See [Migrations](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/migrations)﻿ for more information. 
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#bulk-import-options-overview)Bulk Import Options Overview
There are two ways users can bulk import data into Ashby. View the table below for more information on when to use each option.  
| **Import Option**  | **Upload Candidates?**  | **Upload Openings?**  | **Upload Employees?**  | **Use this option if…**  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Email Import  | 🟢  | 🔴   | 🔴   | You want to import small batches of resume files.  |  
| CSV Import   | 🟢  | 🟢  | 🟢  | You want to import structured data like candidate demographics or headcount plans.  |  
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#email-import-overview)Email Import Overview
If you’d like to add candidates to Ashby via email, the email import feature supports creating candidate profiles by forwarding resume files to a specific address. You can attach up to 10 resumes per email to be uploaded. A common use case for this feature is sharing the upload links with external recruiters - then, if a candidate sends their resume over to a recruiter via email, they can send the resume over to Ashby via the import email address.
If you have many resume files you’d like to upload, contact support@ashbyhq.com. This is something we can assist with on the backend.
﻿  

﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#accessing-the-feature)Accessing the feature
There are both job-specific, and general candidate upload email addresses. These email addresses can be accessed in two places:
All email addresses including the general email address can be found on the [Import via Email](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/data-import/email-import "Import via Email") page in Admin, in the Data Management section.
To access the general candidate upload email address, select Upload Candidate on the Email Import page. Uploading candidates via the general candidate upload email address will add them as a lead without considering them for a specific job.
Job-specific email addresses can also be found on the Settings page of the corresponding job. These email addresses are only active/available on a job when the job is open.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/45b5ec538fcbd5fbf18c78e414f7cc59.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8c2e8ef14393fe39cc593a9acac7385f.png)
﻿
﻿  

Anyone with access to these email addresses can use them, so be thoughtful about who you share them with. We advise against sharing the email address directly with candidates or on your job board to avoid spam applications.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#upload-confirmations)Upload confirmations
You’ll receive a confirmation email letting you know if the resume file you imported was successfully uploaded. Unsuccessful uploads occur when our parsing service can’t parse a file. The reasons files wouldn’t be able to be parsed are:
  1. The file is an image of a PDF (someone took a picture and converted it to a PDF)


  1. The file is in a unique format (the candidate’s demographic information is in an unexpected place in the file)


If a file can’t be parsed, you’ll need to manually upload that file and create a candidate profile. Even when manually uploaded, the candidate’s data won’t be parsed from the file.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#reviewing-uploaded-candidates)Reviewing uploaded candidates
When resumes are uploaded, a candidate profile is created, the resume details are parsed and stored in the Summary tab of the candidate profile, and the resume file is stored in a viewable format next to the Summary tab.
Candidates uploaded using the general candidate upload URL will be added to your account as leads and can be found using Candidate Search.
Candidates uploaded using job-specific URLs will be added to the Application Review stage of the job they were uploaded to.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c4bd6155040f5c3a62bc038a5ddb83e7.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4e4cb9ffc473ccd46f2f1a39ff9fa758.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#csv-import-overview)CSV Import Overview
The CSV import feature supports creating candidate profiles and openings in bulk by uploading a CSV file containing the details you want to be uploaded. There is no explicit limit to the number of candidates or openings that can be uploaded at once. Large files will be uploaded in batches. Once the upload starts you can leave the page and return when it is complete. Common use cases for this feature include:
  * Migrating candidates from your legacy ATS (if Ashby doesn’t support an alternative)


  * Uploading your annual headcount plan


  * Uploading leads from projects or talent pools in your legacy sourcing tool


When very large files are imported (over 100k rows of candidates) you may experience a network error. We typically recommend splitting the file into a few smaller files and uploading them as separate CSVs in these cases.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#accessing-the-feature-1)Accessing the feature
See the table below for information on the roles that have access to bulk import data using a CSV file.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Only Organization Admins can import CSV files by default. Elevated Access users can be given access to bulk import data if needed. More on this at [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿.
The CSV import option can be accessed within Admin > Data Management > [Bulk Data Import](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/data-import/bulk-import "Bulk Data Import").
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#prepare-your-file)Prepare your file
The quality of the data you import into Ashby impacts the quality of data you get out of it. To ensure high quality, we recommend preparing your file before uploading it to Ashby.
**Before you start, ensure your file meets the:**
  * CSV file requirements


  * File uses the .csv file format


  * File has headers for every column


  * File uses comma delimiters


  * File has the same number columns in every row


  * Data quality suggestions


  * Data is complete (Are there gaps in your data? If so, consider resolving this before importing)


  * Data is accurate (Is this data correct?)


  * Data is relevant (Are there data points that don’t add to your knowledge about the candidates? If so, consider archiving outside of Ashby)


  * Data is timely (Is this historical data that will be frustrating to filter out later? If so, consider archiving outside of Ashby)


  * Additional tips


  * Use Google Sheet’s cleaning tools


  * Make sure there isn’t any formatting, just data


  * Make sure there isn’t any unexpected metadata at the top or bottom of the file


  * If you are importing information into a custom checkbox field (more on custom fields﻿ at [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿), then be sure to use a semi-colon if multiple values are being added (for example Remote; In-Office; Hybrid)


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#how-to-import-a-csv-file)How to import a CSV file
Once you’re ready to upload your data, you’ll do so in four stages:
  * Import


  * Mapping


  * Import


  * Summary


**Import**
To import a file, click Upload CSV in the top right corner of the [CSV Import](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/data-import/bulk-import "CSV Import") page. Next, select the import type, then locate the file on your computer.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cfed53d420730b477550e31296761df9.png)
﻿
﻿  

**Manage Import Settings**
Next, select the settings options:
  1. Import Mode: This setting allows you to determine whether the import should only create new candidates, only update existing candidates, or both create new candidates and update existing ones.


  1. Enable email enrichment? You have the option to enrich profiles with emails in bulk. This option requires the candidate name and LinkedIn URL to be included in the import.


  1. Only match open jobs: By default we only map applications to open jobs but you can opt to uncheck this checkbox to map applications to all jobs.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/dfbff5a9bfd14b9e429f2c90442305e9.png)
﻿
**Mapping**
The mapping step allows you to match any structured candidate field to a corresponding column in your file. If there are columns you don’t want to upload, ignore them (by clicking the _Ignore this column_ button) and the importer will skip them. Please ensure to check that all fields you’d like to import have been mapped. If a field is defaulting to being ignored initially, click Select Field on Candidate to then determine the field the value should be mapped to.
You can use the following templates as a starting point (optional):
  * ﻿[Candidate Import Template](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1SZmAn605_5Z5OOql38bf4_kBSEJYY-FzAe2rmvxYtE0/edit#gid=1204658108 "Candidate Import Template")﻿


  * ﻿[Opening Import Template](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1w82IBSPYNDEm92_J9ebJMVXLcNmrm36zn_ZGzFLXN7s/edit#gid=1347042328 "Opening Import Template")﻿


![mapping GIF.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/W-t7OtdB0gsSKeDvhuys8_mappinggif.gif)
﻿
**Import**
Once you’ve initiated an import, it can take some time to complete. There are no explicit limits on the number of candidates or openings you can upload at once. If your list is large, they’ll be uploaded in batches and will take longer to complete. You can leave the page and come back later once an upload finishes.
![import GIF.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ZcdMofFA_wjNyHz8R7ENg_importgif.gif)
﻿
**Summary**
The summary view will show a list of the candidates or openings you’ve uploaded, any skipped due to errors, and allows you to delete the upload if something went wrong. For candidates, you have a variety of bulk actions you can take on the candidate profiles that are generated.
![summary GIF.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ZiBf5pUU6z_esBtFcXReL_summarygif.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#importing-candidates)Importing Candidates
Using this option creates individual candidate profiles. In general, you can CSV upload data located on the left side of the candidate profile (except for files). Feed data like emails, feedback forms, applications, resumes and surveys cannot be uploaded using the bulk CSV import feature. Many structured data fields can be uploaded so review the upload options in Ashby before completing your upload. If you’re unsure, confirm by reaching out to support@ashbyhq.com.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fa947905238308afbd2ee47632b0b12c.png)
﻿
**You can**[**use this template**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1SZmAn605_5Z5OOql38bf4_kBSEJYY-FzAe2rmvxYtE0/edit#gid=1204658108 "use this template")**to ensure your data is formatted correctly before uploading.**
To import data like the job and stage a candidate is associated with or the source of their application, you’ll need first to ensure that information exists in Ashby:
  * If you’d like to include your candidate’s source, you must include both a Source Type and a Source column in your file. Source Types are top-level values that Sources are nested under to simplify reporting. To add a source, you must also include a source type. These should already be added [on the Sources page](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/sources "on the Sources page") in Ashby Admin before importing.


  * If you’d like to upload candidates to specific jobs, those jobs must be set up in Ashby first. By default we only map applications to open jobs but you can opt to uncheck the Only match open jobs checkbox to map applications to all jobs. The name of the job in Ashby must exactly match the name of the job in the CSV. You can archive any historical jobs once the import is complete.


  * When adding or updating existing candidates, ensure that each row contains the candidate’s name and an identifying field. An identifying field can be an email address, a phone number or social profile URLs (LinkedIn or GitHub).


  * If two rows contain the same identifying data (for example, two candidate rows having the same email address), you will get an error. Please ensure that each candidate is only listed once in the CSV file to avoid this issue.


  * If importing any dates as part of your candidate data, the date/time should be an ISO 8601-compliant string (e.g., 1982-05-25, 2017-04-22T20:47:05.335-04:00)


  * Generally speaking, if you have any concerns about your data quality before importing, we suggest starting fresh with Ashby, and archiving your historical candidates’ data somewhere you can find it.


  * If you are importing hired candidates, it is not possible to import data like the Hired At date or other offer information directly into offer fields in Ashby.


**Candidate Attribution**
Credited To and Source are collectively known as the ‘Attribution’ Fields. Candidates may have attribution fields. Individual applications may have attribution fields. They may not necessarily be the same. The candidate’s attribution fields are intended to be the original source for that candidate’s entry into Ashby. Therefore Ashby attempts to keep the Candidate source from being updated as you consider candidates for new jobs or make changes to applications. In those cases the application’s attribution fields will be updated. The attribution fields in the CSV uploader reflect this behavior:
  * If the information being imported will create a new candidate, then the attribution fields in the import file will always apply to the candidate.


  * If a candidate already exists in Ashby for the information being imported and information about an application is also being imported, then only the application’s attribution field will be updated.


  * If you would like to update a candidate’s attribution fields, then only include candidate information in your import.


  * If you are updating the Credited To field, include the email of the user/employee who the candidate should be credited to, not the user/employee's name. This field will only accept email addresses.


**Post upload options**
Once candidates are imported, you can take bulk actions on the import list. To complete these actions, select individual candidates, or all candidates, then click the button that corresponds with the action you’d like to take. If there’s an issue with the upload, you can delete it and try again.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/14b3709b0c9bb316e93a145d8e6710aa.png)
﻿
_Bulk Actions Supported_
  * Consider for Job - add candidates to a job consideration (if the file didn't include the job consideration already).


  * Add to Project - add candidates to a project. Projects are a simple way to keep track of candidates that are not currently interviewing but whom you want to stay in touch with for the future.


  * Set Tags - add tags to candidate profiles


  * Email - send candidates a one-off email using a shared email template or one of your personal email templates.


  * Enroll in Sequence - add candidates to an email sequence.


  * Request Review


  * More 


  * Transfer


  * Anonymize


  * Delete


  * Remove from Project


  * Mark as Do Not Contact


  * Set Tags


  * Send Data Consent Request


  * Add to Event

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#importing-jobs)Importing Jobs
If you need to add or update jobs in bulk, you can now upload them by doing the following:
  * Click Upload CSV in the Bulk Upload section of Admin, then Jobs _._


  * Select your CSV file to start uploading it.


  * Choose Create or Update to determine whether the import will create new jobs or update existing jobs (please note that _Create & Update_ is not available for importing jobs - if both creating and updating jobs, you will need to do two separate imports).


  * You can then map your file fields to fields in Ashby before clicking Start Import to kick off the import.


If you are looking to update jobs that are already in Ashby, be sure to include either the requisition ID﻿ or the Ashby job ID fields in your CSV file.
You can use [this template](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bpK7LI58FoQtyP4_Ek4wc8Xn0DCWyQ19UOx68qlYrBg/edit?gid=0#gid=0 "this template") to ensure your data is formatted correctly before uploading, or check out the info below on exporting a CSV of existing job data to use as a template for an import to update jobs.
The following fields can be mapped and imported for creating and updating jobs in Ashby:  
| **Field**  | **Description**  | Formatting Notes  | **Example**  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| **Title**  | The title of the job  | ﻿  
 | Backend Engineer  |  
| **Team**  | The department or team connected to the job  | Must exactly match an existing team in your Ashby account.  | Product  |  
| **Location**  | The location connected to the job  | Must exactly match an existing location in your Ashby account.  | New York  |  
| **Job Status**  | The status of the job  | Can be Open, Closed, Archived or Draft  | Open  |  
| **Requisition ID**  | The requisition ID linked to the job.  | ﻿  
 | 123  |  
| **Ashby Job ID**  | The internal job ID within Ashby.  | This field should only be used for updating jobs.  | ﻿  
 |  
| **Employment Type**  | The type of employment linked to the job.  | ﻿  
 | Full-time, Part-time, Contractor  |  
| **Closed At**  | If the job has been closed, the date that the job was closed at.  | The date/time should be an ISO 8601-compliant string (e.g., 1982-05-25, 2017-04-22T20:47:05.335-04:00)  | 2025-05-25  |  
| **Hiring Manager**  | The hiring manager linked to the job (must be an active user in Ashby - please add in the user’s email address)  | Multiple hiring managers should be added as comma-separated values  | abbye@learn-ashby.com, jared@learn-ashby.com  |  
| **Recruiter**  | The recruiter linked to the job (must be an active user in Ashby - please add in the user’s email address)  | Multiple recruiters should be added as comma-separated values  | abbye@learn-ashby.com, jared@learn-ashby.com  |  
| **Recruiting Coordinator**  | The recruiting coordinator linked to the job (must be an active user in Ashby - please add in the user’s email address)  | Multiple recruiting coordinators should be added as comma-separated values  | abbye@learn-ashby.com, jared@learn-ashby.com  |  
| **Sourcer**  | The sourcer linked to the job (must be an active user in Ashby - please add in the user’s email address)  | Multiple sourcers should be added as comma-separated values  | abbye@learn-ashby.com, jared@learn-ashby.com  |  
| **Custom Fields**  | See Custom Fields for more information on supported field types.  | Ensure that the fields are in the appropriate format to match the job-specific custom field (for example, importing yes/no answers into a yes/no custom field)  | High Priority (Y/N)?  |  
**Creating Jobs**
When creating jobs via bulk import option, the job Title, Team and Location are required. Ensure that these fields are included in your CSV file.
**Updating Jobs**
When updating jobs via bulk import, either the job Ashby ID or Requisition ID is required (the job title, location, and team are not required). If both are provided in the import, then Ashby Job ID will be used, and Requisition ID can then be updated via the bulk import.
The template above can also be used for updating job details, or you can opt to create a job export using the steps below.
**Creating a CSV export of job data for bulk updates**
Please note that custom reports are not available on the Foundations plan.
To create a CSV export of job data to use as a base to update your jobs in Ashby:
  * Head to Reports > Build Reports 


  * Select the custom list report option 


  * Set the subject to Jobs


  * Use the filters to determine the jobs you’d like to export (for example, filtering by the job department) 


  * Click Add Field to Show to select the specific job fields you’d like to bulk update 


  * Be sure to include the Ashby Job ID and/or Requisition ID fields in your report, as one of these fields must be provided to update your jobs.


  * ﻿  



![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8fb1deba9498847f39791bbb56a5e9c8.png)
﻿
Click Export CSV (listed above your results on the right-hand side) to export the data in the CSV format. 
**Manually location the Ashby job ID**
  * In addition to using CSV exports, you can locate the job ID by heading to Jobs > Jobs, clicking into the job in question and then copying the ID number from the URL.


  * The requisition ID is listed within the Settings section of the job. If you do not have requisition IDs assigned to your jobs, use the Ashby job ID instead.


**Mapping your job fields for updating jobs**
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/158dd08a09cc85d76d753f013f00efcb.png)
﻿
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/19f13cbf11c20a7eda2fa26bfb7feb8f.png)
﻿
When mapping your job data, map only the field with the job or requisition ID and any fields with updated data to the fields on your jobs. Click Ignore this column beside any fields that you don’t want to import/update on your jobs.
When updating existing jobs via CSV import, any mapped hiring team field (Hiring Manager, Recruiter, Recruiting Coordinator or Sourcer) will overwrite the hiring team role on the job.
To avoid removing existing team members by accident, include all hiring team roles you'd like to keep on the job in your CSV and map all hiring team role fields during import.
For example, if you want to update the Recruiter but keep the current Hiring Manager on a job, your CSV must include both fields with the appropriate data.
You will need to ensure that all fields in your CSV are mapped or ignored before you’ll have the option to start your import.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9ba99c8210e1bcd1bad807d16196e957.png)
﻿
**Post upload actions**
We don’t currently support taking bulk actions on jobs after they’ve been uploaded. To manage your jobs in Ashby, navigate to Jobs > Jobs and select the job in question to make amendments.
