##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#what-candidate-data-can-be-uploaded-via-the-bulk-c)W**hat candidate data can be uploaded via the bulk CSV import feature?**
  * Things you can import: 


  * Candidate demographics


  * Current job application


  * The current interview plan stage


  * Current status


  * Things you can’t import: 


  * Resumes*


  * Other files


  * Notes


  * Long text fields


Resume files can be uploaded separately by the Ashby team, reach out to support@ashbyhq.com to request this
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#what-opening-data-can-be-uploaded-via-the-bulk-imp)W**hat opening data can be uploaded via the bulk import feature?**
  * Opening ID (required)


  * Description


  * Team 


  * Expects the name of an existing team in Ashby


  * Jobs 


  * Expects a comma-separated list of titles of job that already exist in Ashby


  * Locations 


  * Expects a comma-separated list of names of locations that already exist in Ashby


  * Roles 


  * Expects a comma-separated list of emails of existing Ashby users


  * Employment Type 


  * Expects one of the following values: Full time, Part time, Intern, Temporary, Contract 


  * Spelling is case insensitive and multi-word values can be separated with spaces, dashes, or underscores (i.e. Full time, full-time, full_time are all acceptable values)


  * Is Backfill 


  * Expects a true/false value 


  * Accepted true values: true, yes, y, 1 (case insensitive)


  * Accepted false values: false, no, n, 0 (case insensitive)


  * Target Hire Date 


  * Expects an ISO 8601-compliant string (ex: 1982-05-25, 2017-04-22T20:47:05.335-04:00, 2017-04-22T20:47:05.335)


  * Target Start Date 


  * Expects an ISO 8601-compliant string (ex: 1982-05-25, 2017-04-22T20:47:05.335-04:00, 2017-04-22T20:47:05.335)


  * Compensation Range 


  * Expects a string formatted as min_value/max_value/currency_code/interval 


  * At least one of min value or max value must be provided


  * Currency code should be a 3 character code as appear in the Currency dropdown menus


  * Accepted interval values: once, per hour, per day, per week, bi-weekly, per month, per year, per half year, per half month, per quarter 


  * Spelling is case insensitive and multi-word values can be separated with spaces, dashes, or underscores (i.e. Per Hour, per-hour, per_hour are all acceptable values)


  * Additional custom fields


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#how-are-duplicate-candidates-handled)How are duplicate candidates handled?
The bulk import feature catches duplicates by checking across candidate information including GitHub and LinkedIn URLs and emails. If matches are found it won't create a new candidate and will update the existing candidate with any new information. If you are using the email import feature and sending over a resume for a candidate profile that already exists, the duplicate will be flagged and you will receive an error.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#something-went-wrong-how-do-i-undo-the-upload)**Something went wrong; how do I undo the upload?**
If you’d like to undo your upload, there’s an option to delete all uploaded candidates or openings at the bottom of the summary page once an upload is completed. This will not affect candidates or openings that were only updated during the import.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a0dda0c7ffe398ea831500cd66039aa9.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#can-i-give-a-user-access-to-import-candidates-via)C**an I give a user access to import candidates via the bulk CSV importer?**
This is now an additional permission option for users with Elevated Access, yes. When amending a user’s permissions, scroll to Additional User Permissions and check the Bulk Data Imports option.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/542010c3ec57ae7eaecc3da6e6f10c8a.png)
﻿
**Please note:** if the import would take actions that require additional user admin permissions (e.g. creating tags, creating sources), then the user must have those permissions. If they do not, the import row will fail with an error message. Duplicate candidates may appear if existing candidates exist on roles the user does not have access to.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#can-i-import-data-into-custom-fields-when-importin)C**an I import data into custom fields when importing via the bulk CSV importer?**
You can, yes. Custom fields can be selected at the mapping stage. More information on custom fields can be found in our [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿ guide.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#can-i-set-up-auto-forwarding-for-importing-candida)C**an I set up auto-forwarding for importing candidates via email?**
This currently isn’t possible. Resumes would need to be sent over to the provided email addresses manually to create candidates.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#can-i-import-a-candidate-and-consider-them-for-mul)C**an I import a candidate and consider them for multiple jobs as part of the import process?**
No, when importing candidates, they can only be added to the pipeline of one job. Further job considerations would need to be added to the candidate’s profile post-import.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#how-can-candidates-email-to-submit-their-resume)H**ow can candidates email to submit their resume?**
We advise against sharing the candidate upload or job-specific email addresses on your job board or directly with candidates to avoid spam applications. We would recommend advising candidates to send resumes to an internal company email address and then the resumes can be forwarded over to Ashby manually using the [email import option](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#i-have-multiple-jobs-with-the-same-job-title-how-d)I **have multiple jobs with the same job title. How do I ensure the candidate is imported to the correct role?**
If you have multiple jobs with the same job title in Ashby, you can do the following to map the candidate to the correct job based on requisition ID:
  * Add an existing or create a new requisition ID within the Requisition ID field within the job 


  * You can add a requisition ID to a job in Ashby by navigating to Jobs > Jobs _,_ then head to the Settings tab within the job or jobs in question.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e5e5befea79541aafad1d4e744fd4781.png)
﻿
  * Ensure that the requisition ID is also added to your CSV file (if you didn’t have a requisition ID for the role to begin with)


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8cec2e8ab892758015cb29f3df272baa.png)
﻿
  * Import your candidates and opt to map the Requisition ID field in your CSV file to the Job Consideration’s Job Title field


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7cea9e2a33cc90fb5fcb59a0117368a1.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#can-i-apply-a-job-template-to-a-job-i-import-via-a)Can I apply a job template to a job I import via a CSV file?
It's not currently possible to apply a job template to jobs imported via a CSV file.
﻿  

﻿

Candidate Texting
# Candidate Texting Onboarding
10 min
Copy
This guide covers how to get ready to use Candidate Texting. See [Candidate Texting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EQo_-candidate-texting)﻿ for information on how to use the feature. Texting currently requires setup support; please contact your CSM or Support to enable it for your account.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#candidate-texting-onboarding-overview)Candidate Texting Onboarding Overview
Before your team can use Candidate Texting, A2P 10DLC registration must be completed. This process can take a few days. Ensure the information you provide is accurate and up-to-date to streamline onboarding.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#onboarding-to-do-list)Onboarding to-do list
Complete these steps to ensure the process is completed successfully.
Work with your legal team to ensure your company’s privacy policy contains compliant language relevant to texting (Ashby can not provide legal advice).
Input your privacy policy URL, terms and conditions URL, and notification email under **Admin > Organization Setup > Candidate Texting > General, **or click the following link:[https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-texting](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-texting "https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-texting")﻿
Submit the registration form in Ashby. See [What is the registration process?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#what-is-the-registration-process)﻿ for more details.
  * Typical wait times (subject to change):


  * **Brand approval:** 1-7 days


  * **Campaign approval:** 7+ days


See [Twilio's registration FAQ](https://help.twilio.com/articles/1260800720410-What-is-A2P-10DLC-#h_01K3V7F5C20C6T9XFV1N6ERA4E "Twilio's registration FAQ") for up-to-date timelines and details.
Use our [Candidate Texting ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EQo_-candidate-texting)﻿ guide to get set up and started with the feature 🎉
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#what-is-the-registration-process)What is the registration process?
This process is facilitated through an in-app form in Ashby. You are not required to register through The Campaign Registry directly.
Access the in-app form by navigating to **Admin > Organization Setup > Candidate Texting**, and selecting the 10DLC Onboarding tab. Make sure you've filled out the details on the **General** tab first, or you will not be able to proceed.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ZzLNVKpzNYieUV5SxCugo_candidate-texting-acent-ashby-2026-03-05-at-124920-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. **Brand Registration:** First you must register your brand with The Campaign Registry (TCR) and provide details about your company, such as the legal business name, industry, and EIN.


  1. **Campaign Registration:** After brand approval, Ashby must register your messaging campaign. This includes specifying the use case (e.g., customer care, marketing), message volume, and sample messages.


  1. **Selecting a Phone Number:** Once your campaign is registered, you will select a phone number from the Ashby platform to send your messages.


  1. **Team Access Setup:** Finally, you'll configure which team members have access to send messages, ensuring control and compliance.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#what-is-a2p-10dlc)What is A2P 10dlc?
A2P 10DLC (Application-to-Person Ten Digit Long Code) is a new standard for sending business-related SMS and MMS messages to mobile devices in the United States. This system is designed to provide higher message deliverability, increased throughput, and better compliance with regulatory requirements compared to traditional long codes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#what-is-the-difference-between-a2p-10dlc-and-short)What is the difference between A2P 10DLC and short codes?
A2P 10DLC allows businesses to use standard 10-digit local phone numbers for high-volume messaging, whereas short codes are 5-6 digit numbers designed for mass texting with even higher throughput.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#how-long-does-the-registration-process-take)How long does the registration process take?
See the [Onboarding to-do list](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#onboarding-to-do-list)﻿ above for typical wait times.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#how-can-i-ensure-my-texting-campaigns-comply-with)How can I ensure my texting campaigns comply with regulations?
Always obtain explicit consent before sending messages, provide clear opt-out instructions, and adhere to the principles outlined by the CAN-SPAM Act and CTIA guidelines.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#who-needs-to-complete-the-10dlc-process)Who needs to complete the 10dlc process?
Anyone who will be texting candidates with United States phone numbers.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#weve-already-completed-this-process-with-our-legac)We’ve already completed this process with our legacy texting platform. Do we have to do it again?
You’ll need to complete A2P 10dlc registration any time you sign up for a new texting service.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#what-does-regions-of-operation-refer-to-within-the)What does Regions of Operation refer to within the registration form?
This refers to where the business operates.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding#what-does-the-authorized-representative-section-of)What does the Authorized Representative section of the form refer to?
This is the person Twilio should contact to verify the form details - typically this is the user completing the registration form.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)

Security Information
# Understanding And Managing Ashby’s Access Requests for Security Teams
13 min
Copy
This guide outlines frequently asked questions from security teams. Please note that this page is for informational purposes only. Although we do our best to keep this information up-to-date, you should always refer to our [Terms](https://www.ashbyhq.com/resources/terms "Terms"), [Privacy Policy](https://www.ashbyhq.com/resources/privacy "Privacy Policy"), our [Trust Center](https://trust.ashbyhq.com/ "Trust Center") or your signed agreements with Ashby for the most accurate information. Please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com if you have questions not addressed in this page.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#overview)**Overview**
This guide helps IT and security teams understand how Ashby integrates with your systems and what access it requires, to enable evaluating tradeoffs. It includes:
  * A breakdown of Ashby’s platform and security model


  * Integration options for SSO, and SCIM


  * Links to more detailed, relevant documentation


Use this guide to make informed decisions that balance integration benefits with your organization’s risk posture.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#about-ashby)About Ashby
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#what-does-ashby-do)**What does Ashby do?**
Ashby is an all-in-one recruiting platform that replaces several software classes your Talent team may use. As a result, Ashby’s integration with IT systems requires access similar to the software it replaces. Below is a summary:  
| **Software Class**  | **Comparable Software**  | **Data Access**  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| **ATS**  | Greenhouse, Lever  | HRIS Read/Write (e.g., Workday)  |  
| **Sourcing/CRM**  | Gem, SourceWhale, Interseller  | Email Read/Send  |  
| **Scheduling**  | Calendly, GoodTime, Modernloop  | Calendar Read/Write  |  
Ashby requests the minimum access necessary to provide full functionality. While you can limit access, it may impact the Talent team’s experience. This document aims to help you assess your options and their tradeoffs to make an accurate risk assessment of Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#security-privacy-and-reliability-at-ashby)**Security, Privacy, and Reliability at Ashby**
The integrations we request give Ashby access to data sensitive to your company, and we take this responsibility seriously. Here are the measures we take:
  * Conduct annual SOC2 Type II audits and White-Box pentests.


  * Use vulnerability detection tools for infrastructure, third-party packages, and applications.


  * Employ a global team of reliability engineers for high availability and security.


  * Provide resources for compliance with privacy laws like GDPR and CCPA (GDPR Overview).


All data in Ashby:
  * Is encrypted at rest and in transit.


  * Is logically separated per customer and stored behind a VPC.


For details, see our [Security Overview](https://www.ashbyhq.com/resources/security "Security Overview").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#what-access-does-ashby-request)What access does Ashby request?
Ashby integrates with Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 to provide features that enhance hiring speed and efficiency. These integrations increase the risk profile of Ashby as a vendor, but provide significant benefits to your Talent team.
Ashby requests access to the emails and calendars of your team members involved in the hiring process. Ashby will store a subset of this data in our database to allow us to provide a fast and reliable user experience. Our database is encrypted at rest, logically separated by customer, and behind a VPC. All data transmitted between your IT systems, Ashby’s servers, and Ashby’s clients are encrypted in transit. For more information, please read our security overview.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#email-via-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365)Email (via Google Workspace and Microsoft 365)
For more on these two integrations, check out [Integration options for Google Workspace and Microsoft 365](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365)﻿
Ashby supports email syncing via Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 when relevant to the hiring process. Only emails from Ashby users with an Elevated Access or Organization Admin role are synced since these roles are assigned to employees involved in the hiring process.
**From these users, we only store emails sent from Ashby or involving a candidate:**
  * Emails containing a to, cc, or bcc with the candidate’s email and subsequent emails marked by the email provider as being in the email thread.


  * Emails sent from Ashby and subsequent emails marked by the email provider as being in the email thread.


  * If the candidate's domain matches your organization's auth domain, emails will not be synced for that candidate. If the candidate has two email addresses linked to their profile (one with the organization's auth domain and one without) emails including the email address without the organization's auth domain will still be synced.


**Ashby provides additional controls within the application to:**
  * Mark emails as private and automatically mark emails during the offer stage as private. Viewing these emails requires an explicit access role in Ashby.


  * Hide emails when a candidate moves to offer and hired stages.


  * Not sync emails related to a candidate after they are hired.


  * Not sync emails related to a candidate considered for a confidential job


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#calendar-via-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365)Calendar (via Google Workspace and Microsoft 365)
Ashby supports calendar syncing via Google Workspace and Microsoft 365.
**What gets synced:**
  * All accessible calendars and events, including shared calendars (e.g., “Interviews” calendar).


  * Personal calendars are used to determine interviewer availability.


**Privacy and visibility controls:**
  * Ashby respects calendar settings: 


  * Events marked as "Private" are not shown in detail.


  * Events shown as "Free" or "Busy" will only display availability.


  * Calendar visibility settings are honored during scheduling to protect sensitive information.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#how-does-ashby-manage-user-access)How does Ashby manage user access?
Ashby offers a secure and flexible authorization model with granular permissions to manage user access. We also apply a strict permission system within Ashby where users are granted access to jobs and candidate records on a need-to-know basis (see “Access Control” below).
Our default user access is very limited, and can’t access any candidate or job data. Employees cannot access their candidate profile (their candidate profile must be linked to their employee profile in Ashby to prevent this). And we support truly confidential jobs that only the creator and those they give access to can see.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#our-authorization-model)Our Authorization Model
By default, Ashby assigns new users a "Limited Access" role. These users can:
  * Submit interview feedback for interviews they participated in.


  * Submit referrals or applications for internal jobs.


Limited access users cannot:
  * View sensitive information, such as jobs or candidates.


  * Sync or send emails through Ashby.


Ashby provides granular permission settings for administrators to customize user access. Full details are available in Ashby Knowledge Base.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#single-sign-on-sso-and-directory-sync)Single Sign-On (SSO) and Directory Sync
Ashby supports SSO via Google Workspace, Office 365, or Okta.
  * **User Offboarding:** Offboarded users in your directory automatically lose Ashby access.


  * **User Onboarding:** New users are automatically assigned the **"Limited Access"** role.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#account-sync-for-non-employee-accounts)Account Sync for Non-Employee Accounts
When syncing with your domain provider (Google or Microsoft), non-employee accounts, such as contractors or service accounts, may also appear in Ashby.
**By default:**
  * Non-employee accounts are assigned the "Limited Access" role.


  * These users can log in and be scheduled for interviews but cannot view sensitive data like jobs or candidates.


**Management Options:**
  * **Disable Access:** Organization Admins can revoke access for any synced account.


  * **Contractor Access:** For contractors requiring limited access, Ashby supports Agency Users. Learn how to share your account selectively.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#audit-logging)Audit Logging
Ashby maintains audit logs to track changes to important records.
  * Logs are not currently accessible via the product or API but can be provided upon request through Ashby Support.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-and-managing-ashbys-access-requests-for-security-teams#ashby-bot-and-ashby-success-users)Ashby Bot and Ashby Success Users
On the Employees page, you will see two system users:
  * **Ashby Bot** : Used to associate API usage and automation with a specific user for auditing purposes.


  * **Ashby Success (success@ashbyhq.com)** : Used by our support team (with your permission) to assist with troubleshooting or changes.


These users cannot be removed, as they are required to provide support and maintain system functionality.
﻿

building-your-first-dashboard:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Getting Started with Analytics
# Building Your First Dashboard
24 min
Copy
The goal of this document is to familiarize yourself with the basics of building a dashboard to give you the foundational knowledge you need to fully take advantage of Ashby Analytics.
Custom Dashboards are available on the Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#dashboards-overview)Dashboards Overview
Dashboards help organize, present, and visualize your reports in various ways, allowing you to tailor the Ashby experience to specific audiences and purposes. Dashboards allow you to use the same report across multiple dashboards to save you time. They ensure metrics are calculated the same and that team members speak the same recruiting data language.
The process of building a dashboard from scratch is covered in the short video below. The video touches on the key points of this article, with the sections below available for quick reference.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#create-a-dashboard)Create a dashboard
Before creating new reports or finding reports to add to a dashboard, you need to create a dashboard. On the top menu bar, click Dashboard and then select Create Dashboard.
You can now name the dashboard and save it. Make sure the name clearly identifies what or who the dashboard is for.
When saving the dashboard, you will have the option to enable Access Control. By default, dashboards are viewable by anyone in your organization. A private dashboard is only viewable by you, organization admins, and anyone who you specifically grant access to.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 6.57.10 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jgEfzpZQSFkG59ZMtYPVo_screencast2022-11-04at65710am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#create-and-add-a-new-report-to-your-dashboard)Create and add a new report to your dashboard
Now you can build your first report for your dashboard. You can build a report using a template as a base or create one from scratch in Build. For some report ideas, take a look at our guides [Report Catalog: Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates)﻿, [Report Catalog - Essentials](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-essentials)﻿ and [Report Catalog - Advanced](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#creating-a-report-from-a-template)Creating a report from a template
The [Template Library](https://app.ashbyhq.com/reports/templates "Template Library") is a repository of pre-built reports that you can apply filters and groupings to. Before you can add a template report to your dashboard, you need to create one. Navigate to the template library by clicking Reports on the top menu bar and thenTemplates.
Select the template report you would like to build. You can then update the filters and groupings to your liking, name, and save your report.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 7.11.58 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/7xZHc5XmgNxUTu9V0HPwJ_screencast2022-11-04at71158am.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#building-a-custom-report)Building a custom report
You can find further details on building custom reports at [Custom Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports)﻿
If the report you would like to add to your brand new dashboard doesn’t exist as a saved report or as a template, you can create a report from scratch in Build.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 7.15.30 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3UDQxc4j5dFKpieUnAdWn_screencast2022-11-04at71530am.gif)
﻿
There are four report types you can choose from:
  1. Segmentation Report - A visualization report that segments and counts candidates, interviews, and more. Can be filtered and grouped by a related field.


  1. Count Over Time Report - A visualization report that counts candidates, interviews, and more over a specified time field. Can be filtered and grouped by any related field, and the X-axis in this report will always be time.


  1. Candidate List Report - A list report that provides a list of filtered candidates. Can be embedded as a list or number on a dashboard or home screen. Choose this report if you are reporting on a smaller period of time or are not trying to count each application and are more interested in the number of candidates. This list cannot be exported.


  1. Custom List Report - A list report that allows you to build a filtered list of any type (e.g. job, interview) and pick specific fields to display. Can be embedded as a list or number on a dashboard or home screen. Choose this report if you are looking to add a single number tile to your report and are counting anything besides candidates.


Looking for information on our AI Report Builder? Check out [Report Builder & AI-Assisted Report Builder](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-builder-and-ai-assisted-report-builder)﻿. Running into issues understanding your report results? Our AI Report Interpreter can generate a summary and insights. More on this at [AI Report Interpretation](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-report-interpretation)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#adding-your-report-to-your-dashboard)Adding your report to your dashboard
After you save your new report, the Dashboard button is exposed. To add the report to a dashboard, click Dashboards, then + Add to Dashboard, and find/search for your dashboard. Make sure to click Save once you have added the report to the dashboard.
  * The name of the dashboard you have added the report to will now be listed when you click the Dashboards button.


  * The number that appears in the button indicates how many dashboards that particular report appears in.


![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 7.33.09 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/4JCOToKa5oXprmK0ZzJIH_screencast2022-11-04at73309am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#add-a-saved-report-to-a-dashboard)Add a saved report to a dashboard
If the report you would like to add to your dashboard already exists, navigate to the top right corner of your dashboard and click Add Item, then Report. A pop up will appear, allowing you to search for the report you would like to add. Make sure to click Save once the report has been added.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 7.49.15 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/rs3NULP5T2md7xAu7ui1s_screencast2022-11-04at74915am.gif)
﻿
If you do not see the edit options at the top right corner of your dashboard, you may be viewing the dashboard on your home page. Adding a dashboard to your home page gives users easy access, but it is meant for viewing only. To go to the edit page, go to the top right corner and click Edit to navigate to the Dashboard Edit View.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 7.54.38 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/9gpPYV8WTNPiXZ-y8aY1M_screencast2022-11-04at75438am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#add-a-filter-and-connect-it)Add a filter and connect it
﻿[Dashboard Filters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters)﻿ reduce duplicative work, while also providing flexibility for the audience of the dashboard.
  1. To add a filter, navigate to your dashboard make sure you are on the Dashboard Edit view and click Add Filter.


  1. Find the field you would like to filter by. For filters attached to a user, use the User field. You will be able to identify what user role you are filtering for at the report level.


  1. Connect the filter to each of the reports you want the filter to be applied to. When you click on the filter icon, you will be prompted to select/search for the field you want to attach the filter to. Pay attention to the subject the filter is anchored on. For example, a recruiter at the job level and the job consideration level could be different.


You can easily identify what reports have filters attached to them by the color of the dot next to the filter icon.
Green - All dashboard filters on the dashboard have been applied to the report Grey - Some dashboard filters on the dashboard have been applied to the report Red - No dashboard filters on the dashboard have been applied to the report
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 8.06.45 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/URpQJs21pwMAeQdExcdqF_screencast2022-11-04at80645am.gif)
﻿
If you would like, you can require a filter to be applied to all of the reports on a dashboard. To do this, check the box next to Require value. You will see the alert below if any of the reports are not connected to the required filter.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 8.14.30 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/S2xAuSVIKDXUw5Vz6dcjc_screencast2022-11-04at81430am.gif)
﻿
Dashboard filters are applied in the dashboard view and do not change the filters of the underlying report.
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#delete-a-filter)Delete a filter
To delete a dashboard filter, select the Trash icon next to the filter and click Delete.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 8.02.08 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/UGPUr4sZtyFhBIcZzx4bV_screencast2022-11-04at80208am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#rename-a-filter)Rename a filter
You can click on the pencil icon on your dashboard filter to rename it. Amend the filter name on the Change Filter Name pop-up, then click Submit to confirm.
This option allows you to customize filter names so users can clearly identify what each filter is used for. If you add multiple filters of the same type (for example, a User filter connected to the hiring manager field for a report and a User filter connected to the recruiter field of your report), this also allows you to clearly mark the difference between the two filters.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/42ea201043a27045f64308ac475f8a07.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e9a952eb5300a02e9dffde7ea10b33ab.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#navigate-to-a-report-vs-drilling-down)Navigate to a report vs drilling down
It is important to distinguish the difference between the way a report appears on a dashboard (especially a filtered dashboard), drill down reports, and the underlying report.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#view-underlying-report)View Underlying Report
To view or edit the underlying report, click the Go to Report icon, located at the top right corner of each report.
If you apply filters at the dashboard level, the icon changes to Go to Filtered Report, meaning that the unsaved dashboard filters will be applied when viewing the report. You can click Save when viewing the filtered report to add the filter changes to your existing report. If you make any changes to the underlying report and save it, these changes will be reflected on the dashboard.
If you need to get to the underlying report without any dashboard filter context being applied, the action menu on any report tile provides the option (the three dots to the right of the Go to Filtered Report button) to go directly to the report via Go to Report.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#drill-down-reports)Drill down reports
Many of the reports you build in Ashby have drill downs available. For more on these, check out [Report Drill Downs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-drill-downs)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#organize-and-format)Organize and format
Reports will be dropped into the dashboard as you add them. There are a couple ways you can organize and format your dashboard. If you are not able to make any changes, please make sure you are in Edit mode.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#resize-reports)Resize reports
By default, the reports are added in half size, but you can customize the size of any report on the dashboard.
To adjust the size of a report report, click and drag the bottom right corner of any report. Make sure to save once you are done making your changes.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 8.56.17 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/CLounYFtWYQ-1IZMQjHJl_screencast2022-11-04at85617am.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#move-reports)Move reports
To move reports, click and drag the report to the location you would like it to be. Use the purple box that appears when you drag the report as a marker of where the report will be. Make sure to save once you are done making your changes.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 9.03.50 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/EzGbA6G-xtXB6lrV-BxEF_screencast2022-11-04at90350am.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#change-report-view)Change report view
You can change the way the report is visualized (chart or table), by clicking … in the top right corner of any report, then Report View.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 10.18.42 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RA2aIp3YH8hw_7q8UdFQ-_screencast2022-11-04at101842am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#additional-dashboard-options)Additional dashboard options
The options below can be found when clicking More on any dashboard.
Allow empty spaces: keep empty vertical space between tiles
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 9.16.12 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/D1uEPpulW7JxXk7D3e3xw_screencast2022-11-04at91612am.gif)
﻿
Access: Manage who can view a dashboard
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 9.22.44 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yDsqTm1i7tlKqmXCPasr4_screencast2022-11-04at92244am.gif)
﻿
Export: download a dashboard as a PDF
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 9.19.59 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/YAuy5Xz9ynBz5COEh2_W8_screencast2022-11-04at91959am.gif)
﻿
Copy: create a copy of a dashboard
![Screen Cast 2022-11-04 at 9.28.15 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vE05ENei3qDXfVAb8z-Qq_screencast2022-11-04at92815am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#how-often-are-reports-and-dashboards-refreshed)How often are reports and dashboards refreshed?
  * Report results are refreshed live when a report or dashboard is opened. The results are then cached for 1 hour. If report parameters or dashboard filters are changed within that hour, the report or dashboard will be refreshed.


  * Reports can be refreshed manually at any time on the dashboard or when reviewing a report on its own.


  * To refresh a report on a dashboard, click on the … in the top right-hand corner of the report tile and click Refresh Report.


  * Refreshing the page will not refresh a report.


﻿  

﻿  

![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/xNBoKD7g1dM9jLsvNyKQx_image.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#can-i-add-the-same-dashboard-filter-type-to-my-das)Can I add the same dashboard filter type to my dashboard twice?
You can! For example, if you wanted to filter results by hiring manager and by recruiter, you could opt to add two User filters to your dashboard, then connect each filter up to the appropriate field on each report accordingly. We would recommend renaming any duplicate filters to avoid confusion. More on renaming dashboard filters [Building Your First Dashboard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#rename-a-filter)﻿. Adding a value in both filters would mean that only results that match both filters requirements on connected reports would show. For example, if I set my Hiring Manager user filter to only show results where the hiring manager on the job consideration is listed as Gemma and then set my Recruiter user filter to only show results on connected reports where Tim is listed as the recruiter, then only the job considerations where both Gemma is listed as the hiring manager and Tim is listed as the recruiter would be displayed on reports connected to both filters. 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard#can-i-export-my-dashboard)Can I export my dashboard?
You can, yes. Click More, then Export. Your dashboard will then be exported in a PDF format.
﻿  

﻿

# Sourcing
27 Articles
### Betterleap
5 min
Betterleap is an AI copilot that helps recruiters 10x their productivity by streamlining sourcing, automating outreach, and leveraging ATS integrations for data analysis. Connecting Ashby to Betterleap ensures that your data is in sync between both platforms All candidates that are sourced on Betterleap or via Betterleap Chrome extension will be automatically added to Ashby.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/betterleap)
﻿
### Bravado

approvals:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Admin & Organization Settings
Organization Setup
# Approvals
42 min
Copy
You can create an approval process when multiple stakeholders need to review and sign off on a workflow. Ashby currently supports configuring approvals for openings, jobs and offers.
Approvals can be set up on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#when-would-i-use-each-type-of-approval-process)When would I use each type of approval process?  
| **Approval Type**  | **Use Case**  | **Common Approvers**  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Opening Approval  | Commonly use to validate that headcount has been approved by finance.  | Finance, Talent  |  
| Job Approval  | Commonly used to ensure the accuracy of the interview plan stages and other job settings and the quality of job postings.  | Talent  |  
| Offer Approval  | Commonly used to ensure appropriate stakeholders have reviewed offers.  | CEO, Finance, Talent, Hiring Manager  |  
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#permissions-approval-creation-and-approvers)Permissions: Approval Creation and Approvers
See the table below for information on the permissions required to configure approvals and to be an approver.  
| Access Role  | Can configure an approval?  | Can make one-off edits to an approval process for a specific candidate (if enabled)?  | Can be set as an approver?  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
When an approval request is sent, the user can approve or reject the request from their inbox using the approval notification. They do not need to have a specific access role or level of permissions to approve or reject the approval request via email or via Slack. They will need to have at least Elevated Access to the role in question to be able to view the candidate’s profile prior to approving but users with Limited Access can be set as an approver and submit an approval.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#create-an-approval-process)Create an approval process
Navigate to [Approvals](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/approvals "Approvals") in Admin to build an approval process. Before you click + New make sure that you’ve selected the correct object type tab. Options include:
  * Offer


  * Job


  * Opening


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/YibDpjoZnaJIUI8o3u6Uc_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-111937-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#configure-a-new-approval-process)Configure a new approval process
To configure a new process, you’ll first set your scope, then add steps and add approvers to each step.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#setting-a-scope)Setting a Scope
You set criteria by clicking + Add Field to Match, choosing your fields, and then determining your criteria using field values. The approval's scope contains the criteria that must be met in order to trigger an approval flow.
As an example, you could set your scope to only run this approval process for jobs within the Recruiting department. Then the approval process would only run for roles that have the Recruiting team assigned to them.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Tr4Pwc_4jdnsaFPN9F566_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-103620-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#steps)Steps
The steps are the rounds of review within an approval. Click + Add Step to add additional stages to your review process.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/L7_XMBluvuinfDWO_5Hxj_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-103707-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can add names to your steps to more easily identify them. Click the pencil icon to the right of the step name to amend it.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/psAPz52rIVncV89ObPHfP_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-103824-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Conditional Steps**
Conditional steps allow you to set scopes within approval steps. This means you can create additional stages for offers, jobs, and openings that may need further review without the need to configure a whole new approval flow for those edge cases.to handle unique situations or edge cases within a single approval flow, rather than having to create new approval flows for each unique case.
Conditional steps in Approvals are only available on the Plus and Enterprise plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ✅  | ✅  |  
﻿  

To set a step to only run if specific conditions are met by the offer, job, or opening, click the Always dropdown within your step and set it to When condition matches. 
The conditions of your scope are evaluated at the point of an approval request being submitted. For example, if you are creating an opening and an approval process is in place, your approval flow scopes will be reviewed at the point of the approval request being submitted. The opening will use the approval flow it matches the scope for.
﻿  

![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vAlLgj3Ff6AgA7JXmBb9w_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-104014-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
To set your step conditions, click + Add Field to Match to select a field and then determine the values that are a part of your criteria. 
In the example below, this step of a job approval process will only run if the job's location is set to EMEA. If the job's location is not EMEA, then the step in the pictured example will be skipped in the approval process.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/NmXEvaHhrH8npALvPGnuG_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-104440-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#approvers)Approvers
The approvers are the specific stakeholders completing the review. Click + Add Approver within a step to add a stakeholder to a step. You can include multiple approvers at each step or select a pool of users based on their role on the job consideration. You can then choose whether all, one or some approvers need to provide approval.
Once you’ve finished configuring your approval, click Create _._ It will then become active.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vJbnGDL7L1POBb3RKz7Mw_approvals-setup.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#ordering-the-process-list)Ordering the process list
Activated processes in your list of approvals are processed in the order they are listed, so it's key to review your approval list as part of your setup. If multiple processes have a scope that would apply, the one highest on your list of approvals will be used. In the example pictured below, if an offer linked to a job in the Product Support department was also linked to a job with the location set as New York City, as the Product Support Offer Approval flow is higher on the list, this approval flow will be used.
![An approvals list for offer approvals. The Product Support Offer Approval flow is higher on the list and will take priority if the offer meets the conditions of multiple flows.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/8e2vgrDtgVY_VDdQM5Ja8_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-111857-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can drag and drop each process to re-prioritize as needed. Hover over the six dots to the left of the approval flow's title, then click and drag the flows to re-order them. 
![The offer approval flow list with the area to click and drag flows to order them highlighted.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/XEZyXocH1QpGDbCJFLHgG_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-112253-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
As an example, if you have a general catch-all approval flow, you would want to move this to the bottom of your Approvals list so that all other flows would be considered first.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#example-approval-processes-by-scope-types)Example approval processes by scope types
Setting the scope is highly flexible, but the most common options selected are the department and the role. Examples of each are below.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#scope-by-department)**Scope by department**
In this example, the scope field is set to the job's department. All job considerations that an offer is generated for in the engineering department will follow this example process.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#scope-by-role)**Scope by role**
In this example, the scope field is set to rely a specific recruiter. All job considerations that an offer is generated for where Arthur is set as the recruiter on the hiring team will follow this example process.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#use-an-approval-process)Use an approval process
Once activated, all approval types follow a similar flow. The approvers will receive a notification prompting them to approve the offer. Once they submit their response, the process will move on to the next approver.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#opening-approvals)Opening approvals
You can learn more about openings at [Openings Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management)﻿
Opening approvals are used to approve new headcount in Ashby.
When you create an Opening, it will be in a Draft state. You can then move it into the next state by clicking on the Draft tag. You will either have the option to approve it or create an approval request.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BxnFqKl_UCmq1YC9lnj88_opening-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-112822-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You'll then be prompted to review the approval flow details prior to clicking Submit Request to start the approval flow. When submitting for approval, you will have the option to add a note.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/IPOZA8qve-SqaY19L38T3_opening-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-112951-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Once the approval flow is complete, the opening will move into the Approved state. From there, you can Open the opening and allow people to hire against it.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#sending-a-reminder-for-an-opening-approval-request)Sending a reminder for an opening approval request
If you need to send a reminder for an opening approval:
  1. Navigate to Jobs > Openings


  1. Click on the opening in question and click View Approval


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BWlCZYoJsprGaQ3BaWQR-_opening-view-approval.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. On the following popover, click Send Reminders.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/CxWzBhGBf1LPrcPdi7BtB_opening-acent-ashby-2026-04-10-at-125112-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Confirm the recipients and add in a message if needed, then click Send Reminder to X Approvers.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#job-approvals)Job approvals
You can learn more about jobs at [Creating & Posting Jobs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/creating-and-posting-jobs)﻿
Job approvals are used to approve the opening of a role. Once you’ve created your job, click on the Draft drop-down under the job title to see the option to Send for Approval. Click this to then view the approval process and click Submit Request to kick off the approval flow.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/26b45c37cdf35841e00619d46d7af611.png)
﻿
Reviewers commonly ensure the interview plan has all steps needed to assess candidates’ role fit, and the job postings meet quality standards.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8b868a85808788c0cdb33bd28b22e893.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#sending-a-reminder-for-a-job-approval-request)Sending a reminder for a job approval request
If you need to send a reminder for a job approval request:
  1. Navigate to Jobs > Jobs


  1. Click on the job in question and click View Approval


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dJJr4JPyG7V4uVZf8K9Oe_view-approval-on-a-job.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. On the following popover, click Send Reminders.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/5soaEoh4QVrsbc96Epnwa_send-reminders-for-job-approval.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Confirm the recipients and add in a message if needed, then click Send Reminder to X Approvers.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#cancelling-and-restarting-an-in-progress-job-appro)Cancelling and restarting an in-progress job approval
If you edit a job and make amendments to a job field that requires approval, this will restart the approval request using the updated approval process, if one exists.
To set job [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿ as fields that require reapproval if edited:
  1. Head to Admin > Organization Setup > Custom Fields


  1. Click on the Jobs tab


  1. Create a new custom field or click on an existing custom field to amend it.


  1. Ensure that the Require Reapproval checkbox is checked.


![A custom field with the Require Reapproval checkbox highlighted.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qnnglPSQHNPQuR7MFsJ5S_custom-fields-acent-ashby-2026-04-14-at-35412-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
To edit a job:
  1. Navigate to Jobs > Jobs


  1. Locate and click on the job in question.


  1. Navigate to the Settings section of the job and edit a field that requires reapproval. 


  1. Confirm the correct approval process is showing and click Submit Request.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#offer-approvals)Offer approvals
You can learn more about offers at [Offers and Offer Letters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters)﻿
Offer approvals are used to approve the offer details prior to generating an offer letter and extending an offer to a candidate. Once you've created the offer and clicked Submit, you will see the approval process details. Review these and the offer details prior to clicking Submit Request to start the approval flow.
![You can see the approval flow details prior to submitting your request on an offer.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kRirKy65wzACI05MDMBnw_jolene-parsons-product-support-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-113444-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#sending-a-reminder-for-an-offer-approval-request)Sending a reminder for an offer approval request
If you need to send a reminder for an offer approval request:
  1. Navigate to the candidate's profile, then click View Offer on the offer activity


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Al_EFIuvM3H4XRZdKuPRx_sally-enrique-marketeer-acent-ashby-2026-04-10-at-10218-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Within the Offer Overview popover, click Send Reminders.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/UMApo2fqS5Uub3sborCmm_send-reminders-for-offer.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Confirm the recipients and add in a message if needed, then click Send Reminder to X Approvers.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#offer-re-approval-fields)Offer re-approval fields
On offers, changes like the candidates’ last name or address often don’t require reapproval but adjustments to fields like salary and start date may require the approval process to run again. Reapproval fields allow your team to determine which fields do and don’t require a new approval process. You can select your reapproval fields via the Configure Offer Reapproval Fields option on the [Approvals](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/approvals/offer "Approvals") page.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Fa5q1_PmvETjsbf3Z2AYi_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-113738-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Use the Yes/No radio buttons to determine the fields that should trigger an offer reapproval in the popup. Your changes will be saved automatically.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ic5cp1nfwMKtzzxqd8x5P_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-113836-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#enabling-editing-offer-approval-processes-on-a-one)Enabling editing offer approval processes on a one-off basis
Having the option to edit offer approval processes on a one-off basis can be useful in the case of any unexpected changes, such as assigning a delegate if the usual approval is on PTO or looping in an additional approver for a specific offer.
If you’d like to have the option to edit an approval process on a one-off basis, either before the offer approval is submitted or during the active approval process, head to Admin > Organization Setup> Approvals > [Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/approvals/settings "Settings"). From here, using the Allow Editing One-Off Offer Approval Processes dropdown, select an option to determine the users who are allowed to make these edits.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/VkHu585E2aK07IaOhmBRY_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-114202-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Never:** Never allow offer approval processes to be adjusted on a one-off basis.
**Organization Admins Only:** Only those with Organization Admin access can adjust offer approval processes on a one-off basis.
**Organization Admins and Elevated Access Users:** Organization Admins and those with the required permissions as part of their Elevated Access role will be able to adjust offer approval processes on a one-off basis.
The user would need to have **Elevated Access - Admin Private** access in order to view the offer and adjust the offer approval.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#adjusting-the-approval-process-for-an-offer)Adjusting the approval process for an offer
Please note, you cannot make ad-hoc changes to the approval process once the offer has been approved.
You can make one-off amendments to your offer approval process either before the approval request is sent for an offer, or during the approval process.
If you’re making changes to the approval process on the offer prior to submitting your request for approval or while the approval process is in flight, click on View Offer on the offer within the candidate profile.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/IEiiFkl8jyWNbT01HdEex_jolene-parsons-product-support-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-114554-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Click Configure Approval Process from within the Approval Process section of the offer.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/NJr3BE1TjV1tyuae8MoqV_jolene-parsons-product-support-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-114731-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
On the following screen, you can add or remove approvers from steps, add steps or remove steps.
Once you’ve made your adjustments, click Use Approval Process to confirm your changes. Your amendments will now be reflected on the offer in question.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/8JwyG04jNBP18RG7h7oC2_jolene-parsons-product-support-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-114857-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
The original offer approval process set up within Admin > Organization Setup > Approvals will not be impacted by these changes.
Please note that the option to make one-off approval amendments is only available for the offer approval process. This is not available for opening or job approval processes.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#approval-settings)Approval Settings
You can access and amend your approval settings on the [Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/approvals/settings "Settings") tab of the Approvals page.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/iN4Yxg0qzbX1XPe5NEjfl_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-115106-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. **Allow Unauthenticated Approvals From Email Notifications:** If you enable this option, users in the approval chain can approve or reject approval requests directly from email notifications they receive without needing to log in to Ashby. Each approval notification sent via email will include a secure unique link that will automatically authenticate users when used. Users will need to have email notifications enabled for approval requests to use this setting.


  1. **Include Interview Feedback in Offer Approval Email Notifications:** Interview feedback will be included in the body of approval email notifications, alongside the offer and opening details. As such, approvers will be able to see interview feedback in their approval email if this option is enabled. 


  1. **Attach Candidate's Resume to Offer Approval Email Notifications:** When enabled, offer approval email notifications will include the candidate's latest resume.


  1. **Include AI Summary in Offer Approval Email Notifications:** Offer approval email notifications will include an AI-generated summary of the candidate and interview feedback if this option is enabled.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#what-an-approver-sees)What an approver sees
Once an approval request has been sent, approvers can receive notifications via Slack and/or email depending on their notification settings.
For more on notifications, check out [Notifications & Notification Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/notifications-and-notification-settings)﻿.
In an approval request email or Slack notification, the approver will see the key job, offer or opening details. If any previous approval notes have been left, these will also be included in the notification. The approver will have the option to approve or deny directly from the notification. If the approver clicks Approve Xfrom the email notification, they will also have the option to add a note to their approval.
![An email notification for an approval request.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/4vIP2rfOJl2hL2uc_4SjV_ashby-the-marketeer-job-is-aan-ashbycom-learn-ashby-mail-2026-04-10-at-123733-pm.jpg?format=webp)
An email notification for an approval request.
﻿
If the approver receives a notification via Slack, they will have the option to approve, approve with a note or deny.
![A Slack reminder for a job approval](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/28pCqCpRkj4ja68XCVzIQ_ashby-dm-learn-ashby-slack-2026-04-10-at-123337-pm.jpg?format=webp)
A Slack reminder for a job approval
﻿
An approver does not need to have a specific access role or permission level to approve or reject the approval request via email or via Slack. They will need to have at least Elevated Access to the role in question to be able to view the candidate’s profile prior to approving.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#reporting-on-approvals)Reporting on Approvals
A list of available fields and reporting options can be found in [Reporting on Approvals](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-approvals)﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#how-many-processes-can-i-have)How many processes can I have?
You can create unique approval processes which take effect under certain conditions. As only one approval process will apply to each offer, the order of processes in the list determines the priority with which these processes will take effect. You can optionally configure a default fallback process that runs when no other process applies.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#is-it-possible-to-set-the-hiring-manager-on-the-hi)Is it possible to set the ‘Hiring Manager’ on the hiring team of a job as an approver instead of selecting a specific person?
It is! You can now select a pool of users as approvers based on their role on the job. You can pick from pulling an approver from the Job Consideration’s Recruiters, Recruiting Coordinators, Sourcers and/or Hiring Managers.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cfb3a9a7c0183b8e93399bc35cc76195.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#can-a-user-be-ccd-in-to-receive-approval-notificat)Can a user be cc’d in to receive approval notifications?
Currently, only those involved in the approval process will be send notifications about the approval flow.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#if-an-organization-admin-forces-through-an-approva)If an Organization Admin forces through an approval, does the approver receive a notification?
The approver will not receive a notification but if they try to then click Approve they'll see that the approval already occurred and action is no longer needed.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#can-i-set-an-approval-chain-via-the-api)Can I set an approval chain via the API?
The enterprise offer approval API tool is only available on the Enterprise plan. When creating an approval, you have the option to set it to be managed via the API.
To create an approval within Ashby, head to Admin>Organization Setup>Approvals and click + New.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7b2e0bb42c2e16d5024e9199c6fa2f32.png)
﻿
Toggle on the Managed by the API? option in the Steps section. You can opt to set up criteria within the scope section if only offers who meet a certain criteria should be managed via the API.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3636ea11a353dc3843f740533aa3f1b1.png)
﻿
From now on, when an offer is created and submitted, a **Waiting for external process** message will show in the offer overview. This means that Ashby is waiting for an approval chain to be submitted via an API call.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/79ee634485cdfa55f0cd9871157c0440.png)
﻿
To pull data via the API to update the approval chain, you can use the approvalDefinition.update request. More on this [here](https://developers.ashbyhq.com/reference/approvaldefinitionupdate "here"). Once the approval chain has been received, the approval steps will be added to the offer. Please note: This option is currently only available for offers.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#will-incomplete-or-blank-offer-fields-be-included)Will incomplete or blank offer fields be included in the email sent to an approver?
The offer approval email will only include fields that have been filled in, including offer custom fields.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#why-isnt-the-hired-stage-appearing-in-the-dropdown)Why isn’t the ‘Hired’ stage appearing in the dropdown when I click on ‘Change Stage’?
The Hired stage will not appear in the Change Stage dropdown if there are active Offer Approvals set up in the [Admin > Approvals > Offers section](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/approvals/offer "Admin > Approvals > Offers section"). You will need to complete the offer process by clicking the Offer Accepted option within the offer’s settings; once that has been done, the candidate’s stage will automatically change to Hired.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#how-do-i-archive-an-approval-process)How do I archive an approval process?
Click on the approval process within Admin > Organization Setup > Approvals. You will have the options to edit, copy or archive the approval process.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7a658c8b57c6714720e392d57ec9aed1.png)
﻿
You can restore archived approval processes by changing the Active dropdown to Archived when viewing your approvals. 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7f67c955b3d6de45d91730070d637775.png)
﻿
You can then click on the process you’d like to restore and click Restore _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/aacb1df067e67cddcbedeb0674504d90.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#can-i-trigger-a-reapproval-process-for-jobs-and-op)Can I trigger a reapproval process for jobs and openings once they’ve been approved?
After a job or an opening is approved, it’s currently not possible to trigger a reapproval process when changes are made. If reapproval fields are configured for specific fields, they will only trigger reapproval at the initial point of approval. So if you have multiple steps in your approval process and a change was made after approver 1 had approved the opening but approver 2 had yet to approve the opening, the approval process would be triggered again and the approver in the first step of the process would need to provide approval again.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#how-do-job-approval-processes-work-with-confidenti)How do job approval processes work with confidential jobs?
As part of the setup process for a job approval workflow, you can check the Is Confidential? checkbox within the Scope section.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Tn4jIceBBIG3VSpsBzv8O_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-03-02-at-115719-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If you check this checkbox, then the approval process will only apply to confidential jobs that meet the criteria set in the approval scope.
If the checkbox is left unchecked, then the approval process will not be applied to confidential jobs, even if they meet the other scope criteria.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/approvals#why-am-i-seeing-an-approval-gap-message-for-openin)**Why am I seeing an approval gap message for openings and how do I resolve it?**
You’re seeing an approval gap message because your existing opening approvals have filters applied in their configurations. For example, you may have specified that an opening must be a part of a specific team for that approval process to then be triggered, or some other similar criteria. When an opening is created that doesn’t meet the criteria set for any of your organization's configured approval flows, an approval process isn't triggered, resulting in an approval gap. To prevent approval gaps, we recommend creating one approval flow with no filters as a fallback, as shown below. This ensures that any openings that don't match criteria in your existing configurations will still trigger an approval workflow.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sL6m_iQn4mb0V9gHFiIyx_approvals-acent-ashby-2026-02-20-at-102538-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
﻿

# Post Hire
26 Articles
### ADP Workforce Now Connector
15 min
Ashby's integration with ADP Workforce Now® lets you automatically send new hire information from Ashby into ADP as pre-hires, saving time and reducing manual data entry. The following fields are supported by the integration today.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/adp-workforce-now-connector)
﻿
### BambooHR
10 min
Once a candidate has accepted an offer you can push their data into BambooHR to kick off the new employee onboarding process. When pushing a candidate from Ashby to BambooHR, the following candidate details will be included by default Name Email Address Phone Number LinkedIn Profile Salary (this is the default Salary Offer field) Start Date You also have the option to sync additional BambooHR fields as Offer custom fields https //app.

# Advanced Setup
7 min
Copy
This guide covers some more advanced areas of setup that aren't included in the [Ashby Admin Onboarding](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-admin-onboarding)﻿ guide.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-setup#outbound-sourcing)Outbound Sourcing
This section covers features related to sourcing candidates. If you’re still gaining brand awareness, we recommend investing in this section relatively early.
  * Start curating [Sequences](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences)﻿ to send to leads you’re interested in


  * If you’re working with external recruiters/agencies, set them up as [agencies](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/agencies-setup)﻿ in your account to start adding candidates (Note: if they are doing activities beyond sharing candidates, you may need to set them up as an Employee)


  * Some agencies have software that can be integrated with your account. Check out the **Sourcing** section of the [Integrations Marketplace](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/marketplace "Integrations Marketplace") and set up any third-party sourcing tools you use


  * Familiarize yourself with [Projects](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/projects)﻿ and [Tags](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags)﻿. You should understand when to use each:


  * **Projects** are lists of candidates that you can take bulk action on


  * **Tags** can be applied to candidates to make them more discoverable in searches


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-setup#inbound-applications)Inbound Applications
If your brand is a bit more established, we recommend investing in this section to save time through automation and create a streamlined experience for your inbound applicants.
  * If you have high-volume jobs like internships or junior engineer roles, set up [Auto-Reject Rules](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications)﻿ to reject applications that don’t fit criteria set from questions on your application forms


  * If you have internal openings, setup the [Internal Job Board](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/internal-job-board)﻿ to enable your existing team to apply to roles before opening them to the public


  * Setup [EEOC Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/eeoc-surveys)﻿ on your application forms to capture self-reported demographic information for compliance


  * If you’d like to capture additional details like pronouns, you can also set up [Diversity Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/diversity-surveys)﻿﻿


  * If your candidates need to sign an NDA before interviewing, set one up using [E-Signature Documents](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-documents)﻿﻿


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-setup#interviewing)Interviewing
This section covers more automation when you’re actively interviewing and tools on the candidate profile that help you practice good candidate management.
  * By now, you’ve hopefully chosen and implemented an embedded scheduling option; next, explore other [Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities)﻿ that can be added to interview plans like [Take-home Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/take-home-activities)﻿﻿


  * Practice good candidate management by getting into the habit of taking the following actions on the [Candidate Profile](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile)﻿ :


  * Setting follow-up reminders for yourself and other team members


  * @ mentioning your colleagues on candidate profiles to create notes and tasks


  * Setting [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿ to capture additional details about your candidates to create a richer profile


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-setup#post-interviewing)Post Interviewing
This section covers features for when candidates exit your pipeline.
  * Setup [Candidate Experience Surveys](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-experience-surveys)﻿ to capture feedback on your process from your candidates


  * Setup [Offers and Offer Letters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters)﻿ (optionally, using [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿) to send and store offers in one place


  * Integrate your HRIS and/or other **Post Hire** applications in the [Integrations Marketplace](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/marketplace "Integrations Marketplace") to enable sending candidate data from Ashby once someone joins the team


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-setup#reporting-and-alerts)Reporting and Alerts
This section covers resources for getting up and running with Ashby Analytics.
﻿  

  * Review [Navigating Ashby Analytics](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/navigating-ashby-analytics)﻿ to understand the basics


  * Review the [Job Dashboard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard)﻿ on one of your jobs to familiarize with Ashby Analytics


  * Build custom [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿ to cover a variety of use cases. Check out the [Alerts Library](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library)﻿ for inspiration


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-setup#admin-and-customization)Admin and Customization
This section covers more of Admin, including customization options.
  * Visit [Admin](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/overview "Admin") to add custom:


  * ﻿[Archive Reasons](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-archive-reasons "Archive Reasons") to personalize the reasons applications are rejected


  * If you’ve added [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields)﻿, and [select which should be included in the Interviewer Briefing](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interview-briefing-settings "select which should be included in the Interviewer Briefing")﻿


  * Manage your accounts various [Visibility Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/visibility-options-for-users)﻿﻿


  * If your team does headcount planning at regular intervals or has evergreen roles, explore [Openings Management ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management)﻿﻿
