[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Initial Setup
# Scheduling & Interviews: An Introduction
7 min
Copy
This guide provides an overview of the different tools used for scheduling in Ashby and how they work together to help provide an excellent candidate experience.
We have a number of tools in Ashby to help streamline your interview process. In this guide, we’ll cover each tool and the contribution they make.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction#initial-setup)Initial Setup
Prior to setting up your interview plans and interviews, we would recommend setting up a shared calendar and reviewing our meeting hours guide. Click the links below to find out more:
﻿[What is a shared calendar?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-shared-calendar)﻿﻿  

﻿[Meeting Hours](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours)﻿﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction#interview-plans-creating-your-pipeline)Interview Plans: Creating your pipeline
The [Interview Plans](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interview-plans/shared-plans "Interview Plans") section of Ashby allows you to set up the stages that candidates will go through when considered for a role. The plan is split into three sections;
  1. **Lead stages:** The stages used to gauge a sourced candidates interest.


  1. **Active stages:** The application review and interview stages the candidate will go through prior to being archived (rejected) or receiving an offer.


  1. **Completed:** The result of the candidate’s application; either Archived or Hired.


You’ll be asked to specify the interview plan you’d like to use when creating a job - more on this at [Creating & Posting Jobs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/l-NcIsZrB9ztdr4U3idZr)﻿.
For more on the creation of interview plans, check out [Interview Plans](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans)﻿﻿
﻿  

﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f8b5b8d96eb5750bc5ece3d8dc74abe0.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction#interviews-adding-a-briefing-and-assigning-a-feedb)Interviews: Adding a briefing and assigning a feedback form.
﻿[Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews") allow you to provide:
  1. **Instructions:** Information added in this tab will show as a briefing to your interviewers when they are on a panel.


  1. **Feedback Form:** Assign the form that interviewers should add their feedback to post-interview.


You can select your interview type when scheduling within each stage of your interview plan. You can select your interview type either when scheduling a one-off interview on a candidate’s profile, or when setting up schedule interview activities to automate your scheduling process.
When the interview is scheduled, interviewers will be able to click on the interview on their home page to view the briefing and feedback form within the interview details.
﻿  

﻿  

![Creating the interview](https://cdn.zappy.app/a7abc72ae41dfcfc4dd651bffb2c46be.png)
﻿
![Where interviewers can find their briefing and feedback form for a scheduled interview.](https://cdn.zappy.app/d215ca0d1f65529d9dea823498971e2f.png)
﻿
Feedback forms are created in the [Feedback Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-forms "Feedback Forms") section. More on setting up feedback forms at [Feedback Forms and Interviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/feedback-forms-and-interviews)﻿ and [What are ‘Shared’ Interviews, Application Forms, and Feedback Forms?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms)﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction#schedule-interview-activities-automating-elements)Schedule interview activities: Automating elements of your process.
Schedule interview activities can be set up within each stage of your job’s interview plan. These activities allow you to select an interview type, interviewers and set the interview’s settings in advance.
Then when a candidate is moved to the stage that this activity is in, a prompt to schedule them in will automatically be added to their profile.
Based on the activity’s settings, a direct booking link with the panel’s availability can also be generated automatically to be sent out to the candidate. You can also send a candidate availability link.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/91cb76fadb6014048c4b96e069bca22f.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0daa39ba351b749acfc636535f72f22d.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction#what-is-a-direct-booking-link)**What is a direct booking link?**
A direct booking link provides the candidate with the interviewing panel’s availability so they can schedule in their interview directly.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction#what-is-a-candidate-availability-link)**What is a candidate availability link?**
A candidate availability link can be sent out to the candidate so they can enter in their own availability for an interview. The interview is then scheduled manually based on the slots the candidate provides. This is commonly used for multi-event interviews (like an onsite with multiple interviews testing different skills).
You can read more on creating activities at [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿ and [Interviewers: Assigning, Rescheduling and Setting Interviewer Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction#grouped-interview-plan-reporting-on-your-pipelines)Grouped Interview Plan: Reporting on your pipelines
If you have multiple interview plans, we’d recommend setting up a [Grouped Interview Plan](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/data-mapping/grouped-interview-plan "Grouped Interview Plan") to bucket the stages within your interview plans together when reporting on your pipeline.
The grouped interview plan allows you to set overarching stages that the stages within your separate interview plans can be mapped to (for example, a _Screening_ stage in your grouped interview plan could cover Initial Screen, Recruiter Screening and Hiring Manager Screen stages across three different interview plans).
Once your grouped interview plan is in place, you can then head back to your [interview plans](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interview-plans/shared-plans "interview plans") and map their stages to the grouped interview plan stages you’ve created. 
When creating a report relating to your interview pipeline, you will then see the checkbox option to Group Stages?
Checking this box will use your grouped interview plan stages, bucketing your individual interview plans and ensuring you have tidy reports.
More on setting up a grouped interview plan can be found at [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿﻿
The option to Group Stages on a report will give you the option to use your grouped interview plan when displaying results.
![Setting up a grouped interview plan](https://cdn.zappy.app/82816f3a82097b9ddf46f1bdafcdef1b.png)
﻿
![Mapping stages of your interview plan to a grouped interview plan](https://cdn.zappy.app/2bf7d8be5612569d323bc96cca4a7d88.png)
﻿
![The option to ‘Group Stages’ on a report will give you the option to use your grouped interview plan when displaying results. ](https://cdn.zappy.app/428ea5ee86827fea1c3c05d1f4e5c1d3.png)
﻿
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)

# Tags
7 min
Copy
This document outlines the tag options currently supported in Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#tags-overview)Tags Overview
Tags can enhance the discoverability of candidate profiles, create more flexibility in reporting, and more. Ashby currently supports candidate and interviewer tags.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#candidate-tags)Candidate Tags
Candidate tags are applied directly to the candidate profile in the top left corner near the candidate’s name.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d53daddaed68fe655e59c2036fcc16da.png)
﻿
**To use the tags features, you can:**
  1. Add tags by opening the Add tags… dropdown menu and searching for your desired tag


  1. Add new tags inline by typing the name of the new tag and selecting Create new tag


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#settings)Settings
Only Organization Admins can create new tags while assigning them to a candidate profile. This option can be disabled if teams don’t want tags added ad-hoc. To manage this setting, visit the [Candidate Tags](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-tags "Candidate Tags") page in Admin and check/uncheck Enable inline tag creation
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bf88228f30710052ddb2cd7fbd5e9056.png)
﻿
Candidate tags are sorted in ascending alphabetical order in the [Candidate Tags admin section](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-tags "Candidate Tags admin section").
You can search for a specific Candidate tag using the Search bar.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#using-candidate-tags)Using Candidate Tags
Tags can be used on candidate searches, candidate pipelines, reports, and to trigger alerts.
**Candidate Search**
Use candidate tags on search by clicking Add Field to Match, then searching for Tags. Once selected, choose the tag(s), and the candidate list will be filtered to display all candidates with that tag applied to their profile.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/23798805b04dbf11664f15657a5e93db.png)
﻿
**Candidate Pipeline**
Use candidate tags on the candidate by clicking Add Field to Match and then searching for Tags. Once selected, choose the tag(s), and the candidate pipeline will be filtered to display all candidate
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9ba403694d966de204e5f8d9d2c92250.png)
﻿
**Reports and Alerts**
Use candidate tags to filter and group reports. For example, you can create a [segmentation report](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#segmentation)﻿ to group new candidates that entered your pipeline this month by tag.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/059c639b4ebf7df95095ac3cabbf1345.png)
﻿
Use candidate tags to create [custom list reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#custom-list)﻿ and corresponding [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿. For example, you can create an alert that emails a hiring manager whenever the Calibration Needed tag is added to automatically prompt them to review new leads you’ve in the pipeline.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/12162e2550924852644ad26b5ba2fd4d.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#who-can-create-new-tags)Who can create new tags?
Organization Admins and Elevated Access users with access to the Tags[Additional User Permission](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿ can create new tags.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a2b4cac3ee9b09eae14b1ec9f3422cf7.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#who-can-use-tags)Who can use tags?
All users who have access to edit candidate profiles can add tags to them. Details on which access levels can do this can be found in [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#should-i-use-tags-to-nurture-candidates)Should I use tags to nurture candidates?
Projects are dedicated to building talent pools, tags can be used for a variety of efforts. Overusing tags can create visual clutter on candidates’ profiles
We have features on the roadmap to bring more utility to Projects over time, if there’s something they can’t do right now that you’d like to see, please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com or your Customer Success Manager to let us know!
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags#can-i-automatically-apply-a-tag-to-a-candidate)C**an I automatically apply a tag to a candidate?**
This kind of automation currently isn’t possible, tags would need to be added manually.
﻿  

﻿

what-is-a-meeting-organizer:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews & Scheduling FAQs
# What is a Meeting Organizer?
11 min
Copy
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#how-do-i-set-a-meeting-organizer-for-an-interview)How do I set a meeting organizer for an interview?
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#schedule-interview-activity)Schedule Interview Activity
💡 For more on setting up schedule interview activities﻿, please check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿
﻿
If you’ve created schedule interview activities as part of your scheduling setup, you can set the meeting organizer from within the activity itself. We recommend using this option if you know the main point of contact for this interview in advance.
To edit a schedule interview activity:
  * Navigate to Jobs > [Admin](https://app.ashbyhq.com/jobs/all "Admin")﻿


  * Select a job


  * Click on the Interview Plan tab.


  * Click on the activity you’d like to amend, or click Modify, then Editon the activity.


  * Navigate to the Direct Booking & Candidate Availability tab.


  * At the bottom of this tab, you will see the Meeting Request Organizer drop-down. Select a user from this drop-down to determine who should receive notifications for this specific interview.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fcc4f2192c4890d78eabe3dcb117b0fe.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/315c54798e758cfeebf7ec0e178ce223.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4318a8d366859e07f12f57a030798323.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#instant-booking)Instant Booking
If you’re creating an interview from the candidate’s profile, either by clicking + New Interview or by clicking More, then Schedule New Interview on the candidate’s profile, you can specify the meeting organizer both when sending out a direct booking link or a candidate availability link.
**Direct Booking Links**
﻿
To set a meeting organizer for an interview booked via an instant booking link:
  * Click + New Interview on the candidate’s profile, then Create Direct Booking Link


  * Determine the interview details like interview type, duration and interviewers.


  * In the Organizer drop-down, select your meeting organizer.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/74dd88437c46c4104e0f8e23641c0caf.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Candidate Availability links**
If you’re generating an instant candidate availability link to send out to the candidate.
  * Click + New Interview, then Schedule Interview on the candidate’s profile.


  * Click Template on the _Schedules_ sidebar


  * Navigate to the Direct Booking & Candidate Availability tab.


  * Scroll to the Meeting Request Organizer drop-down within the _Organizer_ section.


  * Select the user you’d like to set as the organizer.


  * Create your candidate availability link from the Current tab.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a0ead6e410fbe2c7992c12cbe387384f.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2f749ae7914ac07e9d2bf3931c623b9c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#can-i-change-the-meeting-organizer)Can I change the meeting organizer?
You can only change the meeting organizer up until the candidate schedules via a direct booking link or submits their availability for an interview.
﻿
To change the meeting organizer on an interview activity that has already been generated:
  * Head to the candidate profile


  * Click Schedule on the interview activity, then click View All Scheduling Options


  * Click Template on the Schedules sidebar


  * Navigate to the Direct Booking & Candidate Availability tab.


  * Scroll to the Meeting Request Organizer drop-down within the Organizer section.


  * Select the user you’d like to set as the organizer.


Please note that if you are switching the meeting organizer for an interview generated from a schedule interview activity on your interview plan, you will need to click Unlink & Edit to make any adjustments for that specific interview. The schedule interview activity template in the interview plan will not be impacted by your edits.
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#what-does-the-error-the-meeting-organizer-needs-pe)What does the error The meeting organizer needs permissions to communicate with the candidate mean?
This means that the user that has been specified in the Meeting Organizer field doesn't have the permission to communicate with the candidate, when this is a required permission for a meeting organizer to have.
To amend the user’s permissions, navigate to Admin, then to Employees. From here, update the user to have at least Elevated Access - Hiring Manager access to the job or department the role is a part of. You can read more on permissions at Ashby Permissions.
If you are unable to access the Employees page, please reach out to an Organization Admin to make the update.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#what-happens-if-i-leave-the-meeting-request-organi)What happens if I leave the Meeting Request Organizer field blank?
If this field is left blank, the user who creates the Candidate Availability or Direct Booking link will be set as the Meeting Organizer.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#can-i-set-multiple-meeting-organizers-for-one-inte)Can I set multiple meeting organizers for one interview, or send notifications to other users about candidate availability if they’re not the meeting organizer?
This is not currently possible. If another user should be notified when a candidate submits their availability, we would recommend creating a custom list report (more on these at [Custom Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#custom-list)﻿): **Subject:** Job Considerations **Filters:** Job Consideration’s Activity Status (equals Availability Submitted). You can also add additional filters to only pull results from a specific job, department, etc.
**Fields to Show:** Select any job consideration information you would like to have displayed in the notification.
Once you’ve saved the report, you can opt to set up an alert so notifications are sent to users. You can read more on setting up alerts at [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/40cfaacd3be63e16ad44f7bf20df0dbe.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#can-i-determine-a-default-meeting-organizer-within)Can I determine a default meeting organizer within my Scheduling Defaults?
No, this can only be done for schedule interview activities﻿ or specific interviews.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#can-i-change-the-meeting-organizer-on-an-already-s)Can I change the meeting organizer on an already scheduled interview?
No, once the candidate has scheduled via the direct booking link or submitted their availability, the meeting organizer can no longer be changed on that interview. The interview would need to be cancelled and then either created again or amended from within the interview activity in order to make changes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#what-happens-if-i-manually-schedule-the-interview)What happens if I manually schedule the interview generated by a schedule interview activity?
The meeting organizer will automatically be set to the user scheduling the interview if the interview is manually scheduled from the schedule interview activity. The meeting organizer set within the schedule interview activity will be overridden.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer#help-interviews-are-being-scheduled-on-the-meeting)Help! Interviews are being scheduled on the meeting organizer’s calendar, rather than on our shared calendar?
This can happen if the meeting organizer hasn’t been given the appropriate permissions to schedule to the shared calendar the interview is being scheduled on. For more on shared calendar setup, check out [What is a shared calendar?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-shared-calendar)﻿
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
User Access & Sharing Metrics
# Permissions & Licenses in Analytics
6 min
Copy
This guide covers adding permissions and licenses to users in Ashby Analytics. If you are using Ashby All-In-One, review our [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿ guide. For more on adding and removing Ashby users, check out [How do I provision new users in my Ashby account manually?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-provision-new-users-in-my-ashby-account-manually)﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-licenses-in-analytics#overview)Overview
When adding new users in Ashby Analytics make sure you:
  * ﻿[Grant Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿: the permissions tab of the user profile will let you specify which data the user has access to, e.g. limiting reporting to a specific job or department.


  * Assign Licenses**:** the License tab lets you assign a View Only or admin license. The license type determines what features the user has access to, e.g. if they are able to build reports or not. 


Licenses are an additional permissions option available in Ashby Analytics only. 
  * Configure the user's home page (optional): pin specific reports or dashboards to the user home page for easy access.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-licenses-in-analytics#organization-admin)Organization Admin
This is common role for analysts, admins, heads of talent, recruiting ops folks, etc. Many companies also grant the entire recruiting team this access level.
If you make someone an organization admin, they have access to all data in Ashby (excluding their own linked profile). Organization Admins will also not have access to confidential roles by default. To provide the user access to all confidential roles, switch the Has access to all confidential jobs and projects within assigned permissions to Yes. If you'd prefer to only provide access to some confidential jobs, you can add access to these roles in the Access Restrictions section of the Permissions tab.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5ee969335ad43cb824315d61006f42e4.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-licenses-in-analytics#admin-for-particular-departments-and-jobs)Admin for Particular Departments and Jobs
If you want to grant someone partial access, you do so by picking Elevated Access as the global role. From here on you can grant individual access roles for departments or jobs.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/63fae20ba56c1686ad9337ea2736248f.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/826889c588561613f5378a1495221a3e.png)
﻿
In the example above, Ronald has Admin access to the engineering department. The Admin access role grants access to all data, except for private fields. Ronald for example would not be able to see/report on salary information on offers, since these fields are marked as private.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-licenses-in-analytics#admin-private-for-particular-departments-and-jobs)Admin Private for Particular Departments and Jobs
This role configuration is very similar as above, except that the user can report on private fields as well.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-licenses-in-analytics#license-types)License Types
Ashby Analytics has two different license types.
**Analytics - Admin**
Users assigned this license have the ability to explore report templates and save and edit reports and dashboards. They have access to all features in Ashby Analytics.
**Analytics - View Only**
User with this license are limited to viewing reports created by "Analytics - Admin" users. They can configure their personal home page to show their preferred dashboards and reports, but they cannot create new reports or explore data (e.g. via filtering) on their own.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-licenses-in-analytics#user-home-page-configuration)User Home Page Configuration
New users start with no reports or dashboards in their home page by default. Admin users have the option of pinning a custom selection of any report or dashboard to each user's home page to ensure they see the required reporting when they log in to Ashby. Visit the Employees Section of the Admin view, and select the Home Page tab of any employee profile to add, remove or arrange Home Page content.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4d653124a6950970c93b623119f952a5.png)
﻿
  1. Click and drag usingthe grid icon to the left of the reports or dashboards you'd like to move to re-order them


  1. Click the trash can icon to remove a report or dashboard from the user's home page


  1. Click + Add to add a new report or dashboard to the user's home page

do-not-contact-company-lists:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/do-not-contact-company-lists -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Sourcing & Outreach
# Do Not Contact Company Lists
7 min
Copy
This feature allows your organization to define a list of companies from which your recruiting team prefers not to source or reach out to candidates. Organizations use this feature to support internal hiring guidelines, such as when managing sensitive partnerships, client relationships, or active M&A scenarios.
This feature is designed solely to support unilateral, internal recruiting policies. It is not intended to implement any agreement with other employers to limit hiring or recruiting. Customers control and manage all list entries and are responsible for ensuring that their use complies with applicable laws and company policies.
Do Not Contact company lists are available on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/do-not-contact-company-lists#configuring-a-recruiting-exclusion-list)Configuring a Recruiting Exclusion List
﻿
To configure Do Not Contact rules from scratch:
  * Navigate to _Admin > Sourcing > Do Not Contact Rules_


  * Click _+ Add New Rule_


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8958c89533241edf042c847aab6af2eb.png)
﻿
  * Select a company from the list provided (all companies across existing candidate profiles will be pulled into this list)


  * Confirm the rule name and configure the following rule information: 


  * Internal notes (optional)


  * Expiry date (optional)


  * Block Outbound Sequences: Enable this if you would like to prevent users from enrolling this candidate in an outbound sequence.


  * Employee scope. The following employee scope options are available for matching against this rule: 


  * Current employees only


  * All employees (employed at the specified company at any time)


  * All employees (employees employed within a specified limited time period)


  * Click _+ Add_ to add any additional variations on the company name to the rule.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e4e65f4eda429291b04a3aa37cc37e53.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/do-not-contact-company-lists#importing-a-do-not-contact-list)Importing a Do Not Contact List
If you have multiple rules you’d like to configure, you can import a CSV of rules. To import a Do Not Contact rule list:
  * Navigate to _Admin > Sourcing > Do Not Contact Rules_


  * Click _Import CSV_ and select your CSV file to upload from your computer


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9a6b94a53cc51763b6cd72fcda0a0879.png)
﻿
The following fields can be included in the CSV file. Please note that the name and name variant is required for creating the rule.  
| Header name  | Description  | Example  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Rule Name  | The rule display name. Rows with the same rule name are grouped under the same rule.  | Portfolio Companies  |  
| Company Name  | Employer/Company names to match against.  | Acme Inc  |  
| Notes  | Optional notes to display the reason for the rule or other important information internally.  | These are companies within our portfolio. Employees from these companies should not be sourced or contacted about our roles.  |  
| Employment Scope  | Controls which employment history entry is considered for this rule. Use ‘current_only’ to match only current employer, or ‘all’ to match all employment history.  | all  |  
| Scope Months  | Number of months to look back on when employment scope is set as ‘all’. Leave empty to check all employment history without a time limit.  | 3  |  
| Expires At  | Expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format.  | 2025-04-22  |  
| Blocks Email Sending  | If enabled, produces an campaign limiting error that blocks sending. If not enabled, only a warning will show and campaign emails will not be blocked.  | Yes  |  
You can use [this template](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ZiPgk61Jg3Sg-bMC0P-lKccpb5K6T_824ZtgoHKyaLw/edit?gid=0#gid=0 "this template") to ensure your data is formatted correctly before uploading.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/do-not-contact-company-lists#how-does-the-do-not-contact-messaging-show-in-ashb)How does the Do Not Contact messaging show in Ashby?
If a candidate matches a configured Do Not Contact rule, a _Do Not Contact_ red tag will show in the top-right of the candidate’s profile, to the left of the Follow-up, Email, Archive and other action buttons. You can hover over the tag to view the rule that is being applied to this particular candidate.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6a38b7bfb924773bdab435e69172a11b.png)
﻿
If you opt to email the candidate and sequence sending has been blocked by your configured rule, then when a user tries to send a sequence to the candidate, they will see an error referring to the configured rule.
If sequence sending has not been blocked as part of your rule configuration, then the email will be sent to the candidate.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/28f5fc8312e927e926b7a3b6a8fd54ae.png)
﻿
If viewing the candidate in the Chrome Extension, you’ll also see a banner displayed during your sourcing workflow. Note that a candidate must be added or exist in Ashby to trigger the red banner warning. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/f5nWuwoxZeP_Vw49aZjVZ_image.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)

Alerts & Exports
# Alerts
28 min
Copy
This document reviews Alerts - a reporting feature that allows report delivery based on robust, highly configurable scheduling, custom conditions, and dynamic routing of select results to involved team members.
A library of Alert templates can be found [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library)﻿. Please note: Those on the Foundations plan are unable to create custom alerts but do have access to use Alert templates.
Custom Alerts can be configured on the Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#alerts-overview)Alerts Overview
Alerts was designed to allow customized and timely delivery of data that matters to the recipients who matter. We offer a way to automate the delivery of this data with our difference detection and dynamic routing. A single alert alleviates the need to create many customized report delivery schedules.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#example-use-cases)Example Use Cases
We’ve identified a number of use cases where our alerts can immediately be applied. They range from data quality and operational excellence to milestone notifications. Some of the use cases we have identified and use ourselves at Ashby are given below.
Surveys & Feedback: use alerts to be notified of feedback on candidate interviews or survey results
  * ﻿[Alert when a low NPS Score comes in on a Candidate Experience Survey](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#nps-detractors)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert whenever a candidate receives a 4 on a scorecard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#feedback-form-4-scores)﻿


  * ﻿[Alert when a candidate post-recruiter screen reaches an average score of >3](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#avg-overall-recommendation-3-past-initial-screen)﻿


Data Quality: use alerts to be notified when important fields are empty
  * ﻿[Alert when someone makes it past recruiter screen but has no source associated](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#candidates-passed-recruiter-screen-wo-source)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert when a job was opened without assigned recruiter](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#jobs-wo-a-hiring-managerrecruiter-assigned)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert when a referral was submitted and has no recruiter assigned](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#referral-submitted-wo-recruiter)﻿﻿  



Recruiting Operations: use alerts to stay on top of candidate, interview, or job activity
  * ﻿[Alert when a new role is opened](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#new-jobrequisition-opened)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert when a job has become “stale”](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#stale-jobs)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alerts to individual recruiters when they are breaking custom SLAs (e.g. time in stage, time to review referral, etc.)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#slas-vary-by-organization)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[New hire alert](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#new-hire-alert)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert when a candidate applies who is at one of the top 10 companies you recruit from](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#most-recent-company-in-top-10)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert when an interview needs to be scheduled](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#interviews-needs-scheduling)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert when candidates who had made it to onsite were archived, with archive reason](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#had-onsite-interview-outcome-archived)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Alert whenever a new candidate makes it to offer stage](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#reached-offer-stage)﻿**﻿  
**


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#building-a-report-and-alert-from-scratch)Building a report and alert from scratch
In this example, we create a report that looks at candidates in the recruiter screen stage for 10+ days and set up an alert to automatically notify individual recruiters of only the candidates they are managing. In a matter of minutes, you can set up a single alert with dynamic routing to notify team members of records that are important to them.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#how-to-create-an-alert)How to Create an Alert
An alert can be created for any saved custom list report. To add a new alert, navigate to **Reports > Saved Reports** and click on the custom list report in question, then click the Alerts button.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 7.52.15 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/p0XX3kDuvPIaUu0IahJ0J_screencast2022-11-02at75215am.gif)
﻿
Once an alert has been added to a Custom List report, you can configure the following:
  1. What records to deliver


  1. When the alert should be delivered


  1. Who will receive the Alert and Who should be listed as the sender.


  1. What the delivery email says


  1. How the alert will be delivered


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#what-records-to-deliver)What records to deliver
You can decide what records you want delivered. This allows for customized focus on details relevant to your particular use case.
Report data of an alert gets generated on behalf of the user who creates the alert, so all of the creator’s accounts permissions apply.
If the Entire List is sent to a specified user and not a User involved as (more on this [in our FAQ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#when-sending-with-to-users-involved-as-what-record)﻿), then the user receiving the report will be able to see all of the report data in the alert email. Be sure to add filters to remove any jobs (including confidential jobs) or specific data that you don’t want users receiving the alert to see as part of your alert setup process.
  * Entire List: this will send the entire set of report results at a fixed period, daily, weekly, or quarterly with customization


  * Added Records: this will regularly run your report and compare the report results since the previous run to intelligently send only the records that were added


  * Removed Records: this will regularly run your report and compare the report results since the previous run to intelligently send only the records that were removed


![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 8.28.29 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/eZKh_KFLvbeIwdPmXO5Sg_screencast2022-11-02at82829am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#when-the-alert-should-be-delivered)When the alert should be delivered
After selecting what records to deliver, you can select when the alert should be delivered.
  * Periodically: runs the report at a fixed period and sends an alert when records were added/removed since the previous run. You can opt to send alerts daily, weekly, monthly or quarterly. You can also set a custom frequency by selecting Set custom frequency from the dropdown.


![The alerts menu with an open dropdown showing the cadence options for sending alerts.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/6xGUb0n6bbOJ9GeBo37KU_slack-candidate-alert-report-acent-ashby-2026-02-19-at-21042-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * Immediately: runs the report on a very short regular period and sends an alert whenever records were added/removed since the previous run. From least to most complex, checks for new results will occur every 5, 30, or 60 minutes. Most basic reports will send new results within 5 minutes, but particularly complex reports can take up to 1 hour for delivery of new results.


The option to send an alert immediately is only available when the alert type is set to Added or Removed Records.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 8.16.57 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wfnZjP0rdiOdykEgK7Ami_screencast2022-11-02at81657am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#who-will-receive-the-alert)Who will receive the Alert
After your report type and delivery schedule are set, you can specify the Alert’s recipients. Ashby Alerts allows standard email or a dynamic routing option to only deliver records to the involved users. You can opt to send individual emails to each specified user as part of the alert, or send a single email including all users who are set as recipients.
![routing.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/WTzXKZC5E4ViDp31psHzp_routing.png?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Deliver any record as**


  * Use the Individual Emails dropdown to specify if the record should be sent as individual emails or as a group email. This option can only be used when adding specific users or emails directly.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8fd5f1267df916f922d16b7168e1c978.png)
﻿
  * You can then use the + Add user or email button to add users or specific emails to send the alert to:


![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 8.34.25 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/35sUb76mPvZm49eCC1eEA_screencast2022-11-02at83425am.gif)
﻿
  * If your report includes user roles, such as recruiters, you can send just the records those users are involved with to them automatically using the + Add user role button.


![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 8.49.54 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3-qwe2QmcIoCdtD2RD_xf_screencast2022-11-02at84954am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#choosing-a-sender)Choosing a sender
You now also have the option to select who the alert should show as coming from, either by leaving is as the generating user (the default), or selecting Ashby from the drop-down.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/00d1066f6b81d89c0aab81cc719b11e3.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#what-the-delivery-email-says)What the delivery email says
You can customize the title of the alert and email body. The alert results will be inserted inline to the email after any custom message content you add.
Once you have entered an alert title, the button to Lock Report & Activate or Activate will turn purple, meaning the Alert can now be activated.
  * When activating an Added Records or Removed Records alert, you will see Lock Report & Activate. These alert types depend on differences in report results to identify the added and removed records, thus editing the report itself will impact the alert behavior. The report will remain locked to prevent any modification of your delivered content without your review.


![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 9.12.40 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/d3q6KGdCeyaaYx6I62nRr_screencast2022-11-02at91240am.gif)
﻿
Below is an example of an alert delivery email. In this instance, there are links to the candidate profile, job page, and the underlying Custom List report. There is also a link to view the report in Ashby.
![Screen Shot 2022-11-02 at 9.52.58 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/svebJ4XGz_sMBogvV3nRw_screenshot2022-11-02at95258am.png?format=webp)
﻿
If you would prefer for Ashby links (for example, a link to the candidate profile) to not be included, you can toggle off the Include Links to Ashby option within the sender settings. Please note that this setting applies only to Ashby links and doesn't affect non-Ashby links (for example, a candidate's social URL). The link to view the report in Ashby will still be present within the email.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/91802a159da63b598678f64886ab6eff.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#how-the-alert-will-be-delivered)How the alert will be delivered
When adding an alert, the default delivery method is email. If the integration for Slack is enabled, you have the option to select Slack.
For more information on configuring the integration for Slack, see [Slack](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/slack)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#delivery-via-slack)**Delivery via Slack**
With the integration for Slack enabled, your Alert will have a “Delivery” field which allows you to select email or Slack. When the Slack option is selected, you have two delivery options:
  * **Direct message:** send report results directly to the specified or dynamically routed users


  * **Send to Channel:** specify a Slack channel you would like the report results to be delivered to


  * After you add the Ashby application to a private Slack channel, you will be able to send notifications to that channel.


  * To add the Ashby application: type @ashby as a message in the channel (where you would type a message to send to the whole channel) and select Ashby APP not in channel.


![Screen Shot 2023-11-06 at 8.33.01 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/cP8Oh1af4kI4ffQUWzvqw_screenshot2023-11-06at83301am.png?format=webp)
﻿
  * Syncing private channels may take up to 30 minutes after the Ashby app is initially added.


  * To prevent private channel names from being revealed, you must type in the exact private channel name when creating your alert.


![Screen Shot 2022-11-02 at 1.00.57 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/2MnWJwTpGwlQlZDvoB4Wm_screenshot2022-11-02at10057pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#slack-message-formatting)**Slack message formatting**
The results delivered via Slack messages follow a formatting determined by the custom list report: the first column (often the Candidate) is the top-level element, and the subsequent columns are listed as sub bullet points. An example of our new hire notification, which incorporates linking to process documents, is shown below.
![slack_alert_example.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/h0efO9a87MTtMGfXU7WUw_slackalertexample.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#view-active-alerts)View Active Alerts
The saved reports directory has a folder titled Reports with Alerts in which any report with an alert will be listed. When hovering over the bell icon, you can see the number of active and inactive alerts. The bell icon will be dark purple if a report has an active alert.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 10.58.48 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Y_yXYb-qffs06sv0qtNrh_screencast2022-11-02at105848am.gif)
﻿
At the report level, the Alerts button will show the number of alerts that have been added to the report. If you hover over the alert count on the Alerts button, it will show the number of active and inactive alerts.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 11.15.49 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/V6nAIKKh0U1AeZJu9XjNl_screencast2022-11-02at111549am.gif)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#make-changes-to-alerts)Make Changes to Alerts
  * You can make changes to an alert by clicking Alerts > Deactivate & Edit. Any of the alert’s configurations can be updated after it has been deactivated.


  * Make sure to click the Activate button once you are done making your changes.


![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 11.36.03 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BtqgITKWrZlu85fM50wnO_screencast2022-11-02at113603am.gif)
﻿
  * Added/Removed Records reports function by comparing sequential snapshots of a report. To start, the first snapshot is taken when the alert is enabled. Thereafter, a snapshot is made at each scheduled delivery time. If an alert is deactivated and reactivated, the starting snapshot is reset to the report results at the time of reactivating the alert.


  * Scheduled immediately: Any record that is added to or removed from a report during the time it is deactivated is no longer considered added or removed, and no alert will be sent once the alert is activated again.


  * Scheduled periodically: If a record is added to or removed from a report before the alert is scheduled to be sent and then the report is deactivated, that record is no longer considered added or removed, so it will not be included in the alert once the alert is activated again.


  * For example:


  * Alert is scheduled to be sent at 2 pm every day.


  * A record is added to the report at 11 am.


  * The alert is deactivated to make changes to the underlying report at 12:30 pm.


  * The alert is activated at 12:35 pm.


  * The record added at 11 am will not be included in the 2 pm alert because it is not considered added/removed since it was already on the report when the alert was activated at 12:35 pm.


  * You can see when an alert was enabled or updated by clicking the Alerts button and hovering over the info icon next to Enabled By.


![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 11.45.31 AM.gif](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ROeAOvVorBSBJFIiD8OM3_screencast2022-11-02at114531am.gif)
﻿
  * If you need to make changes to the Custom List report upon which an alert has been added, you will need to unlock the report itself. This will deactivate the alert, so make sure to reactivate the alert once the report changes have been saved.


![Screen Cast 2022-11-02 at 12.11.36 PM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jZQeCPRAHKCbYZkP8XbCl_screencast2022-11-02at121136pm.gif)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#delete-an-alert)Delete an Alert
You can delete an alert by clicking the Alerts button and then the Trash icon.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-03 at 7.58.02 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/N-Zr1eV31qUuZ7yZD0dC9_screencast2022-11-03at75802am.gif)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#can-an-alert-be-sent-on-the-last-day-of-every-mont)Can an alert be sent on the last day of every month?
Yes; we recommending selecting the 31st day of the month. The alert will be sent on the last day of the month, even on months with less than 31 days.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#do-alert-links-to-candidate-profiles-work-for-non)Do alert links to candidate profiles work for non-Ashby users?
For users that do not have access to Ashby, or are logged out, the candidate profile link from an Ashby alert will redirect to your host ATS candidate profile (e.g. directly link to Greenhouse or Lever).
For any Ashby user that is logged in, the candidate profile link in an alert will open the Ashby profile, from which the user can opt to view the profile in the host ATS by selecting the View in [ATS] button.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#when-sending-with-to-users-involved-as-what-record)When sending with “to users involved as,” what records are included?
When selecting a user field such as recruiter or hiring manager in the to users involved as, then only records which those users are associated with will be delivered.
For example: if you have a custom list report scheduled to send the entire list every Monday, opting to send records to the associated recruiter will result in each recruiter seeing the entire list of candidates they are associated with, but no other records.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#why-did-i-receive-an-empty-alert)Why did I receive an empty alert?
For recurring alerts (e.g. those not set to immediate), we always send emails to all of the fixed recipients, regardless of whether the alert and underlying report is empty or not.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#why-didnt-i-receive-my-recurring-alert)Why didn’t I receive my recurring alert?
For recurring alerts, to users involved as recipients will not receive an alert when the underlying Custom List report is empty. No one is “involved as” when the record set is empty.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#can-i-attach-the-results-as-a-csv-file-to-the-aler)Can I attach the results as a CSV file to the alert?
You can, yes! Both email alerts and alerts in Slack support attaching the results as a CSV file. When setting up your alert or editing it, select CSV from the Attach dropdown within the Advanced section.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7751848b0dfdffa50b3bff65aabcb58a.png)
﻿
Please note that if the attachments exceed the platform's limits (10MB for email, 1GB for Slack) they will not be attached, but the normal message will still be sent out.
﻿  

﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#can-i-create-an-alert-for-a-segmentation-count-ove)Can I create an alert for a Segmentation, Count Over Time or Candidate List report?
Alerts can only be configured for custom list reports. If you do have a dashboard with reports that you’d like to send out to users on a regular basis, check out [Scheduled Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduled-dashboards)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#can-i-attach-candidate-resumes-to-an-alert)Can I attach candidate resumes to an alert?
Currently this isn’t possible. You can opt to include the candidate profile URL in your custom list report details though, so the candidate profile can be easily accessed from the report. Please note that users will need to have access to the job, department, team or location that the candidate is linked to in order to view the profile.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#is-markdown-formatting-supported-for-alerts-in-sla)Is markdown formatting supported for alerts in Slack?
There is limited markdown formatting support for alerts sent to Slack. You may now format alerts sent to Slack with hyperlinks, bold, italic and headings.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts#why-did-i-receive-an-alert-i-didnt-expect)Why did I receive an alert I didn't expect?
Report alerts that are configured to send on added or removed rows trigger on any added or removed row, regardless of the reason. Some common causes of unexpected alerts are:
  * Permission changes for the user the report results are generated as (such that they can now see a different set of rows)


  * Changes to filters. Rows that were previously in the report may end up filtered out or rows that weren't previously in the report are now included.


﻿

candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-a:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Applications
# Candidate Fraud Detection Overview & Admin Settings
11 min
Copy
Candidate Fraud Detection allows you to manually run fraud checks as well as automatically flag inbound job applications that contain potential fraud signals. These fraud signals are identified based on specific criteria, and the applications are segmented for manual review, helping you to streamline the identification of potentially fraudulent applications. 
This article explains how Candidate Fraud Detection works and steps to configure it in your Admin settings.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#permissions-access)Permissions & access
Candidate Fraud Detection is available across all plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
Only those with **Organizational Admin** privileges can configure Candidate Fraud Detection settings.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#how-it-works)How it works
When candidate fraud detection is enabled, this tool analyzes four signal categories on applications:
  * Device


  * IP Address


  * Email address (only if this information has been provided as part of the candidate’s application)


  * Phone number (only if this information has been provided as part of the candidate’s application)


The analysis of these areas is used to identify suspicious patterns that could be indicative of fraudulent activity.
Once candidate fraud detection is enabled, Ashby automatically analyzes new applications as they are submitted. Existing applications are not analyzed automatically, but you can run fraud checks on them manually or in bulk. See [Running Candidate Fraud Detection Checks & Reviewing Fraud Signals](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/running-candidate-fraud-detection-checks-and-reviewing-fraud-signals)﻿ for more info.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#enabling-the-candidate-fraud-detection-tool)Enabling the Candidate Fraud Detection tool
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#enabling-admin-settings)Enabling admin settings
Candidate Fraud Detection must be enabled in your admin settings before it can be enabled for certain applications. 
  1. Navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > Opt-In Features and scroll to the **Security** section.


  1. Enable the **Fraud Detection** toggle:


![Screenshot showing the Fraud Detection tile toggled ON. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/zGVxOAhNPisfFkL9tr6G3_opt-in-features-acent-ashby-2026-03-18-at-45035-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Scroll back up and locate the **Candidate Fraud Signal Summary** tile. If you would like an AI-generated summary of fraud signals (recommended), turn this on.


![Screenshot showing the Candidate Fraud Signal Summary tile toggled ON.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ABEd4Yf-vQmsOFErZEexE_opt-in-features-acent-ashby-2026-03-18-at-45229-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Using Candidate Fraud Detection requires 2 AI credits per candidate. Enabling Candidate Fraud Signal Summaries **does not** add any additional charge on top of this. 
  * Information on how AI credits are allotted can be found in [AI Credits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-credits)﻿ .


  * For info on purchasing additional credits, check out [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#enabling-fraud-detection-on-all-jobs)Enabling fraud detection on all jobs
To enable fraud detection across all jobs by default:
  1. Navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > Security


  1. Check Enable Fraud Detection on All Jobs


![Screenshot showing the checkbox to enable fraud detection on all jobs as checked.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/DM3gREJ0TVOf3Cw92acF2_security-settings-acent-ashby-2026-03-19-at-42545-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#enablingdisabling-fraud-detection-for-specific-job)Enabling/disabling fraud detection for specific jobs
By default, fraud detection will run across all jobs when enabled within the Security section of Admin. If you’d like to only enable fraud detection for specific jobs, or turn off fraud detection for certain jobs if you have it set as enabled by default, this is also possible.
To access and amend the fraud detection settings for a specific job:
  1. Head to Jobs > Jobs and select the job in question


  1. Navigate to the Settings section of the job


  1. Use the Enable Fraud Detection setting to enable or disable the tool as required


![Screenshot showing the Fraud Detection setting enabled under the Settings tab of a job.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/JxfrWNvn0wNYgtccrHtBd_backend-engineer-acent-ashby-2026-03-19-at-43315-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#can-i-give-feedback-on-the-ai-summary-provided-whe)C**an I give feedback on the AI summary provided when fraud signals are detected?**
You can, yes. Click the three dots on the AI summary and select Give Feedback. From there you can give a positive or negative rating and add any comments. We really appreciate any feedback provided.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8fa801e2a213087be60ca316e279f766.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/537c556a273395596abd29db7ffd2aa8.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#how-do-i-enable-the-ai-generated-candidate-fraud-s)H**ow do I enable the AI-Generated Candidate Fraud Signal Summary option?**
  * Navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > Opt-In Features


  * If you have yet to opt-in to enable AI features, opt-in there is required to enable this feature


  * Check the AI-Generated Candidate Fraud Signal Summary checkbox to enable this specific AI feature.


Please note that this feature is not enabling the Candidate Fraud Detection tool as a whole, just the AI-Generated Summary.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#can-i-filter-candidates-by-their-fraud-status)C**an I filter candidates by their fraud status?**
Yes, you can use the Fraud Status field to filter candidates down by their status when it comes to fraud detection. You can then use the drop-down to select the fraud status that you’re looking for. You can choose from the following:
  * Multiple Fraud Signals - Unreviewed


  * Marked Fraudulent


  * Marked False Positive


  * Marked Uncertain - Flagged for Monitoring


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cd6cc2535b3aee6e30c33b1e422fc691.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#are-fraud-signal-detection-checks-only-run-at-the)A**re fraud signal detection checks only run at the Application Review stage?**
Currently, candidate fraud signal checks are only automatically run in the Application Reviewstage and are only run at the point of the application being submitted, yes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-fraud-detection-overview-and-admin-settings#are-there-reporting-options-for-candidate-fraud-si)Are there reporting options for candidate fraud signals?
Yes, you can find some report examples and further information at [Reporting on Candidate Fraud Status and Signals](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-on-candidate-fraud-status-and-signals)﻿.
﻿  

﻿

Activity Configuration
# Activities
9 min
Copy
When set up, activities automatically appear on a candidate's profile when they are moved to a stage. This streamlines the candidate management process and enables a consistent experience across candidates. For more on structured interviewing, check out [Scheduling & Interviews: An Introduction](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#activities-overview)Activities overview
Activities enable you to automate tasks like drafting emails, and setting up calendar invites for candidates that are in your interview process.
Ashby currently supports six activity types:
  * Schedule Interview


  * Take-Home


  * Application Review


  * Send Email


  * Schedule Questionnaire


  * E-Signature Document


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c6fbcdb13342f682856ee18e499cbdee.png)
﻿
﻿  

See the table below for information on the roles that have access to set up and edit activities.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin (Private)  | ✅*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Elevated Access - Admin and Elevated Access - Admin Private users will only be able to edit the activities on the roles they have access to.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#schedule-interview)Schedule interview
The Schedule Interview activity automatically adds a [schedule template](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿ to a candidate's profile when they are moved to a particular stage, determining the interview, feedback form, interviewers and interview settings in advance. For more on configuring these, please check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#take-home)Take-home
The Take-home activity automatically adds a prompt to send a take-home assignment activity to a candidate's profile when they are moved to a particular stage. This activity supports uploading the candidate's submission so the reviewer can view and score directly in Ashby.
Learn more in the [Take-home Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/take-home-activities)﻿ guide
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#application-review)Application review
The Application Review activity is a default activity on the application review stage in an interview plan. It enables the profile to be included in bulk application review (more on this at [Application Review](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review)﻿. The Application Review activity can also be added to other active stages in the interview plan (except for the Offer stage), to provide a prompt in other stages for the candidate’s application to be reviewed.
Once you add a review application activity to your job or job template’s interview plan, you’ll be prompted to select a reviewer (or a role on the hiring team to determine that users with that role will complete the review application activity). You will also be able to determine a feedback form.
If you are adding or editing an Review Application activity, you’ll be prompted to determine the job considerations you’d like new assigned reviewers applied to.
You’ll be able to choose between current and future job considerations (so adding or amending the activity for those currently in the pipeline and applications moving forward) or only future job considerations.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/491c2cf02e23f380244a9394d6408a87.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1f22752911c8f974074e3d3ab6f02382.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#send-email)Send email
The Send Email activity automatically adds a prompt to email a candidate, along with an email template to a candidate's profile when they are moved to the specified stage. Want more information on setting up email templates? Check out [Emails](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities "Emails").
Please note that the Send Email activity should only be used to email the candidate. If you’d like to receive notifications when a candidate has moved stages, this is an option you can enable within your notifications. More on this at [Notifications & Notification Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/notifications-and-notification-settings)﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#schedule-questionnaire)Schedule Questionnaire
The Schedule Questionnaire activity allows you to select a questionnaire and an email template in advance. Then when a candidate is moved to the specified stage, a prompt will appear to send the questionnaire out to them using your specified email template. You can read more on configuring questionnaires at [Questionnaires](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities "Questionnaires")﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#e-signature-document)E-Signature Document
The E-Signature Document activity prompts you to specify the document template you’d like to use. When the candidate is moved to the specified stage, you’ll be prompted to send them the e-signature document. More on configuring e-signature documents can be found at [E-Signature Documents](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-documents)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#can-i-automate-an-activity-so-action-is-automatica)Can I automate an activity, so action is automatically taken when a candidate moves to a new stage?
You can automate activities, yes. More on this can be found at [Automated Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/activities#can-i-copy-an-existing-activity-from-an-interview)Can I copy an existing activity from an interview plan stage on one job to an interview plan stage on another?
You can, yes! When you are on a job’s interview plan and click Add Activity To… on an interview plan stage you’ll see the option to add a new activity and a Copy Activities option.
Click Copy Activities, then select the job, interview plan, interview stage you’d like to copy activities from. Choose the activities you’d like to copy. The selected activities will be copied into the original stage you chose.
﻿
Once an activity is copied over, if the activity uses a shared interview type or feedback form (more on those at [What are ‘Shared’ Interviews, Application Forms, and Feedback Forms?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-are-shared-interviews-application-forms-and-feedback-forms)﻿ , then those will be reference in the copy. Any custom interview types or feedback forms will be copied over and can be amended without impacting the original activity that was copied. 
﻿
