sequences:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Sourcing & Outreach
# Sequences
31 min
Copy
This article covers how to create and use sequences in Ashby. Sequences let you reach out to candidates and automate emails to gauge their interest in a role.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#sequence-overview)Sequence overview
Sequences let you:
  * Create personalized email templates to send to individual candidates or in bulk.


  * Schedule initial outreach and set time intervals for follow-up stages.


  * Streamline sourcing by contacting candidates from the Ashby Chrome extension while on a candidate’s LinkedIn profile.


  * Track email opens, clicks, and responses from the candidate’s side.


Sequences are primarily built for reaching out to leads to gauge interest. If you’d like to trigger emails automatically when candidates reach a certain stage in a job pipeline, use [Automated Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities)﻿ instead.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#sequence-access)Sequence access
Use sequences to reach out to leads and candidates, but access is controlled by user permissions and sequence settings.  
| Access Role  | Has access to view shared sequence information?  | Can enroll candidates in a sequence?  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Agency User  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked with an agency)  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin (Private)  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  | ✅  |  
  * **Limited Access users** and **users linked to an agency** cannot see any sequence data or enroll candidates in sequences.


  * To enroll candidates in sequences, you need **Elevated Access** or higher.


  * Elevated Access users can enroll candidates in **non-confidential** sequences if those candidates: 


  * Do not have any job considerations, or


  * Are considered for a job (or jobs) where the user has **Hiring Manager** access or higher.


If you don’t want Elevated Access users to view data for certain sequences, you can set sequences to **personal** or **confidential**.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#personal-sequences)Personal sequences
If you create a sequence with **personal** access:
  * Only you, **Organization Admins** , and Elevated Access users with a **direct link** to the sequence can: 


  * View the sequence.


  * View sequence data.


  * Enroll candidates in that sequence.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#confidential-sequences)Confidential sequences
If you create a **confidential** sequence:
  * Only **you as the creator** can: 


  * View the sequence and its data.


  * Enroll candidates in the sequence.


Confidential sequences are available on **Legacy Plus** , **Plus** , and **Enterprise** plans.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#creating-email-sequences)Creating email sequences
To get started with sequences, you first create a **sequence template**.
You can access sequences from **Sourcing > Sequences**. From here, you can create and edit multi-stage templates with customizable tokens you can use from the Ashby app or the Chrome extension.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/834e3cf02cbcf429c90139f1e44afa53.png)
﻿
To create a new email sequence:
  1. Navigate to **Sourcing > Sequences**.


  1. Select + New.


  1. Enter a title for your sequence.


  1. Choose whether access is Shared, Personal, or Confidential.


  1. Optionally link the sequence to a job (more about this in [Sequence Linking](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#linking-a-sequence-to-a-job)﻿).


  1. Select Create Sequence to open the template editor and configure your stages.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a7256f5cc28b7bbd470d8b3c4ce49aee.png)
﻿
Once you create the template, Ashby automatically saves it so you can immediately start enrolling candidates. You can return to the **sequence manager** at any time to edit the sequence.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#template-editor)Template editor
The template editor lets you build and manage each stage of your sequence.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5d2d845e0e77dca86160c68c9839142c.png)
﻿
  1. **Name** – View and edit the name of your sequence.


  1. **Sender** – Confirm the sender of your sequence. If different stages use different senders, multiple email addresses appear here instead of a specific user’s email address. You cannot select a no-reply address or default shared email aliases﻿ as a sender.


  1. **Stages** – View all stages in the sequence and when each will send if you enroll a candidate now. Use the trash can icon to delete a stage.


  1. **Add stage** – Add additional stages to your sequence.


  1. **X error(s)** – Shows how many issues are preventing candidates from being enrolled in the sequence.


  1. **Error** – When viewing a stage, this banner explains what needs to be added or changed so candidates can be enrolled.


  1. **Type** – Choose the stage type: 


  * Email.


  * Prompt to send a LinkedIn InMail.


  * Prompt to send a LinkedIn connection request.


More on LinkedIn InMail and Connection Request stages in sequences at [Sequences: InMail and Connection Request Stages](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages)﻿.
  1. **Send** – Set when this stage should send relative to the date and time the candidate is enrolled.


  1. **From** – Choose who the email is sent from or who receives the prompt to send the LinkedIn message.


  1. **Subject** – Enter the subject line of the email or LinkedIn InMail.


  1. **Body** – Write the content of the email, InMail, or connection-request note.


You can use:
  * **Tokens** to personalize subject lines and body text with fields like candidate name, company, or sender details.


  * **File attachments** and **links** from the formatting menu below the body.


Tokens are a quick way to personalize messages without manually editing common properties. To add a token, select the t icon (hover text: “Personalize with token”) and choose a property.
There are additional guides with:
  * A full list of [Substitution Token](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens)﻿options.


  * Details on [AI Tokens in Outreach Emails](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-tokens-in-outreach-emails)﻿.


Refer to those if you need more advanced personalization.
Once you exit the editor, the sequence template will auto-save and you can immediately start to enroll candidates. You can also return to the sequence manager at any point to edit the sequence.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#linking-a-sequence-to-a-job)Linking a sequence to a job
You can link a sequence to a **job** so Ashby can automatically manage your outbound pipeline.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7e3740f69be9653f9bc38eca9ff69a58.png)
﻿
When a sequence is linked to a job:
  * Leads are automatically moved to the **Reached out** interview stage when they receive the first sequence email.


  * They are moved to the **Replied** stage when they reply.


  * They are **archived** in the linked job when you mark them as not interested.


  * They can be **automatically archived after a delay** if they never reply based on settings you configure.


When setting up a sequence, link it to a job by selecting the job from the job dropdown. Once set, you can configure the sequence template to adjust when and how candidates are archived.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#enrolling-candidates-in-an-email-sequence)Enrolling candidates in an email sequence
Once you’ve created an email sequence template, you can enroll candidates right away. The most common path is via the **Ashby Chrome extension** while on a candidate’s LinkedIn profile, but you can also enroll from the candidate profile in Ashby and from the sequence manager.
Organizational admins can set outreach limits so that candidates do not receive too many emails in a short amount of time. These limits can be set by navigating to **Admin > Sourcing > Outreach Frequency Policy**.
With an outreach frequency set, candidates who have been contacted within a set number of days will not be enrolled in sequences.
  * One-off emails are still allowed.


  * Policy is based on the organization’s timezone.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#enrolling-a-candidate-from-the-ashby-chrome-extens)Enrolling a candidate from the Ashby Chrome extension
You can add candidates to Ashby and enroll them in a sequence directly from the Ashby Chrome extension.
**Reason for Outreach and other custom fields**
Before sending a sequence, populate details in the reason for outreach and other [custom fields](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/custom-fields?objectType=candidate "custom fields") you create. If you have tokens in your sequences that rely on information added into custom fields, then this will ensure that information is populated.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5d6c6f70964b123b53ea3713e666595b.png)
﻿
To enroll a candidate from the Chrome extension:
  1. Open the candidate’s public LinkedIn profile.


  1. Open the **Ashby Chrome extension**.


  1. Add or update fields like **Reason for outreach** and any relevant candidate custom fields.


  1. Select Draft Sequence.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ec34ba41a16f84cf9bac534013399c66.png)
﻿
In the **Draft Sequence** dialog:
  1. Choose a **sequence template** from the Template dropdown.


  1. Select the candidate’s **email address** from the Email dropdown.


  1. Choose whether to: 


  * Start the sequence immediately, or


  * Review the stages before sending.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ed7a6f930bcf3a19a0bf1dae52364645.png)
﻿
If you select Review, you can:
  * Review each stage.


  * Adjust subject lines, bodies, or delays.


  * Confirm the sender and timing.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6402ba838da138b9d7cffbc6e56f4e37.png)
﻿
When you are ready, select Start to begin the sequence.
If you close the dialog without starting the sequence:
  * The sequence is saved as a **draft** on the candidate’s profile.


  * You can start it later from within Ashby.


If you enroll a candidate in a sequence via the Chrome extension, that enrollment is treated as an **individual sequence** and cannot be edited in bulk with other enrollments.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#enrolling-a-candidate-from-their-ashby-profile)Enrolling a candidate from their Ashby profile
To enroll a candidate from their profile in Ashby:
  1. Open the candidate’s profile.


  1. Select More > Enroll in sequence.


![Enrolling a candidate from their Ashby profile](https://cdn.zappy.app/6305cdc39753d6024402d2a461e30d08.png)
﻿
  1. Choose a **sequence template** from the dropdown.


  1. Review and personalize the stages as needed.


  1. Select Start 1 sequence to kick off the first stage for that candidate.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5493d8aa43b13cb8ca1f8ec632b5618c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#enrolling-candidates-from-the-sequence-manager)Enrolling candidate(s) from the sequence manager
You can enroll one or multiple candidates from the **sequence overview** page.
To enroll from the sequence manager:
  1. Go to **Sourcing > Sequences**.


  1. Select the sequence template you want to use to open the **sequence manager**.


  1. Select Enroll to open the enrollment manager.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b4c305807c216e97af1050916fe0db70.png)
﻿
  1. In the **Add candidates** field, search for the candidates you want to enroll and add them to the list. 


  * Any existing **sequence drafts** for those candidates will also appear here.


  1. When your list is complete, select Edit sequences.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a212d190a5f73641c1563a7d2ba35713.png)
﻿
Ashby opens the sequence editor with a **candidate-level draft** for each enrollment. From here you can:
  * Customize each candidate’s stages.


  * Fix any issues flagged in the left-hand panel (for example, missing emails).


  * Choose whether to include an **unsubscribe link** in the email stages.


When you’re ready, select Start X sequence(s) to start the sequences for all selected candidates.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5f6a412496efc930b5677f2754cccdfc.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#stopping-or-cancelling-an-email-sequence-for-a-can)Stopping or cancelling an email sequence for a candidate
Ashby automatically stops a candidate’s sequence if they reply to one of the emails in that sequence.
To manually cancel a sequence that is in progress:
  1. Go to **Sourcing > Sequences** and open the template.


  1. Select the **In Progress** tab.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5dc1903a93afb46487296876f9e06cb5.png)
﻿
  1. Check the box next to the candidate(s) whose sequence you want to cancel.


  1. Select Cancel Sequence.


  1. In the bulk action dialog that appears, select Start to confirm the cancellation.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a1e47e4800047b0ee1bfba2473bb7380.png)
﻿
Note:
  * Once a sequence has started, you cannot delete the record of that enrollment from the sequence details.


  * Canceling a sequence stops further **sequence stages** , but does not affect any **non-sequence emails** already scheduled separately for that candidate.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#sequence-reporting)Sequence reporting
Once you start enrolling candidates in a sequence, Ashby automatically tracks email activity and provides reporting to help you manage outreach and measure sequence effectiveness.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#sequence-pipeline)Sequence pipeline
Each sequence has a pipeline overview on its sequence manager page that shows current and historical enrollments at the candidate level.
To view the pipeline for a sequence:
  1. Go to **Sourcing > Sequences**.


  1. Select the sequence you want to view.


![Using the Sequence Pipeline to manage sequence progress and effectiveness.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/HA1BGC8FWRsC41pSk9uaw_usingthesequencepipelinetomanagesequenceprogressandeffectiveness.png?format=webp)
﻿
Ashby groups enrollments into the following stages:
  * **Enrolled** – All candidates you’ve enrolled to date, including those with active, completed, and canceled sequences.


  * **In progress** – Candidates currently enrolled in active sequences. 


  * Use this stage to find all active enrollments or to cancel active sequences in bulk.


  * **Completed, no reply** – Candidates whose sequence ended without a reply. 


  * These may require a different email or sequence to re-engage.


  * **Replied** – Candidates who replied to at least one email in the sequence. 


  * Replies may or may not indicate interest; review the response and manually set the **response** status.


  * **Interested** – Candidates you’ve determined to be a potentially good fit and who have expressed interest. 


  * These are good prospects to nurture or move into a specific job pipeline.


  * **Converted** – Candidates who have been added to a job opening and are now in the job pipeline. 


  * This count indicates how successful the sequence is at sourcing candidates who proceed into the pipeline.


Important:
  * Candidates are filtered into these stages based on their **current state**.


  * If a candidate is marked **interested** on your sequence but is later **archived** from the job consideration linked to that sequence, they no longer appear in the **Interested** segment.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#sequence-metrics)Sequence metrics
Ashby also tracks sequence-level metrics, including:
  * **Open rate** – The percentage of recipients who opened a specific email.


  * **Reply rate** – The percentage of recipients who replied.


  * **Click rate** – The percentage of recipients who clicked any embedded links.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a7bec78c57139ae3311d79e4b3d0af68.png)
﻿
You can:
  * View metrics for a specific sequence on the **top right** of the sequence pipeline overview page.


  * Get a broader view of sequence activity from **Sourcing > Home**: 


  * The top chart shows weekly sequence activity for the past three months.


  * You can switch the report type to: 


  * Sequences sent.


  * Sequence reply rate (% of candidates who replied).


  * Conversion rate (% of candidates who were converted and added to a job).


  * You can toggle between **Mine** and **Everyone** to change scope.


  * Selecting a chart value drills down to candidate-level details.


![Sequence metrics like open rate, reply rate, and conversion rate](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Vbf00uSmPnHePfxORRxQH_sequencemetricslikeopenratereplyrateandconversionrate.png?format=webp)
﻿

#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#is-there-a-limit-to-how-many-recipients-can-be-add)Is there a limit to how many recipients can be added to a sequence at once?
Yes, 500 recipients can be added to a sequence at one time.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-copy-or-duplicate-a-sequence)Can I copy or duplicate a sequence?
You can yes. To duplicate a sequence:
  1. Navigate to **Sourcing > Sequences**


  1. Click on the sequence you would like to copy


  1. Click More, then Copy


  1. Determine the type of copy you're looking to make; a shared copy, a personal copy or a confidential copy.


![The More option on a sequence, with the Copy action highlighted and the pop up to confirm the copy type.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/N7kWzeEN5SPgQzRJ3OKnd_duplicating-a-sequence.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-limit-how-frequently-candidates-are-contacte)Can I limit how frequently candidates are contacted?
Yes. Organizational admins can set an outreach frequency policy that prevents candidates who have been contacted in a certain number of days from receiving emails from sequences. One-off emails can still be sent. Access these settings at **Admin > Sourcing > Outreach Frequency Policy**.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#i-enrolled-a-bunch-of-recipients-into-a-sequence-h)I enrolled a bunch of recipients into a sequence, how long will it take to send? 
To avoid email provider rate-limiting, Ashby:
  * Adds an incremental delay when emailing more than **10** recipients.


  * Sends emails in **batches of 10** once they’re queued.


This means large batches take longer to fully send than small batches.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-disable-email-tracking)C**an I disable email tracking?**
Individuals have the option to disable email tracking on the [Email Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/email "Email Settings") page in Personal Settings. Tracking is only supported on sequences and doing so will impact the open and click stats on the sequence overview.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/18d3c185c8b8917aad94e71cb2840871.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-ashby-automatically-classify-a-leads-response)Can Ashby automatically classify a lead’s response to a sequence as ‘Interested’ or ‘Not Interested’?
Yes! This is one of Ashby’s AI Features that can be opted-in to and enabled within Opt-In Features in Admin. You can also set your preferences within the Email Settings section of Personal Settings. You can opt to enable the automatic reply status option, disable it (regardless of the organization-wide settings) or use the organization default.
The person who initiated the sequence will receive a notification email with the option to change the reply status.
When enabled the AI Assistant will automatically set the sequences reply status based on the candidate’s email response to a sequence. You can override this classification if needed via the Set Response dropdown.
For more on AI features, check out [AI Features in Ashby](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-features-in-ashby)﻿.

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-leads-or-candidates-unsubscribe-from-sequences)Can leads or candidates unsubscribe from sequences?
We do now have the option to include an Unsubscribe link in your sequence emails. On clicking the link, leads can update their subscription preferences and determine whether they’d like to unsubscribe from emails about the current role and/or all future roles.
You can enable the Unsubscribe option in the Settings section of your sequence.
Check the Include unsubscribe link by default checkbox to add the option to email stages moving forward for this specific sequence.
Candidates on clicking the Unsubscribe link in an email will see the following checkbox options:
  * Current Outreach


  * Outreach about New Roles


Once they’ve checked or unchecked the boxes based on whether they’d like to receive or not receive future emails, the lead can click Save Preference
When the lead unsubscribes, an activity will be added to their profile showing that they’ve unsubscribed.
If you try and enroll a candidate in another sequence when they’ve unsubscribed, you will see an error when editing or reviewing the stages of your sequence, confirming that the candidate has unsubscribed. This error will show both when enrolling candidates in Ashby, and when enrolling via the Chrome extension.
Users can also unsubscribe or re-subscribe leads and candidates from their profile in Ashby, in case the lead reaches out. To amend the subscription status for a candidate or lead, click More on their profile, then Manage Subscriptions. From there, you can check or uncheck the Sourcing Sequences box, then hit Save Preferences to confirm.
Should a candidate want to re-subscribe, they can do so by clicking the Unsubscribe link again in their email. They can then re-adjust their preferences on the following page.
Should a candidate want to re-subscribe, they can do so by clicking the Unsubscribe link again in their email.

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-edit-a-sequence-after-it-has-been-started)Can I edit a sequence after it has been started?
You can! If a sequence has been started and you need to make any amendments (like correcting a typo or adding additional information), you can edit and adjust unsent stages for live sequences.
**Edit sequence template for all existing live sequences and future enrolments.**
To amend your sequence template, click Edit Template when viewing your chosen sequence. Scroll to any unsent stages and make your edits. Your changes will be saved automatically.
If you close out the editing wizard or navigate to another page in Ashby, you will see the option to apply your edits to active sequences.
The details of the active sequences will be listed. Click Apply Edits to update the unsent stages of your live sequences. If you’d only like the edits to apply to those enrolled in the future, click Don’t Apply _._
Once the sequences have been successfully edited, you’ll receive a confirmation message.
If any sequences were not successfully edited, click View Detailed Results to review the sequences that weren’t updated and the failure reason.

**Edit the sequence template for all /existing live sequences during a new enrolment.**
After making edits to a sequence during a new enrolment, when starting the sequence and enrolling new leads, you will have the option to save the changes for only future enrolments and/or save the changes for currently enrolled candidates.
Check both checkboxes to save these changes for future recipients and those unsent stages for running or paused sequences. If you only want the changes to apply to this current enrolment, leave both boxes unchecked.

**Bulk edit live sequences for multiple candidates.**
When viewing sequence data, check the boxes beside the candidates you’d like to amend the unsent stages for. From there, click Edit Sequence.
Please note: only those enrolled at the same time can have their unsent stages amended in bulk. If you select sequences from different enrolments, Ashby will identify the largest group of selected enrolments that occured at the same time and the edits will only be applied to that group. Click Edit Included Sequences to move forward with editing. Sequences sent via the Chrome Extension will be counted as individual sequences, so can’t be amended in bulk.
The bulk editor will ready the sequences for editing. Click Edit Sequences, make your changes in the editing wizard, then click Edit X Sequences to confirm your changes.

Click View Detailed Results to view the sequences and the failure reasons. To make further amendments to the failed sequences, click Edit Failed Sequences _._

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-resume-a-paused-sequence)Can I resume a paused sequence?
Yes, you can. When viewing the list of candidates or leads enrolled in a sequence, check the boxes next to the canceled or paused sequences you’d like to resume. Then click Resume Sequence _,_ then Start on the Bulk Action wizard.
The specified sequences will then continue to run. If the scheduled date has passed, the next stage in the sequence will be sent immediately. All follow-up stages will be rescheduled to maintain the initial delay.

﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b60a42521ab1f72a9272a8818b77c856.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-resume-a-canceled-sequence)Can I resume a canceled sequence?
No, this isn't currently possible.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#is-a-sequence-canceled-when-a-candidate-is-archive)I**s a sequence canceled when a candidate is archived from a job consideration?**
When a candidate is archived on any job consideration, all running sequences are canceled (even if the sequence is not linked to that job consideration). You will see a warning showing the sequence that will be canceled when selecting your archive reason in the rejection process.
If you don’t want to archive the candidate from a job consideration but would like to remove them from the sequence, you can opt to stop the sequence for that specific candidate. More on this here. Please note: Any separate emails scheduled to be sent to the candidate (not within a sequence) will not be canceled automatically.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-enroll-a-candidate-in-more-than-one-sequence)Can I enroll a candidate in more than one sequence at a time?
No, a candidate can only be enrolled in one sequence at a time. If a candidate is already enrolled in a sequence and a user attempts to enroll them in another sequence, then they will see the following error message.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e8a0281a16f82e022ccc042e3cdbf108.png)
﻿
If the user tries to enroll the candidate in a sequence and they are enrolled in a personal or confidential sequence that the user does not have access to, they will see the following error message.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/839b90ffc516a3e109b7d15e2ee8530c.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-mark-an-existing-sequence-as-confidential-or)Can I mark an existing sequence as Confidential or share a confidential sequence?
You can. When viewing the enrollments within the sequence template, click More. From here, you will be able to either share your confidential sequences with other users, or mark it as confidential. Please note, you will need to be the creator of the sequence to make these changes.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bf218017581d5a708d19ab0f159eb263.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#what-permissions-do-i-need-to-enroll-candidates-in)What permissions do I need to enroll candidates in a sequence?
You will need to have Elevated Access or higher to enrol candidates in sequences.
If you are an Elevated Access user, you will be able to view non-confidential sequence data and enrol candidates in non-confidential sequences who meet one of the following criteria:
  * Do not have any job considerations or


  * Are considered for a job or jobs where you have a Hiring Manager access role or higher.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#can-i-view-more-details-on-a-specific-recipients-s)Can I view more details on a specific recipient’s sequence?
When viewing the recipient list, you can click on a recipient to get more information on their sequence, including if they’ve clicked links within your sequence in each stage, opens, clicks and replies and when the next stage will be triggered.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7117cebc42f2aa08294c84cd6945c0f8.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences#a-candidate-replied-to-a-sequence-but-the-email-wa)**A candidate replied to a sequence, but the email was not logged and the sequence did not stop. Why?**
This typically happens due to [Email Visibility Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-settings "Email Visibility Settings") on your Ashby account.
If email sync is disabled for any of the following candidate types, replies from those candidates will not be logged, and Ashby will not detect the reply to stop the sequence:
  1. Hired candidates


  1. Candidates on confidential jobs


  1. Internal candidates


When email visibility is restricted for these groups, Ashby cannot record inbound replies or use them as a trigger to automatically stop a sequence.
What we recommend:
  1. Review your email visibility settings to confirm which candidate types have email sync enabled.


  1. If you expect replies from these candidates to stop sequences automatically, ensure email visibility is turned on for the relevant group.


This behavior is expected when email visibility is limited and does not indicate an issue with the sequence itself.
﻿

# Understanding Funnel Metrics
7 min
Copy
This document reviews funnel metric concepts, how to build funnels in Ashby and how to interpret the results.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-funnel-metrics#building-a-funnel-in-ashby)Building a funnel in Ashby
Funnels are part of Ashby's standard report template library. Simply navigate to Reports > Templates and select Funnel Report from the library. By default, Ashby's funnel report selects all Job Considerations (for Greenhouse users: Applications, Lever users: Opportunities) with interview activity in the last 6 months.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/KFN72EEKNDEOlPUM4a2nw_funnelreport2.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-funnel-metrics#what-goes-into-a-funnel-how-selections-form-a-coho)What goes into a funnel? How selections form a cohort
When Ashby's funnel report defaults to selecting all Job Considerations that had an interview in the last 6 months, a selection is taking place. That selection results in a fixed group to analyze, sometimes referred to as a cohort**.** The starting stage of the funnel shows the size of the cohort, giving a total count above the bar. In the example above, a cohort of 1,962 Job Considerations were selected for analysis in the funnel.
As a single candidate can have multiple Job Considerations it's possible that an individual candidate contributes to a funnel multiple times, and progresses to different stages per Job Consideration.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-funnel-metrics#completed-job-considerations-only)Completed Job Considerations Only
For a funnel to be accurate, it can only analyze Job Considerations that have completed their interview process (either hired or archived). As we do not know in advance how far any consideration will progress, the funnel will be skewed if any counting occurs prematurely.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-funnel-metrics#interpreting-funnel-results)Interpreting Funnel Results
Reading our funnel report from left to right, we see that the first stage shows the total cohort size, as noted above. The subsequent stages show a percent conversion from the prior step. The 78.2% in the second stage is the percentage of the first stage that proceeded, and so on. This percentage is sometimes referred to as a "passthrough rate".
Passthrough rates near 0% or 100% indicate that a stage is either extremely difficult, or extremely easy, respectively. In such cases, it is often recommended you consult your hiring team to evaluate the process at that stage.
Job Considerations that skip a stage or are inserted into your interview process at a late stage, such as going straight to onsite, will be counted as having passed all earlier stages. This is done to guarantee consistency of all numbers, otherwise funnels could have greater than 100% progress for a given stage.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-funnel-metrics#funnels-summarize-progress-not-activity)Funnels summarize progress, not activity
It is common for funnel reports to be mistaken for activity reports. As noted above, a funnel report is a combination of two things: a set of stages, and a cohort. The funnel report shows the summary outcome of many considerations over time, but it does not summarize when the activity at each stage took place.
A funnel report that shows 600 onsites is stating that 600 onsites took place amongst the Job Considerations selected in the cohort, but not when. You may be able to infer roughly in what period those onsites took place from the definition of your cohort, but it is recommended you use an interview activity report or stage transition report for summarizing activity in a specific timeframe.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-funnel-metrics#summary)Summary
Ashby's template funnel report examines Job Considerations that have completed their consideration by summarizing how far through the interview process they progressed. An initial selection is made by selecting a timeframe and any combination of filters, which results in an initial group of considerations, sometimes referred to as a cohort. The cohort is defined by your selection criteria, and the size is given by the initial bar in the funnel report. The subsequent progress from stage to stage is expressed as a percentage of the prior stage, highlighting where great ease or difficulty is found in your interview process.

# Email Deliverability Best Practices
14 min
Copy
This guide covers best practices for factors that influence your email deliverability, including sender reputation, email content, and compliance with email authentication standards.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#overview)Overview
Ashby integrates with your team’s Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 account to send emails directly from your inboxes. This means that your entire team has a hand in ensuring the deliverability of your emails. This guide covers best practices for factors that influence your email deliverability, including sender reputation, email content, and compliance with email authentication standards.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#what-is-email-deliverability)What is email deliverability?
Email deliverability refers to the ability of your emails to reach the intended recipients' inboxes without being marked as spam or rejected by email servers.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#what-influences-email-deliverability)What influences email deliverability?
There are many factors that influence email deliverability; in this guide, we focus on
Sender Reputation and Email Authentication. Other factors include:
  * Content Quality: The relevance, wording, and design of your email content, including avoidance of spam-like features.


  * Recipient Engagement: How recipients interact with your emails, such as opening rates and marking emails as spam.


  * Email List Health: Regular cleaning of your list to remove inactive or invalid addresses helps in maintaining a good sender reputation.


  * Compliance with Email Regulations: Adherence to laws like the CAN-SPAM Act, GDPR, and CASL is crucial for legal compliance and deliverability.


  * Frequency and Volume of Emails: Consistent and balanced email sending practices help in establishing a stable sender reputation.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#what-are-email-deliverability-best-practices)What are email deliverability best practices?
  * Regularly Update Records: Keep your SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records up to date, especially when changing email service providers or server configurations.


  * Monitor Email Deliverability: Regularly check if your emails are landing in inboxes and not spam folders. Tools like Google Postmaster can provide insights.


  * Stay Compliant with Email Standards: Ensure your email content and practices comply with standards like CAN-SPAM or GDPR, depending on your region.


  * Educate Your Team: Make sure everyone involved in email campaigns understands the importance of these authentication methods.


  * Seek Professional Help if Needed: If you're unsure about setting up these protocols correctly, consider consulting with IT professionals specializing in email systems.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#what-are-email-authentication-methods)What are email authentication methods?
Email authentication is a technical method used to verify that an email message is from the sender it claims to be from. This process is crucial for preventing email spoofing and phishing attacks, where attackers might forge emails to seem like they come from legitimate sources. Though there are many, the best practice methods include SPF, DKIM, and DMARC.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#what-are-email-authentication-best-practices)What are email authentication best practices?
There are well-established best practices that your IT Administrators are likely already familiar with. As of February 2024, Google is enforcing the following best practices. Failure to comply will result in deferred and eventually rejected emails.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#spf-sender-policy-framework)SPF (Sender Policy Framework):
This method allows the domain owner to specify which mail servers are permitted to send emails on behalf of their domain. When an email is received, the receiving server checks the SPF record in the DNS (Domain Name System) to verify if the email comes from an authorized server.
  * Note: SPF should be implemented by all senders


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#dkim-domainkeys-identified-mail)DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail):
DKIM adds a digital signature to each outgoing email message. This signature is linked to the domain and is verified against a public cryptographic key in the domain's DNS records. It ensures that the content of the email has not been tampered with in transit.
  * Note: SPF should be implemented by all senders


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#dmarc-domain-based-message-authentication-reportin)DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance):
DMARC is a policy framework that utilizes both SPF and DKIM. It allows domain owners to indicate that their emails are protected by SPF and DKIM, and tells receiving mail servers what to do if neither of those authentication methods passes – such as rejecting the email or flagging it as spam. DMARC also includes reporting capabilities, enabling domain owners to receive reports on how their emails are being handled.
  * Note: DMARC should be implemented by senders who send 5,000+ emails per day


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#how-do-i-implement-email-authentication-best-pract)How do I implement email authentication best practices?
Below are quick overviews of how to implement each of the best practices; check out the linked guides for more detailed information.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#how-to-implement-spf-sender-policy-framework)How to Implement SPF (Sender Policy Framework)
✉️ [Review Google’s detailed guide on how to set up SPF](https://support.google.com/a/answer/33786?sjid=13141639566626348837-EU)
  * Create an SPF Record: This is a TXT record in your domain's DNS settings. It lists all the mail servers authorized to send emails on behalf of your domain.


  * Publish the Record: Add this record to your domain's DNS settings.


  * An example SPF record looks like v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all.


  * Test the SPF Record: Use online tools to ensure your SPF record is valid and correctly implemented.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#how-to-implement-dkim-domainkeys-identified-mail)How to implement DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail)
✉️ [Review Google's detailed guide on how to set up DKIM](https://support.google.com/a/answer/174124?sjid=13141639566626348837-EU)
  * Generate a DKIM Key: This is typically done through your email service provider. It involves creating a public and private key pair.


  * Publish the Public Key: Add the public key to your domain's DNS settings as a TXT record. Your email service provider will use the private key to sign your emails.


  * Configure Email Server: Ensure your email server or service provider is configured to sign outgoing emails with the DKIM key.


  * Test DKIM Setup: Verify that your emails are being signed correctly using DKIM testing tools.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#how-to-implement-dmarc-domain-based-message-authen)How to implement DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting & Conformance)
﻿[Review Google’s detailed guide on how to add a DMARC record](https://support.google.com/a/answer/2466563?hl=en&ref_topic=2759254&sjid=13141639566626348837-EU "Review Google’s detailed guide on how to add a DMARC record")﻿
  * Create a DMARC Record: This is a TXT record in your domain's DNS that defines your DMARC policy. It uses SPF and DKIM to validate emails.


  * Publish the DMARC Record: Add this record to your domain's DNS settings.


  * An example DMARC record is v=DMARC1; p=reject; rua=mailto:report@yourdomain.com.


  * Monitor Reports: DMARC provides feedback on how your emails are processed by receivers, helping you identify and fix authentication issues


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#what-is-a-sender-reputation)What is a Sender Reputation?
Your company’s email sender reputation is a numerical score (0-100) assigned by Internet Service Providers (ISPs) to each organization that sends emails. This metric is a critical factor in determining your email deliverability.
A higher sender reputation score indicates to ISPs a higher level of trust in the legitimacy and quality of your email communications. Consequently, a high score increases the probability that your emails will be successfully delivered to the inboxes of recipients in the ISP's network. Maintaining a strong sender reputation is therefore essential for ensuring reliable and effective email delivery in a business context.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#how-do-we-check-our-sender-reputation)How do we check our Sender Reputation?
Many free tools can be used to check your sender reputation; we suggest one of the following:
  * ﻿[Google Postmaster Tools](https://www.gmail.com/postmaster/ "Google Postmaster Tools")﻿


  * ﻿[Learn and Test DMARC](https://www.learndmarc.com/ "Learn and Test DMARC")﻿


  * ﻿[Mail Tester](https://www.mail-tester.com/ "Mail Tester")﻿


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-deliverability-best-practices#why-did-ashby-send-an-email-about-email-deliverabi)**Why did Ashby send an email about email deliverability to our Google Workspace Admin?**
In an effort to further curtail spam, Google and other email service providers have begun enforcing the use of best-practice email authentication methods. Ashby reached out to ensure your team is ready for this change to ensure your team’s emails continue to be delivered successfully. [Read Google’s blog post](https://blog.google/products/gmail/gmail-security-authentication-spam-protection/ "Read Google’s blog post") for more information about this change.
﻿

# SSO and SCIM
13 min
Copy
We support SSO (SAML and OIDC connections), along with Directory SCIM through WorkOS. SSO can be configured on the Foundations, Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
SCIM can be configured for accounts on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#sso-and-scim-overview)SSO and SCIM Overview
Single Sign-On (SSO) and System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) are essential tools for modern businesses. Our platform integrates with WorkOS to provide seamless SSO and SCIM capabilities, ensuring secure access and efficient user management for your organization.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#getting-started)Getting Started
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#how-do-i-set-up-ssoscim-for-my-organization)**How do I set up SSO/SCIM for my organization?**
  1. Reach out to our support team at support@ashbyhq.com with the following details:


  1. The email address where you’d like any error messages or notifications directed (IT service account recommended)


  1. If you’d like to setup SSO, or both SSO and SCIM.


  1. Support will follow up, providing you with a setup link. Once you receive the link, follow the on-screen instructions to integrate your identity provider with our platform via WorkOS. The steps provided will be tailored to your identity provider.


  1. Test the integration to ensure everything is working correctly. Please confirm with support once you’ve completed the setup process.


For SSO, you can connect multiple domains, with different identity providers if needed. 
You can also set up multiple WorkOS connections for SCIM and users from the connected systems will flow into Ashby.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#what-attributes-are-pulled-via-scim)What attributes are pulled via SCIM?
Once you've configured SCIM on your company's account, you can determine the attributes that are synced over by heading to Admin > Integrations > SCIM. In the Fields to Sync section, use the toggles to confirm the information that will be synced from WorkOS.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/AFZRULsD0DpHcolyNBwpY_scim-fields-to-sync.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
The following fields can be synced:
  * Job Title


  * Name Updates


  * Cost Center


  * Department


  * Division


  * Groups


  * Manager


Job Title, Name and Manager are immediately useful for storing up-to-date information on all your employees without having to manually maintain it in Ashby. Cost Center, Department and Division can be useful pieces for further reporting on or grouping employees. 
These fields will be pulled over as Employee custom fields in Ashby and information synced to these fields will show in the Employment Info section of the employee's profile in Admin > Organization Setup > Employees.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/HYWv_8e8KhqnD6eGpKgHD_synced-employee-fields-on-the-employee-profile.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#pricing)Pricing
**How much does the SSO/SCIM service cost?**
  * SSO is included for Plus and Enterprise plans.


  * For those on the Foundations plan, SSO is a paid add on. It costs $100/per month, billed monthly or annually, depending on your plan.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#what-is-sso)What is SSO?
SSO (Single Sign-On) allows users to access multiple applications with a single set of credentials. This means you don't have to remember multiple passwords for different services.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#what-is-scim)W**hat is SCIM?**
SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) is a standard that automates the exchange of user identity information between identity domains or IT systems.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#which-identity-providers-do-you-support)Which identity providers do you support?
Through our integration with WorkOS, we support a wide range of identity providers, including but not limited to Google Workspace, Microsoft Azure AD, and Okta. See [WorkOS Integrations](https://workos.com/docs/integrations "WorkOS Integrations") for a full list of IDPs.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#do-you-support-jit)Do you support JIT?
We don’t currently support Just in Time provisioning, please submit a feature request to support@ashbyhq.com if you’re interested in this option!
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#i-have-users-in-ashby-that-are-not-in-my-directory)I **have users in Ashby that are not in my directory. If I set up SCIM, will those users still be active when the Directory Sync is enabled?**
No, any users that are in Ashby, but not in your directory will be deactivated in Ashby once the sync is enabled. Prior to Directory Sync being enabled, you should first confirm that the list of users in your directory is the full list of users that need to access Ashby.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#do-you-support-log-steams-or-domain-verification)Do you support log steams or domain verification?
We do not support log streams or domain verification.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#how-do-i-get-the-setup-link-for-ssoscim-integratio)How do I get the setup link for SSO/SCIM integration?
Please contact your Customer Success Manager or our Support Team (support@ashbyhq.com).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#is-the-ssoscim-integration-secure)Is the SSO/SCIM integration secure?
Absolutely! Our integration with WorkOS ensures that all data is encrypted and securely transmitted. We adhere to industry best practices to ensure the safety and privacy of your data. Learn more about our security practices [here](https://www.ashbyhq.com/resources/security "here").
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#im-facing-issues-with-the-ssoscim-setup-what-shoul)I'm facing issues with the SSO/SCIM setup. What should I do?
If you encounter any issues during the setup or have questions about the integration, please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com. We're here to help!
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#can-i-use-both-sso-and-scim-together)Can I use both SSO and SCIM together?
Yes, you can use both SSO and SCIM together. While SSO streamlines the login process, SCIM ensures efficient user management across platforms.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#can-permissions-be-mapped-with-scim)Can permissions be mapped with SCIM?
At this time, we only support provisioning users, not mapping permissions or access roles via SCIM. Users will be added, via Directory Sync, as Limited Access users; you may change their permissions level can be changed in their employee profile once the user account has been created within Ashby via the sync. For more on permission levels, check out [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim#once-configured-how-often-do-scim-syncs-happen)Once configured, how often do SCIM syncs happen?
SCIM syncs happen hourly. We also respond to web hooks.
﻿

meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Advanced Setup
# Meeting Rooms & Meeting Room Pools
7 min
Copy
Just like with interviewers, you can sync over and create a pool of meeting rooms. You can assign a pool of meeting rooms to an interview activity so one room is assigned from a selection when an interview is scheduled.
This is a helpful tool if you have multiple rooms that are suitable for interviewing in and you don’t want to assign just one room to interviews.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#setting-up-and-syncing-meeting-rooms-from-google-w)Setting up and syncing meeting rooms from Google Workspace
Meeting Rooms that have been set up as resources within the Google Admin console will sync over to Ashby and can be assigned to interviews, schedule interview activities and meeting room pools.
To add a meeting room as a resource in Google Workspace:
  * Sign into the Google Admin console (you must have an administrator account)


  * Navigate to Menu > Directory > Buildings & Resources > Overview


  * In Resource Management, click Open _._ Click the yellow + button to add a new resource


  * Enter the resource information, then click Add Resources


For other options, including uploading resources in bulk, check out Google’s documentation [here](https://support.google.com/a/answer/1033925?hl=en#zippy= "here").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#setting-up-and-syncing-meeting-rooms-from-microsof)Setting up and syncing meeting rooms from Microsoft 365
You can set up room and equipment mailboxes in Microsoft 365 - these will then sync over to Ashby to be used as part of scheduling. More on setting up these mailboxes can be found in Microsoft’s documentation [here](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/admin/manage/room-and-equipment-mailboxes?view=o365-worldwide#set-up-room-and-equipment-mailboxes "here").
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#creating-a-meeting-room-pool)Creating a meeting room pool
To create a meeting room pool, navigate to Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > [Meeting Room Pools](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/meeting-room-tags "Meeting Room Pools"). Click + New to create a new pool. You’ll be prompted to add in a name for your pool.
Once your pool has been created, click on it within the list and then click + Add Meeting Room to add the a meeting room to your pool.
Select a meeting room, then click Submit to add it to the pool. Repeat this until you have all of the appropriate rooms added to your pool.
To remove a room from your pool, click Actions beside the room on your list, then Remove from Pool.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0eb8ce9bad4c372155ee93c1d405d7f5.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c4deb39890a4cc055b0da5a8cc4c6670.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b07c4c740ba098b0729857d503ab603f.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c8e22b63317341964b3819b9150c4d87.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#assigning-a-meeting-room-pool-to-a-schedule-interv)Assigning a meeting room pool to a schedule interview activity.
For more on setting up schedule interview activities, please check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿
To add your meeting room pool to your schedule interview activity, head into the Interview Plan on your job and click on the schedule interview activity in question to amend it.
On the Schedule tab, scroll to the _Room_ section and click Set default room _._ This will set the default room/rooms used for this particular activity.
From here, use the first drop-down menu to select Meeting Rooms from Pool, then select your chosen pool using the second drop-down menu.
Now when scheduling this particular interview, a room will be selected from our pool, rather than a specific room being used every time.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3b62a6315dba701b247b3bc345ff81d4.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bdf4ec0dfc9583d8e1606fb772dbbc50.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#where-are-meeting-rooms-pulled-from)Where are meeting rooms pulled from?
Meeting Rooms are synced over via your Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 integration. Please note that it can take up to 4 hours for rooms to sync over depending on how many meetings room are being synced.
Any meeting rooms listed under All Rooms and Resources when you click Add Rooms for an event on your Google Calendar should show as room options in Ashby.
In Microsoft 365, meeting rooms can be seen on the Rooms & Equipment page within the Microsoft 365 admin center.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bb83b0ac89f3134bdb2b13225439de9f.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#can-i-pick-a-room-from-a-meeting-room-pool-when-ma)Can I pick a room from a meeting room pool when manually scheduling interviews?
You can, yes. When using the calendar interface to select the interview type, interviewers and date and time of the interview, click the Add Room By Pool option. You can then select the pool you’d like to choose rooms from.
Once you’ve selected your chosen pool (or pools) via the drop-down menu, click Choose Meeting Room to see the rooms in your selected pool/pools.
Review the meeting rooms available within your pool, then click Select Meeting Room to confirm the meeting room you’d like to add to your interview.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/091807c6de5bc7aa526fc09cec14e3fd.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e343f4ce47b2bc4b40836589103830c1.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/24dd90ffd1d57917fa4fe0ad91d1ac62.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools#can-i-schedule-multiple-meeting-rooms-for-a-single)Can I schedule multiple meeting rooms for a single interview event?
This isn’t currently possible. Only one meeting room can be assigned when scheduling the interview.
﻿

candidate-search:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Candidates
# Candidate Search
22 min
Copy
This guide covers how to search for candidates, the filters available and how to save regularly used candidate searches.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#overview)Overview
﻿
See the chart below for more information on the permissions needed to view candidate profiles.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ✅*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Elevated Access users will have access to these for the roles and departments they have been given the access role for. Agency users will only be able to view the candidate profiles that have their agency listed as the source.
Ashby’s Candidate search option will allow you to specify filters and pull a list of candidates that meet that criteria, then take bulk action on them if needed. You can also easily save and pin a candidate search to your home page (like any report) so you can access it quickly in the future.
You can create a candidate search by heading to Candidates _,_ then Search in the top navigation bar.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7623374254003456de666d0b9cbc35a3.png)
﻿
You can also create a candidate search by heading to Reports and Build. Candidate searches are listed as a custom type of report here.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/dca6af2be64119ce0dd2d916d44d0ccc.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#basic-filters)Basic Filters
When it comes to setting the criteria for your candidate search, Ashby has basic filters. These are the most commonly used filters. Basic filters are enabled when the Advanced toggle is switched off.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/270778e543bbfad658a623c1259d7e50.png)
﻿
  1. **Advanced** This toggle allows you to switch between basic and advanced filters.


  1. **+ Filter** Click this to open up a list of commonly used filters. These include a full text search of the candidate’s resume, location, current interview stage, tags added to the candidate profile and projects they’ve been added to.


  1. **Job is…** Click this to select a job to filter by. All candidates considered for the job or roles selected will show in the search results.


  1. **Department is…** Click this to select a department to filter candidates by. All candidates considered for a job/jobs in that department will show in the search results.


  1. **Candidate Source is…** Click this to select a source to filter candidates by. All candidates that have this source applied to their profile will show in the search.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#advanced-filters)Advanced Filters
You can switch on the Advanced toggle to search through a wider range of filters. When turning on this toggle, you will see an option to + Add Field to Match instead. Click this to search through the full range of filters available. Examples of advanced filters include filtering by a value added to a custom field on the candidate’s profile or by the answer to a question on an application form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a9b5390b45e69fc4a90c1a36f03d3f20.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#saving-and-pinning-a-candidate-search)Saving and Pinning a Candidate Search
When you click Save on a candidate search, it will be added to your Saved Reports.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/86e7edd5887467a353cfc1351de14f51.png)
﻿
You’ll be asked to title your candidate search and also determine whether other users can access it or not using the Access Control toggle.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a3379f52223473512a7a68173e49dc7b.png)
﻿
Once a report is saved, you can pin it to your home page by clicking the + Add button under Views on the left-hand side.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cec67dd0daf14b71b6b2284f43768d3d.png)
﻿
Then search for and select your candidate search to pin it. You’ll be able to then click on the candidate search to pull it up immediately.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9610e63117f3e526a1eb337ccab8a59e.png)
﻿
To remove a pinned report, click Edit and then click the trashcan icon next to the report you’d like to remove. Click Done to confirm the changes.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/aefde349d5e0f2aec2aba78b001e407c.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#save-as-new)Save as ‘New’
You can also save a specific set of filtering criteria as a filter tab on the Candidate Search page. Once you’ve set up your filters, click the Save as ‘New’ tab option. You’ll be asked to name this filter. This can be particularly helpful when you’re looking to jump between multiple candidate searches.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/18d851d371c6acc61cf5a602430ad482.png)
﻿
Once named, this will show as an additional filtering option beside the My Candidates tab.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2f329b7bf0288dc26eec8bc19a9a4b3b.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#my-candidates)My Candidates
Along with the New Filter tab and saving new filters, you can also use the My Candidates tab. This will pull all candidates that you are following, or candidates who are being considered for a role you are on the hiring team for.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5a09b45b0ce7081fcbfb5befd10d0d45.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#ai-assistant-filter-for-candidate-search)AI Assistant Filter for Candidate Search
If you would like to opt-in for this feature, head to Admin >Organization Setup > Opt-In Features within Admin. Both Ashby All-In-One and Ashby Analytics customers can opt in to use this feature.
You can use the AI Assistant filter option to automatically generate a filter for your candidate search.
To get started, click on the AI Assistant option and start typing the filter you’re looking to set up. 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/eb9e20f291b5348e49b5fbc1bfedefa8.png)
﻿
The assistant will then add filters based on your prompt when you click Generate Filter.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c7d76ada56e2ded1ec092faae0603dad.png)
﻿
If your filters look good, you can click the 👍 icon to provide your feedback. If the filters don’t match what you were expecting, you can leave feedback by hitting the 👎 icon.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5076e8d4687003ca46d6855ac382d7da.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#taking-bulk-actions-on-candidates)Taking Bulk Actions on Candidates
Once you’ve created a list of candidates that meet your criteria, you can then take action on them in bulk (for example, sending a mass email). To do this, check the checkbox beside each candidate you’d like to take this action on.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/10b69f53ee5c96f62b6b8260a42a0d5b.png)
﻿
To select all candidates on the list, check the checkbox within the list’s header to select all candidates on the list.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ed114d06d5a237f65086df3f0f037835.png)
﻿
Once candidates have been selected, the buttons relating to the actions you can take will appear. 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/35f6e43ac6c7b1d02794eba2bf2c663d.png)
﻿
You can take the following actions in bulk on your selected candidates:
  * Consider for Job


  * Add to Project


  * Archive


  * Email (emailing the selected candidates in bulk)


  * Enroll in Sequence


  * Transfer - more on transferring candidates at [Can I transfer a candidate’s job consideration to another job or interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan)﻿﻿


  * Set Tags


  * Anonymize (more on this at [Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates)﻿)


  * Delete (more on this at [Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/anonymizing-and-deleting-candidates)﻿)


  * Remove from Project


  * Mark as Do Not Contact (more on this at [Can I mark a candidate as Do Not Contact?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-mark-a-candidate-as-do-not-contact)﻿)


  * Remove Do Not Contact status 


  * Send Data Consent Request (more on this at [Data Privacy and Compliance](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/data-privacy-and-compliance)﻿)


It is not possible to change the source on a candidate in bulk via this route. To make amendments to candidate sources in bulk, you can opt to export, update and then import a CSV file of candidate data to make updates. More on this at [Bulk Import Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options#importing-candidates)﻿.
Once you have determined the candidate records and the action to be taken on them, you will then be taken to the bulk action wizard to confirm the bulk action.
In the Selection section, you’ll be able to see the criteria you’ve set for this bulk action and the number of records that match this criteria. Be sure to take time to review this information.
If the action is destructive (for example, deleting candidates), you will be asked to confirm that you are aware the action is destructive by checking the I confirm that this action is destructive checkbox.
Once you’ve reviewed the bulk action and the records that the action will be taken on, click the Start X on X records button to continue.
If you are performing a bulk action on a small set of records, you will be asked to confirm the bulk action to be taken, along with the number of records the action is to be taken.
If the action is being taken on a large number of records, you will be prompted to type the action into the specified field before the action across the chosen records can be performed. The number of records impacted will also be listed in the prompt window.
Once you’ve typed the action into the field, click Perform to perform the bulk action across your specified records.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3bbcdf2fae657f2ea5afd0db337b897e.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5f5697a4e267539e5d6ddde28343fcaf.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/93f39730978d3180c46005b4422a6ed4.png)
﻿
The bulk action type and the number of records included in the bulk action determines the number of confirmation prompts you’ll receive:
Deleting or Anonymizing Candidates Up to 24 candidates: Inline prompt. 24+ candidates: Confirmation window with typing prompt.
Archiving, Marking as Do Not Contact or Sending a Survey Up to 24 candidates: Inline prompt. 24+ candidates: Confirmation window with typing prompt.
With any other bulk actions, if you are taking the action on 24 candidates or more, you will see the inline prompt to confirm your bulk action before it is performed.
If you opt to close out the bulk action wizard window at the point where the bulk actions are being process, you will be able to see the progress in a minimized version of the wizard.
To open the wizard back up, click the Restore bulk action pullout button.
Once the bulk action has been performed, you will be able to view a log of all the changes on the confirmation page. Click Close to close out the bulk action wizard when you’re done.
﻿  

﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e56c241e10064ba280b9c69408341d99.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9074248ae416cc8615e70e829bc20c82.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#boolean-search)Boolean Search
You can use the Full Text Resume Search filter option to search for specific terms and pull candidates based on the text in their resume.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c57eaad9341436f8138c4b7d74f11de6.png)
﻿
You can type your chosen term into the provided field and select from the following options from the drop-down to further build out your search filters.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d67326ec24d3ab31e7887530ccd68c20.png)
﻿  
| Boolean Search Option  | Description  | Example  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Matches (supports the boolean functions OR, NOT)  | A case insensitive exact match of all the individial words, regardless of order. Use this when the resume needs to mention multiple exact keywords but order does not matter  | Searching javascript coder brings resumes containing both javascript and coder anywhere  |  
| Contains (supports the boolean functions AND, OR, NOT)  | Returns records that contain the exact _phrase_ , in the exact order. Not case-sensitive.  | Searching javascript coder brings resumes containing javascript coder and Javascript Coder as a phrase, but not javascript code  |  
| Equals (supports the boolean functions OR, NOT)  | Returns records that contain the exactphrase with exact capitalization.  | Searching javascript coder brings resumes containing javascript coder as a phrase, but not Javascript Coder  |  
| Similar (supports the boolean functions AND, OR, NOT)  | Returns records with variations of the word. Not case-sensitive. Good for partial matches or different word forms.  | Searching localization _may_ bring resumes containing localized or localize  |  
**Boolean Operators**
Boolean Operators help you connect multiple terms as part of your search. Below you can find a list of commonly used operators for reference.  
| Boolean Operator  | Description  | Example  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| OR (use any of |, ,, OR, or,,)  | OR allows you to search for multiple terms and have results that match one or multiple terms searched  |  contractor, freelance, or consultant A candidate will be returned if the resume contains any of the terms listed above  |  
| AND (use any of &, +, :, AND, and, )  | AND requires both terms (or all terms following AND) to be included in the results  |  python & react & nodejs Results will be returned for candidates who have all three terms in their resume. • software engineer The search finds instances containing the words software and engineer in any order, together or separately  |  
|  NOT (use any of !, -) Using the text NOT/not will not work for this operation  | NOT excludes records with the specified terms from being included in the search result  |  • !remote Excludes records with remote included in the resume  |  
| WORDS (combine multiple words to match the term exactly by wrapping in " ")  | Combine multiple words to match the desired search term exactly. This helps to ensure results contain a full phrase, rather than single words from the term.  |  • "software engineer" The quotation marks ensure that results contain _software engineer_ , rather than only _software_ or  _engineer_  |  
﻿  

﻿  

**Boolean Modifiers**
Boolean Modifiers allow you to create searches that include the stem of words or searches with sub queries.  
| PREFIX (use *)  | Searches for results where the term is included as part of a word in the candidate’s resume  |  • *script Matches javascript, typescript, etc.  *end Matches frontend, backend, etc.  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| GROUPING (use either ( ) or [ ] )  | GROUPING allows you to form sub queries to pull results that meet criteria from multiple groups  |  • (remote or hybrid) AND  [backend and python] will pull results for candidates who have either remote or hybrid included in their resume AND backend and python.  |  
﻿  

﻿  

**More advanced Boolean Search examples**
Below are some search examples with clarification in natural language as to the results expected.
﻿  
  
| Boolean Search  | Search Clarification  |  
| --- | --- |  
| "senior software engineer" AND (python OR java) AND (remote OR hybrid) AND !(junior OR intern OR contractor)  | Find resumes that contain "senior software engineer" AND mention either "python" OR "java" AND indicate they've held either "remote" OR "hybrid" roles AND exclude any resumes that mention "junior" OR "intern" OR "contractor" roles  |  
| `"full stack developer" AND react AND nodejs AND !remote AND !(freelance contractor)'  | Find resumes that contain "full stack developer" AND mention "react" AND mention "nodejs" AND do NOT mention "remote" work AND exclude any that mention "freelance" OR "contractor" roles   |  
| (frontend & react) | (backend & python) & *cloud & -junior  | Find resumes that contain either (both "frontend" AND "react") OR (both "backend" AND "python") AND mention any cloud technology (like "AWS cloud", "cloud computing", "cloud architecture") AND exclude any that mention "junior" roles  |  
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#can-i-filter-for-candidates-who-meet-a-specific-cr)Can I filter for candidates who meet a specific criteria from within a job?
If you’d like to filter candidates within the Candidate Pipeline section of a job, you can click + Add Field to Match to add filters and specify your criteria. The candidates who meet that criteria will then be displayed in your pipeline.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2bb3a47b0098934de79d10be0786b445.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#what-is-the-difference-between-application-review)What is the difference between Application Review Average Score and Average Overall Recommendation?
The Application Review Average Score is the average score given by all users in application review. If an application passes through application review multiple times, this will consider all scores.
The Average Overall Recommendation filter considers scores given across all interviews for the job consideration.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#can-i-search-for-terms-or-keywords-used-in-notes-a)Can I search for terms or keywords used in notes added to the candidate profile?
No, it’s currently not possible to search for candidates based on content within notes on their profile.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#can-i-bulk-schedule-interviews-for-multiple-candid)Can I bulk schedule interviews for multiple candidates at once?
This isn’t currently possible. If you’re looking for a way to streamline your scheduling process, check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search#ive-added-a-candidate-searchcandidate-list-report)I've added a candidate search/candidate list report to my dashboard and just see a number, rather than a list, what can I do?
Please note that if you add a candidate list to a dashboard, the candidate count is displayed, rather than the full candidate list. If you would like to display a customized table on your dashboard, you can create a custom list report on the candidate subject (or any subject) and select the desired columns to be displayed. More on custom list reports at [Custom Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#custom-list)﻿.
﻿  

﻿
