# How do I export candidate data?
5 min
Copy
This document explains how to export candidate data from Ashby.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-export-candidate-data#exporting-data-via-a-custom-list-report)Exporting Data via a Custom List Report
For more information on creating custom reports, check out [Custom Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports)﻿﻿
Custom Reports can be set up on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
﻿
If you’d like to download a list of candidates that meet a certain criteria, you can do the following:
  1. Navigate to Reports > Build > Custom List.


  1. Select the Candidates subject, and add the relevant Fields to Show (Name, Email Address, LinkedIn URL, etc).


  1. Add filters to the report if necessary, e.g. Started At time range, Job, Source, etc.


  1. Click Export CSV in the Results section.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-export-candidate-data#exporting-data-via-the-candidate-profile)Exporting Data via the Candidate Profile
You can also export candidate data from an individual candidate’s profile under More _._ This option can be used if a candidate requests a copy of their data.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a1b31a1c4c5cfe04dc9747c54973c2f6.png)
﻿
Once you click Export Candidate Data, you’ll then be able to select whether you’d like to export the candidate’s data, or their profile and interview feedback.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/57615154a7c05910bc46733bf8f74170.png)
﻿
If you opt to export their data, you can use the checkboxes to select the specific fields you’d like to export.
You can select whether you’d like to export the candidate profile information, social links, employment and education history, emails and notes.
You can opt to export emails and notes that have been marked as private too. Once you’ve selected everything you’d like to export, click Download PDF _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a01b7c2ea723554822bb2440127bab90.png)
﻿
If you opt to export candidate feedback, you will also have the option to include the AI feedback summary for the candidate and private feedback answers in the exported file.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ed1e1a640ff8d8113251293ecf0719c1.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-export-candidate-data#can-i-bulk-export-resumes)Can I bulk export resumes?
Currently it’s not possible to bulk export resumes in-app. You can export resumes on a candidate-by-candidate basis by clicking on the resume’s file name on the Summary tab of the candidate’s profile, then downloading the file from there. If you do need to export resumes in bulk, please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com for assistance.

# Alerts Library
3 min
Copy
This document provides common Alert examples and set-up recommendations.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#introduction)Introduction
Alerts was designed to allow customized and timely delivery of data that matters to the recipients who matter. We offer a way to automate the delivery of this data with our difference detection and dynamic routing. A single Alert alleviates the need to create many customized report delivery schedules.
Learn more about alerts at [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿
This library provides common Alert examples and recommendations for setting up your Custom List reports and Alert automations.
Report data of an Alert gets generated on behalf of the user who creates the Alert, so all of the creator’s accounts permissions apply.
If the Entire List is sent to a specified user and not a User involved as (more on this at [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿), then the user receiving the report will be able to see all of the report data in the Alert email. Be sure to add filters to remove any jobs (including confidential jobs) or specific data that you don’t want users receiving the alert to see as part of your Alert setup process.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts-library#surveys-feedback)Surveys & Feedback

billing-faq:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Billing & Plans
# Billing FAQ
16 min
Copy
Welcome to the Billing FAQ section for Ashby! Here, we've compiled answers to the most commonly asked questions about billing. If you can't find the answer you're looking for, please reach out to our support team for assistance.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#general-information)General Information
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-can-i-update-my-billing-information)How can I update my billing information?
If you have access to [Billing](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/billing "Billing") on your Ashby account, you can amend your billing information and payment settings in the Settings tab.
If you see a message that your billing settings are managed outside of Ashby, please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com to make changes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#what-payment-methods-do-you-accept)What payment methods do you accept?
We accept most major credit cards, including Visa, Mastercard, and American Express. For plans over a certain amount, we support invoicing. Please contact support@ashbyhq.com for further details.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#when-will-i-be-billed-for-my-subscription)When will I be billed for my subscription?
You will be billed for your subscription on a monthly or yearly basis, depending on the specific plan you have selected. Your billing date will typically be the same day of the month that you originally signed up for the subscription.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#what-happens-if-my-payment-fails)What happens if my payment fails?
If your payment fails for any reason, we will attempt to contact the billing email on file for an alternative payment method. If the payment continues to fail or we cannot reach you to settle your outstanding balance, your subscription may be suspended until the balance is paid.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-do-i-pay-a-past-due-invoice)How do I pay a past due invoice?
If you have an overdue invoice, you will see a red dot beside Admin on the navigation bar. On the Admin page, you’ll see a banner saying you have an overdue invoice.
You can click Go to Invoices to then see the overdue invoice. An overdue invoice will have a red Pay Now button beside it, next to the option to download the invoice.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a8f6f7950bfee1b329b2a5c6b2c1b22f.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-do-i-download-an-invoice)How do I download an invoice?
In [Billing](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/billing "Billing"), head to the Invoices tab. From here, you should see a list of invoices with a Download button next to each.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fdb025ec9b84aa7ea42f2abe3f1b10f0.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#i-would-like-to-switch-my-payment-method-to-invoic)I would like to switch my payment method to invoicing.
Invoicing is currently only available to customers on an annual plan. We require monthly plan customers to have a credit card on file that can be processed on their monthly billing date. For any additional billing or payment support, please reach out to billings@ashbyhq.com.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#whats-your-refund-policy)What's your refund policy?
We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee for month-to-month plans. If you're not satisfied with our service within the first 30 days, contact support@ashbyhq.com, and we'll process a full refund.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-can-i-request-access-to-the-billing-section-of)How can I request access to the Billing section of Ashby?
You can request access by reaching out to any of the listed Billing Users on the Billing page [here](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/billing "here"). If there are no Billing Users listed, please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com to be added. Please note as a Billing User, you will be the point-of-contact with Ashby's Billing Team for any billing-related correspondence.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-do-i-assign-a-user-access-to-the-billing-secti)How do I assign a user access to the Billing section of Ashby?
Only an existing Billing user can add a new Billing user. You can either give a user access to Billing by amending their permissions to include access to Billing on the [Employees](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/users "Employees") page, or by adding them as a Billing user within Billing _._
The option to add additional users to the list of Billing Users can be found within the Settings tab.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4be81041e4fafb8fbe789382a92baf53.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-do-i-update-my-billing-address)How do I update my billing address?
If you have access to [Billing](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/billing "Billing") on your Ashby account, you can amend your billing address within your billing information. Clicking the Edit in Stripe link next to Billing Information to be taken to a new tab where you can amend your billing information.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#what-is-the-billing-email-listed-under-billing-set)What is the Billing Email listed under Billing > Settings > Billing Information and how is that different from the "Billing User"?
We manage our customer billing and process payment with Stripe which requires a billing email. This email will be the recipient of automated email notification from Stripe, like invoices, receipts, or failed payment notifications. The Billing User can view and manage all billing settings and invoices in Ashby but will not receive these automated Stripe emails. However, should there be any billing issues, our Billing team may reach out to any Billing User(s) to help resolve. Any Billing User can update the Stripe Billing Email.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#subscription-cancellation)Subscription Cancellation
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-do-i-cancel-or-pause-my-ashby-subscription)How do I cancel or pause my Ashby subscription?
If you are on annual billing, or are month-to-month but have a dedicated CSM and you’d like to cancel your Ashby subscription, please reach out to contracts@ashbyhq.com or your contract manager for assistance. Please note that once you cancel, you’ll still have access to your subscription until the end of the current billing cycle.
If you are on a month-to-month billing and do not have a dedicated CSM, you have the ability to cancel your subscription in-app. To do this:
  * Navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > Billing.


  * Click Manage Subscription.


  * Confirm your reason for cancelling - you will also have the option to add in any feedback.


  * After confirming your cancellation reason, you will be provided with an option to submit a request to export your data. This request will be sent to the Support team for processing.


If you request a copy of your data as part of the cancellation process, please note that the export is not an instant, in-app download. Once your request is submitted, our team will process it manually in accordance with our data policies.You’ll receive an email notification with a secure link to access your export once it’s ready.
  * You will then have the option to permanently delete all of your account’s data after it is deactivated. Please note that this action is irreversible.


  * Confirm your cancellation request. Your request will then be sent to the team for processing. You will still have full access until the end of your billing cycle.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#ive-submitted-a-cancellation-request-but-i-want-to)I'**ve submitted a cancellation request but I want to reverse it - is this possible in-app?**
It is! Once you’ve submitted your cancellation request, you can reverse it by navigating back to Admin > Organization Setup > Billingand then clicking the Reactivate your subscriptionlink in the red banner listing your deactivation date. You can then review your next charge date and confirm the reactivation accordingly. 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#can-i-get-a-refund-if-i-cancel-my-subscription)Can I get a refund if I cancel my subscription?
We offer a 30-day money back guarantee for new subscribers. After this period, we do not offer full or partial refunds for canceled subscriptions. However, if you cancel your subscription before the next billing cycle begins, you will not be charged for the upcoming period.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#my-account-has-been-suspended-for-billing-issues-w)My account has been suspended for billing issues, what do I do?
In order to re-activate your service, any overdue invoices will need to be paid. You can view and pay any overdue invoices on the _Invoices_ tab in [Billing](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/billing "Billing"). Once paid, the Billing team will reach out to confirm your account has been reactivated.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#other)Other
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#can-i-switch-to-an-annual-subscription)Can I switch to an annual subscription?
Yes! Please get in touch with support@ashbyhq.com, and you’ll hear back from our Contracts Management Team within 2-3 business days.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/billing-faq#how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups)How do I purchase additional email lookups?
For more on purchasing email lookups in-app, check out [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿

# Job Dashboard
22 min
Copy
This document provides an overview of the Job Dashboard you can find in each Job’s Activity section. This dashboard provides data on a specific role. For key metrics across all jobs, check out the job dashboard within [Core Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/core-dashboards)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#introduction)Introduction
Ashby comes with powerful reporting functionality and pre-built templates, but we aim to decrease the time to value for bringing immediate insights to the most common reporting needs per job. The Job Dashboard offers a great starting point, and custom reports, templates, and dashboard building are available if more refinement is required.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#overview)Overview
﻿
  * The Job Dashboard can be found on each Job’s page under the Activity section. The video below explains how to navigate to a Job Dashboard and provides an overview of its sections.


  * The Dashboard contains three sections: [Summary](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#summary)﻿, [Interviews & Feedback](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#interviews-feedback)﻿, and [Source Details](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#source-details)﻿, and detailed information about the reports in each section can be found below.


  * You can find more information about a report by clicking on the “i” icon next to each report title.


  * The reports on the Job Dashboard are examples to help guide your organization’s reporting and are designed with further customization in mind.


  * By clicking Go to Report, you can see the underlying report and its Subjects, Filters, and Group By selections.


![Screen Cast 2023-03-22 at 9.14.30 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/rR8wzkhspfTgKdUal5u9d_screencast2023-03-22at91430am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#summary)Summary
This section contains reports regarding: overall job activity, interviews, applications and passthrough rate information.
**Total Candidates**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * This number represents the top of funnel count of job considerations for this job, excluding leads that do not convert.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * This number can be used as a quick reference to check expectations against other downstream activities. Interpretations may vary depending on your sourcing strategy.


![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.21.48 AM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/B_nsC_ZfIi6howhLn6TW4_screenshot2023-03-10at72148am.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Active Leads**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * A count of job consideration currently in this job’s lead stages.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A high number can reflect heaving sourcing activity or that some leads are getting delayed in process.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.22.52 AM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/0WqVqVNtPvQbV2dDkLE65_screenshot2023-03-10at72252am.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Active Candidates**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * All job considerations in active interview or offer stages. Excludes leads and candidates in application review.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A high number can reflect either a large amount of current activity, or that some candidates are getting delayed in process.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.23.00 AM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/GMi7gzpTxn0bXp3zEv1tG_screenshot2023-03-10at72300am.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Candidate to Offer Ratio**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * A ratio formed by comparing the number of job considerations at the top of funnel to the number with an offer extended.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A results of “100:1” suggests you need approximately 100 candidates at the top of funnel to result in 1 offer being extended.


  * Learn more about ratio reports in our [Ratio Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ratio-reports)﻿ documentation.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-21 at 5.08.48 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BH-d7NR9boXVZthcRbIwy_screenshot2023-03-21at50848pm.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Hires**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * The total number of job considerations hired on this job.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * The count should reflect the total hires made on this job. Pending your permissions, you may not have visibility into hired candidate profiles.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-21 at 5.10.20 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/8WheDWYQxUQybPbVicT_5_screenshot2023-03-21at51020pm.png)
﻿
﻿  

**﻿  
**
**Overall Funnel**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * This is a general funnel, the total starting candidate pool is shown along with the passthrough rate from the prior stage.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * Depending on the stage, both low and high passthrough rates can indicate an issue. A low passthrough rate may indicate a stage is too challenging or candidates that are not a good fit are being brought into the process. Conversely, a high passthrough rate can suggest a stage may not be an effective contribution to the overall process.


  * Learn more about Funnel Reports in [Understanding Funnel Metrics](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-funnel-metrics)﻿.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.07.53 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/IYhTLtdkI2AdSZznBmy4b_screenshot2023-03-22at70753am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**Interviews Week over Week**
  * **What’s in the report?**


  * The number of interviews, week over week, grouped by the stage name they were scheduled in.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * Use this report to see your overall volume of interviews and where in your process they are occurring.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.08.17 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kHqSwM6joD59pJeALYPU2_screenshot2023-03-22at70817am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**New Candidates by Source Type**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * Count of job considerations by start date grouped by source type. Leads are excluded from this report.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * You can use this report to examine source type trends over time and better understand the underlying origin of candidates.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.09.22 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RMAR2US1KAr-JhBVVm4Uk_screenshot2023-03-22at70922am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Pipeline History**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * The count of job considerations that were present in a stage in a given week.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * This report addresses the question of how many candidates had a job consideration in a stage at a historical point in time. A large count indicates many candidates were in that stage at that point in time, a sign of progress or possible delays in your process.


  * Learn more about pipeline history reports in our [Pipeline History](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard "Pipeline History") documentation.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.09.53 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/t91GEplTZni3Kk6HOKIFJ_screenshot2023-03-22at70953am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**Weekly Candidate Movement From Stage Type**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * This report counts stage transitions from the current stage type. Each movement of a job consideration from one stage to another counts as a single stage transition.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A high count in “Application Review” indicates many candidates are being moved through the application review process. Similarly, counts in the other stages types (”Lead” and “Active”) indicate candidates are being moved through the associated stages.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.10.32 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BEU92yEZ5Qtnl3qtUU2_1_screenshot2023-03-22at71032am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Candidate Stage Changes, Last 14 Days**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * Details for any candidate that has had a stage transition in the last 14 days. Activity in lead and application review stages is not included in this report.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * The report provides a quick reference to candidates that have recently been moved forward or archived. Few results indicates low overall activity in your active interview stages for this job.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.30.59 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/6vH6hbIwyaM2NQzmMxOoT_screenshot2023-03-10at73059am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Passthrough Rate Details**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Passthrough rates grouped by stage outcome. For every stage, active counts are displayed on the left, and processed counts are displayed on the right.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * This report allows you to see your overall passthrough rates per stage, but also highlights whether candidates are skipping, passing through, or being archived (by archive reason type).


  * Learn more about passthrough reports in our [Passthrough Report Features & FAQ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard "Passthrough Report Features & FAQ") documentation.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.31.15 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/nS4hEynP5h8zeO9IoZjvn_screenshot2023-03-10at73115am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Time in Process**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * The median days in process per stage. This is calculated per stage based on job consideration stage entry and stage exit times.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * You can use this report to check whether candidates are spending an appropriate amount of time per stage and identify areas in need of process improvement.


Learn more about Time in Process reports work in [our documentation.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates#time-in-process)﻿﻿
﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.11.00 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/OI3cmJjIXHugYopxYfRG4_screenshot2023-03-22at71100am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Archive Reasons by Stage & Type**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * A count of job considerations archived by their furthest stage name reached. The results are grouped by the archive reason type and archive reason details.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * This report aims to help assess at what stage and for what reasons candidates are being archived. Pending results, you may modify either top of funnel or downstream processes to improve overall candidate experience and hiring results.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.43.11 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/B_YQLD8wZkbE1SwJ0HYa3_screenshot2023-03-10at74311am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Source Type of Candidates**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Count of job considerations by source type. Leads that do not convert are excluded from this report.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * This report shows the total composition by source type at the top of funnel and can be used to see where candidates are originating from.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.31.52 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yT2LRED-1bqRthFoFPG1T_screenshot2023-03-10at73152am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#interviews-feedback)Interviews & Feedback
This section contains reports regarding: interview history, upcoming interviews, and interview load and participation. Feedback details on completion rates, score distribution, and calibration per interviewer and interview are also provided.
**Interviews Week over Week**
  * **What’s in the report?**


  * The number of interviews, week over week, grouped by the stage name they were scheduled in.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * Use this report to see your overall volume of interviews and where in your process they are occurring.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.08.17 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kHqSwM6joD59pJeALYPU2_screenshot2023-03-22at70817am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Upcoming Interviews, Next 14 Days**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * The number of scheduled interviews in the next 14 days grouped by the stage name they are scheduled in.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * Use this report to quickly assess whether this job has interviews in the next two-week period.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-22 at 7.15.38 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/KhLQ2s22R5pgcxPnGuHa9_screenshot2023-03-22at71538am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**﻿  
**
**Interviews per Hire Summary**
  * **What’s in this report?** This report shows how many interviews your team is conducting per hire. It’s calculated by taking the total number of interviews divided by the number of hires made. The total number of interviews includes interviews for candidates both hired and rejected.


  * Learn more about Interviews per Hire reports [in our documentation.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates)﻿﻿


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.57.11 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/7-aWpmymzQxX6ea1iua1g_screenshot2023-03-10at75711am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Interview Load by Interviewer**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * This report how many hours interviewers on your team have spent interviewing.


  * Learn more about Interview Load reports [in our documentation.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates#interview-load)﻿﻿


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.49.10 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/mW-iG_7mmIEKCC7Svy9la_screenshot2023-03-10at74910am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Interview Load by Stage**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * This report shows how many hours are spent interviewing per interview stage.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.49.25 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/cMXxyQFyq_Go9Z0yWOVcq_screenshot2023-03-10at74925am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**﻿  
**
**Feedback Completion by Interviewer**
  * **What’s in this report?**


  * This report shows which % of feedback forms have been completed by each interviewer and how long on average it takes them to submit feedback.


  * Learn more about Feedback Completion reports at[Report Catalog: Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates#feedback-completion-report)﻿


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.49.42 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sw6aVavoaLWOtKwTRgvgB_screenshot2023-03-10at74942am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Feedback Calibration by Interviewer**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * A count of feedback forms grouped by the interviewer and overall recommendation.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * Assess each interviewer's feedback submissions in total to identify whether there is strong bias towards a "yes" (4, 3) or a "no" (2, 1).


  * Learn more about Feedback Calibration reports at [Report Catalog: Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates#interviewer-feedback-calibration)﻿


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.48.43 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ksLgfMo-KEAxA51GFgnr-_screenshot2023-03-10at74843am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**Feedback Calibration by Interview**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * A count of feedback forms grouped by the interview and overall recommendation.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * Assess each interview's feedback submissions in total to identify whether there is strong bias towards a "yes" (4, 3) or a "no" (2, 1).


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 7.50.36 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/1IaJUqF04hqMOV8SsyR5h_screenshot2023-03-10at75036am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**﻿  
**
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard#source-details)Source Details
This section contains reports regarding: a more detailed accounting of your candidate source types and source details, information on sourcing efforts by presenting lead information, sources contributing to qualified candidates, and overall referral activity.
**New Candidates by Source Type**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Count of job considerations by start date grouped by source type. Leads are excluded from this report.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * You can use this report to examine source type trends over time and better understand the underlying origin of candidates.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.11.07 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yvX-Brm9ig_Y8iqnFhgCe_screenshot2023-03-10at81107am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**﻿  
**
**Total Leads**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * The number of leads, counted as all job considerations with a source type of sourced.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * The total count of sourced leads should reflect the amount of sourcing effort put into this job.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.11.21 AM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RWAWmJAGp6iKXSaWT3HQ6_screenshot2023-03-10at81121am.png)
﻿
﻿  

**Leads Converted**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * The number of sourced leads that reached application review or active interview stages.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A higher number of converted leads means you are moving more candidates into the interview process.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.11.30 AM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/5jXTVF3DXP4tJazzq4FpO_screenshot2023-03-10at81130am.png)
﻿
﻿  

**New Leads by Source**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * A count of leads (job considerations with a source type of "sourced") over time by their source.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * You can use this report to examine source trends over time and better understand the underlying origin of sourced leads.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.11.41 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bem7pKgYQuEevjO1mCqJS_screenshot2023-03-10at81141am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**﻿  
**
**Passthrough Rate by Source Type**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Passthrough rates by source type. For every stage, active counts are displayed on the left, and processed counts are displayed on the right.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A high passthrough rate means most candidates are moving through the stage, while a low passthrough rate means the opposite. Compare this by stage and source type to identify any areas for improvement or review. Learn more about passthrough rate counting at[Passthrough Report Features & FAQ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq)﻿


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.11.53 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/5qz5W48ydnj5UOoNM_bXl_screenshot2023-03-10at81153am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Source Type of Candidates:** Source type of candidates. Candidates include any inbound job considerations or leads that have converted and reached application review or further stages. Leads that do not convert are excluded from this report.
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Count of job considerations by source type. Leads that do not convert are excluded from this report.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * This report shows the total composition by source type at the top of funnel and can be used to see where candidates are originating from.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.12.04 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qHxOb1hfY8M7m1PkbDwyJ_screenshot2023-03-10at81204am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

**Qualified Candidates by Source Type:** Qualified candidates are those that move beyond the Application Review stage.
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Count of job considerations by source type for qualified candidates only. A candidate is considered qualified if their job consideration moves beyond application review.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * You can compare this report to the Source Type of Candidates report to see how the composition by source type changes between your top of funnel and your active process. This report shows which source types are contributing most to candidates that move forward into your interview process.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.12.13 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qsUWS32VeEY3zL5mheKuO_screenshot2023-03-10at81213am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Passthrough Rate by Source Details**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Passthrough rates by source. For every stage, active counts are displayed on the left, and processed counts are displayed on the right.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A high passthrough rate means most candidates are moving through the stage, while a low passthrough rate means the opposite. Compare this by stage and source to identify any areas for improvement or review. Learn more about passthrough rate counting at [Passthrough Report Features & FAQ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/passthrough-report-features-and-faq)﻿ .


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.12.26 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Adcm5Fr6jurb1Be4NmG3P_screenshot2023-03-10at81226am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿  
**
**Referrals**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * All job considerations with source type "referral", including leads.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A higher number reflects more referral activity over the history of this job.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.12.37 AM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/FjykeuaUUWvbD3dGjT2cy_screenshot2023-03-10at81237am.png)
﻿
﻿  

**Active Referrals**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Count of job considerations of source type "referral" that are in currently in process. Excludes leads.


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * A higher number reflects more referral activity in your current pipeline.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-21 at 5.12.01 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/XoaM_WQL8sGQD_99MPQo9_screenshot2023-03-21at51201pm.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

**Referred By**
  * **What's in this report?**


  * Count of job considerations of source type "referral" grouped by referrer and outcome (lead, active, archived or hired).


  * **How do I interpret the results?**


  * You can use this report to see which teammates have referred the most candidates for this job, grouped by the outcome of those referrals.


﻿  

![Screen Shot 2023-03-10 at 8.13.01 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/4Mu-dY_cCumtuNPI6uCYv_screenshot2023-03-10at81301am.png?format=webp)
﻿
_﻿  
_
﻿

job-templates:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Jobs & Openings
# Job Templates
8 min
Copy
In Ashby, you can create job templates to save you time when configuring and opening similar jobs. This guide covers both creating templates from scratch and generating templates from existing roles.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#creating-a-job-template-from-scratch)Creating a Job Template from scratch
To create a new job template, head to Jobs > Jobs > [Job Templates](https://app.ashbyhq.com/jobs/templates "Job Templates") and click Create Job Template.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/573d81680e8a3f7b5f320d9aa234e586.png)
﻿
Add in the template’s title, team and location. Only a title is required for a job template - you can add the location and department to each job generated from this template if you’d prefer to keep this blank for now.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ebeb0210328e7e919e64d9163745460c.png)
﻿
From here, your template will be generated. You can then configure the job template’s settings, interview plan and any postings.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/95d7ed742583af886c6fb124b14945d2.png)
﻿
**1) Settings:** Here you can amend the title, team, location, employment type, any custom fields, compensation settings and the hiring team for this template.
**2) Interview Plan:** Confirm the stages of your pipeline and add in activities to configure interviews, take-home activities, automated emails, etc in advance. You can read more on each activities via the guides below:
﻿[Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿﻿  

﻿[Take-home Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/take-home-activities)﻿﻿  

﻿[Automated Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities)﻿﻿  

**3) Job Postings:** Create a draft posting, add in a job description and configure job posting settings for your template here.
Be sure to activate your job template once you’re ready to start using it. You can activate your template by clicking the Inactive drop-down and selecting Activate.
If you’d like to deactivate or archive a job template, you can click the Active drop-down and opt to deactivate it.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f2d88e87e7fe31c5327af2f2d277d9d8.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bc819f82cdacbdc669e3a772e11a8e12.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#creating-a-job-template-from-an-existing-job)Creating a Job Template from an existing job
If you already have a job that you’d like to create a template from, click into the job from Jobs > Jobs and then click More. Select Create Template from the drop-down. You’ll be asked to title your template before it’s created for you.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2d24413afdb6c6b86dd4967b32fd73a2.png)
﻿
Remember to activate your job template before you try to use it for a new role! You can make adjustments to the template at any time.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f2d88e87e7fe31c5327af2f2d277d9d8.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#creating-a-job-from-a-template)Creating a job from a template
To create a new job, head to Jobs > Jobs and click Create Job. Use the From Template drop-down to select the template you’d like to generate a job from.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3a5964ded7c9f8703b9524b5b26d249f.png)
﻿
If you didn’t specify a location and department on your template, this will need to be added to the job before it can be created.
You can make amendments to the job that has been generated from a template. Adjustments make to these jobs will not impact the template they’ve been generated from.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#why-is-direct-booking-unavailable-on-my-job-templa)Why is direct booking unavailable on my job template?
This message will disappear on jobs generated from this template. It shows as unavailable on the template because the template itself is not an active job.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#can-i-delete-job-templates)Can I delete job templates?
It’s not possible to delete job templates in-app. Please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com for assistance with this.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#can-i-limit-elevated-access-users-with-access-to-c)C**an I limit Elevated Access users with access to create jobs to only use job templates?**
You can. To enable this option navigate to Admin > Jobs & Applications > [Job Management](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/job-management "Job Management") and switch on the Elevated Access Users Must Create Jobs From Job Templates option.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates#can-i-make-a-job-template-confidential)Can I make a job template confidential?
You can! Click More when viewing your template and select Make Confidential. Only the creator of the job template can adjust the confidential status.
﻿

ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Getting Started with Analytics
# Ashby Analytics: Key Reporting Concepts
23 min
Copy
Ashby is powerful and flexible, but you only need to understand a few key concepts in order to build your own reports. The sections below discuss these key concepts.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#fields)Fields
One of the powerful concepts in Ashby is the ability to search, filter and group by any field on any object or subject. The better you understand the concept of fields, and which fields are available to you, the faster you will be at building reports.
A field is an information point within the subject that can be used for filtering your report results, grouping your report results or be included in your results if creating a custom report.  So, for example, a job’s department is a field and could be used to:
﻿  

  * Filter results to only show data for the jobs within that department. 


  * Be used to group results so data is displayed per department so comparisons can be drawn. 


  * Be shown on a custom list report, perhaps as part of an export of job data, or to be included as part of the report details within an Alert.


﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6707ac008d20aac132878c8631e1c189.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fcba3c13e6f9cf82f28980d48cc8a3b3.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b63c022cb6738e5fe7e735d00cfed95e.png)
﻿
For more on a specific subject and/or field, please check out [Ashby Subject & Field Definitions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-subject-and-field-definitions)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#filtering)Filtering
Filtering is one of the most essential pieces in Ashby. Filters allow you to set specific limits to the view the most relevant data to you.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#time-range)Time Range
Some reports (such as Count Over Time or Segmentation) require you to set a time range, which serves as a filter. You’ll be prompted to select a timestamp on your subject (for example Started At on a job consideration or application) and then a time range. Any data that falls within the time range specified (based on the determined timestamp), will then be displayed.
So, for example, if a time range is set to Started At and within the last three months, all job considerations that were created within the last three months will be shown on the report, providing that no further filters are added.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/68772273bf531aa57a15fc17c419fab5.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#filters)Filters
If you’re looking for very specific information, you can use filters to set criteria. Only the data that matches your set criteria will then be shown in your results.
Filters are commonly used to filter data by department, source, location, job and more.
In Ashby, you can use basic filters, or advanced filters if you’d like to filter by something more specific.
If the Advanced option is not enabled, then you can quickly select a job, source, team and/or location from the dropdowns to quickly filter results by those fields.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/621c3c09314fedaf045d769fb1bf8442.png)
﻿
If the Advanced option is enabled, click + Add Field to Match to then pick from a wide range of filter fields.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ecda142f53a0ee724ede0f5b70bad509.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#grouping)Grouping
The last important building block in Ashby is the concept of grouping values.
You can group values to display your data in the most relevant way to you by created subgroups. One common example is grouping your results per department or location.
You can click Add Field to Group By to select your chosen field to group your results by. The fields available are based on the Subject selected for the report.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1bd4018ef76d6f28b1ee358a768010b8.png)
﻿
Some report templates are configured to group by a specified field already. The Passthrough Rates with Groupings report template will automatically group by the application or job consideration’s source. You can click the trash can icon to remove the field from the Group By section and then select an alternative field if needed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ff7b0b64544d8117bb5c02f875bd1f2b.png)
﻿
You can also add multiple groupings to a report if you’d like more granular results. If you’d like to adjust your groupings, you can click and drag the fields you are grouping by to re-order them.
For example, pictured we have a passthrough rate report grouped by department and by job - as such, the department results are listed, then these results are broken down further by each specific job.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/10c2a207b22835b87a197a97fb57aaed.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#date-ranges-and-best-practices)Date Ranges and Best Practices
Most reports report on specific timeframes, and some even report on trends over time. This video gives a quick overview of the most important things to understand about date fields in Ashby.
When building a report, you’ll be prompted to select a time field and time range to determine the data shown within your report.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#time-ranges)Time Ranges
Once you’ve selected your time field, the To Show dropdown allows you to then select your time range. Some options are dynamic and some are static.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8a1e8276c12913358c55b44c3a636421.png)
﻿
The dynamic options update results as time goes by, whereas the static options remain fixed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/055395de1907f479e5a9fcc0d07f4fb9.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#open-for-a-list-of-time-ranges)Open for a list of time ranges
**Dynamic time ranges**
This Week: Displays the current week (Monday to Sunday)
This Month: Displays the current calendar month
This Quarter: Displays the current quarter so far
This Fiscal Quarter: Displays the current fiscal quarter so far
This Year: Displays the current calendar year so far
This Fiscal Year: Displays the current fiscal year so far
Today: Displays results from today
Last Week: Displays the previous week (Monday to Sunday)
Last Month: Displays the previous calendar month
Last Quarter: Displays the previous quarter
Last Fiscal Quarter: Displays the previous fiscal quarter
Last Year: Displays the previous calendar year
Last Fiscal Year: Displays the previous fiscal year
Yesterday: Displays results from yesterday
In the last…: A rolling time window that displays results that fall within the specified time frame in the past (for example, in the last three months)
In the next…: A rolling time window that displays results that fall within the specified time frame in the future (for example, in the next year)
**Fixed time ranges**
Since…: From the specified date until now.
Before…: Any results prior to the specified date.
Between…: Results between a specified start date and end date.
On…: Results on a specific date.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#intervals)Intervals
For Count Over Time reports, you’ll also be prompted to select an interval. The interval will group the qualifying data so you can track results across each day, week, month, quarter, fiscal quarter, year, fiscal year.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/41faa90ed7168dfe7dd06305208897c8.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#advanced-filtering-via-multiple-fields)Advanced Filtering via Multiple Fields
Advanced filtering allows you to build a nested query, so you can specify multiple fields that should be true for data to qualify.
To filter by multiple fields, click Add Field to Match and then search for and select Multiple Fields.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/53a1c8b0882bc8345df600cd21048147.png)
﻿
Make sure to check that the Multiple Fields option you select is one that will have the fields you are looking to filter by.
Be aware that, depending on your subject, there may be a number of Multiple Fields options (for example, if searching by candidate, you may see Multiple Fields options for the candidate’s application, interview, etc.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cada048b203f335fd43e66f34fec907b.png)
﻿
From here, you will then see that an All are true clarifier is added to the Filter section. Use the Add Fieldto Match option again to add your fields and specify your criteria.
And, Or and Not options are also supported:
And: Sets the criteria to All is true. All criteria specified must be met to be shown in the report results.
Or: Sets the filter to Any are true. If any of the filters configured are met, then the data will be shown in the report’s results.
Not: Sets the filter to The following is not true. Only data that doesn’t meet the criteria specified in the filter will be shown in the report results.
![The example here filters for any applications that haven’t been archived and that weren’t created or started before February 26, 2025. As such, only active candidates with applications submitted after this date will be shown in the results. ](https://cdn.zappy.app/0b48f52c84d2c7b1c363831cc2ec340f.png)
﻿
The example above filters for any applications that haven’t been archived and that weren’t created or started before February 26, 2025. As such, only active candidates with applications submitted after this date will be shown in the results.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#calculated-fields)Calculated Fields
Calculated fields are an advanced feature that allow you to remap ATS data for reporting purposes. For example, you can take all sources from your ATS and group them into a custom set of buckets. You can read more on this at [Calculated Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/calculated-fields)﻿.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/47heuydXVPjmJl2qpxKqG-ei.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#using-a-calculated-grouping-field-to-remap-sources)Using a calculated grouping field to remap sources.
The videos below provides a quick introduction. Starting with Calculated Grouping Fields.
﻿
This video show how to use Filter Bucket Fields, a more advanced version of the bucketing field shown above:
﻿
﻿  

#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#timezones-and-reporting)Timezones and Reporting
When creating reports in Ashby the default behavior is to preset results in the user’s timezone. This behavior can be adjusted by using the timezone selector. There are three timezone options to choose from:
  * User Timezone (Default): The report will dynamically adjust to present results in the viewer’s User Timezone


  * Organization Timezone: The report will present results in the organization timezone for all viewers


  * Custom Timezone: The report will present results in the specified timezone for all viewers


Your organization timezone is configured by your admin under the Admin > Data Mapping > Timezone section. Each user can set their user timezone in their personal account settings. By default, all user timezones are set to the organization timezone.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#mapping-interview-stages-to-stage-groups)Mapping Interview Stages to Stage Groups
For the purpose of reporting, all interview plan stages are mapped into a "grouped" interview plan. During setup we have created a grouped interview plan for you, but you can edit it at any time in Admin > Data Management> Grouped Interview Plan. To read more about this process, see [Utilizing Stage Groups](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/utilizing-stage-groups)﻿.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/20df8c26d976cd59f6267752fd5eae0b.png)
﻿
If you are using Ashby Analytics only and are using data from Lever or Greenhouse, you'll want to map the individual stage names into one of your grouped stages. To do this, head to Admin > Data Mapping > Greenhouse Interview Stage Mapping. You can then using the Pick stage group dropdowns to determine the Ashby stage group your interview stages should map to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4398654bdb237943bf5cbac41a20b761.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#fiscal-year-support)Fiscal Year Support
Custom Fiscal Year support is available on the Legacy Plus, Plus, and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
Ashby supports reporting on a shifted fiscal year. To enable this functionality, navigate to your Admin > Data Mapping > Fiscal Year section. After you enable fiscal year support, select the first day of your fiscal year.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/WEeV7vmwpS5_Im7eYkkDDbyX.png)
﻿
After you enable this feature, when selecting timeframes or filter on date fields, you'll see quick options for selecting fiscal quarters or years, when reporting. Below is a short video demonstrating the use of fiscal calendar fields.
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#how-are-interviews-counted-in-ashby)How are interviews counted in Ashby?
We are planning on changing the counting behavior. Feedback about the way interviews are currently counted is welcome!
Currently, how interviews are counted depends on the report type and the groupings that are applied.
When counting Interviews, in the Count Over Time and the Segmentation report, Ashby counts one interview per candidate and stage
We find this to be the most common report users need; and they typically want to answer "How many onsite interviews did we have this week?" In most cases that means "How many candidates had an interview in the onsite stage in a given week"? Therefore we apply a special counting mechanism in these report types. We count only one interview per candidate & stage. So if a graph shows 22 onsite interviews for a given week, this means that 22 candidates had an onsite in the interview stage in that week (independent of how many interviews took place in that stage or how many interviewers participated in the interviews). In this week, 22 candidates had an onsite interview. The exception to this rule is if you group the report by Interviewer. In that case we break out each individual participation of each interviewer. E.g. a panel of 4 interviews, with 2 interviewers each, would count as 8. A bit more on Interviewer Participations, below. This special rule currently only applies to the Count Over Time and the Segmentation Report, as these are most commonly used to report on this type of metric. 
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/rNLFvV9hDZ-Bdyk0c_lYBzvo.png)
﻿
﻿  

When counting Interview Participations in any report, Ashby counts all the participants across all interviews
When counting interview participations Ashby sums up all the interviews and all their participants. E.g. a panel of 4 interviews, with 2 interviewers each, would count as 8.
When counting Interviews in a custom list reports, all individual interviews within a round are counted
In this case, we assume the user simply wants to see all individual interviews. A panel of 4 different interviews will result in 4 entries in the custom list report (independent of how many participants are in each interview).
When counting Interviews in the interview : hire ratio report and in the interview load report, Ashby counts individual interviews
In these reports, as of today, a panel of 4 interviews, with 2 interviewers each, would count as 4. In the near future we will likely change this to count interview participations instead. In that case a panel of 4 interviews, with 2 interviewers each, would count as 8.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#how-often-are-reports-and-dashboards-refreshed)How often are reports and dashboards refreshed?
Report results are refreshed live when a report is opened. As a dashboard is essentially a collection of reports, all reports on the dashboard are refreshed upon initially opening said dashboard. If the dashboard page itself is refreshed or any of the reports from that dashboard are then viewed directly, they’ll pull from that cached version created upon the dashboard initally being opened.
While on the dashboard, every report has via their top-right option menu the choice to refresh and re-run the report, rather than pulling from the cached version. This option is also available when viewing a report directly.
﻿  

![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BQnV-Fyd4XXjLVHKCtcrm_screenshot2022-11-04at102607am.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8d197cdd3607dbc57f08a0fd24085d91.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#how-do-chart-legends-work)How do Chart Legends work?
When viewing a chart in Ashby, you’ll see the groups that your results are grouped by listed beneath the chart. These group labels are referred to as chart legends.
For example, here is a stacked bar chart showing the number of job considerations started per week over the last three months. The results are grouped by department and we can see each department listed as a chart legend below the chart for reference:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f896e43243d058959c6b76fd0b0dd8ee.png)
﻿
This is not only for clarifying colour coding. You can hover over each legend item to view results within that group across the report. Here, for example, we’re hovering over the Product legend item so those results are highlighted:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2d6d9f0236a5e57f7ce3d02a4259b6e6.png)
﻿
You can also click on a legend item tag or colour swatch to remove that group from the report. The legend item won’t disappear completely, you will be able to click on the colour swatch again to add those results back into the report. In this example, we’ve clicked the swatch for the Marketing department legend item to hide results from that department on this report.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d87a8669141b82a9aa5a5a33ffe1f651.png)
﻿
If you click on the legend item name, you can focus the report on that specific legend item without needing to remove all other legend items from the report. So here, we’ve clicked on People Operations to see only the results for the People Operations department.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5e1a34468d9a59581b74c71c7b6dae26.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#what-is-the-difference-between-the-two-recruiter-f)What is the difference between the two "Recruiter" fields in Ashby?
You might have noticed that Ashby has two recruiter fields. This is because the recruiter assigned to an individual application can be different than the recruiter assigned to a job (this is true for all other hiring team roles as well). In most cases you want to use Recruiter field associated directly with the application, as that yields the most accurate results.
![Document image](https://static.slab.com/prod/uploads/l73blu9g/posts/images/0_mwF0ohoD9zwXaPcFTKoQ_s.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#what-happens-when-i-group-by-hiring-manager-on-a-j)What happens when I group by hiring manager on a job that has multiple hiring managers assigned?
We will count activity towards all hiring managers that are assigned to a job. For example if hiring manager Simon and Alexa are both assigned to a Head of Design job in your ATS, and there are 10 offers associated with that job, we would show 10 offers for each Simon and Alexa (assuming they don't have offers belonging to other jobs).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts#how-do-i-copy-or-duplicate-a-report)How do I copy or duplicate a report?
When viewing your report, click the Save button. From here, you’ll have the option Save As New Report… Click this to create a new copy of your report, or, if you’ve made changes to the existing report, create a new report that reflects those changes without applying them to the current report.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f025a44b42f20878d8ca52c0cab1bd93.png)
﻿
﻿

User Access & Sharing Metrics
# View Only Users
7 min
Copy
This document provides a quick overview of the View Only user experience and functionality within Ashby Analytics.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/view-only-users#introduction)Introduction
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/view-only-users#access-and-functionality)Access and Functionality
View Only users have the ability to:
  * Access any existing report or dashboard in relation to the Global, Team, and Job Access Roles they have been given


  * Configure their home page for easy access to any report or dashboard


  * Export dashboards as PDFs


  * Export data from individual reports


  * Receive notifications via [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿﻿


  * Receive scheduled dashboard deliveries via [Scheduled Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduled-dashboards)﻿﻿


  * Use dashboard filters to adjust reporting results as needed


  * View Jobs list, Job Profile, and default [Job Dashboard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-dashboard)﻿﻿


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/view-only-users#navigating-the-dashboard-interface)Navigating the dashboard interface
Use the + icon to add quick links to reports or dashboards on your home page
![Screen Cast 2022-11-10 at 7.50.28 AM.gif](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/-b8XpF74KW3tAYMLEqaOw_screencast2022-11-10at75028am.gif)
﻿
Select any dashboard filter option to apply it to all connected reports on the dashboard.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-10 at 7.52.24 AM.gif](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ZeNVEYFA6A_qakfLPS5_-_screencast2022-11-10at75224am.gif)
﻿
Finally, if you'd like to export a dashboard, click More then Export to download a dashboard as a PDF.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-10 at 10.03.54 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dIVV8iaK3ssqv053xZcza_screencast2022-11-10at100354am.gif)
﻿
**Change report View Type on a dashboard**
Click … then View Type to change the report View Type.
![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 8.03.47 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ZxQv8yywlE5gdmM7sIkJy_screencast2023-01-10at80347am.gif)
﻿
**Export report images or PDFs**
Click … then Export Image or Export PDF. You will be able to select the export file type and size.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-10 at 7.56.19 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qsTzBzFEn2tPjYZeojmQ6_screencast2022-11-10at75619am.gif)
﻿
**Export report data as CSV**
Click … then View Type. Change the View Type to Table, and then click … again. You can now select Export CSV.
![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 8.11.00 AM.gif](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/stG0qXvZRUUYZURb3GpXd_screencast2023-01-10at81100am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/view-only-users#navigating-underlying-reports)Navigating underlying reports
**View underlying reports**
Click ... then Go to Report to directly see any underlying report.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-10 at 8.01.35 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/H3AibF17O3O6j5vhorkk__screencast2022-11-10at80135am.gif)
﻿
**Change report View Type on underlying report**
Click the … on any report on the dashboard and select Go to Report. You can change the view option of the report in the View Options section.
If you select the Table view option and enable Pivot Mode, you can export the results in that format via CSV.
![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 8.15.57 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dJnbeldfCcrmDlJqYmpyN_screencast2023-01-10at81557am.gif)
﻿
**Export report images or PDFs**
When viewing a report, navigate to the Results section and select Export PNG or Export PDF. You will be able to select the export file type and size.
![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 8.32.27 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/1Eh2585GeOBlZlJpmH-3f_screencast2023-01-10at83227am.gif)
﻿
**Export report data as CSV**
When viewing a report, navigate to the Results section and select the Table View Option. Then select Export CSV.
![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 8.37.55 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jpeSq86KLndt3ebG2X1aS_screencast2023-01-10at83755am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/view-only-users#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/view-only-users#will-changes-i-have-made-to-reports-or-dashboards)Will changes I have made to reports or dashboards be saved?
View Only users do not have the permissions to save any updates made to reports or dashboards.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Surveys, Assessments & Activit...
Activity Configuration
# Automated Activities
16 min
Copy
You can now automate the sending of direct booking links, candidate availability links, questionnaires, emails and take-home assessments from within your job’s interview plan. These automations are triggered as candidates transition from stage to stage in your pipeline.
Assessments sent via an integration (for example, via HackerRank or Bryq) can also be automated.
Automated activities can be configured on the Foundations, Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#setting-up-automation-on-a-schedule-interview-acti)Setting up automation on a Schedule Interview activity.
For more information on setting up {{sch}}, check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿
﻿
When setting up or amending your schedule interview activity, click on the Automation tab. Then check the Automate on Stage Entry checkbox.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f3065da31b1215f2f9ba261b0ed06f62.png)
﻿
From there, you’ll be asked to specify the automation type (whether you’d like to send out a direct booking link or a candidate availability link automatically).
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a6b27da478cad29ca875a953b5a9af26.png)
﻿
If you haven’t already, ensure that an email template is set up for direct booking links or candidate availability links in the Communication tab of your schedule interview activity.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4cc2939c73e3d060f3737f1a67006a6f.png)
﻿
This is the email that will be sent out to the candidate automatically when they’re moved to this stage of your pipeline.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#setting-up-automation-for-a-send-email-activity)Setting up automation for a Send Email activity.
Please note that only emails to the candidate can be automated - it’s not currently possible to automate Send Email activities for interviewers or hiring team members. If you are looking to set up automated booking confirmation emails or interview reminders, please check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿.
﻿
On the interview plan for your job, click + Add Activity _,_ then select the _Send Email_ activity option.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4571cff3bed4314a66cb3317e853a069.png)
﻿
Select the email template that you’d like to send out as part of this activity. Click Submit to create the activity.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5f8e8855f29052ed70d5581d5182bb83.png)
﻿
To automate the sending of the email, click Modify _,_ then Edit for this activity, then click on the Automation tab. Check the Automate on Stage Entry? checkbox.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/96e42da8019d46cc77f96e6fc5c8c728.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2d9b18dbe762662501dde6c9ad855bee.png)
﻿
The email will then be automatically sent when the candidate is moved to the stage the automated activity is in.
When a candidate is initially moved to the stage with the automated activity in it, you will have the option to stop the automated email from being sent.
If you haven’t already, ensure that an email template is set up for direct booking links or candidate availability links in the Communication tab of your schedule interview activity.
This is the email that will be sent out to the candidate automatically when they’re moved to this stage of your pipeline. You will have the option to undo the automation each time it is triggered.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#setting-up-automation-for-a-questionnaire-activity)Setting up automation for a Questionnaire activity.
﻿
Head to the interview plan for your job, then click + Add Activity, then select the Questionnaire option.
When selecting your questionnaire and email template, check the Automate on Stage Entry? checkbox so the questionnaire is sent when the candidate is moved to the specified stage.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/79e054f205991308ce1299ce8235e45c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#automation-for-a-take-home-assessment)Automation for a Take-home assessment.
﻿
On the interview plan for the role in question, click + Add Activity in the stage you’d like to add the take home assessment to, then select the Take-home option.
When setting up your take-home, you can opt to select an email from your email templates to be sent out as part of the automation on the Communication tab.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/15850bf531459b0654a3f353241988eb.png)
﻿
On the Automation tab, you can then check the Automate on Stage Entry? checkbox, then select the user that the take-home email should be sent on behalf of.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f2159e3b303b487711c3b21de24e1c88.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#setting-up-automation-on-an-assessment-sent-via-an)Setting up automation on an assessment sent via an integration
﻿
To create an activity on your interview plan, head to your job, then click on the Interview Plans tab.
From there, you can click the + Add Activity to… option on the stage you’d like to add the assessment to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/96dfb11b9cc49b6f404a0fc3d95d3287.png)
﻿
Then select the Assessment integration you’d like to use from the Add Activity sidebar. Select the assessment that you’d like to send to candidates to complete from the dropdown.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/30c30b02913afeb0c62a0a52ceac6d4f.png)
﻿
Once selected, you can head to the Settings tab of the Assessment Configuration page and check the Automate on Stage Entry? checkbox.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c3a441537219c569ff1315138d172b26.png)
﻿
From now on, candidates moved to this stage will have the assessment sent to them automatically. To stop automation for future candidates, uncheck this checkbox.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#how-to-confirm-a-stage-has-an-automated-activity)How to confirm a stage has an automated activity
If a stage has an automated activity added to it, a lightning bolt icon will show beside the stage name.
This icon will show on the candidate pipeline for your job, on the _Interview Plan_ for the job on the stage name when moving or advancing a candidate to the next stage (both via the Change Stage button and in Application Review if the following stage has an automated activity). This iconography can also be seen when choosing the stage candidates should be added to when considering them for a job.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5d66d8e80ecbdeb96d9715492fea143c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#can-i-stop-an-automatic-activity-for-a-specific-ca)Can I stop an automatic activity for a specific candidate or candidates?
**Changing a stage for a single job consideration**
When a single candidate is moved to a stage with an automated activity, you will see the option to Undo the activity. You’ll have 15 seconds after the candidate is moved into the stage to undo the automated activity.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8a03f6ee076a46f63574d184664f444e.png)
﻿
**Changing a stage in bulk for multiple job considerations**
When changing job consideration stages in bulk, a warning message will appear confirming that some activities are configured to automatically start.
You can opt out of starting these activities automatically by unchecking the Start Automatic Activities? checkbox.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3083164a24d5550691b6f80040ab9087.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#can-i-delay-or-schedule-the-automation-of-the-acti)Can I delay or schedule the automation of the activity as part of the automated activity setup?
Currently, automated activities will run as soon as the candidate has been moved to the specified stage. It’s not possible to delay or schedule the automation of the activity as part of the activity automation setup.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/automated-activities#can-i-set-up-an-automated-activity-to-move-a-candi)Ca**n I set up an automated activity to move a candidate to another stage automatically?**
No, it’s not possible to create an automation to change a candidate’s stage.
﻿
