[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
# Getting Started with Analytics
17 Articles
### Navigating Ashby Analytics
17 min
This guide provides an overview of Ashby Analytics. Learning some of the key concepts of Ashby Analytics will help you quickly build your own reports and visualizations and get previously hidden insights about your recruiting efforts.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/navigating-ashby-analytics)
﻿
### Ashby Analytics: Key Reporting Concepts
23 min
Ashby is powerful and flexible, but you only need to understand a few key concepts in order to build your own reports. The sections below discuss these key concepts.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ashby-analytics-key-reporting-concepts)
﻿
### Building Your First Dashboard
24 min
The goal of this document is to familiarize yourself with the basics of building a dashboard to give you the foundational knowledge you need to fully take advantage of Ashby Analytics. Custom Dashboards are available on the Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans Dashboards help organize, present, and visualize your reports in various ways, allowing you to tailor the Ashby experience to specific audiences and purposes.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard)
﻿
### Dashboard Filters
8 min
This document reviews dashboard filters and how to add or remove them from dashboards. Dashboard filters are a powerful feature in Ashby that allow you to add or configure filters at the dashboard level that can be quickly applied to all reports on a dashboard.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters)
﻿
### Report Drill Downs
5 min
When reporting on specific subjects, you can open a report drill down to view the data making up the results. Many of the reports you build in Ashby will have drill downs available.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-drill-downs)
﻿
### Saved Reports & Dashboards
17 min
If you have a large number of saved reports and dashboards, the Saved Reports layout will allow you to filter and locate your saved content easily. When navigating to Reports > Saved Reports or Dashboards > Saved Dashboards, you can filter, create saved views and search for specific reports or dashboards by title.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/saved-reports-and-dashboards)
﻿
### Permissions & Sharing Dashboards
25 min
In this guide, we’ll cover how permissions impact viewing reports and dashboards and how to share reports and dashboards you’ve created with other users. In Ashby Analytics, there are two license types.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards)
﻿
### Understanding Interview Stage Transitions
5 min
This document aims to review the details of the Interview Stage Transition subject and provides some common reporting examples. When building reports in Ashby one of the subjects available is Interview Stage Transitions.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/understanding-interview-stage-transitions)
﻿
### Reporting Errors & Resolutions
6 min
This document reviews different types of errors you may encounter when using Ashby, what they mean, and how to resolve them. If you ever encounter an error screen of any type, you are welcome and encouraged to reach out to support\@ashbyhq.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/reporting-errors-and-resolutions)
﻿
### Exporting Reports and Dashboards
9 min
This guide gives an overview of the various report and dashboard export options. Ashby supports exporting both reports and dashboards.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/exporting-reports-and-dashboards)
﻿
### Analytics Jobs List & Dashboard
5 min
You can access your job list and job-specific dashboards in Ashby Analytics. This guide walks through the information available in the jobs list, job dashboards and within individual job views.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/analytics-jobs-list-and-dashboard)
﻿
### Calculated Fields
9 min
This document reviews the Calculated Fields feature and the two different types of fields you can create. If you are looking to create fields to house custom information for a candidate, job, project or offer, please check out Custom Fields .
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/calculated-fields)
﻿
### Formula Fields
17 min
Formula Fields are a new type of that allow mathematical and logical operations on top of your existing field data, bringing more robust customization to your reporting needs. After a Formula Field is created, it is available for all filtering and grouping in reporting across Ashby.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/formula-fields)
﻿
### Field Comparison Filters
8 min
The traditional filter interaction follows a paradigm of letting you pick a field or property and then specifying a property value to pull results for. For example, filtering for Interview Stage = Onsite would be specifying the value Onsite for the field Interview Stage to match results accordingly.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/field-comparison-filters)
﻿
### Report View Options
15 min
This document reviews the different report view options in Ashby. Every report has View Options to allow for configuration and customization.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options)
﻿
### How do I enable Advanced filters?
3 min
This document reviews how to enable and utilize Advanced filtering options in Ashby's report building interface. The Advanced filter interface allows you to select individual and multiple fields while applying logical And, Or, and Not operations.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-enable-advanced-filters)
﻿
### Quality of Hire Reports
10 min
Check out Quality of Hire Surveys to get more information on setting up Quality of Hire forms and sequences. Quality of Hire Surveys are available on the Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans We recommend you explore Quality of Hire reporting by starting with the template reports to help familiarize yourself with the available data.
[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/quality-of-hire-reports)
﻿
﻿

permissions-and-sharing-dashboards:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Getting Started with Analytics
# Permissions & Sharing Dashboards
25 min
Copy
In this guide, we’ll cover how permissions impact viewing reports and dashboards and how to share reports and dashboards you’ve created with other users.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#permissions-overview)Permissions Overview
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#ashby-analytics)Ashby Analytics
In Ashby Analytics, there are two license types.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#analytics-view-only)Analytics - View Only
Users with this license are limited to viewing reports created by Analytics - Admin users. They can configure their personal home page to show their preferred dashboards and reports, but they cannot create new reports or explore data (e.g. via filtering) on their own. For more on what view only users can see, please check out [View Only Users](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/view-only-users)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#analytics-admin)Analytics - Admin
Users assigned this license have the ability to explore report templates and save and edit reports and dashboards. They have access to all features in Ashby Analytics.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#ashby-permissions)Ashby Permissions
In Ashby there are three global access roles. For more on configuring access roles, check out [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#limited-access)Limited Access
Limited to being scheduled for interviews, providing interview feedback, and referring candidates. These users will not be able to view reports and dashboards directly in Ashby although will be able to see data in notifications sent to them via the [Scheduled Dashboards](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduled-dashboards)﻿ tool, as scheduled dashboard content is generated using the permissions of the user who configured the scheduling.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#elevated-access)Elevated Access
Starts with the same basic permissions as Limited Access users, but can be granted additional permissions for parts of your organization via Access Roles. Employees with this global role do not have access to anything in the Admin tab unless they’ve been explicitly been granted access to tools via [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿.
When viewing reports and dashboards, these users will only be able to see the data for the roles that they have access to by default unless data is shown using a specific user’s permissions (more on this at [Share My Dashboard View](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#share-my-dashboard-view)﻿).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#organization-admin)Organization Admin
Starts with full access to the entire organization but access can be narrowed down for parts of your organization via Access Roles. They can edit other user’s permissions and can edit all organization data: tags, interviews, email templates, etc. Users do not have access to confidential jobs unless explicitly granted.
  * This is a common role for analysts, admins, heads of talent, recruiting ops folks, etc. Many companies also grant the entire recruiting team this access level.


  * When viewing reports and dashboards, these users will only be able to see the data for the roles that they have access to by default unless data is shown using a specific user’s permissions (more on this at [Share My Dashboard View](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#share-my-dashboard-view)﻿).


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#content-access)Content Access
For more on content access and locking reports, check out [Confidential Reporting & Content Access Control](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control)﻿.
In Ashby, you can restrict access to both reports and dashboards. There are two states content can be in:
  * Private: restricts access to the user and anyone on the access list


  * Public: shared to your organization, i.e. viewable by any Ashby user


Upon saving new content, the user will have the choice of making it private or public content using the Access Control option when saving their report.
The default option is for the report or dashboard to be public. The access control settings for any report can be adjusted at a later time under More > Access.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f330d013a71b8bd9f2d6630ef4ef4f47.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#sharing-filtered-dashboards)Sharing Filtered Dashboards
If you are unfamiliar with adding filters to dashboards in Ashby, please see our documentation for an overview of [Dashboard Filters](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/dashboard-filters)﻿.
Please note that this isn’t currently available for Core Dashboards.
Custom Dashboards are available on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
﻿
Sharing dashboards in Ashby is usually as simple as copying and sharing the dashboard URL.
If your dashboard has filters and any filters are applied, we will dynamically update the URL to encode your dashboard’s filter state. At any point, you can simply copy the URL and share it as needed. You can now also click the Share button when viewing a dashboard.
![dashboard_sharing.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dUOpfeGBr7OKRnXIN_EeO_dashboardsharing.png?format=webp)
﻿
When clicking the Share button you will be presented with an option to copy the dashboard URL and you can use the Include Filters checkbox to determine whether the filters should be applied to the dashboard via the sharing URL.
At times when you want to get the underlying dashboard URL without any filters applied, deselect the Include Filters option, then copy the URL.
![share_url.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/iCHakUzgkK8iH8CyrW3WC_shareurl.png?format=webp)
﻿
When you are in the process of editing a dashboard, the same Share function is accessible through the More menu in the top right-hand corner.
![edit_mode_sharing.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/N6cNrmxSDJPTm9lMEwXFd_editmodesharing.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#saved-views)Saved Views
If you have various combinations of dashboard filters that are used to meet different use cases a saved view can be used to store any configuration of dashboard filters with a custom title. You can then access that set of dashboard filters at a later date for immediate convenience. At its core, a saved view represents a set of dashboard filters and values.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#creating-selecting-a-saved-view)Creating & Selecting a Saved View
If you are using the dashboard filters for the first time you will have the option to save them as a new view by clicking the Save as View button.
![first_saved_view.png](https://cdn.zappy.app/20f8ac83928008273ec6559b590c2263.png)
﻿
You’ll then be prompted to give your view a name and determine whether it is private (only visible to you) or public (accessible to all users who can view the dashboard) before saving the view.
Please note that a shared view can be viewed by all users with access to the dashboard but only the Organization Admin or creator of the view can make amendments to the view configuration.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c609f5b1da64169e9c907e7514d0a619.png)
﻿
Saved views are accessible from the filter component at the top of any dashboard. Any private views will have a padlock identifier for your reference:
![selecting_views.png](https://cdn.zappy.app/2bd64f7bbb8966744ffe5079416c4cda.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#updating-and-managing-saved-views)Updating and Managing Saved Views
After you’ve selected a view, any subsequent edits to the filters within the view will be indicated in in the UI, as the Save View button will become active. On clicking Save View, you will have the option to update the existing view or save your new filter state as a new view if desired:
![update_or_save.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dHSyRmWz2Zc0-Qe21mu50_af625fc7-e0b9-47ad-a7e2-4d37a593c501.png?format=webp)
﻿
To rename or delete any existing views you can click the Manage View button, then select either Rename or Delete. You will also have the option to make the view public or private (depending on the initial configuration of the view) and you can also pin the view to your homepage.
![modifying_a_saved_view.png](https://cdn.zappy.app/2133cf6d629f60fe65ab26982da482c3.png)
﻿
If you opt to pin the view to your homepage, it will appear in the Views section on the left, listed with another other reports or dashboards you have pinned to your homepage.
The view name will be listed first, followed by the dashboard name.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c2b2b6976d4d93c04272b46176d23d0d.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#going-to-a-filtered-report)Going to a Filtered Report
﻿
When viewing a dashboard with filters applied you have the option to view the underlying report directly by selecting the Go to Filtered Report action.
![filters_applied.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/H0wmSanzauP7k5Fjl93_N_dd582adc-c214-4601-9f9a-a6ed489505b1.png?format=webp)
﻿
If you need to get to the underlying report without any dashboard filter context being applied, the action menu on any report tile provides the option (the three dots to the right of the Go to Filtered Report button) to go directly to the report via Go to Report.
![report_menu.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/FrWS-ZTU1AMqfsiZ78MZC_reportmenu.png?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#share-my-dashboard-view)Share My Dashboard View
By default, users will only see data for the roles that they have access to when viewing a dashboard shared with them. You can now set a dashboard to always show data based on a specific user’s permissions so even those without access to the appropriate jobs will be able to see the same data points as you.
If the dashboard link is shared with a user after you have shared your dashboard view, they will be able to see same data points as you regardless of their permission settings. Please note that the user will need to log in to Ashby and have Elevated Access to see the dashboard.
The Share My Dashboard View option is only available on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans, along with Analytics only users:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#sharing-your-dashboard-view)Sharing your dashboard view
To set up a dashboard to display the data you see to all users accessing the dashboard:
  * Navigate to the dashboard you’d like to share


  * Click More, then Access


  * Click Share My View under Data Shown using viewer’s permissions


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e6754e632b448e85d2e1bded558bb433.png)
﻿
  * You will then be listed as the viewer and your permissions will be used for other users viewing the dashboard. Moving forward, only the user listed as the viewer (or sharing their view) here will be able to add or remove reports.


Please note that users viewing the dashboard with the viewer’s permissions will only have those permissions applied when viewing the dashboard. Their own permissions will be applied when opening specific reports within the dashboard.
Users viewing the underlying reports on your dashboard will be able to edit those reports if they have access to edit reports so we would recommend locking your reports prior to sharing your view.
To lock a report:
  * Navigate to the report in question


  * Click the Unlocked tag under the report title


  * Select Lock this Report


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e15572408678ff8d8c578a43cf0989e1.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#removing-yourself-as-the-specified-user)Removing yourself as the specified user
If you would like users to only view the dashboard with their own permissions applied moving forward:
  * Navigate to the dashboard in question


  * Click More, then Access


  * Click the trash can icon beside the user listed in the Data shown using viewer’s permissions section


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/acdd9c4f61bbe12f7553921fb06ba233.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#can-i-set-myself-as-the-viewer-or-share-my-own-das)Can I set myself as the viewer or share my own dashboard view on private dashboards?
Yes, you can set users to see a dashboard with your own permissions on both a public and a private dashboard. Please note that only users with access to your private dashboard will be able to see it.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/permissions-and-sharing-dashboards#what-happens-to-saved-views-if-the-dashboards-filt)What happens to saved views if the dashboard’s filters are modified (added or removed)?
_Removing filter fields_
When a filter field is removed from the dashboard, any saved view that referenced that field will be updated to no longer include that field and any pre-configured values. For example, if you had a Department filter with some values such as Engineering, Product and Design in a saved view, removing the Department field from the dashboard filters entirely will remove the reference from any saved view too.
_Adding filters fields_
When the dashboard has a new filter field added, any pre-existing saved view will have no prior value for that field. If the user wants to incorporate the new field into any existing saved views, they must select the view by name, update the filters, then select the Update Existing View option.
﻿

auto-reject-applications:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Applications
# Auto-Reject Applications
14 min
Copy
This guide covers how to save time on application review using the Auto-Reject feature.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#auto-reject-overview)Auto-Reject overview
Auto-Reject allows you to reject candidates whose application submissions match certain conditions automatically. Rejection conditions apply to the selected application form for a given job. For example, if you have a location-based application question, you can configure a rule within the job posting to automatically reject candidates located outside of the targeted area. Please note that auto-reject rules can only be configured to run at the point of the candidate's application submission and can't be run at other stages in the interview process.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#how-to-set-up-an-auto-reject-configuration)How to set up an auto-reject configuration
To set up an Auto-Reject condition, navigate to Jobs > Jobs and select a job. Within the job, navigate to the Job Postings section, then to the Automation tab (you will need to click on a listing first to access the posting tabs).
The _Automation_ tab contains job-specific automation options including automatic application confirmation emails and auto-reject _._ Click Create a Configuration _,_ then configure the fields highlighted below.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/eaa9bc42e427728cab5a0927a5c45081.png)
﻿
**This screenshot highlights 6 key areas of auto-reject configuration setup:**
  1. Conditions will be run in order, and if a candidate is rejected by one, future conditions will be skipped. If a rejection condition is empty, it will be skipped.


  1. The Rejection Condition the based on application questions. Select a question and configure the conditional behavior. For example, if the rejection condition is 'Application Form's Name contains A', 'Aaron' would be rejected, Bob' would not be.


  1. The Archive Reason is added to the candidate profile when an application is auto-rejected using the rejection condition.


  1. Configure the number of business days to delay the rejection email to candidates.


  1. Select the rejection email template to send to the candidate. These must be preconfigured in Email Templates _._


  1. Select the from address the rejection email is sent from.


You can click + Create New Auto-Reject Rule to create additional auto-reject rules. Auto-reject rules will be applied to applications in the order they are listed in.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fda16761e55c3eadbdc5faaeaea288c2.png)
﻿
Once configured, and activated, Auto-Reject will evaluate all new applications. Auto-reject doesn't currently support applying the rule to historical applications.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#setting-up-a-rejection-condition)Setting up a Rejection Condition
When setting up your auto-reject conditions, first click Add Field to Match. You will be able to select a question on your application form and determine the answers that will then trigger the auto-reject tool.
Once you’ve selected your question, you’ll see the following options when determining an answer that would lead to the application being rejected automatically. This example is for a question with a multiple-choice answer field.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/53991ba5f52e5ecdec7e62f12f165919.png)
﻿
**Is empty/unassigned:** An answer has not been added or selected. Select this if you want to automatically reject a candidate if they have not answered the question.
**Is not empty/unassigned:** Any answer has been added in to respond to the application form. Selecting this will automatically reject any candidate who has answered the question.
**Equals:** Select or add in a value here. If the candidate has responded with the exact same answer, they will be rejected automatically.
Depending on the field type, you may see additional options. Here is another example where the field type is one that can be typed into directly, rather than selecting a specific answer, as above.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/47d900c578db43d7ac433bab0ece0da9.png)
﻿
**Contains:** The answer provided contains the text you add to the Add text to match field beside the drop-down menu. If the applicant’s answer contains this text, they will be rejected automatically.
**Starts with:** The application will be rejected if the answer provided on the application form starts with the text specified in the Add text to match field.
**Ends with:** The application will be rejected if the answer provided on the application form end with the text specified in the Add text to match field.
**Similar to:** If the answer provided is similar to the text provided in the Add text to fuzzy match field, then the application will be rejected. An example of a fuzzy match would be if localization was added to the Add text to fuzzy match field and the applicant included the word _local_ in their application.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#auto-reject-operators)Auto-Reject Operators
While configuring your auto-reject rules, you’ll see the operators And, Or and Not.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9c10e5c763b72f0c1c2cb40fb061b735.png)
﻿
﻿  

**And:** This option adds an All are True condition to the rule, so you can set multiple conditions and, so long as all of the conditions are met, the candidate will be rejected.
So in this example, the candidate will be rejected if they select No for both of the specified questions.
If they select _Yes_ for one of the two, they will not be rejected automatically.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7f8bc7bf26014835281029faac53de8b.png)
﻿
**Or:** This option adds an Any can be True condition to the rule. If any of the conditions listed directly underneath are met, then the candidate’s application will be rejected automatically.
So, in this example, if the candidate selects No for either question, their application will be rejected automatically.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6d5512ddbf0e403178d7fc2331249e49.png)
﻿
﻿  

**Not:** This option adds a The following is not true condition to the form. Candidates who do not meet the criteria specified in the rules will be rejected automatically.
So here, if the candidate does not select yes as their answer to the question, they will be automatically rejected.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7f4b00c3333497f5e5fe7efcabd85110.png)
﻿
﻿  

You can also add these operators within one another!
So if you were using the _Not_ operator and wanted to add multiple conditions that the candidate needs to meet, you can select the And operator once you’ve set up your first condition to then add in another.
Here in our example, you can see that both questions listed need to be answered _Yes_ in order for the candidate to pass through to the Application Review stage. If an application responds Yes to one question and No to the other, then they will be rejected automatically.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e08916b762be08263329d91fb031c593.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#how-to-edit-an-auto-reject-configuration)How to edit an auto-reject configuration
When there is an active configuration (and Auto-Reject is enabled), to edit you'll need to create a new draft condition. The active condition will continue to run until the draft condition is activated. To edit, click View Current Configuration, then Create a New Draft... Configure the draft condition, then click Activate _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/02980bbc8a7c9e6f85d11893f3808d44.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#turning-off-auto-reject-on-a-job-posting)Turning off auto-reject on a job posting
To deactivate an active configuration, switch off the Auto-Reject Enabled option within the Automation tab of the selected job posting.
Once disabled, the tag beside the Auto-Reject Application heading should turn red and say Disabled for your reference.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8b236d361a778d64d355282eb87c50d7.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c40e5108296fe4584d044a600ce52124.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#search-for-candidates-that-have-been-auto-rejected)Search for candidates that have been auto-rejected
If you'd like to review rejected candidates, you can find all archived candidates within the candidate pipeline of your job in the Archived view. You then add an advanced filter to only display auto-rejected candidates.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8df02919e16f4b11bde97dd84bc23158.png)
﻿
**You can search for those candidates by:**
  1. Navigating to the Archived candidates view in the candidate pipeline _._


  1. Checking the Advanced filter option.


  1. Searching for Auto-Rejected filter, and set it to equal Yes _._


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#auto-reject-information-on-candidate-profiles)Auto-reject information on candidate profiles
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/70a0f2a08088be9f254e397b6b9808f6.png)
﻿
**This screenshot highlights two key auto-reject activities on the candidate profile:**
  1. For delayed emails, you'll find a Scheduled Email activity under Current Activities _._ You have the options to view the scheduled email, modify it, and/or delete it.


  1. In the feed, you'll find a notification that the candidate was moved to the Archived stage by Ashby Bot.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#how-do-auto-reject-rules-interact-with-application)H**ow do auto-reject rules interact with application requests?**
If an application is submitted through an application request link, we don't check those applications against auto-reject or application limit rules. 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#can-i-reject-candidates-who-have-applied-multiple)Can I reject candidates who have applied multiple times?
You can set up application limits if you are seeing candidates apply multiple times for the same role or department. More on configuring these at [Application Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications#can-i-configure-auto-reject-rules-for-interview-st)Can I configure auto-reject rules for interview stages?
No, auto-reject rules can only be configured on the job posting and so will only be applied to applications at the point of submission via your job board.
﻿

report-view-options:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Getting Started with Analytics
# Report View Options
15 min
Copy
This document reviews the different report view options in Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#introduction)Introduction
Every report has View Options to allow for configuration and customization. These options vary by report type and whether Group By fields have been added.
You can find a report’s View Options just above the Results section.
![Screen Cast 2022-12-14 at 8.01.07 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/t7hpLErFQZrrMWwprd3qb_screencast2022-12-14at80107am.gif)
﻿
The View Options types can be easily changed by clicking on each tab (Line Chart, Bar Chart, Stacked Bar Chart, etc). In addition, the options underneath each View Options tab are unique to the type selected and may change.
![Selecting each tab changes the report visualization. The options underneath each tab change as well. For example: the Line Chart option shows the “Count As” drop down and the Stacked Bar Chart option shows “Count As”, “Y Axis”, and “Group Labels” drop downs. ](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/9hHJHCikUEYCS7do2BNpD_screencast2022-12-14at80339am.gif)
﻿
Selecting each tab changes the report visualization. The options underneath each tab change as well. For example: the Line Chart option shows the Count As drop down and the Stacked Bar Chart option shows Count As, Y Axis, and Group Labels drop downs.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#line-chart)Line Chart
Line charts make clean, basic reports. They do not stack, which means trends on individual components can be seen more clearly.
![This is a Count over Time report showing the number of internal applicants and referrals in the last 3 months. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/o_4V8KAE8z1YWxQCOPgp2_screenshot2022-12-14at81537am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This is a Count over Time report showing the number of internal applicants and referrals in the last 3 months.
You have the option to view the report results as a rate or a running total when you click the drop down for Count As.
![Line Chart results counted as a rate. ](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/o_4V8KAE8z1YWxQCOPgp2_screenshot2022-12-14at81537am.png)
Line Chart results counted as a rate.
﻿
![Line Chart results counted as a running total. ](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/5XRoUYkLHKeJbpp3gVvfh_screenshot2022-12-14at93425am.png)
Line Chart results counted as a running total.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#bar-chart)Bar Chart
Bar charts allow you to easily compare different categories of data. This View Option does not stack.
![This is a Count over Time report showing the number of referred applications each month over the last 6 months. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/hPPILgzrA94gs_nKYGxAa_screenshot2022-12-14at82815am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This is a Count over Time report showing the number of referred applications each month over the last 6 months.
When a Group By field is selected, one bar per group is added.
![This is the same Count over Time report as above, but the results are now grouped by Outcome. There is a bar for Active, Archived, and Hired applications.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/CAlwEEm0nwuCrhnV1hGqT_screenshot2022-12-14at83438am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This is the same Count over Time report as above, but the results are now grouped by Outcome. There is a bar for Active, Archived, and Hired applications.
We recommend avoiding fields that have many values, as it may make a cluttered view.
You have the option to view the report results as a rate or a running total when you click the drop down for Count As.
﻿  

![Bar Chart results counted as a rate. ](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/XUpM78aaoQDmZyVz9SWoQ_screenshot2022-12-14at92820am.png)
Bar Chart results counted as a rate.
﻿
![Bar Chart results counted as a running total. ](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/omYVV7p5l5b9bNomLE687_screenshot2022-12-14at92900am.png)
Bar Chart results counted as a running total.
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#stacked-bar-chart)Stacked Bar Chart
Stacked bar charts allow you to easily compare different categories of data, and one stack per group is added to the bar when a Group By field is selected.
![This is a Count over Time report showing the number of referred applications each month over the last 6 months, grouped by Outcome. ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/R6y89Uj3OjUv5VGv0lcc4_screenshot2022-12-14at91300am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This is a Count over Time report showing the number of referred applications each month over the last 6 months, grouped by Outcome.
In addition to the Count As drop down, the Stacked Bar Chart view options also includes Y Axis and Group Labels.
![% composition charts can be made if you select Show Percent in the “Y Axis” drop down. ](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ijIBdJAgwTruhxWwHw1LW_screencast2022-12-14at91957am.gif)
﻿
% composition charts can be made if you select Show Percent in the “Y Axis” drop down.
  * **Count As:** the report results can be be counted as a rate or a running total


  * **Y Axis:** the Y axis results can be shown as a count or as a percent


  * **Group Labels:** the group labels can be shown as a count, percent, or not at all


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#table)Table
The Table view option provides you with a raw table of data. You have the option to view the report results as a rate or a running total when you click the drop down for Count As.
![This is a Count over Time report as above, shown in the Table view.](https://cdn.zappy.app/3c6766dad2f63806ecf26231517eb637.png)
This is a Count over Time report as above, shown in the Table view.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#pivot-mode)Pivot Mode
Available on reports that have a Group By selection.
Allows easy pivoting on any of the Group By fields, and multiple pivots are permitted.
An example of collapsing and expanding the Application’s Outcome in the table.
![This report has Pivot Mode turned on and is pivoting on “Application’s Started At.”](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/LFlpGF7ltg-_MUApbO1CD_screenshot2022-12-14at103518am.png)
This report has Pivot Mode turned on and is pivoting on “Application’s Started At.”
﻿
![An example of collapsing and expanding the Application’s Outcome in the table. ](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/hdHq8fx94uiQPVQAJn01f_screencast2022-12-14at103844am.gif)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#row-totals-column-totals)Row Totals/ Column Totals
Row Totals and/or Column totals can be shown in the table when they are checked.
![An example of adding row totals and column totals. ](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/zKQMBuTPWmbUziX8xhGqb_screencast2022-12-14at104245am.gif)
An example of adding row totals and column totals.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#pie-chart)Pie Chart
Pie charts allow you to view your data in a circular graphic, divided into sections that each represent a proportion of the whole.
This View Option is only available on Segmentation reports.
You can measure the report results by Percent or Count. If you select count, the results are shown as a bar chart visualization.
You report results can be sorted Ascending, Descending, or None.
![A pie chart showing the % of applications by department over the last 6 months. ](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wjcKpzaKH6rAfRPMY300k_screenshot2022-12-14at105252am.png)
A pie chart showing the % of applications by department over the last 6 months.
﻿
![Screen Cast 2022-12-14 at 10.56.10 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/pClBflv89VxTUhjl11BB4_screencast2022-12-14at105610am.gif)
﻿
![Screen Cast 2022-12-14 at 10.59.18 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/2assaKUJ6JZtoEzMcjUuV_screencast2022-12-14at105918am.gif)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#number)Number
The Number view option allows you to see a total count and is a available on Custom Lists.
You can see that 173 interviews were held this month by selecting the **Number** View Option.
﻿  

![You can see that 173 interviews were held this month by selecting the Number View Option.  ](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/nSZKy0IvGcBzgQ-HZKMbA_screenshot2022-12-14at110634am.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#single-number)Single Number
**Ratio:** the number of all records relative to the number of records matching the selected Group By value, expressed as a ratio
![Screen Shot 2022-12-14 at 11.17.01 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Yj_PP-cIOu3zYWnVTwdbs_screenshot2022-12-14at111701am.png?format=webp)
﻿
Learn more about ratio reports in our [Ratio Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ratio-reports)﻿
**Percent:** the selected group’s value as a percent of all matched results, e.g. percent of hired applications relative to all application results
![Screen Shot 2022-12-14 at 11.17.27 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jwe42TSlQWzsuBENwh7zV_screenshot2022-12-14at111727am.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-view-options#can-i-adjust-the-color-palette-on-my-reports-i-wan)Can I adjust the color palette on my reports? I want to see my brand colours in charts I create.
You can! To do this, navigate to Admin, then Organization Setup > Default Visualization Colors. Here, you can set up your default visualization colors﻿ to use in reporting. Choose a minimum of 12 unique colors but up to a maximum of 32.
﻿

meeting-hours:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Initial Setup
# Meeting Hours
16 min
Copy
Meeting Hours allow you to set regular availability for scheduling interviews. Meeting hours can be set for the company, for individual interviewers, and for schedule interview activities. 
Please note that busy events on Google or Microsoft calendars will also sync over and block off availability if you have either integration configured. As such, any specific dates your interviewer is unavailable or OOO should be synced over and taken into account when showing availability.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#permissions-setting-meeting-hours)Permissions: Setting Meeting Hours
See the table below for information on the permissions needed to set company-wide, personal and interview-specific meeting hours.  
| Access Role  | Can set company-wide meeting hours?  | Can set personal meeting hours?  | Can set interview-specific meeting hours?  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌  | ✅  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌  | ✅  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
Only Organization Admins can amend company-wide meeting hours. Each user that has their calendar connected in Ashby will be able to adjust their own personal meeting hours. Those with Elevated Access - Admin access or higher are able to set specific meeting hours on schedule interview activities.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#set-company-meeting-hours)Set Company Meeting Hours
﻿
In Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > [Meetings & Time Zone](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/meetings-and-time/meeting-hours "Meetings & Time Zone"), you can set the meeting hours for the company as a whole.
If personal meeting hours or hours for the interview schedule activity have not been set, the hours listed here will show as the interviewer’s availability. Events marked as busy on the interviewer’s Google calendar will be taken into account when showing their availability.
You can click and drag on the schedule to add or remove meeting hours. Purple indicates available hours, whereas the white areas indicate times that are not available for scheduling.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/82d3d9089c4fb2d4c51847f4abae31fb.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#how-do-meeting-hours-work-with-interviewer-timezon)How do meeting hours work with interviewer timezones?
Meeting hours will show in the interviewer’s time zone, so if your interviewer is in MDT and the company meeting hours are set from 9:00am-5:00pm, this would be reflected as 9:00am-5:00pm MDT for that interviewer (provided they have not set their own personal meeting hours).
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#set-personal-meeting-hours)Set Personal Meeting Hours
﻿
Each user can set their own personal meeting hours by heading to Personal Settings > [Meeting Hours](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/meeting-hours "Meeting Hours"). They can check the Enable Custom Hours? checkbox and then set the hours they are available.
Meeting hours set here will override the availability set for company meeting hours if the user is specified as an interviewer. Conflicts on the user’s Google calendar will be taken into account when showing the interviewer’s availability.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1196b7e933dab139f1aaedfb85c23521.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#company-holidays)**Company Holidays**
On the Company Holidays tab, you can determine any company-wide or public holidays that are on the company calendar. Once company-wide holidays are added, the entire team will be marked as unavailable for interviews for the full day.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4b4be3de7da7ee41d4a419e9788a8b8a.png)
﻿
This means:
  * When using Manual Scheduling, interviewers will be shown as Unavailable for meetings on company holidays.


  * When using Direct Booking, candidates will not be able to schedule interviews on company holidays.


  * When requesting candidate availability, company holidays will not be selectable by candidates.


  * When requesting proposed schedules for debriefs, company holidays will be excluded from the proposed dates.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#time-zone)**Time Zone**
Here you can set your organization’s default time zone. This timezone will be used for any users who haven’t synced their Google Calendar in Ashby, or who haven’t specified their own time zone in their personal settings. You can read more on how time zones work [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#how-do-timezones-work-in-ashby)﻿.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b7481162fb5c4c14059cfc93806d9d0f.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#set-meeting-hours-for-a-schedule-interview-activit)Set Meeting Hours for a Schedule Interview Activity
See [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#meeting-hours-optional)﻿ for more information on setting meeting hours within schedule interview activities.
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#troubleshooting-availability)Troubleshooting Availability
﻿
After generating a direct booking link for an interview, you can use the Troubleshoot Availability option to check that availability is showing as expected. To access the Troubleshoot Availability option, click Actions on the activity in question, then Troubleshoot Availability _._ If you test your booking link and don't see your availability reflected correctly, this tool will help you diagnose why.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/37f717e4c2b80344eeb68833d6e5acde.png)
﻿
If you are seeing an option that is unavailable when it shouldn’t be, you can click on the exclamation point beside the time in question to see why.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/da5fce72ef7f3a5b63ca7ea448f8b879.png)
﻿
If unavailable times are showing a This time is outside user’s meeting hours message when troubleshooting, be sure to check the meeting hours for the schedule activity first. Then have the user or users listed as interviewers check their meeting hours in their personal settings. If either are active and set, they will override company meeting hours.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#how-can-i-ask-the-candidate-for-their-availability)How can I ask the candidate for their availability for an interview?
When setting up an interview for a candidate, you can create a candidate availability link. This link that will prompt the candidate to select when they are available. You can then schedule in the interview based on the availability they provide. You can read more on this at [Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links#candidate-availability)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#my-google-events-arent-blocking-off-my-availabilit)My Google events aren’t blocking off my availability - why?
If the event is on an external personal calendar that is shared with your company Google account, those events will not be taken into account. Those events would need to be added onto your company calendar to block off from your interview availability.
If the event is on your company Google calendar, check if the event is set to Free _._ Only events set to Busy will block your availability.
If interviews have been booked in Ashby but the calendar invites have not been sent out, those interviews will block availability for the interviewers on the panel.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#do-my-google-working-hours-translate-to-meeting-ho)Do my Google working hours translate to meeting hours in Ashby automatically?
They do not - we’d recommend adding in personal meeting hours that match your working hours in Google.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#how-do-timezones-work-in-ashby)How do timezones work in Ashby?
The timezone set on your account, along with your meeting hours , determines your availability for interviews. You can check the timezone on your user account in [Personal Info](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/personal-info "Personal Info"). If you have the Google Workspace or the Microsoft 365 integration in place, then your timezone will sync over from your Google or Microsoft calendar. 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2343671c3415f3ec6e29b707a3a79675.png)
﻿
You can also set interview activity-specific timezones in the Meeting Hours tab on each schedule interview activity. Setting a Primary Interview timezone allows you to ensure that any custom meeting hours you set for that schedule interview activity will be reflected in that timezone, even if interviewers have a differing timezone set in their personal settings.
_Schedule Activity Timezones_
You can also use the drop-down to select your timezone if it is not the same as the Organization’s default timezone (set in [Meetings & Time Zone](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/meetings-and-time/timezone "Meetings & Time Zone")) and it’s not syncing over from your Google Calendar.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7e65aa7a24e8f5f6ab0929e8f2d32e23.png)
﻿
If you do not see Synced from Google Calendar or Synced from Calendar in the Your Timezone field, this may be because the domain on your email does not match the domain of the Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 tenant integrated with Ashby.
If you have a secondary domain on your Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 tenant, please reach out to Support at support@ashbyhq.com to get this added.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ceabd4027f6bca2a3888a4fe5d3c9701.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#help-one-of-my-team-is-showing-as-available-for-in)Help! One of my team is showing as available for interviews all-day everyday.
Users can only set personal meeting hours if their Google Calendar is synced. If the user was added manually and not provisioned automatically via the Google Workspace integration, ask them to them sign out and then log back in using the Sign In with Google option. This should help ensure their calendar is synced and they will then be able to set personal meeting hours.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#can-i-sync-additional-personal-calendars-with-ashb)Can I sync additional personal calendars with Ashby?
﻿  

You can - you can read more on adding additional personal calendars [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours "here"). If you're looking to sync company holidays, we would recommend using the company holidays tool listed [above](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#company-holidays)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#can-i-add-meeting-buffers-to-avoid-back-to-back-in)Can I add meeting buffers to avoid back-to-back interviews?
You can! If you'd like to apply meeting buffers to interviews by default, check out [Setting Scheduling Defaults](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults)﻿. For more on adding meeting buffers to a schedule interview activity, check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours#candidates-are-only-seeing-a-limited-amount-of-ava)Candidates are only seeing a limited amount of availability, why?
The availability shown to the candidate does depend on your meeting hours and calendar availability but also on the settings for your scheduling interview activity or your scheduling defaults. Make sure to check the Minimum Scheduling Notice and Rolling Days Available settings to ensure that these are correct. For more on configuring this, check out [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿ and [Setting Scheduling Defaults](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults)﻿.
﻿  

﻿

sourcing-forms:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Sourcing & Outreach
# Sourcing Forms
12 min
Copy
Sourcing forms are a route to capture details of candidates that are interested in your company but have yet to actively apply. You can send out a link to your form or forms so candidates can sign up to be a part of your talent community and keep themselves in the loop on new opportunities.
Sourcing Forms are available on all plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#overview)Overview
Sourcing forms are now available to capture and maintain candidate interest, particularly if there isn’t a suitable open role at the time. You can also automate adding signed up candidates to your projects﻿ to create talent pools and assist with sourcing for future roles. 
If you’re planning to create a talent pool, we’d recommend setting up a project to add candidates to first. For more on setting up projects, check out [Projects](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/projects)﻿.
﻿
To create a form, navigate to Sourcing, then [Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/sourcing/forms/all "Forms"). Click + New to create a new sourcing form. Add in a title for your form and click Submit.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9612847b7567dbd560408a0220054f85.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#configuring-the-form)Configuring the form
Click Configure Form to start setting up your form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/909153512467d1dce93ed39fa92e8eff.png)
﻿
By default, the form will ask for the candidate’s name and email address. You can click Add Question to add additional fields for the candidate to complete.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2e8c75f3ea91ad94d69d16c2c280e9be.png)
﻿
You can add the following fields:
  * **Short Answer** Single line of unformatted text. LinkedIn url responses will be auto-populated on the candidate's profile.


  * **Long Unformatted Answer** Multiple lines of unformatted text.


  * **Phone** Only domestic or international phone numbers.


  * **Email** Only valid email addresses.


  * **Multiple Choice** Select a single option.


  * **Checkboxes** Select multiple options.


  * **Date** Only dates that can be selected via a date picker.


  * **Yes/No** Yes, no, or, if not required, no answer.


  * **Number** Only allows whole numbers (e.g. 1) or decimals (e.g. 1.5).


  * **Resume** Upload a single file and set it to the candidate's resume field. Can only be added once to a form.


  * **Candidate's location** Set the candidate's location. Can only be added once to a form.


  * **Other location** Allow another location to be added to the referral.


  * **URL** A clickable link to a website.


  * **Candidate's Education History** See the candidate's education history. Can only be added once to the form.


When adding the candidate's education history field to your form, you will be able to specify the information asked for. You can opt to include or exclude the school, degree, field of study, start and end date fields as needed. Check the Allow multiple entries checkbox to allow those completing the form to add multiple qualifications. You can also set a minimum and maximum number of entries using the Min and Max fields.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/f2NAhMfa7xJdZfczD0K___education-history-field-on-a-form.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Information added to this field will always complete the Education section of the candidate's profile. 
The fields you add to your form can also be reported on.
If your form is looking a little long, click Add Section to add a new section. You can click and drag questions across sections to organize your form as needed.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#automation-rules)Automation Rules
Automation rules can be added to your form setup. Candidates meeting specific criteria will automatically be added the projects you specify in Ashby on completion of the sourcing form.
To set up automation, click Configure Automation Rules _,_ then Create a Draft Configuration on the following pop-up.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d34b57ecd95121680ce73594e5138f64.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c625e1c8b388421e2490860d0676557e.png)
﻿
From there, create a draft automation by clicking + Add Field to Match in the Condition section to select the fields and responses that would signify the candidate should be added to the specified project. Use the Project drop-down to determine the project the candidate would then be added to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/17f73f1db296980d1390d7178a6dfba2.png)
﻿
To add additional automation rules, click + Create New Automation Rule _._ This option allows you to create additional sets of criteria that would result in the candidate being added to a different project (for example, adding them to a Customer Support project if their response confirms their interest in working in that department).
If no conditions are set, all candidates who submit the form will be added to your specified project.
Once all of your automation rules are in place for the form, click Activate Draft Configuration to enable the automation rules.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a6f491130d37af0d402c8f6b32bb7887.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#form-page-info)Form Page Info
In the Form Page Info section, you can add a public and internal title to your form, determine the source applied to candidates who are added to Ashby via your form and more.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/72dfc78de622f494e7526cc35656d6fe.png)
﻿
  1. **Title:** The name of your sourcing form. This is a public title that candidates will see.


  1. **Header Description:** This description is displayed below your title and above the form that the candidate completes.


  1. **Footer Description:** This description is displayed underneath the form fields that the candidate completes.


  1. **Success Message:** The message that will show to candidates after they have successfully submitted the form.


  1. **Submit Button Text:** Determine the wording on the button to submit the form.


  1. **Slug:** The last segment on the form’s page URL. If this is left blank, the form’s ID will be displayed instead. If you'd like to create a QR code for your sourcing form, click QR Code, then Download.


  1. **Internal Title:** An internal name for your sourcing form.


  1. **Source:** Determine the source applied to a candidate added to Ashby via the form. More on creating sources at [Source Types and Sources ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/source-types-and-sources)﻿.


  1. **Confirmation Email Template:** Determine the email template sent to candidates after they’ve submitted the form. The confirmation email will come from no-reply@ashbyhq.com. More on creating email templates at [Emails](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management)﻿. Please note: Only substitution tokens for candidate information can be used in a template for your sourcing form.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#publishing-the-form)Publishing the form
Once your form, automation and form settings are in place, you can publish your form by clicking the Draft drop-down under the form title and selecting Publish.
Once the form is published, the options to view the form and copy the form URL will then be available on the right-hand side of the page.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e5eb518a330c6e8bd662ff1a466ca940.png)
﻿
If you’d like to add the sourcing form to the bottom of your job board, copy the form URL using the Copy URL button and navigate to Admin > Job Board > [Themes (Job Board)](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/theme "Themes \(Job Board\)"). 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/aec0822472707065b9f3ba61c2033777.png)
﻿
You’ll then be able to add the form URL to a message in the Bottom Description field, either by adding the URL directly or by using the Link option in the toolbar to create a hyperlink.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d5c4ae7a81946f97c6cc11b5479102f5.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#can-i-allow-leads-to-upload-a-resume-and-auto-fill)Can I allow leads to upload a resume and auto-fill the form from there?
You can! Within the Settings tab, you can check the Show Autofill Field? checkbox. Then leads will see the option to upload their resume on your form. Be sure to include a Resume field in your form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5dd9e6e4ecbe3f0bd41932999f07c8fd.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#can-i-create-a-qr-code-for-my-sourcing-form)Can I create a QR code for my sourcing form?
You can, yes. Once you've added your slug into the Slug field within the Form Page Info area of the Settings section of your sourcing form, you'll see a QR Code button. Click this to then have the option to download a QR code leading to your sourcing form.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e2d7f0940e5644628c783c2da018f6c6.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms#can-i-create-a-copy-of-a-sourcing-form)Can I create a copy of a sourcing form?
You can. To create a copy of a sourcing form.
  1. Navigate to **Sourcing > Forms**


  1. Click on the form in question


  1. Click More then Copy


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kIYQ2g6mqZNA14yABLpIJ_newsletter-form-acent-ashby-2026-05-01-at-24310-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Automation rules are not copied over with the new form. New rules would need to be configured.
﻿  

﻿

interviews-and-scheduling:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviews-and-scheduling -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
# Interviews & Scheduling
5 min
Copy
Scheduling in Ashby is very flexible and we have options for both manually scheduling and for setting up automation in Ashby. In these guides, we walk through how to set up scheduling in Ashby, from the basic setup, right through to more advanced options and our Advanced Scheduling Add-On.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviews-and-scheduling#initial-setup)Initial Setup
A structured hiring process will help you identify & choose the best suited candidates. These guides will take you through the best practices for your scheduling setup, including setting up activities to set your interview settings in advance
🕙 [Meeting Hours](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours)﻿
🗓️ [Scheduling & Interviews: An Introduction](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/scheduling-and-interviews-an-introduction)﻿
📝 [Interview Plans](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interview-plans)﻿
📋 [Feedback Forms and Interviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/feedback-forms-and-interviews)﻿
📅 [Setting Scheduling Defaults](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults)﻿
⚙️ [Setting up Schedule Interview Activities](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿
🔗 [Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿
👥 [Interviewers: Limits, Assigning & Rescheduling](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits)﻿
🗓️ [Manually Scheduling Interviews](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/manually-scheduling-interviews)﻿
❓ [How do I use multi-day scheduling?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-use-multi-day-scheduling)﻿
Looking for more information on Ashby's scheduling features? Check out the [Scheduling in Ashby](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/course/ashby-scheduling "Scheduling in Ashby") course in Ashby Academy! For more on conducting interviews and writing up feedback, [Conducting Interviews with Ashby](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/course/interviewing-in-ashby "Conducting Interviews with Ashby") is also available.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviews-and-scheduling#advanced-setup)Advanced Setup
Looking to scale up your processes? Here are some more advanced options for your scheduling setup.
📅 [Keywords](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/keywords)﻿
🌎 [Public Booking Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/public-booking-links)﻿
👥 [Interviewer Pools](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewer-pools)﻿
🗓️ [Meeting Rooms & Meeting Room Pools](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools)﻿
💼 [Debriefs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs)﻿
