setting-up-schedule-interview-activities:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Initial Setup
# Setting up Schedule Interview Activities
39 min
Copy
This guide covers how to add schedule interview activities﻿ to job interview plans to set up a structured interview process within Ashby. These activities are typically used whenever you need to schedule interviews with specific interviewers, as opposed to take home activities, application reviews, or questionnaires which do not have interviewers assigned.
Research has found that running a structured interview process in which each candidate is assessed in the same way better predicts job performance and reduces the impact of bias on hiring decisions.
Ashby facilitates building a structured interview process by adding schedule interview activities to the interview plan associated with a given job. These activities contain complete interviewer instructions, feedback forms, and standardized candidate communication, ensuring consistency across the interview cycle.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#permissions-configuring-schedule-interview-activit)Permissions: Configuring Schedule Interview Activities
See the table below for information on the roles that have access to set up and edit schedule interview activities.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ✅*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ✅*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Elevated Access - Admin and Elevated Access - Admin Private users will only be able to edit the schedule interview activities on the roles they have access to.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#creating-a-schedule-interview-activity)Creating a schedule interview activity
If you’d like to establish settings to be used by default across schedule interview activities, check out our guide on [Scheduling Defaults](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-scheduling-defaults)﻿.
﻿
  1. Navigate to the job, then go to the **Interview Plan**. 


  1. Identify the interview stage that you'd like to add activities to, and click Add Activity to... and then select Schedule Interview.


  1. Next, move on to configuring the activity's various settings below.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#configuring-a-schedule-interview-activity)Configuring a schedule interview activity
There are six tabs within a schedule interview activity that can be configured, and each one is covered in its specific section below.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/790a689badabe11dfda297f7c03fa25f.png)
﻿
  * **Schedule:** Confirm the interview type (including feedback form), your interviewing panel, and any interview rooms.


  * **Communication:** Configure the calendar invites sent out to both candidate and interviewer.


  * **Meeting Hours:** You can set specific meeting hours for this interview activity. More on setting meeting hours at [Meeting Hours](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours)﻿.


  * **Direct Booking & Candidate Availability:** Set scheduling limits and a meeting buffer﻿ here.


  * **Reminders:** Configure any reminders that should be sent to interviewers and candidates pre-interview.


  * **Automation:** Automate the sending of a direct booking link or candidate availability link.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#schedule)Schedule
﻿
On the **Schedule** tab, you'll add one or more interviews along with their interview instructions and feedback forms. You’ll also be able to assign specific interviewers or pools of interviewers to each slot on the panel.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#selecting-the-interview-type)Selecting the interview type
The first step on the **Schedule** tab is selecting the interview type. You'll have the option to either link to an existing shared interview or create a new interview for the job in the **Add Event** section.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/54e7bae3228d0b56bda478f112e1ce3d.png)
﻿
We would recommend clicking + Add Shared Interview when determining your interview event to select from the list of interview types you set up in **Admin > Interviews & Scheduling >**[Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews"). The feedback form you have linked to your shared interview type will be used for the activity.
If you click + Create New Interview this will create a totally new interview type that can only be used for this specific activity. You can either assign a shared feedback form or create a new one specifically for the activity as part of the setup.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fe10966f7cb6d69548969aa3a37b426d.png)
﻿
You can add multiple events to a schedule interview activity; however, you will not be able to use direct booking links to schedule and should instead send out candidate availability links. Multiple events may be used when you want to schedule a series of activites all at once, such as a virtual panel event with multiple rounds of interviews with various interviewers. For more on scheduling activities with multiple interview events, please check out [How do I use multi-day scheduling?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-use-multi-day-scheduling)﻿
Indicators in the top-left corner show whether direct booking links or candidate availability links can be generated for the activity automatically. If these show in green, you can send out direct booking links and/or candidate availability links to schedule in your interview.
More on both direct booking and candidate availability links in [Direct Booking & Candidate Availability Links.](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4461ef8ceada9fe4a270d4b724bdbdb7.png)
﻿
If you're adding multiple interviews within one schedule template and are setting Zoom, Google Meet, or Microsoft Teams as the location, a unique meeting link will be generated for each calendar invite sent out to the candidate. 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#assigning-interviewers)Assigning interviewers
Once you’ve configured your interview type or types, you can then assign interviewers so Ashby can determine the calendars that availability is being pulled from for direct booking links or manual scheduling.
When you click Interviewers within the **Configure** section, you’ll be able to determine how many slots are on the panel, and either assign specific users to those slots or select a pool of interviewers if multiple interviewers could take one slot on that panel. 
For more on assigning interviewers, check out [Interviewers: Assigning, Rescheduling and Setting Interviewer Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits)﻿﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a0d49a44fa3b4a61ee11a487a4057b5e.png)
﻿
If you have a rotating interviewer pool, and you don't know up front who will be conducting a given interview, you can create a separate schedule interview activity within the same interview stage for each interviewer. You can then remove the schedule interview activities that don’t need to be used for that specific candidate once they’re moved to that stage.
Along with assigning your own pools to interviewer slots, you’ll also have the option to select Hiring Team Roles and pull users with those roles on job considerations to be on the panel.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/198f5a1492f18ed6ebc56d12a5ce40f9.png)
﻿
Hiring team roles are assigned within each job’s settings, and these roles are not pulled from the permissions set on a user’s profile on the Employees page.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#meeting-rooms)**Meeting rooms**
If you have meeting rooms synced over via your Google Workspace integration, you can configure a default room to be used for your schedule interview activity. Click Set Default Room to specify your interview room.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c9e1ff00d0e1959d6e55843f990614c1.png)
﻿
If multiple rooms could be used, you can also set up a meeting room pool. More on this at [Meeting Room Pools](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-rooms-and-meeting-room-pools)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#timing-and-sequencing)**Timing and sequencing**
The ability to set **Timing** settings on schedule interview activity events is only available if you have the [Advanced Scheduling Automation Add-On](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/advanced-scheduling-automation-add-on)﻿ or are an Enterprise customer. This is a part of the auto-schedule feature set.
If you are an Enterprise customer or Plus customer with the Advanced Scheduling Automation add-on, you may also see a Timing button under the **Configure** section.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bxI8Rx3B8G2HR_OZMYvmL_group-product-manager-acent-ashby-2026-04-01-at-33250-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Selecting this button allows you to indicate whether an event under your schedule interview activity must be scheduled at a fixed time. Additionally, if you have multiple events as a part of this interview activity (such as a panel with multiple interview sessions) you can determine the sequence in which they should occur, if any. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/64YJJDdlbt0Y6y3f6QAvx_group-product-manager-acent-ashby-2026-04-01-at-42348-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#communication)Communication
On the **Communication** tab, you can modify the details of both the Candidate and Interviewer calendar invites.
![Communication Settings.jpg](https://cdn.zappy.app/b35c994bca4fb9293d8dc6646a54590d.png)
﻿
**Expand for more details on the Communication tab**
**﻿  
**
  * The Calendar dropdown menu lets you select which calendar the calendar events will be scheduled to. All calendar events for interviewers and interviewees are added to this calendar. The calendar cannot be changed on rescheduling. If you are not permitted to add events to this calendar, events will be added to your personal calendar instead. We would recommend keeping this as Default Interview Calendar, or selecting the name of the shared calendar you have set up to add interview events to.


  * The Location dropdown menu allows you to add physical or remote location details. Set custom text, or select one of the integrated options. The integrated options will automatically generate a unique video conference link and include it in the invite. Current video conference integrations include Google Meet, Microsoft Teams and Zoom. You can also choose to set a Phone Call as the location, which will pull in the candidate’s primary phone number.


  * The Is Private? toggle lets you set invites as private. When toggled on, the calendar invite details (e.g. title, attendees) are hidden from others and interviewers are only marked as busy


  * The Direct Booking Email Template dropdown allows you to choose an email template. This [email template](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-links)﻿ will be used when sending a Direct Booking request from the candidate profile. 


  * The Candidate Availability Email Template dropdown allows you to choose an email template. This email template will be used when sending a Candidate Availability request from the candidate profile.


  * The Candidate Confirmation Email Template dropdown allows you to select an email template. This email template will be used when a confirmation is sent out once an interview has been scheduled. Once you’ve selected your email template, you’ll then see the option to automatically send this confirmation email if the candidate books via a direct booking link and the option to select a sender.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fd063d13c7d998d54fa69723c78fb72a.png)
﻿
  * Please note: When a user schedules an interview manually for the first time, if a confirmation email is set within the interview activity, the confirmation email will default to being sent. This can be switched off in the Communications tab when manually scheduling the interview. If the user chooses to turn off the email confirmation, confirmations will default to being off for the future interviews that user schedules (unless they opt to switch them on again).


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1382dc1f1651a3983ac7e85131433e09.png)
﻿
  * The Invite to Candidate Template and Invite to Interviewers Templates let you enter the title and description for each calendar invite, for both candidates and for the interviewers on your panel. Use tokens to fill in standard details, or add entirely custom text that the candidate will see.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#meeting-hours-optional)Meeting hours (optional)
On the **Meeting Hours** tab, you can set a primary interview time zone as well as any custom meeting hours for this interview. These meeting hours will be used when determining what interview times are available to candidates when they reach this interview stage. By default, meeting hours are set to 9 to 5 in the interviewer's timezone, but you can set a primary interview timezone to override individual timezones.
Setting specific meeting hours for an interview activity isn’t commonly used. Relying on the interviewer’s calendar availability or their own meeting hours is typically enough. You can read more on this at [Meeting Hours](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/meeting-hours)﻿ .
﻿
To set meeting hours for a schedule interview activity, head to the **Interview Plan** section of your job, then click Modify on the schedule interview activity in question. In the **Meeting Hours** tab, you can toggle on Enable Custom Meeting Hours?to set specific hours for that interview activity only.
This will override the availability set for the company meeting hours and the meeting hours set in the user’s personal settings. Conflicts on the user’s Google Calendar will still be considered when displaying availability.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/00e344bafed063db55af676d576f6655.png)
﻿
Looking for how to update the timezone on a particular interview? See [How are interview time zones determined and how do I change them?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-are-interview-time-zones-determined-and-how-do-i-change-them)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#direct-booking-and-candidate-availability-optional)Direct booking and candidate availability (optional)
﻿
If you're using direct booking or candidate availability links, you have options to set parameters around when meetings can be scheduled and how much candidate availability should be submitted. You have the following options:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6fd075d7a47abfe9a0c25607183b3baf.png)
﻿
**Enable Public Booking Link:** By default, booking links are unique to the candidate and the interview they are scheduling. Enable this option if you’d like to create a public link that you can send to any candidate or lead. This will then schedule them in for the interview, even if they haven’t been considered for the job or added to Ashby previously. More on this at [Public Booking Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/public-booking-links)﻿﻿
**Minimum Scheduling Notice:** Here you can determine how far in advance a candidate can schedule to ensure that interviewers have appropriate notice.
**Rolling Days Available:** Here you can determine how far into the future the candidate can schedule. If you set the number of days to 14 for example, the candidate would only be able to book within the next two weeks.
**Direct Booking Availability Increments:** This setting allows you to determine the frequency of available time slots. By default this is set to 30 minutes, so if an interviewer was available from 9am to 11am, the slots would show as 9am, 9:30am, 10am, 10:30am.
**Minimum Days of Availability:** Use this field to configure the minimum number of days a candidate can provide when submitting their availability.
**Allow Candidates To Suggest Times?** : If enabled, the candidate can share their availability and suggest interview times if they can’t find a suitable time when booking with a direct booking link.
**Meeting Buffers:** allow you to add time before and after an interview to avoid Ashby interviews being scheduled in back-to-back. We would recommend using these if you need breaks between interviews.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/208d5f658c75aa16ea7ea80f3089df42.png)
﻿
Here you can also determine the meeting organizer, the user who should receive notifications about candidate’s scheduling or adding in their availability. More on this at [What is a Meeting Organizer?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-meeting-organizer)﻿﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8cbce98ae42b4099974cf13ad7b739f5.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#interview-reminders)Interview Reminders
This section covers reminders about the upcoming interview. If you are looking to set up scorecard or feedback reminder notifications, these are sent automatically if the interview is set as required. You can read more on setting up interviews at [Feedback Forms and Interviews ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/feedback-forms-and-interviews)﻿.
﻿  

﻿
If you have an upcoming interview with multiple events in one day, you can opt to either limit the reminders to one per day, so all events are included in one reminder email, or send a reminder for each calendar event.
Multiple calendar invites are sent if there’s a gap between interview events.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#set-up-interview-reminders)Set up interview reminders
Two default reminder email templates are included for you to use immediately. You can customize these templates or create new email templates in Admin > Organization Setup > [Email Templates](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-templates "Email Templates"). More on setting up these templates can be found at [Email Template Creation and Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management)﻿.
Interview reminders can be configured in the Reminders tab, either for all interviews in Admin> Interviews & Scheduling > [Scheduling Defaults](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/scheduling-defaults/communication "Scheduling Defaults"), or within specific schedule interview activities on the interview plans of your jobs.
![Setting up a reminder in the Schedule Defaults section of Admin](https://cdn.zappy.app/b1b691426f7001b450e1a6bdef9a6d28.png)
﻿
You can also amend or add additional interview reminders when scheduling a candidate’s interview via Manual Schedule.
Click Add Candidate Reminder or Add Interviewer Reminder to get started. You can then specify when the notification should be sent, who the notification is from and the template used for the reminder.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8d98ff649e05f5584ecd3ed156801ae4.png)
﻿
If you haven’t already, ensure that an email template is set up for direct booking links or candidate availability links in the Communication tab of your schedule interview activity.
This is the email that will be sent out to the candidate automatically when they’re moved to this stage of your pipeline. You will have the option to undo the automation each time it is triggered.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b8f601149cc870b2ec886689c9271d98.png)
﻿
﻿  

  1. **Send:** Determine how the reminder should be sent. For candidates, you will only have the option to send an email. If you have the integration for Slack in place, you can opt to send an email or Slack reminder to interviewers.


  1. **From:** Determine the sender email address for the candidate’s reminder. This can either come from the default no-reply email address or the scheduler’s email address.


  1. **When:** Configure the number of days, minutes or hours prior to the interview that the reminder should be sent.


  1. **Using Template:** Select the reminder template to use.


  1. **+ Add Candidate Reminder:** Create or add an additional reminder.


  1. If your interview has multiple events (and potentially gaps between those events), you can opt to send one reminder for all calendar events that day or a reminder for each event.


  1. **Reset:** If you have Scheduling Defaults in place, click this option to revert back to those defaults.


When the reminder email is sent, the substitution tokens within your interview template will be pre-filled with the appropriate details.
![An example of a candidate’s interview reminder.](https://cdn.zappy.app/ff9957869ef832027ae1d889d823ef91.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#multiple-interview-events)Multiple interview events
If you have an upcoming interview with multiple events in one day, you can opt to either limit the reminders to one per day, so all events are included in one reminder email, or send a reminder for each calendar event.
Multiple calendar invites are sent if there’s a gap between interview events.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6929e475191bb9ad4038907aa75fca6a.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#configuring-candidate-scheduling-reminders)Configuring candidate scheduling reminders
If you are generating candidate availability or direct booking links from your schedule interview activities, you can also configure candidate scheduling reminders on the Reminderstab of your schedule interview activity. These reminders are automatically sent if the candidate has yet to take the following action:
**Candidate Availability Link:** If the candidate hasn't submitted their availability by the time specified in your reminder configuration, then an email or text will be generated from the template selected and sent to them as a reminder.
**Direct Booking Link:** If the candidate hasn't scheduled an interview using the direct booking link provided by the time specified in your reminder configuration, then an email or text will be generated from the template selected and sent to them as a reminder.
Please note that if the candidate submits availability or schedules using their direct booking link, the reminders will be cancelled automatically.
For SMS reminders, you will need to have candidate texting enabled on your account. For more on this, check out [Candidate Texting Onboarding](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-texting-onboarding)﻿ and [Candidate Texting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/EQo_-candidate-texting)﻿.
To add a candidate scheduling reminder, click + Availability Request Reminder or + Direct Booking Reminder to create the reminder. From here you can then set your reminder settings.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/KYS98MrZw1wlezOy_nQAZ_candidate-scheudling-reminder.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When configuring your scheduling reminders, you can configure the following:
**Send:** How the reminder is sent to the candidate. Either via SMS or email.
**From:** Determine the sender of the email. This can be the meeting organizer or a no-reply email.
**When:** Configure when the reminder should be sent. This can be specified in days, hours or minutes since the request was sent.
**Using Template:** Select the email or text template to use to generate the content of the reminder.
Default reminder templates for candidate availability and direct booking link schedule reminders are available within Admin > Organization Setup > Email Templates. These can be edited as needed.
You can set up multiple reminders too. After you've finished setting up your reminder, click + Availability Request Reminder or + Direct Booking Reminder to add another one.
Reminders will only be created and sent if the candidate availability link or direct booking link has been sent directly via Ashby. If the link has been copied and shared with the candidate outside of an email from Ashby, then reminders will not be sent.
Once you've sent the link to the candidate, you will see the scheduled reminders on the interview activity on the candidate's profile. These can be manually deleted as needed. Hover over the tab with the bell icon to view when the reminders are due to be sent. To make amendments, click on the tab.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vhQa7Zm_ffqCxBkPHVJFz_ariadne-shepard-product-marketing-manager-acent-ashby-2026-02-02-at-31903-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Click the trash can icons beside any reminders you'd like to remove on the Manage Scheduled Reminders pop up that follows. You can click Delete All to remove all of them.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/PrU2-ZpxWWOfS5YpKzy1l_ariadne-shepard-product-marketing-manager-acent-ashby-2026-02-02-at-32052-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#automation)Automation
﻿
When setting up or amending your schedule interview activity, click on the Automation tab. Then check the Automate on Stage Entry checkbox.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f3065da31b1215f2f9ba261b0ed06f62.png)
﻿
From there, you’ll be asked to specify the automation type (whether you’d like to send out a direct booking link or a candidate availability link automatically).
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a6b27da478cad29ca875a953b5a9af26.png)
﻿
If you haven’t already, ensure that an email template is set up for direct booking links or candidate availability links in the Communication tab of your schedule interview activity.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4cc2939c73e3d060f3737f1a67006a6f.png)
﻿
This is the email that will be sent out to the candidate automatically when they’re moved to this stage of your pipeline. You will have the option to undo the automation each time it is triggered.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/234a5e6c08a706c882fd0a59f9c585ce.png)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#how-do-i-assign-interviewers)H**ow do I assign interviewers?**
For a full guide on assigning interviewers to interviewer slots, check out [Interviewers: Assigning, Rescheduling and Setting Interviewer Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/interviewers-assigning-rescheduling-and-setting-interviewer-limits)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#are-interviewers-required-for-a-schedule-interview)Ar**e interviewers required for a schedule interview activity?**
Yes, interviewers do need to be assigned to interviewer slots for a schedule interview activity. Changes will not be saved to your schedule interview activity until interviewers are assigned to all interviewer slots or the empty interviewer slots have been deleted. If interviewer slots are not created for a schedule interview activity, you can only schedule this interview via the candidate availability or manual schedule options.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#why-should-i-set-up-schedule-interview-activities)Why should I set up Schedule Interview Activities?
Adding schedule interview activities to your job or job template’s interview plans will help to automate your scheduling process. You can set up the interview type, interviewer and interview location in advance. From there, when a candidate is moved to that stage in your pipeline, you’ll be prompted to schedule an interview and all of the interview settings will be generated automatically. If direct booking links or candidate availability links can be generated, then they will be produced automatically. 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#if-i-add-a-new-schedule-interview-activity-to-my-j)If I add a new schedule interview activity to my job's interview plan, will candidates already in that stage have the activity added to their job consideration?
Yes, the interview activity will be added to candidates already in that stage. With other activity types (such as _Send Email_), the activity will not be added to job considerations already in that stage.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#why-does-an-interview-slot-in-my-schedule-template)W**hy does an interview slot in my schedule template show that the slot is invalid when I add an interviewer into that slot?**
This can happen if the interviewer:
  * Is a service account


  * Has their user account is disabled in Ashby


  * Is globally paused for all interviews


  * Doesn’t have a Google Workspace or Microsoft calendar


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#why-am-i-seeing-two-calendar-events-for-an-intervi)W**hy am I seeing two calendar events for an interview on my personal calendar?**
When booking in an interview and sending out calendar invites, calendar events are sent out to three calendars. Separate calendar events are sent out for interviewers and candidates so party-specific interview information can be included.
  * One for the calendar the overall interview is scheduled on.


  * One for the interviewer’s calendar*.


  * One for the candidate’s calendar


*_If there are multiple interviewers on the panel, an event will be sent out for each interviewer’s individual calendar._
If the overall interview is scheduled on an interviewer’s personal calendar, they will receive the calendar event for the calendar the overall interview is scheduled on along with the calendar event for them as an interviewer.
To avoid multiple events, ensure that the shared calendar is selected when setting up a schedule interview activity and when booking in one-off interviews. Once scheduled, the calendar the interview is scheduled on cannot be changed. The interview would need to be cancelled and rebooked.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#what-does-default-interview-calendar-mean)Wh**at does ‘Default Interview Calendar’ mean?**
When setting up a schedule interview activity, if you have a shared calendar in place, you will see this option, along with the name or names of shared calendars you have set up as part of your [Google Workspace integration](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/marketplace/google/shared-calendars "Google Workspace integration").
If only one shared calendar is set up in your account, the ‘Default Interview Calendar’ is referring to that shared calendar, as it will automatically be set as the default. If you have set up multiple shared calendars, you can select the default [here](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/marketplace/google/shared-calendars "here") within the Google Workspace integration settings.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/641c57f3e67a860595be15e80887097d.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#ive-updated-the-interview-type-on-a-schedule-inter)I've updated the interview type on a schedule interview activity in my job’s interview plan. What happens to scheduled interviews?
If you change the interview type on your schedule interview activity the changes will be applied to candidates who are currently in the interview stage, as well as candidates entering that stage moving forward. If the interview has already been scheduled for the candidate, the interview type will not be updated and the interview would need to be rescheduled to update the interview type as needed.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#what-happens-to-scheduled-interviews-when-i-make-c)Wh**at happens to scheduled interviews when I make changes to the feedback form or briefing assigned to them?**
If you make changes to the shared feedback form on the [Feedback Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-forms "Feedback Forms") page or to the briefing on the [Interviews](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interviews "Interviews") page, any interviews with that feedback form assigned will reflect those changes.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#can-i-copy-an-existing-activity-from-an-interview)Can I copy an existing activity from an interview plan stage on one job to an interview plan stage on another?
You can, yes! When you are on a job’s interview plan and click Add Activity To… on an interview plan stage you’ll see the option to add a new activity and a Copy Activities option.
Click Copy Activities, then select the job, interview plan, interview stage you’d like to copy activities from. Choose the activities you’d like to copy. The selected activities will be copied into the original stage you chose.
﻿
Once an activity is copied over, if the activity uses a shared interview type or feedback form (more on those at What are ‘Shared’ Interviews, Application Forms, and Feedback Forms?), then those will be reference in the copy. Any custom interview types or feedback forms will be copied over and can be amended without impacting the original activity that was copied.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#on-the-direct-booking-candidate-availability-tab-w)O**n the Direct Booking & Candidate Availability tab, which date determines the dates available in the **_**Rolling Days Available**_**option?**
This is calculated from the date the candidate is viewing the calendar on the direct booking link. For example, if a candidate goes to the booking link today and the rolling days available is set to 7, the candidate will see availability up to 7 days from today. If they view the link again tomorrow, they’ll see dates up to 7 days from tomorrow.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#can-i-assign-multiple-potential-locations-to-a-sch)Can I assign multiple potential locations to a schedule interview activity? For example, Zoom or a phone call?
You can only assign one location to a schedule interview activity or direct booking link. It’s not currently possible to assign multiple locations, or set conditional locations. If your interview has two locations (for example, one interview happening over Zoom and the other in-person), you can create two schedule interview activities, one for each location.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities#for-candidate-availability-links-can-i-specify-a-w)F**or candidate availability links, can I specify a window in which candidates can add their availability? For example from 23rd November to 15th December? Or up to 2 weeks away (excluding Wednesdays)?**
Yes, you now have the option to specify a window of dates that candidates can submit their availability in. When preparing to send out your candidate availability link, click Schedule on the interview activity on the candidate’s profile, then hover over Request candidate availability. From here, click Edit Availability Window.
Within the Edit Availability Window pop-up, select either Rolling Days or Custom Date Range _:_
Rolling Days:A dynamic window, starting from the earliest available interview date. This will adjust depending on when the candidate clicks on the link. So if the window is set to be 14 days and the candidate clicks the link on the 1st, they will see suitable availability up until the 15th. If they then opt to go back and add their availability on the 3rd, they will be able to add in availability up until the 17th of the month.
If you see a date greyed out, this is because it is outside of your minimum notice - you can hover over it to confirm.
Custom Date Range: Set a specific start and end date for availability, or just an end date if you need the candidate to book before a specific day. The specified range will remain the same regardless of when the candidate visits the page.
Once you’ve established your scheduling window, you can then finalize your availability window. If you’d like to remove or add dates within the availability window, you can click on them on the Finalize Availability calendar.
Company holidays and weekends will be excluded by default. You can then click Save and Copy Link to confirm the availability window and copy the candidate availability link to send out to the candidate.
If you make changes to your availability window, you will need to click Save and Copy Link in order for the changes to apply to the existing candidate availability link.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/14112b3563df189611795b781ca8a9a4.png)

Analytics Glossary
# Greenhouse Subject & Field Definitions
17 min
Copy
If you're an Ashby Analytics customer using Greenhouse, here you can find a list of the subjects and filters available to you, along with definitions for your reference.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-subject-and-field-definitions#subject-definitions)Subject Definitions  
| Subject  |  Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Application  |  A candidate’s record on a specific job.   |  
| Prospect Application  |  Leads/prospects in Greenhouse. These prospects can be converted to candidates.   |  
| Job  |  A requisition.   |  
| Interview  |  Scheduled interviews in your ATS. Interviews can be counted as one per stage (ie. an onsite panel with 4 different interviews will count as 1 onsite interview) or one per scheduled interview (ie. an onsite panel with 4 different interview sill count as 4 interviews).   |  
| Interviewer Participation  |  Scheduled interviewers. Each interviewer counts as an interview participation. These tie closely to feedback forms.   |  
| Interview Stage Transition  |  Moves to and from stages. Accidental stage moves will be detected and counted.   |  
| Sequence  |  Sequences initiated through Gem.   |  
| Feedback Form  |  Scorecards in Greenhouse. We pull in the person who submitted, when they submitted, and the overall recommendation.   |  
| Opening  |  Headcount for a requisition.   |  
|  Candidate  |  Candidates in Greenhouse.   |  
| Diversity Survey  |  Custom diversity surveys that are created in Greenhouse. This is not attached to EEOC responses since those have a very strict formatting, whereas the diversity surveys are customized per organization.   |  
| Sourcing Activity   |  Sourcing activities initiated through Gem.  |  
﻿

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-subject-and-field-definitions#fields-definitions)Fields Definitions
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-subject-and-field-definitions#application-fields)Application Fields

| Hired At  | The point in time at which a candidate was hired for a job.  |  
| Archived At  | The point in time at which a job consideration was marked as archived.  |  
| Consideration Ended At  | The point in time at which an application was either marked as ‘Hired’ or ‘Archived’.  |  
| Candidate  | The individual candidate of this application.  |  
| … Stage Group Interview At  | The time of the first scheduled interview in a given stage group.  |  
| Passed Through Stage Group/Name  | Allows filtering applications that have passed through a certain stage group/name, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will only return applications that have passed through that stage group/name, not applications that skipped the stage group/name. Note, passed through stage group/name will include candidates currently in the stage group/name.  |  
| Reached Stage Group/Name  | Allows filtering applications that have reached a certain stage group/name, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will return all applications that have passed through this stage group/name or have skipped this stage/stage name but passed through a later stage group/ name.  |

File-based Migrations
# Historical Import Migrations
5 min
Copy
Follow this guide to import your historical data from your previous ATS into Ashby
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/historical-import-migrations#import-overview)Import Overview
File-based migrations are a two-step process:
  1. Importing active candidates and job considerations so they can be added to open jobs


  1. Importing historical data for your records and reporting purposes


Below, you will find a table listing the supported ATS options for which we can run a file-based migration and where you can submit a request to export your data.  
| **Legacy ATS**  | **Supported Data**  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Lever  | https://help.lever.co/hc/en-us/requests/  |  
| iCIMS  | https://careers.icims.com/customer-team  |  
| Team Tailor  | https://www.teamtailor.com/en/contact/  |  
| SmartRecruiters  | https://www.smartrecruiters.com/about-us/contact-us/  |  
| Workable  | https://help.workable.com/hc/en-us/requests/new  |  
| Jobvite  | https://help.jobvite.com/hc/en-us/requests/  |  
| Comeet  | https://help.comeet.co/  |  
| Pinpoint  | https://www.pinpointhq.com/contact/  |  
| Paycor  | https://www.paycor.com/services-support/  |  
| Homerun  | https://help.homerun.co/en/  |  
| Gem ATS  | Reach out to your CSM at Gem or Gem's support team for assistance  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/historical-import-migrations#importing-active-candidates)Importing Active Candidates
If you have open roles in your previous ATS with active candidates, we would recommend setting up the departments, teams and jobs you’re actively hiring for manually in Ashby so you can continue hiring activities with minimal downtime.
For more information on creating jobs, check out [Creating & Posting Jobs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/l-NcIsZrB9ztdr4U3idZr)﻿ . Once you’ve set up these jobs in Ashby, you can import your active candidate data using the CSV import tool. You can access the CSV import tool by navigating to Admin > Data Management > Bulk Data Import. More on this at [Bulk Import Options](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bulk-import-options)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/historical-import-migrations#exporting-historical-data)Exporting Historical Data
To import historical data, you will need to export your data from your previous ATS. Once you’ve received your exported data from your previous ATS, your CSM or Onboarding team will send you a link via SendSafely so you can upload your exported files.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/historical-import-migrations#importing-historical-data-into-ashby)Importing Historical Data into Ashby
Once we’ve received your files, our engineering team will get to work on the import.
We import the following data:  
| **Legacy ATS**  | **Supported Data**  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Lever  | • Historical Jobs • Candidates • Applications • Resumes • Users • Notes • Interview Feedback • Interview Stages  |  
| iCIMS  |  Job (Optional) • Title • External Req ID  Candidate • Name • Email • LinkedIn • Phone number • Added at date  • Source • Credited to • Resume • User-selected custom fields  Application • Submitted at • Last activity • Source • Credited to • Recruiter • Archive Reason • Interview stage (mapped to Lead, Active, Hired, or Archived)  Offer • Offer status • Created at • Decided at • Offer custom fields  Forms Selected forms will be imported as candidate notes. The note's created at date will be the date of submission, so they should show chronologically in the feed. The author of the note will be the submitter of the form (if they exist as a user in Ashby).  Interviews A record of the interview, all meetings of the interview with times and attendees will be imported as a candidate note. The note's created at date will be of the time of the first meeting in the interview, so they should show chronologically in the feed.  |  
| Teamtailor  |  _We import the following data_ • Candidates ◦ Name, email, phone, social links, tags, source ◦ Notes, Feedback (imported as notes) Candidates will be matched against existing candidate profiles (based on email address).If a matching candidate profile isn’t found, a new candidate will be created.  • Applications (added to Ashby as job considerations) Existing applications will be searched for when importing applications - if an existing job consideration isn’t found in Ashby a new one is created. If a new job consideration is created, it will be added to one of the following stages of the job’s interview plan: Lead, Application Review, Hired, Archived. If an existing application already exists, it will not be updated with the historic information (in the case of the candidate’s application being moved forward in Ashby, rendering the imported information obsolete).  • Jobs (optional) Jobs will be matched against the job titles of existing jobs before creating a new one. If a new job is created, then they will follow the default interview plan on your Ashby account.As far as Team and Location go, they will be added to the job if a match is found when importing. If no match is found, a generic location and team is used. If jobs are not imported, then applications will be imported to a generic job.  _We will not import the following:_ • Job Postings • Interview Plans • Openings  |  
| SmartRecruiters  |  _What do we import?_ • Jobs • Users • Candidates ◦ Name, email, phone, location ◦ Notes, Feedback (imported as notes) • Applications (with rejection reasons) ◦ Stages are mapped to default interview plan stages5  _What don't we import?_ • Candidate sources • Files • Resumes  _Additional Information_ • Stages will be mapped to matching default interview plan stage • Feedback will be added as notes on the candidate profile  |  
| Workable  |  _We will import the following_ • Jobs • Candidates (including tags if these are in the candidate CSV file) Interview stages for candidates aren’t mapped due to lack of data provided from Workable. Therefore we import candidates into the following stages: Application Review, Hired, or Archived.  • Applications • Messages and Comments (these are added as notes in Ashby) • Ratings (these are mapped as scorecards in Ashby) • Resumes  |  
| Jobvite  |  • Jobs • Candidates ◦ Name, email, resume, offers, phone, location • Applications • Notes • Feedback  _What don't we import?_ •Candidate sources   |  
| Comeet  |  _We import the following_ • Candidates (including feedback listed as a note, notes, tags, resume, email, phone, social links and source) Candidates will be matched against existing candidate profiles (based on email address). If a matching candidate profile isn’t found, a new candidate will be created.  • Applications (added to Ashby as job considerations) Existing applications will be searched for when importing applications - if an existing job consideration isn’t found in Ashby a new one is created. If a new job consideration is created, it will be added to one of the following stages of the job’s interview plan: Lead, Application Review, Hired, Archived. If an existing application already exists, it will not be updated with the historic information (in the case of the candidate’s application being moved forward in Ashby, rendering the imported information obsolete).  • Jobs Jobs will be matched against the job titles of existing jobs before creating a new one. If a new job is created, then they will follow the default interview plan on your Ashby account. As far as Team and Location go, they will be added to the job if a match is found when importing. If no match is found, a generic location and team is used. If jobs are not imported, then applications will be imported to a generic job. _We will not import the following_ • Job Postings • Interview Plans • Openings  |  
| Gem ATS  |  _What do we import?_
  * Candidates


  * Candidates are matched to existing Ashby profiles using email.


  * If no match is found, a new candidate is created.


  * Any resumes included in the export are imported and linked to the correct candidate/application (Gem assigns internal resume IDs and we map those back to real filenames).


  * Applications


  * Gem ATS exports application data across multiple CSV files; we combine these into Ashby job considerations.


  * Existing applications are matched when possible; otherwise, new applications are created.


  * Application history is reconstructed from Gem’s application_stages:


  * Duplicate and conflicting entries (common in Gem exports) are cleaned up.


  * If Gem did not include a proper “Hired” terminal stage, we infer and create a final Hired stage.


  * Archived applications are recreated with the correct archive reason, and the default Ashby archive stages are removed/rebuilt for accuracy.


  * Application Feedback


  * Scorecards from Gem are converted into notes


  * If question/answer files are populated, we include mappings into the note where possible.


  * Jobs


  * Job records can be imported if the customer includes them.


  * Job titles are matched against existing Ashby jobs before creating a new one.


  * Location and Team are added to the job if a match exists; otherwise, a generic option is used.


  * If jobs are not imported, applications are added to a generic job.


  * Departments & Offices


  * Gem exports include CSVs for Departments and Offices.


  * We support matching or creating these entities, but:


  * Department hierarchy is partially supported.


  * Office hierarchy (like regions → offices) is not currently supported.


  * Customers with complex hierarchies may require mapper updates.

_We do not import the following from Gem ATS:_
  * Job Postings


  * Interview Plans


  * Openings


  * Complex hierarchical location/department structures

 |  
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/historical-import-migrations#post-import)Post-Import
Once the import is complete, you’ll be notified by your Success Manager or the Scaled Success team. If any information you’re expecting to see is missing, do let them know.

ai-assisted-application-review:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby AI Features
# AI-Assisted Application Review
25 min
Copy
You can now accelerate candidate reviews by using AI to evaluate candidates in Application Review based on criteria you set.
You can opt-in to use AI features in the [Opt-In Features](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/early-access "Opt-In Features") section of Admin.
The AI-Assisted Application Review feature is available on the Foundations, Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#define-your-criteria)Define your criteria
To define your criteria, click into one of your jobs from the Jobs > [Admin](https://app.ashbyhq.com/jobs/all "Admin") page. You can set your criteria in your job’s AI Features tab. Hit Set up criteria to start configuring your criteria.
On the following page, you have two options to add criteria.
Add Empty Criterion: Click _+_ Add Criteria; you’ll then be asked to add in a title and a prompt. If you have quite complicated criteria, we’d recommend breaking this down into separate elements.
There is some additional guidance further below on generating effective criteria.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5c21bb9714bdb41ef613444c6b9daac3.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/41201d9f8154ea5c2ab755022535196f.png)
﻿
Title: The name of your criterion. This title will be displayed in your job settings, on the application review page and will also be the name of the filter generated for this criterion.
Prompt: This is what will be used to evaluate applicants. The more precise and clear your criteria, the better the AI will perform in evaluating candidates.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/257a85b22c273d9fa60e055bd829cef4.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#search-criteria-pool)Search Criteria Pool
You can click Search Pool for suggestions generated by AI based on the job description in your posting. You can also use this option to add criteria previously used for other roles. Please note: You will only see the criteria previously used for only the other roles you have access to.
When viewing suggestions or previously used criteria, you can click Add in the Action column to add the criteria to your role.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7837d8d93f8dbc3460449006a96c1744.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b20c73b0acf618d148f745adcf0cd089.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#validating-criteria)Validating Criteria
Once you’ve set up your criteria, click Validate. Your criteria will then be validated for clarity to ensure that the criteria are objective and that is can be evaluated by the AI accurately. It will also be checked to ensure there are no EEO violations (either implicit or explicit).
If any criteria have issues, this will be raised. Click Review Criteria to take a look at any warnings.
To review specifics, click Show details listed underneath any warnings. You can then make adjustments if needed and re-validate, continue with your original criteria or opt to remove the criterion completely.
Once you’ve made any amendments and your criteria have been validated, click Save and Evaluate all active candidates to kick off the AI evaluation. If you are making adjustments to existing criteria, you will see Save and Re-Evaluate all active Job Considerations.
Ashby’s AI Criteria Evaluation will then assess candidates (in particular, candidate resumes) and return an evaluation of each candidate against each criterion.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5d72476aab24ff86e03af2cdd54eeb43.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/57b2c71cdb247a698956008a6b205013.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/882c1b8f7dcbf4e8638bc6cb4c63ab34.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/59c65258a8e564e869346d2f9a046619.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#how-do-i-create-good-criteria)How do I create good criteria?
A general guideline: Write your criteria as if you were describing them to a pair of junior recruiters. You’ll want to phrase the prompt in such a way to make sure that they both understand the same (and correct) version of what you’re asking.
Below you can find some additional guidance for setting up great criteria to ensure you use the tool as effectively as possible.
_Stand-Alone_ Criteria should be able to be understood on its own without further context. “Can appropriately assimilate feedback” is vague as to whether the feedback is in regards to the applicant’s own performance or something else like product-related feedback. It could be more effectively rephrased as "Effectively incorporates feedback related to personal job performance"
_Singular_ Ensure each criterion focuses on a single skill or type of requirement. For example, splitting up “Has experience in Python, Typescript, and AWS experience” into 3 separate requirements “Has experience building application backends in Python”, “Has experience building B2B SaaS applications in Typescript” and “Has experience building microservices architectures in AWS”. A good rule to follow is if you find your criteria has lots of ands in it, you may want to split it up into multiple criteria.
_Clear_ Avoid vague terms. Instead of saying "Has a technical background," specify the exact type of technical expertise you're looking for.
_Specific_ Provide detailed requirements. Instead of "experienced," mention the number of years of experience or the exact role they should have held.
_Unambiguous_ Stay away from general terms like "good communication skills." Be more specific about the context, such as "experience in public speaking" or "proficient in technical writing."
_Objectively Verifiable from the Resume_ Criteria should be objectively verifiable from the candidate’s resume. While “has good taste” may generally indicate what you’re looking for, it is unlikely to yield positive results in the same way that “has led design for consumer SaaS products with millions of users” would.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#application-form-criteria)Application Form Criteria
You can also set criteria assessing whether a candidate answered a specific application form question or not within the Application Form Criteria section. You can find this underneath the AI job criteria you’ve configured.
You’ll have the option to enable the Did the Application answer the question? option for individual application form questions. These will then be added as criteria to be used when candidates are reviewed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3e5b634b36440525b53c0cf296c4df98.png)
﻿
Please note, this criteria only determines whether or not the question was answered and not the quality or accuracy of the answer. Blank or unrelated answers will be flagged as not meeting the criteria.
Application Form Criteria can only be enabled for long, unformatted text field questions on your application form.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#reviewing-candidate-evaluations)Reviewing Candidate Evaluations
Once your active candidates have been evaluated, you’ll be able to view the full evaluation both in the Feedback tab on the candidate’s profile and when viewing the candidate in the bulk application review tool.
You can click on any of the specified criteria to see all reasoning behind the criteria being labelled as Meets, Does Not Meet, or Undecided. You can click Expand All to view all reasoning across all criteria.
You can also filter candidates by whether or not they meet certain criteria from within the stages of your pipeline. Click the AI Criteria Quick Filter option to open up the full list of your criteria and select the evaluation status you’d like to filter by for each criterion.
You can then filter your applicants accordingly and review or take bulk action on the filtered candidates as needed.
AI criteria can also be combined with other applicant filters that would be normally used.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4d6f3a77cf7f4f8bcbb9db7091b71e87.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/065fc0dcef58ba87fa4dd168efd14e98.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d2d8e99a71280344e11dd6bbcfa31d80.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/244b3be3c1b6cbc027940aa7d5cf5878.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#automated-processing-legal-notice)Automated Processing Legal Notice
﻿
You can head to Admin > Data Management > [Data Privacy and Compliance](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-compliance "Data Privacy and Compliance") to enable and configure a legal notice to inform candidates that their data may be processed using AI and give them opt-out options.
Is Enabled? : If this option is disabled, the candidates will not be shown the legal notice.
Rule Scope: Configure filters here if the legal notice should only be shown for specific jobs. If filters aren’t added here, the notice will be shown for all roles.
Hide Scores if a Candidate Hasn’t Seen this Legal Notice: If you enable this option, then if a candidate has not seen this legal notice, AI-informed scores will be hidden. This will apply to all jobs in the rule’s scope.
If Hide Scores is enabled, a message will display on the candidate's evaluation results that says "The candidate has not seen the legal notice required by this job." This can happen when a candidate is added to Ashby through a path other than the Ashby-hosted job application, such as a direct referral, a manual add, the Chrome extension, or possibly Indeed Apply.
Legal Notice Text Configure the text that will be shown to candidates for jobs that match your filters here.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#how-this-looks-to-the-candidate)How this looks to the candidate
When the candidate is on the Application tab of a job, a Learn More link shows under the Submit Application button if you have enabled the automated processing legal notice and the job matches the criteria set in the Rule Scope section (if any was set).
The candidate can click this link to then view the configured legal notice and opt-out if they’d like to or check their opt-out status using their email address.
If they choose to opt out, a confirmation link will be sent to the entered email address so they can confirm. If they check their status, if they’re opted out, they will have the option to opt back in.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d8599b4ca51e888f760475b328f81aa0.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2f74f4f1191486a252221b598087dde6.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/23ca2d5ba0cda5fdd2989e0daf38da39.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#ai-opt-out-filters)AI opt-out filters
In your candidate pipeline, you can filter to see all candidates who have opted out of AI processing by following the below steps.
  1. Navigate to the **Candidate Pipeline**.


  1. Enable the Advanced Filters toggle on the right of the screen .


  1. Select + Add Field to Match on the left.


  1. Search for "AI opt out" in the search bar and select the criterion.


  1. Select Equals = Yes


This criteria is available when building reports as well.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#ai-credits)AI Credits
The AI-Assisted Application Review feature is available on all plans and is priced per credit. 1 credit = 1 evaluation of a job consideration, with a max of 50 criteria. Please refer to the table below as to how many credits are included for each plan.
_﻿  
_  
| **Foundations AI credits**  | **Legacy Plus AI credits**  | **Plus AI credits**  | **Enterprise AI credits**  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| 1,500/mo. Or 18,000 per year account wide  | 1,500/mo. Or 18,000 per year account wide  | 2,500/yr, per paid seat  | 12,500/yr, per paid seat  |  
### **﻿  
**
For customers on annual plans, additional credits can be purchased in blocks of 5,000 at $0.10 USD per credit (so $500 USD for 5000 credits.) For monthly Foundations customers, additional credits can be purchased in blocks of 1,000 at $0.10 USD per credit (so $100 USD for 1000 credits). For more on purchasing additional credits, check out [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#ai-assisted-application-review-feature)AI-Assisted Application Review Feature
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#if-i-edit-or-amend-my-criteria-can-i-re-evaluate-c)If I edit or amend my criteria, can I re-evaluate candidates?
Yes - if you edit a prompt or add or remove criteria, you’ll see a Save and Re-Evaluate all active Job Considerations option. Click this to save your changes and re-evaluate candidates with your new criteria.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#can-i-flag-ai-evaluations-if-theyre-not-quite-righ)Can I flag AI evaluations if they’re not quite right?
Yes! You can click the three dots in the top-right corner of the evaluation to open up the option to flag it as incorrect or misleading.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/316365183da88fa14ae39df27d6cd2c9.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#can-i-add-the-evaluations-as-a-column-on-the-appli)Can I add the evaluations as a column on the Application Review page?
You can! Click Add Column when viewing candidates on the application review page, then select Job Consideration>Criteria>Evaluations. Please be aware you may need to scroll to the right or remove other columns to view this additional column.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f406765e9089a5b081022649684078c3.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#what-permissions-are-needed-to-edit-criteria-on-a)What permissions are needed to edit criteria on a job?
A user would need to have Elevated Access - Admin **access or Elevated Access - Admin Private access to the role, or be an Organization Admin to edit the job and the job criteria.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#the-candidate-doesnt-have-a-resume-but-does-have-e)The candidate doesn't have a resume but does have education history and work experience listed in their profile in Ashby. Would this be considered and evaluated by the AI-assisted application review tool too?
It will be considered, yes.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#ai-credits-1)AI Credits
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#im-a-plus-plan-customer-how-many-included-candidat)I’m a Plus plan customer. How many included candidates do I get as part of my plan?
Plus plan customers get 2,500 annual AI candidate evaluations included as part of their plan per seat. To give an example, let’s say you have purchased 50 seats in your plan. In that case, you would have 50 seats x 2,500 included candidate evaluations per seat = 125,000 candidate evaluations included annually. If you needed more, you could purchase additional credits in blocks of 5,000.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#what-if-i-purchase-more-seats-part-of-the-way-into)What if I purchase more seats part of the way into my contract on a seat-based plan?
Purchasing more seats adds the corresponding number of credits on a prorated basis. So, if you purchase one seat 6 months into a 1 year Plus plan contract, you’ll get 2,500 x 0.5 = 1250 additional AI credits on that contract.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#how-many-ai-credits-do-i-need)How many AI credits do I need?
Customers can better estimate how many AI credits they may need in a given billing cycle by looking at their historical data and target hiring numbers together. Using Ashby's reporting capabilities, customers can quickly look at the number of applicants per role historically and multiply that by the number of planned hires over a given period to get a rough estimate of the number of applications they expect to receive. This number should provide an estimate of the number of AI Credits they will need over a given period. To provide an example, if you have gotten 500 applicants per role historically and plan to add 20 new hires over the next year, you can roughly expect to have 10,000 applicants (and therefore can expect to use roughly 10,000 AI Credits) over the next year.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#will-you-warn-me-if-im-close-to-using-up-all-of-my)Will you warn me if I’m close to using up all of my credits?
We will warn you in the application when you’ve used up 80% of your credits. We will also let you know how many credits you have remaining before you create new criteria for a job.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#if-i-update-criteria-for-a-job-will-i-be-billed-an)If I update criteria for a job, will I be billed another credit? How do credits work with multiple criteria applied?
You can run up to 50 criteria for any single candidate. This means that you can update 1 criterion 50 times or 10 criteria 5 times without getting billed another credit. We think this should be plenty for almost any reasonable use case. We want to support fair use at a fair cost, and just want to make sure the flexibility doesn’t get abused.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#if-i-dont-use-all-of-my-credits-do-they-roll-over)If I don’t use all of my credits, do they roll over to the next period?
Credits are tied to a contract period. Unused credits don’t roll over to the next contract year.*
*credits are granted yearly for customers on annual or multi-year agreements; credits are granted monthly for customers on monthly agreements
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#how-long-do-i-have-to-use-additional-purchased-cre)How long do I have to use additional purchased credits?
You have until the end of the current year of your subscription term. Additional credit “blocks” are prorated based on your contract term. This way you have one contract with Ashby with one renewal date, allowing you to budget easier.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#what-happens-if-i-run-out-of-credits)What happens if I run out of credits?
We won’t bill you any more than what you’ve already paid. We will pause any new AI evaluations and the feature will turn back on automatically once you’ve added additional credits. Existing customers can contact their Customer Success Manager; new customers can contact their Account Executive.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#can-you-give-me-an-example-of-how-add-on-credits-w)Can you give me an example of how add-on credits would work?
Let’s say you are on a Plus plan with 10 seats. You’d get 2,500 annual credits / seat x 10 seats = 25,000 annual credits to start with. If you needed to evaluate 29,000 candidates in the course of the year, you’d add a block of 5,000 credits at $500 at any point into your contract. That block of credits would be available to use any time throughout the remainder of your agreement term. The following year, you’d have an additional 5,000 credits added to your contract and a $500 line item for those credits that you could adjust up or down, based on your needs.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#for-seat-based-plans-are-credits-tied-to-individua)For seat-based plans, are credits tied to individuals?
Credits are allocated based on the number of seats for Plus and Enterprise plans and tied to an organization, not an individual. So, if you have 50 seats on a Plus plan, you can use 50 x 2,500 credits = 125,000 credits across any number of team members or jobs.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#how-do-application-form-criteria-work-with-credits)How do application form criteria work with credits?
﻿  

In the same way as your job criteria. You can run up to 50 criteria for any single candidate for one credit. This means that you can update 1 criterion 50 times or 10 criteria 5 times without getting billed another credit.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ai-assisted-application-review#where-can-i-view-my-remaining-ai-credits)Where can I view my remaining AI credits?
You can view your remaining AI credits and purchase additional AI Credits by heading to Admin > Organization Setup > Billing. For more on this, check out [How do I purchase additional email lookups, AI credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app)﻿
﻿

# Accessing Ashby Academy
4 min
Copy
Ashby Academy is an on-demand learning hub designed to help you build confidence and practical skills in Ashby. The courses provided cover best practices and workflows ranging from foundational concepts to advanced use cases.
Ashby Academy is open to anyone and does not require an Ashby product account. Ashby Academy is open to anyone and does not require an Ashby product account.
Please note that your Academy login is separate from your Ashby platform login.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/accessing-ashby-academy#how-to-access-ashby-academy)**How to access Ashby Academy**
Navigate to the Ashby Academy here: [ _https://academy.ashbyhq.com_](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/ "https://academy.ashbyhq.com"). From there, you have two ways to sign in or create an account.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/accessing-ashby-academy#option-1-sign-up-or-log-in-using-a-connected-accou)**Option 1:** Sign up or log in using a connected account
On the sign-up screen, you can choose to continue with one of the following login methods:
  * Google


  * Facebook


  * LinkedIn


  * Apple


If you’ve used one of these methods before, be sure to use the same option again when logging back in.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/accessing-ashby-academy#option-2-create-an-account-with-email-and-password)**Option 2:** Create an account with email and password
You can also create an account manually by entering:
  1. Your name


  1. Your email address


  1. A password of your choice


Once your account is created, you can log in anytime using that email and password.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/accessing-ashby-academy#common-login-issues-and-quick-fixes)**Common login issues and quick fixes**
If you’re having trouble accessing Ashby Academy, here are a few things to check:
**Using the wrong login method** If you originally signed up with Google, LinkedIn, Apple, or Facebook, you’ll need to use that same option to log back in.
**Account not found** This usually means an Academy account hasn’t been created yet for that email. Try signing up first.
**Page not loading or acting oddly** Try opening the Academy in an incognito/private browser window or clearing your browser cache.
Forgotten your password? Use the Forgot your password? link on the login screen to reset it.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SUSP2sKpJUavy4p11woDB_home-2026-01-20-at-35509-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**﻿
