# Recruiting Planner
20 min
Copy
This document reviews how to create and configure the Recruiting Planner template report.
The Recruiting Planner is available on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#recruiting-planner-overview)Recruiting Planner Overview
The Recruiting Planner template report allows you to plan required recruiting activities based on historical performance, input headcount goals, and a hiring date range (e.g. this quarter). Ashby will then compute the total available time available to achieve your target headcount hires while incorporating the customizable passthrough rates to summarize the total number of activities at each stage required to hit those goals. Additionally, the overall goals will be presented as a weekly pace of events to better understand the required weekly efforts.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d885ddea3bc17c27f82d9b53d8b83d9e.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#tutorial)Tutorial
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#configure-the-recruiting-planner)Configure the Recruiting Planner
To create a new recruiting planner report:
  * Navigate to Reports > Templates or Reports > Report Builder if you are using the new report builder layout


  * Select the Recruiting Planner option.


  * Enter the number of hires you are looking to make, the positions being hired for and the target timeframe in the I need... field.


  * Click Get Started


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kD2L3epIxhhikAQEapDKH_recruiting-plan-acent-ashby-2026-01-16-at-35610-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When creating your recruiting planner, Ashby will automatically pull historical data to generate historical passthrough rate baselines. If the data pulled is too sparse or not available, you will see a warning and you can either manually set the passthrough values or change the data window (i.e. a larger historical timeframe or broader department inclusion, etc).
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bc22e4bf5e95f07f41e29dc4a2df4b36.png)
﻿
﻿  

A recruiting planner table will then be generated for you based on the targets set, showing you the overall and weekly numbers for activities in each stage group, broken down by the contributing plans.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3ba3d03a4e987d3bc3ace9d4550f9665.png)
﻿
The recruiting planner defaults to selecting the last 6 months of historical data to generate representative passthrough rates for your organization. If you wish to customize the passthrough rate, then you can customize the passthrough rate data by editing your targets.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#editing-your-targets-and-timeframe-on-the-recruiti)Editing your targets and timeframe on the recruiting planner
You can amend your timeframe and targets using the left-hand menu of the recruiting plan.
To adjust the timeframe, click on the current time frame within the Duringsection. You can then adjust your timeframe accordingly for your targets.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/824dbb244fff69f669d647b26d02cba3.png)
﻿
Once you’ve adjusted and saved your timeframe, the recruiting plan and your targets will adapt to the new timeline.
If you’d like to adjust a target, click on the hires target within the _Targets_ section on the left-hand menu.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2f03b5752488a21c76fb2f00fa1163a9.png)
﻿
On the Edit targetmenu, you can adjust the hire target, the positions being hired for, the target name and the passthrough rate data being used to calculate your activity recommendations within the recruiting planner.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e768716026f89f1125f7ea97db85064a.png)
﻿
Within the Passthrough Rates field, you can click the arrow to view the calculations being made and adjust the data used. You can either amend the simple filters used to generate the provided passthrough rates (positions hired for and timeframe) or manually update the passthrough rates within each stage.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8f00cef1633afc1112b0dc8c5b3eb06c.png)
﻿
Show advanced filters will allow you to add advanced filters for both your target (in terms of positions being hired for) and for your passthrough rate historical data so you can fine-tune further if needed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cf0d03b8b5126003a7ac6cded95901a6.png)
﻿
Click Save to confirm your target changes and the recruiting planner will update accordingly. If you’d prefer to switch back to simple filters, click Switch to simple, then save your changes by clicking _Save_ on the original menu.
Please note that all advanced filters will need to be removed before you are able to switch back to simple filters
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#adding-a-new-target)Adding a new target
If you’d like to add an additional target to your recruiting planner, click + Add Target _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9978eba6c56e304cd8e0c2d0a4e035e6.png)
﻿
You will then be prompted to fill out the hire target, the positions you are hiring for and the passthrough rate data being used. You will also have the option to use advanced filters to refine your target as needed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/412b77e856ad6737cf54d41cf173bc4c.png)
﻿
Once you’ve configured your target, click Add target to add it to your recruiting planner.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#view-time-elapsed-and-progress-to-goal)View time elapsed and progress to goal
The progress across plans is tracked and presented against goal per stage group once configured. Hovering over the progress column will provide additional information on time elapsed and your progress to meet your targets.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3d8f11b47291b747b91ba8217eb03a73.png)
﻿
If you’d like to hide specific stage groups or metrics within your recruiting planner, click the eye icon. You can then opt to show or hide stage groups or metrics so only the data most relevant to you and your team is displayed on the recruiting planner. In terms of metrics, you can hide the overall, weekly, progress and/or forecast columns as needed.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/07e6fb42c5897ce370a348725cd8444e.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#recruiting-planner-visualizations)Recruiting Planner Visualizations
The recruiting planner presents a detailed summary in the plan table. However, there are various manners of visualizing your overall plan and progress towards the final hiring targets. Each visualization option suits a different manner of reviewing goals and progress and is briefly summarized below.
You can click the Summary dropdown to switch between visualization options within your recruiting planner.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a75b9341e3eaaa45c8b72b047154a6f1.png)
﻿
Some visualization options that are common to chart types are:
  * **Conditional color formatting:** specify pacing targets to color code your results in a red-yellow-green format. To adjust the thresholds on your color-coding, click the cog icon above your recruiting planner summary. Hover over Customize colors and then adjust the Within x of pace field accordingly.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b820c915ed23c9900d6203ffddd86236.png)
﻿
  * **Progress tracking:** enable progress tracking to show overall progress in each stage. Make sure you’ve configured your progress tracking as outlined above.


  * **Forecasting:** enable the forecasting option to extrapolate the likely hiring outcomes by the end of your plan period. This forecast is based on extrapolating your rate of hiring and the remaining time in your plan.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#plan-table)Plan Table
The plan table visualization summarizes your recruiting planner results in a dense table format. You can further adjust this table by hiding stage groups and other plan summary columns, as well as including forecasts, progress tracking and conditional color formatting.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9ead7d7e03aa62ade74648bb1cb9d543.png)
﻿
In the above chart you can see:
  1. The timeframe and elapsed time for your plan duration based on the current date


  1. The targets set with headcount summary


  1. Progress tracking and color-coded pacing


  1. An estimated forecast on the hiring outcome at the end of the plan period


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#stage-progress-chart)Stage Progress Chart
The stage progress chart aims to show the progress for each stage group for each individual plan. This chart can show results on an absolute count basis, or a percent progress basis. This visualization can aid immediate visual insight into which parts of the funnel are proceeding at what rate.
In this example, you can see that the Analytics plan is trailing at the top of funnel stages, but that onsites, offers and hiring outcomes have turned out well and are leading expectations.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bHZvzqS-TQfN8IHYat_Bc-20260116-161120.png?format=webp)
﻿
In the above chart you can see:
  1. Each individual plan


  1. The individual stage groups, which can be toggled on/off or hidden entirely as an option


  1. The percent progress against the planned goal.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#plan-progress-chart)Plan Progress Chart
The plan progress chart provides quick and immediate access to a view that focuses on hiring targets and outcomes. When the optional forecast is turned on, the end of plan outcome is summarized by showing likely hires and the likely deficit against plan, if any.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/m-m49SNMNgGxCOGlPndD0-20260116-161152.png?format=webp)
﻿
In the above chart you can see:
  1. Each individual hiring target


  1. The forecasted “expected hires” at end of plan per target


  1. The forecasted “expected deficit” at end of plan per target


  1. The current hires made to date per target


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#progress-over-time-chart)Progress Over Time Chart
This view is provided for teams looking to analyze their progress over time against a hiring target. The total hiring target is taken and presented as a linear goal over the specified plan period. An optional forecast can be included, as well as options to view these results on a weekly, monthly or quarterly time basis.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kZrC4NC_aUM2T9oYg6Phf-20260116-161037.png?format=webp)
﻿
In the above chart you can see:
  1. The total, cumulative hires made at a point in time


  1. The linearized total headcount goal over time


  1. The optional forecast line showing the likely outcome at the end of plan


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#pace-to-goal-chart)Pace to Goal Chart
Another, intuitive way of summarizing all progress is to use a pace to goal chart: this shows the percent progress against the target against the percent time elapsed for the plan duration. This allows for a quick visual assessment of which plans are on track or off track as paced against time elapsed.
For example, in the below chart it can quickly be seen that the Analytics plan is roughly on target and will likely end at ~92% of plan, while the Marketing plan is severely behind targets and likely to end at only ~65% of plan.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ZQ0jMniPV1sJTveQ-6LM3-20260116-161217.png?format=webp)
﻿
In the above chart you can see:
  1. A visual indication of the time elapsed


  1. The progress at present with conditional color coding for each target


  1. The forecasted end of plan outcome shown in a lighter tone for each target


  1. The total blended progress and forecast across all targets


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#how-is-the-expected-deficit-calculated-on-the-plan)How is the expected deficit calculated on the plan progress chart?
For the expected deficit metric, Ashby takes the current forecast for the end-of-period outcome and then compares that against the plan target/targets to estimate where you will be once the time period is complete.
As an example, if you have a target of 10 hires over a quarter, halfway through the quarter you may only have made 5 hires, but if at your rate you expect to complete 10 hires based on our forecast then by the end of quarter the expected deficit is 0, (so you hit your target). However, if your rates are lagging and halfway through the quarter the estimate is you may make only 8 hires by the end of the period, the expected deficit will be 2 (i.e. 8 hires out of 10 targeted).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/recruiting-planner#are-drilldowns-available-for-the-recruiting-planne)Are drilldowns available for the Recruiting Planner report?
Yes. Each view of the Recruiting Planner has drilldown support, utilizing the candidate search based on the Recruiting Planner's pre-filtered data points. Here's what to expect in each view:
  * **Summary** : Click on the Progress cell in the table to view the pre-filtered Candidate Search showing candidates who reached the specific stage in the specified timeframe for that Team.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/M-4wuMT6EfSBnJ5DGSHE9_recruiting-plan-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-123653-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Stage Progress** : Clicking on the interview stage bar will take you to the Candidate Search page, pre-filtered with the data of candidates in progress in the specific stage within the set timeframe.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/8bMvNEXa1tncL5vfs-AYH_recruiting-plan-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-123751-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Plan Progress** : Drilldown on the **Hires made** bar to view which hires count towards the Recruiting Plan.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Uh8xUAU5KUS3FjlZixcBz_recruiting-plan-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-123912-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Progress over time** : Drilldown on the **Hiring Plan** bar to view which hires count towards the Recruiting Plan.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/2Ip21gSvHbOv0ZwqElYmc_recruiting-plan-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-123952-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Pace to Goal** : Clicking on the bar for the current progress will take you to the Candidate Search, pre-filtered with the current hires.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sOz8lUsNgxtSxAB1W-0lY_recruiting-plan-acent-ashby-2026-03-23-at-124048-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

﻿

# Can I transfer a candidate’s job consideration to another job or interview plan?
6 min
Copy
You can! This section covers using the Transfer option to move an existing active job consideration on a candidate’s profile to a new job or interview plan. Please note that if transferring the candidate to another job, the job must be open.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan#overview)Overview
Ashby allows users to transfer applications to a different job and/or interview plan. This can be useful when you've decided that a candidate is a better fit for a different role or should be evaluated through an alternate interview plan.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan#when-should-i-transfer-a-candidate-to-another-job)**When should I transfer a candidate to another job and when should I consider a candidate for another job instead?**
_Transfer a candidate's job consideration when:_
• The role is essentially the same or a similar opportunity (for example, a renamed job or corrected posting) that the candidate should be considered for instead
• You want to preserve all historical context and timing for this specific job consideration and just move it to a different job
• Time-to-hire continuity taking into account when the candidate was first considered for a role
_Consider the candidate for another job when:_
• The candidate is being considered for a new or different opportunity
• You want reporting dates to reflect both job considerations
• The opportunity represents a new hiring process
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan#how-to-transfer-an-application)How To Transfer An Application
There are two ways to initiate a transfer.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan#candidate-profile)Candidate Profile
You can find the Transfer option under the More dropdown on a Candidate Profile. You’ll then be able to specify the job and stage of the interview plan candidates will be transferred to.
﻿  

![Screen Shot 2021-12-08 at 4.54.12 PM.png](https://cdn.zappy.app/117a0c2dfdc04676cd54dfeb97b3cb71.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan#candidate-pipeline-job-pipeline)Candidate Pipeline / Job Pipeline 
Using the Select option, select one or more candidates that you’d like to transfer to another job. From there, you can click the More button and select Transfer from the dropdown.
Once selected, you can specify the job, and the interview stage you’d like candidates to be transferred to. You will also have the option to override the attribution fields (Source and Credited To _)._ Once you’ve configured your transfer, click Start.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ca2b35932a0bf4d475fd4e8b09bf425b.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/cf069b2ddfb72d8a8dbe7a6a69860a88.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan#what-happens-when-i-transfer-an-application)What happens when I transfer an application?
When you transfer an application in Ashby to another job pipeline, we retain all historical information within the application history. That means on the Candidate Profile you'll be able to see all of the details prior to the change. We also retain any notes, application forms, completed surveys, feedback forms and referral forms, so if the candidate is moved to a new job via the Transfer option, you won’t lose that data.
For reporting, since we retain all the application history, your Time in Process, Time to Hire and Time to Offer calculations will continue to be accurate for transferred candidates.
Any scheduled and upcoming activities such as interviews, take-home activities, etc. will still be scheduled after a transfer. You may delete any activities you won't need manually before transferring an application.
In terms of the job consideration’s hiring team, this will be overwritten and the job consideration will take on the hiring team of the job that the candidate is transferred into.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan#how-do-i-remove-or-reject-a-candidate-from-a-job-c)How do I remove or reject a candidate from a job consideration?
While viewing the job consideration on the candidate profile, click the Archive button. You can then select an archive reason and optionally schedule a rejection email to be sent to the candidate.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/dac61c6ccd0dd6956ffec02854427abb.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿

# Offers and Offer Letters
34 min
Copy
In this guide, you'll learn about managing offers and offer letters for candidates in the Offer stage of the pipeline. This guide assumes you already have a .docx version of your offer letter.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#permissions-offers-and-offer-letters)Permissions: Offers and Offer Letters
See the table below for information on the access roles that can create offer letter templates and generate offers for candidates in the Offer stage. Only Elevated Access - Admin Private users and Organization Admins can create offers for candidates.  
| Access Role  | Can create offer letter templates?  | Can create offers for candidates in the Offer stage?  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌*  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌*  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌*  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌*  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌*  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌*  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  | ✅  |  
*Elevated access users who have been given the additional permission Offer Letter Templates will be able to configure offer custom fields, offer document substitution tokens, offer templates, and offer template settings. More on this at [Additional User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/additional-user-permissions)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#create-offer-custom-fields)Create offer custom fields
The first step in setting up offers is creating custom fields in the [Custom Fields section](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/offers/offer-fields "Custom Fields section") of Offers in Admin. Once set up, you can set offer custom fields to have a generated substitution token. When adding a substitution token into your offer letter template, when an offer is generated, the information from the associated custom field on the Offer Details form will replace the token placeholders. Head to [creating an offer letter ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#create-an-offer-letter-template)﻿for more information on setting up an offer letter template.
The following default tokens are available for offer letters:
{{CANDIDATE_NAME}}
{{CANDIDATE_FIRST_NAME}}
{{CANDIDATE_LAST_NAME}}
{{JOB_TITLE}}
{{JOB_LOCATION_NAME}}
{{JOB_STREET_ADDRESS}}
{{JOB_CITY}}
{{JOB_STATE}}
{{JOB_POSTAL_CODE}}
{{JOB_COUNTRY}}
{{CANDIDATE_EMAIL_ADDRESS}}
{{CANDIDATE_PHONE_NUMBER}}
{{HIRING_MANAGER}}
{{OPENING_IDENTIFIER}} (available if [Openings Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management)﻿ is enabled)
{{CANDIDATE_CITY}}
{{CANDIDATE_REGION}}
{{CANDIDATE_COUNTRY}}
{{TODAY_DATE}}
Please note Offer fields and Candidate fields are not currently interchangeable. You'll need to create separate fields for both, even if it's the same information.
The following default tokens are available for e-signature offer letters (more on these at [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿):
{{CANDIDATE_E_SIGNATURE}}
{{CANDIDATE_E_SIGNATURE_DATE}}
{{CANDIDATE_E_SIGNATURE_INITIALS}}
{{CANDIDATE_CHECKBOX}}
{{COMPANY_E_SIGNATURE}}
{{COMPANY_E_SIGNATURE_DATE}}
{{COMPANY_E_SIGNATURE_INITIALS}}
You also have the option to sync fields from your employee management system using the [Rippling](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/rippling)﻿ or [BambooHR](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/bamboohr)﻿ integrations (let us know if you'd like access at support@ashbyhq.com).
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#adding-text-input-fields-to-your-e-signature-docum)**Adding text input fields to your e-signature document or offer letter**
E-Signature text input tokens can be used for e-signature documents and offer letters generated via Dropbox Sign or Docusign.
If you'd like to add text fields into your offer letters so the candidate or the company signatory can fill out additional information within the offer itself, you can also add in the following tokens:
{{CANDIDATE_TEXT_INPUT}} This text input field will provide the candidate a field to add in additional text.
{{COMPANY_TEXT_INPUT}} This text input field will provide the company signatory a field to add in additional text.
When clicking the token in the Offer Letter Tokens tab, you'll be prompted to select the field size, either by selecting a preset size or by setting the width and number of lines. You can also check the Required checkbox to require the signer to complete the field as part of the signing process.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Vdblf0p9HKE999LinHV6L_offers-acent-ashby-2026-01-29-at-91815-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Once you've determined the text input field size and settings, click the token generated to then copy it. You can then paste this token into your offer letter. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BU8cdsM2wJWApPF-KIfDO_offers-acent-ashby-2026-01-29-at-92749-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
We recommend placing this token in it's own line within your offer letter template to prevent layout issues. Here's an example of how the token should be placed and how it will look when an e-signature offer letter has been generated.
![An offer letter template with a company input text token added on a new line.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Jkh5u64P68HUKY6HYK675_e-signature-text-input-tokens-restricted-early-access-slab-2026-01-29-at-93834-am.jpg?format=webp)
An offer letter template with a company input text token added on a new line.
﻿
![How the company signatory will see the text input field at the point of signing the e-signature offer.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/tjlA_m5-wrGagUHpnHLlJ_e-signature-text-input-tokens-restricted-early-access-slab-2026-01-29-at-93802-am.jpg?format=webp)
How the company signatory will see the text input field at the point of signing the e-signature offer.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#offer-custom-field-options)Offer custom field options
You can create an offer custom field by navigating to Admin > Offers and then to the Offer Custom Fields tab or by heading to Admin > Organization Setup > Custom Fields and selecting the Offer tab.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e828f175e5e7ebfab7f5e8abb0bf7074.png)
﻿
This screenshot highlights the 9 configuration options for setting up custom fields:
  1. Enter a title to show up in the offer form, it will be used to generate the substitution token.


  1. The object type will automatically be set as the Offer option, it is not interchangeable with the Candidate or Job object type.


  1. Enter a brief Description to make things clear in the offer details form for other users.


  1. Choose a form Field Type. You have the option to choose from Yes/No, Short Answer, Date, Multiple Choice, Checkboxes, Number, and Currency types.


  1. Check Is Required? to make the field required on the offer details form.


  1. If this value in this field is changed when amending an offer, check Require Reapproval if the offer then needs to go through the approval process again.


  1. Click + Generate Token in the Substitution Token section to allow for this custom field to be used in an Offer Letter Template. Once you've enabled this option, you can customize a token string that can be used in your Offer Letter Templates. When you are generating an Offer Letter, the value of the field will be substituted in place of the token in your template. If you opt to generate a token for a multiple choice custom field, you will get a follow up option Customize Substitution Text. More on this [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#offer-custom-field-options)﻿﻿


  1. The Default value section allows you to determine the default field to pull a value from if the token is used in a fillable form.


  1. Enabling the Restrict which Offer Forms include this field? option allows you to filter which Offer Forms will include this field. You can specify certain teams and locations for which this field will be used.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#create-an-offer-letter-template)Create an offer letter template
Review [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿ for more information on creating e-signature offer letters. Please note, you do not need your own Dropbox Sign integration to use E-Signature in Ashby. It's ready to use just by adding the tokens to your offer letters!
Offer Letter Templates can be configured on the Foundations, Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plan:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅*  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
Users on the Foundations plan can configure one offer letter template. Users on the Legacy Plus, Plus and Enterprise plans can add as many offer letter templates as needed.
Once your custom fields are set up and the Enable Substitution Token has been configured on each, you'll be using those custom fields as tokens in your offer letter. To configure, copy the tokens from the [Offer Letter Tokens page](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/offers/offer-letter-tokens "Offer Letter Tokens page") and paste them into the body of your offer letter template.
Adding _RAW to the end of any **numeric** or **currency** substitution token will strip formatting from the field. For example, a large number MY_FIELD might be formatted as 1,000,000, but MY_FIELD_RAW would be rendered as 1000000. This can avoid problems when using Word Tables.
On that page, you'll also find [E-Signature Tokens](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿ that can be used to automatically enable e-Signature for your offer letters. Adding candidate e-Signature tokens enables e-Signature for an offer template. You can additionally add company e-Signature tokens to require a counter signer.
Once the template is configured with tokens, upload it on the [Offer Letter Templates](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/offers/offer-letter-templates "Offer Letter Templates") page by clicking New, entering a title, and uploading the file in .docx format. Next, click the refresh button 🔄, and the form will be validated for any errors. You'll be notified if anything in the form needs to be updated.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#offer-letter-template-upload)Offer letter template upload
For information on troubleshooting formatting issues, please check out [Offer Letter Template Formatting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-letter-template-formatting)﻿.
There is a limit of 8MB when uploading an offer letter template.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/aa7fa776335bce4e5083dcc2ca4c6368.png)
﻿
  1. Enter a descriptive Name to avoid confusion when generating templates


  1. Upload the Template File by dragging and dropping or browsing for the file


  1. Ensure the file Is Valid by clicking the refresh button to check that all tokens are entered correctly. When uploading the template file, the check should run automatically.


  1. You can set conditions on your offer letter to limit the job considerations it can be used for. In the example pictured above, the offer letter template can only be used for job considerations for roles in the Design department.


  1. To enable this option, check the Only allow using this Offer Letter Template on specific job considerations? checkbox.


  1. Use the Advanced option to confirm whether you are using Advanced or Basic filtering to determine your conditions. Click + Filter to determine the fields your filtering by if using basic filters, or + Add Field to Match if you are using advanced filtering to set your conditions.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#create-an-offer-on-a-candidate-profile-to-send-to)Create an offer on a candidate profile to send to a candidate
When a candidate moves to the Offer stage in the candidate pipeline, an offer activity will be generated in the Activities section of their candidate profile. Click Create Offer to complete the Offer Details form. The form data will be used to populate substitution fields in the PDF Offer Letter that is generated. Use the Offer Activity to track the candidates' responses.
To edit an offer, click View Offer, then Update Offer under the offer details.
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#generate-an-offer-letter-and-draft-an-offer-letter)Generate an offer letter and draft an offer letter email
To learn how to send e-signature offer requests, see [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿ .
  1. Click View Offer on the offer activity and select Generate Offer Letter to create a populated pdf version of the offer letter.


  1. You can review the offer letter by clicking on either the Document or PDF links provided, or click Draft Offer Letter Email to generate an offer email with the letter attached. 


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/440ccdfd6e28ada7b12a43acd9fdfad9.png)
﻿
  1. Apply an existing offer email template, or draft your message anew. 


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/rfWXd7aeLI6acWG5X2yjM_carolina-zulauf-backend-engineer-acent-ashby-2026-03-12-at-54246-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Create an [offer email template](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management)﻿ to save time even more time extending offers to candidates.
  1. Hit Send when ready to send. These messages will be marked as private when sent.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#upload-a-custom-offer-letter)Upload a custom offer letter
Offer letters can be uploaded directly to an offer. If you have a .docx file locally that you’d like to use to generate the offer letter for a particular candidate, you can add it directly without adding it as an offer letter template first. You can also first generate a letter based on an offer letter template, download it, make adjustments and re-upload it.
  1. Ensure that the Enable Custom Offer Letter Uploads option is enabled in the [Settings tab of Admin > Offers > Offers.](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/offers/settings "Settings tab of Admin > Offers > Offers.")﻿


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9b72013c4e2f6029d1a6f22d17558a79.png)
﻿
  1. Next, create your offer from the candidate’s profile.


  1. Once you’ve added in your offer details and created the offer, in the **Offer Letter** section, click Upload Custom Offer Letter. 


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/913fc6535d408b683c9c2d277612e0aa.png)
﻿
  1. Then click Browse files… and select the docx file you’d like to use.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/09318a2f12c4d7eed18c4e3d4cb090f5.png)
﻿
  1. Once uploaded, the file will then be validated and you can select the formatting style using the Number Formatting and Currency Formatting drop-downs prior to clicking Generate Offer Letter. If you’d added offer tokens to the document, they will be validated and used, just like with an offer letter template.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/17a89961cdf52cf4b497f966297f59d1.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#push-hired-candidate-to-rippling-or-bamboohr)Push Hired Candidate to Rippling or BambooHR
When a candidate accepts an offer, you see an option to push hired candidates to your HRIS system. Clicking Select a Post Hire Integration will create a new Employee in the system, including that latest Offer data.
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#conditional-offer-fields)Conditional Offer Fields
Conditional offer fields enable your team only to display offer questions where they are relevant. There are two ways to use conditional offer fields:
  * Restrict offer fields to particular forms


  * Include questions based on other questions in your form


Restrict offer fields to particular forms
This option enables you to display certain fields on certain forms. For example, you could display a variable compensation field only on offers in your Sales department.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b4136fefcc2415c9dc947296c52e2f56.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8909eb8fd95dcea83c1877766da2f4d5.png)
﻿
﻿  

Include questions based on other questions in your form
This option lets you display questions based on responses to other questions. For example, you display a date input field specifically on offers where the hire works remotely.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/296cff8c0f372512f29bc3d14dd54560.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4a4e72dd0a5ee761435e7f3b357ed92d.gif)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#rescinding-or-withdrawing-an-offer-and-handling-de)Rescinding or withdrawing an offer and handling declined offers
If you need to rescind an offer for a candidate and nullify the e-signature request sent to them, click Archive on the candidate’s profile (do not delete or amend the offer that has been created). While archiving, set the archive reason to one that specifically mentions the offer being rescinded.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b7c1f773d6729200f8e760e761ecb6dc.png)
﻿
If you do not already have an archive reason in place for rescinding an offer, you can set one up in Admin > Jobs & Applications > [Archive Reasons](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-archive-reasons "Archive Reasons"). We would recommend having one in place for reporting purposes and ease of reference.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#how-do-i-rescind-an-offer-and-archive-a-candidate)How do I rescind an offer and archive a candidate in the _Hired_ stage?
If the candidate is already in the Hired stage but needs to be archived, you can take the following steps:
  * Navigate to the candidate’s profile and click Change Stage.


  * Move the candidate back to the Offer stage of the pipeline. You should then see the Archive option on their profile.


  * If the candidate has come back and rejected the offer, then update the candidate response to the offer to ensure it's reported on accurately in the future. 


  * To do this, click on View Offer within the offer activity on the candidate’s profile, then select Update Response to amend the offer response.


  * If you do not need to amend the offer, click the Archive option and select an appropriate archive reason as above.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#offer-version-history)Offer version history
Offers can undergo changes and the offer version history allows your team to track amendments made to offers, including the approval record and any approval comments.
To view an offer version history:
  * Click View Offer on the offer activity on the candidate's profile


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/tWzcTdtaiAVZRdg7UpWu3_offer-version-history-updates-archbee-document-2026-03-27-at-95954-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * Click the View Previous Versions button within the Offer Details section


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/s8cf6qvqjgptqVSShsVs2-20260325-154834.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * The versions will be listed with an overview of any changes made. Click View Details on the version in question within Version History. 


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/RCrVC6F4Zj3Tq3cdAFdTb-20260325-154936.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If any approvals or approval comments were added to the offer, these will also be listed.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/hkG3aQvhpVu80RCkZsBM3-20260327-125456.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Want to learn more? Check out the [Offers](https://academy.ashbyhq.com/course/offers "Offers") course in Ashby Academy!
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#how-do-i-make-offer-emails-private)How do I make offer emails private?
You can set the visibility of emails in the Offer and/or Hired stages from within the [Email Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-settings "Email Settings") section of Ashby. For other stages, you'll need to manually mark notes and emails private.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b7ab3b690421ce11501b14bb39764c3e.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#do-i-need-a-dropbox-sign-account-to-use-the-e-sign)Do I need a Dropbox Sign account to use the E-Signature feature?
No, you do not! The Dropbox Sign integration is built-in and can be enabled just by adding the E-Signature Tokens to your Offer Letter Template. Check out the [E-Signature guide](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿ for more information on setting up and using E-Signature! If you'd like to connect up your company's Docusign account, you can read more on this at [Docusign](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/docusign)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#can-i-designate-a-user-to-sign-on-behalf-of-the-co)Can I designate a user to sign on behalf of the company?
To assign a company signatory, you can create an offer letter including candidate and company e-signature tokens on the [Offer Tokens page](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/offers/offer-letter-tokens "Offer Tokens page"). Once you’ve created the offer and your offer letter, you’ll see the option to send an e-signature request and can assign your company’s signatory from there. More information on e-signatures at [E-Signature Offers](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/e-signature-offers)﻿﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b0f0430e9f0c1149859c4a6996c69acb.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#which-users-can-create-offers-and-view-offer-detai)Which users can create offers and view offer details?
Only Organization Admins and Elevated Access - Admin Private users can create offers and view offer details.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#what-notifications-does-a-person-ccd-on-an-offer-l)What notifications does a person CC’d on an offer letter signature process receive?
A person CC’d on an offer letter will receive an email when: the signature process has started, when the candidate views the offer, when the company signatory views the offer, and when the offer has been signed by both the candidate and the company signatory.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#are-there-any-file-size-requirements)Are there any file size requirements?
When uploading Offer Letter templates to Ashby, there is an 8MB file size limit. This is due to performance requirements for PDF conversion.
You may notice that templates that are just under the 8MB limit might be accepted but fail to process after it’s been populated with candidate data that takes if over that 8MB threshold. We recommend trying to keep the template size under 7MB when possible for the best reliability.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#how-does-the-hiring-manager-substitution-token-wor)How does the Hiring Manager substitution token work with offer letters?
When adding the {{HIRING_MANAGER}} token into an offer letter, please be aware that this will pull the Hiring Manager from the Hiring Team section of the job consideration on the candidate’s profile, rather than the hiring manager set in the offer details.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#can-i-set-the-offer-as-accepted-or-declined-within)Can I set the offer as accepted or declined within Ashby?
You can. Click View Offer on the offer activity, then Set Response within the offer details. You can then set the offer as accepted or declined on the candidate’s behalf.
Once an offer is accepted or declined, this button will show Update Response instead.
If the offer is marked as accepted, then the candidate will be moved into the Hired stage. If the offer is marked as declined, then you will be prompted to archive the candidate (but the candidate will not be archived automatically).
If a candidate was linked to an opening and then the offer is marked as declined, they will be removed from that opening.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2ae11a50e1497cf0a93a294091f37bee.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/05837831c03a581fd73ca9e7b2299964.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#can-i-view-the-offer-letter-or-offer-details-once)Can I view the offer letter or offer details once the candidate is hired?
You can, as long as you are an Organization Admin or have Elevated Access - Admin Private access to the job, location or department. Navigate to the candidate’s profile and then click on View Offer. Here you can view the offer details and click on the document links within the Offer Letter section to view the offer letter and documents sent to the candidate. E-Signature details are available in the Offer E-Signature section.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#how-do-i-change-the-name-of-my-offer-letter-templa)How do I change the name of my offer letter template?
To change the name of your offer letter template, navigate to Admin, then Offers. On the Offers page, click on the Offer Letter Templates tab, then select the offer letter template in question. You will then be able to amend the name of the template in the Name field.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#can-i-adjust-the-formatting-of-numbers-dates-and-c)Can I adjust the formatting of numbers, dates and currencies on my offer letters?
You can choose between U.S-style formatting and European-style formatting for numbers and specify currency formatting for offer letters and e-signature offers and documents. Navigate to Admin > Offers and click on the Settings tab to determine your offer settings. Alternatively, head to Admin > Integrations > E-Signature Documents and click on the Settings tab to configure your e-signature document settings.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ntpdOKQnAcUA5C3xll8aq-20260306-105723.png?format=webp)
﻿
  1. **Default Number Formatting:** Determine whether you would like numbers displayed in European style or U.S Style.


  1. **Default Currency Formatting:** Determine whether you would like currency to be displayed as a symbol ($) or Country Code (USD).


  1. **Default Date Formatting:** Select your preferred date display format from the drop-down list.


You will also have the option to override the default number and currency settings when generating offer letters.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/50db029cb014378141eadad4c98909ec.png)
﻿
It is not possible to override the date formatting setting in your offer or e-signature settings for a single offer letter.
If you override the number or currency settings when creating an offer, other e-signature documents added to the same packet inherit the same setting. If the default setting was not overridden, the global default is used for e-signature documents.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offers-and-offer-letters#can-previous-offer-versions-expire)Can previous offer versions expire?
There is not cut off or expiration date for offer versions. 
﻿  

﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
Analytics Glossary
# Lever Subject & Field Definitions
14 min
Copy
If you're an Ashby Analytics customer using Lever, here you can find a list of the subjects and filters available to you, along with definitions for your reference.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#subject-definitions)Subject Definitions  
|  Subject  |  Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Opportunity  |  A candidate’s record on a specific job.   |  
| Job  |  A job posting.   |  
| Interview  |  Scheduled interviews in your ATS. Interviews can be counted as one per stage (ie. an onsite panel with 4 different interviews will count as 1 onsite interview) or one per scheduled interview (ie. an onsite panel with 4 different interview sill count as 4 interviews).   |  
| Interviewer Participation  |  Scheduled interviewers. Each interviewer counts as an interview participation; these tie closely to feedback forms   |  
| Interview Stage Transition  |  Moves to and from stages. Accidental stage moves will be detected and counted.   |  
| Feedback Form   |  We pull in the person who submitted, when they submitted, and the overall recommendation (score out of 4) from feedback forms.   |  
| Requisition  |  Definition of hiring needs.   |  
| Candidate  |  Candidates in Lever.   |  
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#time-frame-filter-definitions)Time Frame Filter Definitions  
| Subject  | Time Frame Filter  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Opportunity  | Started At  | The time at which the opportunity was created. Either when a candidate applied or when a user considered them for the job in Ashby.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Last Activity At  | The point in time of the last activity performed on the opportunity.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Snoozed Until  | The date that the opportunity has a snooze alarm for. This can be found on the opportunity and is usually set by the recruiter.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Offer Stage Entered Date  | The point in time the opportunity entered the offer stage of the application process.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Hired At  | The point in time at which a candidate was hired for a job.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Archived At  | The point in time at which an application was marked as archived.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Consideration Ended At  | The point in time at which an opportunity was either marked as ‘Hired’ or ‘Archived’.  |  
| ﻿  
 | … Stage Group Interview At  | The time of the first scheduled interview in a given stage group.  |  
| Job  | Created At  | The time at which the job was created.  |  
| Interview  | Start Time  | The time when an interview took place. If the interview is a multi-panel interview, this time refers to the start time of the first interview.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Created At  | The time when an interview was created by the organizer. If the interview is a multi-panel interview, this time refers to when the first interview was created.  |  
| Interviewer Participation  | Start Time  | The time when an interviewer event started.  |  
| ﻿  
 | End Time  | The time when an interviewer event ended.  |  
| Interview Stage Transition  | Entered At  | Point in time at which a stage was entered.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Left At  | Point in time at which a stage was left.  |  
| Feedback Form  | Submitted At  | The point in time at which the feedback form was submitted.  |  
| Requisition  | Created At  | The time at which the requisition was created.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Closed At  | The time at which the requisition was closed.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Approved or Created At  | The date that a requisition completed approvals. If there is no approval flow, then it will be the date the requisition was created.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Estimated Start Date  | Requisition’s estimated start date.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Approved At  | The date that a requisition completed approvals. Since the Lever API does not expose their requisition open date, it is recommended to use this time filter as a proxy for open date.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Approval Started At  | The date the first step of the approval flow is initiated.  |  
| Candidate  | Last Activity At  | The point in time of the last activity performed on the candidate.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Added At  | The point in time at which the candidate was added to the system.  |  
| ﻿  
 | Hired At Date  | The point in time the candidate was first hired. When used in filters, finds candidates that have been hired during the given date range.  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#fields-definitions)Fields Definitions
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#opportunity-fields)Opportunity Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Current Interview Stage Name  | The title of the current interview stage name.  |  
| Current Interview Stage Group  | The title of the current interview stage group.  |  
| Days in Current Interview Stage Group  | The number of days the opportunity has been in the current interview stage group.  |  
| Average Overall Recommendation  | The average overall recommendation score given by interviewers for the opportunity.  |  
| Started At  | The time at which the opportunity was created. Either when a candidate applied or when a user created the opportunity in Ashby.  |  
| Last Activity At  | The point in time of the last activity performed on the opportunity.  |  
| Furthest Stage Group Reached  | Allows selecting and filtering by by the furthest stage group this opportunity has reached.  |  
| Furthest Stage Name Reached  | Allows selecting and filtering by by the furthest stage title this opportunity has reached.  |  
| Outcome  | The overall outcome of the opportunity. Can be one of Lead, Active, Archived, or Hired.  |  
| Outcome with Archive Reason  | The overall outcome of the opportunity, including the type of archive reason. Can be one of Lead, Active, Rejected by Org, Rejected by Candidate, Rejected Other, or Hired.  |  
| Job  | The job the candidate applied to or is considered for.  |  
| Hiring Manager  | The Hiring Manager assigned to this opportunity  |  
| Recruiter  | The Recruiter assigned to this opportunity.  |  
| Sourcer  | The Sourcer assigned to this opportunity.  |  
| Origin  | The opportunity’s origin in Lever.  |  
| Snoozed Until  | The date that the opportunity has a snooze alarm for. This can be found on the opportunity and is usually set by the recruiter.  |  
| Tags  | The opportunity’s tags in Lever.  |  
| Source  | The opportunity’s source in Lever.  |  
| Extended Offer?  | Whether or not your organization extended an offer for this opportunity.  |  
| Offer Stage Entered Date  | The point in time the opportunity entered the offer stage of the application process.  |  
| Archive Reason  | The reason an opportunity was archived.  |  
| Hired At  | The point in time at which a candidate was hired for a job.  |  
| Archived At  | The point in time at which an opportunity was marked as archived.  |  
| Consideration Ended At  | The point in time at which an opportunity was either marked as ‘Hired’ or ‘Archived’.  |  
| Candidate  | The individual candidate of this opportunity  |  
| Lever Profile URL  | The URL for the opportunity’s profile on the Lever ATS.  |  
| … Stage Group Interview At  | The time of the first scheduled interview in a given stage group.  |  
| Passed Through Stage Group/Name  | Allows filtering applications that have passed through a certain stage group/name, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will only return applications that have passed through that stage group/name, not applications that skipped the stage group/name. Note, passed through stage group/name will include candidates currently in the stage group/name.  |  
| Reached Stage Group/Name  | Allows filtering applications that have reached a certain stage group/name, e.g. 'Onsite'. Will return all applications that have passed through this stage group/name or have skipped this stage/stage name but passed through a later stage group/ name.  |  
| Most Recent Active Stage Group  | Allows selecting and filtering by the stage group the application was in when being hired or rejected.  |  
Other Subjects available in reports with the Opportunity Subject:
  * Candidate


  * Job


  * Interview


  * Requisition


  * Stage Transition


  * Offer


  * Feedback Form


  * Referral


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#job-fields)Job Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Job  | The job’s identity.  |  
| Title  | Represents the name or title of the job.  |  
| Requisition ID  | Alphanumerical identifier assigned to the job that is unique to your company’s internal processes.  |  
| Creator  | The user who created the job.  |  
| Hiring Manager  | The Hiring Manager who is on the hiring team of this job.  |  
| Recruiter  | The Recruiter who is on the hiring team of this job.  |  
| Team  | The department or team directly associated with this job. E.g. if a job is associated with ‘Product Marketing’ which is nested under ‘Marketing’, then this field will return ‘Product Marketing’.  |  
| Department  | The top-level department associated with this job. E.g. if a job is associated with ‘Product Marketing’ which is nested under ‘Marketing’, then this field will return ‘Marketing’.  |  
| Location  | The job’s location that is used as the default location for job postings of a job. Allows searching for jobs based on their location or the region in which the location is located.  |  
| Job Status  | The current status of the job. Can be Open, Closed, Archived, or Draft.  |  
| Employment Type  | The type of employment offered for the job. Can be Full time, Part time, Intern, Contract, or Temporary.  |  
| Created At  | The time at which the job was created.  |  
| Active Candidate Count  | How many candidates have applications in active interview or offer stages. Excludes candidates with applications in application review.  |  
| Number of Candidates in …  | The number of opportunities currently in a given stage group.  |  
| Commitment  | Synonymous with Work Type in Lever. The type of employment offered for the job.  |  
| Involves  | Returns any jobs where the user is on the hiring team or is specifically following the job.  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#interview-fields)Interview Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Scheduled in Stage Name  | The title of the stage in which this interview was scheduled.  |  
| Scheduled in Stage Group  | The group of the stage in which this interview was scheduled.  |  
| Start Time  | The time when an interview took place. If the interview is a multi-panel interview, this time refers to the start time of the first interview.  |  
| Duration  | Represents the length of time the interview took place.  |  
| Interviewer  | The user who conducted the interview.  |  
| Organizer  | The user who scheduled the interview.  |  
| Interview Title  | The title of the conducted interview.  |  
| Created At  | The time when an interview was created by the organizer. If the interview is a multi-panel interview, this time refers to when the first interview was created.  |  
Other Subjects available in reports with the Interview Subject:
  * Interviewer Participation


  * Opportunity


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#interviewer-participation-fields)Interviewer Participation Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Interviewer  | The user who was the interviewer of this interviewer participation.  |  
| Start Time  | The time when this interviewer event started.  |  
| End Time  | The time when this interviewer event ended.  |  
| Has Completed Feedback?  | Whether or not this interview has a completed feedback form.  |  
| RSVP Status  | The interviewer’s response to the interview calendar invitation.  |  
Other Subjects available in reports with the Interviewer Participation Subject:
  * Interview


  * Feedback Form


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#interview-stage-transition-fields)Interview Stage Transition Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| From Interview Stage Group  | The stage group transferred from.  |  
| To Interview Stage Group  | The stage group transferred to.  |  
| Outcome  | The outcome of this stage change. Possible values include: Moved Forward, Rejected by Org, Rejected by Candidate, Rejected - Other.  |  
| Entered At  | Point in time at which the stage was entered.  |  
| Left At  | Point in time at which the stage was left.  |  
| Days in Stage  | Number of full days within the stage.  |  
| Archive Reason  | When the new stage represents the archived stage, this represents archive reason.  |  
Other Subjects available in reports with the Interview Stage Transition Subject:
  * Opportunity


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#feedback-form-fields)Feedback Form Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Overall Recommendation  | The score submitted on the Overall Recommendation form field.  |  
| Submitted By  | The person who submitted the feedback form.  |  
| Submitted in Stage Group  | Allows filtering by the stage group the application was in when feedback was submitted, whether or not there was a scheduled interview.  |  
| Interview Title  | Title of the interview a feedback form was submitted for.  |  
Other Subjects available in reports with the Feedback Form Subject:
  * Opportunity


  * Interview


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#requisition-fields)Requisition Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Job  | The job the requisition is assigned to.  |  
| Team  | The department or team directly associated with this requisition. E.g. if a requisition is associated with ‘Product Marketing’ which is nested under ‘Marketing’, then this field will return ‘Product Marketing’.  |  
| Department  | The top-level department associated with this job. E.g. if a requisition is associated with ‘Product Marketing’ which is nested under ‘Marketing’, then this field will return ‘Marketing’.  |  
| Requisition ID  | Alphanumerical identifier that is unique to your company’s internal processes.  |  
| Created At  | The time at which the requisition was created.  |  
| Creator  | The person who created the requisition.  |  
| Closed At  | The time at which the requisition was closed.  |  
| Requisition Status  | Indicates the status of this requisition. Can be one of Open, Closed, On Hold, Draft, Rejected, or Pending.  |  
| Hiring Manager  | The Hiring Manager who is on the hiring team of this requisition.  |  
| Recruiter  | The Recruiter who is on the hiring team of this requisition.  |  
| Sourcer  | The Sourcer who is on the hiring team of this requisition.  |  
| Headcount Total  | Total headcount allotted for this requisition.  |  
| Open Headcount  | The remaining number of open headcount for this requisition.  |  
| Approved or Created At  | The date that a requisition completed approvals. If there is no approval flow, then it will be the date the requisition was created.  |  
| Approved At  | The date that a requisition completed approvals. Since the Lever API does not expose their requisition open date, it is recommended to use this time filter as a proxy for open date.  |  
| Approval Started At  | The date the first step of the approval flow is initiated.  |  
Other Subjects available in reports with the Requisition Subject:
  * Job


  * Opportunity


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/lever-subject-and-field-definitions#candidate-fields)Candidate Fields  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |

| Name  | The full name of the candidate.  |

| Email Address(es)  | All of the candidate’s email addresses.  |  
| LinkedIn Profile URL  | A URL to the candidate’s LinkedIn profile.  |

Other Subjects available in reports with the Candidate Subject:
  * Opportunity

| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |

💡 If you are using the race/ethnicity or gender inference, the fields below will be available to you.  
| Field  | Definition  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Gender  | Gender as reported in the candidate’s EEOC form when available. When unavailable, uses most likely gender based on candidate’s name.  |  
| Likely Gender  | The most likely gender based on the candidate’s name. When we are not confident enough in the gender, we return ‘Unknown’.  |  
| Race/Ethnicity  | Race/Ethnicity as reported in the candidate’s EEOC form when available. When unavailable, uses an estimate based on candidate’s name.  |  
| Likely Race/Ethnicity  | The most likely race and ethnicity based on the candidate’s name. When we are not confident enough in the race and ethnicity, we return ‘Unknown’.  |

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
