Analytics: Best Practices
# Best Practices: Managing & Analyzing Archive Reasons
22 min
Copy
This guide aims to review what makes for a good set of archive reasons﻿ and how to set your team up for success. With archive reasons in place, a set of example analyses to explore results is given, wherein the common field names and report types are reviewed.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#introduction)Introduction
This article will give a quick overview of utilizing and analyzing archive reasons to understand your recruiting trends. Successfully doing so can yield useful insights for both your top-of-funnel and late-stage recruiting activities, such as understanding trends in offer decline reasons. Before effective analysis can begin, you’ll need to make sure your archive reasons are amenable to analysis, so we’ll start with a basic recommended taxonomy and some comments on special cases.
Once your archive reasons are in good shape, we’ll discuss how to create a custom grouping of reasons to facilitate analysis, and we’ll conclude with some short [example analyses](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#hands-on-with-ashby-analytics-analysis-examples)﻿:
  * Looking at archive reasons by stage group


  * Taking a closer look filtering and exploring archive reasons


  * Examining archive reason trends over time


  * Utilizing archive reasons to re-engage candidates


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#how-to-set-up-archive-reasons-in-ashby)How to set up archive reasons in Ashby
You can add and/or edit archive reasons by heading to Admin > Jobs & Applications > [Archive Reasons](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/candidate-archive-reasons "Archive Reasons"). Click + New to add a new archive reason, or click on an existing archive reason to make any edits.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#creating-an-effective-taxonomy-of-archive-reasons)Creating an Effective Taxonomy of Archive Reasons
The purpose of adding archive reasons is to facilitate a review of why candidates are being rejected or dropping out, and that why should drive insights that help improve your team’s process. You may find, for example, that the primary offer decline reason shifted from relocation to compensation in the quarter you implemented new policies. This raises the point that you’ll want to examine these reasons, generally speaking, via two broad methods: by trend over time and in aggregate.
![The trend in reasons over time to reveal changes due to your team’s process and external market factors.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/n7JOLTtYK7cMq3p5LopWV_rejectedatonsiteinterviewbycategoryovertime.png?format=webp)
﻿
The trend in reasons over time to reveal changes due to your team’s process and external market factors.
![A segmentation analysis, e.g. by department or interview stage group, helps identify dominant reasons.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/naFbEtx-DSXfTDtE4a0sV_onsiterejectionsbycandidate.png?format=webp)
﻿
A pitfall to avoid: ambiguous reasons. You want your reasons taken in aggregate to be informative.
Some recommendations to help identify the archive reasons that matter to your team the most
  * What stage of the recruiting process are we most invested in improving? Do the archive reasons we have facilitate that process?


  * Do you have any unique factors at your company or team that need to be accounted for? This can show up in archive reasons unique to evaluating certain company values or evaluating a very specific industry experience or skills that go beyond “lacks skills” type reasons.


  * Pay close attention to whether the given reason is informative. One of the most common archive reasons we see is “offer declined.” This reason makes sense when doing a very basic analysis of comparing all archives and seeing how many are offer declines. However, given you can group and filter by stage (and other conditions of interest) with Ashby, such a general offer reason does not provide much insight. Six months from now when you analyze offer declines, you’ll want to identify actionable signals about this critical stage in your recruiting process.


  * Another common archive reason is “Other”, which in aggregate can be taken to represent what fraction of your candidate archives lack informative categorization. In our experience, the “other” option is a well-intentioned but easy default for recruiting teams that need to move quickly. We recommend checking if “other” is a significant portion of your archive reasons and reviewing your available reasons if so.


  * Consider what stage in your process you might use a particular reason. “Not a fit for role” might be acceptable at the application review or screen stage, but for later stages, more specific reasons may match hiring criteria, such as values or skill and level-based reasons can help optimize your process.


  * Another common archive reason is “Withdrew.” This, in our opinion, is more of a category than a reason: there are many reasons a candidate may withdraw from the interview process and your analysis should reveal them. Remember to ask yourself whether your archive reason informs a potential insight or action for your team.


  * Archive reasons can also help other teams in your organization. For example, using the "Fraudulent candidate" reason can enable your security teams to identify and track suspicious activity more easily through a centralized marker.


If you’re just getting started, or looking to improve your current setup, the next section presents a standard set of archive reasons by category.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#recommendation-an-example-of-a-set-of-archive-reas)Recommendation: An Example of a Set of Archive Reasons
Ashby supports archive reason categories and our Greenhouse customers inherit the Greenhouse categories Rejected by Org or Rejected by Candidate. Lever does not support archive reason categories, but Ashby’s [Calculated Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/calculated-fields)﻿ can help resolve that challenge by creating custom groupings.
Depending on the size of your organization the level of detail in your archive reasons may vary. Typically, larger organizations can support more reasons as the larger volume of applicants permits more specificity. For this reason, there is a judgment call here: if you’re a small team just getting started, you may not want to get too detailed on all the reasons a candidate can exit consideration. For starters, an example set of archive reasons by archive reason groupings is given below.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#category-rejection-by-organization)Category: Rejection by Organization
The reasons your organization will utilize may vary depending on your interview process, industry details, or certain policies. The reasons below are given as a standard starting set, but you may need to add more, e.g. specific values-related reasons.  
| Rejection by Organization Reason |  
| --- |  
| Lacks work authorization |  
| Role fit: general skills/qualifications |  
| Role fit: experience level - too senior |  
| Role fit: experience level - too junior |  
| Reference/Background Check |  
| Location - unwilling to relocate / remote policy |  
| Values fit reason(s), e.g. "Value fit - team player" |  
| Follow up - Role Filled |  
| Fraudulent/fake candidate |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#category-rejection-by-candidate-reasons)Category: Rejection by Candidate Reasons
The offer-specific reasons below are given as a more detailed example. You may not find all of them necessary at your current company size.  
| Rejection by Candidate Reason  | Offer Specific Reasons  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Remain with current company  | Offer Declined - Leveling  |  
| Not interested - General  | Offer Declined - Compensation Salary  |  
| Not interested - Industry  | Offer Declined - Compensation Equity  |  
| Not interested - Role / Project  | Offer Declined - Career growth  |  
| Not interested - Career Growth  | Offer Declined - Accepted competing offer  |  
| Accepted other offer  | Offer Declined - Remain at current company  |  
| Unresponsive  | Offer Declined - Other  |  
| Bad Timing  | Offer Reneged  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#category-rejection-due-to-process-or-data-clean-up)Category: Rejection due to process or data clean up (aka, “Other”)
The reasons that fall in this category should be thought of as those you most likely do not want to include in your archive reason analysis, barring analysis that is looking at, e.g., data quality or re-engagement. For example, when analyzing offer rejection reasons you will want to exclude cases of archiving candidates that were moved to a different role.  
| Other Reasons |  
| --- |  
| Did not respond to outreach |  
| Position closed / on hold |  
| Transferred to another role |  
| Position filled |  
| Cleanup - Inactivity |  
| Non-compete |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#custom-groupings-with-calculated-fields)Custom Groupings with Calculated Fields
If you _do_ specify many reasons you can minimize the fragmentation of reasons by creating custom groupings with Ashby’s [Calculated Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/calculated-fields)﻿ feature. Apart from the standard grouping above, you may want to consider special use cases like grouping all archive reasons that permit following up with the candidate at a later date. A custom grouping of the above reasons to select candidates for re-engagement may look like the following.  
| Custom Grouping Reasons |  
| --- |  
| Position filled |  
| Position closed / on hold |  
| Location - unwilling to relocate / remote policy |  
| Follow up / Future consideration |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#hands-on-with-ashby-analytics-analysis-examples)Hands-On with Ashby Analytics: Analysis Examples
Below we present a few pragmatic examples types related to archive reasons. A short description and recorded example is provided per analysis, but for reference we find the below fields to be some of the most useful when navigating and analyzing archive reasons.  
| Ashby Fieldname  | Why it's helpful  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Archived At  | Allows you to select the timeframe in which the applications you want to analyze were archived.  |  
| Furthest Stage Group Reached  | Allows you to group by or filter to the furthest stage group the application made it to.  |  
| Archive Reason  | Gives the specific archive reason used.  |  
| Archive Reason Type  | Gives the broad archive category, rejected by org, rejected by candidate or other.  |  
﻿
The above is just one of many useful fields for analysis, others being Department or Team, Source Type (Origin for Lever users), or potentially hiring team member fields such as Recruiter and Hiring Manager. If you’ve created [Calculated Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/calculated-fields)﻿ for custom grouping, you’ll be able to use those for your analysis, too!
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#archive-reason-by-stage-group)Archive Reason by Stage Group
With Ashby, you can examine archive reasons by the stage group in which the candidate was archived. The composition of reasons should change as candidates progress through your interview process, so this analysis permits you to keep an eye out for things like on-site reasons being appropriately matched to the on-site stage. If you find that candidates are being archived for reasons such as lack of fit, or not interested in relocating, that’s a sign that prior stages which should be screening for such factors are not functioning, resulting in precious time and resources being expended at the on-site.
![Candidate Rejections by Stage Group.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ld80FyIO9zofvjj-aqOhm_candidaterejectionsbystagegroup.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#narrow-the-scope-of-analysis-explore-onsite-stage)Narrow the Scope of Analysis & Explore: Onsite Stage Group
We can focus on the previous analysis by examining the onsite stage group specifically. With this narrowed scope we can examine other variables that may be significant factors. In the example below we take a closer look at the onsite rejections by candidate by source type, revealing that agencies result in a higher rate of candidates that weren’t interested in the opportunity. In general, as you proceed with an exploratory analysis consider filtering and grouping your analysis to a specific department, region, or interview stage.
![Candidate Rejections by Source Type at Onsite.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/cqaRpWQkIgdngMBl_qIxA_candidaterejectionsbysourcetypeatonsite.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#look-at-the-trend-over-time)Look at the Trend Over Time
Plotting archive reasons over time can be challenging if your recruiting efforts are not at a sufficient volume. Two approaches to help with that are to look for trends in the broader archive reason categories (e.g. rejected by candidate) or custom groupings or to use longer time periods, such as a quarterly time series as opposed to a weekly time series. This type of analysis is especially important in evaluating policy changes or external market factors. In the example below we see some reasons change quarter to quarter, while others (accepting another offer) remain present, suggesting that time to offer may be a strategic initiative to improve.
![Engineering Candidate Rejections Over Time.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/muQmkwk4xpduvPFZ83KI9_engineeringcandidaterejectionsovertime.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#utilize-archive-reasons-for-re-engagement)Utilize Archive Reasons for Re-engagement
Candidate re-engagement is a cost-effective manner of sourcing talent. With targeted archive reasons you can filter by previously interviewed candidates to drive contextualized outreach at a later date. For example, if your remote work policy changes, you can follow up with all candidates archived under the “relocation / remote policy” reason and let them know of new opportunities.
![Screen Shot 2021-11-03 at 9.27.55 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/UBSca4nw1WjWMT_MwzfVp_screenshot2021-11-03at92755pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#conclusions)Conclusions
We hope the above summary is useful as a refresher and recommendation on how to manage and analyze your archive reasons. We covered the rationale for setting up helpful archive reasons, some prompts to evaluate what makes a good archive reason, and how to set your team up for success in the long run with some example analyses.
If you have any feedback, thoughts, or recommendations on this topic we’d love to hear from you! Feel free to reach out to support@ashbyhq.com - we love hearing from you!
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-managing-and-analyzing-archive-reasons#who-has-access-to-add-or-edit-archive-reasons)Who **has access to add or edit archive reasons?**
See the table below for information on the roles that have access to configure archive reasons.  
| Access Role  | Has Access?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ❌*  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin Private  | ❌*  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
*Only Organization Admins can set up archive reasons by default. Elevated Access users can be given access to archive reasons if needed. More on this at Additional User Permissions.
﻿

application-requests:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Applications
# Application Requests
14 min
Copy
You can send out application requests to candidates who have been entered into Ashby without filling in an application form (for example, those who have been sourced or referred). This tool helps to ensure you have complete information across your candidates and job considerations.
You can either send application requests manually or configure automation rules to send out application requests automatically. Once completed, the application form will be added directly to the candidate's job consideration.
Application request links are candidate and job consideration-specific. The same link cannot be used across multiple candidates. Application requests are always tied to the default job posting on a job. If you do not have a default job posting, you'll need to set one for application requests to work. See [Default job posting](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-postings#default-job-posting)﻿ for more information.
Application requests are available on the Plus and Enterprise plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#creating-an-application-request-email-template)Creating an application request email template
We would recommend setting up an email template with an application request link substitution token as a first step. An email template with this substitution token is required for automation rules and can also help streamline the process of manually generating application requests. 
To create a new email template for application requests:
  * Navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > [Email Templates](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/email-templates "Email Templates")﻿


  * Click New Template


  * Add in the subject and body for your email template. To add in the application request link substitution token, click the T icon on the template toolbar and select the Application Request Link token.


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/c-F5Emokh2Qz3yKAfFiy8_email-templates-acent-ashby-2026-01-09-at-25647-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#configuring-application-request-automation)Configuring application request automation
Application request automation allows you to set criteria and your email settings in advance. Then any candidates meeting the set criteria will receive an email with an application request link so they can complete their application form.
To configure an automation rule:
  * Navigate to Admin > Jobs & Applications > [Application Request Automations](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/application-request-settings "Application Request Automations")﻿


  * Set up your draft configuration:


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/UdTEFqRL-DBUsOAZ4KhNX_application-request-automations-acent-ashby-2026-01-09-at-30212-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * Click Add Field to Match within the Condition section to determine your fields and values. Candidates who match the conditions you create here will trigger the automation.


Please note that conditions are evaluated each time a candidate moves to a new stage in your process. The following conditions are also built in to your application request conditions:
  * The candidate must not be in an Archived state


  * The candidate must not already have an application submitted for this job consideration.


  * Use the Email Template dropdown to select the email template used to email the candidate. Please note that the email template must include the Application Request Link substitution token.


  * Confirm the sender of the email. If you would like it to come from another user in your Ashby account, check out [Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿ for more information on sending emails on another user's behalf.


  * If you'd like to configure an additional automation rule, click Create New Automation Rule﻿


  * Once you've configured your automation rules, click Activate Draft Configuration and then Enable Automation? to activate your configuration. Moving forward, candidates who meet your criteria will trigger the automation.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#manually-generating-an-application-request)Manually generating an application request
If you'd like to send out application requests manually, you can take the following steps:
  * Navigate to the profile of the candidate in question


  * Click More, then Send Application Form to Candidate


  * Confirm the job listed in the Job dropdown


  * Click Create to generate the application request activity, or Create and Email to generate the activity and draft up an email to the candidate


﻿  

![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/W5Igt5J5jaPizTvpAycRs_irene-mcintosh-customer-support-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-01-09-at-30347-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#viewing-and-amending-the-application-request)Viewing and amending the application request
Once generated, an activity will be added to the candidate's profile so you can track the activity status. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/6PNXkVbjGqzBuQnLWcqe4_trudy-wilson-infrastructure-engineer-acent-ashby-2026-01-09-at-30639-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can click the three dots to the right of the activity to take the following actions:
  * **Copy Application Request Link:** Copy the application request URL to share with the candidate directly


  * **Mark as Sent:** Mark the application request link as sent and update the status of the activity


  * **Delete Application Request:** Remove the application request activity from the candidate's profile.


Once the candidate has completed the application request, the activity will be resolved and removed from the Current Activities section of their profile. The completed form will show within the Forms section of the candidate's feed, along with a note that the candidate applied via an application request.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/DcercfpXkWrk-YNDoPyYT_irene-mcintosh-customer-support-specialist-acent-ashby-2026-01-09-at-30821-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#why-isnt-the-email-template-showing-up-under-the-a)W**hy isn't the email template showing up under the automation configuration?**
The application request token must be used in the email template for it to show up.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#what-happens-after-a-candidate-completes-the-appli)W**hat happens after a candidate completes the application?**
  * The request is removed from the current activities section of the candidate's profile


  * The application is marked as originating from a request


  * Moving them to a new stage will not trigger another request


﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#does-transferring-a-candidate-to-a-new-job-trigger)D**oes transferring a candidate to a new job trigger a new application request?**
No. Transfers do not generate a new request because the system already has the candidate's information.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#does-considering-a-candidate-for-a-different-job-t)D**oes considering a candidate for a different job trigger a new application request?**
Yes. If you consider a candidate for a separate job (not a transfer), they can receive an application request for that job if they meet the criteria you have set.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#which-job-posting-is-used-when-a-job-has-multiple)W**hich job posting is used when a job has multiple postings?**
The one marked as the default job posting.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#what-are-the-requirements-for-a-job-posting-to-be)W**hat are the requirements for a job posting to be valid for application requests?**
  * The job's job posting must be published (does not need to be publicly listed)


  * Must be on the external primary job board as internal-only postings will not work


﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#what-happens-if-the-job-posting-becomes-inaccessib)W**hat happens if the job posting becomes inaccessible after the email is sent?**
The email cannot be recalled, but an error will display on the application request on the candidate's profile indicating the issue. The candidate's link will no longer work when clicked.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d44bd397a75cb2a6bc4dbf641772ccce.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#what-conditions-cause-an-error-or-prevent-the-requ)W**hat conditions cause an error or prevent the request from sending?**
  * The job consideration's job is closed


  * A published job posting doesn't exist for that job


  * The default job posting is set to display on the internal job board only.


﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#will-automation-work-on-all-candidates-who-meet-th)W**ill automation work on all candidates who meet the conditions configured by the user?**
There are some additional conditions we have under the hood that the user can't see but the candidate must meet. These are:
  * 1) Candidate must be not in the Archived stage for that job consideration 


  * 2) Candidate must not already have an application form submitted for this job consideration


﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#how-do-auto-reject-rules-and-application-limits-in)H**ow do auto-reject rules and application limits interact with this?**
If an application is submitted through an application request link, we don't check those applications against auto-reject or application limit rules.
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#how-do-i-set-up-application-requests-to-go-out-to)How do I set up application requests to go out to direct referrals automatically?
We don't support adding application requests directly in the [referral confirmation](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/referrals-and-referral-links#referral-confirmation-email)﻿ sent to referred candidates. If the {Application Request Link} substitution token is added to your organization's [referral confirmation template](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/referral-forms "referral confirmation template"), the email will fail.
Instead, for direct referrals, we would recommend [configuring an application request automation ](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-requests#configuring-application-request-automation "configuring an application request automation ")with conditions specifying that the job consideration is a direct referral (for example Job Consideration's Is Direct Referral is Yes). You can also opt to configure a catch-all for candidates who don't have an application completed by a certain stage (for example, setting a condition field to be Job Consideration's Reached Stage Type with the value as Active) to ensure any candidates in active stages are prompted to submit an application for the job consideration. If you create application request automations for referrals, then we would recommend disabling your referral confirmation template. The application request automation can be used instead to send the referral confirmation and application request in a single email.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Q5tMOHK8mo0l7QKEzjg_E_application-request-released-slab-2026-03-09-at-115603-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

﻿

<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Emails & Candidate Texting
Emails
# Can I send emails in bulk?
11 min
Copy
You can! In this guide, we’ll cover how to send out emails to multiple candidates at once. For more on configuring templates, please check out [Email Template Creation and Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-template-creation-and-management)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#create-the-audience-for-your-email)Create the audience for your email
You can use filters on the Candidate Search page, or select multiple candidates in a job pipeline to determine who should receive an email.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#candidate-search)Candidate Search
You can read more on filtering with candidate searches at [Candidate Search](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-search)﻿.
Once you’ve set up the filters and determined the audience for your email, you can either click the Select All button to select all candidates who meet your criteria, or use the checkbox next to each candidate to select them.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d2d441fa14651043e19d574c03df7036.png)
﻿
Once your audience has been selected, the bulk action options will become available. Click the Email option to open up the Bulk Action panel.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/479906823ef4f079b848f00c83e8fd6a.png)
﻿
Drafts will automatically be drawn up for each candidate. Click Review Drafts to be taken to the email editor.
You can then determine your email template, make any adjustments and fix any errors prior to sending.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/553036fe59d0c4224aad3f82c629ae29.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#job-pipeline)Job Pipeline
When in a job, click the Select button. You can then select the candidates in the pipeline you’d like to send a mass email to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5a191e3421f583cab95f7e79f839b429.png)
﻿
Once you’ve selected the candidates who should receive the email, the bulk actions you can take should become available. Click Email button to start the drafting process.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/45a45ee4d14c71ffd0143465c1cd1119.png)
﻿
The drafts will be generated for the selected candidates. Click Review Drafts to select an email template, personalize the emails further and to fix any errors.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/553036fe59d0c4224aad3f82c629ae29.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#reviewing-and-editing-your-drafts)Reviewing and Editing Your Drafts
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5d84041266175beb35a416873e453017.png)
﻿
1) No Issues/With Warnings/Cannot Send: This key helps you to determine if any emails have errors that need fixing prior to sending. In this case, both email drafts are missing a subject and a body so neither can be sent. The overall email draft is shown when All Recipients is selected, so any adjustments impact all emails sent. To adjust and fix errors for individual emails, click on a recipient in the Personalize Per Recipient section.
2) Personalize Per Recipient: View each email recipient and click on a recipient to view and amend each email draft individually.
3) Template: Select the email template you’d like to use for the email.
4) Send: Determine when you’d like the email to be sent to recipients.
5) From: Select the sender of the email. You can send on behalf of someone else if they have shared their email address with you. More on this at [Email Sharing](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-sharing)﻿.
6) Subject: Add in the Subject line here.
7) Body: Add in the body of the email here.
8) Cc/Bcc: If other users or email addresses need to be looped into the email, add them here.
9) Subject Substitution tokens: Add any substitution tokens﻿ here. Our full list of tokens can be found at [Substitution Tokens](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/substitution-tokens)﻿.
10) Cancel: Leave the editor. You can opt to either keep the drafts for editing later or delete them entirely.
11) Send X Emails: Send out your emails in bulk.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#bulk-email-send-times)Bulk Email Send Times
If you’re sending 10 campaigns or more at a time, then they will be sent over a period of time.
  * < 10 campaigns go out simultaneously and immediately.


  * 10 - 100 campaigns are sent out over a 30-minute period.


Please note that scheduled emails may send 0-15 minutes after the scheduled time.
You can send an email to up to 500 candidates at a time if sending from a user email address. When using the no-reply@ashbyhq.com email address, this upper limit is 5000.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#im-seeing-a-missing-value-for-message-when-pulling)**I’m seeing a Missing Value For message when pulling up an email template. Why?**
This error will show if the value cannot be found. 
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/51dac655543e89e6bcb6c50c0518db50.png)
﻿
This is particularly common when:
  * You’re trying to send a direct booking link but an interview activity hasn’t been generated and a booking link hasn’t been created for the candidate.


  * You’re trying to send a candidate availability link but an interview activity hasn’t been generated and a candidate availability link hasn’t been created for the candidate.


  * If you’re trying to send out a survey and the survey request and link haven’t been generated yet.


  * If an interview has not been scheduled yet (panel information can only be pulled for interview emails if the interview has already been scheduled).


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#can-i-bulk-email-archived-candidates)Can **I bulk email archived candidates?**
Yes. When on the Archived stage of a job’s pipeline, select the candidates you would like to email using the checkboxes and click Email. You will then be able to either email out a template or draft an email to send out in bulk.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#who-can-send-bulk-emails)Who can send bulk emails?
Users can only send bulk emails to candidates that they have edit permissions for. Depending on the user's permissions, they may or may not be able to email candidates.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-send-emails-in-bulk#can-i-set-limits-on-how-bulk-emails-are-sent)Can I set limits on how bulk emails are sent?
Yes! The ability to send bulk emails can be limited in the below ways:
  * Bulk emails are capped at 500 per bulk action. Additionally, Google and Microsoft (depending on your email provider) may impose limits on the number of daily emails that can go out.


  * Users can see the date of the last communication with the candidate, and Admins can determine an [outreach frequency policy](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/outreach-frequency-policy "outreach frequency policy") for sourcing to prevent candidates from being contacted too frequently.


﻿

newsletters:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Sourcing & Outreach
# Newsletters
18 min
Copy
You can now create, customise and send newsletters to leads and candidates within Ashby. If someone signs up for a talent pool, applies for a role or attends a virtual or in-person event you can send out updates and information on future roles.
Newsletters can be configured on the Enterprise plan only:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#configure-a-newsletter-domain)Configure a Newsletter Domain
To configure a domain, you will need access to manage the DNS records of the domain you’re configuring. You can find our guide on adding your domain at [Email Domain Setup](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/email-domain-setup)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#creating-a-newsletter)Creating a Newsletter
To create a newsletter, head to Sourcing _,_ then [Newsletters](https://app.ashbyhq.com/sourcing/newsletters/all "Newsletters"), then click New. You’ll be asked to give your newsletter a title before proceeding.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e3ac7f997e9e2a6dbdc4312e6b12cbbb.png)
﻿
You will then be taken to the newsletter editor from here.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#newsletter-editor)Newsletter Editor
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#page-overview)Page Overview
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/549d63e265f8343be5d5216d2dfbe78a.png)
﻿
  1. **Audience:** Here you can review candidates who have received your newsletter and track metrics like opens and clicks.


  1. **Edit:** The editing page for your newsletter.


  1. **Desktop/Mobile view:** Click between the views to see what your newsletter looks like on a desktop and on a mobile device.


  1. **Review + Send:** Once your newsletter contents, your audience, subject line and sender are determined, you can send out your newsletter from here.


  1. **Settings:** Amend your newsletter title, add filters to create your audience and add your subject and sender for your newsletter email here.


  1. **More:** Here you have the options to create a copy of your newsletter, or archive it.


  1. **Content:** Here you can drag and drop blocks to add content to your newsletter.


  1. **Rows:** You can click and drag row structures for your content.


  1. **Settings (Newsletter Content):** Content-specific settings like the default font and background color can be set here.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#adding-content-to-your-newsletter)Adding Content to Your Newsletter
When building your newsletter, you can drag row structures over from the Rows tab to create your structure. Once you have rows in place, you can drag your content blocks over to start adding in text, videos, images and more.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4a9097edf7a355bcfb10960eafe0dc45.png)
﻿
When dragging and dropping a row into your newsletter, be sure to drag it to the very bottom of the previous row or between two rows (you will see a Drag it here message before dropping it). If you drag and drop a row without seeing the Drag it here message, the row will not be added.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c3b749d5b03447b4befb35f8249dbfab.png)
﻿
Once you’ve added a new row, the spaces to drop content blocks in should appear. Click on the Contents tab to access your content options and click and drag the blocks as needed.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/234c297311f3b3b21087529222b22e53.png)
﻿
Be sure to hit the Save button to save your newsletter as you make edits!
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#content-block-options)Content Block Options
**Title:** Add a heading to your newsletter or titles throughout.
**Paragraph:** Add text in a paragraph format.
**List:** Add text in a list format.
**Image:** Add an image.
**Button:** Add a button - the button can send users to a web page, prompt them to send an email, make a call or send an SMS. Clicks are tracked using buttons you add to your newsletter.
**Divider:** Divide content with a single line.
**Spacer:** Add space between content blocks.
**Social:** Add icons that link to social pages. The icons and URLs can be amended as needed in the content properties tab.
**HTML:** Add your own code to one of these blocks. Please be aware that adding your own code can have an impact on how the code is rendered.
**Video:** Add a Vimeo or YouTube video link to generate a preview image in your newsletter. This image will then link to the video in question.
**Icons:** Configure and add any company-specific icons you’d like to use in newsletters.
**Menu:** Configure a menu with options that can link out to website or prompt the user to complete actions like sending an email, SMS or making a call.
**Text:** A text block with no specific structure.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#previewing-a-newsletter)Previewing a Newsletter
Once you’ve added content and saved your newsletter, you can preview it by heading to Actions, then Preview.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/24526ec4fa3e645db20ea09d0a0562b9.png)
﻿
On the following preview screen, you’ll be able to view how the newsletter will look to candidates, both on a desktop and a mobile device. To exit the preview screen, click the X icon.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/76d021de2505ecd6b362a77d52a751c8.png)
﻿
**Note:** Clicking on Youtube and Instagram social links in a newsletter preview leads to an error. These links will function correctly when the email is officially sent.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#content-settings)Content Settings
The Settings tab in the editor allows you to make adjustments to how the content is displayed in your newsletter. You have the following options:
**Content Area Width:** Determine how wide the area the content is contained in should be.
**Content Area Alignment:** Determine whether you’d like content to be left or center-aligned.
**Background Color:** Determine the background color of the newsletter.
**Content Area Background Color:** Determine the background color of the area content is contained in.
**Default Font:** Select the font that should be used by default in your newsletter.
**Link Color:** Confirm the color that hyperlinked text should show in.
You also have the option to add a preheader in the Optional Properties section.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/602bdfc756bcf004dbbd35c0f1b6063f.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#settings-and-adding-an-audience)Settings and Adding an Audience
Once you’ve added your newsletter content and structure, you can set up your audience, or determine the candidates and leads that should receive this newsletter.
To start setting up your audience, click the Settings button above the editor.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1506f33fc39ed840251b2c9f072d52c8.png)
﻿
You’ll see the following options:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3390d55abeef8262d4b8540519dca51d.png)
﻿
**Title:** Adjust your newsletter’s title.
**Audience:** Add filters here to determine the candidates that you would like to send this newsletter to. Please note that candidates who have already unsubscribed or are marked as ‘Do not contact’ will automatically be excluded.
**From:** Determine the sender of the email from the drop-down.
If you send your newsletter from an email that doesn’t have an inbox that’s regularly monitored, you can set a custom reply-to address here by unchecking the Use this email as the newsletter’s reply-to address checkbox. The reply-to address added will receive replies to the newsletter instead.
**Subject:** Add in the email subject here.
**Include unsubscribe link:** Newsletters are required to include an unsubscribe link, so you will not have the option to uncheck this box.
The Unsubscribe link will be listed in the bottom-left corner of your newsletter.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/1aoMj37tzfzWQHjeQKK7J_screenshot-20251124-082056-email-2026-04-10-at-101809-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#using-filters-to-create-your-audience)Using Filters to Create Your Audience
To set up your audience, click Add Field To Match and add in filters as needed. In our example, I’m searching for leads and candidates that have been added to a specific project (more on projects at [Projects](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/projects)﻿).
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b9db6a1bb6e2f7e3084e4d89def7e3da.png)
﻿
Once your audience has been filtered for, you can click View Audience to see the full list of leads and candidates to confirm this is the correct audience for your newsletter.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d26f473b19e14780df32a9f18d29010a.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#sending-your-newsletter)Sending Your Newsletter
To start the process of sending out your newsletter to your audience, click Review and Send. From here, you’ll be able to review your settings and view the newsletter contents and your audience one more time
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f4d454e05497b3b0b61f01217fb87019.png)
﻿
If you need to make any adjustments to the settings or the content, click Cancel to be taken back to the editor. To ship out your newsletter, click _Send Newsletter._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c7ca1d7a8ba2507ce0dae199f9b6eda5.png)
﻿
If you are sending out your newsletter to a large audience, these will be sent out over a longer period of time. You can track the send status for each candidate on the Audience tab for your newsletter once the sending process has started.
If your organization's bounce rate for newsletter emails becomes too high, Ashby may disable your ability to send newsletters. To maintain a healthy email sending reputation and ensure continued delivery of your newsletters, it's crucial to keep your bounce rate low by only sending newsletters to candidates that have actively signed up to receive newsletters from your organization.
To build a list of newsletter subscribers, we recommend using [Sourcing Forms](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms)﻿.
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/newsletters#tracking-open-rates-and-click-rates)Tracking Open Rates and Click Rates
You can track the size of your newsletter audience, how many emails have been sent and your open and click rates for your newsletter on the Audience tab of your newsletter. You can add filters or search by specific candidate name as needed.
You can also use the Audience tab to track the status of each candidate’s email when sending out newsletters.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/95663521f1a381fe821cbfc3aa946cb4.png)
﻿
To track clicks, be sure to add a link or button to your newsletter.
Looking to build up an audience? Check out our guide on [Sourcing Forms](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sourcing-forms)﻿
﻿

# Greenhouse Integration Setup
6 min
Copy
This document reviews how to enable direct links into your Greenhouse instance by making sure your Greenhouse subdomain is configured correctly. Please note that this route is for Analytics only customers. For more on the Greenhouse Migration for all-in-one customers, check out [Greenhouse Integration Setup](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-integration-setup)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-integration-setup#greenhouse-integration-setup)Greenhouse integration setup
Your Greenhouse integration configuration can be found in your admin section by navigating to [Admin > Integrations > Greenhouse](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/greenhouse "Admin > Integrations > Greenhouse"). To complete your initial Greenhouse integration setup you will need to
  * Create a Harvest API key and enter it in Ashby


  * Confirm your custom subdomain


  * Setup Greenhouse web hook events


![Screen Shot 2022-11-21 at 2.13.07 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/3bsmYig7qvtcKJRdT_VNu_screenshot2022-11-21at21307pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Harvest API Key: enter your harvest API key here to kick off your initial data sync


  1. Custom Subdomain: input your custom subdomain to enable linking directly to candidate profiles from Ashby


  1. Sync Status: review your current sync status to ensure regular operation


  1. Last sync time: review the last time your data sync updated


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-integration-setup#harvest-api-key-sync-status)Harvest API key & sync status
First, you should create a Greenhouse API Key. You can do that yourself through the admin section in Greenhouse. These are the steps:
  * Go to [API Credential Management](https://app2.greenhouse.io/configure/dev_center/credentials "API Credential Management") in Greenhouse


  * Create a Harvest type API key and grant permissions for all "GET" endpoints


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/0aa2d4f4039dae8547a68c9c9a433c3b.png)
﻿
Once you have created your API key you can add it to Ashby:
  * Go to [https://app.ashbyhq.com](https://app.ashbyhq.com "https://app.ashbyhq.com") and sign in.


  * Add the API key to your [Ashby Greenhouse settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/greenhouse "Ashby Greenhouse settings")﻿


Adding the API key will initiate your initial data sync. After your initial data sync completes regular syncs will continue once per hour to automatically ingest new data and updates. The timezone of the last successful sync is displayed in your Ashby user’s timezone.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-integration-setup#set-up-greenhouse-web-hooks)Set up Greenhouse web hooks
To capture certain events in real time, such as candidate stage transitions, hires and various record deletions, you’ll want to ensure your Greenhouse web hooks are configured properly. Please make sure to [follow these setup instructions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-greenhouse-web-hooks)﻿ and reach out to support@ashbyhq.com if any assistance is required.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-integration-setup#configure-custom-subdomain)Configure custom subdomain
Your Greenhouse instance may have a custom subdomain, such as your company name or one of Greenhouse’s multiple servers. To find your subdomain, login to Greenhouse and view any candidate or job profile, your url will have the form of [subdomain].greenhouse.io/.... Once you have found your subdomain you can enter it in the [Admin > Integrations > Greenhouse](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/integrations/greenhouse "Admin > Integrations > Greenhouse") settings.
![Screen Shot 2022-05-13 at 3.38.09 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/K5la_OOFduxHsrhyv1dDB_screenshot2022-05-13at33809pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
After your subdomain is updated, you will have a “View in Greenhouse” button when viewing a candidate profile. This links directly to your candidate profiles. Additionally, [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿ will provide links to your candidate profiles for convenient access.
![Screen Shot 2022-11-21 at 2.15.35 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/0TWcvEeYOlyFWlCyGcFkX_screenshot2022-11-21at21535pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/greenhouse-integration-setup#enable-candidate-actions-from-ashby)Enable candidate actions from Ashby
If you would like to take action on candidates from within Ashby, such as drag-and-drop from the candidate pipeline view, stage change, or adding notes, you will need to update your Harvest API key permissions. To do so, please add the following permissions and contact support@ashbyhq.com or your success manager to request these features be enabled.
![Screen Cast 2022-05-13 at 4.31.45 PM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/CGh4CKJatEOuEFor2lOY-_screencast2022-05-13at43145pm.gif)
﻿
**Under application:**
PATCH: Update Application
POST: Advance Application
POST: Move Application
POST: Reject Application
POST: Moves Application to Job
POST: Unreject Application
POST: Hire Application
**Under candidate:**
POST: Add Note

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Advanced Setup
# Debriefs
11 min
Copy
Debrief activities can now be scheduled for your hiring team and interviewers. You can add a debrief to a schedule interview activity as part of the template, or add it via the candidate’s profile as a one-off activity.
Debriefs are available on the Foundations, Plus, Legacy Plus and Enterprise Plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#creating-a-debrief-activity)Creating a debrief activity
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#setting-your-debrief-defaults)Setting your debrief defaults
You can configure your default debrief settings in Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > [Debrief Defaults](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/debrief-defaults/debrief-schedule "Debrief Defaults"). These settings will be applied to debriefs you create moving forward but can be amended for each individual debrief.
On the Schedule page, you can determine the duration and who should attend the debrief. You can set attendees based on their hiring team role on the job, opt to include past interviewers or not and also add any additional attendees.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e8f0a885dfee691187dfe816c0babc85.png)
﻿
On the Communication tab, you can determine the calendar the debrief should be scheduled on, the location and customize the calendar invite templates sent out to interviewers.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/bd498201ea8c0fa382f6631085044dd9.png)
﻿
On the Reminders tab, you can configure any reminders to be sent out to interviewers attending the debrief.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/85c33b858c7edd8f122726a466803860.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#adding-a-debrief-activity-to-a-schedule-interview)Adding a debrief activity to a schedule interview activity
When creating or amending a Schedule Interview activity on your job’s interview plan (more on this [here](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/setting-up-schedule-interview-activities)﻿), you’ll see the option to add a debrief activity at the bottom of the Schedule tab.
Toggle on the _Include ‘Schedule Debrief Activity’_ option to automatically create a debrief activity once the interview has been scheduled in.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fd76c01d1d11d063a8e1bea4a484884d.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#adding-a-debrief-activity-to-the-candidates-profil)Adding a debrief activity to the candidate’s profile.
You can also schedule one-off debriefs as needed directly from the candidate profile. If you need to manually schedule a debrief click the + Add Activity option. on the candidate profile. From there, select Debrief.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/23da7885da78dae7278c0f335cc775a2.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#scheduling-the-debrief)Scheduling the debrief
To start scheduling the debrief, click on the Schedule button on the debrief activity. Select Manual Schedule to get started.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7e6824dc1e31dfdea7267f4d7faf2063.png)
﻿
The panel for the debrief will be determined by your debrief default settings but you can opt to remove and add attendees as needed. You will also have the option to title your debrief and select the timezone if needed. Hit Continue to be taken to the Proposals tab.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/855eb1f4b413eab7af6e918f207611d2.png)
﻿
The auto-scheduler will automatically generate some proposed times for your debrief. You can click View Details for more information and then choose the option you’d like to schedule in with the Use Schedule button. If you’d prefer to manually schedule the debrief, click on Manually Schedule _Instead._ This will take you to the Panel tab.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8d5f703e01b621990d3e7278c9bff3f5.png)
﻿
The Panel tab will show you each user’s calendar so you can determine a time that works for everyone. Once you’ve found a time that’s suitable, click Configure Calendar Invite _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5ae0a4c0cd12ea91380cf78e54f2a825.png)
﻿
On the Calendar Invite tab you can view the calendar invites that will be sent out to your panel. You can click the Edit tab to adjust the debrief location and make any edits to the calendar invites. The Reminders tab will allow you to create or amend any reminders to be sent out.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5e1adfcb9fc7dbbe19ebe3a3a8c1ad61.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#the-debrief-activity)The debrief activity
Just like with an interview, once scheduled, a debrief will show as an upcoming interview on each panel member’s home page.
At the time of the debrief, each panel member can click into the debrief on their home page to see the candidate’s resume and all feedback left so far.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/721e2e7c952c64bcfcb12f9d2bc53067.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#can-i-leave-feedback-on-a-debrief)Can I leave feedback on a debrief?
No, there isn’t a tab to leave feedback for a debrief or an option to link a feedback form to a debrief. You can review the candidate overview, the debrief briefing and the feedback left for the candidate within the debrief details if you are on the debrief panel.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#does-the-candidate-get-notified-or-receive-a-calen)Does the candidate get notified or receive a calendar invite for the debrief?
No, only the users or interviewers you specify as on the debrief panel will be notified of and receive calendar invites for the debrief. The candidate will not be notified.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/debriefs#how-do-i-cancel-a-debrief)How do I cancel a debrief?
To cancel an upcoming debrief, navigate to the candidate’s profile and click Actions on the debrief activity, then select Cancel Interview.
﻿

# How do I report results in a specific timezone?
3 min
Copy
This document will teach you how to use Ashby's timezone options to customize report results.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-report-results-in-a-specific-timezone#tutorial)Tutorial
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-report-results-in-a-specific-timezone#change-report-timezone)Change report timezone
When creating reports in Ashby, the default behavior is to display results in the user's timezone. This behavior can be adjusted by using the timezone selector. There are three timezone options to choose from:
![Screen Cast 2022-12-16 at 8.06.01 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/D8fSrwTrCmz3YpH8zdIbc_screencast2022-12-16at80601am.gif)
﻿
  * User Timezone (Default): uses timezone of the user viewing the report


  * Organization Timezone: uses the organization’s default timezone for all viewers


  * Custom Timezone: allows you to pick a custom timezone


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-report-results-in-a-specific-timezone#change-organizationuser-timezone)Change Organization/User timezone
Visit [How do I change my Ashby timezones?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-change-my-ashby-timezones)﻿ to learn more about changing your organization or user timezone.

# Analytics Tutorial: Days in Current Stage Group
2 min
Copy
This tutorial is a walkthrough of using the powerful Days in Current Stage Group field for reporting, with a bonus highlight of creating a calculated field that allows custom categorization for pipeline insights and setting up alerts to drive progress.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/analytics-tutorial-days-in-current-stage-group#tutorial)Tutorial
In this tutorial you’ll explore and learn:
  * How to setup reports that identify stale candidates in specific stages


  * How to create a custom calculated field to segment candidates by their time in process


  * How to analyze your pipeline by bucketing candidates by time in stage


  * How to setup scheduled alerts that ensure timely progress for all candidates


﻿
The Days in Current Stage Group field, which counts the number of days since a candidate’s application has moved to their most recent stage within a stage group, enables a variety of reporting. You’ll review creating the example report shown below, where you can see active candidates segmented by how long they’ve been in each stage group. In this example an insight is found showing the hiring manager stage group contains a disproportionate volume of slow-moving candidates.
