# Integration options for Google Workspace and Microsoft 365
30 min
Copy
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#why-does-your-team-want-to-integrate-with-ashby)Why does your team want to integrate with Ashby?
Please note that the integrations outlined in this section are the platform integrations found in the Integration Marketplace. Ashby also supports authentication-only integrations that enable users to log in to Ashby without a platform integration being enabled (Google Sign In, Microsoft Sign In)
Ashby integrates with Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 to provide features that enhance hiring speed and efficiency. These integrations increase the risk profile of Ashby as a vendor, but provide significant benefits to your Talent team.
Ashby requests access to the emails and calendars of your team members involved in the hiring process. Ashby will store a subset of this data in our database to allow us to provide a fast and reliable user experience. Our database is encrypted at rest, logically separated by customer, and behind a VPC. All data transmitted between your IT systems, Ashby’s servers, and Ashby’s clients are encrypted in transit. For more information, please read our [Security Overview](https://www.ashbyhq.com/resources/security "Security Overview").
Ashby can only integrate with one platform integration at a time. See [Does Ashby support integrating with both Google and Microsoft at the same time?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/does-ashby-support-integrating-with-both-google-and-microsoft-at-the-same-time)﻿ for more details.
You can also click the links before to be taken to the platform integration you're planning to use for more information.
﻿[Google Workspace](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#google-workspace-platform-integration)﻿[Microsoft 365](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#microsoft-365-platform-integration)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#benefits-of-access-with-examples)Benefits of access (with examples)
This section explains the benefits Ashby provides for the access we request. Without this access, the functionality described will not be available.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#email-sync)Email Sync
When Ashby can send email through your email provider (e.g., Gmail), your Talent team and hiring managers can utilize the following functionality:
  * Send emails using predefined templates in Ashby. This creates a consistent candidate experience, from standardizing rejection explanations to how offers are made.


  * Send emails on behalf of another user. For instance, this can be used to automatically email a candidate from the hiring manager or recruiter upon moving them to a certain stage.


When Ashby can read emails through your email provider, your Talent team and hiring managers can utilize the following functionality:
  * Send sequences of emails to attract passive candidates. The sequence is paused when the candidate replies; this requires reading the email of the sequence sender. Most Talent teams make a significant percentage of hires from passive candidates.


  * Display communication with a candidate on the candidate’s profile. This allows a hiring team to coordinate communication (e.g., not repeat things already shared with the candidate, make sure the hiring team reaches out to congratulate the candidate on their offer) and not have to transcribe notes from their email communication (e.g., determine what links or information were already shared with the candidate).


﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#scheduling)Scheduling
Your Talent team will need to schedule interviews in Ashby to request and capture interviewer feedback. When Ashby can create calendar events, your Talent team can schedule interviews in Ashby without copying that information to your calendar provider (e.g., Google Calendar).
When Ashby can read the calendars of interviewers and their calendar events, it allows your Talent team to schedule with a more efficient interface manually and even automate scheduling:
  * With candidate booking links, candidates can pick an interview time convenient for them based on the availability of potential interviewers. This saves your talent team the back and forth of requesting availability and cross-referencing it with interviewers' calendars.


  * Specific event titles can be considered free or soft conflicts with our scheduling keywords feature. This saves your talent team of contacting the meeting owner to determine if a meeting can be booked over. For instance, most organizations consider one-on-ones (e.g., using the keywords “1:1” or “1-1”) to be easily rescheduleable and consequently a free or soft conflict or scheduling interviews.


  * In some cases, your team will need to schedule a candidate manually. Ashby provides a more efficient interface than calendar clients to cross-reference multiple calendars, the candidate’s availability, and consider recruiting-specific information like interviewer load.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#google-sign-in-and-microsoft-sign-in)Google Sign In and Microsoft Sign In
Ashby supports signing in with Google and Microsoft without having a platform integration enabled. This integration simply verifies identity and is unrelated to the platform integrations found in Ashby Admin. These integrations require access to user information to work.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#single-sign-on-and-directory-sync)Single Sign On and Directory Sync
Ashby supports integrating with many third-party External Identity Providers (External IDPs). We use WorkOS as middleware to facilitate this. Supported External IDPs include, but are not limited to:
  * Okta


  * Entra ID (SAML in SCIM)


  * Google OAuth


  * OneLogin


See [SSO and SCIM](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim)﻿ for more details.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#google-workspace-platform-integration)Google Workspace Platform Integration
This section details the Google Workspace integration that powers email and calendar sync functionality. It does not include Ashby’s Google Sign-In or SSO/SCIM integrations. See Google Workspace for more details about setting up the email and scheduling integration.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#what-does-it-do)What does it do?
Ashby integrates with Google Workspace to enable key features like displaying your conversations with candidates in the candidate profile feed and scheduling interviews directly in Ashby. The integration supports the following:  
| User  | Sync Google Workspace users to automatically provision/de-provision them as Ashby users  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Email  | • Sync emails between candidates and users (based on inspecting to/from headers)  |  
| ﻿  
 | • Send emails and email sequences from within Ashby  |  
| Scheduling  | • Sync employee calendars  |  
| ﻿  
 | • Send event invites  |  
| ﻿  
 | • Sync meeting rooms  |  
None of these options are required, but all are recommended for the best user experience with Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#what-access-controls-are-in-place)What access controls are in place?
We understand that email and calendar data are very sensitive information, and we put access controls in place in Ashby to only show data that is relevant to the hiring process.
We also apply a strict permission system within Ashby where users are granted access to jobs and candidate records on a need-to-know basis (see “Access Control” below). Our default user access is very limited, and can’t access any candidate or job data. Employees cannot access their candidate profile (their candidate profile must be linked to their employee profile in Ashby to prevent this). And we support truly confidential jobs that only the creator and those they give access to can see.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#email)Email
Ashby syncs and displays email when relevant to the hiring process. Only emails from Ashby users with an “Elevated Access” or “Organization Admin” role are synced since these roles are assigned to employees involved in the hiring process.
From these users, we only store emails sent from Ashby or involving a candidate:
  * Emails containing a to, cc, or bcc with the candidate’s email and subsequent emails marked by the email provider as being in the email thread.


  * Emails sent from Ashby and subsequent emails marked by the email provider as being in the email thread.


  * If the candidate's domain matches your organization's auth domain, emails will not be synced for that candidate. If the candidate has two email addresses linked to their profile (one with the organization's auth domain and one without) emails including the email address without the organization's auth domain will still be synced.


Ashby provides additional controls within the application to:
  * Mark emails as private and automatically mark emails during the offer stage as private. Viewing these emails requires an explicit access role in Ashby.


  * Hide emails when a candidate moves to offer and hired stages.


  * Not sync emails related to a candidate after they are hired.


  * Not sync emails related to a candidate considered for a confidential job


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#calendars)Calendars
Ashby syncs all accessible calendars and events on those calendars. When visibility controls are enabled on calendars and calendar events, for instance, only showing a specific event as "Free" or "Busy" or marking an event as a private event, Ashby respects such controls and does not display the event details during scheduling.
Ashby supports shared calendars (e.g., “Interviews” calendar). Personal calendars will still be used to determine interviewer availability during scheduling.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#what-are-the-options-for-integrating)What are the options for integrating?
We currently support domain-wide delegation and individual user OAuth. We recommend using Domain-Wide Delegation, but also support using OAuth for email and scheduling.
We also offer lower privilege alternatives to these integration options, which you can view below.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#domain-wide-delegation)Domain-Wide Delegation
Using domain-wide delegation, all users in your workspace will be added to Ashby, and Ashby will have the same permissions relative to all users (specifically, their emails and calendars). However, Ashby does not sync email from limited-access users, even if its permissions technically allow it to.
The permissions requested through domain-wide delegation are:  
| Permission Requested  | Google’s Description  | Required for Feature  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Sync users  | See info about users on your domain  | Automatically adding users to Ashby. If turned off, Ashby users will need to be added, removed, and modified (e.g. name updates) manually by Ashby admins. Even if domain-wide calendar/email sync is enabled, only users who have logged in to Ashby at least once will be synced.  |  
| Sync meetings rooms  | View calendar resources on your domain  | Add meeting rooms to Ashby scheduling. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see or view meeting room availability when scheduling.  |  
| Read/write shared calendars  |  See, edit, share, and permanently delete all the calendars you can access using Google Calendar. View and edit events on all your calendars. Shared interview calendars in scheduling.  | If turned off, Ashby users cannot see calendars such as shared interview calendars or schedules on shared calendars.  |  
| Read/write personal calendars  |  See, edit, share, and permanently delete all the calendars you can access using Google Calendar. View and edit events on all your calendars.  | Scheduling interviews through Ashby. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see calendars of potential interviewers when scheduling nor schedule on personal calendars.  |  
| Read email  | Read all resources and their metadata—no write operations.  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see email correspondence; emails and sequences can only be sent from no-reply@ashbyhq.com. This is still possible with OAuth but to function properly needs all users corresponding with candidates to OAuth. As an alternative to disabling this, see Limited Email Sync below.  |  
| Send email  | Send messages only. No read or modify privileges on mailbox.  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates through Ashby using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, sequences, offer letters, and one-off email cannot be sent from user emails, only no-reply@ashbyhq.com.  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#individual-user-oauth)Individual User OAuth
Using OAuth, each user can separately authorize Ashby for a full list of scopes, provided below (in their personal settings). It is not possible for a given user to only allow some of the scopes (they can accept all or none). Here is the full list of requested scopes:
  * https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.profile


  * https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.email


  * https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send


  * https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly


  * https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar


  * https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly


  * https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.events


Any users without OAuth will not have their candidate communication synced with Ashby, cannot send emails from Ashby, and cannot be automatically scheduled through Ashby.
The permissions available with OAuth are, for a recruiter or member of the hiring team that accepts those scopes:  
| Permission Requested  | Google’s Description  | Required for Feature  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Read/write shared calendars  |  See, edit, share, and permanently delete all the calendars you can access using Google Calendar. View and edit events on all your calendars.  | Shared interview calendars in scheduling. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see calendars such as shared interview calendars nor schedule on shared calendars.  |  
| Read/write personal calendars  |  See, edit, share, and permanently delete all the calendars you can access using Google Calendar. View and edit events on all your calendars.   | Scheduling interviews through Ashby. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see calendars of potential interviewers when scheduling nor schedule on personal calendars. (The interviewer has to also accept this in the case of individual OAuth.)  |  
| Read email  | Read all resources and their metadata—no write operations.  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see email correspondence; emails and sequences can only be sent from no-reply@ashbyhq.com. To function properly, this needs all users corresponding with candidates to OAuth. As an alternative to disabling this, see Limited Email Sync below.  |  
| Send email  | Send messages only. No read or modify privileges on mailbox.  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates through Ashby using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, sequences, offer letters, and one-off email cannot be sent from user emails, only no-reply@ashbyhq.com.  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#lower-privilege-alternatives-for-google-workspace)Lower Privilege Alternatives for Google Workspace
While we recommend the above integration methods for Ashby customers—widely adopted even in sensitive industries like finance and health care—we understand that some security teams may have different risk assessments. To address this, we offer alternatives with more restricted access, though these come with reduced functionality for your Talent team.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#limited-email-sync-for-google-workspace)Limited Email Sync for Google Workspace
For customers using domain delegation, we offer a "Limited Email Sync" option. This syncs only email metadata—such as recipient, sender, BCC, and subject line—while excluding the email body. As a result:
  * Ashby no longer requires the https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly scope. The scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.metadata can be used instead.


  * Ashby cannot reference email content.


Functionality Impact:
  * Email sequences will continue to work, as Ashby can detect candidate replies.


  * However, the Talent team will need to contact users directly to view the email body.


OAuth a Separate Google Workspace Account for Each Hiring Team Member
If you prefer not to provide access to internal employee accounts, you can create dedicated email accounts for hiring team members (e.g., [employee name]@[secondary domain]) under a separate domain within your organization.
Limitations:
  * Team members must manage two inboxes: their primary inbox and the separate hiring account.


  * Internal calendar access is restricted, impacting Ashby’s automated and manual scheduling features. 


  * Workaround (Google Workspace): Calendars can be manually shared with these accounts for partial visibility, but ongoing maintenance is required as new accounts or calendars are added.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#oauth-a-shared-google-workspace-account)OAuth a Shared Google Workspace Account
Alternatively, you can create a shared email/calendar account (e.g., hiring@[your domain]) for the Talent team to use. This account can be shared in Ashby for sending emails and managing scheduling.
Limitations:
  * Candidate experience is compromised: 


  * Emails will not show the recruiter’s name.


  * Communication cannot be visually separated by role (e.g., recruiter vs. hiring manager).


  * Restricting access to interviewer calendars prevents the use of automated and manual scheduling features.


  * In Google Workspace, calendars can be shared with the shared account to enable partial visibility.


#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#microsoft-365-platform-integration)Microsoft 365 Platform Integration
This section details the Microsoft 365 integration that powers email and calendar sync functionality. It does not include Ashby’s Microsoft Sign-In or SSO/SCIM integrations. See [Microsoft 365](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/microsoft-365)﻿ for more details about setting up the email and scheduling integration.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#what-does-it-do-1)What does it do?
Ashby integrates with Microsoft 365 to enable key features like displaying your conversations with candidates in the candidate profile feed and scheduling interviews directly in Ashby. The integration supports the following:  
| User  | • Sync Microsoft users to automatically provision/de-provision them as Ashby users.  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Email  |  • Sync emails between candidates and users (based on inspecting to/from headers)  • Send emails and email sequences from within Ashby  |  
| Scheduling  | • Sync employee calendars • Send event invites • Sync meeting rooms  |  
If provisioning users via the default integration, all users in your Microsoft account will be synced. For large organizations we recommend skipping this and managing user sync using Directory Sync. See [SSO and SCIM](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sso-and-scim)﻿ for more information. None of these options are required, but all are recommended for the best user experience with Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#what-are-the-options-for-integrating-1)**What are the options for integrating?**
Microsoft 365 can integrate with Ashby in the following ways. The full documentation on how the integration is set up is available at [Microsoft 365](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/microsoft-365)﻿:
  * Ashby’s managed Azure application (default and recommended for most teams)


  * Custom Azure Application


We also offer lower privilege alternatives to these integration options, which you can view below.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#custom-azure-application)**Custom Azure Application**
With a custom application, your team can fully configure a custom application and integrate it with Ashby. This option is recommended for teams who want full control over the access granted to Ashby from the outset and is required if their tenants are hosted in Microsoft’s GCC. See [Microsoft Custom Applications (GCC High)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/microsoft-custom-applications-gcc-high)﻿ for more details.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#authorization)**Authorization**
Ashby uses a combination of application and delegated permissions to integrate with Microsoft 365. Application permissions allow an admin to grant access to resources on behalf of all users, while delegated permissions require every user to grant access individually. 
**Application Permissions Requested**
When using Ashby’s managed Azure application, Ashby requests the following application permissions.   
| **Permission Requested**  | **Microsoft's Description**  | **Required for Feature**  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| User.Read.All  | "Allows the app to read user profiles"  | Automatically adding users to Ashby. If turned off, Ashby users will need to be added, removed, and modified (e.g. name updates) manually by Ashby admins. Even if domain-wide calendar/email sync is enabled, only users who have logged in to Ashby at least once will be synced.  |  
| Group.ReadWrite.All  | "Allows the app to read group properties and memberships, and read conversations for all groups"  |  Shared interview calendars in scheduling. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see calendars such as shared interview calendars nor schedule on shared calendars.  |  
| MailboxSettings.Read  | "Allows the app to read user's mailbox settings. Does not include permission to read [or send] mail."  | Employee timezone sync. Allows accurate timezone settings in scheduling.  |  
| Mail.Send  | "Allows the app to send mail"  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see email correspondence; emails and sequences can only be sent from no-reply@ashbyhq.com. To function properly, this needs all users corresponding with candidates to OAuth. As an alternative to disabling this, see Limited Email Sync below.  |  
| Mail.ReadWrite  | "Allows the app to create, read, update, and delete mail. Does not include permission to send mail."  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see email correspondence; emails and sequences can only be sent from no-reply@ashbyhq.com. To function properly, this needs all users corresponding with candidates to OAuth. As an alternative to disabling this, see Limited Email Sync below.  |  
| Calendars.ReadWrite  | "Allows the app to create, read, update, and delete calendar events." Does not include events on group calendars.  | Scheduling interviews through Ashby. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see calendars of potential interviewers when scheduling nor schedule on personal calendars. (The interviewer has to also accept this in the case of individual OAuth.)  |  
**Delegated (OAuth) Permissions Requested**
When users individually authorize Ashby for Microsoft Office 365, we request delegated versions of the above application scopes, and depending on your intended calendar and video conferencing setup, a few additional scopes that are requested that Microsoft does not make available as application scopes.   
| **Permission Requested**  | **Microsoft's Description**  | **Required for Feature**  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| User.Read.All  | "Allows the app to read user profiles"  | Automatically adding users to Ashby. If turned off, Ashby users will need to be added, removed, and modified (e.g. name updates) manually by Ashby admins. Even if domain-wide calendar/email sync is enabled, only users who have logged in to Ashby at least once will be synced.  |  
| MailboxSettings.Read  | "Allows the app to read user's mailbox settings. Does not include permission to read [or send] mail."  | Employee timezone sync. Allows accurate timezone settings in scheduling.  |  
| Mail.Send  | "Allows the app to send mail"  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see email correspondence; emails and sequences can only be sent from no-reply@ashbyhq.com. To function properly, this needs all users corresponding with candidates to OAuth. As an alternative to disabling this, see Limited Email Sync below.  |  
| Mail.ReadWrite  | "Allows the app to create, read, update, and delete mail. Does not include permission to send mail."  | Sequences (sourcing) and communications with candidates using a recruiter’s email address. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see email correspondence; emails and sequences can only be sent from no-reply@ashbyhq.com. To function properly, this needs all users corresponding with candidates to OAuth. As an alternative to disabling this, see Limited Email Sync below.  |  
| Calendars.ReadWrite  | "Allows the app to create, read, update, and delete calendar events." Does not include events on group calendars.  | Scheduling interviews through Ashby. If turned off, Ashby users cannot see calendars of potential interviewers when scheduling nor schedule on personal calendars. (The interviewer has to also accept this in the case of individual OAuth.)  |  
| Group.ReadWrite.All  | "Also allows the app to read and write calendar, conversations, files, and other group content for all groups the user can access."  |  Shared interview calendars in scheduling. If turned off, Ashby users cannot schedule on shared calendars.  |  
| OnlineMeetings.ReadWrite  | "Allows the app to read and create online meetings on your behalf."  | Microsoft Teams Video links in Meeting invites in scheduling. If using Microsoft Teams meeting with your Microsoft 365 account and this is turned off, Ashby won’t be able to automatically add meeting links to scheduled interviews.  |  
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#details-on-microsoft-365-email-access-and-metadata)Details on Microsoft 365 Email Access and Metadata Handling
Ashby’s Microsoft 365 integration enables customers to sync user inboxes and calendar data for recruiting workflows such as candidate replies, sourcing sequences, and interview scheduling. This section outlines what data Ashby accesses, how it is processed, and what is stored when using the Microsoft Graph API with Mail.ReadWrite permissions.
_**Summary of Email Processing**_
When a user connects their Microsoft 365 account in Ashby, Ashby registers change notifications (webhooks) for that user’s Inbox and Sent Items folders. When a new or updated message is detected, Ashby:
  1. Fetches the message using Microsoft Graph.


  1. Evaluates a defined set of metadata fields.


  1. Determines whether the message is relevant to an active candidate or job application (e.g., by matching the sender or recipient to a known candidate email address).


  1. If the message is relevant, Ashby retrieves and stores the full email body.


  1. If not, Ashby discards the message and does not persist it.


_**Metadata Fields Accessed**_
Ashby uses the Microsoft Graph API’s $select parameter to restrict the fields it accesses when evaluating a message. These fields include:
  * bccRecipients


  * ccRecipients


  * conversationId


  * conversationIndex


  * from


  * id


  * internetMessageHeaders


  * internetMessageId


  * isDraft


  * receivedDateTime


  * replyTo


  * sender


  * sentDateTime


  * subject


  * toRecipients


These fields are used exclusively to determine whether the email should be associated with an active candidate record.
_**Data That Is Stored**_
Ashby only stores the full content of an email if it is determined to be related to a candidate or sourcing activity. Otherwise, the email is evaluated in memory and discarded.
This selective access pattern ensures that Ashby only persists recruiting-relevant communications while operating within the broader constraints of Microsoft’s Graph API (which does not offer more granular email scopes like Google’s .metadata).
_**Access Scope**_
Ashby uses the Mail.ReadWrite permission scope as required by Microsoft to access messages. This permission enables reading, writing, updating, and deleting emails, however, Ashby only uses it to read and associate relevant emails with candidate workflows and does not write or delete messages in user inboxes.
_**Compliance Notes**_
  * Ashby does not store or process non-recruiting-related emails.


  * Ashby does not fetch the full email body unless the message is recruiting-relevant.


  * This behavior applies across both standard Microsoft 365 and GCC tenants.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#lower-privilege-alternatives-for-microsoft-365)**Lower Privilege Alternatives for Microsoft 365**
While we recommend the above integration methods for Ashby customers - widely adopted even in sensitive industries like finance and health care - we understand that some security teams may have different risk assessments. To address this, we offer alternatives with more restricted access, though these come with reduced functionality for your Talent team
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#oauth-a-separate-google-workspace-or-office-365-ac)OAuth a Separate Google Workspace or Office 365 Account for Each Hiring Team Member
If you prefer not to provide access to internal employee accounts, you can create dedicated email accounts for hiring team members (e.g., employee name@secondary domain) under a separate domain within your organization.
Limitations:
  * Team members must manage two inboxes: their primary inbox and the separate hiring account.


  * Internal calendar access is restricted, impacting Ashby’s automated and manual scheduling features. 


  * Workaround (Google Workspace): Calendars can be manually shared with these accounts for partial visibility, but ongoing maintenance is required as new accounts or calendars are added.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/integration-options-for-google-workspace-and-microsoft-365#oauth-a-shared-google-workspace-or-office-365-acco)OAuth a Shared Google Workspace or Office 365 Account
Alternatively, you can create a shared email/calendar account (e.g., hiring@[your domain]) for the Talent team to use. This account can be shared in Ashby for sending emails and managing scheduling.
Limitations:
  * Candidate experience is compromised: 


  * Emails will not show the recruiter’s name.


  * Communication cannot be visually separated by role (e.g., recruiter vs. hiring manager).


  * Restricting access to interviewer calendars prevents the use of automated and manual scheduling features.


  * In Google Workspace, calendars can be shared with the shared account to enable partial visibility.

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Getting Started: User Guides
# Getting Started for Hiring Managers
10 min
Copy
Welcome to Ashby! We couldn't be more excited to help you get started using Ashby as a hiring manager. This guide assumes your Ashby instance is already set up. We will cover how to navigate Ashby and use the most commonly used features for hiring managers.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#log-in-to-ashby)Log In to Ashby
To sign in, head to [https://app.ashbyhq.com](https://app.ashbyhq.com/ "https://app.ashbyhq.com"). Ashby uses Google sign-in, Microsoft 365 sign-in and magic links. If your company has SSO set up with Ashby, please use this option to log in. More on signing in can be found at [Signing in to Ashby](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/signing-in-to-ashby)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#configure-your-personal-settings)Configure your personal settings
A key first step is configuring your personal settings in Ashby. In [Personal Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/personal-info "Personal Settings"), you can do the following:
  * Set your personal info, including your job title, user avatar, timezone and social links


  * Set your notification settings - determine the activities you should be notified about and where you'd like to be notified


  * Set up personal email templates


  * Set up OAuth if needed (please confirm with your admin if this needs to be completed as part of your Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 setup)


For a full walkthrough, please check out [Updating Personal Settings](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/updating-personal-settings)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#navigate-ashby)Navigate Ashby
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#home-page)Home Page
If you will only be interviewing candidates in the pipeline and won't have any other interaction with candidates, then you'll likely have Limited Access in Ashby. 
To get started, log in to Ashby at [https://app.ashbyhq.com](https://app.ashbyhq.com/ "https://app.ashbyhq.com") using one of the login options. You should already have an account set up. Once logged in, you'll land on your home page, where you'll be able to access interview briefings and tasks. For a full breakdown of the home page, please check out [Home Page](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/home-page)﻿
**Add and track referrals**
To submit referrals﻿:
  * Head to the Referrals section of the [Home](https://app.ashbyhq.com/home/upcoming "Home") page


  * Click + Referral


  * Creating a direct referral? Click Add Direct Referral then complete the referral form with the candidate details.


  * Creating a referral link? Click Create Referral Link and give your referral link a title. You can then opt to link to a specific job posting using the Link to specific job posting? checkbox and selecting a job posting from the dropdown


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/26278f4b47c38aac31f08c40ab4ba5c3.png)
﻿
﻿  

Our full guide on referrals can be found at [Referrals and Referral Links](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/referrals-and-referral-links)﻿﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#candidate-profile)Candidate Profile
Depending on the permissions you are given, you may have access to view candidate profiles and take action on job considerations. For further information on navigating a candidate profile, check out [Candidate Profile](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-profile)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#candidate-pipeline)Candidate Pipeline
The Pipeline Overviewprovides you a view of candidates across all positions you have access to and allows you to quickly jump between them. 
For more on the pipeline view, check out [Candidate Pipeline](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/candidate-pipeline)﻿.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#jobs-dashboard-view)Jobs - Dashboard View
The Job Dashboard at Jobs > [Dashboard](https://app.ashbyhq.com/jobs/pipelines "Dashboard") is a high-level overview of the active jobs you follow, have a role on, and have access to. You can also access the Admin page and Job Board from this page. We recommend bookmarking this page to quickly see all of your active jobs in one place.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7b273a2cee774b2dd7e373ee143c5799.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#common-use-cases-for-hiring-managers)Common use cases for hiring managers
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#application-review)Application Review
Check out [Application Review](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-review)﻿ for a full guide covering bulk reviewing candidate applications and advancing and archiving candidates.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#opening-and-posting-jobs)Opening and posting jobs
To create a job, navigate to [Jobs→Admin](https://app.ashbyhq.com/jobs/all "Jobs→Admin"), then click the Create a Job button and follow the steps in the job creation wizard. At this point, you can open the job right away — or configure it further. In the Settings section, there are further configuration options on four tabs.
When you're ready to open the job, click the _Job Status_ button and selectMark as Open. An open job isn't automatically posted to your Ashby job board or any third-party job boards but you will be able to add candidates to the pipeline.
When you're ready to post the job to your job board, you can set up a job posting on the Job Postings tab. You can create as many postings as you'd like for a job. Once published, the posting will show up on your Ashby job board (e.g., jobs.asbhyhq.com/ashby).
For a full guide on creating a job and job postings, check out [Creating & Posting Jobs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/l-NcIsZrB9ztdr4U3idZr)﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/getting-started-for-hiring-managers#my-profile-picture-has-synced-over-from-google-wor)My profile picture has synced over from Google Workspace but it’s very pixelated. Why?
Images synced over from Google Workspace do not have a high enough resolution. As such, we’d recommend users upload their avatar within [Personal Settings](https://app.ashbyhq.com/settings/personal-info "Personal Settings") to fix this.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3bd9ae2c6b84fa648351e2310d15c63c.png)
﻿
﻿

Analytics: Best Practices
# Best Practices: Maintaining Data Quality
22 min
Copy
This document reviews how to maintain a high level of data quality in your ATS by utilizing Ashby reports, dashboards and alerts. The options for long term data quality are presented, along with a summary of the most common challenges and a data quality checklist.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#introduction)Introduction
Ashby has a standard _Data Quality_ example dashboard to help get you started. If you’d like to see what this looks like, reach out to your dedicated Success Manager or Ashby Support.
Historically, maintaining data quality within your ATS has been a major challenge due to the lack of visibility into the underlying data. Ashby’s native reporting capabilities make insight into the quality of your ATS data readily available. The goal of this article is to first review some of the most common challenges in ATS data and present the recommended reporting solutions.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#long-term-data-quality-solutions)Long term data quality solutions
Long term health and data quality is fundamentally about data visibility. Easy review of your data allows you to know _when_ and _what_ data issues are at hand. This, in turn, permits designing easy resolution steps and alerts.
In the absence of data visibility, quality issues are subject to one-off discoveries at the time of trying to run a report, with the outcome being a slow accumulation of ever more issues that are hard to identify and resolve, followed by a decrease in trust in the data overall. This eventually results in a data-less culture, subject to a “garbage in, garbage out” sentiment.
With Ashby you have the following tools to maintain data quality:
  * **Customized reports:** you can build reports that look for specific compliance or SLA violations to surface problematic records immediately.


  * **Scheduled Alerts:** in addition to reviewing reports you can configure automated scheduled alerts that notify responsible individuals immediately or at a set cadence to help raise awareness and maintain quality before any issues grow out of hand.


  * **Dashboards:** custom reports can be combined into a central data quality dashboard, combining the highest priority items to review.


  * **Home page configuration:** each user’s home page can be configured with pinned content, adding critical data quality reports and dashboards to their space for easy access and review.


The above options can be used individually or in combination, depending on the scope of your needs and size of your team.
In the case of managing a large team, assignment to individual owners can be incorporated to almost all reports and dashboards, permitting a distribution of responsibility. This is one way Ashby can facilitate creating an ownership culture of data quality.
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#common-types-of-data-quality-issues)Common Types of Data Quality Issues
Below is a summary review of the most common data quality challenges we’ve seen.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#stale-candidates)Stale candidates
You can define stale candidates according to your team’s process, but a general example would be categorizing any candidate that has been in process but has not had an activity for 30+ days as stale. The easiest way to select for this is by using _NOT_ logic applied to any activity-related field, like so:
![A basic selection for any application NOT active in the last 30 days.](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vCzjmfyIVczWhuJmWUReQ_screenshot2022-09-09at111134am.png)
﻿
Your particular selection may vary, or use other fields of interest or even filter down to specific stages or jobs. The key breakthrough is beginning to get an easy selection non-compliant records, after which you can begin grouping or refining the results to be more actionable or reveal insights about your process.
Below are some examples of how to further refine your stale candidates to gain insight into what may be happening.
**Stale candidates by stage group**
Here you can see stale candidates by their application’s current stage group. This reveals that the application review process is a significant contributor, but that there are candidates that have been inactive 30+ days all the way down to the final rounds!
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.16.01 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/juwhRYgb2Q6ipUqTJlijS_screenshot2022-09-09at111601am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Stale candidates on open jobs**
When grouping by open jobs you may find that there are a small subset of jobs that have a disproportionate number of stale candidates. One response here would be to review the hiring team or review capacity and load balancing.
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.18.48 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/FKkJt4KLQwOZKVbDMW7Wj_screenshot2022-09-09at111848am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Stale candidates by source type**
You can easily review the stale candidate selection by source type too. The plan of action may vary here, but seeing that many candidates are stale from direct inbound application may suggest review process adjustments, while noting that 50+ referrals are stale may inform a higher urgency SLA process for referrals.
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.20.20 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/pW_loscU9AjaGmMFs7Mbk_screenshot2022-09-09at112020am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Stale candidates by recruiter**
For load balancing and team management, you can review stale candidates by their assigned recruiter. This could be filtered down by stage group, to identify in-process candidates, or used to identify that most stale candidates have no recruiter assigned at all. More on this latter point below.
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.22.20 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wmQ3nWiVuWWndbkfgGddX_screenshot2022-09-09at112220am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**More advanced reporting on stale candidates**
The above examples are single slices of data, but Ashby does permit multiple groupings to give more insight. In this example you can see stale candidates by their department and stage group, revealing which departments have the greatest volume but also how far in process the stale candidates are.
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.26.03 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/277AZnhVY65ufSKcFDWd6_screenshot2022-09-09at112603am.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#active-candidates-on-closed-jobs)Active candidates on closed jobs
Another common data quality challenge is finding active candidates on jobs that have been closed. These candidates should be dispositioned, but the real goal is to understand why and when this happens. This basic filter selection will identify all active applications on closed jobs:
![Example filter conditions to find active applications on closed jobs, a common data quality issue.](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bD4-awf-0vngUXm8x5-qE_screenshot2022-09-09at112825am.png)
﻿
With this selection in place, similar to the many variations on stale candidate review you can create reports that identify sources, owners or patterns in this case. This example report identifies which closed jobs have the most active applications:
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.27.45 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/h2vrryVlYENzz3FQkHim3_screenshot2022-09-09at112745am.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#not-scheduling-interviews-in-your-ats)Not scheduling interviews in your ATS
Some teams opt to schedule candidate interviews outside of their ATS. This is most common at the top of funnel (initial calls, initial screens), but can be a critical set of data to track to see if upstream activity is feeding into downstream results.
How to assess this will depend on your teams expectations, but the simplest way to get a top-level summary is to create a count over time by week of scheduled interviews per stage group. This way if you see numbers that are definitively too low you can investigate further.
![Screen Shot 2022-09-14 at 5.20.56 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/xLHVT5x6lYmo3282s_TP8_screenshot2022-09-14at52056pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
Additionally, you could use the Interview Load template report, or segmentation reports to look at interview records in more detail. See these examples in our report catalogues: [Report Catalog: Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-templates)﻿, [Report Catalog - Essentials](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-essentials)﻿ and [Report Catalog - Advanced](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/report-catalog-advanced)﻿.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#missing-fields)Missing Fields
Across candidate, jobs, applications and all the other ATS-based records, there is a ton of information to manage. It’s not uncommon to see candidates with no recruiter assigned, or missing custom field values. Ashby can facilitate managing this by identifying non-compliant records easily via filters.
The general recommendation here is to use filter selections for “is empty/unassigned” to identify records with no value.
![Example of selecting for active applications that have no source.](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/kv-5ZGrCPOjJ-bnfzIxAy_screenshot2022-09-09at113514am.png)
﻿
In this example we can select for all applications that have no source and group by the responsible recruiter to distribute responsibility for updating the application records.
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.35.52 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/M8fXLdIzlb4G1oR3kVTM3_screenshot2022-09-09at113552am.png?format=webp)
﻿
**Common missing fields to review**
The most common fields your team may want to establish data quality reporting for are the following:
  * Source


  * Credited To


  * Recruiter (on the Job and on the Candidate)


  * Hiring Manager


  * Department / Team


Your team’s process may vary on some of these fields being present, so it may make sense to add an additional filter. For example, you may want to review data quality specifically for _hired_ candidates more carefully. This would involve checking offer letter details, hiring team information and so on. Here we see a list of hires with no hiring manager on record, by department:
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 11.44.01 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/szevKmtz0ePOWy2qdgP06_screenshot2022-09-09at114401am.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#ownership-on-related-records)Ownership on related records
Your ATS may permit assigning an owner to related records, such as a Job’s recruiter or a Candidate’s recruiter. This ownership can often diverge, creating uncertainty.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#jobs-recruiter-candidates-recruiter)Job’s Recruiter & Candidate’s Recruiter
Ashby permits easily reporting on the fields and ownership associated with either the job or a candidate’s application. At the bare minimum, your team may want to maintain awareness by reporting on both, but a diagnostic report can be made that groups all application’s by their job’s owner first with a second grouping on the application’s owner:
![Grouping by the Job’s recruiter and the Application’s recruiter allows you to see the mix of ownership](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yMx7XewA_uLL287zaIw9q_screenshot2022-09-09at120226pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
The results, after some additional filter options are added per your need, may look like this:
![Screen Shot 2022-09-09 at 12.04.18 PM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/BGXQXCsW-dYFKQ_VAW5N0_screenshot2022-09-09at120418pm.png?format=webp)
﻿
In this view, we see Chastity Okuneva (bright pink) has ownership over almost all applications on Job’s owned by other recruiters. Conversely, Tevin (teal), is the owner of 96% of their job’s applicants.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#opening-requisition-and-job-metadata)Opening, Requisition and Job metadata
Your ATS may have a complex data model that permits setting fields such as departments or hiring managers on multiple distinct objects, e.g. the Job Posting may have a department while the associated Requisition may have a _different_ department. Similar to the above example with recruiter ownership on Jobs and Applications, you should consider this across other objects where it may apply.
If you’re a Lever-based customer, see our writeup on [The Lever Data Model](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/the-lever-data-model)﻿ for a detailed summary of how fields can differ across Lever objects.
﻿  

#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#putting-it-all-together-dashboards-alerts-and-home)Putting it all together: dashboards, alerts, and home page configuration
Once you’ve identified the critical reports of interest to your team, the recommendation is to compose your reports into respective dashboards. Some data quality issues are best dealt with immediately or at a weekly review, which is a perfect fit for Ashby’s Alerts. In addition, once you’ve setup your reports and dashboards, you’ll want to ensure they are pinned to your team’s home page for easy access. The below references cover all of these topics in greater detail:
  * ﻿[Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Building Your First Dashboard](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/building-your-first-dashboard)﻿


  * ﻿[Pin Reports to User Home Pages](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/pin-reports-to-user-home-pages)﻿﻿  



#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/best-practices-maintaining-data-quality#the-data-quality-checklist)The data quality checklist
At a high level the above examples can be summarized in a check list you may want to review with your team. Based on your priorities you may not want to address all of these at once.
Define a general “stale candidate” condition
Report on stale candidates by:
Report on active candidates on closed jobs
Define mandatory fields for all applications (recruiter, source, HM, etc)
Are there stage-specific SLA’s for time in process?
Review your object ownership fields
Create an overall data quality dashboard(s) with the above reports
Pin the dashboard or individual reports to appropriate user home pages
Celebrate and rest well knowing data quality is under control with a stable process 🥳
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Jobs & Openings
# When and how do I close out a job?
6 min
Copy
In this guide, we’ll cover when and how to mark a job as Closed in Ashby.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/when-and-how-do-i-close-out-a-job#when-should-i-close-out-a-job)When should I close out a job?
We recommend closing out a job:
  * If you’ve hired for all positions available for a role and don’t plan on hiring for that role again in the near future.


  * If the role is no longer being hired for and won’t be opened up again soon.


If you have any evergreen roles that you are always hiring for, we recommend using Openings to track positions accordingly. You can read more about these in our [Openings Management](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/openings-management)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/when-and-how-do-i-close-out-a-job#how-to-mark-a-job-as-closed)How to mark a job as closed
To close out a job, head into the role in Jobs > Jobs, then click on the Open tag under the job title. From the dropdown, select Mark as Closed _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a5c899cdacd2cf4a3856942af7827711.png)
﻿
You’ll then be asked to select a reason from the Reason for closing drop-down. Select your reasoning, then hit Close _._
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/9b7a10078b4e4dc8784fb70c66e6ffce.png)
﻿
If you need to set up more job closing reasons, you can do this in Admin>Organization Setup>[Close Job Reasons](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/close-job-reasons "Close Job Reasons")﻿
Once confirmed, the job will be marked as closed. All job postings will be removed from your Ashby job board.
You’ll then see the option to Archive Job Consideration/s _._ Click this to archive any existing candidates in the pipeline.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/93c8f9b36c21e49d147dc77af6d1013f.png)
﻿
You’ll be asked to select an archive reason for the candidates and can opt to notify candidates of the rejection and cancel any feedback notifications for interviewers.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/5f30060b31c7be3ce31e5d67a6352a23.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/when-and-how-do-i-close-out-a-job#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/when-and-how-do-i-close-out-a-job#can-i-take-a-copy-of-or-create-a-template-from-a-c)Can I take a copy of or create a template from a closed job?
You can, yes! You can search for the job by title, or adjust the filters in Jobs > Jobs to view a list of closed jobs. You can either click the trash can icon to remove the filters completely or amend the existing ones to only show closed jobs.
Click on your job, then click on More. From here, you’ll see the options to create a copy of the job or create a template from it. For more on templates, check out [Job Templates](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/job-templates)﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1fffba522a21e824e44170bf3309ef1e.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/when-and-how-do-i-close-out-a-job#how-do-i-stop-new-applications-without-closing-out)How do I stop new applications without closing out the job’s pipeline completely?
You can opt to unpublish your job postings on your jobs to stop them from showing on your job board. Candidates will then be unable to apply for the role. You can still add candidates to your pipeline internally in Ashby.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/08ecf00142d1e38362d12097cd8bc68f.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/when-and-how-do-i-close-out-a-job#can-i-amend-the-open-or-close-date-of-a-job)C**an I amend the open or close date of a job?**
For assistance with this, please reach out to support@ashbyhq.com.
﻿

<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Ashby Analytics
User Access & Sharing Metrics
# Confidential Reporting & Content Access Control
11 min
Copy
This document reviews Ashby Analytics content access control. You will learn about making reports and dashboards private or public to your organization's users, and how to share private reports with specific individuals.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control#content-access-vs-permissions)Content Access vs Permissions
In Ashby Analytics, reports and dashboards are thought of as "content". Making a report or dashboard private to yourself, shared with a restricted set of users, or shared with your entire organization is the focus of Content Access Control. What results show up in a report, e.g. whether a user sees a summary including all departments and jobs versus a summary that shows only the Engineering department, is the focus of Permissions, sometimes referred to as data access control.
  * **Content Access:** controls what content, reports or dashboards, a user is allowed to view.


  * **Permissions:** controls what data is available in reports or dashboards.


If you want to restrict what results users see in their reports, see [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control#content-access-in-ashby)Content Access in Ashby
In Ashby, you can restrict access to both reports and dashboards. There are two states content can be in:
  * **Private:** restricts access to the user and anyone on the access list


  * **Public:** shared to your organization, i.e. viewable by any Ashby user


Upon saving new content, the user will have the choice of making it private or public content. The default option is to create public content. The access control settings for any report can be adjusted at a later time.
![Screen Cast 2022-12-16 at 8.15.16 AM.gif](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/6wbRjnMgKRtSNMglG13BY_screencast2022-12-16at81516am.gif)
﻿
**Change access settings**
Access settings can be adjusted at any time by Admin users. The access settings can be viewed under the More menu, with an open or closed lock icon indicating whether the content is public or private.
![Screen Shot 2022-11-21 at 2.37.31 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/qSPupku-BaMnYl3EDMDuN_screenshot2022-11-21at23731pm.png)
﻿
**Make a public report private**
A public report can be made private at any time by selecting More > Access > Make Private. Doing so will remove all access to the report and remove the report from any private dashboards that are private to more than just you as a user. As this action can impact other users' experience, we ask that you confirm the choice in a two-step process.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-21 at 2.39.54 PM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jVEtHDfxan5TiiLkuri_0_screencast2022-11-21at23954pm.gif)
﻿
**Make a private report public**
A private report can be made public at any time by selecting More > Access > Make Public > Remove access restrictions. When making a private report public, we ask for confirmation as the report will become accessible to all Ashby users.
![Screen Cast 2022-11-21 at 2.44.08 PM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/QC7CyoajitJSlh67VvH2V_screencast2022-11-21at24408pm.gif)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control#manage-access-of-private-content)Manage access of private content
Any private content can be shared with other users by adding them to the access list. The content author and only the author is given the option of enabling further access management by users that are added to the access list by toggling a "Allow Sharing" option.
![Screen Shot 2022-11-21 at 2.46.07 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wU6ZbfVD7uqf-tay0SICU_screenshot2022-11-21at24607pm.png)
﻿
**The primary content access management options are:**
  1. Make the content public, granting all organization users access


  1. Add additional users to the access list


  1. Permit whether users on the access list can add the report to dashboards and grant access to other users


  1. Revoke all access to make the report strictly private


Revoking all access will remove access to the report from all users in addition to removing the report from all dashboards that are not private to you. As this choice may impact other users' experience, we make it a two-step confirmation:
![Screen Shot 2022-11-21 at 2.47.43 PM.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/WtUCkgoy-zUyTFAm37uNm_screenshot2022-11-21at24743pm.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control#dashboard-access-and-report-access)Dashboard Access and Report Access
Similar to reports, dashboards can be created as public or private. Dashboards can also have a restricted access list to enable sharing with a select set of users. Items to keep in mind when adding reports to dashboards:
  * **Add report to dashboard:** After a report is added to a dashboard, it is accessible to everyone who has access to the report directly and everyone who has access to the dashboard. If a user has access to the report by having access to the dashboard, they can also view the report on its own (outside the dashboard in the saved report view).


  * **Remove report from dashboard:** Anyone who had access to the report solely by having access to the dashboard will no longer have access to the report.


  * **Remove user from report access control list:** If the removed user does not have access to any dashboard that the report is on, this action will remove the user’s access to the report. If they do have access to a dashboard the report is on, the user will continue to have access to the report.


**Private reports on dashboards**
It is possible to add a private report to any dashboard. In doing so, you grant users with access to the dashboard access to the report. As dashboards and reports have separate access lists, the access list for the report may be empty (apart from you). If any other users have access via a dashboard you will see the Revoke All Access button, allowing you to enforce strict privacy and remove access to the report from all users.
![1. A report with no users explicitly on the access control list, but the "revoke all access" option being present alerts you that users have access to this report via dashboards.](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Zr05owJYhAPBIQ4D6IflP_screenshot2022-11-21at25559pm.png)
﻿
  1. A report with no users explicitly on the access control list, but the "revoke all access" option being present alerts you that users have access to this report via dashboards.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control#lock-or-unlock-a-report)Lock or unlock a report
Users with an Admin license can lock or unlock a report in order to prevent other users from making changes or accidental edits. Only the user who locked the report will be able to unlock it.
  * If a report is unlocked, the button will say Unlocked and the lock icon will be open.


  * If a report is locked, the button will say Locked, show closed lock icon, and be a golden yellow color.


![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/TCUBDHC_8W-5z0Fp2jKti_screenshot2023-01-10at85541am.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dL8cURjNoUM_yOo6jNeUx_screenshot2023-01-10at85550am.png)
﻿
To lock a report, click the button that says “Unlocked” and select Lock report.
To unlock a report, click the button that says “Locked” and select Unlock report
![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 8.59.26 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bwlYXAFWgYnDSBe2EPifp_screencast2023-01-10at85926am.gif)
﻿
![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 9.00.41 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/n5CZozLL73w608StRvmbp_screencast2023-01-10at90041am.gif)
﻿
﻿  

If the user who has locked a report has been deactivated or terminated, then any Organization Admin can unlock the report.
﻿  

![Screen Cast 2023-01-10 at 9.10.19 AM.gif](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/OH3K-I_EEZDlkzyTAbc4s_screencast2023-01-10at91019am.gif)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/confidential-reporting-and-content-access-control#what-should-i-do-if-a-report-is-locked-and-the-use)What should I do if a report is locked and the user who locked the report no longer works for our organization?
Reach out to an Organization Admin as they will be able to unlock the report. If you do not know who is an Organization Admin in your organization, please reach out to us at support@ashbyhq.com.
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Candidates
# Can I mark a candidate as Do Not Contact?
4 min
Copy
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-mark-a-candidate-as-do-not-contact#overview)Overview
You can mark a candidate as Do Not Contact. To do this, head to the candidate profile and click the More button. From here, select Mark as Do Not Contact _._
Marking a candidate as Do Not Contact will cancel all scheduled emails and prevent further email outreach from being sent via Ashby.
Please note: This will not remove the candidate’s profile from Ashby. For more on deleting and anonymizing candidates, please check out Anonymizing and Deleting Candidates.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/4d18afa2a280debf4bf8351aaa834000.png)
﻿
Once a candidate has been marked as Do Not Contact, a red Do Not Contact notification will show at the top of their profile.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ba417fc2b533753ca84fc6c9e2ee9c9f.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-mark-a-candidate-as-do-not-contact#attempting-to-send-an-email-to-a-candidate-marked)Attempting to send an email to a candidate marked as Do Not Contact
If a candidate has been marked as Do Not Contact, you will not be able to send them emails or enroll them in a sequence. If you try to email them, you will see an error that says _Cannot start the campaign or send the email because it is invalid_.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f91795c9a258595ee49fccfc58b590e0.png)
﻿
If you try to enroll them in a sequence, you will see an error that says This candidate is marked as Do Not Contact.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-mark-a-candidate-as-do-not-contact#can-i-remove-the-do-not-contact-status)**Can I remove the Do Not Contact status?**
You can! Click More on the candidate’s profile, then select Allow Contact _._ You’ll then be able to send emails to the candidate via Ashby moving forward.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1941c90162227cfb07226d73ea264216.png)
﻿
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2b2d5dcf5b18075c6010511f5fe50efa.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-mark-a-candidate-as-do-not-contact#can-i-stop-candidates-who-have-been-marked-as-do-n)Can I stop candidates who have been marked as _Do Not Contact_ from applying for roles multiple times?
You can set up Application Limits to avoid duplicate applications. For more on this, check out [Application Limits](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/application-limits)﻿ .

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Applications
# Global Application Questions & Global Auto-Reject Rules
10 min
Copy
This feature allows you to create global application questions that are displayed below your application form on the job postings that meet the criteria you set. You can also set auto-reject rules that will automatically archive candidates based on their answers to your global application questions.
This gives you the option to configure application questions and auto-reject rules across many job postings, so any company-wide or department-specific questions can be linked to job postings quickly.
Global application questions and auto-reject rules for these questions can only be configured on the Enterprise plan:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ❌  | ❌  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#configuring-global-application-questions)Configuring Global Application Questions
To configure global application questions, head to _Admin_ > _Jobs & Applications_ > [_Global Application Questions_](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/global-application-questions/questions "Global Application Questions"). Click + New to add a question. You’ll then be asked to pick a question field type. You can pick from the following:
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/97f273d512ea8cb2880a51935c7631a1.png)
﻿
  1. Short Answer Single line of unformatted text. LinkedIn url responses will be auto-populated on the candidate's profile.


  1. Long Unformatted Answer Multiple lines of unformatted text


  1. Phone Only domestic or international phone numbers


  1. Email Only valid email addresses


  1. Multiple Choice Select a single option


  1. Checkboxes Select multiple options


  1. Date Only dates that can be selected via a date picker


  1. Yes/No Yes, no, or, if not required, no answer


  1. Number Only allows whole numbers (e.g. 1) or decimals (e.g. 1.5)


  1. File Upload a single file


  1. Other location Input a location


  1. URL A clickable link to any website


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#question-settings-scopes)Question Settings & Scopes
On the Create Application Form Question menu, you can add your question and determine whether it is required or not.
You can then set your scope, so your application form question will only be included on the job postings that meet your criteria. Click + Add Field to Match to add your requirements. For example, you could opt to only display your created question for jobs in a specific department.
If you’d like the question to be displayed on all postings, leave the _Scope_ section blank.
Once you’ve determined your criteria in the Scope section, you can then choose the job boards the posting should show on using the Select job boards drop-down.
Click Create to add your job to the Active Questions list.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ab615cb89daf6096d78084f6e50b443c.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#editing-or-archiving-a-global-application-question)Editing or Archiving a Global Application Question
If you need to make any amendments or archive a question, click on the question within the Active Questions list, then click Edit if you need to make changes, or Archive if the question should not be removed from all postings moving forward.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/496ccbde8566d2ee1f142002e4ef5b3e.png)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#re-ordering-global-application-questions)Re-ordering Global Application Questions
The global application questions you configure will appear beneath the job posting’s application form and above any diversity or EEOC surveys.
If more than one question meets the criteria in your scope, the questions will be shown in the order they are listed in the Active Questions list.
If you’d like to change the order in which your global application questions are shown, you can click and drag them into your desired order using the sliders to the left of each question.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/835d0252f3bf1132cf319ca3d667673d.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#configuring-auto-reject-rules-for-global-applicati)Configuring Auto-Reject rules for Global Application Questions
For more on configuring rejection conditions including more information on operators, check out our [Auto-Reject Applications guide](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/auto-reject-applications)﻿.
To create an auto-reject rule for a global application question, click the Auto-Reject Rules tab, then click _+ New_. Give your auto-reject rule a title, then click Submit.
From here, you can create a draft configuration. Click + Add Field to Match to determine your rejection conditions. The fields you can choose from will be from your global application questions.
Once you’ve determined the conditions that would need to be met for the candidate to be rejected, you can use the Archive Reason drop-down to confirm the archive reason that would be applied when they’re rejected.
Finally, the Rejection Email Template drop-down allows you to determine the email template used to send a rejection email. Once an email template is selected, you can then set the delay for the rejection email (in days) and the sender address.
Once your draft configuration is complete, click Activate. Your auto-reject rules will then be live and applied to candidates meeting your set criteria moving forward.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/c900a0f13d86c1ebd11254029682f927.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/6d91ff9802e629fd6333d7ba18960d57.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/939f6a0b0be4e48d0ac1b3ef79c06190.png)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#editing-archiving-an-auto-reject-rule)Editing & Archiving an Auto-Reject Rule
Click on an auto-reject rule within the _Rules_ list to pull up the rule configuration. You can click Edit to create a new draft configuration. Remember to activate the draft to ensure your changes are applied on future applications.
Click Archive to archive the auto-reject rule. It will not be applied to future applications.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/81949835a7298cef2606865b9c70559c.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/global-application-questions-and-global-auto-reject-rules#can-i-restore-an-archived-global-application-quest)Can I restore an archived global application question or auto-reject rule?
No, once a global application question or auto-reject rule for a global application question has been archived, this cannot be undone.
﻿
