Building Custom Reports & Dash...
# Custom Reports
29 min
Copy
This guide provides an overview of all the custom report options available in **Reports >**[Report Builder](https://app.ashbyhq.com/reports/builder "Report Builder").
Custom reports can be configured on the Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans:  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
**Prerequisites:** If you are reporting on your pipeline or interview stages, we recommend setting up a grouped interview plan, so you can report on stage group, rather than stage name to streamline reporting. More on this at [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#creating-a-custom-report)Creating a custom report
To create a custom report, navigate to **Reports > Report Builder**. In the Custom Reports section, you'll be able to choose from the following report types:
  * ﻿[Segmentation](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#segmentation)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Count Over Time](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#count-over-time)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Candidate List](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#candidate-list)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Custom List](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#custom-list)﻿﻿  



  * ﻿[Recruiting Planner](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#recruiting-planner)﻿﻿  



![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/fikFouB0NFuUH077oLVtD_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-17-at-111739-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Click on a report type to start building your custom report, where you can set the subject, apply filters and timeframes and more to find key metrics. The visualization options will give you options to help present your results in the clearest way possible.
Remember to click Save Report once you've configured your custom report. Reports do not auto-save.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#segmentation)Segmentation
The segmentation report allows you to group data by a field of your choosing. You can use this to count and group applications, candidates, interviews and more.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#initial-setup)Initial Setup
When setting up your segmentation report, you can populate the following fields:
![The report builder for a count by segment report.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/S4ekw2XHQyc_g-YmU3zVm_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-16-at-115550-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Subject (required):** You can use the drop-down here to determine the topic you’d like the report to cover. Job Considerations shows by default but other options include Jobs, Interviews, and Candidates.
**Timeframe (required):** Here you can select the date field that will be used to determine the data displayed in your report. For example, if you’re looking to report on job considerations, grouped by source in the last three months, you can select the Started At date field. Job considerations that were started within the last three months will then be included in the report.
**Filters (optional):** If you’d like to further filter your results (for example, only looking to show applications by source for a specific job), you can add additional filters here using the + Add Field to Match option.
If you have the Advanced toggle turned off for filters, click + Filter instead to pick from the most commonly used filters. Advanced filtering does provide the widest range of options.
**Groupings (required):** Select how you would like your data to be grouped on the segmentation report. If viewing applications by source, click + Add Field to Group Results By and select Source.
When creating a segmentation report, you can add two fields to group by. For example, if you were creating a segmentation report on interviews per stage group they were scheduled in, you could select Current Interview Stage Group in the Groupings section but also click + Add Field to Group Results By a second time if you’d like to also view the job these interviews were scheduled for.
The first specified field will be prioritised on the x axis. You can click and drag your fields specified in the Groupings section to prioritize the second field if needed.
![A count by segment report with groupings set.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/VviAvKNsnx3Id6kWNnddC_report-grouped-by-interview-stage-name-and-job.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#visualization-options)Visualization Options
Once you’ve created your report, you can adjust the format of it to suit your needs. Use the Visualization tab's Chart Style drop-down to set your chart style type.
![A count by segment report using the pie chart style.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yQyoVhIQGUUPhkbXBdHca_visualization-segmentation-report.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can use the Measure drop-down to determine whether you are measure in percent (pie chart) or via a count (bar chart).
![A count by segment report with the Measure field set to Percent.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/_judnlqXu8pNFVjS0Kc29_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-16-at-40917-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can use the Sort By drop-down to sort your results. You can pick from the following options:
**None:** No sorting is applied
**Ascending:** Percentage increases clockwise from the top (for the percentage format) or from left-to-right (for the count format).
**Descending:** Percentage decreases clockwise from the top (for the percentage format) or from left-to-right (for the count format).
![An example of a referral leaderboard report.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jR8bqhoWYm_5yKPRO6XQH_referral-leaderboard.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#common-uses)Common Uses
**Pending Offers by Department**
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/LHrxc7e-zRBiBE9mp8tfl_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-17-at-21404-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Report Type:** Segmentation
**Subject:** Job Considerations (or Applications)
**Time Range:** Offer Stage Entered at (or Last Activity At)
**Filters:** Current Interview Stage Group (set to is and select the Offer stage group)
**Groupings:** Department.

**Referral Leaderboard**
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jR8bqhoWYm_5yKPRO6XQH_referral-leaderboard.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Report Type:** Segmentation
**Subject:** Job Considerations (or Applications)

**Filters:** Source Type > Referral
**Groupings:** Credited To
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#count-over-time)Count Over Time
The count over time report allows you to look at metrics over a specified time frame and interval breakdown (i.e weekly, monthly and so on).
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#initial-setup-1)Initial Setup
When creating a count over time report, you can populate the following fields:
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/TZVY0NUMBEwWFT-5vDcVA_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-17-at-95136-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Subject (required):** Where you can specify the topic you’re reporting on. The most commonly used subjects are Job Considerations (or Applications), Candidates, Interviews and Jobs.
**Timeframe & Interval (required):** Here you can select your time range and the interval you’d like to use for your report. You’ll be required to set the date field on your job consideration to let Ashby know the time stamp that should be used when pulling data for your report.
For example, if you’re reporting on hires, you’d look at setting the date field to be Hired At. This will ensure that only applications with an Hired At date within your specified time frame will be included. The data for that hire will then only be displayed in the interval the Hired At date falls in.
**Filters (optional):** If you don’t want to include all hires in your report (for example, if you’re only looking at hires for a specific team or department), you can click + Add Field to Match and select a filter here to ensure only the data you need is included in your report.
If you have the Advanced toggle turned off for filters, click + Filter instead to pick from the most commonly used filters. Advanced filtering does provide the widest range of options.
**Groupings (optional):** If you’d like to segment your data within your count over time report (for example, report on hires by department), then you can click + Add Field to Group Results By.
![A count over time table report.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sZqOCXV6_bw2wLcb75CS4_table-view-report.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/H8MXpDDMRH-gc0ZiMP3IN_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-17-at-95337-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#visualization-options-1)Visualization Options
You can choose from the following options in the Visualization section of the report builder.
**Line Chart:** Results are tracked across the specified intervals in a line chart format.
![An example Interviews by Stage over time report.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/J1K-prC_qwKuQfNNHFhhi-20260417-091424.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Bar Chart:** Results are tracked across the specified intervals in a bar chart format.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/hk-RTWwvoP0s6nYyLfloD_bar-chart-report-builder.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Stacked Bar Chart:** Used for reports that are grouped by a specific field.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/M4UZnwLPqphRSGA13jlxf_stacked-bar-chart.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Table:** Each week is listed with the count of results displayed beside it.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sZqOCXV6_bw2wLcb75CS4_table-view-report.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can also specify whether the count is listed as the number per interval (rate), or the count tallies as a running total across your time frame (running total).
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yBKdpputqcv_szmXGl2v4_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-17-at-100544-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#common-uses-1)Common Uses

**Subject:** Interviews

Please ensure you have a grouped interview plan in place if reporting by stage group. More on this at [What is a grouped interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/what-is-a-grouped-interview-plan)﻿

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#custom-list)Custom List
This report will allow you to create exportable lists where you can specify the data listed. Once you've configured your custom list report, the option to Export will appear above the option to set filters and below the Save Report button.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/6gd-PZyJ-QAvh5vqO1EmS_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-17-at-21914-pm.jpg?format=webp)

You can also create alerts from these reports so you can receive regular updates or notifications on new entries. More on this at [Alerts](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/alerts)﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#initial-setup-2)Initial Setup
When configuring your custom list report, you can configure the following sections:
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/dZvuynrDtsGW-cXERlvtl_report-builder-acent-ashby-2026-04-17-at-103930-am.jpg?format=webp)

**Subject:** You can select the topic you’re looking to report on here. Subjects include Job Considerations (or Applications), Candidates, Interviews, Surveys and Jobs.
**Filters:** Add filters using the + Add Field to Match option to ensure only the information you need is included in your report. For example, if you’re looking to pull all jobs with an open status, you would select Jobs as your subject, then choose the Job Status filter. Use the drop-down to ensure the job status equals open.
If you have the Advanced toggle turned off for filters, click + Filter instead to pick from the most commonly used filters. Advanced filtering does provide the widest range of options.
**Columns:** Select fields using the + Add Field to Show option to determine the data you would like to show in your list. For open jobs, you could opt to select the Job Title field, the Department field and the Location field for example. This data will then be displayed in your results.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#visualization-options-2)Visualization Options

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#how-do-time-ranges-work-in-reporting)How do time ranges work in reporting?
After selecting a date field on your job considerations to group them by (for example, grouping job considerations by the date they were started at), you’ll see a wide range of time range options to pull by.
Some of these are dynamic, meaning that the report will update automatically as time moves forward (so, for example if you selected This week, the current week will always be displayed).
Some are static, meaning that the results will only be shown for the selected time frame (for example, if you selected to count job considerations and specified to only count those between 8/5/24 and 8/12/24 that meet your criteria, the data for the same week would be shown every time that report is visited).
**Dynamic time frame examples -** This week, Last week, This Month, Last Month, This Year, Last Year, In the last three months, In the next three months.
**Static time frame examples -** Before X date, Between X date and X date.
When selecting a date using the In the last picker, we'll show the date used for the report. For example, if today is June 11, 2025, the picker will show June 10, 2025 if In the last 1 day is selected. If you'd like to include the current date too, ensure that the Include Today? checkbox is checked, then both today and the day before will be included (so June 10th-11th).
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/5TjusNW4cNGOpWa7kRg3k_in-that-last-1-day-including-today.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When selecting the In the next date picker and today is June 11, 2025, June 12, 2025 will show if In the next 1 day is specified. Again, if you'd like to include the current date, check the Include today? checkbox.
![The 'In the next' date picker.](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/hBvql_2xz56qvx3TQSIq8_in-the-next-date-picker.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#what-is-the-difference-between-time-frames-like-la)What is the difference between time frames like Last Week and In the last week?
The Last Week time range will cover Monday-Sunday of the previous week.
The In the last 1 week time range will cover the last 7 days. When selecting your time frame using an In the last… time range, you will be able to see the dates covered with this filter.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ArbBj2O1HZoRvYJ_FbrRa_in-the-last-week.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#can-i-report-on-notes-left-on-the-feed-of-the-cand)Can **I report on notes left on the feed of the candidate’s profile?**
It currently isn’t possible to report on notes on a candidate’s feed. You do have the option to report on follow-ups added to a candidate profile if you’ve added follow-up tasks.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#how-do-i-access-custom-reports-that-ive-built-and)How **do I access custom reports that I’ve built and saved in Ashby?**
You can access the reports you’ve built by navigating to Reports > Saved Reports. Here you can view the reports you’ve built, along with any that you have access to within your organization.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-reports#ive-build-a-custom-list-report-and-one-of-the-colu)I've build a custom list report and one of the columns is a private field - who can view the report?
Only users with access to the private field will be able to see the custom list report. If you'd like users without Organization Admin or Elevated Access - Admin Private access to be able to view the report, consider removing the private field from the Fields to Show section.

# Offer and Offer Acceptance Reports
10 min
Copy
This document provides an overview of offer processes and their impact on offer and offer acceptance reports.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#offer-processes)Offer Processes
Talent teams can glean a lot of insight from offer details and offer acceptances.
Before we can start reporting on offers and acceptance rates, it is important to determine your offer process and what parts of the process you want to report on. There are a couple of typical processes and general reporting capabilities that come with each:
**All offer and offer data is in the ATS [Recommended]:** Can report on offer activity, offer stage activity/movement, and offer details.
**Offer and offer data is partially in the ATS and partially external:** Can report on offer stage activity/movement and on offer details that are in the ATS.
**Offer and offer data are all stored outside of the ATS:** Can report on offer stage activity/ movement.
**Expand to see detailed explanations of each of these processes.**
**Offer sent in ATS [Recommended]**
This is the recommended offer process to ensure maximum report-ability and consistency. Offer are created and sent through your ATS (this could be through an integration). All offer details are added to the ATS and the ATS acts as the source of truth. Most ATSs do not allow you to report on more than one offer per application, so all offer reports are going to pull the data from the most latest version of the offer. Using this process will allow Ashby to use the following data points to create offer reports:
  * Date candidate entered offer stage (could be used as a sign of a verbal offer or an intent to offer)


  * Date offer was created


  * Date offer was extended


  * Date offer was rejected or accepted (for Greenhouse customers, this will be the date a candidate was either rejected or hired after their offer has been created)


  * Offer status


  * Any custom offer fields


**Offer Data is in ATS but is sent externally**
Some companies are not ready to send all offers through their ATS. The second best alternative for report-ability and consistency, is to send offers outside of the ATS but still enter the offer details back into the ATS. This is not the same as uploading the signed offer (Please see next section). Since the offer details and offer object are present, we are able to still pull some meaningful insights, but since the action of sending the offer was outside of the ATS, we are not able to capture that specific date. Using this process will allow Ashby to use the following data points to create offer reports:
  * Date candidate entered offer stage (could be used as a proxy for offer extended date)


  * Date offer was created in ATS (could be used as a proxy for offer extended date)


  * Date candidate was archived or hired


  * Offer status (except extended and/or pending)


  * Any custom offer fields


**Offers sent outside of ATS but completed offers are uploaded into ATS (no details added) or offer activity is outside of the ATS**
Although uploading the completed offers into your ATS allows for you to have a source of truth and repository of all your offers in one place, the offer details are not filled out and therefore, we are not able to pull in most data. Using this process will allow you to report on the following data points in Ashby:
  * Date candidate entered offer stage (could be used as proxy for offer extended date)


  * Date candidate was archived or hired (could be used as proxy for offer rejected or accepted)


﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#offer-reporting-basics)Offer Reporting Basics
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#commonly-used-offer-fields-and-definitions)Commonly Used Offer Fields and Definitions
Fields in purple require an offer object (offer details) to exist on the application (either process **A or B**). Please note that this is not an exhaustive list. Ashby pulls in and allows you to report on all custom fields.
  * **Time and Date Fields**


  * **Decided At:** the date a candidate accepts or rejects an offer (for Greenhouse customers, this is the same as the date a candidate is hired or archived)


  * **Created At:** the date the offer was generated in the ATS


  * **Extended At or Sent At:** the date the offer was sent through the ATS


  * **Start Date:** the start date from the offer letter


  * **Offer Stage Entered At:** the date a candidate moves into the offer stage (can be used as a proxy for offer sent if offers not sent in ATS)


  * **Consideration Ended At:** the date a candidate was either archived or hired (this is not specific to the offer stage, so you will need to add a filter for candidates that reached offer stage)


  * **User Fields**


  * **Requester:** user who requested the approval chain to start (you must have an approval workflow in your ATS)


  * **Creator:** user who created the offer in the ATS


  * **Recruiter** (job or candidate level): user who is assigned as the recruiter


  * **Other Fields**


  * **Status (Offer):** Created, Extended, Rejected, or Accepted


  * **Status (Approval Process):** Pending, Approved, or Rejected


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#template-offer-acceptance-rate-report)Template Offer Acceptance Rate Report
Ashby offers a prebuilt report that allows you to show and group acceptance rates. You can group by month or any other time cadence, or by another field. If you are grouping by a time frame, the time field is assumed to be decided at and cannot be changed.
If you are not adding any offer details in your ATS, this will not be an effective report and it is recommended to create a [Offer Acceptance Count Over Time Report](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#offer-acceptance-count-over-time-reports)﻿﻿
﻿  

![Offer Acceptance Rate Overall.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/amyUuzVHqpBpLQhzZlRoA_offeracceptancerateoverall.png?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#custom-offer-acceptance-rate-reports)Custom Offer Acceptance Rate Reports
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#define-your-organizations-offer-acceptance-rate)Define Your Organization’s Offer Acceptance Rate
Before creating custom offer acceptance rate reports, make sure to keep in mind what offers you would like to include in this percentage. Some things to consider:
  * **Do you want the offer acceptance rate to be the percentage of offers accepted out of the offers extended?**


  * By selecting the Decided At date as your time range filter, this will automatically filter for all candidates that received an offer. You can either group by Outcome or by Offer Status.


![Untitled](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/n0x54xmgIglKtCSVDQEOr_untitled.png?format=webp)
﻿
  * **Do you want the offer acceptance rate to be the percentage of offers accepted out of all intended offers (including verbal offers)?**


  * The Consideration Ended At filter is not stage specific, so you will need to add a filter for candidates that Reached Stage Group Offer. You should only be grouping by outcome because an offer status only exists for candidates that have an offer object (either created or extended).


![Untitled](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/IwW8lVme8VoOXoToS2eN9_untitled-1.png?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#offer-acceptance-rate-tile-report)Offer Acceptance Rate Tile Report
A popular report to add to any dashboard is an offer acceptance rate tile report. This report appears as just a simple percentage and can be filtered by dashboard level filters.
To create this report, follow the same steps as you would for a [Ratio Reports](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/ratio-reports)﻿ and visualize as a percentage.
![Untitled](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/zBX39eC66sfjlRRwPWbX2_untitled-2.png)
﻿
**Best Practices**
  * Pending offers and active candidates in offer can skew the acceptance rate lower. Since these offers have not had the opportunity to either be accepted or rejected, it is recommended to exclude Outcome: Active and/or Status: Pending.


  * Typically, customers report offer acceptance rate by decision date.


  * Whatever time field you choose to report by (decision date, extended at date, entered offer stage date, etc…), make sure other reports on the dashboard are aligned. For example, if your offer acceptance rate is by extended at date, you can include another tile with the total offers extended in that period of time. However, it is not recommended to add a tile with the total offers decided on in that period of time.


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/offer-and-offer-acceptance-reports#offer-acceptance-count-over-time-reports)Offer Acceptance Count Over Time Reports
A count over time report provides more flexibility than the template report.
![Offer Acceptance Rate, Last 12 Months.png](https://app.archbee.com/api/optimize/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Fr4bVbijhCBpcjOpIGMrt_offeracceptanceratelast12months.png)
﻿
**Time Field & Interval**
The first thing you need to do is determine what date/ time field you want to report by. The most popular ones are:
  * Offer Start Date


  * Extended At/ Sent At


  * Decided At


  * Offer Stage Entered at


  * Consideration Ended At - must add Reached Stage Group: Offer as a filter


The field you select is how the report will determine what time frame an offer will count for. For example, if you are doing a month over month report, an offer that was sent in February and was signed in March has a February Extended At/Sent At date and a March Decided At date.
**Filters**
If you are including offers that were not sent in the ATS, it is recommended that you add a NOT filter for candidates with an active outcome since these offers have neither been rejected or accepted.
**Group By**
If you are only considering offers that were sent through the ATS, it is recommended to use Status as the field to group by.
If you are including verbal offers or any other offer stage activity in the offer acceptance rate, you must group by Outcome, because there will be candidates who technically Declined but have no offer object. (This was used for the example report)

# Can I search Ashby from my Chrome browser?
2 min
Copy
You can, yes. This guide walks through how to configure a new search option in your browser with Ashby as the query engine. This option is only available for the Chrome browser.
If you’re looking to add Ashby as a search option in Google Chrome:
  * Navigate to your Chrome settings.


  * Head to the Searchengine tab.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e85d2aa60f0fef5c71d63b3e50193d1a.png)
﻿
  * Click Manage search engines and site search.


  * In the Site Search section, click Add.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b49506f88e4580ecec24775f655a8b42.png)
﻿
  * From here, complete the Name, Shortcut and URL fields on the following screen.


![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/37e23b1cc4074379222c2e146602aa0d.png)
﻿
When adding in your URL, add ?search=%s to the end of your homepage URL.
  * Click Add to confirm.


﻿  

To use your newly configured site search, use the shortcut you specified in the Shortcut field to then see the option to search Ashby in your browser search bar.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b8d5cadc9d56a514f44bdd2870769c06.png)
﻿
﻿

# Form Field Connectors
16 min
Copy
You are now able to link your form questions to your candidate fields, so an answer on a form will automatically fill the linked field on their profile (including custom fields﻿).
Form Field Connectors can be configured on the Plus, Legacy Plus and Enterprise plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ❌  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#linking-an-application-form-field-to-a-candidate-f)Linking an Application Form Field to a Candidate Field
Head into your shared [Application Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/application-forms "Application Forms") under Admin>Jobs & Applications and select the form you’d like to amend. Scroll to the question you’d like to connect to a candidate field and click the link icon.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/65245a02d04a279d47fa11fa76a623fd.png)
﻿
From there, you will be prompted to either select an existing field to connect to this question, or create a new custom field. You can use the search bar to find the exact field you’re looking for if you already have a lot of custom fields in place.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d8e934ea7086a93f193877d02f74db8a.png)
﻿
To select an existing field, click on it within the Choose an existing fieldto connect: list. Click into and amend the Title and Description fields and then click Create and Connect Custom Field to create a new field for your question to be linked to.
Once you’ve selected or created your field, the connected path will be confirmed. You will have the option to disconnect the link or switch the field the application question field is mapped to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/222fa2d576db71c2f4d0cce27f21f696.png)
﻿
Once the link is established, we will also pull any applications completed before this link was configured and offer to backfill them. Click Backfill Now to complete the backfill process.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#linking-a-referral-form-field-to-a-candidate-field)Linking a Referral Form Field to a Candidate Field
Head to Admin > Sourcing > [Referrals](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/referral-forms "Referrals"), then click on your referral form. Click the link icon underneath the field you’d like to link to a candidate field.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/a87263d6a3bf49c50786429405b8025a.png)
﻿
From there, select the field on the candidate’s profile you’d like to map this question’s answer to, either by selecting from the list of existing candidate fields, or by creating a new custom field.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/73a126f3c9182e5f014fb096e43c674a.png)
﻿
Once you’ve selected or created your new field, you will then have the option to backfill previously referred candidates and pull the information added initially to the matching candidate field on their profile.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/848673b120db93dd35ee665fcaf922f5.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#linking-a-survey-or-questionnaire-field-to-a-field)Linking a Survey or Questionnaire Field to a Field on the Candidate Profile
Head to Admin> Jobs & Applications> [Surveys](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/surveys/diversity-surveys "Surveys"). From there, go to either the Candidate Experience tab or the Questionnaires tab and select the survey and field you’d like to map to a candidate field.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/fc01d97b5fdb3148545c11b49ccae2fa.png)
﻿
Click the link icon and then select the existing field you’d like to map the candidate’s answer to. If the field doesn’t already exist, you can create your own new custom field to map this information to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/33f22bcea926c0e5f1eaaff5c46b804e.png)
﻿
Once you’ve selected or created your field, the connected path will be confirmed. You will have the option to disconnect the link or switch the field the application question field is mapped to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/8b71dd21d1d6ecf0701a5317aa439ad5.png)
﻿
Once the link is established, we will also pull any applications completed before this link was configured and offer to backfill them. Click Backfill Now to complete the backfill process.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#linking-a-sourcing-form-field-to-a-candidate-profi)Linking a Sourcing Form Field to a Candidate Profile Field
Head to Sourcing>[Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/sourcing/forms/all "Forms"). From there, select the sourcing form you’d like to edit, then head to Settings, then Configure Form.
Click the link icon and then select the existing field you’d like to map the candidate’s answer to. If the field doesn’t already exist, you can create your own new custom field to map this information to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/7964b5f85d374a0802483719a7b027a7.png)
﻿
Once you’ve selected or created your field, the connected path will be confirmed. You will have the option to disconnect the link or switch the field the application question field is mapped to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/89ac61e3bdac8cb953b4151d10c3d8ae.png)
﻿
Once the link is established, we will also pull any applications completed before this link was configured and offer to backfill them. Click Backfill Now to complete the backfill process if that option is available.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/87c4c1c2c9900ea81f3d29ca2f4eb1e7.png)
﻿
﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#linking-a-feedback-form-field-to-a-candidate-custo)Linking a Feedback Form Field to a Candidate Custom Field
Head to Admin > Interviews & Scheduling > [Feedback Forms](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/feedback-forms "Feedback Forms") and select the form you’d like to amend. Scroll to the question you’d like to connect to a candidate custom field and click the link icon.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d84a80ed520d40897ba25ab849465ff0.png)
﻿
From there, you will be prompted to either select an existing field to connect to this feedback field, or create a new custom field. You can use the search bar to find the exact custom field you’re looking for if you already have a lot of custom fields in place and need to find the best match.
To select an existing field, click on it within the Choose an existing field to connect list. Alternatively, click into and amend the Title and Description fields and then click Create and Connect Custom Field to create a new field for your question to be linked to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/2c993ce871d5c5ac92201879376a508f.png)
﻿
Once you’ve selected or created your field, the connected path will be confirmed. You will have the option to disconnect the link or switch the field the feedback form field is mapped to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1a8acce64a2bd5fb76662659915928d3.png)
﻿
Once the link is established, we will also pull any feedback forms completed before this link was configured and offer to backfill the connected field. Click Backfill Now to complete the backfill process. The latest value added to this field within the feedback form will overwrite the value that was previously in the candidate’s custom field. The only exception to this is if you are connecting a phone number field to the Any Phone Number field - in this case, the backfilled phone number will be added to the list under Other Phone Numbers on the candidate’s profile.
Please note that the latest value added to a feedback form field linked to a candidate custom field will overwrite any previous values.
As such, if you have two interviewers completing the same feedback form for an interview, if one interviewer submits a value in the connected field with their feedback at 1pm, then the second interviewer submits a value within the connected feedback form field again at 1:30pm on the same day, the value submitted at 1:30pm will overwrite the value added at 1pm and will be displayed within the candidate custom field.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#unsupported-field-types-for-feedback-form-connecto)Unsupported Field Types for Feedback Form Connectors
Please note that it’s not possible to connect the following field types using feedback form field connectors:
**Candidate Custom Field Types**
You will be unable to link feedback form fields to the following candidate custom field types:
  * Long Unformatted Answer


**Feedback Form Field Types**
The following feedback form field types are not supported when connecting fields to a candidate custom field:
  * Score


  * Location


  * Long Formatted Answer


There is also limited support for Phone Number fields. They can only be connected to the following fields: **The Phone Number (Primary, Personal) field** - the value would then replace the existing number listed in this field on the candidate profile **The Any Phone Number field -** the value would then be added to the Other Phone Numbers field on the candidate’s profile. 
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#pulling-candidate-information-through-to-interview)Pulling Candidate Information through to Interviewers with Limited Access
If you have information being mapped to custom fields that you’d like interviewers to see before an interview regardless of their access (for example, the candidate’s pronouns), then you can add the custom fields you’d like to display in [Interview Briefing Info](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/interview-briefing-settings "Interview Briefing Info").
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1d99faf0ce1c449b547c498d21faaa83.png)
﻿
Any custom fields specified here will then be shown to your panel within their interview briefing.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/d2f02a97a707a629853c87d5a98dcd40.png)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#how-does-this-work-with-multiple-answer-question-f)H**ow does this work with multiple-answer question fields with answers that differ from the form’s range of answers?**
In the case that you are backfilling a Multiple Choice or Checkboxes field, you’ll also get the opportunity to map any options on previous versions of the form to the new connected field. This allows you to backfill the previous forms, even when the connect field’s options don’t line up perfectly with the previous submissions. You’ll also be able to map all other choices to a specific value.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/3b556758ee733c7b32eccf8db2cdeb61.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#how-does-linking-forms-to-fields-impact-reporting)How does linking forms to fields impact reporting?
Instead of reporting on a specific form field, you can report on that specific candidate field directly, streamlining your reporting process.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#what-happens-if-a-candidate-submits-two-forms-both)What happens if a Candidate submits two forms, both of which have questions connected to the same field?
The most recent submission will override the previous ones.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/form-field-connectors#how-can-i-view-the-forms-that-are-connected-to-a-s)How can I view the forms that are connected to a specific field?
If you navigate to Admin > Organization Setup > Custom Fields, then click on the field in question, you’ll be able to view the form fields the custom field is connected to.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/ed90d04c5dc23f8445640bee223d8064.png)
﻿
﻿

[Read more](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/climatebase)

CodeAlly's GitHub-powered Job Board works with your hiring process & word-of-mouth to reach talented technical candidates & their peers. Please note that on initial setup, the sync can take up to 48 hours.

﻿
### Everyrole

Connect Everyole to your Ashby account, syncing your open positions to display them on job boards that want to feature them. Please note that on initial setup, the sync can take up to 48 hours.

This integration will allow your Ashby job posting to the Welcome to the Jungle job board. For Welcome to the Jungle (formerly Otta), please check out Welcome to the Jungle (formerly Otta) Step 1 Head over to the Welcome to the Jungle integration settings page in the Marketplace at https //app.

Easily sync your jobs with your X profile. Jobs can be shared with your followers and be featured on your company profile, making them discoverable by over 450m active users.

chrome-extension:
<!-- source: https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension -->

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Sourcing & Outreach
# Chrome Extension
35 min
Copy
You can download the Ashby Chrome extension from the [Chrome Web Store listing](https://chromewebstore.google.com/u/1/detail/ashby/ncbbpdnmfeilfegjpilgdjajafbcganl "Chrome Web Store listing"). 
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#ashby-chrome-extension-overview)Ashby Chrome extension overview
The Ashby Chrome extension allows you to source new talent with just a few clicks. Use powerful search to comb through passive and past candidates. Keep prospects engaged via email campaigns and tracking - all within Ashby.
See the table below for information on the access roles that can use the Chrome extension to source candidates and consider them for a role in Ashby. Only those with Elevated Access - Hiring Manager access or above will be able to source candidates using the Chrome extension.  
| Access Role  | Has access to source candidates and consider them for jobs?  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Limited Access  | ❌  |  
| Agency Users  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - External Recruiter (not linked to an agency)  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Analyst  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Team Member  | ❌  |  
| Elevated Access - Hiring Manager  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin  | ✅  |  
| Elevated Access - Admin (Private)  | ✅  |  
| Organization Admin  | ✅  |  
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#install-the-extension)Install the extension
Once installed, the purple Ashby icon will appear in the top right corner of the window. Click the 🧩 icon, then pin the Ashby icon to the extension bar.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#download-in-the-chrome-web-store)Download in the Chrome web store
﻿[Click here to download the extension](https://chromewebstore.google.com/u/1/detail/ashby/ncbbpdnmfeilfegjpilgdjajafbcganl "Click here to download the extension") in the Chrome Web Store. Click the blue Add to Chrome button. Once installed, the purple Ashby icon will appear in the top right corner of the window. Click the 🧩 icon, then pin the Ashby icon to the extension bar.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vxRpjf7krKDO92kOozJnK_the-ashby-chrome-extension-in-the-chrome-store-with-the-add-to-chrome-button-highlighted.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/HZ56q4RXG1mIY3qWPsCBX_the-puzzle-piece-icon-to-the-right-of-the-address-bar-in-chrome.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
#  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#using-the-extension)Using the extension
To open the extension, click on the Ashby A icon or use the shortcut Cmd+Shift+E. You can provide a social link URL to lookup or add a candidate at any time. If you are on a social link URL (e.g. a LinkedIn, GitHub or X profile) that matches to a social profile URL in a candidate profile already in Ashby, we’ll show that existing candidate profile.
The extension also automatically works in Gmail. When viewing a conversation between a candidate and the hiring team Ashby will automatically show the relevant candidate profile in the extension.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Mmspi1uSRs0ZrdZJ7BYFV_ashby-a-icon-chrome-extension.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
When you open up the Chrome extension, it will open up as a side panel, rather than a popover. You can resize this panel by clicking and dragging on the left edge of the panel.
If you are already logged into your Ashby account in another window or tab, you will automatically be logged into the same account in the Ashby extension. If you are not already logged into Ashby, you will be prompted to log into your Ashby account before you can start using the extension.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/p_tioVxZq4C-JlTR1zC_1_sign-in-to-ashby-chrome-extension.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#using-the-extension-with-linkedin)Using the extension with LinkedIn
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#adding-and-viewing-candidates-when-using-the-chrom)Adding and viewing candidates when using the Chrome extension with LinkedIn
When viewing a public LinkedIn profile, the Chrome extension will show whether or not the candidate has already been added to your Ashby organization. This is assessed using the LinkedIn URL associated with the candidate's profile in Ashby.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wsJF7TAAPzrnMsxQeIrmY_adding-a-candidate-to-ashby-chrome-extension.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If the candidate is not in your Ashby account already, you can add them via three routes:
  * You can copy and paste the LinkedIn profile URL or a GitHub profile URL into the Paste a linkedIn or GitHub profile URL field on the extension. Then click Import Candidate to add them to Ashby. 


When taking this route, we will create the candidate record using publicly available information. In rare cases that can mean that no information or outdated information is surfaced. We recommend checking that the work history provided is relevant against the candidate's profile.
  * Click More on the candidate's LinkedIn profile, then Save to PDF. This will download the profile information as a resume and also upload the profile information as a candidate in Ashby. The information in the download will be parsed and the PDF will be added as a resume to the candidate's profile. 


Please note that LinkedIn has a limit of 200 downloaded resumes per month per user. The Save to PDF option is only available when viewing the candidate's public profile.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/Zpb5v2PUqUjRUuPzcDFmz_chrome-extension-archbee-document-2026-03-13-at-93508-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  * You can also click enter their details manually on Chrome extension and can then add their information to Ashby from there.


By default, the source will be set to Sourced: Ashby Chrome Extension and a Source Site will be detected and included within the attribution details (for example, LinkedIn, LinkedIn Recruiter, GitHub). Both of these fields, along with the Credited To field can be amended after adding the candidate to Ashby. To edit the attribution details, navigate to the candidate's profile and scroll to the Attribution section in the Activities & Progress tab. Click the X to the right of the field to clear the current value, then select the appropriate alternative.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/eovwvHltaAy95G34P2t9W_sophie-ashby-summary-acent-ashby-2026-02-26-at-104112-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If you switch the source to something other than Sourced: Ashby Chrome Extension, then the Source Site field will no longer be included in the Attribution details on the candidate's profile.
If the candidate is already in Ashby, you will see a pink indicator on the Ashby Chrome extension icon.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/-E0mMYIpAeoheLM8OsDjJ_sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-03-25-at-21812-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#using-the-extension-on-linkedin-recruiter)Using the extension on LinkedIn Recruiter
When viewing a candidate in LinkedIn Recruiter, you will need to add the candidate's LinkedIn public profile URL in order to see whether they are already in your Ashby account or to add them to Ashby. Copy the URL and paste it into the Profile URL field in the extension. The Chrome extension will then be able to either show the candidate in Ashby or prompt you to add them to Ashby. 
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/xeAg4juaOrlbCL55j6l_O_image-3-png-2026-02-26-at-114740-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#bulk-importing-candidates-to-ashby-in-linkedin-rec)Bulk importing candidates to Ashby in LinkedIn Recruiter via CSV export
LinkedIn Recruiter offers the option to bulk export candidates to a CSV file (more on this in LinkedIn's guide [Export profiles in Recruiter](https://www.linkedin.com/help/recruiter/answer/a412149 "Export profiles in Recruiter")). The Chrome extension will detect the CSV once it has been exported, enrich the exported profiles with publicly available information and then import them into Ashby in bulk.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/vA3K4-qX8Qi4Pw4Z81lXB_image-8-png-2026-02-26-at-115413-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Please note that LinkedIn Recruiter has a limit of 25 candidates per export.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#using-the-chrome-extension-with-github)Using the Chrome extension with GitHub
When on a GitHub profile, the Chrome extension will search through existing candidates in Ashby to find candidates with the same name. If there is a match, you will have the option to add the GitHub profile link to the existing candidate’s profile as a social link.
Click Confirm Candidate to match the GitHub profile to the candidate profile in Ashby. Alternatively, click Create a New Candidate Instead to create a new candidate profile including the GitHub link as a social link.
Once you’ve linked the candidate profile, or created a new profile, whenever you revisit that GitHub profile, you can view the candidate’s profile details and take action using the Chrome extension.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/yjxTWC6DdT8PLOlE0FNnS_github-profile-chrome-extension.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#using-the-extension-with-gmail)Using the extension with Gmail
When replying to a candidate in Gmail, the Ashby Chrome Extension allows you to review key details from their candidate profile. You can add tags, notes, and reminders for yourself or someone on your team to follow up with the candidate at a later date and time.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/NyQDPVasm_MoxrdkC3t8I_gmail-chrome-extension.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#key-features)Key Features 
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#data-enrichment)Data enrichment
Ashby’s [data enrichment feature](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-enrichment "data enrichment feature") allows you to find personal emails for candidates directly in the extension. Once found, these emails can be used to reach out to the candidate.
**Data enrichment settings**
The [**Data Enrichment Settings**](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-enrichment "Data Enrichment Settings") display the remaining lookups your team has for the month. For more on purchasing additional lookups, check out [How do I purchase additional email lookups, ai credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app "How do I purchase additional email lookups, ai credits or texting credits in-app?")﻿
Ensure that Enable On-Demand Email Lookup is checked in Admin > Sourcing > [Data Enrichment](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-enrichment "Data Enrichment") if you'd like your team to proactively search for emails by clicking the Find Email button when on a candidate profile.
![Screen Shot 2023-07-07 at 8.56.17 AM.png](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/mmfjA-LzTjCj4Hr1-2HPH_screenshot2023-07-07at85617am.png?format=webp)
﻿
This option gives your team more control over when lookups occur. Alternatively, disabling this option results in a lookup occurring automatically when you add the LinkedIn profile URL to the Chrome extension and add the candidate to your Ashby account.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#send-email-sequences)Send email sequences
You can enroll a candidate in an email sequence that will message them at a set cadence until they follow up. Check out [Sequences](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences "Sequences") for a more detailed overview of this feature. For more information on InMail and Connection Request nudges in a sequence, check out [Sequences: InMail and Connection Request Stages](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/sequences-inmail-and-connection-request-stages "Sequences: InMail and Connection Request Stages").
Please note that if you enroll a candidate in a sequence via the Chrome extension, this is counted as an individual sequence and can’t be edited in bulk along with other enrollments.
**Reason for Outreach and other custom fields**
Before sending a sequence, populate details in the reason for outreach and other [custom fields](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/custom-fields?objectType=candidate "custom fields") you create. If you have tokens in your sequences that rely on information added into custom fields, then this will ensure that information is populated.
For more on configuring custom fields, check out [Custom Fields](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/custom-fields "Custom Fields"). 
﻿  

![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ND7WERa4rtzXuDeGun1gQ_5-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-26-at-22031-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
**Select a sequence, customize it, and send it to a candidate**
To send a sequence out to a candidate via the Chrome Extension, click Draft Sequence.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/S0CNomLuYq3O-OsHvuLnD_5-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-26-at-22057-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
From here, you can determine the sequence template and the candidate’s email address. Once both fields are completed, you’ll have the options to start the sequence immediately, or review it before sending.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/ZdRZ3PTipngwNZlfzRq5E_5-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-26-at-22229-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If you’d like to make any edits to your sequence before sending it, select Review _._ You’ll be able to review each stage and make any adjustments. Once you’re ready to start the sequence, click Start _._
If you don’t opt to send the sequence, it will be kept as a draft on the candidate’s profile so you can send it later from within Ashby.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#consider-the-candidate-for-a-job)Consider the candidate for a job
Check out [Creating & Posting Jobs](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/creating-and-posting-jobs "Creating & Posting Jobs") for a more detailed overview of creating jobs.
You can add candidates directly into a job pipeline within the Chrome extension by clicking the Consider for Job button.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/0F0dvvmjTuEscLLX2YExj_2-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-93252-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Once clicked, you’ll be able to select the role and the stage in the interview plan the candidate should be added to. Check the Update experience checkbox if you'd like the candidate's education and work experience on their LinkedIn profile to be analyzed and updated in Ashby. 
This will override existing education and work experience information already on their profile.
﻿  

![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/jO71jV6HswbrNWhjjulFr_2-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-93435-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
Click Add to confirm and add the candidate to the job pipeline.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#change-candidate-stage-or-archive)Change candidate stage or archive
You can change a candidate’s current stage by clicking the Change Stage button and selecting the new stage.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/FpK05GEKY-fw9wZY7-MjV_2-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-93938-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
You can archive a candidate by clicking the Archive button. Then you’ll select an archive reason, decide if feedback is required, and click Archive.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/bgT6itQxB0eZJQFToQm_i_2-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-94010-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
If a candidate has already been archived or for a job consideration, the Archive button will not be present.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#add-candidate-to-a-project)Add candidate to a project
Check out [Projects](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/projects "Projects") for a more detailed overview of this feature.
**Add candidates to a project from the Chrome extension**
Once you have set up a project, you can add candidates to that project from the Chrome Extension or on their candidate profile. Click the + icon in the Projects section of the Chrome extension, then select the project from the list. The candidate will then be added to the selected project.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/xVf0v5-sETTCR_swdGKFC_2-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-94214-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#adding-tags-to-a-candidate-via-the-chrome-extensio)Adding tags to a candidate via the Chrome extension
For more on tagging candidates, check out [Tags](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/tags "Tags").
Click Add Tags within the Chrome extension to add tags to the candidate profile in Ashby. To create a new tag in the Chrome extension start typing the name of your new tag, then click Create new tag "X" to add the tag to the candidate profile and to your list of available tags.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/_1XGE2dBANlvgjTeHwbhO_2-sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-94702-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#creating-a-follow-up-using-the-chrome-extension)Creating a follow up using the Chrome extension
If you’d like to be reminded to return to a candidate’s profile later, you can create a follow-up. Click Follow up... in the Chrome extension to set an assignee and a date to send the notification to follow up on the candidate's profile. You will also have the option to add a note.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/f1nLIRcHnCZ_E5_a5dzet_sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-95637-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#log-outreach-or-responses-in-ashby)Log outreach or responses in Ashby
If you've reached out to the candidate or have received a response from them in LinkedIn, this information can be manually logged in Ashby. Click Log Activity and select whether you are logging outreach or a response. 
If you are logging outreach activity, determine the outreach type and outreach text, then click Log Activity to add the activity to the candidate's profile.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/VxqxYUov8BgbJqOxUDPqe_sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-95942-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
For response activity, set the response type, the response status (whether the candidate is interested or not) and then add in the response text. Click Log Activity to add the activity to the candidate's profile.
![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/wwo8CKNZ6t-9XgY8OLQS7_sophie-ashby-linkedin-2026-02-27-at-100314-am.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#updating-the-chrome-extension)Updating the Chrome Extension
Chrome typically handles extension updates automatically, but you can optionally force an update. To do this:
  1. Open Chrome and go to **chrome://extensions**


  1. Click on the Details button under the Ashby Chrome extension


  1. Toggle on Developer mode (in the top-right corner)


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/A4Yx-3vYbT5wD05ERFT5Z_extensions-ashby-2026-05-01-at-123718-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Click Update (on the left-hand side)


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/sxsGkJGjjnu3LvsFXza4N_extensions-ashby-2026-05-01-at-123804-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Toggle Developer mode back off


You can verify your version and that you have the extension installed from the [Chrome Web Store listing](https://chromewebstore.google.com/u/1/detail/ashby/ncbbpdnmfeilfegjpilgdjajafbcganl "Chrome Web Store listing").
If you run into any issues updating or do not have access to your browser's extension settings, try uninstalling and reinstalling the extension will also update to its latest version.
To do this:
  1. Navigate to the [Chrome Web Store listing](https://chromewebstore.google.com/u/1/detail/ashby/ncbbpdnmfeilfegjpilgdjajafbcganl "Chrome Web Store listing")﻿


  1. Click Remove from Chrome


![Document image](https://images.archbee.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/PwXrH8p8adbxsX8H4N8DA_ashby-chrome-web-store-2026-05-01-at-124336-pm.jpg?format=webp)
﻿
  1. Once uninstalled, reinstall by clicking Add to Chrome.Doing so will automatically download the extension's latest version.


##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#how-many-email-lookups-do-i-get-each-month)How many email lookups do I get each month?
The number of lookups you receive depends on your plan. You can check how many you have on the [Email Lookups](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-enrichment "Email Lookups") tab in Admin.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#what-is-the-difference-between-on-demand-and-autom)W**hat is the difference between on-demand and automatic email lookups?**
You have the option to enrich candidate profiles from the Chrome Extension automatically (after the candidate has been added to Ashby), or by clicking a button. On-demand allows you more control over the number or email lookups you use, while automatic enrichment saves you a click. Automatic enrichment is the default option, and you can enable on-demand enrichment on the [Data Enrichment](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/organizational-settings/data-enrichment "Data Enrichment") tab in Admin.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#how-often-do-my-email-lookups-reset)How often do my email lookups reset?
Email Lookups reset at midnight in your local time on the last day of each month. Unused lookups do not roll over.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#can-i-add-more-email-lookups)Can I add more email lookups?
Yes you can purchase additional email lookups if needed. You can read more on this at [How do I purchase additional email lookups, ai credits or texting credits in-app?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-purchase-additional-email-lookups-ai-credits-or-texting-credits-in-app "How do I purchase additional email lookups, ai credits or texting credits in-app?")﻿
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#can-the-chrome-extension-be-used-on-other-browsers)Can the Chrome extension be used on other browsers?
The Chrome extension is supported on the following browsers:
  * Chrome


  * Chromium


  * Edge


  * Arc


  * Opera


  * Brave


  * Vivaldi


The Chrome extension is not supported on the following browsers:
  * Firefox


  * Safari


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#which-linkedin-products-does-the-extension-work-wi)Which LinkedIn products does the extension work with?
  * LinkedIn


  * LinkedIn Recruiter


  * LinkedIn Recruiter Lite


  * Sales Navigator


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#how-do-i-prevent-duplicate-candidates)How do I prevent duplicate candidates?
The extension matches duplicates based on name, email address, and LinkedIn URL. If a LinkedIn URL is found on the page, the extension will display the existing profile (if the name is common and the email does not come from LinkedIn).
If no match is found based on the LinkedIn URL, a new profile can be added. Once a profile is added, it will be checked across all three criteria. If a duplicate is found at that point, a duplicate notification will appear, and the two profiles can be merged.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#what-permissions-do-i-need-to-add-candidates-to-jo)What permissions do I need to add candidates to jobs via the Chrome extension?
You need at least Elevated Access - Hiring Managerpermissions for the job in question in order to be able to add a candidate to the job. Our guide to permissions is at [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions "User Permissions").
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#the-extension-has-created-candidates-with-incomple)The extension has created candidates with incomplete experience/education - is there a way to get that missing information into Ashby?
When adding the candidate via a social URL we are creating the profile using publicly available information, which may at times be limited or outdated. The alternative is to add the candidate via a PDF resume. When adding a candidate via PDF resume download, we will reliably parse the entire work/education history of the candidate.
Please note: while it is technically easy to capture the entire LinkedIn profile in an extension reliably, that is not permitted by LinkedIn’s terms of service.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#im-seeing-multiple-source-sites-across-job-conside)I'**m seeing multiple source sites across job considerations for a candidate - why?**
If you consider the candidate for one role while viewing their profile on LinkedIn, then the Source Site on their job consideration will be set to LinkedIn or LinkedIn Recruiter. If you then consider them for another role using the Chrome extension while viewing their GitHub profile, then the job consideration will be created with the Source Site listed as GitHub. This is expected behaviour. The Source Site can be overridden for an application or job consideration if you transfer it to another role. More on this at [﻿[Can I transfer a candidate’s job consideration to another job or interview plan?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/can-i-transfer-a-candidates-job-consideration-to-another-job-or-interview-plan "Can I transfer a candidate’s job consideration to another job or interview plan?")﻿](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension)﻿
﻿  

###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/chrome-extension#can-i-pin-or-view-pinned-notes-via-the-chrome-exte)Can I pin or view pinned notes via the Chrome extension?
Yes. Each note will show an option to pin or unpin in the Chrome extension. Any pinned notes will show at the top of the Profile and Feed sections in the extension.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/1205dfbbe12417f85f1fc88464f427e9.png)
﻿
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Interviews & Scheduling
Interviews: Advanced Setup
# Keywords
6 min
Copy
Scheduling Keywords allow you to identify events on interviewer’s calendars that can be scheduled over by either you or a candidate when booking interviews.
Scheduling Keywords are available on Foundations, Plus, Enterprise and Legacy Plus plans.  
| Foundations  | Legacy Plus  | Plus  | Enterprise  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| ✅  | ✅  | ✅  | ✅  |  
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/keywords#overview)Overview
﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/keywords#adding-scheduling-keywords)Adding Scheduling Keywords
Scheduling Keywords can be created within Admin > Interviews and Scheduling > [Scheduling Keywords](https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/interview/scheduling-keywords "Scheduling Keywords"). When setting up your scheduling keywords, you can assign them to one of two different categories:
**Free:** If these keywords are detected in a calendar event title, candidates will be able to schedule interviews over this event when booking via a direct booking link. When manually scheduling using ManualSchedule, interviews booked over or overlapping these events will not be counted as a scheduling conflict.
**Soft Conflicts:** Calendar events with these keywords in the event title can’t be booked over by candidates booking with a direct booking link. If scheduling an interview using ManualSchedule, any interviews that overlap with these events will be seen as a soft conflict.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/b890e1fe326958c79ac8d6e5cb969c38.png)
﻿
Please note that if the calendar event is set as Free instead of Busy within the event settings in the interviewer’s Google or Microsoft 365 calendar, then candidates will be able to schedule over these when scheduling with a direct booking link, even if it’s labelled as a soft conflict in Ashby.
Click in the New keyword… field, type in your keyword and then click the _+_ icon to add the keyword to the list. Keywords are not case sensitive but any spaces or special characters will be respected. You cannot add the same keyword to both the Free and Soft Conflict lists.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/keywords#scheduling-when-scheduling-keywords-are-being-appl)Scheduling when Scheduling Keywords are being applied
_Free Scheduling Keywords_
When using ManualSchedule to book in interviews, any calendar events with titles that contain any scheduling keywords in the Free category will show up in light purple.
When scheduling over that event, the interviewer will show as available. Here is an example where ‘Quick sync’ is a scheduling keyword that has been added in the Free category.
If sending a direct booking link out to the candidate, they will also see this time as available to book.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/174ab4eb1a96e54cfbed5839e3ee7b6d.png)
﻿
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/e8bf1e3946b0a7bf44da8f7d1c214408.png)
﻿
﻿  

_Soft Conflict Scheduling Keywords_
When using Quick Schedule to schedule in an interview, calendar events with titles that contain any scheduling keywords in the Soft Conflict category will show in light orange.
If scheduling over that event, a soft conflict will be highlighted beside the interviewer’s details.
A soft conflict event will not show as available to candidates booking via a direct booking link.
﻿  

![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/f3c7eda74eef742427d616707b31a337.png)
﻿
﻿  

﻿  

##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/keywords#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/keywords#are-scheduling-keywords-still-detected-on-calendar)**Are scheduling keywords still detected on calendar events that are set as** _**Private**_**?**
If an event set as Private includes a scheduling keyword in the event title, we will still identify and mark is as Free or Soft Conflict accordingly. The title of the event would still show as _Busy_ and the keyword identified will not be shown when scheduling.
![Document image](https://cdn.zappy.app/dedcdbf93993f0ee11eb13f83f50155e.png)
﻿
﻿

[![Website logo](https://archbee-image-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/0p8tQ--ZZOxRtkVp6Qr-e/SIHH4C2peMjgawXW2Y0OC_ashby-docs-logo-light.svg)](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/)
Billing & Plans
# Seat-based Pricing
8 min
Copy
This guide provides more information on seats and seat-based pricing. Have additional questions not answered here? Please feel free to reach out to contracts@ashbyhq.com for further assistance.
**Please note:** Not all Ashby accounts are on seat-based pricing. Our Foundations Plan, for companies of <100 employees, is priced based on their total workforce size, structured into 5 simple pricing tiers. You can view the Foundations Plan pricing tiers [here!](https://www.ashbyhq.com/pricing "here!")﻿
While many ATS providers choose to price their product(s) based on the total size of your organization, here at Ashby we believe that seat-based pricing more closely aligns to the range of needs at larger organizations. We also believe that the transparency created in this model means you won’t be surprised by black-box price hikes at renewal.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#how-you-are-billed-for-users)How you are billed for users
You are billed for the users who have been provisioned a paid seat in your organization. Other user types are free.
**Paid Seats**
  * Elevated Access Users


  * Organizational Admin Users


**Free**
  * Limited Access Users


  * Agency Users


More information on these roles and their permissions can be found at [User Permissions](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/user-permissions)﻿﻿
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#adding-paid-seats-during-your-billing-period)Adding paid seats during your billing period
If you pay monthly, we’ll charge for the number of paid seats you have provisioned at the start of each monthly billing period. You will not be charged for increases in paid seats that take place during the billing period.
If you pay annually, and you’ve provisioned more paid seats than you’ve already paid for, we’ll calculate the prorated cost and send you an invoice or charge your card once per calendar quarter for any outstanding balance.
_Here’s an example_
In this example, let’s say you’ve purchased 20 Ashby All-in-One paid seats at $795/user/year each. One month into your annual subscription you’ve provisioned 5 additional paid seats, to bring your total to 25.
To calculate the prorated cost for each new user, we divide the cost per user by the 365 days in the year, then multiply by the days remaining: ($795 / 365 days) * 335 days = $729.65.
Additional 5 paid seats * $729.65 = $3,648.29
If you pay by credit card, this amount will be charged, or if you pay by invoice this amount will be invoiced at the end of the quarter.
##  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#faq)FAQ
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#who-needs-a-paid-seat)**Who needs a paid seat?**
  * Your TA team, including operations


  * Hiring Managers with open roles to fill


  * Leadership or heads of departments for analytics


###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#how-many-paid-seats-do-i-need)**How many paid seats do I need?**
There are many factors that influence this including company size, growth rate, and hiring practices. Your Ashby Account Executive can help you scope your deployment.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#do-i-need-to-talk-to-ashby-sales-or-support-to-add)**Do I need to talk to Ashby Sales or Support to add seats?**
No. We’ve designed the provisioning process to minimize friction when you need to add additional paid seats to keep hiring moving quickly. Billing takes place after the fact with the calculations noted above.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#can-i-reassign-paid-seats)**Can I reassign paid seats?**
Yes! By transitioning one Elevated Access user to Limited Access and promoting a different Limited Access user to Elevated Access you have reallocated permissions, without increasing the total number of paid seats. This is common practice for occasionally active Hiring Managers.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#if-i-reduce-the-quantity-of-paid-seats-i-have-prov)**If I reduce the quantity of paid seats I have provisioned will I be issued any credit?**
No, once you have purchase a paid seat, you have it available to use through to the end of your subscription term. At your renewal you could reduce the quantity of paid seats.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#why-does-ashby-success-have-a-user-will-that-count)**Why does Ashby Success have a user? Will that count against my paid seats?**
No, the Ashby Success user will never count against any of the seats that you’ve purchased. Our Support and Customer Success teams use it to access your account upon request when working with your team.
###  [](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/seat-based-pricing#how-do-i-provision-a-new-user)**How do I provision a new user?**
More can be found on provisioning users at [How do I provision new users in my Ashby account manually?](https://docs.ashbyhq.com/how-do-i-provision-new-users-in-my-ashby-account-manually)﻿﻿
